articles.headline: Two antimalarials plus primaquine equally effective against P vivax
articles.title_original: Efficacy of chloroquine plus low-dose primaquine and pyronaridine-artesunate plus low-dose primaquine against Plasmodium vivax malaria in adults and transmission to mosquitoes in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.
articles.authors: Bezabih MK, Sisay S, Behaksera SW, Ramjith J, Teka H, Girma S, Shimelash A, Ejigu LA, Kassa FA, Hailegiorgis B, Tadesse M, Misganaw T, Gizat A, Gashaw A, Habtamu G, Legesse NS, Hundera NL, Milki DW, Abdo M, Chali W, Tongren JE, Hwang J, Bousema T, Tadesse FG
articles.details: Phase 2/3 observer-masked randomized trial comparing chloroquine plus low-dose primaquine vs pyronaridine-artesunate plus low-dose primaquine for uncomplicated P vivax in 176 adults (79-83 analyzed per group). Day-28 adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR): 98.7% for chloroquine vs 98.8% for pyronaridine-artesunate; day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine-artesunate was 96.4%. Both regimens were well tolerated with similar mild adverse events.

articles.headline: Age, wealth, education, nets predict malaria in sub-Saharan children
articles.title_original: Determinants of malaria prevalence among children under 5 years of age in 17 sub-Saharan African countries: insights from Malaria Indicator Surveys.
articles.authors: Kassie AT, Bazezew AM, Tamir TT, Tekeba B, Zeleke GA, Mekonen EG, Endalew HL, Wassie M, Zegeye AF
articles.details: Secondary analysis of Malaria Indicator Surveys (2016-2022) from 51,457 children under 5 in 17 sub-Saharan African countries. Multilevel logistic regression identified risk factors: age 1-2 years (AOR 1.46), low household wealth (AOR 1.94), rural residence (AOR 2.69), high community illiteracy (AOR 1.76). Protective factors: maternal primary education (AOR 0.84), insecticide-treated bed nets (AOR 0.80). More than one-third of children in several countries were infected.

articles.headline: Caretakers struggle with children's post-malaria neurologic deficits
articles.title_original: Perceptions and experiences of caretakers of children with neurologic deficits after severe malaria in Uganda.
articles.authors: Okullo AE, Kisakye S, Idro R, Boele van Hensbroek M, John CC, Kaawa-Mafigiri D
articles.journal: Int J Qual Stud Health Well-being
articles.details: Qualitative phenomenological study via 23 in-depth interviews and 4 focus group discussions in Uganda examining experiences of caretakers of children with neurologic deficits following severe malaria. Deficits included motor, movement, cognitive, behavioral, and sensory deficits plus epilepsy. Eight themes emerged: mixed perceptions about deficits, difficulty educating impaired children, community mistreatment, mental/emotional distress, financial strain, misconceptions, limited specialized care access, and adaptation to new lifestyle.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine and artesunate-pyronaridine effective despite drug-resistant markers
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the effectiveness of artemether-lumefantrine and artesunate-pyronaridine each combined with single low-dose primaquine in adults with Plasmodium falciparum infection in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.
articles.authors: Bezabih MK, Misganaw T, Ejigu LA, Rosillo S, Teka H, Alemayehu DH, Adnew B, Hailegiorgis B, Girma S, Tadesse M, Kassa FA, Gebremichael M, Tefera DA, Chali W, Behaksera SW, Alemayehu L, Deressa JD, Abdo M, Habtamu G, Gizat A, Yalew WG, Jember TH, Bitew M, Mumba P, Plucinski MM, Souza S, Patel DS, Talundzic E, Taffese HS, Geleta KE, Mehari D, Bayissa GA, Bousema T, Tongren JE, Hwang J, Tadesse FG
articles.details: Phase 2/3 observer-masked randomized trial with 199 adults with uncomplicated P falciparum (median age 25) in a region with high pfkelch13 and pfhrp2/3 gene deletions. Artemether-lumefantrine plus primaquine achieved day-28 PCR-corrected ACPR of 96.8% vs 98.9% for pyronaridine-artesunate; day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine-artesunate was 96.5%. No early treatment failures occurred; both had similar mild adverse events.

articles.headline: Respiratory distress, DIC, and neurologic involvement predict malaria-AKI mortality
articles.title_original: Prognostic factors in malaria patients with acute kidney injury: a systematic review and meta analysis.
articles.authors: Tear AA, Fauziah N, Riswari SF, Purba AR, Akhlaq MM, Susanto NH
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 16 observational studies with 1,104 malaria patients with acute kidney injury identified prognostic mortality factors. Strongest associations: ventilator requirement (OR 30.35), respiratory distress syndrome (OR 18.50), hypotension (OR 18.23), neurological involvement (OR 12.83), DIC (OR 12.00), jaundice (OR 5.03), acidosis (OR 4.88), leukocytosis (OR 4.87), hyperkalemia (OR 3.99), hyponatremia (OR 3.67), oliguria (OR 3.41).

articles.headline: Malaria more common in family replacement than voluntary blood donors
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted infections among blood donors in Saudi Arabia: demographic and malaria risk differences by donation purpose.
articles.authors: Alshalani A, Alzamil L, Alrezaihi A, Alsughayyir J, Bin Muhannaa NI, Alshehri FS, Almuqrin AM
articles.details: Retrospective cross-sectional analysis of 49,590 blood donors at King Fahad Medical City, Riyadh (10.6% family replacement, 89.4% voluntary). Malaria prevalence significantly higher in family replacement donors (5.3%) vs voluntary donors (2.9%), particularly among non-Saudi nationals. No significant differences between groups for hepatitis B, C, HIV, or syphilis. Regression confirmed nationality modified the association between donation type and malaria positivity.

articles.headline: Matrix-M-adjuvanted vaccines safe across multiple diseases including R21
articles.title_original: Safety of Matrix-M-adjuvanted COVID-19, seasonal influenza, combination influenza-COVID-19, and malaria vaccines: a review of the evidence.
articles.authors: Fix J, Lee S, Nachbar J, Sadadia P, Lövgren Bengtsson K, Stertman L, Palm AE, Walker R, Draghia Akli R, Sellers S
articles.details: Comprehensive review of 66 clinical trials and post-marketing studies of Matrix-M-adjuvanted vaccines. 64,101 participants received ≥143,170 doses: 55,939 COVID-19, 2,477 influenza, 1,422 combination COVID-19-influenza, 4,263 malaria vaccine doses. R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine administered to >10 million people worldwide. All authorized and candidate vaccines well-tolerated across wide geographic distribution, immunocompromised populations, and children with favorable reactogenicity profiles.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets overcome pyrethroid resistance but risk emerging resistance
articles.title_original: Chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets for pyrethroid-resistant malaria vectors: efficacy, resistance risks, and policy implications.
articles.authors: John Mwang'onde B
articles.details: Systematic review of 31 studies (2010-2024) on chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets for pyrethroid-resistant vectors. Nets demonstrated 1.8-fold increase in mosquito mortality vs pyrethroid-only nets (95% CI 1.5-2.1). Community trials reported 40-60% reductions in malaria incidence and entomological inoculation rates. Early signs of chlorfenapyr resistance in Anopheles gambiae in Central Africa (RR 2.4, p=0.01) linked to CYP6P4 metabolic overexpression; agricultural pesticide use correlated with vector resistance (r=0.62).

articles.headline: Asymptomatic carriers harbor artemisinin-resistant P falciparum in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic malaria: a hidden reservoir for the transmission and circulation of artemisinin partial-resistant Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Mazigo E, Lee WJ, Jun H, Fitriana F, Syahada JH, Louis JM, Muh F, Lu F, Lee KJ, Nam JW, Han JH, Kwon TH, Lee SJ, Na S, Chun W, Park WS, Han ET, Todd J, Manjurano A, Kidima W, Han JH
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional study in Tanzania (December 2022-July 2023) of 3,489 participants. Among 802 sequenced isolates: 3.0% carried pfkelch13 partial-resistance markers (Y493H 0.2%, R561H 2.0%, A675V 0.7%) in high-transmission areas; all low-transmission isolates wild-type. pfmdr1 N86 retained in all; NFD haplotype detected in 48.1% (high-transmission) and 48.4% (low-transmission) regions. Mutations more frequent in children <5 and females.

articles.headline: Cold stress inhibits P falciparum growth by impairing erythrocyte invasion
articles.title_original: Cold stress restricts malaria parasite growth by affecting erythrocyte invasion.
articles.authors: Odagawa T, Saiki E, Kanuka H
articles.details: In vitro and in vivo experimental study of cold stress on P. falciparum development. At 27-33°C, parasite growth suppressed in temperature-dependent manner; mouse exposure to 4°C reduced parasitemia in rodent malaria model. Cold stress enhanced growth suppression in combination with low-dose artesunate. Parasites exposed to cold showed decreased erythrocyte invasion efficiency with broad downregulation of invasion genes and reduced ama1 expression.

articles.headline: P falciparum exhibits stage-specific RNA editing in nuclear genes
articles.title_original: Genome-wide detection of conserved stage-specific RNA editing events for nuclear genes in the Plasmodium falciparum 3D7 malaria parasite.
articles.authors: Azad MTA, Sugi T, Qulsum U, Kato K
articles.details: Genome-wide analysis of RNA editing in tightly synchronized P. falciparum 3D7 at 16h, 24h, 32h, and 40h stages using REDItools computational approaches. Detected extensive A-to-G, G-to-A, and T-to-C editing events with stage-specific patterns; G-to-A and C-to-T events at highest levels. RNA editing peaked at 32h trophozoite stage and declined at 40h schizont stage. RNA binding and catalytic activity pathways downregulated at 40h stage.

articles.headline: P relictum avian malaria shows cryptic species and genetic introgression
articles.title_original: Phylogenomic analyses reveal introgression and cryptic speciation in the globally distributed, vector-transmitted pathogen Plasmodium relictum.
articles.authors: Weyer E, Theodosopoulos AN, Palinauskas V, Bensch S, Westerdahl H, Duc M, Ciloglu A, Huang X, Kim KS, Tsuda Y, Atkinson CT, Zehtindjiev P, Fleischer RC, VanTassel NM, Taylor SA, Hellgren O, Ellis VA
articles.details: Phylogenomic analysis of avian malaria pathogen P. relictum using sequence capture from 52 infections across multiple continents. Maximum likelihood and Bayesian phylogenomic trees with multi-species coalescent models revealed two cryptic species with genetic introgression between them. One species (GRW4) devastated endemic Hawaiian avifauna following introduction ~100 years ago. Hawaiian GRW4 clade showed no evidence of positive selection despite hypothesis of release from host pressure.

articles.headline: Indonesian medicinal plants as antimalarial sources
articles.title_original: Harnessing Indonesian natural products against neglected tropical diseases: Malaria.
articles.authors: Nugraha AS, Nawiyanto, Muhyidin A, Qonitah ZN, Anggraeni BA, Triatmoko B, Pratama ANW, Keller PA, Siregar JE, Khasanah U, Wangchuk P
articles.details: Review of 208 Indonesian medicinal plants with antimalarial activity; 99 crude extracts from 61 species evaluated against P. falciparum strains. Seventeen species underwent chromatographic isolation yielding 146 compounds with several showing potent anti-plasmodial activity: samaradine X (IC50 14 nM), norcaesalpinins E (90 nM), methyl 15-acetoxylansiolate (170 nM), dehatrine (170 nM), compared to chloroquine (IC50 290 nM). Findings highlight potential of Indonesian medicinal flora for new antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: Modern housing reduces malaria risk across African settings
articles.title_original: Housing and malaria risk: Evidence across ecological and climatic gradients in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: AlAbbas M
articles.details: Analysis of pooled DHS and MIS data from 17 countries (2006-2022; n=206,723) examining housing quality and malaria RDT positivity. Modern housing was associated with lower odds of RDT positivity (β=-0.543, p<0.001), independent of socioeconomic status, WASH, net usage, and ecological-climatic factors. Predicted probabilities of positive RDTs varied by ecological context but remained lower in modern dwellings, supporting housing as a non-insecticidal determinant of malaria vulnerability.

articles.headline: Simplified mathematical model preserves malaria transmission dynamics
articles.title_original: Model reduction and analysis: A case study of a malaria control model.
articles.authors: Korsah MA, Johnston ST, Tiedje K, Day K, Walker CR, Flegg JA
articles.details: Study comparing a reduced mathematical model with a comprehensive malaria control model using Bayesian MCMC, with parameter identifiability and sensitivity analyses. The reduced model captured essential transmission characteristics while providing computational flexibility. Results demonstrate the simplified system exhibits similar dynamics to the full model, making it valuable for evaluating and implementing malaria control strategies.

articles.headline: Comment on non-endemic malaria and Chagas disease Brazil
articles.title_original: Comment on "Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024)".
articles.authors: V VKK, Kshirsagar P, Mishra M, Panwar S
articles.details: Comment on national surveillance data comparing malaria and Chagas disease epidemiology in non-endemic settings in Brazil (2007-2024).

articles.headline: P. vivax MSP-142 genetic diversity in Vietnamese isolates
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of merozoite surface protein 1 C-terminal 42 kDa in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates.
articles.authors: Nguyễn ĐTD, Nguyễn TH, Võ TC, Lê HG, Van Khanh C, Trinh NTM, Hanh NTL, Cho M, Goo YK, Quang HH, Na BK
articles.details: Analysis of 88 Vietnamese P. vivax isolates from four endemic provinces examining PvMSP-142 genetic polymorphism as a vaccine candidate. Found genetic polymorphisms from nucleotide substitutions and meiotic recombination; most polymorphic sites in PvMSP-133, while PvMSP-119 was relatively well-conserved with balancing selection preserving diversity. Global PvMSP-142 displayed similar polymorphic features; findings inform vaccine development for this antigen.

articles.headline: pfcrt and pfmdr1 mutations declining in Gabon P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Status of pfcrt and pfmdr1 mutations in Plasmodium falciparum isolates in Gabon in 2019 after chloroquine withdrawal.
articles.authors: Dinzouna-Boutamba SD, Iroungou BA, Akombi FL, Yacka-Mouele L, Moon Z, Lee S, Na BK, Hong Y, Goo YK
articles.details: Analysis of 70 P. falciparum isolates from Gabon (2019) via Sanger sequencing found pfcrt SNPs in 25.71% of samples and pfmdr1 SNPs in 20%. Most frequent pfcrt mutation N75(E/K) at 24.3%; wild-type CVMNK haplotype predominated at 74.29%, with mutant CVIET at 17.13%, substantially lower than previously reported. Results indicate persistence of mutant genotypes with declining frequencies, suggesting progressive restoration of chloroquine susceptibility.

articles.headline: Critical care management of HIV, TB, and malaria
articles.title_original: The Global Intensive Care Unit: Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Tuberculosis, and Malaria.
articles.authors: Riunga F, Wambua GM, Lee BW
articles.details: Review of critical care management for HIV, tuberculosis, and malaria in low-income and middle-income countries, where these infectious diseases are leading causes of critical care admissions with high mortality. Highlights disease burden, clinical presentation, diagnostic strategies, and management principles for these conditions in critically ill patients. Emphasizes importance of early recognition and timely initiation of appropriate therapies for improving outcomes.

articles.headline: Anacardic acid reduces inflammation in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Saturated anacardic acid shows promise as adjuvant therapy for preventing severe malaria via attenuation of inflammatory response by modulation of the NF-κB pathway.
articles.authors: Gontijo ACGC, Corazza D, Albuquerque LFF, Ciarlini ACL, de Oliveira AS, Monteiro NC, de Oliveira MS, Couto SCP, Romeiro LAS, Borges TKDS, Muniz-Junqueira MI
articles.details: Experimental study of saturated anacardic acid (SAA) as adjuvant therapy for severe malaria using P. berghei ANKA infection in CBA mice and cultured THP-1 monocytes and BV-2 microglia. SAA increased survival in infected mice and decreased TNF-α, IL-8, MCP-1, ROS, NO, and COX-2 expression while increasing IL-6 production. Demonstrates SAA modulates key inflammatory mediators and NF-κB pathway components involved in severe malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Corrigendum on urbanization and malaria-microbiome interactions
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to 'Interactions between urbanization, malaria infection and avian cloacal microbiome' [Environ. Res. 297 (2026) 124073].
articles.authors: Garrigós M, Jiménez-Peñuela J, Saavedra I, Veiga J, García-López MJ, Garrido M, Ruiz-López MJ, Figuerola J, Moreno-Indias I, Martínez-de la Puente J
articles.details: Corrigendum correcting a prior publication on interactions between urbanization, malaria infection, and avian cloacal microbiome.

articles.headline: Multi-epitope malaria vaccine designed via immunoinformatics
articles.title_original: Designing a next-generation multi-stage vaccine for malaria prevention.
articles.authors: Arya H, Tripathi H, Bhatt TK
articles.details: Immunoinformatic design of three multi-epitope subunit vaccine constructs targeting five Plasmodium proteins, incorporating adjuvants, CTL epitopes, HTL epitopes, B-cell epitopes, and linkers. Constructs assessed as antigenic, non-allergenic, and non-toxic; molecular docking with TLR-4 showed stable binding and molecular dynamics simulations confirmed strong interactions. In silico cloning indicated potential for E. coli expression; in vitro and in vivo validation required.

articles.headline: P. vivax merozoite surface protein 3 allelic diversity Pakistan
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein 3α and β field isolates.
articles.authors: Shaheen N, Ashraf A, Malik N, Naeem M, Zeb I, Nabi G, Alwethaynani MS, Mohamed RAEH, Elkhadragy MF, Al-Hoshani N
articles.details: Molecular epidemiology study of 207 malaria samples from Azad Jammu and Kashmir, Pakistan (March-August 2024) found 188/207 (90.82%) P. vivax and 19/207 (9.18%) P. falciparum. Pvmsp-3α showed three allelic variants (type A 66.33%, B 11.22%, C 13.27%); Pvmsp-3β showed two variants (type A 76.67%, type B 23.33%). Demonstrates P. vivax genotypic complexity and polymorphism relevant to molecular epidemiology and control strategies.

articles.headline: Black cumin-enriched cookies ameliorate malaria-diabetes comorbidity
articles.title_original: Functional-Diet Potential of Black Cumin-Enriched Acha-Based Cookies on Plasmodium berghei-Infected Diabetic Mice.
articles.authors: Nwanna EE, Olanrewaju BT, Oyeleye IS, Ojueromi OO, Oboh G
articles.details: Experimental study in mice with streptozotocin-induced type 2 diabetes (35 mg/kg) followed by P. berghei NK65 strain infection, fed black cumin-enriched acha-based cookies for 14 days (n=8 per group). Measured parasitemia suppression and blood glucose levels at 3-day intervals; assessed pancreatic enzyme activities and inflammatory markers. Results showed reversal of damage in malaria-infected diabetic mice compared to negative control.

articles.headline: Multi-antigen vaccine elicits Th1 response, reduces transmission
articles.title_original: Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi.
articles.authors: Pourhashem Z, Siasi S, Nourani L, Yousefi H, Sani JJ, Vand-Rajabpour H, Raz A, Pirahmadi S, Zargar M, Djadid ND, Zakeri S, Mehrizi AA
articles.details: Researchers evaluated a triple-antigen transmission-blocking vaccine (APN-1, CelTOS, cd-HAP) with adjuvants (CPG, MPL, QS-21) in BALB/c mice. The vaccine induced robust IgG responses against all three antigens without antigenic interference and triggered Th1-type cellular responses (increased IFN-γ and TNF production). Standard Membrane Feeding Assay with P. falciparum NF54 gametocytes and Anopheles stephensi mosquitoes demonstrated substantial reduction in oocyst load.

articles.headline: Copper complex shows anti-malarial activity in vitro
articles.title_original: Synthesis, Characterization, and Anti-Plasmodium falciparum Activity of a New Copper(II) Complex Containing 2-(Tert-Butoxy)-6-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)pyridine.
articles.authors: Patiño-Cubides A, Velasco-Pareja MC, Macías MA, Yasnot-Acosta MF, Rodriguez A, Valderrama-Aguirre A, Hurtado JJ
articles.details: A copper(II) coordination complex containing an imidazole-based ligand was synthesized and evaluated in vitro against P. falciparum (3D7 strain, chloroquine-sensitive). The complex exhibited low hemocytotoxicity (≤20% at concentrations >500 µg/mL) and demonstrated significantly higher activity against P. falciparum than the free ligand alone, indicating coordination to the metal center enhances antiparasitic activity.

articles.headline: Systematic review: antimicrobial peptides for malaria therapy
articles.title_original: Antimicrobial peptides in malaria and tuberculosis management: a systematic review of emerging evidence.
articles.authors: Walter NS, Gorki V, Medhi B, Avti PK
articles.details: Systematic review of antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) against malaria, covering APD3 database (till June 2025) and PubMed (1965–June 2025). Anti-malarial AMPs include defensins and cecropins with α-helical, β-sheet, or random-coil structures. AMPs demonstrate promising anti-malarial efficacy through novel mechanisms despite varying toxicity; synthetic AMPs designed to enhance activity and selectivity while reducing toxicity offer potential alternatives to tackle multidrug-resistant parasites.

articles.headline: Retraction: seaweed nanoparticles study withdrawn
articles.title_original: Retraction notice to 'Seaweed Sargassum wightii mediated preparation of TiO(2) nanoparticles, larvicidal activity against malaria and filariasis vectors, and its effect on non-target organisms' [Environ. Res. 225 (2023) 115569].
articles.authors: Mathivanan D, Kamaraj C, Suseem SR, Gandhi PR, Malafaia G
articles.details: Retraction notice for previously published work on seaweed-derived TiO₂ nanoparticles and their larvicidal activity against malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria prevalence 5% in Yemen; linked to environment
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic malaria and its associated factors in the Al Zuhrah district, Al-Hodeidah Government, Yemen, 2022.
articles.authors: Hajjam MA, Al Amad MA, Nasher SS
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 422 participants in Al Zuhrah District found 5% asymptomatic malaria prevalence, with P. falciparum accounting for 95% of infections. Education was protective (APOR=0.35, 95% CI 0.13–0.97), while absence of window screens (APOR=9.56, 95% CI 2.15–42.51) and proximity to stagnant water (APOR=4.93, 95% CI 1.50–16.23) significantly increased infection risk.

articles.headline: Pf12 and Pf41 show genetic conservation and robust antibody recognition
articles.title_original: Conserved genetic and antigenic profiles of Pf12 and Pf41 in asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum carriers in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Fitriana F, Lee WJ, Sim HY, Jun H, Syahada JH, Muh F, Lee SJ, Na S, Lee KJ, Nam JW, Han ET, Todd J, Manjurano A, Kidima W, Mazigo E, Han JH
articles.details: Analysis of 172 P. falciparum isolates from asymptomatic individuals in Tanzania revealed low nucleotide diversity for Pf12 (π=0.00042±0.00007) and Pf41 (π=0.00061±0.00004), both under selective constraints. Seroprevalence was high: Pf12 97.7%, Pf41 98.3%, with strongly correlated IgG levels (ρ=0.721, p<0.001). The predominant Pf41 substitution was S232R (47.8%), with limited overall polymorphism supporting these merozoite antigens as vaccine candidates.

articles.headline: Review: parasite invasion and immune evasion mechanisms
articles.title_original: Host-Pathogen Interactions in Malaria: Invasion, Neutralization, and Evasion.
articles.authors: Dickey TH, Tolia NH
articles.details: Review of receptor-ligand interactions between Plasmodium parasites and the human host, focusing on clinically relevant antigens. The parasite employs multiple proteins for motility, cell traversal, and invasion at each life-cycle stage. The human immune system targets key antigens, and the parasite employs several immune-evasion mechanisms; understanding this evolutionary back-and-forth has inspired vaccine and therapeutic development.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis: ITN use disparities linked to geography and socioeconomics
articles.title_original: Insecticide-treated nets utilization and determinants in Southeast Asia toward malaria elimination: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Soe BK, Huertas-López A, Nugraheni YR, Adisakwattana P
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 20 studies from 2003–2023 comprising 14,556 households and 122,258 individuals found substantial disparity between insecticide-treated net (ITN) ownership and actual use in Southeast Asia. Geographic region was the strongest determinant of non-use: coastal versus delta regions (OR=4.73, 95% CI 1.59–14.06). Socioeconomic status showed higher non-use in the poorest versus richest (OR=1.70, 95% CI 1.01–2.86).

articles.headline: Synergistic antibody pools enhance RIPR-targeted vaccine efficacy
articles.title_original: Analysis of monoclonal antibodies against the malaria invasion complex protein RIPR reveals the structural basis for synergistic antibody protection.
articles.authors: Williams BG, Barrett JR, Scott JB, Rigby CA, Cagiada M, Quinkert D, McHugh K, Huhn A, Burnap SA, Gourjault C, Byrne F, Raghavan SSR, Rodrigues A, Bergamaschi L, Balzarotti B, Watson S, Miller N, King LDW, Donnellan FR, Gladstone CA, Paterson J, Scalabrino S, Silk SE, Salkeld J, Minassian AM, Skinner K, Struwe WB, Deane CM, Reece ST, Ward AB, Draper SJ
articles.details: Characterization of 83 human monoclonal antibodies against RIPR (RH5-interacting protein), a component of the PCRCR blood-stage invasion complex, revealed that single mAbs had minimal neutralizing activity. Pools targeting the RIPR-tail region showed high synergistic inhibition; mAbs targeting EGF-like domains 6–8 synergized to constrain RIPR-tail conformation. Addition of these mAbs to sera from R78C (RIPREGF (7-8)-CyRPA) vaccine-immunized humans enhanced malaria growth inhibition.

articles.headline: Cambodia digital malaria surveillance enables rapid case response
articles.title_original: Digital Health Center App for Community and National Malaria Surveillance in Cambodia: Implementation Case Study.
articles.authors: Ngor P, Rekol H, Thyda E, Rith R, Kimleng S, Rattana Y, Vunsokserey O, Vanna H, Pumeoung C, Ringwald P, Maude RJ, White L, Sovannaroth S
articles.details: Implementation of Android-based health center app for real-time, case-based malaria reporting integrated with Cambodia's national Malaria Information System. Between January–July 2025, 69 cases were reported (23 locally acquired, 7 domestically imported, 39 internationally imported); 95.7% were notified and classified within 1 day. Reactive case detection occurred within 3 days for all 21 eligible cases, and 16 of 19 foci received response within 7 days, with data completeness at 99% and user satisfaction at 96.3%.

articles.headline: Ginsenoside Rg1 inhibits glucose transporter, blocks resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Selective targeting of Plasmodium falciparum hexose transporter by phytochemical Ginsenoside Rg1 disrupts glucose metabolism and blocks development of parasite.
articles.authors: Shafi S, Maurya P, Bansal R, Sharma J, Mishra SK, Najmi AK, Singh S
articles.details: Natural compound Ginsenoside Rg1 was identified as a selective PfHT (P. falciparum hexose transporter) inhibitor via molecular docking and validated using yeast complementation. G-Rg1 suppressed growth of both artemisinin-sensitive and artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum in vitro and potentiated dihydroartemisinin efficacy in combination and ring survival assays. In P. berghei ANKA-infected mice, G-Rg1 alone and combined with artemisinin reduced parasite multiplication and increased mean survival time.

articles.headline: DHA-PPQ for malaria prevention doesn't alter ARV drug levels in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Effects of malaria intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine on antiretroviral drug concentrations in African pregnant women living with HIV.
articles.authors: Stöger L, Mischlinger J, Nhampossa T, Mombo-Ngoma G, Esen M, Tchouatieu A-M, Brunet M, Mendes A, Dimessa Mbadinga LB, Gamberini S, Ndzebe Ndoumba W, Figueroa-Romero A, Lell B, Lagler H, Zoleko-Manego R, El Gaaloul M, Méndez S, Botella N, Sanz S, Piqueras M, Sevene E, Saúte F, Wicha SG, Soy D, Menéndez C, Ramharter M, González R
articles.details: Clinical trial of 105 pregnant women living with HIV in Mozambique and Gabon evaluated monthly dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) for intermittent preventive treatment. Median dolutegravir was 835.1 ng/mL maternal and 1,204.2 ng/mL cord blood; median lamivudine was 108.3 ng/mL maternal and 186.5 ng/mL cord blood. No significant differences in antiretroviral concentrations were found between DHA-PPQ treatment and placebo arms, indicating DHA-PPQ does not affect ARV exposure.

articles.headline: Iron homeostasis targets mosquito fertility and malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Targeting Iron Homeostasis Curtails Mosquito Fertility and Plasmodium Transmission Potential.
articles.authors: Tupe C, Pasupareddy R, Bhatt S, Goyal B, Kumar A, Gupta A, Lalwani A, Das RN, Sunil S, Singh S, Pandey KC, Chakraborti S
articles.details: Characterized mosquito ferritin as structurally unique with exceptional heme-binding affinity. Functional knockdown of ferritin heavy-chain in adult Anopheles stephensi females caused profound defects in reproductive tissue development. β-thujaplicin was identified as a transmission-blocking agent that significantly suppressed Plasmodium oocyst development, establishing iron homeostasis as a potential dual-target axis for transmission-blocking interventions.

articles.headline: IL-33 SNPs associate with P. falciparum susceptibility and parasite burden
articles.title_original: Association of interleukin-33 gene polymorphisms with susceptibility to Plasmodium falciparum infection.
articles.authors: Aljarba NH, Alghnnam F, Al-Qahtani AA, Al-Hazani TM, Al Marzan A, Bohol MFF, Alhamlan F, Shafeai MI, Rudiny FH, Motaen AM, Alkahtani S, Al-Qahtani AA
articles.details: Case-control study in 250 P. falciparum-infected and 200 healthy controls from Jazan region, Saudi Arabia, genotyped for 13 IL-33 SNPs. rs55726619 CT was protective (OR=0.042; 95% CI 0.002-0.725; p<0.001) while rs10118795 CT increased susceptibility (OR=1.584; 95% CI 1.068-2.350; p=0.022). In children aged 1-5 years, rs10815393 CC and rs55726619 CC were strongly enriched (adjusted ORs >150; p<0.001), suggesting age-dependent genetic effects.

articles.headline: Precipitation timing predicts malaria risk across Mozambique regions
articles.title_original: Exploratory analysis of the correlation between precipitation and storms and malaria cases from national surveillance in Mozambique.
articles.authors: Zhu H, Zhang L, Khan E, Srivastava J, Croal M, Candrinho B, da Silva M, Searle KM
articles.details: District-level analysis of malaria cases and precipitation data using Poisson generalized linear mixed models. Nationally found 8-week temporal lag between precipitation and increased malaria relative risk. Regional heterogeneity emerged: Northern Region 6-8 weeks, Central Region 10-12 weeks, Southern Region 10-13 weeks, highlighting importance of spatial variation for targeted interventions.

articles.headline: p38-MAPK degradation reduces Plasmodium growth and invasion
articles.title_original: Chemical degradation of Human p38-MAPK reveals it as a potential host target to combat parasitic infections by Leishmania donovani and Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Singhal J, Rawat N, Bansal R, Gupta A, Srivastava P, Madan E, Jha N, Ghouse Peer GD, Pandey RP, Ranganathan A, Singh S
articles.details: NR-7h PROTAC degraded host p38-MAPK in dose-dependent manner. In P. falciparum infection, growth inhibition and merozoite invasion assays revealed impaired parasite growth and invasion, indicating p38-MAPK signaling is essential for intraerythrocytic development. Combination with Amphotericin B enhanced parasite killing, suggesting p38-MAPK as a potential host-directed antimalarial target.

articles.headline: Extraction-free CRISPR-RPA assay detects malaria from whole blood
articles.title_original: Extract-free one-pot ambient RPA-CRISPR detection of Plasmodium in whole blood.
articles.authors: Liao J, Su Y, Jiang F
articles.details: One-pot RPA-CRISPR assay detected P. falciparum and vivax directly from crude blood lysates at ambient temperature in 116 positive and 109 negative control specimens. Sensitivity was 93.1% and specificity 100%, with sensitivity reaching 97.8% for high-density, 95.1% for moderate, and 82.8% for low-density infections. Detection limit was 100 copies/μL (~12-20 parasites/μL), completed in 40 minutes.

articles.headline: Selective lysyl-tRNA synthetase inhibitors show multistage antimalarial activity
articles.title_original: Structure-Guided Optimization of Novel Inhibitors of Plasmodium Lysyl-tRNA Synthetase with Multistage Activity against Malaria Parasites.
articles.authors: Forte B, Bellany F, Campbell PS, Chemi G, Dawson A, Anderson M, Aniweh Y, Burkhard AY, Aguiar ACC, Churchyard A, Cooper CA, Dos Santos Ferreira A, Famodimu MT, Fang FG, Hu X, Huijs T, Baud D, Jansen C, Jiménez Díaz MB, Bonnert R, Boyd S, Crespo-Fernández B, Mitasev B, Montagna S, Mok S, Murugesan D, Narwal SK, Norcross NR, Okombo J, Park H, Peet C, Pereira DB, Post JM, Reader J, Riley J, Robinson DA, Shinkyo R, Simeons FRC, Simpson L, Smith A, Smith D, Striepen J, Teles CBG, van der Laak R, Uhlemann AC, Vantaux A, Wilson C, Witkowski B, Wood G, Yeo T, Zuccotto F, Angulo-Barturen I, Baum J, Bolscher JM, Guido RVC, Birkholtz LM, Delves MJ, Dembele L, Fidock DA, Gamo FJ, Kyle DE, Maher SP, Popovici J, Walpole C, Gusovsky F, Willis PA, Read KD, Gilbert IH, Baragaña B
articles.details: Structure-guided optimization identified compound 30 with >1000-fold selectivity for P. lysyl-tRNA synthetase (KRS) and oral efficacy in mouse models. Compound 30 demonstrated activity against drug-resistant and sensitive P. falciparum strains and field isolates with favorable safety and long half-life, though oral bioavailability was ultimately unachievable, limiting clinical development.

articles.headline: Low education and poor prevention access drive malaria in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Assessment of malaria prevalence and determinants among clinically suspected individuals attending Dejen Primary Hospital, East Gojjam Zone, northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Emnetu A, Habtamu A, Lemlemu A, Yeshanew S
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 385 symptomatic individuals at Dejen Primary Hospital (April-November 2024) found 46.2% malaria prevalence, with P. falciparum representing 56.1% of cases. Key determinants included low education (AOR=2.16; p=0.001), residence near breeding sites (AOR=2.15; p=0.020), and non-use of ITNs (AOR=1.72; p=0.001) or IRS (AOR=1.02; p=0.035).

articles.headline: ddPCR monitors P. vivax drug-resistance gene copy variation
articles.title_original: Droplet digital PCR assays for Pvmdr1 and Pvcrt-o gene copy number variation determination in Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Srisutham S, Boonpeng K, Pitchayatanakorn P, Kijprasong K, Nosten FH, Day NPJ, Dondorp AM, Imwong M
articles.details: Developed duplex ddPCR assay for absolute quantification of pvmdr1 and pvcrt-o gene copy numbers using pvtubulin reference, with quantification limits of 22.4±3.5 and 21.5±2.6 copies/μL respectively. Analysis of Thai field samples showed 16% of 2008 Tak isolates (N=25) carried multiple pvmdr1 copies while all possessed single pvcrt-o; 2023-2025 Kanchanaburi samples (N=97) showed no evidence of multiple gene copies.

articles.headline: SNP-based sequencing improves PCR correction for artemisinin efficacy
articles.title_original: Comparison of techniques and markers to distinguish Plasmodium falciparum recrudescence from new infection in Rwanda.
articles.authors: Cantoreggi SL, Golumbeanu M, Zwyer M, Uwimana A, Niyonzima JD, Mbituyumuremyi A, Lucchi NW, Plucinski MM, Nsanzabana C
articles.details: Therapeutic efficacy study in Rwanda compared capillary electrophoresis (msp1, msp2, glurp, microsatellites) with amplicon deep sequencing (cpmp, ama1, cpp, csp SNP-rich markers) using different decision algorithms. Amplicon sequencing of SNP-rich markers provided most consistent results regardless of algorithm used and superior sensitivity for detecting recrudescence versus new infection.

articles.headline: Floods drive one-third of malaria burden in Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Geospatial mapping of malaria risk in flood-prone zones of Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Eudaric J, de Ruiter MC, Sairam N, Camero A, Shahi KR, Smith MW, Zhu XX, Kreibich H
articles.details: Geospatial analysis integrating Global Flood Database (2000-2018), Malaria Atlas Project incidence, and population data across 492 flood-prone zones in 38 countries. Approximately 12 million individuals with P. falciparum cases were exposed to flooding events, representing one-third of flood-affected population, with elevated relative malaria risk in East and West Africa compared to national baselines.

articles.headline: MMV367 rapidly clears blood-stage P. falciparum in volunteer infection
articles.title_original: The pyrrolidinamide antimalarial drug MMV367 rapidly clears blood-stage Plasmodium falciparum in healthy adults with experimental malaria.
articles.authors: Barber BE, Denhardt-Eriksson R, Guirou EA, Flynn J, Sahai N, Leelasena I, Mathison S, Moller C, Lim I, Evangelista C, Webster R, Potter AJ, Llewellyn S, Amante FH, Gower J, Peters J, Khoury DS, Thathy V, Narwal SK, Yeo T, Fidock DA, Marx MW, Gamo FJ, Sharma R, Gibson RA, Sanz L, Cahn A, Bestgen B, Chalon S
articles.details: Volunteer infection study in 12 healthy malaria-naïve adults inoculated with P. falciparum 3D7 receiving single MMV367 doses (3-1500 mg). For doses ≥20 mg, parasite clearance half-life was 2.2-4.3 hours with rapid viable parasite decline (1.1-2.7 hours). Model-derived minimum inhibitory concentration was 6.3 ng/ml (95% CI 5.2-7.8); no in vitro susceptibility loss or genetic resistance markers observed.

articles.headline: Mesenchymal stem cells mitigate experimental cerebral malaria via macrophage rebalancing
articles.title_original: Macrophage markers and gene signature profiling reveals mesenchymal stem cells mediated immune modulation in Plasmodium berghei ANKA infection.
articles.authors: Gupta A, Sharan Thakur R, Ojha RK, Khan T, Kalkal M, Das J
articles.details: MSC administration in C57BL/6 mice infected with P. berghei ANKA reduced parasitemia, improved survival, and preserved blood-brain barrier integrity while attenuating brain pathology. Flow cytometry and gene expression analysis revealed reduced M1 markers (decreased iNOS-2) with maintained or enhanced M2 markers (increased Arginase-1, EGR-2, GPR-18, FPR-1, PTGES-2), indicating macrophage reprogramming toward balanced M1/M2 response.

articles.headline: IMC55 protein not essential for malaria parasite blood-stage development
articles.title_original: The inner membrane complex protein, IMC55, is dispensable for intraerythrocytic development of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Vick GW, Fierro MA, Castiglione A, Park L, Brooks C, Muralidharan V
articles.details: Researchers generated conditional mutants of IMC55, a protein in the inner membrane complex of P. falciparum, to test its role in parasite replication. Despite bioinformatic predictions suggesting essentiality, functional studies found no defect in replicative fitness during the asexual blood stage. These results demonstrate that IMC55 function is either dispensable or redundant for parasite asexual growth, suggesting possible roles in other lifecycle stages.

articles.headline: Novel quinolone targeting malaria parasite cytochrome bc1 complex
articles.title_original: Revisiting 2-Substituted-4(1H)-Quinolones for Targeting the Plasmodium falciparum Cytochrome bc(1) Complex.
articles.authors: Pou S, Liebman KM, Winter RW, Nilsen A, Li Y, Lyche IN, Riscoe TM, Montgomery J, Gramelspacher MJ, Dodean RA, Zakharov LN, Nepal B, Kelly JX, Smilkstein MJ, Kortagere S, Vaidya AB, Alday PH, Doggett JS, Kudyba K, Roncal N, Ashraf K, Leed S, Lee PJ, Madejczyk MS, Roth A, Riscoe MK
articles.details: Researchers synthesized HLQ-102, the 2-position isomer of ELQ-300, and evaluated its antimalarial activity and pharmacokinetics. Cross-resistance patterns indicate HLQ-102 targets the Qi site of the P. falciparum cytochrome bc1 complex, similar to its predecessor. The findings suggest the target protein has two separate pockets capable of accommodating large structural features at either the 2- or 3-position of the quinolone ring.

articles.headline: Community acceptability of mass drug administration in rural Ghana
articles.title_original: Community perspective on feasibility of malaria mass drug administration: a qualitative evidence from the Eastern Region of Ghana.
articles.authors: Ndong IC, Chu CE, Ndang NH, Baah CA, Adu GA, Amponsah E, Peprah NY, Malm K, Ogundahunsi O, Ahorlu CS, Amambua-Ngwa A
articles.details: A cross-sectional qualitative study in Pokrom subdistrict (11 focus group discussions with 97 community members and 8 in-depth interviews) explored community perceptions following mass drug administration interventions. Participants reported notable reductions in malaria episodes and associated household healthcare costs, with adverse events (dizziness, abdominal pain, weakness) perceived as mild and transient. Despite initial challenges with community sensitization, high willingness to participate in future MDA rounds was reported.

articles.headline: Mathematical framework for modeling insecticide-treated net distribution dynamics
articles.title_original: Dynamical and time-series approach to understanding compartmental stock and flow models: a case study in malaria intervention models.
articles.authors: Tan E, van den Berg M, Vargas C, Gething PW, Symons TL
articles.details: Researchers analyzed compartmental stock-and-flow models used to track ITN distribution and attrition and proposed an adapted expectation-maximization algorithm to address calibration challenges from disparate sampling frequencies. The method was applied to delivery, distribution, and household survey data across 44 countries spanning 24 years to provide rigorous estimates of net distribution volumes. Mathematical analysis demonstrated equivalence to linear time-invariant systems and the criticality of attrition functions in model design.

articles.headline: High prevalence of pfmdr1 and K13 mutations in Uganda malaria isolates
articles.title_original: Polymorphism of pfmdr1 Gene Mutation Conferring Resistance to Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapy in Plasmodium falciparum in Patients at Gulu Regional Referral Hospital in Northern Uganda.
articles.authors: Amito FP, Angwech H, Ojok L, Wokorach G, Ochaya S, Ogwang Q, Opiro R, Echodu R
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 353 febrile participants at Gulu Regional Referral Hospital (April 2022 to January 2024) identified 214 P. falciparum cases (60.6%). Genotyping revealed high prevalence of drug-resistance mutations: pfmdr1 SNP at Codon 1034 1042 (29.6%), Codon 86 184 (28%), Codon 1246 (25.8%), and K13 propeller gene (16.7%). Combined prevalence of SNPs 86 184, 1034 1042, and K13 mutations was 90.1%, with median parasite load varying significantly among treated patients.

articles.headline: Socioeconomic and gender factors shape malaria treatment pathways in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: The social experience of malaria treatment-seeking: pathways, gender, and inequality in Jimma Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Gerbi T, Teshome D, Sudhakar M
articles.details: An ethnographic study in Jimma City (32 in-depth interviews, 8 key informant interviews, 2 focus groups) found treatment-seeking follows a pluralistic pathway beginning with home remedies and herbal treatments, progressing to biomedical care when symptoms worsen. Gender dynamics significantly influence care-seeking, with females recognizing illness first but lacking decision-making authority. Structural barriers including poverty, drug stockouts, transport constraints, and inadequate housing constrain treatment access.

articles.headline: Mixed malaria complication of lupus with endocarditis and hemorrhagic stroke
articles.title_original: Libman sacks endocarditis with multivalvular involvement, hemorrhagic stroke, and concurrent mixed plasmodium malaria in systemic lupus erythematosus: a case report.
articles.authors: Khan A, Tahir O, Batool A, Farman J, Javaid MH, Shehzad E, Soroush AR, Hassan F, Wazir HU
articles.details: A 29-year-old with 4-year SLE history presented with fever and encephalopathy (Glasgow Coma Score 7/15) with right hemiplegia. Hemolytic anemia (hemoglobin 6.0 g/dL), severe thrombocytopenia (platelets 39 × 10³/µL), elevated inflammatory markers (CRP 42.2 mg/dL), and mixed P. vivax and P. falciparum infection were documented. Echocardiography revealed multivalvular Libman-Sacks endocarditis with moderate-to-severe regurgitation; neuroimaging showed large hemorrhagic infarct (7 × 5.2 cm). Despite intravenous artesunate and supportive care, the patient had poor prognosis.

articles.headline: Malaria training improves knowledge but not treatment compliance in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Effect of malaria training on community healthcare providers' compliance with malaria treatment guidelines in Nigeria using propensity score matching.
articles.authors: Baruwa S, Akomolafe TO, Okafor EE, Ubuane O, Diallo R, Alagbile M, Ladokun J, Ekele J, Yusuf D
articles.details: A quasi-experimental evaluation (February 2022 to December 2022) using propensity score matching assessed malaria training effects on community pharmacists and patent medicine vendors. Training significantly improved knowledge of malaria diagnosis and treatment (β = 0.052; 95% CI 0.006, 0.097; p = 0.03) and acceptability of WHO test-treat-refer guidelines (β = 0.432; 95% CI: 0.041, 0.822; p = 0.03). However, training had no effect on actual compliance with treatment guidelines and identification of quality drugs.

articles.headline: Neurological manifestations predict ICU transfer in pediatric severe malaria
articles.title_original: Clinical profiles and predictors of intensive care unit transfer among children hospitalized with severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in a tertiary care hospital in Libreville, Gabon: a retrospective study.
articles.authors: Moutombi-Ditombi BC, Lembet Mikolo AM, Ndong Ngomo JM, M'bondoukwé NP, Mihindou CJ, Moussavou Mabika DA, Mawili-Mboumba DP, Ategbo SJ, Bouyou-Akotet MK
articles.details: A retrospective study reviewed children (below 17 years) hospitalized January 2021 to July 2022 at a tertiary hospital in Libreville, Gabon with P. falciparum malaria. Of 3,009 hospitalized children, 480 (15.9%) met WHO criteria for severe malaria (median age 6 years); mortality was 0.7% and 8.5% required ICU transfer. Impaired consciousness (aOR: 14.86; 95% IC [5.58-42.40]) and coma (aOR: 53.3; 95% IC [10.9-178.1]) were the strongest independent predictors of ICU transfer, with risk increasing markedly with ≥3 concurrent WHO severity criteria.

articles.headline: Sysmex XN-31 shows excellent diagnostic performance for malaria detection
articles.title_original: Field diagnostic performance of the Sysmex XN31 automated hematology analyser for malaria screening among febrile children in a sentinel site for malaria surveillance site in Libreville, Gabon.
articles.authors: Chesly MDB, Patrick MN, Bedrich PN, Adissa AA, Joanito O, Tatiana N, Patricia MD, Karine BAM
articles.details: A prospective diagnostic study of 1034 febrile children under 15 years in Libreville, Gabon evaluated the Sysmex XN-31 automated analyzer for malaria detection compared with microscopy. Overall sensitivity was 98.4% (95% CI [96.9-99.9]) and specificity 99.9% (95% CI [98.8-99.9]), though sensitivity was slightly lower in children under 5 years (96.9%) and at parasitemia below 100 parasites/µL (90.7%). ROC analysis yielded an AUC of 0.991 (95% CI [0.984-0.997]).

articles.headline: IL-9 drives myeloid cell expansion and inflammation in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: IL-9 Orchestrates MDSC Expansion and Inflammatory Programming to Amplify Immunopathology During Experimental Cerebral Malaria.
articles.authors: Ghosh P, Ghosh S, Khamaru P, Gangopadhyay A, Choudhury A, Hossain Daptary A, Halder S, Brahma S, Thakur P, Singha P, Karmakar D, Mukherjee S, Bhattacharyya A
articles.details: An experimental study in rodents infected with P. berghei ANKA investigated IL-9 in cerebral malaria pathogenesis. IL-9 promoted myeloid-derived suppressor cell (MDSC) expansion, particularly PMN-MDSCs, and enhanced their pro-inflammatory phenotype. IL-9 also induced inflammatory phenotypes in macrophages and dendritic cells. In vivo IL-9 neutralization reduced immunopathology and increased survival, while MDSC depletion decreased Th-9 cell populations, indicating IL-9 and MDSCs work through a positive feedback mechanism.

articles.headline: Non-imported severe malaria case in historically receptive Italian area
articles.title_original: Cryptic severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Maremma (Tuscany, Italy), 2024: a case report from a historically receptive area.
articles.authors: Cassol C, Rossetti B, Cosma C, Radi A, Gobbi F, Zacchini F, Stella G, Angeli G, Lanari A, Croci L, Chigiotti S, Carli T, Briganti G, Romani D, Ceriale E, Bonaccorsi G, Lorini C, Del Riccio M, Bonanni P, Nencioni C
articles.details: A case of severe, non-imported P. falciparum malaria was diagnosed in August 2024 in Grosseto area (Tuscany) in an Italian patient with no travel to endemic countries. Marked thrombocytopenia was the initial diagnostic clue; blood smear microscopy showed 10% parasitemia, confirmed by LAMP. The patient was treated with intravenous artesunate with clinical improvement. This case illustrates transmission risk in historically receptive areas with residual anophelism and presence of Anopheles labranchiae vector.

articles.headline: Novel DHODH inhibitors identified through computational drug screening
articles.title_original: Virtual screening and molecular dynamics analysis of Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) inhibitors targeting pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway.
articles.authors: Alzahrani AR, Alhuthali HM, Alzahrani S, Hawsawi NM, Alzahrani AYA, Rehman ZU, Almohammed KH, Khan A
articles.details: Virtual screening of a chemical library yielded 1,500 hits for Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH) inhibitors. Three top-ranking compounds underwent structural analysis and 500 ns molecular dynamics simulations. Compound 17433008 showed the most favorable binding free energy (-86.46 kcal/mol), followed by compound 17507474 (-85.91 kcal/mol). Both are proposed as promising lead candidates for further experimental validation and antimalarial drug development.

articles.headline: PfEMP1-specific antibodies show reduced core fucosylation linked to parasite protection
articles.title_original: Fc-fucosylation of Plasmodium falciparum-specific IgG varies with antigen and immunization.
articles.authors: Partey FD, Dos Santos MI, Seidu Z, Tiono AB, Thomson-Luque R, Wuhrer M, Larsen MD, Vidarsson G, Kurtis J, Blackman MJ, Lanzer M, Theisen M, Ofori MF, Hviid L, Lopez-Perez M
articles.details: IgG antibodies specific for PfEMP1 and PfGARP, surface antigens of P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes, were markedly Fc-afucosylated (lacking core fucose), whereas IgG to parasite internal antigens (merozoites, gametocytes, sporozoites) were fully fucosylated in naturally exposed children. Children with Fc-afucosylated PfGARP-specific IgG had reduced risk of carrying P. falciparum parasites independent of antibody levels. Antigen location on host vs. parasite membrane determines antibody glycosylation patterns and may influence parasite protection.

articles.headline: WHO endorses first malaria drug for infants, launches rapid diagnostics
articles.title_original: WHO Announces First Malaria Drug for Infants and New Rapid Diagnostic Tests.
articles.authors: Anderer S
articles.details: The World Health Organization announced approval of a novel antimalarial medication for infants and new rapid diagnostic test platforms.

articles.headline: Hemoglobin digestion pathway genes conserved in lizard Plasmodium parasites
articles.title_original: Hemoglobin Digestion Genes Are Conserved in Lizard-Infective Plasmodium Species With Different Host Cellular Niches.
articles.authors: Pangburn SJ, Borner J, Misra S, Zavalunova J, Perkins SL
articles.details: Three lizard-infective Plasmodium species (P. floridense, P. azurophilum, and P. leucocytica) transcribe genes involved in hemoglobin digestion pathways similar to mammalian Plasmodium species. Two species do not produce hemozoin (brown crystal byproduct of hemoglobin digestion) and apparently employ alternative hemoglobin processing mechanisms. Conservation of hemoglobin digestion genes across diverse Plasmodium species suggests this pathway is fundamental to parasite survival.

articles.headline: Hepatic copper accumulation triggers liver injury in malaria-infected mice
articles.title_original: Copper overload drives hepatic stellate cell activation via cuproptosis to exacerbate acute liver injury in murine malaria.
articles.authors: Mo X, Chen Q, Zhong R, Lin Y, Liu Z, Zhang X, Hou X, Lin H, Zhang M, Liu W, He J, Chen Z, Ye M, Deng Z, Jin X, Liu Z, Song J, Yin H, Huang B
articles.details: In P. berghei ANKA-infected mice, hepatic copper overload was associated with upregulation of cuproptosis markers (FDX1, DLAT, DLST) and hepatic stellate cell activation (α-SMA/Collagen I upregulated). Disulfiram (copper ionophore) exacerbated parasitemia, liver parasite burden, and fibrosis. Tetrathiomolybdate (copper chelator) effectively reversed these pathological changes. These findings implicate copper dysregulation as a mechanism driving hepatic injury during malaria.

articles.headline: Organoids and organ-on-chip systems complement mouse models in malaria research
articles.title_original: Beyond the mouse: organoids, spheroids, and organs-on-chips as the (inevitable) future of malaria research?
articles.authors: Dormoi J, Amalvict R, Pradines B
articles.details: This narrative review examines experimental models for malaria, highlighting limitations of murine models in reproducing human pathophysiology, including differences in parasite biology, antigenic variation, cytoadherence, tissue tropism, blood-brain barrier interactions, and disease kinetics—particularly for P. falciparum cerebral and placental malaria. Micro physiological systems (organoids, spheroids, organ-on-chip) are proposed as complementary platforms that incorporate human tissue architecture, flow, and multicellular interfaces to model sequestration, endothelial activation, and drug responses.

articles.headline: TaqMan PCR assays detect simian Plasmodium species at ~5-parasite sensitivity
articles.title_original: Accurate and sensitive TaqMan real-time PCR assays to detect and quantify simian malaria parasites Plasmodium cynomolgi, Plasmodium inui and Plasmodium coatneyi.
articles.authors: Ariffin MM, Syaripuddin K, Kadir KA, Lubim D, Yaman K, Divis PCS
articles.details: Species-specific real-time PCR assays were developed to detect and quantify three simian Plasmodium parasites (P. inui, P. cynomolgi, and P. coatneyi) using 18S rRNA gene hydrolysis probes. Assays achieved high specificity, limit of detection of approximately 5 parasites per PCR reaction, amplification efficiencies >95%, and linear standard curves across 10-10^6 copy range. Results were equivalent to conventional nested PCR and superior for detecting submicroscopic infections and mosquito samples with low parasite densities.

articles.headline: SPR biosensor with AI modeling enables sensitive malaria stage detection
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence enabled performance evaluation of an enhanced SPR biosensor for malaria diagnosis.
articles.authors: Utshob MAAI, Juwel MB, Ishtiak KM, Rahman A, Rahman MMA, Imam SA, Huq SMI, Khosru QDM
articles.details: A novel surface plasmon resonance (SPR) biosensor with N-FK51a+SiO2+Cu+HfO2+black phosphorus multilayer structure achieved maximum angular sensitivity of 541.42 deg/RIU, full-width half-maximum of 1.98 degrees, detection accuracy of 0.502 deg-1, and quality factor of 272.21 RIU-1. Sensor responses were modeled using artificial neural network (ANN), adaptive neuro-fuzzy inference system (ANFIS), and radial basis function neural network (RBFNN) for parasite stage prediction with enhanced reliability.

articles.headline: Microscopist competence certification supports malaria elimination in China
articles.title_original: Development of human resources in malaria microscopy during and after the malaria elimination phase in China.
articles.authors: Li M, Wang Y, Zhou R, Liu H, Xia Z, Yan G, Deng Y, Deng Y, Wang Z, Wang S, Dai Y, Zhou X, Li S
articles.details: Six rounds of WHO External Competence Assessment of Malaria Microscopists (WHO-ECAMM) from 2017-2024 certified 64 microscopists (average age 41.0 years; 29 at Level 1, 17 at Level 2 covering 29 of 31 provinces). Separately, 72 participants completed revised National Competency Assessment (NCAMM) courses, with 10 certified at Level A and 33 at Level B. Years of experience in malaria microscopy showed moderate positive correlation with competence (ρ=0.4, P<0.01), and species identification scores improved significantly (Z=-7.3, P<0.01).

articles.headline: Plasmodium bubalis Type I infection confirmed in Indian water buffaloes
articles.title_original: Clinical, hematobiochemical and phylogenetic studies of bubaline malaria in Indian water buffaloes (Bubalus bubalis).
articles.authors: Chavhan SG, Awandkar SP, Jadhav RK, Kulkarni MB, Kondre BM
articles.details: Molecular and clinical investigation of P. bubalis in 56 water buffalo samples identified 5 (8.93%) positive cases by PCR and 3 (5.36%) by blood smear examination. Infected buffaloes presented with fever, tachycardia, tachypnea, and icteric/pale conjunctival mucous membranes, with hematobiochemical changes including marked anemia, thrombocytopenia, hyperbilirubinemia, and hypoalbuminemia. Phylogenetic analysis revealed P. bubalis Type I as the circulating strain, representing the first documented report in domestic Indian water buffaloes.

articles.headline: Extracellular vesicles mediate heat-shock survival in Plasmodium falciparum
articles.title_original: Extracellular vesicles participate in proteostasis and heat shock adaptation in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Avalos-Padilla Y, Román-Álamo L, Bouzón-Arnáiz I, Arce EM, Muñoz-Torrero D, Fernàndez-Busquets X
articles.details: During heat shock (>40°C), P. falciparum releases extracellular vesicles (EVs) enriched in aggregation-prone proteins and molecular chaperones (PfHsp70-x, PfHsp110), facilitated by ESCRT protein PfVps60. In PfVps60 knockout parasites, impaired EV-mediated chaperone export led to protein aggregate accumulation, reduced induction of cytosolic PfHsp70-1, and early loss of parasite viability following thermal stress. Supplementation with EVs from heat-stressed wild-type parasites partially restored heat-shock tolerance in dose-dependent manner, implicating EV-mediated stress adaptation as a parasite vulnerability.

articles.headline: Spatial repellent intervention optimized for malaria control in Kenya
articles.title_original: Optimizing the impact of a novel spatial repellent on malaria incidence in Western Kenya.
articles.authors: Huwe T, Koepfli C, Ochomo EO, Gimnig JE, Achee NL, Grieco JP, Abong'o B, Moshi V, Perkins A, Moore SM
articles.details: Study in Western Kenya evaluating optimization strategies for a novel spatial repellent to reduce malaria incidence.

articles.headline: IgG response to PfHRP2 as biomarker for severe malaria
articles.title_original: Differential IgG responses to PfHRP2 across the malaria severity spectrum.
articles.authors: Bhandari S, Krishna S, Verma AK, Ranjha R, Gupta H, Bharti PK
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Study of 240 samples examined Pfhrp2 gene copy numbers and IgG immune responses across mild, severe, and cerebral malaria cases using qPCR and ELISA. IgG responses to PfHRP2 peptide were significantly higher in severe, cerebral, and non-cerebral severe malaria compared to mild malaria (P < 0.0001), with positive correlations to age and var P5 peptide (P < 0.01). Pfhrp2 copy numbers were similar across severity groups. Authors conclude that IgG response to PfHRP2, not gene copy variation, is critical for determining disease severity through host-pathogen interactions.

articles.headline: Wealth index, not net use, predicts malaria in Mozambique children
articles.title_original: Risk factors for malaria among children under five living in net-owning households in Mozambique from the 2022-3 Demographic and Health Survey.
articles.authors: Mooney HA, Muhammad MA, Owusu DN, Adediji PO, Cudjoe NI, Brooks B
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 1,924 households with children under 5 from 2022-3 Mozambique DHS data using multivariable logistic regression. Household net use had no significant association with child malaria positivity after controlling for wealth. Middle-wealth households were 43% less likely (aOR=0.57, 95% CI 0.38-0.84, P=0.0048) and highest-wealth households 84% less likely (aOR=0.16, 95% CI 0.09-0.27, P<0.0001) to have malaria-positive children versus lowest-wealth. Nampula and Cabo Delgado regions showed significantly higher odds (aOR=18.97 and 14.70 respectively, P<0.0005).

articles.headline: Light microscopy misses 24.7% of positive, 18.8% of negative malaria cases
articles.title_original: Diagnostic gaps in light microscopy for malaria case management and control in Dilla Zuria Health Facility, southern Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Bogale A, Tefera S, Mitiku A, Solomon Z, Misganaw T
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 474 cases at a health facility in southern Ethiopia comparing light microscopy to qPCR reference standard. Discordance was 24.7% (95/384) for positive cases and 18.8% (17/90) for negative cases. For P. falciparum specifically, 21% (48/228) discordance; P. vivax 28.3% (32/113); mixed infection 34.8% (15/43). Microscopy-negative but qPCR-positive results (18.8%) likely reflect submicroscopic infections or poor slide quality. Authors conclude that competency-based training and quality assurance are essential to improve diagnostic accuracy.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to transmission-blocking malaria vaccine study
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi" [Vaccine 84 (2026) 128683].
articles.authors: Pourhashem Z, Siasi S, Nourani L, Yousefi H, Sani JJ, Vand-Rajabpour H, Raz A, Pirahmadi S, Zargar M, Djadid ND, Zakeri S, Mehrizi AA
articles.details: This is a corrigendum (correction notice) to a prior publication on a multi-antigen transmission-blocking vaccine. No new data or findings are presented.

articles.headline: Peritrophins Per-44 and Per-48 essential for Plasmodium transmission in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Molecular characterization of midgut Peritrophin-44 and Peritrophin-48 and their role in Plasmodium development.
articles.authors: Dubey A, Chauhan VK, Chaitanya RK
articles.details: Molecular and functional study in Anopheles stephensi characterized two peritrophic matrix proteins (Per-44 and Per-48) as Class III chitin-binding proteins. Both genes are expressed throughout mosquito lifecycle with peak expression in fourth-instar larvae and adults. RNAi silencing impaired mosquito fitness and reduced fecundity. Per-48 knockdown caused most severe phenotype with significant reduction in oocyst burden. Authors conclude Per-44 and Per-48 are essential peritrophic matrix components that modulate midgut integrity and vector competence, and are viable targets for transmission-blocking strategies.

articles.headline: Delayed tafenoquine for P. vivax radical cure: insufficient evidence but promising
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Delayed Tafenoquine Administration for Radical Cure of Plasmodium vivax Malaria: A Systematic Review.
articles.authors: DeVita TN, Hwang J, Gutman JR, Ridpath AD, Williams S, Chang MA
articles.details: Systematic review of radical cure efficacy for P. vivax hypnozoite treatment searched five databases through January 2026 and identified two randomized open-label trials (conducted 1996-1999) meeting inclusion criteria. Both evaluated single-dose tafenoquine (500 or 600 mg) on day 4 or 6 after 3-day chloroquine course. Based on limited higher-dose trial data, authors conclude there is insufficient evidence to recommend for or against delayed tafenoquine, but data suggest it is likely better than chloroquine alone and preferable to 14-day primaquine for adherence concerns.

articles.headline: SPR biosensor simultaneously detects glucose and P. falciparum HRP-II
articles.title_original: Machine Learning Surface Plasmon Resonance Biosensor for Simultaneous Detection of Glucose and Plasmodium falciparum Biomarkers.
articles.authors: Wekalao J, Almawgani AHM, Alhawari ARH, Alammar MM, Abdullah M, Rajakannu A
articles.details: Theoretical design of multilayer surface plasmon resonance biosensor for label-free simultaneous quantification of glucose and P. falciparum HRP-II from single sample. Optimized multilayer stack (Ag/LiB3O5/BiB3O6/Cu/FASnI3/black phosphorus) achieved 1.82-fold sensitivity gain over reference single-layer silver SPR. Machine learning model trained on 18,000 simulated points reached R²=0.997 for glucose and R²=0.994 for HRP-II. Angular sensitivities 221.3 deg/RIU for glucose, 212.7 deg/RIU for HRP-II, with detection limits 0.15 mg/dL and 0.8 ng/mL respectively. Per-chip material cost estimated USD 1.50-4.00.

articles.headline: High-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg) reduces P. vivax recurrence vs low-dose in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Analysis of the effect of primaquine dose on efficacy, safety, and tolerability in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria in Ethiopia: a systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Degaga TS, Thriemer K, Rajasekhar M, Abreha T, Assefa A, Douglas NM, Gadisa E, Hailu A, Hwang J, Koh GCKW, Lacerda MVG, Llanos-Cuentas A, Mekonnen DA, Simpson JA, Tadesse FG, Taylor WRJ, von Seidlein L, Woyessa A, Yeshiwondim AK, Yilma D, Price RN, Bayissa GA, Commons RJ
articles.details: Systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis of 1,378 patients from 5 Ethiopian studies of P. vivax treatment with follow-up ≥28 days. Cumulative incidence of recurrence by day 180 was 61.0% (95% CI 55.3-66.8) without primaquine, 16.6% (12.0-22.7) with low-dose (3.5 mg/kg), and 8.9% (6.4-12.4) with high-dose (7 mg/kg). High-dose reduced recurrence versus low-dose (aHR 0.40, 95% CI 0.24-0.68, p=0.001). Gastrointestinal disturbance on days 5-7 in 16-24% of patients across dose groups. Clinically significant haemolysis was uncommon in patients with ≥30% G6PD activity.

articles.headline: Zoonotic malaria in SE Asia: vector complexity, landscape changes challenge control
articles.title_original: Zoonotic malaria in Southeast Asia's changing landscapes: vector complexity, challenges, and future strategies.
articles.authors: Jeyaprakasam NK, Zulhaimi AH, Lau KW, Pramasivan S, Phang WK, Ponlawat A, Samung Y, Zhong D, Idris ZM, Sattabongkot J
articles.details: Narrative review synthesizing knowledge on zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi and other zoonotic species in Southeast Asia. Notes emerging public health concern with increasing incidence in multiple countries. Key challenge is complexity of vector species assemblages with morphological similarity, making accurate identification difficult. Landscape changes from deforestation and agricultural expansion alter mosquito bionomics and may promote outdoor, forest-associated biting and shift transmission toward settlements. Authors emphasize need for integrated, context-specific, One Health-oriented strategies addressing surveillance and control gaps.

articles.headline: Serological surveillance identifies hidden malaria transmission in SE Asia elimination
articles.title_original: Serological surveillance for malaria elimination in Southeast Asia: a scoping review.
articles.authors: Sangaran K, Hassan NW, Goh SJ, Izwan AAMF, Idris ZM
articles.details: Scoping review (PRISMA-ScR) of 33 studies across seven Southeast Asian countries published 2000-May 2025, with 26 cross-sectional, 5 longitudinal designs. Research focus shifted from high-transmission to low-transmission and elimination contexts, increasingly using multiplex assays and protein microarrays. Studies incorporated P. falciparum, P. vivax, and increasingly P. knowlesi and vector exposure antigens. Seroconversion rate estimation and machine learning effectively identified historical transmission declines and residual hotspots missed by microscopy or molecular methods, enabling precise micro-stratification of transmission foci.

articles.headline: Interceptor G2 maintains efficacy against resistant mosquitoes; other nets decline
articles.title_original: Durability of the insecticidal activity of next-generation insecticide treated nets distributed for malaria control in Mozambique: Findings from the New Nets Project (2020-2022).
articles.authors: Fagbohoun J, Abílio AP, N'Dombidje B, Todjinou D, Baes M, Pigeon O, Padonou GG, Fornadel C, Candrinho B, Koenker H, Robertson M, Wagman J, Ngufor C
articles.details: Evaluation of next-generation ITN durability in Mozambique (2020-2022) assessed pyrethroid-only nets (MAGNet, DuraNet, Olyset Net), pyrethroid-PBO (Olyset Plus), pyrethroid-pyriproxyfen (Royal Guard), and pyrethroid-chlorfenapyr (Interceptor G2) at 6, 12, and 24 months. All retained 100% efficacy against susceptible mosquitoes. Against resistant mosquitoes: Olyset Plus declined from 73% to 40%; Royal Guard improved from 43% to 87%; Interceptor G2 maintained ≥92% across all timepoints. Experimental hut trials confirmed Interceptor G2 superiority with mortality declining from 55% to 39% by 24 months. Chemical analysis showed major losses of PBO (8% remaining) and chlorfenapyr (32% remaining) at 24 months.

articles.headline: Saquinavir competitively inhibits Plasmepsin V with IC50 0.35 μM
articles.title_original: Repurposing amide-based drugs unveils inhibition mechanisms of plasmodium falciparum Plasmepsin V enzymatic activity for antimalarial therapy.
articles.authors: Prajapati AK, Singh G, Lopus M, Choudhary S
articles.details: Study investigated three approved amide-based drugs (favipiravir, 5-fluorouracil, saquinavir) as inhibitors of P. falciparum Plasmepsin V (PfPlmV), an aspartic protease essential for parasite protein export. All three bound PfPlmV with affinities ~10⁴ M⁻¹. Saquinavir showed competitive inhibition (IC50=0.35±0.01 μM, Ki=0.14±0.02 μM) by engaging catalytic dyad residues Asp118 and Asp365. 5-FU showed mixed-type inhibition with higher IC50 and Ki values. Favipiravir bound peripherally without engaging catalytic dyad and showed weak, non-inhibitory binding. Authors conclude saquinavir interaction patterns may guide repurposing strategies for selective PfPlmV inhibitors.

articles.headline: Subnational malaria burden disparities among young children in Somalia
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal inequalities in malaria incidence and mortality among children aged 0-4 years in Somalia: a subnational analysis, 2010-2019.
articles.authors: Hassan YSA, Omar MA, Ali AS, Adebayo UO, Okesanya OJ, Sani J, Ahmed MM
articles.details: Retrospective subnational analysis using modeled annual estimates of P. falciparum and P. vivax incidence and mortality among children aged 0-4 years across Somalia's 18 regions (2010-2019). P. falciparum incidence peaked at 51.6% in Awdal (2015), remaining elevated at 32.0% in Bay and 31.1% in Juba Dhexe through 2019; P. vivax remained low. Despite narrowing incidence gaps, children in central and southern Somalia were over four times more likely to die than those in least affected regions (mortality ratio 4.24 in 2019), reflecting structural healthcare inequalities.

articles.headline: Prenatal and early childhood malaria impairs growth in Ghanaian infants
articles.title_original: Prenatal and child malaria and child growth: evidence from a Ghanaian pregnancy cohort.
articles.authors: Sowole O, Kaali S, Li M, Mujtaba M, Wylie BJ, Gyaase S, Chillrud S, Agyei O, Jack D, Lee A, Asante KP
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 1,144 children from Ghana Randomized Air Pollution and Health Study with prenatal malaria exposure (histopathological placental examination) and early child malaria exposure (active surveillance, RDT testing) measured at birth, 3, 6, 9, and 12 months. Prenatal malaria associated with poorer length growth (multivariable OR=1.46, 95% CI 1.05-2.02), poorer length-for-age z-scores (multivariable OR=1.53, 95% CI 1.14-2.05), and smaller head circumference (multivariable OR=1.43, 95% CI 1.04-1.98). Early child malaria associated with poorer length-for-age z-score (multivariable OR=1.12, 95% CI 1.01-1.24).

articles.headline: P. vivax dominates malaria epidemiology in Papua Indonesia with substandard treatment
articles.title_original: Epidemiological patterns and healthcare utilization among malaria patients in Sorong City, Papua, Indonesia: A cross-sectional analysis of national surveillance data.
articles.authors: Susilawati TN, Susanto H, Pham OKN, Kondororik F, Dewi VNL, Ahmar H, Putri NR, Lee R, Duong MC
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 3,953 laboratory-confirmed malaria cases in Sorong City in 2024 via Indonesia's national surveillance system. Most cases were ≥15 years (65.9%) and male (59.4%); P. vivax predominated (65.5%); nearly all uncomplicated (99.9%). Nearly 21% received non-standard antimalarial regimens. Predictors of hospital attendance included older age (aOR=1.02, 95% CI 1.00-1.02) and non-Papuan ethnicity (aOR=1.77, 95% CI 1.51-2.07); children <15 years more likely hospitalized (aOR=1.9, 95% CI 1.18-3.06).

articles.headline: P. vivax infects Duffy-negative Cameroonians with adaptive DBP variants
articles.title_original: Epidemiological insights and genetic diversity of the Duffy binding protein of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-negative Cameroonians.
articles.authors: Dieng CC, Ning RT, Kipayko C, Schroeder RE, Mdluli-Berndt N, Ayukenchengamba B, Nematchoua Weyou Z, Sona TD, Reward AE, Zhou G, Sumbele Ngole Ule I, Kimbi HK, Lo E
articles.details: Screening of 1,373 samples across three ecological zones in Cameroon via microscopy, RDT, and qPCR with Duffy genotyping; PvDBP1 region II sequenced from 75 isolates. P. vivax prevalence 10.8% in hospital patients (86/793) and 5.5% in community (32/580), all in Duffy-negative individuals. Eight nonsynonymous PvDBP1 mutations identified with I379L (74.1%) and E225K (61.3%) most common. PvLDH-based RDTs failed to detect >85% of qPCR-confirmed infections; phylogenetic analysis clustered Cameroonian isolates with Botswana strains, distinct from East African and Asian lineages.

articles.headline: Herbal remedies for malaria persist despite prescription availability in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Family traditions, accessibility and cost shape the continued reliance on Cajanus cajan and Azadirachta indica for malaria and typhoid despite prescription medicines in urban and rural Nsukka, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Osayi EE, Urama DC, Amujiri AN, Agyo RA, Afyare AAA, Andong FA
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 400 respondents (200 urban, 200 rural) in Nsukka examining indigenous use of Cajanus cajan (pigeon pea) and Azadirachta indica (neem) for malaria and typhoid. Rural residents relied more on herbal remedies due to accessibility and affordability; urban respondents, despite higher education, also used herbal extracts, often alternating with prescription drugs. Residence, occupation, and age significantly predicted knowledge and use (P<0.05); education was not. Perceived efficacy and high prescription costs sustained herbal use.

articles.headline: CNBP transcription factor controls IL-12/IFN-γ immunity to Plasmodium
articles.title_original: Cellular nucleic acid-binding protein (CNBP) dependent cytokine programming shapes host defense against Plasmodium infection.
articles.authors: Rashid R, de Souza Silva L, Banday S, Caffrey DR, Kurt-Jones EA, Fitzgerald KA, Golenbock DT
articles.details: Mechanistic investigation of cellular nucleic acid-binding protein (CNBP) in immune control of P. falciparum, P. chabaudi, and P. berghei ANKA infection. CNBP rapidly translocated to nucleus in dendritic cells upon exposure to infected erythrocytes and was required for optimal IL12β production. Mice with hematopoietic-specific CNBP deletion showed elevated peak parasitemia, impaired parasite clearance, and relapse after initial resolution. Spleens exhibited reduced inflammatory remodeling, altered T-cell composition, and selective downregulation of IL12β-IFN-γ transcripts, though paradoxically showed delayed mortality in lethal infection model.

articles.headline: Severe falciparum malaria presenting with gastrointestinal symptoms
articles.title_original: Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Presenting with Gastrointestinal Symptoms in South Sudan: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Tran AD, Nguyen HT, Pham HK
articles.details: Case report of 29-year-old man with severe P. falciparum malaria initially misdiagnosed as gastrointestinal infection after presenting with nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. RDT positive; microscopy revealed P. falciparum parasitemia (++). Laboratory findings included elevated bilirubin (39.6 µmol/L), creatinine (120 µmol/L), prolonged prothrombin time (22.4s), INR 1.84, and thrombocytopenia (50×10^9/L). Treated with IV artesunate; became afebrile and hemodynamically stable after 3 days.

articles.headline: Optimized non-human primate model for P. vivax blood-stage vaccine testing
articles.title_original: Development of novel Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model for the evaluation of Plasmodium vivax blood stage vaccines and immunoprophylactics.
