articles.authors: Ventocilla JA, Tapia LL, Villena FE, Cedamanos D, Buchta J, Pannebaker D, Valdivia HO, Redinger KR, Bosch J, Carias L, Londono-Renteria B, King C, Wilder BK
articles.details: Optimization study of Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model for P. vivax blood-stage infection using Vietnam-IV strain. Optimal infectious dose determined at 2.5 × 10^6 infected red blood cells with lowest coefficient variation (15-23%). Complete blood count during acute infection showed significant lymphocyte increase (5.0-5.3 to 7.0-8.5 × 10^9/L) and platelet decrease (413-458 to 23-68 × 10^9/L). Pilot testing of anti-DBPII monoclonal antibody 099100 showed 1 of 3 monkeys partially protected against P. vivax challenge.

articles.headline: P. malariae asymptomatic infections underdetected in sub-Saharan African migrants
articles.title_original: Ten years of Plasmodium malariae infections in an International Health Reference Center in Barcelona: temporal trends and insights.
articles.authors: Pérez-Ugarte A, Sulleiro E, Rovira-Plujà J, Bayram M, Zarzuela F, Maturana CR, Serre-Delcor N, Aznar ML, Pou D, Goterris L, Salvador F, Mediavilla A, Bosch P, Martinez-Campreciós J, Treviño B, Martínez-Vallejo P, Silgado A, Molina I, Oliveira-Souto I
articles.details: Retrospective observational study from June 2015-May 2025 at Barcelona international health reference center screening 6,293 individuals; 55 of 404 (13.6%) Plasmodium-positive patients had P. malariae infection. Most infections (52/55; 94.5%) detected in asymptomatic migrants from sub-Saharan Africa with low parasitemia (≤80 parasites/µL). Notably, 13/55 patients had lived in Spain >12 months before diagnosis, supporting long-term parasitemia persistence. One symptomatic traveler experienced clinical relapse after artemisinin-based combination therapy.

articles.headline: Intravenous artesunate for artemisinin-resistant severe malaria
articles.title_original: Intravenous Artesunate in Artemisinin-Resistant Severe Malaria in Uganda.
articles.authors: Maitland K, Okao M, Conrad MD, Etwop T, Akite M, Oguda E, Alaroker F, Aromut D, Muhindo R, Uyoga S, Olupot-Olupot P, Mogaka C, Connon R, Katairo T, Okitwi M, Briggs J, Gibb DM, Day NPJ, White NJ, Williams TN, Dondorp AM, Walker AS, George EC, Rosenthal PJ
articles.details: No abstract provided. Title indicates this is a report on intravenous artesunate treatment for artemisinin-resistant severe malaria, but specific study design, patient population, outcomes, and clinical results cannot be determined from title alone.

articles.headline: Germline-encoded anti-CSP antibodies achieve protection via distinct binding modes
articles.title_original: Structural basis for conserved and distinct antigen recognition by a lineage of malaria-protective antibodies.
articles.authors: Jain M, Cannac F, Agrawal S, Lee WH, Loeffler JR, Fernández-Quintero ML, González-Páez GE, Moskovitz R, Ward AB, Wilson IA
articles.details: Structural study determining X-ray and cryo-EM structures of monoclonal antibodies (Fabs 7160 and 7118) derived from VH3-49/VK2D-29 germline lineages targeting P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP). Both Fabs bound NANP6 repeats with high affinity (KD 6-10 nM). Fab 7160 formed germline-encoded inter-Fab homotypic interactions similar to Fab 399, while Fab 7118 adopts distinct binding mode precluding inter-Fab contacts, demonstrating structural versatility and how CDR loop variations modulate epitope engagement.

articles.headline: Essential mitochondrial ATAD3A complex as novel antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: ATAD3 megadalton complex in Plasmodium falciparum is essential for mitochondrial and cellular viability.
articles.authors: Okoye IC, Lamb IM, Cheung YW, Morrisey JM, Sharma M, Kumar R, Dass S, Shukla A, Rell RS, Mather MW, Llinás M, Chang YW, Vaidya AB
articles.details: Functional characterization study of PF3D7_0707400, an ATAD3A homolog essential to P. falciparum parasite survival and present in megadalton complex. The complex is critical for multiple mitochondrial processes: RNA stability, membrane potential, ultrastructure, and protein import. This is the first characterization of ATAD3A in unicellular organisms, expanding knowledge of apicomplexan mitochondrial biology and identifying novel antimalarial drug targets.

articles.headline: Malaria control program effectiveness in Burkina Faso 2020-2024
articles.title_original: Evolution of the Performance of the Malaria Control Program in Burkina Faso: Analysis from 2020 to 2024.
articles.authors: Ouedraogo WD, Kabore A, Kompaore S, Somda S, Sie A, Sawadogo H, Meda ZC, Drabo M, Meda N, Hien H
articles.details: Ecological analysis of national malaria control program data 2020-2024 using secondary time-series data and ETS forecasting. LLIN use 81.02% in pregnant women, 68.2% in children <5 years; SMC 97.8% coverage; RDT 98.5% adoption; ACT 97.96% adoption. Malaria incidence reduced from 537.5 to 446.4 per 1,000 people annually. Forecast predicts incidence rise to 473.06 per 1,000 by 2030 (95% CI 242.81-703.28) if supply chain and resource challenges persist.

articles.headline: Standardized malaria diagnostic recommendations for French laboratories
articles.title_original: [Malaria diagnosis: French recommendations ANOFEL/LABAC].
articles.authors: Touafek F, Berry A, Giannoli JM, Ait-Ammar N, Coron N, Haim-Boukobza S, Larréché S, Pernot-Marino E, Robert-Gangneux F, Roques C, Thellier M, Houzé S
articles.details: Clinical guidance on malaria diagnosis standardization in France per NF EN ISO 15189. Diagnosis requires detecting parasites or DNA via sensitive techniques: rapid molecular biology or thick smear. Positive results must be confirmed by thin blood smear for species identification and parasitemia estimation. RDTs detecting plasmodial proteins are supplementary tools only, not standalone diagnostics. Results must specify species and parasitemia levels; biologists communicate results promptly and collaborate with clinicians.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 polymorphisms affect primaquine but not tafenoquine for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Differential impact of Tafenoquine and Primaquine on Plasmodium vivax recurrence: the role of CYP2D6 variants.
articles.authors: Godinho VKC, Mwangi VI, Morais MC, Lopes APB, Alves JRdS, Brito MAM, Lacerda MVGd, Trindade Maranhão Costa F, Almeida ACGd, Melo GCd
articles.details: Nested case-control cohort study in Brazil of P. vivax patients treated with primaquine (PQ) or tafenoquine (TQ), stratified by recurrence within 180 days. TQ efficacy unaffected by CYP2D6 polymorphisms (P=0.172). PQ-treated patients had significantly higher recurrence risk within 6 months (P=0.016); intermediate/poor metabolizers (gIM+gPM) had higher recurrence than normal metabolizers (gNM, P=0.036). CYP2D6 variability influences treatment response to PQ but not TQ.

articles.headline: Submicroscopic malaria in pregnancy missed by routine diagnostics, Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic malaria infections and inadequate IPTp-SP coverage among pregnant women in rural Burkina Faso: An observational study.
articles.authors: Tibiri YNG, Soulama I, Zongo D, Ouedraogo O, Sombie S, Millogo ON, Platon L, Nikiema S, Diarra WFS, Yanogo JN, Zoure OADZ, Ily RP, Kabore FCA, Sawadogo S, Saliou YEKI, Wade A, Chala B, Thiebaut L, Caspar E, Strubel PÉ, Muller A, Piola P, Zongo C, Menard D
articles.details: Observational study (June 2023-June 2024) in Koubri, Burkina Faso of 515 pregnant women at first ANC visit. PCR detected infection in 30.1% (155/515) versus 8.2% by microscopy and 8.7% by RDT; approximately two-thirds of infections missed by routine methods. Primigravidae had twice the infection risk (aPR 2.14, 95% CI 1.21-3.79). Each additional IPTp-SP dose associated with lower prevalence (aPR 0.89, 95% CI 0.80-0.99). PCR-confirmed infection at first ANC increased low birthweight risk (aPR 1.52, 95% CI 1.01-2.28).

articles.headline: Historical development of cinchona bark for malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Francesco Torti (1658-1741) and his description on the therapeutic use of quinine: the first effective method of treating malaria based on an extract of the bark of the Peruvian cinchona tree.
articles.authors: Artico M, Costi R, Gazzaniga V, Iorio S, Pastore FS, Cofone L
articles.details: Historical review of Francesco Torti's work developing cinchona bark, source of quinine, for malaria treatment. Torti was noted for meticulous patient care and clinical observation during period of humoral medical theory. His 1712 book detailed intermittent fever forms and therapeutic responses to cinchona. Work contributed to cinchona adoption throughout Europe and reflected broader shift toward patient-centered, experience-based medical practice.

articles.headline: VEGF signaling alterations in placental malaria pathophysiology
articles.title_original: Placental malaria and VEGF modulation by P. falciparum: Bridging the immunopathological gap using evidence from human cerebral and severe, non-cerebral malaria studies.
articles.authors: Jain V, Shanmugam R, Singh PP, Singh VP
articles.details: Review of placental malaria caused by P. falciparum sequestration in intervillous space. P. falciparum late stages internalize host VEGF and express VEGF receptors (primarily activated VEGFR2) on parasitized red blood cells. Evidence derives from immunohistochemistry/immunocytochemistry studies of fatal cerebral and severe non-cerebral malaria and in vitro culture of 3D7 strain. Dysregulated VEGF, angiogenic factors, cytokines/chemokines, and oxidative stress may lead to placental insufficiency, fetal growth restriction, and preeclampsia.

articles.headline: Dynamic species distribution models predict malaria vector suitability seasonally
articles.title_original: Predicting short-life-cycle species suitability via dynamic species distribution models: Applications in dengue and malaria epidemiology in Brazil.
articles.authors: Oliveira D, da Silva FF, Teixeira IBDF, Trindade-Vilanova DM, Rodrigues MS, Moura TA, Martinez PA
articles.details: Dynamic Species Distribution Models developed for An. darlingi using MaxEnt algorithm with monthly occurrence records and climatic variables 2016-2022. Model performance: AUC=0.9, TSS=0.8. Vapor pressure identified as most influential climatic predictor. For malaria in Brazil: incidence concentrated in Northern region and Amazon Basin. Identified seasonal windows of increased transmission vulnerability (p<0.01 for associations between suitability and case counts).

articles.headline: Autopsy findings of cerebral malaria in adult with multiple myeloma
articles.title_original: Malignant malaria: autopsy findings and morphological features.
articles.authors: Colombo D, Del Nonno F, D'Abramo A, Corpolongo A, Ascoli Bartoli T, De Marco P, Garotto G, Bocci MG, Nicastri E
articles.details: Case report of 54-year-old woman with untreated multiple myeloma who traveled to Central Africa without antimalarial chemoprophylaxis. Presented with fever, headache, asthenia; progressed to multiorgan failure and death on day 4 of hospitalization. Autopsy confirmed severe malaria with cerebral involvement. Demonstrates P. falciparum sequestration in cerebral vessel endothelium causing microvascular obstruction, ischemia, and perivascular hemorrhages.

articles.headline: Low combined ITN and IPTp coverage among pregnant women in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Use of insecticide-treated nets (ITN) and intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) for malaria prevention and its associated factors among pregnant women in Sub-Sahara Africa.
articles.authors: Alemu EA, Tesfa MA, Nigatu SG, Birhan TY, Yitageasu G, Demoze L, Tadesse AA
articles.details: Pooled analysis of MIS/DHS data from 18 sub-Saharan African countries (2018-2024) of 63,561 pregnant women who delivered in preceding 3 years. Combined ITN and IPTp (≥3 doses) utilization: 28.9% (95% CI 28.4-29.3%); neither intervention: 17.6% (95% CI 17.2-18.0%). Multilevel multinomial regression identified age, educational status, household head sex, wealth index, mass media exposure, residence, and sub-region as significant predictors. Fewer than one-third of expectant mothers used combined prevention strategies.

articles.headline: 34% malaria prevalence with identified risk factors in Northwest Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria and associated factors among febrile patients visiting Gissa Health Center, Awi Zone, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Amare B, Hailegebriel T
articles.details: Cross-sectional study February-April 2024 of 424 febrile patients at health center in Awi Zone, Northwest Ethiopia; 411 participated (97% response rate). Overall malaria prevalence 34.1% (140/411): P. falciparum 20.9%, P. vivax 9.7%, mixed infections 2.4%. Independent risk factors: age <5 years (AOR 2.77, 95% CI 1.09-7.00), age 6-15 years (AOR 3.85, 95% CI 1.49-9.92), illiteracy (AOR 2.83, 95% CI 1.13-7.08), lack of ITN use (AOR 2.08, 95% CI 1.04-4.17), absence of IRS (AOR 1.54, 95% CI 1.29-3.24).

articles.headline: Malaria elimination progress, barriers, and strategies in Cambodia
articles.title_original: Advancing malaria elimination in Cambodia: a scoping review of progress, challenges, and strategic priorities.
articles.authors: Srun M, Langkulsen U
articles.details: Scoping review of PubMed, Scopus, Web of Sciences (2014-2024) analyzing 25 publications on malaria elimination in Cambodia. Found transmission reductions and decreased case fatalities through various programs. Barriers identified: service gaps, inadequate infrastructure, low intervention coverage, poor access to malaria care, individual-level factors especially in high-risk areas. Calls for public health strategies prioritizing improved surveillance, enhanced case management, and reduced inequities in access to malaria prevention and treatment programs.

articles.headline: PfHRP2 deletions rare in Kenya; RDT reliability maintained
articles.title_original: Absence of false negative RDT results due to pfhrp2 deletion among symptomatic malaria infections in Kenya: findings from the 2022 Kenya national longitudinal surveillance of pfhrp2/3 deletions.
articles.authors: Akala HM, Kandie R, Githuka GN, Kiplagat R, Ahmeddin O, Keitany K, Kiarie J, Wamari A, Machini B, Oyugi E, Mahugu S, Otieno JD, Egbo TE, Juma DW, Sang LP, Haynes RC, Osoti V, Vesely BA, Smith D, Travis J, Cunningham J, Cheng Q, Oyier I, Ogutu B
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 5,394 symptomatic malaria patients across 10 Kenyan counties using both PfHRP2-based and PfLDH-based RDTs. HRP2 and PfLDH positivity rates similar (44% vs 45%) with 1.26% discordance. None of discordant samples carried pfhrp2 deletions; 5.4% (3/56) had single pfhrp3 deletions. Among 598 sequenced samples: pfhrp2-only deletion prevalence 4.5%, pfhrp3-only 12.4%, no double-deletions detected. Pfk13 mutations 0.7%: A578S and A675V detected in multiple counties. Single deletions do not drive false-negative HRP2-based RDT results.

articles.headline: Pf41 antibodies reveal past malaria exposure in low-transmission setting
articles.title_original: Pf41 serological profiling reveals potential malaria exposure in low transmission setting in the Menoreh Hills, Central Java, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Ilyas M, Yuwanita MR, Natanegara S, Daud MM, Rahayu AA, Hitipeuw D, Wusono AD, Nuranindita R, Fitriana F, Syahada JH, Jeon BY, Jun H, Martini M, Nuridzin DZ, Lu F, Han JH, Muh F
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 120 individuals in endemic villages and 24 controls measured anti-Pf41 IgG antibodies using protein microarray. Overall seropositivity was 71% (85/120) with village-level heterogeneity ranging from 47–93%; mean antibody levels were significantly higher in exposed groups (p<0.001). Nighttime behavioral factors including livestock feeding and mosquito bites were associated with higher antibody responses. Pf41 serology could complement surveillance in low-transmission settings by identifying populations with serological evidence of prior exposure.

articles.headline: Mechanical ventilation and hypertonic saline ineffective for cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Adjunctive Mechanical Ventilation or Intravenous Hypertonic Saline in Malawian Children with Cerebral Malaria and Highly Increased Brain Volume: A Randomized, Phase 3 Clinical Trial.
articles.authors: O'Brien NF, Postels DG, Chimalizeni Y, Fonseca Y, Wooldridge G, Parker E, Galeano X, Urbina E, Lintner-Rivera M, Wynkoop HJ, Chetcuti K, Goyal MS, Biondo-Savin E, Juvvadi S, Nicholas J, Vidal L, Raees M, Doctor A, Remy KE, Bhutta A, Holloway A, Moxon CA, Seydel KB, Liomba AM, Chilingulo C, Ibrahim K, Gardiner J, Taylor TE
articles.details: Phase 3 RCT in 57 children with severe cerebral malaria and brain swelling (2018–2023) compared early mechanical ventilation, 3% hypertonic saline, or usual care. Neither intervention reduced 7-day mortality compared to usual care (p=0.59 and p=1.0 respectively). Hypertonic saline increased cerebral blood flow but caused harm in children with baseline cerebral hyperemia (RR 3.0, p=0.04). Non-phenotype-guided neuroprotective strategies are ineffective; endotype-informed approaches are needed.

articles.headline: Precipitation and illiteracy drive malaria incidence in Tete Province
articles.title_original: Climatic and social health determinants of malaria incidence in Tete, Mozambique: Bayesian spatio-temporal modeling of routine data (2016-2022).
articles.authors: Mussafo AJA, da Cruz RF, de Arruda Silveira LV, Govone JS
articles.details: Bayesian spatio-temporal analysis of malaria data from 2016–2022 identified precipitation and illiteracy rate as significant predictors, associated with 6.7% and 1.6% increases in malaria incidence respectively. Incidence rose shortly after rainy season onset and declined during winter. Spatial analysis identified higher-risk districts (Macanga, Chiuta, Moatize) and lower incidence in Tete City. Climatic and social factors play a decisive role in transmission dynamics.

articles.headline: Humidity shifts thermal performance in malaria vector populations
articles.title_original: Beyond Temperature: Relative Humidity Systematically Shifts Juvenile Thermal Performance and Projected Population Growth in a Malaria Vector.
articles.authors: Huxley PJ, Brown JJ, St Laurent B, Johnson B, Cheung OY, Asamoah A, Hollingsworth BD, Bump ER, Wimberly MC, Pascual M, Johnson LR, Murdock CC
articles.details: Laboratory experiments with Anopheles stephensi showed that relative humidity alters juvenile life history trait responses and shifts temperature dependence of projected population growth. Humidity-temperature interactions can change conclusions about environmental suitability compared to temperature-only models. Results highlight importance of incorporating humidity alongside temperature when assessing ectotherm responses to climatic variation.

articles.headline: Settlement expansion driven malaria mortality in 19th-century Ontario
articles.title_original: Ecology of Malaria Mortality: A Spatiotemporal Mapping Approach.
articles.authors: Cooke A, Brickley MB
articles.details: GIS analysis of 2,683 probable malaria deaths from Ontario records (1869–1900) revealed significant spatial clustering that became progressively less concentrated over time. Mortality locations shifted from wetlands as drainage intensified, and settlement proximity emerged as the strongest predictor of malaria mortality density. Results demonstrate how colonial landscape transformation embedded malaria transmission within settlement practices rather than static environmental features.

articles.headline: Photonic crystal biosensor for malaria detection theoretically designed
articles.title_original: Highly Sensitive 1D Photonic Crystal Biosensor for Early Malaria Detection via Refractive Index.
articles.authors: Kaspar K, Karuppiah K, Karuppiah P, Ramanujam NR
articles.details: Theoretical design of a 1D photonic crystal biosensor optimized to detect malaria infection stages via refractive index changes at 1550 nm wavelength. Maximum sensitivity achieved was 1163.22 nm/RIU with quality factor 82.57×10³ and detection limit ~10⁻⁶ RIU. Design demonstrates strong potential for rapid, accurate, cost-effective diagnosis through resonant-peak enhancement and wavelength shifts.

articles.headline: Sub-RDT P. vivax prevalent despite G6PD deficiency in Myanmar
articles.title_original: Sub-RDT Plasmodium vivax infections and G6PD deficiency in Kayin State, Myanmar.
articles.authors: Thu AM, Phyo AP, Pateekham C, Blu SH, Thwin MM, Kajeechiwa L, Watthanaworawit W, Okkararungrot Y, Bancone G, Gornsawun G, Moo PK, Klain K, Aung PL, Nguitragool W, Pan-Ngum W, Nosten F
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey in 23 villages of Kayin State (2020–2021) detected 14.1% sub-RDT P. vivax infections by rRT-PCR while mRDT detected only 0.5% of cases, with median village prevalence 15.2%. G6PD deficiency was present in 21.7% of males and 10.6% of females (p<0.001). Sub-RDT P. vivax prevalence was 6-fold higher in Hpapun township than Myawaddy. No association between G6PD status and sub-RDT infection; high-sensitivity diagnostics needed.

articles.headline: Poverty identified as dominant driver of paediatric malaria in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Geographic Patterns and Drivers of Paediatric Malaria in Nigeria: Walking in the shadow of Poverty.
articles.authors: Osayomi T, Adedun OA
articles.details: Spatial analysis using Global Moran's I and Getis-Ord Gi* identified significant spatial clustering of paediatric malaria across Nigeria with hotspots in various regions and coldspots in the southwest. Stepwise linear regression identified poverty as the dominant predictor (R²=0.435) of paediatric malaria. Study recommends free antimalarial treatment for children and poverty reduction to reduce malaria burden.

articles.headline: Machine learning models accurately predict malaria in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Application of machine learning models in predicting malaria prevalence in Nigeria: an analysis of the 2015-2020 demographic and health surveys.
articles.authors: Ayoka TO, Nnadi CO
articles.details: Machine learning models trained on Nigerian Demographic and Health Survey data achieved high accuracy in predicting malaria prevalence. Random forest regressor achieved R²=0.9937, multiple linear regression R²=0.9916, and ridge regression R²=0.9924 on the test set. Models use readily available clinical information and symptoms, enabling shift from reactive diagnostics to proactive risk prediction for improved resource allocation.

articles.headline: Imported malaria sustains transmission in elimination-phase Iran
articles.title_original: Malaria dynamics in Iran: insights from autochthonous and asymptomatic.
articles.authors: Sadeghi F, Nodez SMM, Khosravani M
articles.details: Analysis of 289 malaria cases from Hormozgan province (April 2018–May 2023) found P. vivax in 78.9%, with 56.7% imported cases from Afghanistan and 30.8% from Pakistan; autochthonous Iranian cases comprised 12.5%. Case numbers peaked in 2023, particularly in Jask County, and 45.25% were asymptomatic. Imported malaria significantly contributes to maintenance of transmission among native populations despite Iran's elimination phase status.

articles.headline: Pfs230 gene highly conserved across East African populations
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of the Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 gene in four East African countries supports its potential as a malaria transmission‑blocking vaccine candidate.
articles.authors: Kisambale AJ, Moshi R, Pereus D, Mbwambo RB, Mandai SS, Chacha GA, Lyimo B, Madebe RA, Budodo R, Bakari C, Semboja HJ, Petro DA, Challe DP, Aaron S, Mbwambo D, Lusasi A, Kajange S, Lazaro S, Mandara CI, Seth MD, Kulohoma BW, Juma G, Ishengoma DS
articles.details: Analysis of 1,312 P. falciparum Pfs230 sequences from Ethiopia, Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda revealed low nucleotide diversity (π=5.9×10⁻⁴) and high haplotype diversity (Hd=0.999 with 496 haplotypes among monoclonal sequences). Only 8.1% of haplotypes were shared between populations. Non-synonymous to synonymous substitution ratio was 0.43. Low genetic diversity and lack of population differentiation support Pfs230's potential as a transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Uganda HMIS managers competent but gaps in supervision remain
articles.title_original: Attitude and practices of district-level HMIS managers towards malaria routine reporting in Uganda: a 2024 cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Kigozi SP, Okiring J, Kigozi LS, Emuron P, Talimusa EA, Kigozi RN, Alegana V, Sartorius B, Giorgi E, Drakeley C, Yeka A
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 34 HMIS managers from 30 Uganda districts (January–June 2024) found 70.6% were biostatisticians and 23.5% HMIS focal persons. 81.6% of expected routine reports were received; 75.8% of managers documented data errors though only 31.6% could produce documentation. 93.9% of submissions received data-quality queries (mean 4.2 per facility). Key gaps included inconsistent support supervision and limited advanced analytical skills for data use.

articles.headline: P. falciparum genome variants linked to sickle hemoglobin in Ghana
articles.title_original: Associations between the Plasmodium falciparum genome and sickle haemoglobin identified in mild malaria cases from Ghana.
articles.authors: Hamilton WL, Forster AJ, Aniweh Y, Asoala V, Drury E, Rockett KA, Awandare GA, Kwiatkowski DP, Band G, Amenga-Etego LN
articles.details: Genome-wide association study of 1,368 mild malaria patients in northern Ghana identified three confirmed parasite loci (Pfsa1, Pfsa3, and Pfsa4 in FIKK4.2 gene) associated with sickle hemoglobin (HbS) heterozygosity, with Pfsa1-4 mutations varying widely in frequency across Africa. Pfsa2 was absent. No associations found with hemoglobin C. Findings suggest parasite genotype influences the protective effect of HbS against severe malaria.

articles.headline: Raman spectroscopy and machine learning for malaria diagnostic staging
articles.title_original: Population-Level Raman Biochemical Staging of Malaria in Human Red Blood Cells Using Interpretable Machine Learning.
articles.authors: Wu L, Gupta A, Tripathi AK, Barman I
articles.details: Framework using confocal and spatially offset Raman spectroscopy coupled with interpretable machine learning (SHAP analysis) identified biochemical signatures distinguishing P. falciparum infection stages in red blood cells. On synchronized cultures, hemoglobin-associated spectral features were discriminative. The approach detected infections beneath a skin-mimetic phantom, providing foundation for noninvasive stage-aware diagnostics capable of assessing transmission potential.

articles.headline: Malaria exposure linked to immature neutrophil expansion in Burkitt lymphoma
articles.title_original: The impact of malaria-induced neutrophil subset shift and a link to Burkitt lymphoma.
articles.authors: Okoth SA, Tonui RK, Maina TK, Agwati E, Oduor CI, Racenet Z, M'Bana V, Njuguna FM, Keitany KK, Chepsiror D, Ayieko C, Moormann AM, Kinyua AW, Forconi CS
articles.details: Prospective study using multiparameter flow cytometry profiled neutrophil subsets in malaria-exposed and low-exposed children and Burkitt lymphoma patients from Western Kenya. Malaria-exposed children exhibited increased aged neutrophil subset frequencies with reduced mature active subsets. Immature neutrophil frequencies showed strong positive correlation (rs=0.7, p<0.0001) between malaria-exposed healthy children and BL patients, suggesting malaria-associated neutrophil shift may create permissive environment for BL.

articles.headline: ICOS polymorphisms do not influence anti-MSP-1(19) antibody response in P. vivax
articles.title_original: No Evidence for Association Between ICOS (a T-Cell Co-Stimulatory Molecule) Gene Polymorphisms and Anti-MSP-1(19) IgG Response in Venezuelan Endemic Plasmodium vivax Malaria.
articles.authors: Sevilla RD, Regalado-Gutiérrez OA, Wide A, Puche-Quiñonez R, Patarroyo MA, Noya-González O, Fernández-Mestre M
articles.details: Study of 91 individuals with uncomplicated P. vivax infection in Venezuela measured IgG antibodies against merozoite surface protein PvMSP19; seroprevalence 70.3%. Cluster analysis distinguished low (RI=0.408), medium (RI=0.862), and high responders (RI=1.267; p<0.001). Genotyping of three ICOS polymorphisms (rs4404254, rs4675379, rs10183087) showed no significant associations across inheritance models. Antibody responses strongly correlated with prior malarial episodes (r=0.803, p<0.0001).

articles.headline: Anopheles darlingi genome reveals insecticide resistance and population structure
articles.title_original: Reassessing malaria-transmitting mosquito evolution with a neotropical lens.
articles.authors: Carter TE, Gunarathna I
articles.details: Commentary on whole-genome analysis of Anopheles darlingi, a major Neotropical malaria vector, revealing geographically structured populations with no evidence of sympatric species. Study identified widespread insecticide resistance signals through convergent evolution across populations, providing insights into evolutionary drivers of vector adaptation.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine efficacy and emerging lumefantrine resistance in Laos
articles.title_original: Artemether-lumefantrine for the treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Laos: a therapeutic efficacy study coupled with genomic and in vitro phenotypic analyses.
articles.authors: Feufack-Donfack LB, Khim N, Tat B, Banouvong V, Khamlome B, Chindavongsal K, Chanthavisouk C, Reyburn R, Shortus M, Kloeung N, Ke S, Khean C, Seng C, Kul C, Eam R, Orban A, Salvador J, Vezain M, Dorgebray PE, Coppée R, Ariey F, Witkowski B, Ringwald P, Popovici J
articles.details: Therapeutic efficacy study of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) in 195 P. falciparum patients (median age 16 years) in four southern Laos provinces from August 2019 to June 2023 reported PCR-adjusted adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) of 96% (95% CI 92-98) at day 28, with 2% treatment failure and 2% reinfection. One recrudescent isolate showed markedly reduced lumefantrine susceptibility (IC50 59.9 nM) and high artemisinin resistance (35.8% ring-stage survival) with pfkelch13 R539T; R539T (5%) and C580Y (23%) mutations associated with day-3 microscopy-positive parasitaemia (p=0.044).

articles.headline: P. vivax serological markers effective despite extensive antigen genetic diversity
articles.title_original: Assessing the effect of parasite antigen genetic diversity on performance of Plasmodium vivax serological exposure markers for malaria: a multicentre observational diagnostic accuracy study.
articles.authors: Bareng APN, Wu KW, Takashima E, Argyropoulos DC, Smith L, Naung MT, Mazhari R, Schoffer K, Kiernan-Walker N, Abraham A, Lamont M, Mehra S, Lim PS, Sattabongkot J, Monteiro W, Lacerda M, Healer J, Chitnis CE, Tham WH, Tsuboi T, Mueller I, Barry AE, Longley RJ
articles.details: Multicentre observational diagnostic accuracy study examined how P. vivax antigen genetic diversity affects serological marker performance using samples from Brazil (923) and Thailand (774) collected April 2013 to June 2014. Genetic analysis revealed extensive diversity in MSP5 (π=14.8 × 10-3) and DBPII (π=7.7 × 10-3), with Sal-1 reference identity 0.4% (MSP5) to 99% (S16). Most antigens (5 of 8) maintained accurate recent-exposure identification despite regional immunogenicity differences; affected antigens improved with multi-antigen models.

articles.headline: Imported pediatric malaria in US hospitals associated with delayed diagnosis
articles.title_original: Malaria in Children at 9 US Hospitals: 2016-2023.
articles.authors: Sundararaman SA, Roper B, Davis AKF, Hanze Villavicencio KL, Mayhew JA, Wang ML, Tang NL, Soma VL, Wang Z, Feeney ME, Trehan I, Weatherhead JE, John CC, Gerber JS, Odom John AR
articles.details: Retrospective study of 171 children treated for malaria at 9 US hospitals from 2016-2023; most had traveled to West Africa. Fever (90%) and abdominal symptoms (66%) were common; 32% diagnosed with severe malaria, no deaths. Delayed diagnosis occurred in 26% of cases at similar rates across hospitals and was significant risk factor for severe malaria, associated with longer hospitalizations and increased morbidity.

articles.headline: Nine imported malaria cases identified in Iranian elimination setting
articles.title_original: Challenges and strategies in malaria control: A case series from East Azerbaijan Province, 2023-2024.
articles.authors: Golshaniniya P, Porkar Khatibi M, Pourhassan A, Mirzaaghazadeh E, Babapour A, Rezaei H, Koosha A, Khayatzadeh S, Deihim M, Bagheri R, Abbasi M
articles.details: Retrospective case series of 9 malaria cases in malaria-free northwest Iran in 2023-2024, all with travel history to endemic areas in southern Iran, Pakistan, Kenya, and Sierra Leone. Cases included 3 P. falciparum and 6 P. vivax infections confirmed by rapid diagnostic tests and microscopy. Emphasizes risk of local transmission and need to strengthen healthcare worker training for rapid diagnosis and surveillance in elimination setting.

articles.headline: Health service utilization and LLIN gaps impede malaria control in India
articles.title_original: Comprehensive Analysis of Health Service Utilization for Febrile Illness and Malaria in Malaria-Endemic States in India: LLINs as a Pillar of Control Strategies.
articles.authors: Chalageri VH, Marinaik SB, Singhal R, Gupta SK, Nathan SN, Eapen A, Yadav CP, Singh PK, Tripathi PK, Malla WA, Pradhan SC, Sinha DP, Singh SP, Singh K, Bhuyan PJ, Rahi M, Jain T, Sharma R, Anvikar AR
articles.details: Cross-sectional study surveying 37,849 households covering 177,644 individuals in 10 malaria-endemic Indian states assessed healthcare utilization and LLIN use. Government facilities accessible within 2.5 km (IQR 1-5 km), but only 57.4% of febrile individuals sought care within 24-48 hours. Adequate prevention knowledge in 89.2% households; 73.6% reported regular LLIN use. Significant gaps existed between facility accessibility and utilization, and LLIN ownership and regular use. Lack of education and tribal status were risk factors.

articles.headline: Crystal structure of Plasmodium vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor
articles.title_original: Crystal structure of Plasmodium vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor.
articles.authors: Nair A, Lin M, Srivastava A, Liu L, Cooper A, Battaile K, Harmon E, Myler PJ, Staker BL, Lovell S, Chakafana G, Asojo OA
articles.journal: Acta Crystallogr F Struct Biol Commun
articles.details: 1.8 Å resolution crystal structure of P. vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor (PvMIF) determined (PDB 9b0m, space group P63), revealing prototypical MIF trimer. PvMIF regulates immune responses, correlates with P. vivax malaria severity, binds host CD74, and suppresses host MIF-CD74 signaling. Plasmodium MIFs recognized as vaccine candidates, making structural characterization relevant for vaccine development approaches.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance using polygenic scores improves insecticide resistance monitoring
articles.title_original: Polygenic scores for genomic surveillance of insecticide resistance in malaria control.
articles.authors: Donnelly MJ, Kamau A, McDermott D, Nagi SC, Summers S
articles.details: Opinion article discusses how genomic approaches using polygenic scores can accurately characterize insecticide resistance patterns in malaria vectors and estimate impacts on control tool efficacy. Approach addresses limitations of phenotypic methods in detecting resistance before intervention failure, enabling evidence-based vector control strategies and earlier intervention adjustments.

articles.headline: Stabilized P. falciparum proteins enable high-throughput drug discovery
articles.title_original: Stabilizing Plasmodium falciparum proteins for small molecule drug discovery.
articles.authors: Amann M, Sträßer T, Einsle O, Günther S
articles.details: Researchers used ProteinMPNN-guided computational design to create stabilized surrogate constructs of three P. falciparum drug targets (PfBDP1-BRD, PfBDP4-BRD, PfK13-KREP) while maintaining binding-site residues. Stabilized variants showed markedly increased thermal stability and retained wild-type-like binding thermodynamics. A PfBDP4-BRD surrogate enabled determination of a 1.25 Å co-crystal structure with a newly identified inhibitor. Computationally stabilized surrogates are practical tools for drug discovery against challenging malaria proteins.

articles.headline: P. vivax recurrent infections show high gametocyte infectivity to mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Parasite and gametocyte dynamics and infectivity to mosquitoes during recurrent Plasmodium vivax infections: A longitudinal study from Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Tadesse FG, Chali W, Ejigu LA, Abdo M, Kassa FA, Bulto MG, Bezabih MK, Alemayehu L, Hundera NL, Bayu SL, Nibret D, Ashine T, Assefa M, Solomon Z, Demisse M, Gashaw A, Shimelash A, Tebeje SK, Hailemeskel E, Gadisa E, Rosado J, Obadia T, Taylor AR, White M, Massebo F, Ramjith J, Drakeley C, Bousema T
articles.details: One-year longitudinal study of 176 P. vivax patients in Ethiopia receiving chloroquine plus 14-day primaquine found 54.6% (96/176) experienced recurrent parasitemia. Gametocyte density was similar between baseline and recurrent symptomatic episodes but lower during asymptomatic parasitemia (p<0.001). Mosquito infectivity was high: 70.2% (167/238) of feeding assays resulted in infection, similar between baseline (70.5%) and recurrent symptomatic (69.4%) episodes. Mosquito infection rates correlated with parasite density (ρ=0.29; p<0.0001) and gametocyte density (ρ=0.32; p<0.0001). Relapsing infections showed lower mosquito infectivity when adjusting for gametocyte density (OR 0.48, 95% CI 0.26-0.87, p=0.016); 45.7% (42/92) of recurrences were genotyped as relapses.

articles.headline: pvmdr1 polymorphisms detected in North Indian P. vivax isolates
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of chloroquine resistance in Plasmodium vivax isolates from North India: Analysis of single nucleotide polymorphisms in pvmdr1 gene.
articles.authors: Nityadarshini N, Bir R, Biswas J, Jadon RS, Mirdha BR, Verma N
articles.details: Among 7530 screened patients, 75 P. vivax mono-infections were confirmed (mean age 29.2 years, male:female 2:1); 36 isolates were successfully sequenced. M908L and F1076L mutations were present in 100% of isolates, while T898A was detected in 5.6%. Phylogenetic analysis showed Indian isolates clustered with Madagascar and Afghanistan haplotypes, distinct from Brazilian haplotypes. Clinical symptoms included fever with chills in all cases, neurological symptoms in 6.6%, jaundice in 6.6%, and haematological abnormalities in 10.6%. Role of these mutations in clinical chloroquine resistance remains uncertain, particularly without Y976F.

articles.headline: BISSAU molecular form: genetically distinct Anopheles lineage in Gambia
articles.title_original: Genomic insights into the population structure of malaria vectors of the Anopheles gambiae complex in The Gambia reveals distinctiveness and spatio-temporal stability of BISSAU molecular form.
articles.authors: De Marco CM, Caputo B, Pichler V, Hamid-Adiamoh M, Gibba B, Clarkson C, Assogba BS, D'Alessandro U, Amambua-Ngwa A, Della Torre A
articles.details: Population genomic analysis of >200,000 SNPs on chromosome-3 from 2283 Anopheles specimens (An. coluzzii, An. gambiae, An. arabiensis, An. melas, and BISSAU molecular form) collected across 35 locations in The Gambia from 2005-2021. BISSAU molecular form was consistently identified as a genetically discrete lineage showing no admixture with sympatric members of the An. gambiae complex, supporting its status as an independently evolved, reproductively isolated unit. The north bank of the Gambia river (10-120 km from seacoast) showed the highest probability of BISSAU occurrence, with breeding sites characterized by brackish and fresh water, flooded swamps, and alluvial deposits near the river.

articles.headline: Microsporidia MB affects cuticular hydrocarbons but not human host-seeking
articles.title_original: Behaviour of the malaria vector Anopheles arabiensis infected with the endosymbiont Microsporidia MB: host seeking and cuticular hydrocarbons associated with mating.
articles.authors: Chepkemoi L, Cheseto X, Nyanjom SG, Herren JK, Tchouassi DP
articles.details: An. arabiensis infected with Microsporidia MB transmission-blocking symbiont were tested for host attraction, blood-meal sources, and cuticular hydrocarbon (CHC) profiles. In olfactometer assays, Microsporidia MB-infected mosquitoes showed no preferential attraction to human versus cattle odours. Logistic regression found no association between endosymbiont presence and selective feeding on humans over livestock, birds, or monkeys. Chemical analysis revealed differences in CHC profiles between infected and non-infected males, but random forest modeling indicated infection status was not strongly driven by a single hydrocarbon. The symbiont imposed no cost on host-seeking behavior but may affect mating behavior via changes in cuticular hydrocarbons.

articles.headline: RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M vaccine efficacy compared in meta-analysis
articles.title_original: Efficacy and immunogenicity of RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccines: - Systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Asjad H, Sadiq MW, Siddiqui S, Sherali S, Yousafzai MT, Taseen S, Qamar FN
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 8 studies published 2000-2024 comparing RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M vaccines. RTS,S/AS01 demonstrated 23.0% malaria risk reduction (RR 0.77) in 6-12-week-old infants and RR 0.61 in 5-17-month-old children, with efficacy declining over time requiring boosters. R21 displayed high efficacy with low event rate of 0.22 and strong antibody response but more frequent adverse events. RTS,S/AS01 was safe and generated strong antibody responses across age groups. Both vaccines demonstrate potential for total malaria elimination and reduction in morbidity and mortality.

articles.headline: Innate immune memory may explain Plasmodium recurrent infection dynamics
articles.title_original: The case for exploring innate memory in Plasmodium infection.
articles.authors: Ho NN, Tongogara T, Tran TM, Kaushansky A, Glennon EK
articles.details: Perspective article proposing that innate immune memory—epigenetic reprogramming of immune responses to sequential infections independent of adaptive immunity—may explain dynamics of recurring Plasmodium infections in endemic areas where individuals receive multiple infected mosquito bites daily. Current laboratory studies typically assess single infections and may not fully explain how recurrent infections in the field impact pathogenesis and immunity upon re-infection. The article summarizes concepts of innate immune memory and supporting evidence in Plasmodium infection control, and postulates that innate memory in non-immune cells may have significant consequences in the complex Plasmodium lifecycle.

articles.headline: DNA markers for metabolic insecticide resistance in malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Metabolic insecticide resistance DNA markers in malaria vectors.
articles.authors: Tchouakui M, Djoko Tagne CS, Tekoh TA, Tatchou-Nebangwa NMT, Kouamo MM, Wondji CS
articles.details: Review of DNA markers in detoxification genes (cytochrome P450s, glutathione S-transferases) indicating metabolic insecticide resistance in malaria vectors. Identified markers include CYP6P4, CYP6P3, and GSTe2 in An. gambiae; and CYP6P9a/b, CYP6P4a/b, CYP9K1, GSTe2, and ASL in An. funestus. These markers support resistance management by assessing spatiotemporal resistance evolution, impact on control tools, cross-resistance patterns, and fitness costs. Review synthesizes marker discovery knowledge, highlights available DNA-based diagnostic methods for operational genomic surveillance, and outlines priorities for extending this framework to new insecticides and other vector species.

articles.headline: Technological advances in studying Plasmodium sporogony in Anopheles
articles.title_original: Demystifying Plasmodium development within the Anopheles mosquito.
articles.authors: Shaw WR, Catteruccia F
articles.details: Review summarizing how recent technological developments—microscopy advances, single-cell RNA sequencing, and genetic screening—have revolutionized understanding of Plasmodium sporogony (parasite development within Anopheles mosquitoes). These essential parasite stages are challenging to study in vivo due to substantial bottlenecks in parasite numbers and increases in parasite ploidy that can rescue mutant phenotypes. Better understanding of mosquito-stage development could potentially inform disruption by novel malaria transmission control tools.

articles.headline: Concerns raised about spatial emanators malaria control study
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control.
articles.authors: Bradley J, Hayes R, Smith PG, Kleinschmidt I
articles.details: Letter raising methodological concerns about a study on spatial emanators for malaria control. [No abstract provided; detailed results unavailable]

articles.headline: Authors respond to spatial emanators study methodology concerns
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control - Authors' reply.
articles.authors: Allan RJ, Cross CL, Messenger LA
articles.details: Authors' reply addressing methodological concerns raised about spatial emanators malaria control study. [No abstract provided; detailed results unavailable]

articles.headline: Pan-Plasmodium RT-qPCR assay for blood donor malaria screening
articles.title_original: Development and validation of a pan-Plasmodium molecular assay as a confirmatory method for blood donor screening.
articles.authors: Milani P, Wen L, Montalvo L, Coco SB, Di Germanio C, Bres V, Yadav M, Livezey K, Levi JE, Linnen J, Busch MP
articles.details: Development and validation of reverse transcription quantitative PCR assay targeting conserved 18S rRNA region to detect all five human-infecting Plasmodium species in blood donors. Assay demonstrated high amplification efficiency (97.2%), linearity (R² = 0.99), and 95% limit of detection of 5.3 infected RBCs/mL (95% CI: 3.2-8.6). Clinical sensitivity was 100% across all five Plasmodium species and clinical specificity was 100% (95% CI: 99-100%), with no cross-reactivity against Babesia microti. Amplicon sequencing enabled accurate species-level identification. This sensitive confirmatory tool supports nucleic acid testing-based blood screening for transfusion-transmitted malaria with species identification capability.

articles.headline: Metabolic shift in monocytes during P. vivax infection
articles.title_original: The mitochondrial metabolic shift in inflammatory monocytes during Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.authors: Antonelli LR, Gazzinelli RT
articles.details: Review examining immunometabolic responses in P. vivax malaria, focusing on metabolic-mitochondrial shift in activated monocytes that demonstrate enhanced phagocytic and microbicidal activity. These monocytes produce high proinflammatory cytokines and reactive oxygen species that may contribute to acute malaria disease signs. Includes experimental malaria models to elucidate mechanistic immunometabolic pathways underlying P. vivax pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic Brazil: surveillance trends
articles.title_original: Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024).
articles.authors: Tudella GCN, Sasazaki FY, Kelnhar I, de Conto T, de Oliveira JVB, Franco FW, Oliveira DH, Cocco OD, Boufleuer IS, Martins FC, Soares TP, da Silva ACM, Wendt JDR
articles.details: Ecological study of 10,146 confirmed cases (9,755 malaria, 391 ACD) from 2007-2024 in non-endemic Brazil. Malaria showed decreasing temporal trend (β = -0.0174; p = 0.0397) with 89.7% non-autochthonous cases predominantly in males (78%) aged 20-39 years (47.8%). Spatial analysis identified five malaria hotspots in southeastern and southern states. ARIMA forecasts indicated stable incidence through 2030.

articles.headline: Micro-level malaria hotspot mapping in India toward elimination
articles.title_original: Identification of micro-level malaria hotspots and its implications for achieving malaria elimination target in a remote district of India.
articles.authors: Misra BR, Mishra A, Tiwari SK, Srivastava DN, Joshi HS, Mishra N, Dwivedi GR
articles.details: Analysis of malaria surveillance data from Sonbhadra district, India (2017-2024) tracking 18,217 cases. Annual parasite incidence (API) decreased from 2.7 (2017) to 0.1 (2024); annual falciparum incidence (AFI) fluctuated late-period to 0.09 (2024). Kulldorf scanning identified significant hotspot at Chopan block with high-risk clusters of both P. falciparum and P. vivax at sub-district level. Mean Annual Rate of Change projections guide elimination targets.

articles.headline: Dengue and malaria coinfection prevalence in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Molecular detection and epidemiological characterization of dengue and malaria coinfection in the Guiriko region, Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Belem LRW, Yao RK, Amara MF, Taita AVW, Kaboré BP, Kouadio KWU, Ibemgbo SA, Sangaré I, Gomgnimbou MK
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 408 patient samples (June-Sept 2023) in Guiriko region, Burkina Faso. Coinfection prevalence between recent dengue (NS1/IgM positive) and malaria (microscopy/PCR positive) was 17.64% (72/408); acute DENV and malaria coinfections were 7.10% (29/408). Among RT-PCR positive dengue cases, 100% showed malaria coinfection by PCR and 68.96% by microscopy. Elevated axillary temperature and high CRP (mean 83.79 mg/l) associated with acute coinfection.

articles.headline: Avian hemoparasites in tropical dry forest birds, Colombia
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of avian hemoparasites (Plasmodium and Haemoproteus) in resident and migratory birds from an urban fragment of tropical dry forest in northern Colombia.
articles.authors: Rodríguez MM, Flores FS, Castro LR
articles.details: Molecular survey of 217 bird individuals (27 resident, 7 migratory species) in urban tropical dry forest fragment in northern Colombia. Haemoproteus or Plasmodium infections detected in 11 individuals representing six species, yielding 5.1% overall prevalence. Infections identified in both resident and migratory birds, with several host-parasite associations representing new records for Colombia. Study demonstrates utility of multiple molecular markers for accurate avian hemoparasite identification.

articles.headline: Peripheral blood erythrophagocytosis in P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Peripheral Blood Erythrophagocytosis in Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Almeida T, Ferreira CA
articles.details: Article reports on peripheral blood erythrophagocytosis as a cellular finding in P. falciparum malaria. Erythrophagocytosis indicates red blood cell consumption through phagocytosis.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccine development and infant immune responses
articles.title_original: Malaria immunity in the infant: progress and pitfalls.
articles.authors: Feeney ME
articles.details: Review of infant malaria immunity and vaccination challenges. Discusses P. falciparum infection in fetus and infants, with focus on RTS,S/AS01E and R21/Matrix-M vaccines and human monoclonal antibodies to circumsporozoite protein (CSP). Notes that current vaccines elicit suboptimal immunity in young infants and are recommended only for children five months or older. Examines age-related immune response differences and emerging approaches to improve infant protection.

articles.headline: Novel approaches to radical cure for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Reimagining radical cure for Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Aung PL, Cui L
articles.details: Article discusses new strategies for achieving radical cure of Plasmodium vivax infections. Radical cure approaches target hypnozoite elimination to prevent relapse and recurrent infections.

articles.headline: EFFORT trial: primaquine and tafenoquine for P. vivax radical cure
articles.title_original: Effectiveness and safety of 7-day high-dose primaquine and single-dose tafenoquine versus 14-day low-dose primaquine in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria (EFFORT): a multicentre, open-label, randomised, controlled, superiority trial.
articles.authors: Degaga TS, Pasaribu AP, Tripura R, Ghanchi N, Rajasekhar M, Adhikari B, Ley B, Bamboro SA, Jabbar FA, Hasibuan N, Tego TT, Zehra S, Qurashi B, Mnjala H, Lee G, Li P, Kazi AM, Safitri W, Yulita Y, Siagian DSP, Syahputra D, Hadi H, Muhammad T, Ibrahim A, Syed N, Dost K, Puspitrasari AM, Kariodimedjo PP, Rai A, Rumaseb A, Sutanto E, Ean M, Khan A, Sokha M, Commons RJ, Weston S, Noviyanti R, Peto T, Callery JJ, Ali U, Mehmood T, Dondorp A, Devine A, Setiawan E, Mwaura M, Cassidy-Seyoum S, Temesgen R, Abate DT, Erjabo EB, Gessa GG, Kiros FG, Usmani MI, Raza A, Woyessa A, Hailu A, Simpson JA, Karahalios A, Beg MA, von Seidlein L, Dysoley L, Auburn S, Price RN, Thriemer K
articles.details: Multicentre RCT in Ethiopia, Pakistan, Indonesia, and Cambodia with 960 adults (G6PD ≥70%) comparing 7-day high-dose primaquine, single-dose tafenoquine, and 14-day low-dose primaquine for P. vivax radical cure. Six-month recurrence: 7-day regimen 13.0% (CI 9.0-18.5), tafenoquine 12.6% (CI 8.8-18.0) with HR 0.64 (p=0.041) vs 14-day regimen 18.5%. Tafenoquine had lower drug-related adverse events (22.2%) versus 7-day regimen (42.9%); four serious adverse events occurred, two probably drug-related.

articles.headline: WBC count discrepancy as diagnostic clue for malaria
articles.title_original: Discrepancy Between White Blood Cell Count Channels as a Clue to Malaria Infection.
articles.authors: Li C, Wang YH, Meng SI, Chen SC, Lee JA
articles.details: Article describes discrepancies between white blood cell count channels detected on automated hematology analyzers as a diagnostic clue for malaria infection. The finding suggests potential utility of hematology analyzer output for malaria detection.

articles.headline: Environmental variability in genetically modified mosquito development
articles.title_original: Beyond the static lab: environmental variability in genetically modified mosquito target gene identification for malaria control.
articles.authors: Dael L, Simões ML
articles.details: Perspective article addressing the 'lab-to-field' gap in genetically modified mosquito (GMM) development for malaria control. Argues that standard insectary conditions (constant temperature/humidity) do not reflect natural environmental variability that affects Anopheles and Plasmodium biology, mosquito immunity, and vector competence. Emphasizes the need to incorporate realistic environmental variability into upstream GMM gene-discovery to ensure modified mosquitoes remain effective despite environmental stress from climate change.

articles.headline: Malaria therapy for neurosyphilis: historical evaluation of efficacy
articles.title_original: Malaria therapy for neurosyphilis at Mont Park Hospital for the insane in Australia, 1927-1928.
articles.authors: Clayton A
articles.details: Historical analysis of malaria therapy for general paralysis of the insane at Mont Park Hospital, Australia (1927-1928). Evaluates treatment effectiveness claims through medical records and published accounts, finding patient outcomes notably less positive than reported. Identifies contributing factors including diagnostic drift, adjunct interventions, and healthy-patient selection bias that likely inflated efficacy estimates.

articles.headline: P. vivax CSP haplotypes show vector competence variation in Thailand
articles.title_original: Genetic signatures of Plasmodium vivax Circumsporozoite Surface Protein during malaria resurgence in Thailand.
articles.authors: Tapaopong P, Holzschuh A, da Silva G, Chintanawiwat P, Bantuchai S, Rungsarityotin W, Cui L, Koepfli C, Sattabongkot J, Nguitragool W
articles.details: Amplicon sequencing of 69 P. vivax isolates from Thailand's 2022-2023 malaria resurgence identified two CSP variants: VK210 (n=66) and VK247 (n=3). VK210 exhibited 21 haplotypes with 13-20 repeat motifs, with haplotype H2 being most prevalent (50% of VK210 sequences). Membrane feeding assays showed H2 produced more salivary-gland sporozoites per oocyst than other haplotypes in Anopheles dirus, suggesting repeat-region polymorphisms modulate vector competence.

articles.headline: RBC polymorphisms associated with malaria infection in Thailand
articles.title_original: Red blood cell membrane protein polymorphisms related to risk of malaria infection in the population living in malaria-endemic areas of Thailand: An analytic cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Muhamad P, Panrit L, Srikhamdokkhae O, Laohapensaeng K, Tarasuk M, Sa-I S, Na-Bangchang K
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 616 individuals (270 with malaria, 346 uninfected) from Thai-Myanmar and Thai-Malaysian borders found distinct RBC genetic associations. NN antigen showed higher frequency in malaria cases (22.2% vs 11.1%, OR 3.7, p<0.0001), as did FYA/FYA or FYA/FYB Duffy wild-type genotypes (76.1% vs 46.9%, OR 4.7, p<0.0001). Geographic analysis revealed distinct genetic distributions by region, suggesting targeted control strategies based on genetic risk profiles.

articles.headline: Natural compound CA0001 emerges as PfDHFR-TS inhibitor from computational screen
articles.title_original: Discovery of novel Plasmodium falciparum PfDHFR-TS inhibitors from ConMedNP natural compounds: a multi-computational approach.
articles.authors: Haiwang Djefoulna VH, Atiya Atiya M, Fifen JJ, Conradie J
articles.details: Molecular docking of 2754 compounds from Central/West African medicinal plants identified novel PfDHFR-TS inhibitors with mean binding affinity -8.8 kcal/mol (median -8.9), with 75% surpassing artemether's -8.0 kcal/mol. Random Forest QSAR model achieved test R²=0.8321 (cross-validation R²=0.8461). Top candidate CA0001 showed binding affinity -13.0 kcal/mol with favorable ADMET profile (non-hepatotoxic, low environmental impact) and confirmed structural stability via MD simulations.

articles.headline: LNS with IPTp shows mixed effects on malaria and birth outcomes
articles.title_original: Effects of Combined Lipid-Based Nutrient Supplements and Malaria Preventive Treatment during Pregnancy on Malaria, Maternal Nutrition, and Birth Outcomes: A Scoping Review.
articles.authors: Parikh P, Zeilani M, Pieret A, Abbeddou S
articles.details: Scoping review of 17 studies (5 main trials) examining lipid-based nutrient supplements combined with intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy. LNS supplementation did not significantly affect malaria incidence, anemia, or maternal nutritional status; however, some studies reported improvements in birth outcomes, particularly among subgroups with baseline undernutrition. Evidence remains inconclusive, requiring further research in vulnerable populations in malaria-endemic regions.

articles.headline: Malaria Scoring System validates well for non-endemic settings
articles.title_original: External validation of the Malaria Scoring System in a non-endemic emergency department.
articles.authors: Paglietta G, Giamello JD, D'Agnano S, Melchio R, Carralero GL, Gianelli A, Giubbini G, Manassero S, Artana N, Barutta L, Basile E, Fenoglio L, Lupia E, Lauria G
articles.details: External validation study of 365 emergency patients (114 with malaria, 31.2%) found the Malaria Scoring System demonstrated excellent discriminatory ability (AUC 0.902, 95% CI 0.869-0.935) and good calibration (Hosmer-Lemeshow p=0.195). Malaria cases showed significantly higher frequencies of fever (96.5%), thrombocytopenia (78.1%), lymphopenia (71.9%), and anemia (53.5%) compared to non-malaria cases, with MSS median score 4 vs 1 (p<0.001).

articles.headline: Ferroptosis in macrophages exacerbates P. yoelii malaria progression
articles.title_original: HIF-1α/HMOX1-Mediated Ferroptosis in Macrophages Exacerbates Malaria Progression.
articles.authors: Liang G, Li J, Xie X, Yuan H, Xiao W, Chen M, Lu K, Xu L, Pan L, Wei H, Li L, Zhao S, Xie H, Pan X, Huang J
articles.details: Study in P. yoelii-infected mice identified ferroptosis in splenic macrophages as mechanism exacerbating malaria progression, triggered by HIF-1α downregulation and HMOX1 upregulation leading to Fe²⁺ overload. Fer-1 inhibitor reversed ferroptosis biomarkers in infected mice; GPX4 conditional knockout mice showed worse parasitemia than controls. Ferroptosis signature confirmed in monocytes from malaria patient PBMCs, suggesting HIF-1α/HMOX1 axis as therapeutic target.

articles.headline: MSP8 deletion protects mice via enhanced CCL19-CCR7 signaling
articles.title_original: Deletion of Plasmodium yoelii Merozoite Surface Protein 8 Protects Mice From Lethal Blood-Stage Malaria and Is Associated With Enhanced Splenic CCL19-CCR7 Signaling.
articles.authors: Sun Y, Fan Z, Ge J, Cui W, Zuo S, Tan L, Han S, Yan Y, Wang B, Cao J, Lv Z, Zhu G, Cheng Y
articles.details: In lethal P. yoelii 17XL malaria, MSP8 deletion (ΔMSP8 knockout) protected mice with reduced peak parasitemia, attenuated splenomegaly, and self-resolving infection. Transcriptomic analysis revealed MSP8 deficiency upregulated splenic CCL19 and preserved CCR7 with increased IFN-γ levels. Functional knockdown of CCR7 via AAV-shRNA partially reversed survival advantage, establishing MSP8 as vaccine target and host-directed therapy candidate.

articles.headline: COVID-19 disrupted malaria surveillance in Cameroon's children
articles.title_original: Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on reported malaria incidence in children under five years of age in Cameroon: an interrupted time series analysis with regional heterogeneity (2017-2022).
articles.authors: Mekontchou LM, Nguefack-Tsague G, Sonegueng AVK, Deffo PY, Sepde E, Samadoulougou S
articles.details: Interrupted time series analysis of DHIS2 data (2017-2022) found COVID-19 caused immediate 36.9% decline in reported malaria incidence in children under 5 (March 2020: IRR=0.631, 95% CI 0.539-0.738, p<0.001), followed by 3.1% monthly recovery (IRR=1.031, 95% CI 1.017-1.045, p<0.001). Counterfactual analysis estimated 69,708 missed notifications in 2020-2021 and 74,831 excess in 2022, with marked regional heterogeneity.

articles.headline: Tropical oceans drive malaria variability through soil moisture in Malawi
articles.title_original: Tropical oceans drive variability in soil moisture and malaria in Malawi.
articles.authors: Elling MT, Karnauskas KB, Kowalcyk M, Mategula D, Chirombo J, Livneh B, McCann RS, Buchwald AG
articles.details: Analysis of climate and malaria incidence identified two distinct ocean-based patterns driving interannual malaria variability in Malawi: warm tropical Atlantic leads to wet conditions and increased cases; warm Indian Ocean drives hot, dry conditions reducing cases. Soil moisture emerged as the mechanistic link between remote climate drivers and disease dynamics. Future climate change projected to reduce soil moisture by 2100, potentially reshaping transmission.

articles.headline: Two hypnozoite proteins suppress liver stage replication in P. vivax
articles.title_original: Two Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage replication.
articles.authors: Vo KC, van Biljon R, Zanghi G, Zavala B, Betz W, Jennison C, Vaughan AM, Painter HJ, Kappe SHI
articles.details: Differential transcriptome analysis of P. vivax hypnozoites identified two RNA-binding proteins (IESI-1 and IESI-2) that suppress liver stage schizogony. When expressed individually in P. yoelii liver stages (which lack native hypnozoites), both proteins blocked schizogony, maintaining parasites in small, uninucleate state. RNA binding assays revealed each protein recognizes distinct, highly specific motifs, providing functional evidence for molecular mechanism maintaining hypnozoite quiescence.

articles.headline: Spatial proteomics maps 3000 P. falciparum schizont proteins across 24 compartments
articles.title_original: The spatial proteome of the Plasmodium falciparum schizont illuminates the composition and evolutionary trajectories of its organelles.
articles.authors: Chisholm SA, Flores V, Kemp A, Breckels LM, Koreny L, Dos Santos Pacheco N, Barylyuk K, Kuroshchenkova A, Lilley KS, Rayner JC, Waller RF
articles.details: HyperLOPIT spatial proteomics analysis of P. falciparum late schizont stages mapped 3000 proteins across 24 distinct cellular niches, with supervised machine-learning classifying 1646 proteins into specific compartments including exported sites within host erythrocytes. Comparative genomic analyses revealed spatial patterns of cell evolution shaping Plasmodium species development. This comprehensive subcellular localization map addresses the large proportion of parasite proteome with unknown cellular location or function.

articles.headline: Age-structured sampling improves malaria SCR estimation in seroepidemiology
articles.title_original: Optimizing age-structured sampling for estimating the seroconversion rate in malaria seroepidemiology: a simulation study.
articles.authors: Ko YK, Li S, Britton T, Kagaya W, Kaneko A
articles.details: Monte Carlo simulations optimized age-based sampling for seroconversion rate (SCR) estimation using reverse catalytic models with Plasmodium falciparum MSP1 antibody responses. In stable transmission, sampling older individuals improved low-transmission precision while younger groups better in high-transmission settings when seroreversion rate was known; unknown rates required balanced age distributions. Application to Sri Lanka serological data demonstrated alternative sampling strategies could substantially enhance precision and statistical power.

articles.headline: Single-cell RNA sequencing reveals drug-specific parasite responses
articles.title_original: Single-cell analysis of Plasmodium falciparum transcripts after drug perturbation identifies feedback regulation as well as increased transmission potential.
articles.authors: Godinez-Macias KP, Calla J, Jepsen K, Winzeler EA
articles.details: Researchers used single-cell RNA sequencing on P. falciparum-infected red blood cells to characterize parasite responses to artemisinin, atovaquone, and ganaplacide treatments. Each drug induced different transcriptional profiles across cell populations. Artemisinin treatment enriched gametocyte-associated genes, while atovaquone showed enrichment in pyrimidine biosynthesis transcripts. Results demonstrate scRNA-seq's potential to classify drug mechanisms of action and identify feedback regulation in parasite drug responses.

articles.headline: Blighia sapida compounds show promise as PfDHODH inhibitors
articles.title_original: In-silico study of the bioactive compounds of Blighia sapida koenig revealed a novel Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor for malaria.
articles.authors: Akinyemi DO, Odunitan TT, Ojeniyi FD, Akinyemi AO, Rahman N, Zafar H, Ehigie LO, Afolabi OK, Ojurongbe O
articles.details: Researchers performed in-silico analysis of compounds from Blighia sapida leaf extract for antimalarial activity. Gas chromatography-mass spectrometry identified 21 compounds; molecular docking and dynamics simulations assessed binding to P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase. Three compounds demonstrated strong binding affinities (-8.1 to -10.0 kcal/mol) with favorable drug-like properties. 2-methyl-6-(4-methylenecyclohex-2-en-1-yl)hept-2-en-4-one showed the greatest stability (RMSD = 0.12).

articles.headline: Nigeria malaria vulnerability mapping reveals north-south disparities
articles.title_original: Topological Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (ToF-AHP) for geospatial assessment of malaria vulnerability in Nigeria.
articles.authors: Atijosan A, Atijosan A
articles.details: This study applied Topological Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process to assess malaria vulnerability in Nigeria using ITN usage, maternal education, socioeconomic status, antenatal care attendance, and distance to health facilities. Results revealed pronounced north-south disparities, with Northeast and Northwest regions showing high vulnerability driven by low maternal education (accounting for 34.63% of overall vulnerability), poverty, and limited healthcare access. Approximately 62.52% of very high malaria incidence zones coincided with high and very high vulnerability areas.

articles.headline: PfMD3 zinc-finger protein regulates male gamete fertility
articles.title_original: The CCCH-type zinc-finger Pf MD3 promotes translation for malaria parasite transmission.
articles.authors: van Biljon R, Bhatnagar S, Kumar R, Gliozzi M, Xu G, Tripathi A, Ashrafi A, Swearingen K, Sinnis P, Llinás M, Painter HJ
articles.details: This study investigated PfMD3, a CCCH-type zinc-finger protein, in P. falciparum gamete formation and transmission. Genetic disruption of PfMD3 revealed a male fertility defect that decreased infectivity to mosquitoes. Molecular characterization showed PfMD3 binds specific mRNA sequences in male gamete development transcripts. Proteins encoded by PfMD3-bound transcripts decreased significantly in knockout parasites, indicating PfMD3 regulates translation and male gamete fertility.

articles.headline: Ecological niche modeling identifies climatic suitability for Argentine vector
articles.title_original: MODELING THE POTENTIAL CLIMATIC SUITABILITY OF ANOPHELES PSEUDOPUNCTIPENNIS, THE HISTORICAL VECTOR OF MALARIA IN ARGENTINA.
articles.authors: Duque PL, León R, Coello Peralta RD, Villarroel Martínez EI, Liria-Salazar J, Dantur Juri MJ
articles.details: Researchers applied ecological niche modeling using MaxEnt to identify climatically suitable areas for Anopheles pseudopunctipennis in Argentina. Analysis used 312 occurrence records from 287 localities and bioclimatic variables from WorldClim 2.1. The best-supported model achieved a validation area under the curve of 0.83 with 10% omission rate. Suitable conditions were concentrated in northwestern Argentina (Jujuy, Salta, Tucumán, Catamarca) with additional suitable areas in other provinces.

articles.headline: Mobility and malaria incidence show context-dependent association
articles.title_original: Mobility and malaria incidence in Indonesia's low-endemic areas during the COVID-19 period.
articles.authors: Arisanti RR, Maula AW, Ahmad RA, Utarini A
articles.details: This study examined the association between population mobility and malaria incidence in four Indonesian districts during COVID-19 (March 2020–May 2022). Researchers analyzed 480,575 mobility events from Facebook data and health records. Multivariable Poisson regression showed increased mobility was associated with higher malaria incidence (IRR 1.25, 95% CI 1.21–1.30), with additional associations for malaria screening intensity (IRR 2.33) and COVID-19 positivity (IRR 1.12). However, no clear temporal pattern was observed outside Java.

articles.headline: Dynamic mode decomposition reveals control program impacts on transmission
articles.title_original: Evaluating the impact of South-North Joint Malaria Control Project on spatiotemporal transmission dynamics in South Korea using dynamic mode decomposition.
articles.authors: Seo H, Kim BN, Abdulali A, Lee S
articles.details: This study applied compatible-window dynamic mode decomposition to national malaria incidence data (2001–2024) from South Korea to evaluate control program impacts on spatiotemporal transmission. The South-North Joint Malaria Control Project (2008–2011) achieved a 63% reduction in national incidence between 2007 and 2011 while disrupting regional synchrony. The subsequent 5-Year Re-Elimination Plan (2019–2023) was associated with approximately 49% incidence decrease from 2018 to 2021, but incidence increased about 30% by 2023.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention extended to older children shows benefit
articles.title_original: Extension of seasonal malaria chemoprevention to older children: a pragmatic comparative study in two health districts in the Zinder region, Niger.
articles.authors: Djedanem M, Ibrahim A, Nazibou MY, Noura MS, Yacoudima EYM, Zakari A, Mbega HO, Mamane Z, Issaka M, Adehossi E, Testa J, Jambou R
articles.details: This retrospective comparative study evaluated extending seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine to children aged 5–10 years in Niger. The study analyzed health facility data from two districts (2021–2023): one implementing SMC extension (Damagaram Takaya) and one maintaining SMC for children under 5 only (Matameye). In 2021, children aged ≥5 years had higher uncomplicated malaria incidence (33.34 per 1,000 vs 12.45 per 1,000 in younger children). Following SMC expansion, significant decreases in uncomplicated and severe cases occurred.

articles.headline: Oxford nanopore sequencing enables long-read P. falciparum WGS surveillance
articles.title_original: Development of an Oxford nanopore sequencing technology-based whole genome sequencing method for Plasmodium falciparum to support malaria molecular surveillance.
articles.authors: Bakari C, Holzschuh A, Seth MD, Madebe RA, Pereus D, Mandara CI, Schneeberger P, Juliano JJ, Bailey JA, Ishengoma DS, Nsanzabana C
articles.details: This study optimized an Oxford nanopore technology-based whole-genome sequencing protocol for P. falciparum using dried blood spots and whole blood samples. Parasite DNA enrichment using McrBC/MspJI digestion, microbiome DNA enrichment, and selective whole genome amplification yielded >75% parasite DNA and >90% genome coverage. Sequencing achieved median read lengths >2 kb, >30× depth, and 99.8% accuracy with high concordance to Sanger sequencing. The protocol successfully detected copy number variations in pfmdr1 and pfgch1 drug-resistance markers.

articles.headline: Care-seeking delays for malaria driven by knowledge gaps and attitudes
articles.title_original: Three delays of malaria care-seeking in Malawi: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Olapeju B, Bride M, Volkmann T, Cash S, Dembo E, Kayange M, Gumbo A, Mafuleka T, Boyle J, Mang'ando A, Mbeye NM, Mukwakwa C
articles.details: This cross-sectional study of 913 female caregivers in Malawi (May–July 2021) examined barriers to prompt malaria care-seeking (same or next day from a qualified provider). Using the Three Delays Model, researchers identified that prompt care-seeking was primarily associated with delay 1 (deciding to seek care) with psychosocial factors being significant (aOR 0.59, 95% CI 0.44–0.79). Key barriers included incorrect knowledge of malaria symptoms (aOR 0.71), negative attitudes toward care-seeking (aOR 0.58), and distance to facility (aOR 0.67).

articles.headline: CD36 and ICAM-1 variants modulate sickle cell severity in malaria
articles.title_original: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes influence the severity of sickle cell anemia in a malaria-endemic region : Short title: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes in sickle cell anemia.
articles.authors: Matos I, Santos B, Gonçalves E, Lopes P, Brito M, Arez AP, Faustino P
articles.details: This study of 65 pediatric sickle cell anemia patients in Angola examined how genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion genes influenced disease severity in a malaria-endemic setting. Genotyping of 15 polymorphic sites in CD36 and ICAM-1 genes revealed that CD36 variants rs3211891_C and rs3211938_G, along with ICAM-1 variants rs5491_T and rs5496_A, significantly impacted hematological severity. Notably, SCA patients carrying the ICAM-1 rs5494_T variant showed a 5.63-fold increased risk of malaria infection compared to wild-type carriers.

articles.headline: PfGBP-130 ligand mediates natural killer cell activation against parasites
articles.title_original: LFA-1 interaction with GBP-130 on Plasmodium falciparum-infected red blood cells mediates NK cell activation and parasite control.
articles.authors: Mukhtar O, Dutt R, Panda A, Kumari P, Singh SS, Paul G, Prakash N, Abbas M, Islam MM, Arora P, Tammour A, Mohmmed A, Kumar D, Malhotra P
articles.details: This study identified glycophorin binding protein-130 (PfGBP-130) as a surface-expressed ligand on P. falciparum-infected red blood cells that binds the integrin LFA-1 on natural killer cells. Using LFA-1 αI-Fc fusion protein binding assays and mass spectrometry, researchers confirmed PfGBP-130 as the cognate parasite ligand. Co-culture assays demonstrated that PfGBP-130 promotes NK cell activation, degranulation, and contact-dependent killing of infected cells; neutralizing antibodies against PfGBP-130 significantly impaired these responses.

articles.headline: Placental malaria highly prevalent among first-time mothers
articles.title_original: High burden of histology-confirmed placental malaria and associated adverse birth outcomes among primigravidae in Northwestern Tanzania: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Buhaga G, Mujuni F, Matiku S, Kiritta R, Matovelo D, Ndaboine E, Mazigo HD
articles.details: Hospital-based cross-sectional study of 422 primigravidae women. Placental malaria prevalence was 39.8% by histology and 23.7% by microscopy, with 19.7% maternal peripheral malaria by microscopy and 6.9% congenital malaria. PM was significantly associated with low birth weight (p=0.01), preterm birth (p=0.001), and small-for-gestational-age infants (p=0.048). Fewer than four ANC visits and moderate anemia were significant predictors of PM.

articles.headline: Malaria parasite virus increases transmission and alters host immunity
articles.title_original: MaRNAV-1, an intracellular virus of Plasmodium vivax , is associated with increased parasite transmission and altered host immune response.
articles.authors: Seng D, Ko K, Orban A, Heng S, Feufack-Donfack LB, Grünebast J, Ya N, Dumetz F, Tebben K, Vignolini T, Dore G, Khim N, Salvador J, Ouaid Z, Lefèvre T, Cohuet A, Sommen C, Flamand C, Ruberto AA, Abebe A, Tsigie M, Tasew G, Tollera G, Malleret B, Shenkutie TT, Lo E, Anstey NM, Petrone ME, Cantaert T, Baumgarten S, Serre D, Popovici J
articles.details: Study of MaRNAV-1, an intracellular RNA virus in Plasmodium vivax parasites. MaRNAV-1 presence was positively associated with increased gametocyte abundance and higher oocyst prevalence in membrane feeding assays. MaRNAV-1-infected patients had elevated body temperature and higher circulating cytokines (IFN-γ, CXCL10, IL-1RA, IL-6) independently of parasitemia, with altered immune responses including dendritic cell activation and increased double-negative B cells.

articles.headline: Fungal metabolites show potential as dual-action vector control agents
articles.title_original: Larvicidal and adulticidal activities of Purpureocillium lilacinum Secondary Metabolites against the Malaria Vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Chen D, Niu G, Lai L, Zerpa CV, Li J
articles.details: Investigation of secondary metabolites from Purpureocillium lilacinum against Anopheles gambiae.
