Leucinostatin A showed LC90 of 9 µg/mL against fourth-instar larvae and LD90 of approximately 1 µg per adult female. Pulixin had LC90 of 68 µg/mL against fourth-instar larvae. Leucinostatin A can inhibit parasite development in mosquitoes, kill larvae in aquatic habitats, and kill adult mosquitoes by contact, providing dual-action transmission blocking.

articles.headline: P. falciparum glutamate dehydrogenase shows promise as diagnostic marker
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of Plasmodium falciparum glutamate dehydrogenase with known Plasmodium falciparum diagnostic targets using quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction.
articles.authors: Ahmad A, Nema S, Krishna S, Gupta H, Singh A, Verma AK, Singh MP, Anvikar AR, Bharti PK
articles.details: Retrospective qPCR analysis of 150 P. falciparum-positive and 50 negative samples comparing five diagnostic targets. PfGDH showed 69% sensitivity, 98% specificity, and AUC 83%, compared to pfhrp2 (72% sensitivity, 100% specificity, AUC 86%), pfhrp3 (71%, 96%, 84%), pfaldolase (29%, 100%, 64%), and pfldh (19%, 100%, 60%). PfGDH demonstrates notable diagnostic potential as an alternative for detecting P. falciparum when pfhrp2 deletions occur.

articles.headline: DHA-PPQ reduces maternal adverse events in HIV-positive pregnant women
articles.title_original: Maternal, Fetal, and Neonatal Outcomes of Additional Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine to Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole in Intermittent Preventive Treatment for Malaria in HIV-Positive Pregnancies: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Yunivita V, Maharani AJ, Bernadus CA, Sinaga EIJ, Simanjuntak ER
articles.details: Meta-analysis of three randomized trials (1,353 participants) comparing TMP-SMX alone (800/160 mg) with TMP-SMX plus DHA-PPQ (40/320 mg, three times) for intermittent preventive treatment. DHA-PPQ significantly reduced maternal adverse events (OR: 0.78, 95% CI 0.67–0.91, p=0.003), including low hemoglobin (OR: 0.83, p=0.05) and gastrointestinal events (OR: 0.66, p=0.03). DHA-PPQ showed non-significant trends toward lower malaria infection. No significant differences in fetal adverse events or birth outcomes were observed.

articles.headline: P. coatneyi culture reveals barriers to zoonotic human transmission
articles.title_original: First continuous in vitro culture of Plasmodium coatneyi reveals insights into barriers to human infection.
articles.authors: Suwanarusk R, Chua ACY, Ezenwanne CS, Snounou G, Malleret B, Kosaisavee V, Lombardini ED, Imerbsin R, Sunyakumthorn P, Katakai Y, Sakaguchi M, Kyle DE, Kawai S, Annoura T, Rénia L, Bifani P, Kaneko O, Russell B
articles.details: First continuous in vitro culture of Plasmodium coatneyi established in Macaca fascicularis erythrocytes, with parasitemia reaching 3% after cryopreservation recovery. While P. coatneyi successfully invaded human erythrocytes, asexual development was arrested post-invasion, with parasitemia falling below detection limits by day 9, revealing a putative biological barrier to sustained human infection. Molecular evidence shows 3.5% human P. coatneyi infections in Malaysian archival samples, indicating zoonotic potential.

articles.headline: High-dose primaquine and tafenoquine for P. vivax radical cure
articles.title_original: Dissemination of EFFORT trial results in Indonesia: a stakeholder meeting report on tafenoquine and primaquine for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Pasaribu AP, Jimanto V, Ainur F, Saragih NR, Hadi H, Siagian DSP, Sinaga BJ, Satyagraha AW, Laihad FJ, Diptyanusa A, Ubra RR, Tekege O, Darmayanti L, Herdiana H, Adella J, Mannion K, Curry E, Poespoprodjo JR, Kenangalem E, Burdam FH, Sutanto I, Nelwan EJ, Ganadhi GJ, Shrestha B, Mnjala H, Hawley WA, Prameswari HD, Theodora M, Silalahi FSM, Susanto H, Immanuella F, Puspadewi RT, Fajariyani SB, Samad I, Supriyanto D, Muhafilah N, Isa R, Price RN, Thriemer K
articles.details: Dissemination report of the EFFORT clinical trial conducted in four countries (Cambodia, Ethiopia, Indonesia, Pakistan) evaluating high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg over 7 days) and single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) compared with standard 14-day low-dose primaquine for P. vivax radical cure. In Indonesia, both regimens were co-administered with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine. Stakeholder discussions emphasized evidence gaps regarding tafenoquine with artemisinin combination therapies, feasibility of high-dose primaquine as a near-term option, and need for G6PD testing.

articles.headline: Nosocomial malaria transmission risk documented in Nigerian hospital wards
articles.title_original: Nosocomial malaria transmission risk in hospital wards in Abeokuta Nigeria: entomological and parasitological evidence from secondary healthcare facilities.
articles.authors: Fawole JK, Lawal NF, Babalola AS, Ojo DA, Sam-Wobo SO, Idowu OA
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of two secondary hospitals in Abeokuta among 134 participants (80.6% patients, 19.4% caregivers). Overall malaria prevalence was 74.6% (75.0% in patients, 73.1% in caregivers), predominantly low-density parasitemia. Among 714 mosquitoes collected, 80 were Anopheles gambiae s.l. females; 81.3% were fed and 38.8% were parous, with 2.5% testing positive for P. falciparum sporozoites. Only 3.7% of participants used bed nets during admission.

articles.headline: RTS,S and R21 vaccines show protective efficacy in African children
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of RTS, S and R21 malaria vaccines in children under five in Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
articles.authors: Nurani KM, Kadernani N, Korir E, Estreed MA, Dhali A, Clinton R, Kadernani K, Kipkorir V, Omullo P, Masika M
articles.details: Systematic review of 11 randomized trials (27,178 participants) comparing RTS,S and R21 vaccines in African children. RTS,S efficacy: 32% vs first malaria (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.61–0.76), 29% vs multiple episodes (IRR 0.71, 95% CI 0.55–0.91), 17–22% vs severe malaria. R21 showed higher efficacy: 68% vs first episodes (HR 0.32, 95% CI 0.26–0.39), 67% vs multiple episodes (IRR 0.33, 95% CI 0.27–0.41). Severe adverse events: 11.2% (RTS,S), 3% (R21); no vaccine-attributable deaths.

articles.headline: Community malaria awareness high but prevention practices inconsistent
articles.title_original: Community and healthcare worker contributions to malaria prevention: a culturally tailored behaviour change approach in Jozini, South Africa.
articles.authors: Baloyi RE, Ngcobo S, Kruger T, de Jager C
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 384 community members and 15 healthcare workers in Jozini, South Africa. Community showed 96% malaria awareness, 97% high knowledge (92% correctly identified mosquito bites as transmission), 79% positive attitudes, but only 64% good prevention practices. Knowledge was significantly associated with age (χ²=9.61, p=0.01) but not gender, education, employment, or residence duration. Healthcare workers possessed strong technical knowledge but reported barriers including irregular training, staffing gaps, and commodity shortages.

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structure reveals ACT-451840 inhibition of drug-resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Structural and mechanistic insights into the inhibition of Plasmodium falciparum MDR1.
articles.authors: Zhao Z, Li J, Wang X, Liu X, Wang N, Xu H, Quan C, Gao Y, Zhang J, Wang X, Guo L, Kato N, Deng D, Jiang X
articles.details: Cryo-electron microscopy structure of Plasmodium falciparum MDR1 (PfMDR1) in complex with antimalarial candidate ACT-451840, determined at 3.42 Å resolution. ACT-451840 binds in the central cavity and locks PfMDR1 in an inward-open conformation, inhibiting basal ATPase activity. Structural comparison with mefloquine-bound state explains how ACT-451840 resistance mutations lead to mefloquine sensitization. Structural analysis reveals selective mechanism of ACT-451840 against PfMDR1 compared to human ABCB1.

articles.headline: Plasmodium pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway reveals multiple drug targets
articles.title_original: Genetic analysis of pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Rajaram K, Sievert ML, Elahi R, Blauwkamp J, Dillard LB, Absalon S, Prigge ST
articles.details: Genetic analysis of Plasmodium falciparum pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway enzymes. Knockdown of aspartate transcarbamoylase (PfATC) proved lethal and was not rescued by apicoplast metabolic bypass; N-terminal truncation impaired growth. Carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II (PfCPSII) was essential for parasite proliferation. Deletion of dihydroorotase (PfDHO) in parasites with uracil salvage pathway resulted in uracil auxotrophy, confirming essential function.

articles.headline: Knowledge-to-practice gap drives silent malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and practices towards malaria in a peri-urban Cameroonian village: Persistent silent transmission despite satisfactory awareness.
articles.authors: Joko S, Poumachu Y, Ouko R, Kala-Chouakeu NA, Bamou R, Elanga-Ndillé E, Awono-Ambene P, Antonio-Nkondjio C, Tchuinkam T, Ndo C
articles.details: Cross-sectional study with 249 participants (microscopy parasitology screening) and 104 with complete data in a Cameroonian village. Overall malaria prevalence 24.9%, significantly higher in 5-18 years age group (13.25%). Despite satisfactory knowledge and attitudes, poor practices and knowledge-to-practice gap persisted, contributing to silent transmission.

articles.headline: PfEMP1 antibody gaps distinguish cerebral malaria and severe anemia
articles.title_original: Distinct Plasmodium falciparum Erythrocyte Membrane Protein 1 Domain Cassette-Associated Antibody Gaps Specific to Cerebral Malaria and Severe Malarial Anemia.
articles.authors: Travassos MA, Han P, Coulibaly D, Stucke EM, Zhou AE, Shrestha B, Nakajima R, Jain A, Taghavian O, Jasinskas A, Lawton JG, Laurens MB, Niangaly A, Pike A, Ouattara A, Berry AA, Adams M, Takala-Harrison S, Kouriba B, Kone AK, Rowe JA, Doumbo OK, Lyke KE, Felgner PL, Plowe CV, Thera MA
articles.details: Custom protein microarray of 79 PfEMP1 fragments (DCs 1, 4, 5, 8, 13, 15) in Malian children with cerebral malaria, severe malarial anemia, concurrent CM+SMA, and uncomplicated malaria controls. Distinct antibody gaps identified: DCs 1, 8, and 13 for SMA; DC13 for CM. Few PfEMP1 DCs were differentially seroreactive, but seroreactivity to downstream domain couplets differed.

articles.headline: PfSR-X2 RNA-binding protein regulates P. falciparum gene expression
articles.title_original: The serine-arginine-rich protein PfSR-X2 modulates human malaria parasite gene expression during the intraerythrocytic developmental cycle.
articles.authors: Hu Y, Zhuang C, Li M, Wu J, Zhou L, Li M, Li X, Du X, Shang X, Bai Y, Zhao D, Chen S, Fan Y
articles.details: Characterized serine-arginine-rich protein PfSR-X2 (PF3D7_0319500) in P. falciparum asexual development via CRISPR-Cas9 conditional knockdown. Conditional depletion reduced parasite proliferation and impaired merozoite production. RNA-seq revealed stage-specific changes in genes involved in RNA metabolism, antigenic variation, host-parasite interactions, and ApiAP2 pathways; rif multigene family strongly perturbed while var transcriptional pattern largely unchanged.

articles.headline: Cardiolipin synthase essential for P. falciparum mitochondrial function
articles.title_original: A prokaryotic-like Cardiolipin Synthase is essential to maintain mitochondrial function, lipid homeostasis and survival of blood stage malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Islam MM, Anwar MO, Yamaryo-Botté Y, Rath PP, Abbas M, Thakur V, Rathore S, Botté CY, Mohmmed A
articles.details: Identified prokaryotic-like Cardiolipin Synthase (PfCLS) in P. falciparum mitochondria. Inducible knockdown disrupted parasite development. Loss of CLS impaired mitochondrial respiratory super-complex assembly and caused hypersensitivity to Atovaquone; lipidomic analysis revealed loss of cardiolipin and phospholipid perturbations.

articles.headline: Subclinical malaria parasitemia in sickle cell disease, Northern Uganda
articles.title_original: Subclinical malaria in sickle cell disease in Northern Uganda: prevalence, predictors, and hematologic impact.
articles.authors: Matejak NJ, Ngolobe H, O'Connell CT, Otika D, McGowan K, Ogwang Q, Nakyeyune ML, Azrin S, Dieckhaus KD, Bongomin F
articles.details: Cross-sectional study (June-August 2025) at an SCD clinic in Northern Uganda with 274 participants (median age 12, 54.4% male). Subclinical malaria prevalence 23.4% by blood smear or RDT (smear-confirmed 11.7%, RDT 22.3%). Mean hemoglobin lower in smear-positive vs smear-negative participants (6.87 vs 7.82 g/dL, p=0.0027); broader RDT-based definitions not associated with hemoglobin differences.

articles.headline: LEF1-LAG3 axis regulates CD4+ T cell responses in malaria
articles.title_original: The LEF1-LAG3 axis regulates CD4(+) T cell function during Plasmodium yoelii NSM infection.
articles.authors: Peng W, Liang G, Lu K, Mo F, Xie X, Chen M, Yuan H, Luo L, Wang X, Xu L, Wei H, Li L, Zhao S, Xie H, Pan X, Huang J
articles.details: Murine model of Plasmodium yoelii NSM using scRNA-seq and molecular assays. Infection induced CD4+ T cell activation with upregulated ICOS/CD69, downregulated CD62L, and marked LAG3 upregulation. LAG3+ CD4+ T cells exhibited activated phenotype (Ki67+), effector phenotype (CD44hiCD62Llo), and upregulation of co-inhibitory receptors (PD-1, TIM-3, TIGIT).

articles.headline: Prime-and-trap vaccination achieves sterile liver stage protection
articles.title_original: A prime-and-trap vaccination strategy to evaluate minimal requirements for malaria liver stage protection in mice.
articles.authors: Watson FN, Reynolds RA, Kalata AC, Shears MJ, Murphy SC
articles.details: Prime-and-trap vaccination strategy in mice with Plasmodium yoelii CSP-specific CD8+ T cells. Progressive de-escalation of CSP-HDT (hydrodynamic transfection) doses tested. Sterile protection against sporozoite challenge achieved with CSP-HDT doses as low as 5 ng DNA, but lost at 50 pg dose with associated low CSP-specific CD8+ T cells in liver.

articles.headline: Malaria parasite genomics evolving toward routine surveillance systems
articles.title_original: Malaria Parasite Genomics: Decentralization, Diversification, and Development Goals.
articles.authors: Amambua-Ngwa A, Neafsey DE, Volkman SK, Diallo MA
articles.details: Review of malaria parasite genomics field evolution driven by changing technology, expanded capacity, and shifting funding. Field shifted from descriptive/discovery to routine surveillance informing public health decisions, enabled by wider sequencing access and cost-effective targeted methods. Key needs include standardizing data formats, analysis pipelines, and sharing practices for translating genomic insights into action.

articles.headline: Climate change increases P. vivax risk across Brazil by 2050
articles.title_original: Climate change and Plasmodium vivax Malaria Risk in Brazil: Developing adaptive tool for Brazilian Municipalities.
articles.authors: C M Sousa T, Hacon SS, Pedra GU, Lemos CMG, Sallum MAM, Ladeia-Andrade S, Reis FB, Arcoverde G, Alves L, Ometto J
articles.details: Multilevel analysis of P. vivax malaria risk under climate change scenarios (2030, 2050; RCP 4.5, RCP 8.5) in Brazilian municipalities. Key climate variables: maximum temperature, relative humidity, Simple Daily Intensity Index (SDII). Growing malaria risk across Brazil with greatest impact in Amazon, expanding to Southeast and Northeast by 2050; maximum temperature (β=0.35), SDII (β=0.19), and relative humidity (β=0.12) were influential factors.

articles.headline: Genetic diversity of Plasmodium vivax invasion protein RIPR
articles.title_original: Unraveling the genetic diversity of RIPR, a key invasion protein in Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.authors: Cebrian-Carmona J, Gonzalez-Ceron L, Valero-Galvez V, Garrido-Cárdenas JA, Mesa-Valle CM
articles.details: No abstract provided; based on title only.

articles.headline: Hydroxamic acids as HDAC inhibitor antimalarial drug leads
articles.title_original: Hydroxamic Acids as HDAC Inhibitor Drug Leads for Malaria.
articles.authors: Dawood WA, Macdonald JR, Anzenhofer C, Skinner-Adams TS, Reid RC, Kurz T, Fairlie DP, Andrews KT
articles.details: Review of histone/lysine deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitors as antimalarial drug targets, given HDACs' essential roles in P. falciparum transcriptional and developmental control. Assessed antiplasmodial structure-activity relationships for >750 hydroxamic acids with in vitro activity against P. falciparum. Provides insights into potency, selectivity, and physicochemical properties to guide investigations of this chemotype as drug leads.

articles.headline: Complex malaria transmission and widespread insecticide resistance, Cameroon
articles.title_original: Complex non-falciparum transmission and widespread insecticide resistance in Penka-Michel, a hyperendemic malaria focus of the Cameroon highlands.
articles.authors: Kiam BC, Ibrahima I, Mbida JAM, Bouopda-Tuedom AG, Abate L, White SJ, V CTN, Tumamo BF, Morlais I, Ayong L, Lin JT, Ali IM, Juliano JJ, Dinglasan RR, Nsango SE
articles.details: 12-month longitudinal entomological study (September 2023-August 2024) in Western Cameroon Highlands identified three Anopheles vectors: An. funestus (43.7%), An. gambiae (42.9%), An. ziemanni (13.5%). Hyperendemic site with annual EIR 839.5 infective-bites/human/night; P. falciparum predominated (70.2%), but significant non-falciparum: P. malariae (27.5%), P. ovale (22.1%). Widespread insecticide resistance (70% resistant homozygotes); L119F-GSTe2 associated with P. ovale curtisi (IRR=1.15, p=0.03), L1014F-kdr with P. malariae (IRR=2.2, p=0.04).

articles.headline: P. falciparum invasion ligand expression varies regionally between Asia and Africa
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum invasion ligand gene transcript profiles in different populations.
articles.authors: Lantero-Escolar E, Stewart LB, Singh B, Claessens A, Amambua-Ngwa A, Conway DJ
articles.details: Reverse transcription quantitative PCR analyzed transcript levels of three eba genes and five Rh genes in P. falciparum isolates from Malaysia (Sabah) and Gambia cultured under identical conditions. Significant regional differences were observed: eba175 levels were higher in Malaysian isolates, while eba181 and Rh2b levels were lower. Among Malaysian isolates, eba181 transcript levels correlated positively with parasite multiplication rates, but this correlation was absent in Gambian isolates. Variation in expression profiles was not associated with single or multiple genotypes within isolates.

articles.headline: P. malariae and multiple Plasmodium species imported to Trinidad with local transmission signal
articles.title_original: Imported malaria in Trinidad, West Indies: Detection of multiple non-endemic Plasmodium species.
articles.authors: Sant C, Mohammed N, Oura C, Sanford B, Kirk S, Ibeleme D, Nicholls S, Georges K
articles.details: Cross-sectional study examined 153 blood samples from suspected malaria cases using microscopy and PCR with cytochrome oxidase 1 sequencing. Fifty-six samples (36.60%) were positive with 100% concordance between methods and 98.21% agreement on species identification. P. vivax, P. falciparum, P. ovale, and P. malariae were identified. Notably, two of three P. malariae cases had no travel history outside Trinidad, suggesting possible local transmission. Symptoms of headache and nausea were statistically significant risk factors for PCR positivity.

articles.headline: Aedes and Anopheles vectors show heterogeneous spatial distribution and distinct temporal biting patterns in Benin
articles.title_original: Species diversity and human biting rates of malaria and dengue vectors in central and northern Benin.
articles.authors: Hoyochi I, Zoungbédji DM, Konkon AK, Salako AS, Konkon AC, Adjovi RV, Adoha CJ, Fongnikin A, Sominahouin A, Baba-Moussa L, Akogbéto MC, Padonou GG
articles.details: Multimethod entomological survey across five communes in central and northern Benin collected 2,337 adult mosquitoes using larval surveys, human-landing catches, oviposition traps, and pyrethrum spray captures. Of 993 breeding sites inspected, 51.0% were positive. Seven mosquito species were identified: Aedes aegypti, Ae. albopictus, Ae. vittatus, Anopheles gambiae, Culex nebulosus, Cx. quinquefasciatus, and Cx. tigripes, with statistically significant spatial heterogeneity among localities (p < 0.001). Aedes aegypti exhibited predominantly diurnal biting activity, while Anopheles gambiae showed marked nocturnal activity increases.

articles.headline: HLA-C*07 shows binding affinity to P. falciparum MSP3 antigen with potential protective association
articles.title_original: HLA polymorphisms shape divergent outcomes of Toxoplasma and Plasmodium infection in Eastern Indian HbE/β-thalassemia cohort.
articles.authors: Bhattacharya S, Rahaman M, Suman S, Rout DK, Mondal S, Purwar S, Dhingra B, Shukla PC, Mukherjee G, Gupta M, Guha D, Bhattacharya MK, Dolai TK, Chakravorty N, Mukherjee B
articles.details: Integrated NGS and Sanger sequencing study of 61 HbE/β-thalassemia patients and 50 healthy controls identified HLA polymorphisms differentially associated with apicomplexan infection outcomes. While HLA-A*33 provided protection against Toxoplasma gondii via enhanced CD8+ IFN-γ+ responses, HLA-C*07 demonstrated strong binding affinity to the P. falciparum MSP3 peptide, with NGS profiling identifying a potential negative association between HLA-C*07 and Plasmodium infection. Protection phenotypes were independent of parasite entry and transfusion frequency, demonstrating pathogen-specific antigen recognition.

articles.headline: Thrombocytopenia strongest independent predictor of P. falciparum malaria in Ghana
articles.title_original: Association between thrombocytopaenia and Plasmodium falciparum infection in Ghana.
articles.authors: Duneeh RV, Amoada ST, Yankey E, Asante JA, Allotey EA, Akorsu EE, Klugah MA, Ablordey K
articles.details: Cross-sectional study across three sites in Ghana examined 388 participants (both malaria-positive and negative) diagnosed by microscopy, measuring haematological parameters including platelet count, hemoglobin, white blood cells, and erythrocyte sedimentation rate. Platelet counts were significantly lower in malaria cases across all subgroups (median 152.0 × 10⁹/L vs 234.0 × 10⁹/L in controls, p < 0.001). Thrombocytopenia showed the strongest association with P. falciparum malaria (adjusted OR = 11.8; 95% CI: 6.18-24.7; p < 0.001). A model incorporating haematological indices demonstrated superior predictive performance (AUC = 77.6%) compared to sociodemographic factors alone (AUC = 57.9%).

articles.headline: Scoping review identifies four main malaria diagnostic modalities used in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Current practices and innovative diagnostics for Malaria in Ethiopia: a scoping review.
articles.authors: Bantie GM, Teshome DF, Asres GD, Moges NA, Tadege M, Awoke AK, Berneh AA, Ferede AG, Abebe DL, Tiruneh EK, Melese AA, Sewagegn N, Wubetu GY
articles.details: Scoping review following Arksey and O'Malley methodology identified 13 eligible studies on malaria diagnostics in Ethiopia (38.5% published 2008-2024). Study designs were predominantly cross-sectional (84.6%), with one RCT (7.7%). Most studies were conducted in Amhara region (43.75%) and Oromia region (31.25%). Four main diagnostic modalities were identified and characterized: light microscopy, rapid diagnostic tests (RDT), polymerase chain reaction (PCR), and loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP), each with distinct advantages and limitations.

articles.headline: Rapid diagnostic test superior to microscopy for malaria detection in pregnancy in Kenya
articles.title_original: Evaluation of microscopy and first response™ malaria Ag PAN/Pf RDT compared to qPCR and hemoglobin levels in pregnant women at delivery in Busia County, Western Kenya.
articles.authors: Kipchirchir D, Wakoli DM, Imbusi G, Makandi R, Chimwani E, Chumbe SA, Ghebremichael M, Ong'echa JM, Bargul JL, Idris AH, Ondigo BN
articles.details: Cross-sectional study enrolled 236 pregnant women delivering at hospitals in Busia County, Kenya, testing peripheral and placental blood for malaria using microscopy, rapid diagnostic test (First Response Malaria Ag PAN/Pf), and quantitative PCR. Peripheral malaria prevalence was 7.2% by microscopy, 8.1% by RDT, and 9.0% by qPCR. Compared with peripheral qPCR, the RDT demonstrated higher sensitivity (90%; 95% CI 0.77-1.0) but lower specificity (80%; 95% CI 0.74-0.85) than microscopy (80% sensitivity, 89% specificity). Plasmodium falciparum predominated in peripheral samples (81.3% by microscopy), and no significant associations were observed between malaria in pregnancy and ABO blood groups or anaemia.

articles.headline: Malaria, anemia, and micronutrient deficiencies widely co-occur across Madagascar populations
articles.title_original: Burden and Co-Occurrence of Anemia, Micronutrient Deficiencies, Inflammation, and Malaria Among Women, Adolescents, and Children in Madagascar: A National Cross-Sectional Survey.
articles.authors: Rohner F, Rakotovao JM, El-Mallah C, Andrianarison R, Randrenarizo N, Wegmüller R, Rakotovao R, Ravaoarisoa L, Joyeux M, Wirth JP, Annan Z, Petry N, Wieringa FT, Bastaraud A, Randrianirina F, Labonté JM, Abdelnour A, Jones KS, Rezzi S, Rajoelina MT, Ralaimiadana AR, Vololontiana HM, Yasmine LL, Galetti V
articles.details: 2024 National Micronutrient Survey across 23 regions in Madagascar using 2-stage probabilistic sampling examined malaria, anemia, inflammation, and micronutrient deficiencies in preschool children, adolescents, and non-pregnant women. Malaria was detected in 4% to 14% of participants, with inflammation present in 21% to 46%. Anemia affected 42% of preschool children and approximately one-third of older groups. Zinc deficiency affected one-third of all groups, riboflavin deficiency affected 75% of preschool children and nearly 90% of older groups, and folate deficiency affected 12% of adolescent girls and 21% of non-pregnant women.

articles.headline: Community-led priority setting shapes national malaria programs in federal Nepal
articles.title_original: Community and stakeholder engagement in national priority setting and participatory research for HIV, Tuberculosis, and Malaria programs in Nepal.
articles.authors: Neupane S, Shiwakoti R, Aryal L, Nzima M, Lohar I, Sitaula A, Karmacharya U, Paudel K, Bhattachan A, Devkota S, Bhattarai RK, Adhikari B
articles.details: Qualitative study conducted January to June 2020 involving over 2,000 community members and at-risk populations across 700 group discussion sessions in all seven provinces and 72 municipalities, plus 100 key informant interviews with government officials and civil society leaders. The participatory priority-setting process identified system gaps including drug stockouts, stigma in care, and gender-related barriers. Participants recommended practical interventions including establishing HIV testing points for migrants at border areas, integrating multi-disease diagnostic platforms, and strengthening community-level surveillance systems. The government pledged 20% domestic co-financing for programs.

articles.headline: Financial constraints and drug stockouts delay malaria treatment in Apac District, Uganda
articles.title_original: Determinants of time to malaria treatment-seeking behaviour among caregivers of children under five in Uganda: a case study of Apac district.
articles.authors: Odur B, Opio B, Nabulya O
articles.details: Cross-sectional analytical study in Apac District, Uganda enrolled 240 households to examine determinants of time to malaria treatment-seeking among caregivers of children under five using Cox proportional hazards regression. All caregivers (70% female) sought treatment for their children. Secondary education increased the time to treatment seeking (HR = 1.67, p = 0.044), while significant delays were associated with unavailable medicine at the hospital (HR = 0.61, p = 0.001) and financial constraints (HR = 0.49, p < 0.001). Key barriers to timely treatment included inadequate drug supply at health facilities and limited caregiver education on early treatment.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine maintains high efficacy for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine for uncomplicated plasmodium falciparum malaria treatment in mozambique: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Nhama A, Aide P, Torres-Fernandez D, Varo R, Nhacolo A, Bassat Q
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis synthesized efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) for uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Mozambique from six studies comprising 1,862 participants. Pooled uncorrected efficacy was 91.0% (95% CI 84.2-94.6), while polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-corrected efficacy was 98.3% (95% CI 96.9-99.1). During 28-day follow-up, early treatment failure occurred in 0.05% (95% CI 0.01-0.38), late clinical failure in 2.73% (95% CI 1.65-4.50), and late parasitological failure in 5.3% (95% CI 3.10-8.96). Recrudescence occurred in 1.8% (95% CI 1.20-2.70) of cases, with new infections accounting for 8.6% (95% CI 7.30-10.10).

articles.headline: South Korea's NMES shaped post-war public health mobilization and malaria control
articles.title_original: Mobilizing Public Health in South Korea: NMES (National Malaria Eradication Service) and its Legacy, 1945-1969.
articles.authors: DiMoia JP
articles.details: Historical analysis examines the South Korean government's strategies and pedagogy for mobilizing public health efforts following the Korean War, specifically focusing on the formation of the National Malaria Eradication Service (NMES) in the early 1960s. The paper documents how NMES transformed from an external force to a familiar partner working with citizens and villagers to address the wartime rise in malaria cases, highlighting how public health programs depend on community participation.

articles.headline: Adjuvant supply chains vital for TB and malaria vaccines
articles.title_original: Understanding adjuvant supply chains for new TB and malaria vaccines.
articles.authors: Gotham D, Frick M, Harrington M
articles.details: Based on title only; no abstract provided. Article addresses vaccine production logistics and supply chain considerations for new TB and malaria vaccine development.

articles.headline: Malaria and helminth prevalence high in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria and Soil-Transmitted Helminths and Associated Factors Among Pregnant Women Visiting Mizan-Tepi University Teaching Hospital, Southwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Duguma T, Fikadu S, Kochito S, Mohammed H, Demango F
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 330 pregnant women in Southwest Ethiopia (March-June 2024) measuring malaria and soil-transmitted helminth (STH) prevalence. Malaria prevalence was 25.2% (83/330) and STH infections 29.4% (97/330), with Ascaris lumbricoides most common. Rural residence (AOR=2.011, p=0.024) and farmer occupation (AOR=4.801, p=0.048) showed significant associations with malaria. Stagnant water presence (AOR=2.150, p=0.008) and insecticide residual spray use (AOR=1.756, p=0.047) also associated with malaria; handwashing before meals and after toilet protective against STH (AOR=6.468, p=0.002 and AOR=8.826, p=0.001).

articles.headline: P. vivax microhaplotype panel enables genomic surveillance
articles.title_original: PvGAP: Development of a globally-applicable, highly-multiplexed microhaplotype amplicon panel for Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Hubbard A, Solares E, Bradley L, Jeang B, Yewhalaw D, Janies D, Lo E, Yan G, Hemming-Schroeder E
articles.details: Describes development of PvGAP (Globally-applicable Amplicon Panel for P. vivax), a microhaplotype amplicon panel evaluated with field samples from Ethiopia and MalariaGEN Pv4 database whole genomes. Panel features 80 high-diversity targets for population genomics and 8 targets for epidemiological interest including putative drug resistance markers. PvGAP achieves robust amplification and provides competitive accuracy for relatedness inference across disparate geographic regions. Authors conclude it is a cost-effective option for genomic epidemiology supporting drug resistance surveillance, discrimination of local vs imported cases, and separation of reinfection, recrudescence, and relapse.

articles.headline: Atriplex halimus extracts show mosquito repellent potential
articles.title_original: Phytochemical profiling, metabolomics, and molecular docking studies of Atriplex halimus aerial parts revealing potential insecticidal activity against the malaria vector Anopheles pharoensis.
articles.authors: Elhawary EA, Waheeb HO, Abdelhafiz AHA, El-Waseif AA, Shehata AZI
articles.details: Phytochemical analysis of Atriplex halimus aerial parts identified 78 secondary metabolites (predominantly flavonoids and triterpenoids) via UPLC/MS profiling. Repellent bioassays against Anopheles pharoensis females demonstrated dose-dependent effects. Dichloromethane (DCM) fraction at 6.67 mg/cm² achieved highest repellency at 81.92%. Molecular docking studies supported antimicrobial and insecticidal findings. Authors conclude A. halimus aerial parts represent a rich source of bioactive metabolites with dual antimicrobial and mosquito-repellent potential for natural product-based vector control.

articles.headline: Open malaria intervention data essential for elimination
articles.title_original: Public Availability of Malaria Intervention Data Will Be Vital for the Acceleration of Its Control and Elimination.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Syed Shah AH, Yadav CP, Rahi M, Sharma A
articles.details: Policy advocacy piece arguing for global policy mandating open access to malaria intervention data (e.g., rapid diagnostics used, antimalarial drugs used, insecticide-treated net distribution, vector control tools). Estimates 610,000 malaria deaths in 2024 in sub-Saharan Africa. Uses India as case study highlighting district-level data gaps. Authors propose standardized data platforms and argue accessible data is essential to innovation, transparency, and equitable resource allocation for malaria control and elimination.

articles.headline: Sustained malaria increases in five high-burden countries
articles.title_original: Turning the tide against malaria in high-burden African countries: Trends, threats, and solutions.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Sharma A
articles.details: Analysis of WHO epidemiological data 2015-2024 using Mann-Kendall trend test identified sustained increases in malaria cases in five High Burden to High Impact countries: Nigeria (MK=3.94, p=0.002), Democratic Republic of Congo (MK=3.76, p=0.002), Cameroon (MK=3.40, p=0.005), Uganda (MK=2.86, p=0.02), and Mali (MK=2.50, p=0.04). Mortality trends were heterogeneous and mostly non-significant. Study identified threats including antimalarial drug resistance, insecticide resistance, pfhrp2 gene deletions, and Anopheles stephensi invasion, alongside climate change and conflict.

articles.headline: Geospatial risk mapping identifies high-risk malaria zones
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria risk using geospatial and AHP approaches in Nsanje District, Malawi.
articles.authors: Banda YM, Nyengere J, Saka K, Tholo H, Chisenga C, Njalammano J, Kamanga BN, Gondwe S, Mtewa AG, Abraham A, Kachedwa L, Chirombo J, Nguvulu A, Chinkaka E, Kadewa W, Mvula RLS
articles.details: Geospatial analysis of Nsanje District, Malawi using environmental variables (temperature, rainfall, elevation, slope, proximity to rivers) and socio-economic factors (proximity to health facilities, population density, roads) to map malaria hazard and vulnerability. Temperature and rainfall most influential for hazard, contributing to 20.4% high-hazard and 35.1% very high-hazard zones. Overall district risk: 43.1% high-risk and 40.5% moderate-risk. Authors recommend enhanced healthcare accessibility and developing early warning systems for targeted prevention and control.

articles.headline: CDC operational guidance for investigating local malaria cases
articles.title_original: CDC Operational Guidance for Investigating Locally Acquired Mosquito-Transmitted Malaria - United States, 2026.
articles.authors: Ranadive N, Barratt JLN, Dotson EM, Gimnig JE, Lenhart AE, Levine RS, Mace KE, McElroy PD, Raphael BH, Sayre D, Steinhardt LC, Sutcliffe AC, Williams SG, Ridpath AD
articles.details: Based on title only; no abstract provided. Document provides operational guidance for CDC investigation of locally-acquired mosquito-transmitted malaria cases in the United States, 2026.

articles.headline: Delayed malaria model reveals insecticide-treatment synergy
articles.title_original: Effects of insecticides and awareness on the dynamics of a delayed malaria model: A real-data calibration.
articles.authors: Khatun MS, Paul P, Ghosh D, Das P
articles.details: Time-delayed mathematical model of human-malaria transmission incorporating incubation period delay and awareness program implementation delay, calibrated on case data from India, Democratic Republic of Congo, and Uganda. Global sensitivity analysis identified key parameters influencing infection dynamics. Model demonstrated supercritical Hopf bifurcation with stable limit cycle and chaotic dynamics with multiple delays destabilizing system earlier. Optimal control analysis found combination of insecticide measures and treatment most effective, with high treatment availability and extensive insecticide use controlling infections despite chaotic behavior.

articles.headline: Birth outcomes, not malaria chemoprevention, reduce vaccine response
articles.title_original: Low birthweight and prematurity, but not malaria chemoprevention, are associated with reduced pneumococcal vaccine immunogenicity in Ugandan infants.
articles.authors: Musinguzi K, Sbarra AN, Bach F, Nankya F, Achom KB, Mwubaha C, Nayebare P, Nansubuga E, Kakuru A, Kizza J, Maato Z, Arinaitwe E, Press KD, Bagaya BS, Tukwasibwe S, Ssewanyana I, Nankabirwa JI, Kamya MR, Dorsey G, Takahashi S, Jagannathan P
articles.details: Cohort study nested within MIC-DroP trial (NCT04978272) of 195 Ugandan infants in high malaria transmission setting, measuring serotype-specific IgG to PCV-10 vaccine at 8 and 24 weeks. Neither infant perennial malaria chemoprevention (PMC) nor malaria exposure (0-14 weeks) were associated with PCV-10 immunogenicity or seroconversion. However, low birthweight (<2500g) associated with 82% lower antibody fold-change (p=0.003) and reduced seroconversion (OR=0.19, p=0.003); preterm birth (<37 weeks) showed similar effects (79% lower fold-change, p=0.018; OR=0.181, p=0.009).

articles.headline: IL-10 and IFN-γ elevations mark severe malaria
articles.title_original: Interferon-γ and Interleukin-10 Profiles Correlate with Disease Severity in Sudanese Children with Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Hajali TA, Mekki MIE, Alaagib NA, Omer IIA, Yahia RM, Hamdan HZ
articles.details: Hospital-based case-control study of 105 Sudanese children (aged 3 months to 16 years): 35 with severe P. falciparum malaria, 35 with uncomplicated malaria, 35 healthy controls. Plasma IL-10 and IFN-γ measured by ELISA. Both cytokines significantly elevated in severe vs uncomplicated malaria and controls (p<0.001), with strong positive correlation (rho=0.688, p<0.001). IL-10 correlated with platelet count (rho=-0.338, p=0.009). IL-10 and IFN-γ demonstrated moderate diagnostic accuracy (AUC=0.720 and AUC=0.687, respectively) in discriminating severe from uncomplicated malaria.

articles.headline: pfmdr1 copy number variation detected in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: The Impact of pfmdr1 Gene Copy Number on the Efficacy of Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine in Treating Malaria in West Sumba and Kupang Districts, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Irdayanti I, Aswad H, Asih PBS, Rozi IE, Herdiana H, Diptyanusa A, Prameswari HD, Yusuf Y, Bahrani B, Hidayah N, Artika IM, Syafruddin D
articles.details: Molecular surveillance study of 41 Plasmodium falciparum isolates from patients treated with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) in West Sumba and Kupang Districts, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia, analyzing pfmdr1 gene copy number variation (CNV) via real-time PCR on dried blood spot (DBS) samples. All 41 patients achieved 100% adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) on Day 42. Pfmdr1 analysis revealed 87.81% single copy (36 isolates) and 12.19% multiple copies (5 isolates), indicating genetic variation suggesting selective pressure against piperaquine. Authors conclude DHA-PPQ remains clinically effective but proactive surveillance necessary.

articles.headline: Chloroquine and SP resistance prevalent; artemisinin resistance absent
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum antimalarial resistance in Angola: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Matias D, Chaves C, Cândido AM, Morais J, Antunes ML, Guler J, Nogueira F, Salamandane A
articles.details: Systematic review of 20 studies (2000-2024) mapped P. falciparum resistance markers. Chloroquine-resistant pfcrt CVIET and SP-resistant pfdhfr/pfdhps IRNGE haplotypes were detected; no validated artemisinin-resistance pfk13 mutations reported. Pfmdr1 showed declining 86Y and 1246Y frequencies with wild-type predominating. Low pfmdr1 and pfpm2 CNV prevalence reported, but emerging amplification noted in southern Angola.

articles.headline: Proactive household visits didn't reduce malaria parasite prevalence
articles.title_original: Impact of proactive community case management of malaria on malaria prevalence in Bankass, Mali: a cluster-randomised controlled trial.
articles.authors: Thwing J, Doumbia S, Traore A, Sylla M, Sogoba M, Kamara I, Samaké Y, Guindo L, Rapp S, Treleaven E, Chiu C, Whidden C, Kone A, Djimde AA, Johnson AD, Kayentao K
articles.details: 3-year cluster-randomized trial covering over 100,000 population in Bankass, Mali compared proactive household case management (ProCCM) visits vs fixed-point community services. No difference found between arms in parasite prevalence among all ages or care-seeking/diagnostic access among children under 5 years. ProCCM CHWs failed to reach target of 2 visits/month in most households.

articles.headline: One-third of African schoolchildren have asymptomatic malaria
articles.title_original: Prevalence and determinants of asymptomatic malaria infection among schoolchildren in Africa: insights from a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Mohamed K, Mussema A, Simieneh A, Woldesenbet D
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis across 33 African countries found 34% (95% CI: 28-40%) asymptomatic malaria prevalence in schoolchildren, ranging 5-53% by region. P. falciparum predominated at 29.3%. Associated with male gender (OR 1.26), anemia (OR 5.51), bednet non-use (OR 1.99), and prior malaria treatment (OR 3.82).

articles.headline: Community barriers and gender dynamics limit indoor residual spraying
articles.title_original: Community-level barriers and facilitators of indoor residual spraying for malaria control: a systematic review of qualitative evidence from sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Pitua I, Okorobe PM, Apili L, Nandera KV, Acoo PG, Nantalaga KC, Lwembawo KD, Otto KA
articles.details: Qualitative evidence synthesis of 10 studies from 7 Sub-Saharan African countries identified 8 major themes shaping IRS uptake: operational/household labor burden, perceived efficacy, socio-political dynamics and trust, programmatic communication, gender dynamics in household decision-making, structural and seasonal incompatibility, implementation equity, and local health infrastructure. Coverage requires human-centered approaches reducing labor burden and strengthening community trust.

articles.headline: Apicoplast metal transporter critical for parasite sexual development
articles.title_original: Identification of a Putative Metal Transporter in the Apicoplast of Malaria Parasites.
articles.authors: Nalder SA, Mayank AK, Liou W, Wohlschlegel JA, Sigala PA
articles.details: Study identified UCT, a doxycycline-interacting transmembrane protein with predicted CorA-family metal transporter structure targeted to the apicoplast periphery. UCT is dispensable for asexual blood-stage parasites, but UCT knockdown in gametocytes impairs sexual parasite development critical for mosquito transmission. Identifies an essential stage-specific role in parasite development.

articles.headline: D-dimer predicts severe malaria with moderate accuracy
articles.title_original: D-dimer as a marker of severe disease in imported malaria: a retrospective observational study.
articles.authors: Poloni A, Rizzo A, Giacomelli A, Colaneri M, Arrigo C, Caronni S, Pieruzzi ME, Behring AH, Grosso S, Dolci A, Antinori S
articles.details: Retrospective study of 85 P. falciparum patients (21/85 with severe malaria, 24.7%) found higher median D-dimer in severe cases [10,943 μg/L FEU vs 2245 μg/L FEU; p=0.0005]. D-dimer associated with severe disease (aOR 1.14 per 1000 μg/L, 95% CI 1.06-1.23). At optimal cutoff 4864 μg/L FEU: sensitivity 0.76, specificity 0.73, overall accuracy 0.74.

articles.headline: Microscopy-confirmed malaria prevalence 7.1% in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated factors of microscopy-confirmed malaria among the general population in Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Faltamo TT, Assele DD, Meskele M, Tadesse T
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 14 studies (8,762 participants) found pooled microscopy-confirmed malaria prevalence of 7.1% (95% CI 4.9-10.2%) in Ethiopia, with regional variation (Amhara 10.7%, southwest 7.3%). Significant determinants included stagnant water (OR 6.71), absence of IRS (OR 5.90), mosquito breeding sites (OR 6.13), travel to endemic areas (OR 5.86), and ITN non-use (OR 4.8).

articles.headline: Trial protocol for ceiling nets as malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Evaluating the impacts of piperonyl butoxide-treated ceiling nets on malaria risk among high-risk populations with limited access to conventional LLINs in Homa Bay Country, Kenya: study protocol for a household randomized control trial.
articles.authors: Ko YK, Kagaya W, Kanoi BN, Kongere J, Gitaka J, Minakawa N
articles.details: Study protocol for 500-household randomized trial in Homa Bay County, Kenya with three arms: PBO-ceiling nets, PBO-LLINs (one per two household members), or both. Primary outcome is Plasmodium infection prevalence by PCR in all age groups at 12 months. Secondary outcomes include entomological indicators (vector density by CDC light traps).

articles.headline: Malaria-HIV coinfection prevalence 9% in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria-HIV coinfection and associated adverse outcomes in Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Yeshanew TM, Gidey B, Abebe B, Waktole G, Getie B, Getu T, Birhane N
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 10 studies found pooled malaria-HIV coinfection prevalence of 9% (95% CI 3-15) in Ethiopia. Studies from 2013-2025 showed higher burden (10%, 95% CI -4-24) than 2002-2012 (9%, 95% CI 1-16). Clinical complications included severe anemia (31%), hyperparasitemia (41%), and CD4 counts ≤200 cells/µL (35%).

articles.headline: 67 malaria RDTs available; most achieve high accuracy
articles.title_original: Advances in Rapid Diagnostic Tests for Malaria: A Landscape Review and Analysis.
articles.authors: Zhang S, Lauff A, Morton JF, Kalanga N, Drain PK
articles.details: Landscape review identified 67 malaria RDTs from 27 international manufacturers. Most are simple to use on whole blood, provide results within 30 minutes, with reported ≥98% diagnostic sensitivity and specificity. Many have received WHO approval for first-line testing. Next-generation assays in late-stage development detect multiple Plasmodium antigens.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistance kelch13 P553L detected in Eswatini cluster
articles.title_original: Genomic Surveillance Reveals Clusters of Plasmodium falciparum Antimalarial Resistance Markers in Eswatini, A Low-Transmission Setting.
articles.authors: Nhlengethwa N, Aranda-Díaz A, Vilakati S, Hubbard A, Routledge I, Lauterbach SB, Makhanthisa TI, De Amaral F, Chisenga M, Mangena B, Sikaala C, Chimumbwa J, Dlamini Q, Raman J, Smith JL, Dlamini SV
articles.details: Genomic surveillance of 437 samples via multiplexed amplicon deep sequencing detected artemisinin-resistance marker kelch13 P553L in 0.9% (cluster of 4, likely imported). Dihydropteroate synthase/dihydrofolate reductase sextuple mutant (SP-resistance) in 7.8%. Multidrug-resistance N86 genotype nearly fixed. More than 60% were polyclonal infections. No hrp2/3 deletions detected.

articles.headline: Larviciding plus bed nets can interrupt malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of larviciding as a supplementary malaria vector control intervention in rural south-eastern Tanzania: A district-level simulation study.
articles.authors: Shirima G, Fairbanks EL, Gavana T, Kiwelu G, Nambunga I, Mlacha YP, Mirau S, Chaki P, Chitnis N, Kiware S
articles.details: District-level modeling study in Tanzania adapted VCOM using field data (2016-2017 and 2019-2021). Immature mosquito mortality from larviciding estimated at 61%. Simulations showed combining 80% ITN coverage with ≥60% larviciding coverage reduced entomological inoculation rate below 1 ib/p/year, threshold needed to interrupt malaria transmission in Anopheles gambiae s.l. populations.

articles.headline: PyCryPH2 protein critical for Plasmodium sporozoite infectivity
articles.title_original: A pleckstrin homology domain protein is involved in crystalloid formation in Plasmodium ookinetes and affects the maturation of infective sporozoites.
articles.authors: Tachibana M, Shinzawa N, Baba M, Torii M, Culleton R, Ishino T
articles.details: This study examined PyCryPH2, a novel crystalloid protein in Plasmodium ookinetes. Gene disruption resulted in irregular crystalloid microstructure. While pycryph2-deficient parasites developed morphologically normal sporozoites, they had significantly reduced ability to invade mosquito salivary glands and infect mouse liver, indicating the protein's importance for developing functional, infectious sporozoites.

articles.headline: Presumptive malaria treatment widespread despite diagnostic availability
articles.title_original: Diagnostic and therapeutic practices for paediatric malaria: Gap between guidelines and clinical reality in south-east Gabon.
articles.authors: Biteghe-Bi-Essone JC, Imboumy-Limoukou RK, Noronha ID, Moukodoum DN, Ontoua SS, Atiga N, Kouna LC, Okouga AP, Lekana-Douki JB, Oyegue-Liabagui LS
articles.details: A prospective observational study of 3,195 children with fever found 41.6% tested positive by thick blood smear, with 21.4% of positives having severe malaria. Despite negative smears, 86.7% of patients received presumptive diagnosis and 98.8% were treated with antimalarials. Asthenia (aOR=1.41, 95% CI 1.01–2.00), headache (aOR=1.71, 95% CI 1.02–3.03), and nausea-vomiting (aOR=1.95, 95% CI 1.20–3.31) were significantly associated with presumptive diagnosis.

articles.headline: ITN/LLIN use shows no significant protection against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Impact of Insecticide-Treated Nets on Plasmodium falciparum Infection Rates: A Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Karaca Biçakçi N, Çalmaz A, Ayyildiz Akin M, Tan CS, Barasarathi J, Oyinloye BE, Umidjon A, Egamberdiyev K, Avazmetova I
articles.details: Meta-analysis of six studies (7,466 participants) examining ITN/LLIN use and P. falciparum infection yielded pooled OR=0.67 (95% CI 0.42–1.07; p=0.078, I²=79.8%), not statistically significant. Adjusted meta-analysis of nine studies produced aOR=0.88 (95% CI 0.42–1.86; p=0.71, I²=88.7%). The analysis found no conclusive evidence of association between ITN/LLIN use and P. falciparum infection in contemporary epidemiological studies.

articles.headline: Three-dose IPTp compliance remains low during pregnancy
articles.title_original: Compliance with intermittent preventive malaria treatment in pregnancy at a tertiary hospital in Sierra Leone.
articles.authors: Lahai WK, Vandy AO, Prajitha KC, Fofanah BD, Kamau EM, Owusu H, Squire JS, Barrie HA, Kamara IF, Falama AM, Sesay N, Kamara ARY, Kanu F, Kanu MS, Kamara MM, Sesay MA, Koroma JA, Moses F, Lakoh S, Kenneh S, Grant LF
articles.details: Among 733 pregnant antenatal care attendees in 2024, only 62 (8%) received the recommended minimum of three IPTp doses; 406 (55%) received the first dose. Women with fewer than four antenatal contacts had substantially higher non-compliance risk (aOR=2.7, 95% CI 1.6–4.5) compared to those with ≥4 contacts. Low IPTp receipt was strongly associated with fewer antenatal care contacts.

articles.headline: Suboptimal artesunate dosing linked to severe malaria mortality
articles.title_original: Parenteral artesunate compliance and hospital outcomes in children under five with suspected severe malaria in Sierra Leone.
articles.authors: Kamara ARY, Kamara IF, Thekkur P, Falama AM, Sillah-Kanu M, Lahai WK, Fiona OO, George A, Fofanah BD, Sesay N, Tengbe SM, Farma-Grant L, Bah A, Thomas F, Tejan YS, Kanu JS, Lakoh S, Mustapha M, Zachariah R, Chandrasekharan Prathija K, Kenneh SM
articles.details: A cohort study of 735 children under five admitted with suspected severe malaria in 2024 found 650 (88%) received all three recommended parenteral artesunate doses; however, only 420 (57%) received correct weight-based dosing. Among those admitted, 87% were discharged and 10% had unfavorable outcomes including 59 deaths. Underweight status, multiple convulsions, and failure to receive correct artesunate dosing were associated with unfavorable hospital exit outcomes.

articles.headline: Spatial malaria hotspots identified with persistent transmission clusters
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria hotspots through spatial and spatio-temporal analysis in Sierra Leone, 2021-2024.
articles.authors: Falama AM, Omoniwa O, Kanu MS, Lahai WK, Kamara ARY, Ameh G, Kamara IF, Fofanah BD, Kenneh S, Kamara MN, Sesay MA, Sesay N, Kanu F, Alwani AA, Thekkur P, Zachariah R, Kanu MS, Lakoh S
articles.details: Spatial analysis of 7.4 million malaria cases reported in Sierra Leone (2021–2024) revealed chiefdom-level incidence ranging from 21.2 to >750 per 1,000 population. Significant spatial clustering was observed (Moran's I > 0; p < 0.01). Space-time analysis identified high-risk districts (relative risk 1.2–1.9; p < 0.01) and low-risk districts (RR 0.5–0.9; p < 0.01). No significant association was found between malaria incidence and rainfall.

articles.headline: Correction to P. falciparum genomic study in Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: Correction: Comprehensive genomic study of Plasmodium falciparum in Sierra Leone: genetic variation and resistance patterns.
articles.authors: Zhan XJ, Chen SB, Wang TY, Duan YW, Shen HM, Chen JH, Xie JJ
articles.details: Correction to a comprehensive genomic study examining genetic variation and resistance patterns in Plasmodium falciparum in Sierra Leone.

articles.headline: Vector ecology and serological exposure in Indonesia malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Anopheles ecology and serological evidence of malaria exposure in clinically malaria-free areas of Menoreh Hills Central Java, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Muh F, Qanita NG, Hitipeuw D, Saputra EY, Nuranindita R, Wusono AD, Natanegara S, Daud MM, Rahayu AA, Pratama DA, Putri FA, Syahada JH, Fitriana F, Jun H, Jeon BY, Cahyani NKD, Pandupuspitasari NS, Lee MR, Ju JW, Han JH
articles.details: A study examining Anopheles mosquito ecology, spatial distribution, and host-feeding behavior in the Menoreh Hills, Central Java, in a low-transmission region. Dominant vector species were present throughout the night and associated with livestock environments; productive breeding sites were shaped by habitat quality. Serological findings revealed marked differences in antibody responses between villages despite absence of clinical cases, indicating spatial variation in malaria exposure.

articles.headline: CRISPR screens reveal human genes supporting P. falciparum liver infection
articles.title_original: Cas9-expressing HC-04 hepatocytes facilitate CRISPR-based analysis of Plasmodium falciparum sporozoite-host interactions.
articles.authors: Verzier LH, Hesping E, Doerflinger M, Herold MJ, Boddey JA
articles.details: This study engineered HC-04 hepatocytes, the only known cell line supporting P. falciparum liver-stage development, to express Cas9-mCherry for CRISPR functional genomics. Disruption of ten human genes previously implicated in bacterial and viral infection confirmed utility of this platform. The study provides the basis for genome-wide CRISPR screens to uncover hepatocyte factors supporting P. falciparum sporozoite traversal, invasion, and intracellular development.

articles.headline: Comment on cryptic malaria cases in UK travelers
articles.title_original: Comment on Rapid Whole genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers provides insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.
articles.authors: Chen Q
articles.details: Comment on a study of whole-genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travelers, examining infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.

articles.headline: Malaria post-acute symptoms resolve faster than arboviral diseases
articles.title_original: Frequency and persistence of post-acute symptoms after chikungunya, dengue, Zika, and malaria in travellers: a prospective multi-centre study.
articles.authors: Hamer DH, Loreau JM, Huits R, Díaz-Menéndez M, Grobusch MP, Barkati S, Yansouni CP, Gobbi F, Bottieau E, Chen LH, Duvignaud A, Malvy D, Castelli F, Odolini S, De Pijper CA, Asgeirsson H, Moro L, Yamamoto K, Sánchez-Montalvá A, Zeana C, Kelly P, Piyaphanee W, Bourque DL, McCarthy A, Simon F, Gautret P, Mardon JB, Texier G, Javelle E
articles.details: A prospective multi-site study of 273 travelers with confirmed vector-borne diseases (35 chikungunya, 110 dengue, 19 Zika, 109 falciparum malaria) evaluated persistent symptoms through 18 months. Among malaria patients, 12/109 (11%) had persistent symptoms at month 3, resolving almost completely by that point. In contrast, chikungunya (69% persistent at M3) and dengue (25% at M3) showed prolonged symptoms. At month 12, only 1/109 (1%) of malaria patients remained symptomatic.

articles.headline: Subclinical malaria maintains transmission despite low parasite densities
articles.title_original: Mechanistic insights into subclinical Plasmodium infections: Unveiling the silent drivers of malaria transmission.
articles.authors: Ranjha R, Gupta A, Singh K, Sikka R, Anvikar AR, Gupta H, Bharti PK
articles.details: A review of subclinical malaria examines mechanisms sustaining asymptomatic infections that evade conventional diagnostics due to low parasite densities. Parasite adaptations including density thresholds, antigenic variation, cytoadhesion, multiplicity of infection, and drug resistance interact with host factors such as naturally acquired immunity, age, pregnancy, genetic polymorphisms, and epigenetic influences. Chronic subclinical infections are associated with anemia, impaired cognitive development in children, and adverse pregnancy outcomes.

articles.headline: Household-level insecticide mosaic IRS regimes in Zambia: feasibility and effectiveness
articles.title_original: Indoor residual spraying of distinct insecticide formulations in a household-scale micro-mosaic format: Feasibility and effectiveness for malaria vector control in rural Zambia.
articles.authors: Chinula D, Mumba K, Zulu R, Ndhlovu K, Kavishe DR, Hobbs NP, Kiware S, Reed T, Hamainza B, Killeen GF
articles.details: Entomological surveillance pilot in south-eastern Zambia (Luangwa district) comparing three IRS deployment regimes: DDT across all houses, clothianidin-deltamethrin across all houses, or alternating household application (micro-mosaic). CDC light trap monitoring captured mosquitoes monthly from 15 houses across 9 health facility catchment areas over 1 year, recording 1620 trap nights total. Anopheles vector densities were comparable across all three regimes (p ≥ 0.1420), with no differences in species composition. Household-level micro-mosaic IRS appears as effective as conventional macro-mosaic approaches over the short term.

articles.headline: Imported P. vivax malaria in New York City rises with migrant population
articles.title_original: An Increase in Imported Plasmodium vivax Malaria in New York City: Clinical and Demographic Trends Following Recent Migration.
articles.authors: Berto CG, Policarpio MA, Vargas-Pena C, Antwi M, Slavinski S, Abdool A, King RN, Kojaoghlanian T, Daily JP, Prasad N, Velasquez MR, Ridpath AD, Coyle CM
articles.details: Retrospective review of P. vivax cases reported to NYC Department of Health (2018-2024) with clinical data from 41 hospitalized patients. P. vivax cases increased from 6 of 206 (2.9%) in 2018 to 63 of 346 (18.2%) in 2023, with 43 of 250 (17.2%) in 2024. Of 41 H+H admissions, 40 (97.6%) were recent migrants primarily from South America and Asia. Severe malaria occurred in 10 (24.4%), with 5 (12.2%) requiring intensive care; relapses in 7 (17%), predominantly among those not receiving liver-stage therapy.

articles.headline: Severe P. falciparum malaria in third-trimester pregnancy: case and management
articles.title_original: Severe malaria in pregnancy in the United States: A case report and management considerations.
articles.authors: Reyes Lizaola S, Kumari S, Di Francesco L, Chedid J, Cay Martinez C
articles.details: Case report of a nulliparous patient at 36 weeks gestation with severe P. falciparum malaria (5.4% parasitemia) presenting with fever, headache, and abdominal discomfort following travel to Ghana. Initial treatment with artemether-lumefantrine escalated to artesunate upon confirmation of severe malaria. Patient developed spontaneous preterm prelabor rupture of membranes, thrombocytopenia, and preeclampsia with severe features, requiring induction of labor with vaginal delivery. Highlights diagnostic challenges, delayed species confirmation risks in pregnancy, and need for trimester-specific therapy guidance.

articles.headline: 4-amino-quinazoline sulfonamides inhibit P. falciparum asparagine tRNA synthetase potently
articles.title_original: Potent reaction hijacking inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum asparagine tRNA synthetase.
articles.authors: Ye X, Esherick LY, Ketprasit N, Narwal SK, Godoy LC, Boonyalai N, Dogovski C, Morton CJ, Rabie T, Famodimu MT, Tai CW, Yeo T, Le LHM, Leeming MG, Laureano De Souza M, Chenu E, Creek DJ, Delves MJ, Birkholtz LM, Duffy J, Lobb K, Durst G, Lee MCS, Fidock DA, Niles JC, Siegel MG, Tilley L, Xie SC
articles.details: Chemical series of 4-amino-quinazoline-based sulfonamides evaluated for antimalarial activity. Exemplars exhibit nanomolar potency against P. falciparum blood-stage cultures with up to 300-fold selectivity versus mammalian cell lines, with activity against transmissible stages and resistance to resistance development. Targeted mass spectrometry identified P. falciparum aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases as mechanism. Subtle structural changes switch the main target from cytoplasmic P. falciparum threonine synthetase (PfThrRS) to asparagine synthetase (PfAsnRS), associated with increased potency and selectivity.

articles.headline: Anopheles species diversity and zoonotic malaria infection prevalence Thailand
articles.title_original: Systematic review of Anopheles abundance and meta-analysis of nonhuman primate malaria infection in mosquitoes in Thailand.
articles.authors: Weerakoon SN, Hii J, Nararak J, Sukkanon C, Ngoen-Klan R, Arunyawat U, Maneeon P, Thongsahuan S, Vythilingam I, Manguin S, Chareonviriyaphap T
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 92 studies on Anopheles abundance, distribution, and zoonotic Plasmodium infection in Thailand (2013-2025). Nine Plasmodium species documented, with P. vivax and P. falciparum predominant nationwide; P. falciparum showed declining trend since 2012. Overall pooled mosquito infection prevalence for nonhuman primate malaria parasites was 0.01 (95% CI 0.00-0.03). Wild-caught An. sawadwongporni, An. minimus, and An. dirus incriminated as zoonotic vectors with 0.2% pooled infection prevalence.

articles.headline: Hybrid deep learning CNN diagnoses malaria from thin blood smear images
articles.title_original: A hybrid machine learning approach for automated malaria diagnosis from thin blood smear images.
articles.authors: Siddiqui GA, Bera A, Lhila A, Rajput AS, Nitika N, Bharti PK, Das A, Mahapatra T
articles.details: Hybrid Inception-v3 convolutional neural network trained on 15,938 preprocessed thin blood smear images from ICMR-NIMR using Otsu thresholding for segmentation and automated cell classification. Model achieved training accuracy 98.9%, sensitivity 97.3%, specificity 99.9%, with high F1 score and AUC. Performance was comparable to or superior to state-of-the-art machine learning classifiers and conventional microscopy-based examination. Demonstrates potential to reduce reliance on expert microscopists in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: P. falciparum MSP-2 genetic polymorphism and multiplicity of infection Chad
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum MSP-2 (3D7/IC) and multiplicity of infection in N'Djamena, Chad.
articles.authors: Cedric Y, Diomède N, Hervé MM, Pacome TK, Abel D, Ngaryedji T, Saleh B, Hu H, Christelle Nadia NA
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 217 participants in N'Djamena conducted July-September 2024. P. falciparum prevalence was 23.5%; molecular frequency of msp2 3D7/IC allelic family was 94.11% with observed genetic polymorphism. Single-clone infections most frequent (64.65%), followed by double infections (33.33%) and triple infections (2.08%). Phylogenetic analysis revealed eight clades with varied distributions. Study demonstrates substantial genetic diversity and multiplicity of infections within the msp-2 3D7/IC allelic family, reflecting ongoing malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Novel Plasmodium borealis species identified in Cory's Shearwater seabird Brazil
articles.title_original: A new Plasmodium Marchiafava & Celli, 1885 (Apicomplexa: Haemosporida) species in Cory's Shearwater (Calonectris borealis) [Cory]) (Aves: Procellariiformes) stranded in a coastal area in Brazil.
articles.authors: Dos Anjos CC, Damato BA, Ferioli RB, Sánchez-Sarmiento AM, da Mata AR, Barbosa CB, Neto HG, Chagas CRF, Valkiūnas G, Kirchgatter K
articles.details: Case report of a stranded adult male Cory's Shearwater (Calonectris borealis) found on northern coast of São Paulo, Brazil. Blood smears revealed parasitemia ranging from 0.04% to 2.09%; PCR of cytochrome b gene showed 100% similarity to Plasmodium lineage pLK06. Phylogenetic analysis placed parasite within Novyella clade with morphological features related to P. vaughani. Necropsy revealed hepatosplenomegaly; histopathology confirmed hepatitis and pulmonary hemorrhage. First haemosporidian infection in this seabird and first pLK06 identification in Procellariiformes (Plasmodium borealis sp. nov.).

articles.headline: Malaria and other febrile illnesses in southern Angola: outcomes and aetiology
articles.title_original: Age-Related and Seasonal Variation in Malaria and Other Causes of Fever and Their Association With Clinical Outcomes in Southern Angola: A Hospital-Based Study.
articles.authors: Gonçalves HEM, Antunes ML, Seixas J, Ferreira MU
articles.details: Hospital-based cross-sectional study in Lubango city enrolling 849 febrile patients (ages 2 months–94 years) in two tertiary hospitals during September-October 2023 and May-June 2024. 67.6% tested negative for malaria; leading nonmalarial causes were upper respiratory infections (13.0%), lower respiratory infections (19.5%), and gastrointestinal infections (16.7%). Malaria associated with hospitalization (OR 3.20, p<0.0001) and mortality (OR 1.90, p<0.0001); lower respiratory infections with hospitalization (OR 5.17); CNS infections with mortality (OR 6.30).

articles.headline: wAnD Wolbachia strain in An. demeilloni reduces Plasmodium sporozoite levels
articles.title_original: The presence of the wAnD strain of Wolbachia is correlated with lower levels of Plasmodium sporozoites and a less diverse microbiome in wild Anopheles demeilloni mosquito cephalothoraxes.
articles.authors: Sougoufara S, Bandibabone J, Chatterley L, Hughes I, Taberes CM, Ball J, Palliaser T, Dhokiya V, Heinz E, Hughes G, Walker T
articles.details: Analysis of wild-caught An. demeilloni mosquitoes from Sud Kivu region of Democratic Republic of Congo (collected 2021 and 2024) examining correlation between wAnD Wolbachia presence and P. falciparum sporozoites using quantitative PCR and 16S rRNA sequencing. Overall sporozoite prevalence was 1.3% (9 of 704); P. falciparum only detected in Wolbachia-negative cephalothoraxes. Wolbachia-positive abdomens associated with lower sporozoite rates versus Wolbachia-negative abdomens (p=0.0329). Cephalothorax microbiome composition differed significantly between Wolbachia-positive and Wolbachia-negative mosquitoes (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Epidemiology of malaria and leishmaniasis in Thailand: systematic review 2004–2025
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria and leishmaniasis in Thailand (2004-2025): A systematic review.
articles.authors: Apajamjarut W, Siriwongsanon N, Tonpon P, Boonlertworakul L, Joosiriwong R, Soe BK, Kaewmee S, Saingamsook J, Walton C, Lee SJ, Lin C, Dimopoulos G, Jariyapan N
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Systematic review synthesizing 57 studies (36 on malaria, 21 on leishmaniasis) on epidemiology and shared ecological characteristics in Thailand. Nine Plasmodium species documented, including P. vivax and P. falciparum (predominant nationwide) and emerging zoonotic parasites P. knowlesi and P. cynomolgi. P. falciparum showed declining trend since 2012. Six Leishmania species identified. Seven provinces reported both diseases, associated with shared forest-related occupational exposure and population mobility. Integrated surveillance and control strategies recommended.

articles.headline: Plasma IL-6 and HO-1 biomarkers in malaria-endemic hospital population Nigeria
articles.title_original: Plasma haem oxygenase-1 and interleukin-6 as adjunct host biomarkers associated with malaria.
articles.authors: Wakai T, Kintung I, Ogundimu T, Chinedu S, Afolabi I
articles.details: Cross-sectional study at hospital in Nigeria with 304 participants (152 malaria microscopy-positive, 152 negative) and 40 with plasma samples for ELISA biomarker analysis. Malaria-positive participants had lower hemoglobin, hematocrit, red-cell count, and platelets, with higher white-cell counts (all p<0.001). IL-6 median 141.28 pg/mL in positive versus 106.58 in negative (p=0.027); HO-1 230.98 versus 193.73 ng/mL (p=0.131, non-significant). IL-6 correlated positively with parasitemia (rho=0.387, p=0.015) and temperature (rho=0.432, p=0.006), negatively with hemoglobin (rho=–0.417, p=0.008). ROC analysis: IL-6 AUC 0.709; HO-1 AUC 0.641.

articles.headline: Natural and synthetic repellents against malaria vectors in Thailand
articles.title_original: Do natural or synthetic excito-repellents work better? A study on coastal malaria vector Anopheles epiroticus in Ko Chang, Thailand.
articles.authors: Sukkanon C, Jabjai S, Ritthison W, Chareonviriyaphap T, Nararak J
articles.details: Laboratory and field strains of Anopheles epiroticus tested with four synthetic and two natural repellents using excito-repellency assay. Laboratory strain showed highest escape responses to deltamethrin (42%) and permethrin (35%) in contact trials; citronella oil (>50% at 5% v/v) in non-contact. Field strain responded best to DEET (64%) and deltamethrin (60%) in contact trials; citronella and vetiver oils (~14%) minimally effective non-contact. Findings indicate behavioral variation between laboratory and field strains requires consideration in vector control design.

articles.headline: Insecticide resistance effects on malaria vector competence
articles.title_original: Insecticide resistance and malaria transmission: divergent effects on Anopheles vector competence.
articles.authors: Adeboye SO, Nwinyi OC, Ayanda OI
articles.details: Review examining the relationship between insecticide resistance mechanisms (target-site mutation, metabolic resistance, cuticular thickening, behavioral resistance) in Anopheles and vector competence for Plasmodium transmission. Some studies report impaired parasite development in resistant mosquitoes; others show enhanced competence. Authors argue that integration of omics-based approaches embracing ecological reality is crucial for understanding contradictions and accurately predicting epidemiological implications of insecticide resistance on malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid resistance variation in Anopheles funestus across Kenya
articles.title_original: Signature of resistance gene evolution and pyrethroid resistance escalation in the major malaria vector Anopheles funestus across Kenyan malaria-endemic regions separated by the Rift Valley.
articles.authors: Tchouassi DP, Mustapha AM, Rotich G, Milugo TK, Musimbi B, Oruni A, Tagne CSD, Kamau L, Gadji M, Tchouakui M, Torto B, Wondji CS
articles.details: Genotyping of Anopheles funestus populations in western Kenya, coastal, and Kerio Valley regions for resistance markers (4.3kb-SV, G454A-Cyp9k1, L119F-GSTe2). 4.3kb-SV and G454A-Cyp9k1 nearly fixed in western Kenya but declined toward coast and Kerio Valley; L119F-GSTe2 increased west-KV-coast gradient. Plasmodium infection lower in L119F-GSTe2-RR than RS genotype (OR 0.2, P=0.046); mosquitoes with 4.3kb R allele had higher infection rates (OR 5.7, P=0.049). High pyrethroid resistance observed, with intensity greater in Kerio Valley. All populations remained susceptible to bendiocarb, pirimiphos-methyl, clothianidin, and chlorfenapyr.

articles.headline: PfMyoF myosin mediates membrane trafficking in Plasmodium falciparum
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum PfMyoF contains a Rab-like tail domain and associates with perinuclear membrane trafficking proteins.
articles.authors: Holmes AJ, Ilani P, Batters C, Kemp A, Aniweh Y, Amenga-Etego LN, Awandare GA, Rayner JC, Buss F
articles.details: Characterization of PfMyoF, a class XXII myosin in Plasmodium falciparum, shows it is a plus-end-directed processive motor with long neck region binding up to six calmodulin homologue (PfCaM) copies. PfMyoF tail contains predicted WD40 and Rab-like domains not identified in other eukaryotic myosins. Pull-down experiments identify interactions with trafficking proteins including vesicle marker PfRab18. Transient knockdown using glmS ribozyme system impairs parasite growth, suggesting important function during asexual replication.

articles.headline: Cross-border P. vivax transmission in South Asian pre-elimination region
articles.title_original: Genetic evidence of cross-border Plasmodium vivax spread in a malaria pre-elimination region of South Asia.
articles.authors: Rai A, Sutanto E, Ghimire P, Wangchuk S, Drukpa T, Alam MS, Ghanchi N, Rahim AG, Rumaseb A, Trimarsanto H, Hoon KS, Adhikari N, Adhikari S, Banjara MR, Rijal KR, Nepal R, Regmi RS, Qurashi B, Zaidi SE, Beg MA, Ley B, Price RN, Thriemer K, Auburn S
articles.details: High-resolution microhaplotype genotyping using 93-microhaplotype vivaxGEN panel on Plasmodium vivax samples from Bangladesh (n=35), Bhutan (n=27), Nepal (n=19), and comparative data from Afghanistan (n=159), India (n=24), Pakistan (n=213) collected 2013-2024. High-complexity infections observed in 27% of Afghan, 16% of Pakistani, and 29% of Bangladeshi isolates; low in Bhutan (3.7%) and Nepal (5.3%). Identity-by-descent analysis revealed three genetic clusters and two sub-populations in Bhutan separating local from cross-border imported cases.

articles.headline: EBV infection associated with severe malaria in young infants
articles.title_original: Infants under 24 months with severe malaria have a serological profile of active infection with Epstein Barr Virus.
articles.authors: Cheng WT, Fogang B, Jain A, Davies DH, Felgner PL, Eboumbou C, Koki PN, Speck SH, Joyner CJ, Ayong LS, Lamb TJ
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 195 infants aged 6-24 months in Cameroon with parasitemia detected by microscopy or RDT. 24% serologically positive for active Epstein Barr Virus infection, with highest proportion of severe malaria cases in EBV-infected children. EBV-infected children showed elevated magnitude and breadth of anti-Plasmodium falciparum antibody response with increased invasion-blocking capacity, though circulating parasitemia in vivo was similar. Primary EBV infection identified as potential risk factor for severe P. falciparum malaria in young children.

articles.headline: Fecal sampling detects P. knowlesi in wild macaque populations
articles.title_original: Tracking Plasmodium knowlesi Through Fecal DNA for Monitoring Zoonotic Transmission in Wild Macaques Across Southeast and South Asia.
articles.authors: Wannigama DL, Amarasiri M, Phattharapornjaroen P, Hurst C, Modchang C, Cynthia B, Miyanaga K, Cui L, Fernandez S, Melhem NM, Luk-In S, Singer AC, Ragupathi NKD, Htun TS, Sei K, Ngamwongsatit N, Hui NS, Shimotai Y, Ounjai P, Kanthawee P, Tacharoenmuang R, Kauba A, Eang C, Romone L, Dharne M, de Araujo JC, Ndatuwong LG, Werawatte WKCP, Chanthasiri T, Zhao J, Mori H, Besa JJV, Kurt Ö, Kanjanabuch T, Zahraei-Ramazani AR, Higgins PG, Aoyagi T, Kicic A, Trowsdale S, Hongsing P, Yang X, Wang Y, Khatib A, Sano D, Shibuya K, Abe S, Hamamoto H
articles.details: Noninvasive surveillance conducted on 4,752 fecal samples from wild macaques across 9 endemic countries. Plasmodium knowlesi DNA detected in 390 samples (8.2%), with positivity rates ranging 1.4-18.4%. First field-based evidence that P. knowlesi DNA in fecal samples shed by macaques can be detected under natural conditions. Findings validate fecal sampling as practical and scalable tool for tracking zoonotic malaria and support integration into forest-runoff and wastewater surveillance systems for early pathogen detection.

articles.headline: DON Phase 1 trial in Malawian adults with malaria
articles.title_original: Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of 6-Diazo-5-Oxo-L-Norleucine in Malawian Adults With and Without Malaria: A Phase 1 Dose-Escalation Clinical Trial.
articles.authors: Riggle BA, Chamzas A, Gopalakrishnan M, Nyirenda OM, Nampota-Nkomba N, Mallewa JE, Masonga R, Manyozo C, Zuze K, Liomba AM, Alt J, Rais R, Slusher B, Moodley N, Miller LH, Pierce SK, O'Brien NF, Laurens MB, Postels DG
