articles.details: Open-label phase 1 dose-escalation trial of single-dose intravenous 6-diazo-5-oxo-L-norleucine (DON) in healthy adults and adults with uncomplicated malaria in Blantyre, Malawi. 40 healthy and 38 malaria-infected adults received DON at 0.1, 1.0, 5.0, or 10 mg/kg. Transient asymptomatic creatinine elevation occurred 12 hours post-infusion in 18% of participants. Nausea and vomiting uncommon at ≤1 mg/kg but more frequent at ≥5 mg/kg; all DON-related adverse events resolved rapidly without sequelae. Terminal half-life 1.7-4.1 hours. Results provide dosing guidance for testing DON as adjunctive therapy in cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Urbanization effects on avian malaria and cloacal microbiome
articles.title_original: Interactions between urbanization, malaria infection and avian cloacal microbiome.
articles.authors: Garrigós M, Jiménez-Peñuela J, Saavedra I, Veiga J, García-López MJ, Garrido M, Ruiz-López MJ, Figuerola J, Moreno-Indias I, Martínez-de la Puente J
articles.details: Study of house sparrows examining association between habitat urbanization, Plasmodium/Haemoproteus infection, and cloacal bacterial microbiome composition. Urban birds showed lower parasite prevalence than natural and rural birds. In natural habitats, infected birds exhibited nearly-significant increase in bacterial richness and significant differences in taxa relative abundance compared to uninfected birds; in rural and urban habitats, infection status not associated with microbiome parameters. Habitat type modulates relationship between parasite infection and bacterial microbiome composition.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic transmission and ovale malaria in Tanzanian reservoir
articles.title_original: Uneven Plasmodium falciparum Transmission and Cryptic ovale Transmission From the Asymptomatic Reservoir in Bagamoyo, Tanzania.
articles.authors: Lin JT, Mathias DK, Yang G, Loya M, Muller MS, Basham C, Nyasembe V, Amagai K, Rutha I, Gaither C, Nyange M, Said H, Chhetri SB, Swinehart B, Lin FC, Dinglasan RR, Juliano JJ, Tarimo B, Ngasala B
articles.details: Direct skin feeding assays conducted on 491 parasitemic individuals >5 years in Bagamoyo, Tanzania with median parasite density 5.1 parasites/μL. 22% of participants were infectious to mosquitoes; RDT-positive individuals infected roughly twice as many mosquitoes compared to RDT-negative/PCR-positive persons. Up to 21% of infectious carriers were PCR-negative at time of feeding. Overall, 9.1% of mosquitoes fed on infectious carriers were parasite-positive; top 16 transmitters (3% of cohort) infected 57% of infected mosquitoes. RDT-positive school-age children (6-15 years, 27% of cohort) contributed 58% of infected mosquitoes. Plasmodium ovale detected in 44% of oocyst-positive midguts.

articles.headline: Pfs230 D7-D10 DNA vaccine induces transmission-blocking antibodies
articles.title_original: Pfs230 Domains 7-10 Encoded by DNA Vaccines Induce Potent Complement-Independent Transmission-Blocking Antibody Against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Cao Y, Hayashi CTH, Tripathi AK, Takashima E, Tsuboi T, Harju TB, Kumar N
articles.details: DNA vaccines encoding Pfs230 domains D7-D10 (D7D10) evaluated in BALB/c mice. D7D10 DNA vaccines elicited potent domain-specific antibody responses. Anti-D7D10 IgGs showed strong dose-dependent transmission-reducing activity (TRA) in standard membrane feeding assays with Plasmodium falciparum gametocytes, independent of active complement. Western blot analysis identified domain 7 (D7) as primary antibody target. DNA vaccines encoding D7 and D7D8 similarly induced antibodies with strong TRA, identifying Pfs230-D7D10, particularly D7, as novel P. falciparum transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Fever augments ring-stage parasite adhesion to brain endothelium
articles.title_original: Febrile Temperature Augments Ring-stage Plasmodium falciparum Adhesion to Brain Endothelial Cells.
articles.authors: Joof F, Hu R, Seydel KB, Cohee LM, Zheng Y, Smith JD
articles.details: Study of ring-stage Plasmodium falciparum-infected erythrocyte sequestration in brain microvasculature under febrile conditions. Febrile exposure increased transcript levels of exported parasite genes involved in PfEMP1 trafficking and accelerated PfEMP1 surface display in ring-stage infected erythrocytes, resulting in 2-fold increase in binding in 3D human brain microvessel model. Fever also enhanced binding of uninfected erythrocytes in brain microvessels. Findings demonstrate complex interplay between fever and parasite biomass in pathogenesis of cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Bivalent VLP vaccine combining SPECT1 and CSP shows protective efficacy
articles.title_original: Bivalent virus-like particles expressing SPECT1 and CSP trigger pre-erythrocytic malaria immunity and protect against transgenic Plasmodium falciparum sporozoite challenge in mice.
articles.authors: Turan G, Mukhopadhyay E, Truby A, Fedorova K, Peralta Alvarez MP, Alharbi NK, Hill AVS, Salman AM
articles.details: Engineered HBsAg virus-like particles expressing PfSPECT-1 combined with CSP NANP repeats and C-terminal region. One bivalent candidate (N4) with Matrix-M adjuvant provided similar efficacy to R21 in BALB/c mice; addition of PfSPECT-1 to another candidate (N2) significantly reduced efficacy. Bivalent VLPs induced IgG responses against both NANP and PfSPECT-1 epitopes; NANP-specific IgG correlated with reduced blood-stage parasite load.

articles.headline: Apical polar ring proteins essential for Plasmodium falciparum cell invasion
articles.title_original: The apical polar ring is essential during the blood stage of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Gurung P, Back PS, Ali I, Hernandez GM, Dvorin JD
articles.details: Identified PfAPR3 as an apical polar ring-resident protein essential for P. falciparum asexual replication. Conditional knockout of PfAPR3 blocked tight junction formation and completely prevented red blood cell invasion. Identified three additional APR proteins (PfAPR4, PfCHAKRA, PfAPR5); PfCHAKRA was critical for cytoskeletal network organization. Ultrastructure expansion microscopy revealed PfCHAKRA at basal APR through mid-schizogony and dual apical-basal localization of PfAPR3 during schizogony.

articles.headline: Environmental factors and geospatial analysis identify malaria transmission risk zones
articles.title_original: Environmental factors influencing mosquito breeding and malaria transmission using geospatial technologies in the Wabi Shebele sub-basin, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Merga BB, Tabor KW, Geletu MH, Zerihun E, Gemeda EA, Moisa MB, Gemeda DO
articles.details: Geospatial analysis of seven environmental factors in Wabi Shebele sub-basin, Ethiopia. High land surface temperature (bare land 33.66°C, grassland 31.76°C, agricultural land 31.42°C), low elevation, high soil moisture, and moderate-high rainfall were primary factors facilitating malaria distribution. Agricultural land (24.78%) provided ideal mosquito breeding sites. Forested areas with lower LST (26.98°C) and high vegetation index showed low malaria risk.

articles.headline: Avian malaria haemosporidian lineages in cerulean warbler population documented
articles.title_original: EFFECTS OF AVIAN MALARIA IN A POPULATION OF CERULEAN WARBLER (SETOPHAGA CERULEA) WITH FIRST DOCUMENTATION OF HAEMOSPORIDIAN LINEAGES INFECTING THE SPECIES.
articles.authors: Grudens JB, Islam K
articles.details: Study of 51 cerulean warblers over 2 years using microscopy and molecular methods. Parasitemia was low (<1% infected cells) for most individuals. Population prevalence was 31.4% across both years. Documented 11 unique haemosporidian lineages (genera Haemoproteus and Plasmodium), including 2 novel lineages. Mean body condition of Plasmodium-infected individuals was higher than uninfected, suggesting possible survivorship bias.

articles.headline: Multiplexed amplicon sequencing panels enable Plasmodium vivax genomic surveillance
articles.title_original: PvGTSeq and PvCRiSP: Two amplicon-based targeted sequencing panels for Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Manrique-Valverde PC, Hasund CM, Kelley KA, Amaya-Romero JE, Arévalo-Herrera M, Brosula R, Calzada JE, Chenet SM, Corredor V, Early AM, Fontecha G, Forero-Peña DA, Herrera S, Lana JT, Laws M, Niles-Robin R, Obaldia N, Santamaria AM, Schwabl P, Auburn S, Neafsey DE
articles.details: Developed PvGTSeq (249 amplicons for drug-resistance and population-structure genes) and PvCRiSP (4 amplicons for complexity-of-infection and clonal transmission). Both achieve ≥75% amplicon recovery at parasitemias ≥5 parasites/μL. PvGTSeq showed high sequencing accuracy (error rate 3.85e-4% to 2.87e-3%). Panels validated across Colombia, Guyana, Honduras, Panama, Venezuela and assessed across 16 countries. Both panels discriminate samples and assign geographic origin to imported cases.

articles.headline: CSP epitope-specific antibodies correlate with RTS,S vaccine protection in children
articles.title_original: Antibody fine specificity correlates with protection from malaria for the RTS,S vaccine in young African children: A post hoc analysis of a phase IIb randomised controlled trial.
articles.authors: Hysa A, Opi DH, Waterhouse J, Chishimba S, Horton JL, Kingston N, Netter HJ, Wetzel D, Piontek M, Feng G, Sacarlal J, Dobaño C, Kurtovic L, Beeson JG
articles.details: Post hoc analysis of RTS,S phase IIb trial in children 1-4 years old (n=735). Children with high IgG responses to NANP2 (NANPNANP) and J1 epitope (KQPADGNPDPNANPN) had significantly reduced clinical malaria risk compared to low responders (NANP2: adjusted HR 0.838, 95% CI 0.716-0.981, p=0.028; J1: adjusted HR 0.718, 95% CI 0.611-0.844, p<0.001). High responses associated with increased Fc-mediated functional activities.

articles.headline: Economic burden of malaria in Brazilian Amazon quantified at $181.9 million
articles.title_original: Economic burden of malaria in the Brazilian Amazon from a societal perspective.
articles.authors: Andrade MV, Noronha KVMS, Guedes GR, Carvalho LR, Bracarense H, Julião NA, Souza A, Diniz BC, Silva VA, Motta-Santos AS, Peterka C, Castro MC
articles.details: Household survey of 1,131 individuals with malaria episodes between January 2019 and May 2022 across 9 municipalities in 5 Brazilian Amazon states. Total economic burden estimated at approximately US$181.9 million PPP-USD in 2019. Public health system (SUS) bore 72.4% of costs, primarily for control and prevention. Household burden predominantly driven by indirect and mortality costs and health-related quality-of-life losses. States of Amazonas, Pará, and Roraima bore highest total burden.

articles.headline: Activated platelets implicated in cerebral malaria pathogenesis mechanism
articles.title_original: The role of activated platelets in cerebral malaria pathogenesis.
articles.authors: Schlesinger M
articles.details: Study investigating the role of activated platelets in cerebral malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Generative models reveal population structure of malaria vector Anopheles gambiae
articles.title_original: On the use of generative models for demographic inference in malaria vectors from genomic data.
articles.authors: Eneli AA, Siu PC, Perez MF, Burt A, Fumagalli M, Mathieson S
articles.details: Applied generative deep learning algorithm to infer evolutionary history of Anopheles gambiae populations from genomic data in Guinea and Burkina Faso. Model selection demonstrated migration-based evolutionary model fit the population pair better than model without post-split migration. Method accurately captured population genetic differentiation. Demonstrates machine learning and generative models can reveal population size changes, migration patterns, and adaptation in malaria vectors to inform control interventions.

articles.headline: Deformable gametocytes display immediate infectivity upon circulation release
articles.title_original: Defining the signature of deformable and infectious Plasmodium falciparum gametocytes.
articles.authors: Ravel D, Rijpma S, Beraldi D, Graumans W, Meerstein-Kessel L, Crouch K, Passecker A, van Gemert GJ, Blanken SL, Arinaitwe E, Rianne S, Ngotho P, Neafsey D, Voss T, Huijnen M, Bousema T, Marti M
articles.details: Combined phenotypic assays and transcriptional analysis of transition from immature to mature Plasmodium falciparum gametocytes. Used microsphere filtration to assess deformability and exflagellation/mosquito-feeding assays to assess infectivity. Found high similarity in gene expression between deformable and infectious gametocytes but major differences from input gametocytes. Results indicate deformable gametocytes are immediately infectious upon release from bone marrow. Identified comprehensive set of infectivity markers for tracking gametocyte development.

articles.headline: Ruthenium-atovaquone complexes block transmission of sexual malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Ruthenium Complexes of Atovaquone Acting on Multiple Stages of the Plasmodium Life Cycle.
articles.authors: Fabbri C, Marcon PHS, Santiago AS, Sousa CC, Quadros HC, de Sena Lamar Nunes L, de Menezes DDM, Santana RAG, Justiniano SCB, D'Alessandro S, Basilico N, Moreira DRM, de Araujo-Neto JH, Lopes SCP
articles.details: Synthesized and evaluated ruthenium complexes of atovaquone (ATV). Structure-activity relationships identified importance of Ru(III) versus Ru(II) oxidation state and hydrophilic/lipophilic coligands. Complexes demonstrated broader activity against both asexual and sexual P. falciparum parasite stages than ATV with faster antiplasmodial action. Complexes blocked transmission from humans to insects at both gametocyte and oocyst stages; ATV solely blocked oocysts. Ruthenium content accumulated gradually and selectively in parasite cells.

articles.headline: Platelet mTOR pathway drives cerebral malaria neuropathogenesis via heme signaling
articles.title_original: Heme-induced ITAM signaling exacerbates malaria-associated neuropathogenesis through activation of platelet mTOR.
articles.authors: Portier I, Denorme F, Tolley ND, Kosaka Y, Andrianova I, Sigala PA, Lamb TJ, Zimmerman GA, Castro-Faria-Neto HC, Morrell CN, Rondina MT, Campbell RA
articles.details: Examined mTOR pathway in platelets using P. berghei ANKA-infected mice. Platelet-specific mTOR knockout mice had significantly increased survival in experimental cerebral malaria independent of parasitemia and thrombocytopenia. mTOR knockout mice showed reduced platelet brain deposition, improved cerebral blood flow, and reduced brain vascular permeability. Plasma heme correlated with platelet brain accumulation in wildtype but not knockout mice. Heme activated platelet mTOR through ITAM signaling predominantly via CLEC-2.

articles.headline: Cumulative P. falciparum exposure does not shorten telomeres
articles.title_original: Cumulative Plasmodium falciparum infections do not drive long-term telomere shortening in Kenyan children.
articles.authors: Miglar A, Amadi D, Grannas D, Widman L, Mwacharo J, Musyoki J, Kai O, Bejon P, Maccharia A, Williams TN, Färnert A, Asghar M, Ndungu FM
articles.details: Cross-sectional study measured telomere length by qPCR in 218 children from endemic and 90 from non-endemic areas in 2007, 2010, and 2013. Repeated asymptomatic and symptomatic P. falciparum infections were not associated with telomere shortening (p>0.05). Asymptomatic parasitaemia showed marginal positive change in telomere length of 0.22 kb (95% CI 0.02–0.42, p=0.03), possibly reflecting suppressed cellular activation in asymptomatic infections.

articles.headline: Merozoite physiology: coordinating invasion mechanisms
articles.title_original: Physiology of invasive Plasmodium merozoites.
articles.authors: Siddiqui MA
articles.details: Review of Plasmodium merozoite invasion physiology, examining how parasites coordinate motility, sense environmental signals (ions, red blood cell contact), and activate secretion of specialized organelles. The review describes the precise biological responses and gliding motility mechanisms that enable successful entry into host cells and identifies potential strategies for malaria control and intervention.

articles.headline: P. vivax culture selectively enriches clones despite similar gene expression
articles.title_original: Stage-aware transcriptomics reveals selective haplotype persistence in short-term ex vivo cultured Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Abagero B, Dumetz F, Ford CT, Tolosa T, Tesefay D, Lukas B, Shenkutie T, Popovici J, Yewhalaw D, Serre D, Lo E
articles.details: Transcriptomic analysis of paired in vivo and short-term ex vivo cultured P. vivax isolates from Ethiopia using stage-aware analysis and PvMSP1 haplotyping. Short-term culture selectively depleted specific haplotypes (clonal bottleneck) and reduced multiplicity of infection while preserving overall gene expression patterns after stage adjustment. Late asexual stages were enriched without major culture-induced transcriptional responses.

articles.headline: FHR-1 deficiency linked to severe malarial anemia in children
articles.title_original: Factor H-related protein 1 deficiency and risk of severe falciparum malaria in Ghanaian children: a cohort analysis.
articles.authors: Byrd AU, Wahler L, Thye T, Reiss T, Bachmann A, Pradel G
articles.details: Cohort analysis of 500 Ghanaian children found approximately one-third were Factor H-related protein 1 (FHR-1) deficient. Logistic regression revealed no association between FHR-1 deficiency and risk of severe malaria overall. However, FHR-1 deficiency was overrepresented among children presenting with anemia and multiple syndromes and correlated with markedly lower hemoglobin levels.

articles.headline: Paper-based lysis device enables malaria diagnostics in low-resource settings
articles.title_original: Creating an improved workflow for paper-based malaria diagnostics by integrating total lysis of whole blood.
articles.authors: Prat-Trunas J, Code A, Avalos-Padilla Y, Fernández-Busquets X, Mace CR, Baldrich E
articles.details: Researchers developed a three-dimensional paper-based lysis device fabricated by laser cutting or wax-printing that achieved 100% red blood cell lysis independent of sample composition. Lysed samples enabled malaria biomarker detection comparable to standard liquid lysis procedures, with antigen stability exceeding 1 week at room temperature, facilitating delayed laboratory testing.

articles.headline: Artemisinin outperforms iron chelators through digestive vacuole mechanism
articles.title_original: Why are iron chelators not as effective as artemisinin in killing malaria parasites?
articles.authors: Sun J, Fang C, Si W, Qin X, Wang F, Li Y, Sun J
articles.details: In vitro and in vivo study compared artemisinin derivatives (dihydroartemisinin, artemether) with iron chelator desferrioxamine using single-cell RNA sequencing and transmission electron microscopy. Artemisinin upregulated iron utilization genes and induced ribosome/protein translation pathways while desferrioxamine showed minimal response. Artemisinin reduced parasites more potently due to digestive vacuole accumulation and heme disruption; artemether + desferrioxamine combination was less effective than artemether alone.

articles.headline: Febrile heat accelerates PfEMP1 trafficking, increasing cytoadhesion
articles.title_original: Physiological febrile heat stress increases cytoadhesion through increased protein trafficking of Plasmodium falciparum surface proteins into the red blood cell.
articles.authors: Jones D, Belda H, Broncel M, Fuchs G, Anaguano D, Nofal SD, Treeck M
articles.details: In vitro study showed physiologically relevant febrile heat stress (39°C) enhanced cytoadhesion through increased trafficking of virulence factor PfEMP1 to infected red blood cell surfaces across four clinical isolates of diverse geographic origin. Using phosphoproteomics and proximity labeling, elevated temperature accelerated export of proteins with transmembrane domains without changing overall expression or developmental progression, suggesting fever increases severity through earlier sequestration.

articles.headline: Dual-targeting pyrophosphatases essential for parasite survival
articles.title_original: Pyrophosphate homeostasis in multiple subcellular compartments is essential in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Nwankwo I, Ke H
articles.details: Study identified two soluble pyrophosphatase isoforms (PfsPPase1 and PfsPPase2) in P. falciparum from a single genetic locus with distinct localizations: PfsPPase1 in the cytoplasm, and PfsPPase2 in the mitochondrion, apicoplast, and lesser extent cytoplasm. Both isoforms are essential for parasite growth during asexual blood stages, representing a unique mechanism for maintaining pyrophosphate homeostasis across multiple subcellular compartments.

articles.headline: Hemozoin-associated proteins disrupt blood-brain barrier in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum hemozoin-associated biomolecules induce brain endothelial cell barrier disruption in an in vitro model of cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Crotty KA, Clotea I, Ueberheide B, Cammer M, Sall J, Liang A, Rodriguez A
articles.details: In vitro study of P. falciparum-infected red blood cell effects on human brain microvascular endothelial cells identified that hemozoin-associated biomolecules (specifically proteins, not the crystal itself) induce brain endothelial barrier disruption. Protease treatment inhibited disruption while nuclease treatment had no effect, indicating proteins bound to hemozoin mediate this key mechanism in cerebral malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Global malaria burden declined; SDI and sanitation drive future reduction
articles.title_original: Global epidemiological trends, distribution of NTDs and malaria, and disease burden projections for the next 15 years.
articles.authors: Huang H, Gao Y, Zhu H, Xu Y, Li M, Meng F, Chen X, Du G, Ma W, Qian J, Li Y, Tan W
articles.details: Global Burden of Disease study (2021) analyzing malaria trends 1990–2021 found global malaria burden decreased, particularly in Sub-Saharan Africa, while neglected tropical disease incidence increased until 2016 before declining. Vector autoregression modeling incorporating Socio-Demographic Index, sanitation, and behavioral factors showed SDI significantly influenced malaria burden, with environmental sanitation and behavioral factors playing major roles in low-SDI regions.

articles.headline: RDT kits viable DNA source for malaria drug resistance surveillance
articles.title_original: Rapid diagnostic test kit as a source of DNA in comparison with filter paper for malaria molecular surveillance and drug resistance monitoring: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Woldegiorgis BT, Wondale B, Tamiru G, Lindtjørn B, Massebo F
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 417 paired RDT and filter paper samples from malaria-positive cases in Gamo Zone, Ethiopia, compared DNA extraction and nested PCR detection. RDT-extracted samples achieved 89.2% positive percent agreement (95% CI 84.7–92.5) for P. falciparum and 81.9% (95% CI 74.6–87.6) for P. vivax detection, with Kappa agreement 0.64 overall (p<0.001), improving to 0.75 for high parasite loads (>100,000/μL). Pfmdr1 amplification success was 96.7% for RDT and 100% for filter paper.

articles.headline: NEK4 regulates meiotic entry and zygote maturation in Plasmodium
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.
articles.authors: Yanase R, Hair M, Zeeshan M, Ferguson DJP, Brady D, Pasquarello C, Bottrill A, Bhanvadia S, Neal A, Tromer EC, Le Roch KG, Hainard A, Holder AA, Vaughan S, Guttery DS, Tewari R
articles.details: Study of Plasmodium berghei NIMA-related kinase NEK4 identified it as a key regulator of meiotic initiation and zygote morphogenesis. Using ultrastructure expansion microscopy, NEK4 was shown to accumulate at the microtubule-organizing center and apical polar complex post-fertilization. Deletion of Pbnek4 caused complete developmental arrest with blocked MTOC duplication, microtubule formation, and nuclear migration. Transcriptomic and phosphoproteomic analyses revealed collapsed transcriptional networks for meiosis and cytoskeletal organization.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria: comparing rodent and human research models
articles.title_original: Comment on: Cerebral malaria: of mice and men.
articles.authors: Carvalho LJM, Sanches GS
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Commentary article examining cerebral malaria research using rodent and human models. No empirical findings provided.

articles.headline: High P. falciparum genetic diversity and transmission in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of the IC/3D7 allelic family of the merozoite surface protein 2 (MSP2) of Plasmodium falciparum and multiplicity of infection in four health facilities in the Mbouda Health District, Cameroon.
articles.authors: Cedric Y, Nadia NAC, Merveille TT, Pacome TK, Mathieu KT, Guy-Armand GN, Abel D, Edna GDM, Sandra WNI, Yval KT, Hu H
articles.details: Analysis of 481 diagnostic samples from Cameroon revealed 137 (28.48%) P. falciparum infections. msp2 gene sequencing identified 15 distinct alleles with overall genetic diversity of 14.15% and mean multiplicity of infection (MOI) of 1.20. Mono-, double-, and triple-genotype infections comprised 81.68%, 18.18%, and 1.33% respectively. Results indicate high transmission intensity in the region.

articles.headline: Severe childhood malaria linked to long-term cognitive deficits
articles.title_original: Long-Term Cognitive Ability and Academic Achievement After Childhood Severe Malaria.
articles.authors: Bangirana P, Mellencamp KA, Ren J, Nakitende JA, Conroy AL, Datta D, Kautzman C, Goings MJ, Opoka RO, Namazzi R, Robberstad B, Idro R, John CC
articles.details: Ugandan prospective cohort of 889 children tested 4–15 years (mean 8.4 years) after severe malaria. Cerebral malaria and severe malarial anemia (but not other severe forms) were associated with lower overall cognition z-scores (−0.41 and −0.31, respectively) and lower math scores (−0.46 and −0.32) compared to unaffected controls. Acute kidney injury, hyperuricemia, and elevated angiopoietin-2 at initial episode predicted worse cognitive outcomes.

articles.headline: Long-term neurocognitive outcomes after severe malaria
articles.title_original: Long-Term Neurocognitive Outcomes After Severe Malaria Infection: No Peace of MIND.
articles.authors: Sundararaman SA, Odom John AR
articles.details: Article addresses long-term neurocognitive sequelae following severe malaria infection. Abstract unavailable; inference from title only.

articles.headline: PX1 haplotype confers artemisinin and lumefantrine resistance
articles.title_original: A Plasmodium falciparum PX1 haplotype is associated with reduced susceptibility to artemisinin and lumefantrine.
articles.authors: Bower-Lepts C, Jangra A, Kanatani S, Tripathi A, Mlambo G, McCotter-González A, Romano L, Orena S, Okitwi M, Niaré K, Rosenthal PJ, Conrad M, Sinnis P, Mok S
articles.details: Genetic cross and drug-selection studies identified P. falciparum PX1 phosphoinositide-binding protein mutations (L1222P, M1701I, D1705N) as strongest correlates of reduced susceptibility to dihydroartemisinin and lumefantrine. PX1 PIN haplotype was highly enriched following drug selection in Ugandan clinical isolates and confers growth advantage to asexual parasites, potentially explaining rapid rise of artemether-lumefantrine resistance in Uganda.

articles.headline: Outdoor residual spraying most cost-effective for zoonotic malaria
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of outdoor residual spraying, insecticide-treated nets, and their combination for zoonotic malaria control in Sabah, Malaysia: a comparative analysis.
articles.authors: Firdaus MH, Wan Puteh SE, Md Idris Z, Zhueng TJ, Abdul Hamid MH, Suli Z, Abdul Manap M, Abdul Manaf MR
articles.details: One-year cost-effectiveness analysis in Sabah for P. knowlesi control compared outdoor residual spraying (ORS), long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs), and combined strategies. ORS averted 13 cases at incremental cost-effectiveness ratio of MYR 2,046 per case averted (~USD 470). LLINs and combined ORS+LLINs were less cost-effective. ORS demonstrated most favorable cost-effectiveness profile across sensitivity analyses.

articles.headline: Global genomic surveillance reveals region-specific P. falciparum resistance
articles.title_original: Global-scale population genetic analysis of Plasmodium falciparum identifies region-specific patterns of malaria parasite adaptation.
articles.authors: Billows N, Dombrowski JG, Thorpe J, Vanheer L, Moss S, Gitaka J, Sutherland CJ, Marinho CRF, Thi Hong Ngoc N, Thi Huong Binh N, Thieu NQ, Campino S, Clark TG
articles.details: Analysis of 17,565 P. falciparum isolates from 39 countries identified distinct geographic patterns of drug resistance. Vietnam isolates all carry pfkelch13 C580Y artemisinin resistance mutation with elevated identity-by-descent around pfKIC3. Brazil showed increased identity-by-descent around pfcrt consistent with drug selection. South America and Horn of Africa exhibited elevated identity-by-descent in pfKIC7 and pfKIC9, suggesting alternative artemisinin resistance pathways.

articles.headline: Nanopore genomic surveillance of P. falciparum across sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Continental-scale genomic surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum malaria across sub-Saharan Africa with rapid nanopore sequencing.
articles.authors: Mwenda M, Mosler K, Bohmeier B, Chomba M, van Loon W, Mambwe B, Gaye A, Olorunfemi A, Suliman S, Yanogo NJ, Sow D, Ngom B, Zoure OAZ, Tibiri YNG, Ojeniyi F, Zongo A, Anoh EA, Achi V, Chiyesu C, Otieno S, Ogola B, Niangaly M, Carrasquilla M, Goboto DK, Feldt T, Tufa TB, Oliveira R, Schallenberg E, Sogoba Y, Ntalla C, Ouedraogo O, Otieno K, Opaleye O, Olowe A, Gibbons M, Drakeley C, Schubert G, Mockenhaupt FP, Portugal S, Deme AB, Soulama I, Ndiaye D, Ojurongbe O, Kariuki S, Shi YP, Schultz JS, Hawela M, Bridges DJ, Hendry JA
articles.details: Decentralized implementation sequenced 1,065 P. falciparum samples across 6 African countries in one year using nanopore protocol (~5 hours, <$25/sample). Surveillance detects antimalarial drug-resistance genes, hrp2/3 deletions, csp vaccine target, and ama1 polymorphisms. Offline laptop-based dashboard provides real-time mapping and variant-calling with robust coverage across parasitemia levels and sensitive minor-clone mutation detection.

articles.headline: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine superior to chloroquine for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine versus chloroquine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium vivax malaria with concurrent or delayed high-dose primaquine in Brazil (Curavivax): an open-label, single-centre, randomised clinical trial.
articles.authors: Baía-da-Silva DC, Machado KVA, Rabelo ALR, da Silva Balieiro AA, Barbosa ASL, Costa MRF, Sampaio VS, Melo GC, Alexandre MAA, Alencar ACC, Nascimento CT, Jerônimo CMP, Marcovski MS, Barbosa LRA, Valle CS, Carrilho TAA, Vieira JLF, de Melo MM, Dinelly KMO, Omena AG, Brito-Sousa JD, Barreiros GA, Rocha YV, Filho ACA, Bassat Q, Alecrim MDGC, Siqueira AM, Lacerda MVG
articles.details: Brazilian randomized trial (n=418) compared dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (40/320 mg or 20/160 mg, 3 days) versus chloroquine (150 mg, 3 days) for uncomplicated P. vivax with primaquine (15 mg, 14 days) given immediately or delayed. Day 42 recurrence rates: DHA-piperaquine+primaquine 1.0% versus chloroquine+primaquine 2.0% (p=0.44); DHA-piperaquine+delayed 2.4% versus chloroquine+delayed 27.3% (p<0.0001). All treatments well tolerated.

articles.headline: Cardiolipin synthase critical for P. falciparum development
articles.title_original: A bacterial-type cardiolipin synthase in Plasmodium spp. supports mitochondrial respiration and is important for liver stage maturation.
articles.authors: Adhur KA, Nirdosh N, Chandramouli A, Basu S, Lahiri A, Kamat SS, Mishra S, Habib S
articles.details: Mechanistic study of Plasmodium cardiolipin synthase (Cls) localized to parasite mitochondria. Cls knockout parasites (P. berghei) exhibited impaired asexual blood-stage proliferation, reduced mitochondrial membrane potential and basal oxygen consumption, and severe defect in liver-stage maturation despite completing mosquito development and generating infectious sporozoites. Cls vital for parasite mitochondrial function and life-stage progression.

articles.headline: Live imaging reveals parasite egress mechanisms and timing
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal dynamics of signal dependent exocytosis and parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupture during Plasmodium falciparum egress.
articles.authors: Dedkhad W, Tran T, Fierro MA, Brooks C, Muralidharan V
articles.details: Live imaging of P. falciparum egress tracked signal-dependent exocytosis and parasitophorous vacuolar membrane (PVM) rupture. Exocytosis of egress-specific vesicles triggered ≥3 hours before merozoite egress. PVM ruptures at single site then rapidly expands, releasing merozoites into host red blood cell. PfERC endoplasmic reticulum calcium-binding protein required for signal-dependent exocytosis.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol: ACT cost-effectiveness for malaria
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapies for first-line treatment of malaria among pediatric and adult populations: a systematic review protocol.
articles.authors: Ma B, Kr S, Jith A, Gr S, Janakiram C, John D
articles.details: Protocol for systematic review evaluating cost-effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs) versus non-ACT alternatives or different ACT dose regimens for first-line malaria treatment in pediatric and adult populations. Review will follow JBI methodology for systematic reviews of economic evidence, using ICER metrics and GRADE approach to assess evidence quality (PROSPERO CRD420251058688).

articles.headline: Plasmodium genetic diversity and multiplicity of infection in Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and multiplicity of Plasmodium spp. infections in Southeast Asia: a scoping review.
articles.authors: Helen AP, Martini M, Jeon BY, Han JH, Muh F
articles.details: Scoping review of 16 studies (2015-October 2025) examining molecular genotyping methods for parasite genetic diversity. P. falciparum showed polyclonal infection rates of 3.9%-73.9% and mean MOI of 1.05-4.9; P. vivax had polyclonal rates of 16.9%-71.4% and mean MOI of 1.1-1.91; P. malariae displayed low MOI; P. knowlesi showed polyclonal rates of 7.3%-21% and mean MOI of 1.04-1.06. Authors concluded P. falciparum and P. vivax remain genetically diverse across Southeast Asia with heterogeneous transmission patterns, particularly in border regions.

articles.headline: Severe Malaria
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria.
articles.authors: Samandari T, Gillespie E
articles.details: No abstract provided; unable to extract detailed findings from title alone.

articles.headline: Data of cultural history of malaria in Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data of cultural history of malaria in Hungary].
articles.authors: Kiss L
articles.details: No abstract provided; unable to extract detailed findings from title alone.

articles.headline: RTS,S vaccine reduces child mortality in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi
articles.title_original: Impact of introducing RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine on mortality in young children in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi: an observational evaluation of a cluster-randomised implementation programme.
articles.authors: Mwapasa V, Asante KP, Milligan P, Akech S, Oduro A, Mathanga DP, Fogelson A, Kwambai TK, Hamel MJ, Kapito-Tembo A, Gyan T, Westercamp N, Okine RNA, Moore KA, Pellaux-Furrer E, Stanley CC, Ansong D, Kariuki S, Njuguna P, Mvalo T, Welaga P, Otieno L, Snell P, Schellenberg D, Chimala A, Afari EA, Bejon P, Maleta K, Agbenyega T, Snow RW, Zulu M, Odei-Lartey E, Chinkhumba J, Samuels AM
articles.details: Cluster-randomized evaluation comparing 79 implementation areas (vaccine introduced 2019) to 79 comparison areas across Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi. RTS,S delivered in 4 doses at ages 6-24 months. By 46 months, 1,289,504 children received dose 1, with 82.8% coverage for dose 1 and 71.1% for dose 3. Mortality rate ratio was 0.87 (95% CI 0.77-0.97; p=0.016), with 5,576 deaths in implementation vs 6,152 in comparison areas, averting approximately 1 in 8 deaths with moderate vaccine coverage.

articles.headline: Fatal avian malaria in critically endangered Fatu Hiva monarch
articles.title_original: First record of fatal Plasmodium relictum (GRW4) infection in a highly endangered Fatu Hiva monarch (Pomarea whitneyi) fledgling: a case report.
articles.authors: Igual I Beltrán M, Duc M, Blanvillain C, Bruslund S, Valkiūnas G, Pendl H
articles.details: Case report of a fledgling Fatu Hiva monarch (fewer than 20 individuals remaining) found dead on 17 June 2022. Necropsy and molecular testing confirmed infection with P. relictum lineage GRW4. High parasitaemia, hemozoin-positive intracytoplasmic pigment, and hepatosplenomegaly were observed. Acute severe P. relictum infection was the proximate cause of death, likely on a background of chronic stress and negative energy balance.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum spreading across Uganda-DRC border
articles.title_original: Cross-border molecular epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum drug susceptibility: insights from the Uganda-Democratic Republic of the Congo border.
articles.authors: Dilufwasayo PM, Balikagala B, Uchopa MA, Amaa JA, Ngoyi DM, Mita T
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey in northeastern DRC (May-June 2023) of 536 individuals, 49.8% P. falciparum-positive. Validated artemisinin partial resistance mutations A675V (4.3%) and C469Y (1.3%) were detected. The pfmdr1 N86 allele was fixed and 50.3% carried the NFD haplotype (reduced lumefantrine susceptibility). Pfmdr1 and pfpm2/3 CNVs were found in 11.7% and 7.0%, respectively. Whole-genome sequencing showed DRC parasites carrying resistance mutations clustered with Ugandan strains, confirming cross-border spread of resistant parasites.

articles.headline: Molecular insights into P. falciparum perforin-like protein pore formation
articles.title_original: Deciphering Membrane Pore Formation Mechanisms of Plasmodium falciparum Perforin-Like Protein 1 (PfPLP1).
articles.authors: Patil SB, Dasgupta S, Bhaumik P
articles.details: Study using molecular dynamics simulations to investigate PfPLP1 structure and membrane pore-formation mechanism. PfPLP1 comprises a canonical MACPF domain and β-pleated sheet APCβ domain. Initial membrane binding is mediated by cationic residues at the APCβ base interacting with lipid polar headgroups. Tetrameric and larger oligomeric forms initiate pore opening with water penetration between oligomers and lipid bilayer, with lipids reorganizing into toroidal edges.

articles.headline: High malaria transmission accelerates tetanus antibody waning in children
articles.title_original: Assessing the effect of malaria exposure history on tetanus antibody waning rates among children in Jinja and Tororo Districts, Uganda.
articles.authors: Sbarra AN, Stone W, Tetteh K, Ssewanyana I, Musinguzi K, Rodríguez-Barraquer I, Jagannathan P, Bach FA, Drakeley C, Takahashi S
articles.details: Longitudinal analysis (2011-2017) of tetanus antitoxin IgG in 3,064 samples from 597 Ugandan children aged 6 months to 10 years from high-transmission (Tororo) and low-transmission (Jinja) districts. Antibody half-lives were significantly shorter in Tororo (2.8 years, 95% CI 2.6-3.1) than Jinja (15.8 years, 95% CI 8.8-28.5). Hazard of sero-reversion was 2.7-fold higher in Tororo (95% CI 1.7-4.1). Antibodies fell below protective levels by age 8.1 years (95% CI 6.4-10.2).

articles.headline: Pvmdr-1 and Pfmdr-1 mutations detected in Republic of Korea malaria
articles.title_original: Molecular Surveillance of Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum Drug Resistance Genes in the Republic of Korea: 2022-2025.
articles.authors: Jung H, Shin HI, Ku B, Park E, Lee MR, Ju JW, Lee HI
articles.details: Drug resistance gene surveillance in blood samples from P. vivax (soldiers, relapsed, imported) and P. falciparum (soldiers, civilians) patients in the Republic of Korea 2022-2025. In P. vivax, mutations S515R, G698S, L845F, M908L, T958M, and F1076L were nearly universal; T529 and E1233 showed moderate prevalence. In P. falciparum, Y184F in Pfmdr-1 was highly prevalent and A675V in PfK13 was detected only in soldiers. Significant differences in mutation prevalence among groups (p<0.05) were identified, with no temporal trends observed.

articles.headline: T cell responses to P. falciparum vaccine candidate GMZ2.6c in Brazilian Amazon
articles.title_original: Cellular Immune Response and T Cell Epitope Mapping of Plasmodium falciparum Chimeric Vaccine Candidate GMZ2.6c and Its Components (MSP-3, GLURP and Pfs48/45) in Individuals Naturally Exposed to Malaria in Brazilian Amazon.
articles.authors: Baptista BO, Soares IF, de Souza HADS, Barros JP, Riccio EKP, Martorano RM, Rodrigues-da-Silva RN, Amoah LE, Singh SK, Theisen M, Lima-Junior JDC, Totino PRR, Daniel-Ribeiro CT, Pratt-Riccio LR
articles.details: Study of cellular immune responses in individuals naturally exposed to malaria in the Brazilian Amazon (Acre and Amazonas states) to vaccine candidate GMZ2.6c and its components (MSP-3, GLURP, Pfs48/45). PBMCs from P. falciparum-infected and non-infected exposed individuals were stimulated. P. falciparum-infected individuals showed greater CD8+ T cell activation to MSP-3 and Pfs48/45. Both groups produced elevated IL-6 and TNF-α; non-infected individuals had high IL-10. Multiple T cell epitopes within vaccine components elicited IFN-γ responses in both groups.

articles.headline: Influenza co-infection protects mice from malaria-associated lung injury
articles.title_original: Protective coinfection: influenza reprograms myeloid cells to limit CD8 T cell-mediated malaria pathology.
articles.authors: Reed JS, Nayan R, Deckers M, Cornwall DH, Ostrand-Rosenberg S, Evavold BD, Lamb TJ
articles.details: Experimental study in mice showing co-infection with influenza X31 protected from malaria-associated acute lung injury (MA-ALI) and death. X31 co-infection expanded Ly6C+ monocyte-derived alveolar macrophages that inhibited pathogenic CD8 T cells in contact-dependent manner. Blocking monocytic myeloid cells with gemcitabine eliminated protection. Protection occurred independently of parasite burden and type I interferon signaling. Pulmonary CD8 T cells showed broad transcriptional reprogramming and impaired inflammatory cytokine production.

articles.headline: Genetic basis of malaria resistance in Hawaiian honeycreeper identified
articles.title_original: Evolution of Resistance to Avian Malaria Infection in an Endemic Hawaiian Honeycreeper.
articles.authors: Neddermeyer JH, Cassin-Sackett L, Paxton EH, Campana MG, Fleischer RC, Foster JT
articles.details: Genome-wide association study of 35 O'ahu 'amakihi identified 582 SNPs associated with resistance to avian malaria infection (determined by real-time PCR). Annotations revealed genes involved in transcription regulation, apoptosis, T-cell and complement activation, autophagosome assembly and transport, and intracellular calcium flux. Genes regulating intracellular calcium—a key signaling molecule for Plasmodium replication—were prominent. Host resistance may involve restricting intracellular components essential for parasite replication.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccine reduces child deaths by 13% in Africa
articles.title_original: Malaria vaccine reduces child deaths by 13% in Africa, WHO study reports.
articles.authors: Iacobucci G
articles.details: WHO study reports malaria vaccine reduces child deaths by 13% in Africa. No additional details provided in abstract.

articles.headline: RTS,S/AS01 vaccine projected to reduce malaria 40% in Benin
articles.title_original: Estimating the impact of the RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine in Benin: A mathematical modelling study.
articles.authors: Alohoutade SV, Hounsell R, Dandonougbo C, Aikpon R, Degila J, Silal S
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study in Benin estimated RTS,S/AS01 vaccine impact in children under five. Vaccination coverage at DTP3 levels combined with current insecticidal net use projected to avert 40% of clinical malaria cases and deaths among children under five from 2025-2034. However, a 1.5-fold decrease in net use could offset these gains. Authors conclude vaccination is an additional control tool but potential unintended consequences warrant monitoring.

articles.headline: NGO maintains malaria services during COVID-19 with phased protocols
articles.title_original: Delivering malaria services during the COVID-19 pandemic: challenges and lessons from an international non-governmental organisation.
articles.authors: Chestnutt EG, Marasciulo M, Magumba G, Maxwell K, Rassi C, Parkes A, Nelson C, Tibenderana JK
articles.details: International NGO coordinated response to maintain malaria service delivery during COVID-19 pandemic, establishing dedicated taskforce and four-phase outbreak management plan adapted to local contexts. Article provides practical insights from decision-making staff on operational adaptations for safer service delivery, offering lessons for organizations preparing for future operational challenges.

articles.headline: Nigeria malaria elimination hindered by low political prioritization
articles.title_original: Accelerating Nigeria towards malaria elimination requires moving away from business as usual: insights from a political economy analysis.
articles.authors: Chestnutt EG, Meredith S, Ipaye B, Getachew D, Tibenderana JK, Anyachukwu E, Obot T, Maxwell K
articles.details: Political economy analysis of Nigeria's malaria program identified barriers: limited government prioritization and resource allocation due to weak citizen advocacy and lack of direct communication channels between program and decision-makers; and suboptimal use of allocated resources due to poor partner coordination. Authors recommend sustained political financing, citizen demand mobilization, integrated partner coordination, and incorporation of malaria priorities into national development planning.

articles.headline: Quintuple pfdhfr-pfdhps mutations linked to gametocyte carriage
articles.title_original: Prevalence of pfdhfr-pfdhps Sextuple and Gametocyte-Associated Quintuple Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine Resistance Mutations in Plasmodium falciparum Isolates from Pregnant Women in Mozambique.
articles.authors: Drissi-El Boukili Y, Rovira-Vallbona E, Guetens P, Chiheb D, Kattenberg JH, Kestens L, Enosse SMM, Rosanas-Urgell A, Arnaldo P
articles.details: Survey of 100 P. falciparum isolates from pregnant women in Mozambique (2014-15) examined sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance mutations and gametocyte presence. Quintuple and sextuple pfdhfr/pfdhps mutant haplotypes detected in 54% and 7% of parasites respectively; gametocytes found in 34% of isolates. Gametocyte carriage significantly associated with quintuple mutant infections (AOR = 7.5, p = 0.001), which accounted for 80% of gametocyte-positive infections. Authors recommend ongoing SP resistance surveillance to guide future intermittent preventive treatment approaches.

articles.headline: Immunization coverage low at 29% in high-transmission communities
articles.title_original: Immunization Coverage and Associated Factors Among Children Aged 18 to 72 Months in Communities With High Malaria Transmission, Bioko Island.
articles.authors: Eburi Losoha E, Lopez Mikue MSA, García GA, Athuman T, Ross A, Daubenberger C, Merten S
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey (September 2019-March 2020) of 297 children aged 18 months-6 years in 13 malaria-endemic communities in Malabo District, Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea assessed routine immunization coverage. Overall 29% (86/297) were fully immunized; coverage similar whether verified by vaccination card (28%) or caregiver recall (30%). Younger children (18 months-2 years) had significantly higher completion (45%) than older children aged 2-6 years (22%), aOR = 0.42 (95% CI 0.20-0.87). Authors conclude overall coverage remains low and recommend studies to identify vaccination gaps.

articles.headline: African artemisinin-resistant k13 mutations show different clearance phenotypes
articles.title_original: Artemisinin partial resistance at a crossroads: evidence for continental variation in Plasmodium falciparumclearance phenotypes.
articles.authors: Hosangadi V, Fidock DA
articles.details: Systematic review of 96 studies (35 with genotype-phenotype data) compared artemisinin resistance profiles across southeast Asia and Africa. In southeast Asia, canonical k13 mutations consistently displayed prolonged parasite clearance half-life with strong concordance to ring-stage survival. In East/Horn of Africa, independently emergent k13 mutations exhibited faster clearance kinetics despite elevated ring-stage survival, suggesting current surveillance thresholds may under-detect artemisinin resistance in Africa. Authors recommend region-specific surveillance integrating parasite clearance half-life, ring-stage survival, molecular markers, and partner-drug susceptibility.

articles.headline: RCT compares low-dose and standard primaquine for gametocytes
articles.title_original: Protocol for a multicentric open-label randomized controlled trial comparing low-dose (0.25 mg/kg) and standard-dose (0.75 mg/kg) primaquine for gametocytocidal efficacy and safety in Plasmodium falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Gulati P, Singhal R, Gupta A, Anjali, Tripathi PK, Kumar D, Sinha DP, Singh SP, Singh K, Baharia RK, Bhatt KN, Vasava AH, Gamit A, Patki MB, Malla WA, Kori L, Savargaonkar D, Chakma N, Parmar D, Alagesan L, Kashyap R, Singh K, Anvikar AR, Bharti PK, Nitika N
articles.details: Protocol for open-label multicentric RCT in India (4 sites, target n=496) comparing low-dose (0.25 mg/kg day-1) versus standard-dose (0.75 mg/kg day-2) primaquine for gametocytocidal efficacy in uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria after ACT. Primary outcomes are gametocyte prevalence and density (by day 14) assessed by blood smears and QT-NASBA, and hemoglobin safety markers (by day 28). Enrollment began August 2025. Study aims to support evidence-based recommendations for national malaria programs.

articles.headline: ITN ownership 77.6%, use 77.2% but below Tanzania's 80% target
articles.title_original: Socio-demographic predictors of insecticide-treated bed net ownership and utilization for protection against malaria by rural community members across five regions of Mainland Tanzania.
articles.authors: Chacha GA, Seth MD, Mandai SS, Petro DA, Challe DP, Kisambale AJ, Budodo R, Madebe RA, Mbwambo RB, Bakari C, Pereus D, Aaron S, Mbwambo D, Lusasi A, Lazaro S, Mandara CI, Ishengoma DS
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional survey (July-August 2023) of 10,228 participants ≥6 months across 15 villages in 5 Tanzanian regions assessed insecticide-treated bed net ownership and use. Overall ownership was 77.6% (7939/10,228) and use was 77.2% (7899/10,228), varying significantly by district (p<0.001). Females had higher odds of ownership and use than males (aOR = 1.27, 95% CI 1.12-1.45); under-fives had higher odds than adults (ownership aOR = 1.83, use aOR = 2.26). Higher education and household socioeconomic status independently associated with increased ownership and use. Authors note coverage remains below 80% national target.

articles.headline: Actinomycin derivatives show 7-fold better selectivity than parent compound
articles.title_original: Discovery of actinomycin derivatives with improved selectivity against malaria parasites from a Streptomyces culture library.
articles.authors: Teshima A, Teklemichael AA, Hirata A, Akimoto M, Taniguchi M, Muslimin R, Ogaki S, Ali A, Suzuki T, Mizukami S, Arakawa K
articles.details: Screening of Streptomyces culture library identified two actinomycin derivatives with antimalarial activity: actinomycin X2 (IC50 = 0.241 nM, CC50 = 6.71 nM, selectivity index = 27.9) and actinomycin X0ß (IC50 = 7.69 nM, CC50 = 818 nM, selectivity index = 106). Compared to commercially available actinomycin D (IC50 = 0.469 nM, selectivity index = 14.3), both derivatives showed improved selectivity indices, with actinomycin X0ß demonstrating 7-fold higher selectivity. Authors propose these actinomycins as potential antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: PfCoronin mutations reduce hemoglobin uptake, conferring artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular basis of PfCoronin function in artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Ullah I, Farringer MA, Khushu M, Burkhard AY, Hathaway E, Willett BC, Shin SH, Sharma AI, Mallick R, Istvan ES, Martin MC, Harrigan O, Shao KL, Dvorin JD, Hartl DL, Volkman SK, Bopp S, Absalon S, Wirth DF
articles.details: Study identified cellular mechanism of PfCoronin-mediated artemisinin resistance in P. falciparum. PfCoronin mutations disrupted protein localization and perturbed PfActin homeostasis, altering ring-stage morphology and reducing hemoglobin uptake. Like PfKelch13 mutants, reduced hemoglobin uptake in ring-stage parasites limits heme-dependent artemisinin activation, reducing drug efficacy. Authors demonstrate that both PfCoronin and PfKelch13 mutations influence endocytic access to hemoglobin, revealing how non-essential proteins shape drug response and resistance.

articles.headline: Soil-transmitted helminths not associated with asymptomatic P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum Infection Was Not Associated with Exposure to Soil-Transmitted Helminths in Children in Esse, Cameroon.
articles.authors: Lajos L, Fogang B, Jensen A, Atchombat D, Cornwall DH, Donkeu C, Nana-Mbianda CM, Slam C, Djeunga HCN, Zhan B, Koki Ndombo PO, Ayong LS, Lamb TJ
articles.details: Longitudinal study (1 month) of 134 primary school children at 3 sites in Esse, Cameroon found high P. falciparum infection (94.8% microscopy-positive, 85.8% asymptomatic) and soil-transmitted helminth exposure (87.4% seropositive for ≥1 STH antigen). Comparing asymptomatic versus symptomatic malaria, no significant difference in STH exposure was observed (85.7% vs 90.9%, P >0.05). Fever development associated with increased STH antibody reactivity (p<0.05), but no correlation between anti-STH antibody level and P. falciparum parasitemia. Authors conclude soil-transmitted helminth exposure was not a major contributor to asymptomatic P. falciparum carriage.

articles.headline: Correction to malaria elimination study in Thai-Myanmar border region
articles.title_original: Correction: Challenges and responses of malaria elimination in a high-endemic area along the Thai-Myanmar border: A health systems perspective.
articles.authors: Saita S, Parker DM, Suk-Uam K, Phuanukoonnon S, Pooseesod K
articles.details: Correction to previously published article on health systems approaches to malaria elimination in a high-endemic area along the Thai-Myanmar border. Original article DOI: 10.1371/journal.pgph.0006286.

articles.headline: dCas9-SSAP genome editing for Plasmodium parasites reviewed
articles.title_original: The dCas9-SSAP as a promising genome editing tool in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Quansah E, Yang S, Jia Y, Yu L, Zhang C
articles.details: Review of dCas9-SSAP (catalytically inactive Cas9 with single-stranded annealing proteins) as a next-generation, cleavage-free genome-editing system for Plasmodium. Discusses advantages over traditional CRISPR-Cas9 nuclease approaches in avoiding double-strand breaks and inheritable genetic damage, and alignment with parasite biology.

articles.headline: Novel P. falciparum surface antigens identified for vaccine development
articles.title_original: Identification of Novel Malaria Antigens Expressed on the Surface of RBCs Infected with Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Shakri AR, Mohapatra AD, Badhai J, Anand A, Varghese A, Raj DK
articles.details: Researchers identified both known and novel antigens on P. falciparum-infected RBC membranes using polyclonal sera from immunized mice and phage-display cDNA library screening. Polyclonal serum showed significant parasite-killing activity in in vitro growth inhibition assays. Results support further evaluation of identified antigens as vaccine candidates.

articles.headline: ACE polymorphisms potentially linked to severe P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Potential Role of ACE Polymorphisms in Severe Malaria: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Pisapia R, Pomicino A, Minei G, Iadevito I, Perrella A
articles.details: Case report of a 40-year-old woman with severe P. falciparum malaria complicated by thrombosis, hyperparasitemia, acute kidney injury, and metabolic acidosis. Genetic testing identified ACE I/D polymorphism alongside other thrombophilic variants (MTHFR C677T and A1298C). Authors suggest ACE polymorphisms may influence malaria severity.

articles.headline: Long-acting ivermectin injection sustains mosquito killing 2+ months
articles.title_original: Selection of the optimal long-acting injectable formulation of ivermectin for use in humans to target malaria vectors in Western Africa: evaluation of pharmacokinetics and mosquitocidal efficacy in cattle under laboratory conditions.
articles.authors: Zela L, Pooda SH, Porciani A, Beneteau S, Sagna AB, Le Lamer-Déchamps S, Moiroux N, Ouédraogo COW, Somé AF, Pennetier C, Roberge C, Belem AMG, Dabiré KR, Dayo GK, Mouline K
articles.details: Three long-acting injectable ivermectin formulations (LAIF) tested in a cattle-Anopheles model. mdc-STM-001 showed optimal pharmacokinetics with peak plasma concentration (Cmax) of 34.5 ± 12.7 ng/ml. A single 0.6 mg/kg injection achieved 10-day hazard ratios >4 and >50% cumulative mosquito mortalities for ≥60 days against both insecticide-susceptible and resistant Anopheles strains, with mosquito lifespan <10 days for ≥90 days, meeting WHO Preferred Product Characteristics.

articles.headline: Climate and vector control shape malaria trends over 23 years
articles.title_original: The influence of climatic factors and vector control on malaria incidence in Southeast Iran: a 23-year longitudinal analysis.
articles.authors: Dehghan A, Rahimi Foroushani A, Rahimi M, Nejati J, Youssefi F, Nikpour F, Raeisi A, Hanafi-Bojd AA
articles.details: Longitudinal analysis spanning 23 years examining how climatic factors and vector control measures influence malaria incidence in Southeast Iran.

articles.headline: High concurrent malaria and arbovirus exposure in remote Congo Basin
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Malaria and Arbovirus Co-Exposure in the Boende Health Zone, Northwestern Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.authors: Tshilumba SM, Larivière Y, Zola Matuvanga T, Mutwadi A, Engbu D, Kapour G, Lemey G, Salloum M, Champagne M, Mangungulu D, Van Damme P, Muhindo-Mavoko H, Tevuzula VM, Mariën J, Peeters M, Geertruyden JV, Mitashi-Mulopo P
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 379 adults in December 2023 in Boende Health Zone, DRC. Malaria prevalence: 51.7% (95% CI 46.7–56.7). Arboviral seroprevalence: 78.4% (95% CI 73.1–81.5), dominated by O'nyong-nyong virus (42.8%), Rift Valley fever virus (32.0%), and chikungunya virus (23.4%). Concurrent malaria and arboviral exposure occurred in 40.4% (95% CI 35.6–45.4).

articles.headline: LLINs rapidly deteriorate and become ineffective in refugee camps
articles.title_original: Physical integrity and bioefficacy of long-lasting insecticidal nets 18 months after use among Central African Republic refugees in Gado-Badzere, a high malaria burden camp in Cameroon.
articles.authors: Nematchoua Weyou Z, Djemna Djieyep F, Lontsi-Demano M, Ngangue-Siewe IN, Tchigossou G, Ning Teh R, Adanzounon D, Kuokuo Kimbi H, Ule Ngole Sumbele I
articles.details: Assessment of 440 long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) at Gado-Badzere refugee camp, Cameroon, 18 months post-distribution. Physical condition: 60.7% good, 14.5% acceptable, 24.8% deteriorated. All 30 nets bioassayed were ineffective against field Anopheles gambiae sensu lato with knock-down and mortality rates of 1.5% and 3.1%, respectively. An. gambiae (86.9%) and An. coluzzii (13.1%) were identified as vector species.

articles.headline: P. falciparum kelch13 mutations mapped in Madagascar, no resistance
articles.title_original: Updated mapping of Plasmodium falciparum kelch13 gene polymorphisms in Madagascar, 2024.
articles.authors: Roesch C, Ratovoson R, Delaunay C, Savary S, Duteurtre L, Saiman J, Villier V, Bourke C, Elison HE, Maminjanahary ET, Rabetokotany RL, Rakotondramanana HN, Mangahasimbola RT, Razafimaharo LY, Raobela O, Steinhardt L, Bowen A, Ariey F, Randremanana R, Coppée R, Witkowski B
articles.details: Nationally representative cross-sectional survey (January–May 2024) of 1,944 P. falciparum-positive samples. Of 963 successful pfkelch13 genotypes: 885 (91.9%) were wild-type 3D7; 1.8% (17/963) carried non-synonymous mutations; 6.2% (60/963) carried synonymous mutations; 0.1% (1/963) carried double mutations. Eighteen distinct mutations identified (5 previously unreported). No WHO-validated artemisinin partial resistance markers detected.

articles.headline: Prior Plasmodium infection protects against lethal Babesia via macrophages
articles.title_original: Immunity beyond borders: non-lethal Plasmodium confers cross-protection against lethal Babesia via macrophage activation.
articles.authors: Zafar I, Li Y, Kondoh D, El-Sayed SAE, Hasan T, Taniguchi T, Li H, Inoue N, Kato K, Xuan X
articles.details: Experimental study in mice examining P. berghei XAT and B. rodhaini co-infection. Non-lethal P. berghei infection significantly reduced B. rodhaini parasitemia and splenic damage. Protection was mediated by splenic macrophage expansion and driven by pro-inflammatory cytokines (IFN-γ, IL-12p70, TNF-α) balanced by IL-10, while NK cells and adaptive immunity were dispensable.

articles.headline: Computational design yields P. chabaudi peptides with antibiofilm activity
articles.title_original: Computer-Aided Design of Plasmodium chabaudi-Derived Peptides with Dual Antibiofilm and Anti-inflammatory Activities.
articles.authors: Quigua Orozco RM, Santos ADO, Rossetto JP, Madalozzo ES, Luchi LV, Rezende SB, Chan LY, Buccini DF, Macedo MLR, Mehta A, Craik DJ, Franco OL, Cardoso MH
articles.details: Three Plasmodium chabaudi-derived peptides (PcDBS1R1, PcDBS1R5, PcDBS1R9) were computationally designed using the Joker algorithm. PcDBS1R5 and PcDBS1R9 adopted α-helical structures and showed antibacterial activity against Acinetobacter baumannii. PcDBS1R1 demonstrated pronounced antibiofilm effects. All peptides were non-cytotoxic to murine macrophages and significantly reduced nitric oxide production in LPS-stimulated macrophages.

articles.headline: Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine remodels microbiome, improves birthweight vs piperaquine
articles.title_original: Beyond malaria prevention: sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine treatment in pregnancy selectively remodels the maternal gut microbiome to increase gestational weight gain and improve birthweight.
articles.authors: Waltmann A, Puerto-Meredith SM, Chinkhumba J, Mzembe E, Kayange M, Carroll I, Roach J, Mathanga DP, Gutman JR, Juliano JJ
articles.details: Longitudinal study comparing intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) versus dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP). Approximately 45% of SP's gestational weight gain (GWG) advantage was explained by gut microbial changes. Microbiome-mediated GWG coincided with 126g higher birthweight in SP recipients (95% CI 22.6–229.3g; p=0.019). SP suppressed gastrointestinal pathobionts and enriched anaerobic commensals.

articles.headline: Quantized transformers enable edge malaria microscopy but risk targeted attacks
articles.title_original: Evaluating the Adversarial Robustness and Clinical Safety of Quantized Hierarchical Transformers for Edge-Based Malaria Microscopy.
articles.authors: Hasan U, Alghamdi TG, Nayeem MA
articles.details: Researchers evaluated quantized Vision Transformers (Swin-Tiny, ViT-Tiny, MobileNetV3) on a 27,558-image malaria dataset. Post-training quantization compressed the Swin model 3.9× with 0.11% accuracy drop, maintaining out-of-distribution reliability. However, under iterative Projected Gradient Descent attacks, both MobileNetV3 and quantized Swin-Tiny collapsed to 0% accuracy. The quantized Swin-Tiny resisted black-box transfer attacks, maintaining 81% accuracy, indicating integer quantization provides no innate defense against targeted perturbations.

articles.headline: Natural product erucamide shows binding affinity to Plasmodium DXR enzyme
articles.title_original: Antimalarial Candidate Study from Three Different Fractions of Streptomyces hygroscopicus subsp. Hygroscopicus targeting Plasmodium DXR: An In Silico Approach.
articles.authors: Faratisha IFD, Fitri LE, Winaris N, Widodo N
articles.details: This in silico study identified compounds from three Streptomyces hygroscopicus fractions: 18 from fraction 13, 24 from fraction 50, and 44 from fraction 51, with 9 selected for antimalarial potential. Molecular docking revealed dibenzylamine and erucamide exhibited higher binding affinity to the DXR enzyme than the native ligand. Molecular dynamics simulation over 100 nanoseconds demonstrated the erucamide-DXR complex was more stable than dibenzylamine or native ligand. Further in vitro and in vivo validation is needed to confirm efficacy and safety.

articles.headline: Multi-channel media exposure improves malaria prevention knowledge in women
articles.title_original: Effect of cumulative exposure to media channels for malaria messages on knowledge of malaria prevention among women (15-49 years) in Uganda.
articles.authors: Natuhamya C, Obiora RU, Nwankwo GI, Agbi DM, Isiko I, Mwebesa E
articles.details: This secondary analysis of the 2018-2019 Uganda Malaria Indicator Survey enrolled 7,124 women aged 15-49 years to assess knowledge of four WHO-recommended prevention methods. Knowledge ranged from 76.5% for bed nets to 4.3% for indoor residual spraying. Cumulative exposure to multiple media channels was significantly associated with knowledge of insecticide-treated nets (p<0.001), preventive medicine (p=0.002), and indoor residual spraying (p<0.001). Women with secondary or higher education were 1.86 times more likely to report multi-channel exposure, and those aged 40+ years were 1.69 times more likely than those under 20 years.

articles.headline: Methylene blue suppresses artemisinin-resistant Plasmodium falciparum ring stages
articles.title_original: Methylene blue retains in vitro activity against early ring-stage artemisinin-resistant Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Verdier O, Christensen P, Kaewkanya C, Kobphan P, Beau C, Phyo AP, Imwong M, Chotivanich K, White NJ, Nosten F, Chaumeau V
articles.details: This in vitro study examined methylene blue activity against early ring-stage P. falciparum using clinical isolates with kelch13 wild-type (n=3), R561H (n=9), and P441L (n=10) genotypes. Isolates were exposed to varying methylene blue concentrations using a modified ring survival assay for 0-3 hour post-invasion rings. Mean 50% inhibitory concentrations (IC50) were 23 nM (95% credible interval 15-37) for wild-type, 27 nM (95% CrI 21-36) for R561H, and 14 nM (95% CrI 10-20) for P441L, indicating no evidence of kelch13-mediated cross-resistance to methylene blue.

articles.headline: Plasmodium vivax infects Duffy-negative populations through genetic adaptation
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax infection in Duffy-negative populations in Sudan: a systematic review and meta-analysis of host-parasite genetic adaptation.
articles.authors: Elfaki M, Ahmed S, Albsheer MMA, Hamid MMA
articles.details: This systematic review and meta-analysis synthesized 16 studies from 2005-2025 involving 5,753 participants. Meta-analysis of five studies showed a pooled prevalence of P. vivax infection of 11.7% (95% CI 7.2-17.3%) among Duffy-negative individuals with moderate heterogeneity (I²=56%). Substantial genetic diversity was observed in the P. vivax Duffy Binding Protein gene, including multiple haplotypes and both Malagasy-type and Cambodian-type gene duplications. These findings suggest parasite genetic adaptations facilitate infection in Duffy-negative hosts.

articles.headline: Environmental factors shape avian Plasmodium communities in Culex species
articles.title_original: Environmental factors affect the communities of avian Plasmodium in two species of Culex mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Martín-López AI, Ruiz-López MJ, Martínez-de la Puente J, Magallanes S, Taheri S, Ruiz S, Figuerola J
articles.details: This study collected Culex pipiens and Culex perexiguus mosquitoes from 16 sites over two years to examine how environmental factors influence Plasmodium communities. Parasite detection was performed using nested PCR and lineages identified by SANGER sequencing. In Cx. pipiens, Plasmodium prevalence increased with NDVI while lineage richness declined with land surface temperature. In Cx. perexiguus, both prevalence and lineage richness increased with minimum temperature, while diversity decreased with land surface temperature, demonstrating vector identity influences Plasmodium community structure.

articles.headline: Merozoite antigen combinations predict complete protection in naturally immune adults
articles.title_original: Controlled human malaria infection in adults identify combinations of merozoite antigens associated with clinical immunity.
articles.authors: Ogwang R, Nkumama IN, Mwai K, Odera D, Nyamako L, Fürle K, Rosenkranz M, Kimathi R, Njuguna P, Sim BKL, Hamaluba M, Frank R, Kapulu MC, Bejon P, Tuju J, Osier FHA
articles.details: This controlled human malaria infection study identified naturally immune Kenyan adults (n=86/142). Researchers used a custom protein microarray (KILchip) to measure IgG antibody reactivity against 70 recombinant merozoite antigens, analyzed using five statistical methodologies. Antibodies against MSP1, MSP11, RAMA, MSP7, PF3D7_1401600 (PHISTB), and PTEX150 were consistently associated with clinical immunity and, in combination, predicted complete protection.

articles.headline: Systemic inflammation markers associated with malaria co-infection in IBS patients
articles.title_original: Systemic inflammation and malaria co-infection in irritable bowel syndrome: a cross-sectional study of 142 Yemeni patients.
articles.authors: Ali NT, Mehdi MAH, Abdullah RS, Gubran ANM, Al-Abd NM, Ali GS, Mohsen Ali H
articles.details: This cross-sectional study enrolled 142 Yemeni adult patients (aged 18-70 years) with irritable bowel syndrome, conducted April-December 2024. The prevalence of malaria co-infection was 45.1% (64/142) confirmed by rapid diagnostic test. Patients with malaria had significantly higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (mean difference 0.56, 95% CI 0.40-0.72; p<0.001) and platelet-to-lymphocyte ratio (mean difference 22.6, 95% CI 17.8-27.4; p<0.001), with lower mean platelet volume. In adjusted logistic regression, elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (aOR 3.21, 95% CI 1.89-5.45) and platelet-to-lymphocyte ratio (aOR per 10-unit increase 4.02, 95% CI 2.24-7.18) were independently associated with malaria positivity.

articles.headline: Regulatory T cells mediate clinical immunity but facilitate parasite persistence
articles.title_original: CXCR6+CD127- Tr1 cells balance immunity and persistence in Plasmodium falciparum infection.
articles.authors: Nideffer J, Bach F, Strubbe S, Lopez L, Zedi M, Nankya F, Briggs J, van der Ploeg K, Musinguzi K, Kim S, Garcia Romero A, Keya A, Camanag K, Lewis S, Abdelbasset M, Wang B, Boss A, Nansubuga E, Nankabirwa JI, Arinaitwe E, Takahashi S, Dorsey G, Greenhouse B, Rodriguez-Barraquer I, Kamya MR, Bacchetta R, Ssewanyana I, Haque A, Roncarolo MG, Jagannathan P
articles.details: This study characterized type 1 regulatory T (Tr1) cells in P. falciparum infection using scRNA-seq and identified CXCR6+ CD127- as phenotypic markers enriching for Tr1 cells. In children in malaria-endemic Uganda, CXCR6+ CD127- Tr1 cells were the dominant subset responding to Pf-infected red blood cell stimulation and potently suppressed other CD4+ T cell proliferation via IL-10 secretion. Higher CXCR6+ CD127- Tr1 cell frequencies correlated with lower probability of symptoms given parasitemia but were also associated with delayed parasite clearance among untreated, asymptomatic children.

articles.headline: Plasmodium malariae shows low diversity and lacks African geographic population structure
articles.title_original: Population genomics of Plasmodium malariae from four African countries.
articles.authors: Popkin-Hall ZR, Carey-Ewend K, Aghakhanian F, Oriero EC, Seth MD, Kashamuka MM, Ngasala B, Ali IM, Mukomena Sompwe E, Mandara CI, Kharabora O, Sendor R, Simkin A, Amambua-Ngwa A, Tshefu A, Fola AA, Ishengoma DS, Bailey JA, Parr JB, Lin JT, Juliano JJ
articles.details: This study performed hybrid capture and whole-genome sequencing of 77 P. malariae isolates collected between 2015 and 2021 from four African countries. Nucleotide diversity was significantly lower than in co-localized P. falciparum isolates, while linkage disequilibrium was significantly higher, and there was no evidence of geographic population structure. Genome-wide selection scans and targeted analyses revealed evidence of selective sweeps around four erythrocyte invasion ligands and six antimalarial resistance orthologs. Demographic modeling suggests African P. malariae is recovering from a population bottleneck.

articles.headline: Pirimiphos-methyl IRS substantially reduces malaria incidence more than clothianidin
articles.title_original: Impact of indoor residual spraying and insecticide-treated nets on malaria burden in 8 districts in West Nile and Acholi regions, Uganda: a quasi-experimental study.
articles.authors: Namuganga JF, McDermott DP, Epstein A, Nankabirwa JI, Gonahasa S, Opigo J, Nabende I, Maiteki-Sebuguzi C, Kamya MR, Donnelly MJ, Dorsey G, Staedke SG
articles.details: This quasi-experimental study compared insecticide-treated nets (ITNs) alone versus ITNs plus indoor residual spraying (IRS) across four years in Uganda. Three malaria reference centers in West Nile served as the intervention arm (IRS + ITNs) and five in Acholi as control (ITNs only). During the first IRS period (December 2022-December 2023) with clothianidin-deltamethrin, baseline incidence decreased from 720.9 to 547.9 per 1,000 person-years with only a non-significant 15% relative difference (adjusted IRR 0.85, 95% CI 0.69-1.04; p=0.11). During the second IRS period (January-December 2024) with pirimiphos-methyl, the intervention arm showed a 79.3% decline to 149.1 per 1,000 person-years with a 73% relative reduction compared to control (adjusted IRR 0.27, 95% CI 0.22-0.34; p<0.001).

articles.headline: Pyrazole inhibitors show activity against Plasmodium falciparum DHORDH enzyme
articles.title_original: Structure-based design of new pyrazole inhibitors targeting Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH).
articles.authors: Pippione AC, Villella N, Fornaseri F, Giorgis M, Sharma M, Rolando B, Sainas S, Salamanca E, Gimenez A, Cojean S, Vyas VK, Buccinnà B, Ramondetti C, Piccinini M, Poli G, Tuccinardi T, Alberti M, Miggiano R, Lolli ML, Boschi D
articles.details: This study applied structure-based optimization to hydroxypyrazole compounds to design selective P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH) inhibitors through three iterative rounds of rational design. The best compounds (derivatives 6, 7, 12, 13, and 22) exhibited enzymatic inhibition with IC50 values of 1.3-5.9 μM, favorable physicochemical properties (PBS solubility 0.03-2.8 mM, logD7.4 0.25-1.7), and antiparasitic activity in P. falciparum 3D7 cultures (EC50 15-22 μM) with negligible cytotoxicity (CC50 > 150 μM except compound 22). Selectivity over human dihydroorotate dehydrogenase exceeded 30-fold for several analogs.

articles.headline: Anti-PfSyn5 antibodies block malaria transmission potently
articles.title_original: Disruption of the AgMOBP1-PfSyn5 interface abolishes malaria transmission.
articles.authors: Ramelow J, Niu G, Kostallas L, Lai L, Wang X, Li J
articles.details: The study identified AgMOBP1, a mosquito midgut protein that promotes P. falciparum transmission through interaction with parasite protein PfSyn5. Anti-PfSyn5 antibodies showed complete inhibition at 1 μg/mL with IC50 ~3 ng/mL (~20 pM), approximately one million-fold lower than total IgG concentration in normal human plasma. The AgMOBP1-PfSyn5 axis is proposed as a target for transmission-blocking vaccines and monoclonal antibodies.

articles.headline: Calcifer protects Plasmodium basal body from mechanical stress
articles.title_original: Organelle mechanics: A brace for the Plasmodium basal body.
articles.authors: Engstler M
articles.details: Calcifer is identified as a late-recruited stabilizer that protects the Plasmodium basal body from mechanical stress during male gamete formation, revealing an evolutionary adaptation to biomechanical challenges during parasite transmission to mosquitoes.

articles.headline: P. vivax dominance in twelve-year Bishoftu malaria surveillance
articles.title_original: The persistent threat of malaria in Bishoftu Town: twelve-year trends and their implications for Addis Ababa.
articles.authors: Dufera BC, Mamo H
articles.details: Analysis of 252,610 blood films from 2011–2022 identified 5,696 (2.3%) malaria cases, with P. vivax at 63.8% and P. falciparum at 31.0%. The highest incidence of 804 cases occurred in 2017, with individuals >14 years constituting 66.3% of cases. Findings indicate persistent malaria with fluctuating trends, highlighting need for aggressive surveillance and control measures.

articles.headline: Invasive An. stephensi detected south of Equator in Kenya
articles.title_original: Serendipitous detection of invasive malaria vector Anopheles stephensi in Kisumu, Kenya in June 2022.
articles.authors: Ndenga BA, Owuor KO, Wambua S, Bartilol B, Maia M, Mwangangi J, Omukuti R, Chemutai S, Arabu D, Miringu I, Bosire C, Mwendwa K, Winter CA, Rono MK, Mutuku FM, Taylor R, Bisanzio D, LaBeaud AD, Gerken KN
articles.details: Molecular analysis of mosquitoes collected from an urban slaughterhouse in mid-2022 confirmed Anopheles stephensi via mitochondrial cox1 gene sequencing. Among 56 mosquitoes, 19.6% (11) of amplicon sequence variants matched mosquito species, including one pool morphologically classified as An. gambiae. This represents the first report of An. stephensi south of the Equator, with haplotypes clustering with populations from northern Kenya, Ethiopia, and Sudan.

articles.headline: Berzosertib predicted effective at 75 mg daily for malaria
articles.title_original: Repurposing Drugs for Malaria through a Human Dose Prediction: A Case Study with Berzosertib.
articles.authors: Redhi D, Mulubwa M, Mmonwa MM, Njoroge M, Krugmann L, Chibale K
