Data extraction includes study characteristics with thematic analysis of extracted data. Results from review not yet available.

articles.headline: High-throughput screening identifies nematocidal chemotypes from antimalarial library
articles.title_original: High-throughput phenotypic screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture's Hit Generation Library 1 identifies new nematocidal chemotypes.
articles.authors: Taki AC, Byrne JJ, Chang BCH, Laleu B, Wells TNC, Jabbar A, Gasser RB
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Large-scale phenotypic screen of 139,916 compounds (98.7% of Medicines for Malaria Venture's Hit Generation Library 1) tested against third-stage larvae of Haemonchus contortus using infrared-based motility and developmental assays. Identified 272 primary hits (0.194%) with 110 confirmed active compounds; 39 showed IC50 < 10 μM and 33 caused complete developmental arrest at ≤12.5 μM. Four compounds were non-toxic to HepG2 cells (CC50 ≥20 μM, MC50 ≥20 μM) with favorable physicochemical properties; 16 met criteria for medicinal-chemistry progression.

articles.headline: P. vivax gametocytes in bone marrow: case of pancytopenia with splenomegaly
articles.title_original: A mysterious foe in a case of pancytopenia with splenomegaly: Plasmodium vivax gametocytes in the bone marrow.
articles.authors: Patel GR, Singh V
articles.details: Case report of pancytopenia with splenomegaly where Plasmodium vivax gametocytes were found in the bone marrow. Limited clinical details available from title alone.

articles.headline: Gut microbiome composition predicts malaria parasitemia in macaques and humans
articles.title_original: Gut check: converging evidence links microbiome to malaria risk.
articles.authors: Berry AA
articles.details: Commentary on research demonstrating pre-infection gut microbiome composition predicts level of Plasmodium parasitemia in both rhesus macaques and human volunteers. Results from Gustin et al. study identify Bifidobacterium as a potentially protective genus. Findings suggest the gut microbiome is an underappreciated determinant of malaria parasite burden beyond host genetics and immunity.

articles.headline: Molecular mechanisms of var gene switching in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Mechanistic insights into coordinated var transcriptional switching in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Visone JE, Florini F, Hadjimichael E, Patel V, Deitsch KW
articles.details: Investigation of coordinated var transcriptional switching in P. falciparum, which enables parasites to avoid antibody recognition through antigenic variation of the hypervariable surface protein PfEMP1. Demonstrates var2csa locus transient activation mediates var switching, while non-coding RNAs from var2csa contribute to its repression and affect switching frequencies. An upstream open reading frame in the 5'-UTR of var2csa transcript destabilizes mRNA through nonsense-mediated RNA decay pathway, promoting transcriptional activation of alternative var genes.

articles.headline: Single drugs with multiple targets offer combination-therapy-like antimalarial efficacy
articles.title_original: Polypharmacology in malaria treatment: single drugs, multiple mechanisms, greater impact.
articles.authors: Milić M, Obi P, Vanderwal CD, Ben Mamoun C, Le Roch KG
articles.details: Review of polypharmacology strategy in antimalarial drug design, where single compounds attack parasites through multiple mechanisms of action simultaneously, reducing resistance development likelihood. Discusses natural-product-derived drugs (quinine, artemisinin) distinguished by multitarget mechanisms and examines opportunities and challenges in developing next-generation antimalarials using this approach. Concluding that understanding and harnessing polypharmacology in antimalarial design could prolong therapeutic durability and reinvigorate the therapeutic arsenal.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistance markers detected in P. falciparum from Brazilian tri-border region
articles.title_original: Emergence of artemisinin-based combination therapy resistance markers in Plasmodium falciparum from the Brazilian tri-border region of the Guiana Shield.
articles.authors: Salazar YEAR, Rezende AM, Puça MCSB, Louzada J, Mascarenhas MEP, Lagström S, Fletcher D, Oliveira-Ferreira J, Gil JP, de Sousa TN
articles.details: Analysis of 99 P. falciparum isolates from Roraima State, northern Brazil (2016-2020) using targeted deep sequencing assessed variants in pfcrt, pfmdr1, pfk13, and copy number variation in pfpm2/pfpm3. Results: pfmdr1-NFD haplotype 6% (6/99), pfcrt-C350R 7% (7/99), pfpm2 amplifications 5% (5/94), pfpm3 amplifications 6% (6/97), pfpm2/3 hybrid 4% (4/99). No validated or candidate pfk13 mutations associated with artemisinin partial resistance detected. Variants suggest reduced susceptibility to lumefantrine and piperaquine.

articles.headline: Anopheles gut microbiome: mechanisms and limitations for malaria control
articles.title_original: Exploring the gut micriobiome in Anopheles mosquitoes: A promising new approach to malaria control.
articles.authors: Minwuyelet A, Atenafu G
articles.details: Critical review evaluating Anopheles mosquito gut microbiome as a complementary malaria control strategy, examining bacterial, fungal, and viral components. Reviews proposed mechanisms including immune modulation, nutrient competition, direct antiparasitic activity, and interference with mosquito physiology to inhibit Plasmodium transmission. Identifies key limitations: laboratory bias, oversimplification of microbial consortia, context-dependent outcomes, and ecological uncertainties that challenge field deployment. Concludes strength, consistency, and translational relevance of current microbiome evidence remain uneven.

articles.headline: Two VAR2CSA lineages circulate in Thailand P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Two divergent VAR2CSA lineages with distinct evolutionary signatures circulate in Plasmodium falciparum from Thailand.
articles.authors: Chaikitgosiyakul S, Kuamsab N, Tia T, Putaporntip C, Jongwutiwes S
articles.details: Full-length var2csa sequences were analyzed from 31 Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Thailand, revealing 24 distinct haplotypes with high diversity (h = 0.97 ± 0.02). Two major lineages co-circulate: a dominant 3D7-type and a distinct Dd2-type (7 isolates). Nucleotide diversity was significantly higher in the 3' region (π = 0.4253) compared to the 5' region (π = 0.1033, p < 0.0001), driven by pronounced divergence between lineages (Dxy = 0.506, Da = 0.444).

articles.headline: Novel bisquinoline antimalarials overcome drug resistance
articles.title_original: Designing novel bisquinoline antimalarials from historical 4-aminoquinolines to combat drug-resistant malaria.
articles.authors: Handford MJ, Li Y, Riscoe T, Zhang X, Kelly JX, Riscoe MK
articles.details: A structure-activity relationship study optimized bisquinoline analogs with 4-anilinoquinoline core features. Multiple compounds showed potent in vitro activity (IC50 < 20 nM) against both drug-sensitive and multidrug-resistant P. falciparum. Lead compound 25 demonstrated improved metabolic stability (t1/2 = 121 min) and potent in vivo efficacy (ED50 = 0.32 mg/kg/day) with complete curative protection at reduced dose versus ADC-028, though with lower bioavailability (F = 43%) and shorter half-life (T1/2 = 27.2 h).

articles.headline: 5-ALA does not potentiate dihydroartemisinin
articles.title_original: 5-ALA does not potentiate dihydroartemisinin against Plasmodium falciparum malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Behrens HM, Henshall IG, Spielmann T
articles.details: A correspondence notes that 5-aminolaevulinic acid (5-ALA), which potentiates artemisinin in cancer research, does not enhance activity of dihydroartemisinin against P. falciparum parasites. The authors conclude that co-administration of artemisinin and 5-ALA is not a promising treatment strategy for malaria.

articles.headline: Digestive enzyme genomics in neotropical malaria vector
articles.title_original: Genomic profiling of digestion related enzymes in Anopheles aquasalis a major coastal neotropical malaria vector.
articles.authors: Alencar RM, Sepulveda CCP, D'Elia GMA, de Souza IB, Duarte APM, Santana RA, Nacif-Pimenta R, Godoy RSM, de Melo GC, Monteiro WM, Secundino NFC, Koerich LB, Pimenta PFP
articles.details: Genomic analysis of Anopheles aquasalis identified 241 digestion-related genes: 56 for carbohydrate, 51 for lipid, and 134 for protein digestion. Phylogenetic analysis revealed genes closely related to other neotropical anophelines. Key findings include conservation of genes relevant for vector control, including larvicidal toxin receptors, alpha-amylase, alpha-glucosidase, maltase, aminopeptidase N1, carboxypeptidase B, and serine proteases, which could support vector control and transmission-blocking vaccine development.

articles.headline: pfhrp2/3 deletions affect RDT performance in travelers
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum histidine rich protein 2/3 gene deletions affecting rapid diagnostic test performance in travellers.
articles.authors: Detzlhofer O, Lötsch F, Kirchner J, Schneider R, Weiss-Tessbach M, Winkler S, Walochnik J, Schubert L
articles.details: Among 115 travelers with malaria, pfhrp2/3 gene deletions were detected in 4 (3.5%), with no double deletions observed. Notably, 51.3% had visited countries projected to exceed the WHO 5% threshold for pfhrp2/3 deletions by 2028. The authors recommend continued monitoring of these deletions in non-endemic settings to guide testing strategies.

articles.headline: Malaria burden in sub-Saharan African refugees in Sweden
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum infections and number needed to screen among refugees arriving from sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Bergstrand T, Wyss K, Eliasson I, Kaldhusdal V, Requena-Méndez A, Färnert A
articles.details: Using Malaria Atlas Project prevalence data and demographic information, researchers estimated that 3930 (7%) of 56,248 sub-Saharan African refugees arriving to Sweden during 2015-22 harbored P. falciparum infections. This included 1789 children and 2141 adults (851 women of reproductive age). Approximately 491 asymptomatic infections were estimated to arrive yearly (range 356-546), predominantly from high/moderate-transmission countries, with a number needed to screen of 2-14.

articles.headline: Transformer-based deep learning for malaria cell classification
articles.title_original: ISTNet: a multi-scale transformer-based architecture for malaria cell classification.
articles.authors: Wu P, Gao X, Yu H
articles.details: ISTNet is a multi-scale transformer-based deep learning architecture designed for automated classification of malaria parasites in cell images. The model applies state-of-the-art computer vision techniques to improve diagnostic accuracy through automated microscopy-based parasite detection.

articles.headline: Correction: Endothelial adhesion receptors in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Correction to: Mapping the expression of endothelial adhesion receptors for Plasmodium falciparum-infected erythrocytes in fatal cerebral malaria in Malawian children.
articles.details: This is a correction to a study investigating the expression of endothelial adhesion receptors for P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes in fatal cerebral malaria in children. The study examined endothelial-parasite interactions relevant to severe malaria pathophysiology.

articles.headline: Piperaquine-resistance mutations spreading in Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Changing genetic profiles of Plasmodium falciparum piperaquine resistance in Southeast Asia over 25 years.
articles.authors: Srisutham S, Suwannasin K, Saejeng A, Khantikul N, Sugaram R, Phyo AP, Proux S, Pongvongsa T, Nguon C, Tripura R, Chau NH, Lek D, Dang Trung NH, Peto TJ, Callery JJ, van der Pluijm RW, Amaratunga C, Dhorda M, von Seidlein L, Mayxay M, Thuy-Nhien NT, Newton PN, Day NPJ, Ashley EA, Nosten FH, Smithuis FM, White NJ, Dondorp AM, Imwong M
articles.details: Analysis of 898 P. falciparum isolates from Thailand, Myanmar, Cambodia, Laos, and Vietnam (1995-2023) tracked pfcrt mutations and pfplasmepsin2 gene amplifications linked to piperaquine resistance. Specific mutations varied geographically: Cambodia H97Y (15%) and T93S (8%); Thailand I218F (24.44%) and G353V (20%); Vietnam T93S (21%). Over 65% of parasites with pfcrt mutations also harbored pfplasmepsin2 amplification. Temporal analysis revealed pfplasmepsin2 amplification peaked at 88% in Cambodia in 2015, with microsatellite analysis indicating selective sweeps of resistant alleles.

articles.headline: Malaria in pregnancy: pathogenesis and intervention challenges
articles.title_original: Malaria in pregnancy at the frontline: a delicate balance.
articles.authors: Kokuhennadige V, Aitken E, Rogerson S
articles.details: This review synthesizes findings on malaria in pregnancy, examining placental pathology and maternal/fetal outcomes. Placental inflammation from Plasmodium infection causes maternal morbidity and adverse birth outcomes, with antenatal infection affecting infant immune and neurocognitive development. While sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine remains effective for improving birth weight despite parasite resistance, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, though more potent against parasitemia, does not improve birth outcomes when used alone or combined with SP.

articles.headline: Blood group and α-globin variants affect vivax malaria risk
articles.title_original: Genetic Determinants for Vivax Malaria Susceptibility in Chiplun (Ratnagiri), Maharashtra, India: A Case-Control Study.
articles.authors: Roshan S, Kanjaksha G, Arti S, Kanchan M, Jyoti J, Shivam S, Ajit G
articles.details: A case-control study in Maharashtra, India compared 228 malaria cases with 2149 controls to identify genetic risk factors. Blood group B+ was associated with increased malaria susceptibility (OR = 1.36, 95% CI: 1.02-1.80, p < 0.05), while A+ appeared protective (OR = 0.72, 95% CI: 0.52-0.99, p < 0.05). Notably, individuals with homozygous-α3·7 deletions showed significantly higher susceptibility (OR = 3.33, 95% CI: 2.00-5.53, p < 0.001). No associations were found with Rh phenotypes, CR1 polymorphisms, or Gerbich variants.

articles.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 applications in malaria drug-resistance research
articles.title_original: Innovative approach in malaria research: Harnessing CRISPR-Cas9 for antimalarial drug-resistance studies in Africa.
articles.authors: Ajibaye O, Dada IS, Mbye H, Nwankwo IC, Oriero E, Amambua-Ngwa A
articles.details: A comprehensive review examines CRISPR-Cas9 applications in antimalarial drug-resistance research, with emphasis on African studies. The technology is discussed for elucidating resistance pathways, validating molecular markers, and improving diagnostic strategies. A systematic scoping review reveals significantly low adoption of CRISPR-based malaria research across Africa. Despite technological and regulatory barriers, the authors conclude that CRISPR holds strong potential for accelerating antimalarial drug discovery and advancing precision medicine approaches.

articles.headline: Monocyte-lymphocyte ratio marks malaria infection in pregnancy
articles.title_original: The clinical significance of monocyte-to-lymphocyte and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratios in malaria infection and parasitaemia among pregnant women in Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Traore O, Bihoun B, Koné FT, Lengane AL, Tahita MC, Kere AR, Bonko MDA, Compaore ZMJ, Dabone KCS, Yaro APK, Zango SH, Kaboré B, Kiemde F, Bouda I, Rouamba T, Ilboudo H, Natama HM, Sorgho H, Valéa I, Traoré-Coulibaly M, Belem AMG, Tinto H
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: In 270 third-trimester pregnant women in Burkina Faso screened for P. falciparum by microscopy and RDT, monocyte-to-lymphocyte ratio (MLR) was significantly higher in malaria-infected women (0.48 vs 0.40; p=0.021) and in those with high parasitaemia (OR=7.16). MLR yielded ROC area of 0.60 for detecting infection and 0.61 for detecting parasitaemia. The authors conclude MLR and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio may serve as supportive diagnostic markers for malaria in pregnancy, though further validation is needed.

articles.headline: Ferroptosis inhibition alleviates experimental cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Ferrostatin-1 alleviates experimental cerebral malaria by regulating immune cell functions and brain endothelial ferroptosis.
articles.authors: Yao S, Zhou X, Liao T, Yao C, Sun M, Gou H, Hu X, Liu J, Zheng L, Zhao Y, Cao Y
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: In a murine P. berghei ANKA cerebral malaria model, ferrostatin-1 (Fer-1), a ferroptosis inhibitor, reduced parasitemia, extended survival, and improved blood-brain barrier integrity by suppressing lipid peroxidation. Mechanistically, Fer-1 enhanced dendritic cell antigen presentation (elevated MHC II, CD80/86), promoted M1 macrophage polarization, increased IFN-γ+ Th1 cells, and reduced cerebral chemokines (CXCL9/CXCL10) and adhesion molecules (ICAM-1/VCAM-1). The authors propose ferroptosis-targeted strategies as a novel approach to prevent and treat cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Indole alkaloids computationally screened as 3-HKT enzyme inhibitors
articles.title_original: Computational evaluation of indole alkaloids as inhibitors of 3-hydroxykynurenine transaminase in malaria vector control.
articles.authors: Makaye AI, Kwiyukwa LP, Paul L, Banyikwa A, Vuai SAH
articles.details: Computational virtual screening, molecular dynamics simulations, and MMPBSA analysis evaluated indole alkaloids as inhibitors of 3-hydroxykynurenine transaminase (3-HKT), required for parasite gametogenesis in Anopheles mosquitoes. Spiroindolone (EP4) and asperpenazine (SP4) demonstrated the strongest binding affinities and stable interactions with 3-HKT (MMPBSA binding energies: EP4 -69.658 and -77.025 kJ/mol; SP4 -68.658 and -75.947 kJ/mol). The authors suggest EP4 and SP4 as potential candidates for antimosquito insecticide design, pending in vitro and in vivo validation.

articles.headline: Salivary gland invasion key target for genetic malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Genetic control of malaria transmission: a salivary gland-centric perspective.
articles.authors: Eze S, Nguyen VT, Morvay A, Lee Y, Burini BC
articles.details: This review examines the complex molecular mechanisms by which Plasmodium sporozoites recognize and invade mosquito salivary glands during parasite development within Anopheles vectors. The authors emphasize that advancing knowledge of this critical developmental stage is instrumental for designing effective transgenes for genetic control strategies, particularly mosquito population replacement approaches. Understanding the precise molecular interactions at the salivary gland interface remains an important gap for malaria vector control innovation.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic P. falciparum common in wet season, underdetected by microscopy
articles.title_original: Longitudinal Assessment of Asymptomatic Malaria Infections in a Suburban Community in Lagos, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Owoloye A, Ligali FC, Musa AZ, Aina O, Idowu ET, Oyebola KM
articles.details: Among 1,860 asymptomatic individuals screened, PCR detected 11.7% (218/1,860) P. falciparum infection, compared to 8.8% by RDT and 2.7% by microscopy. Asymptomatic malaria peaked in June at 36.0% during the wet season (seasonal prevalence 14.9% wet vs 7.2% dry). Prevalence was highest in school-age children (5–14 years, 14.1%) and males (12.9% vs 10.7% females). The authors conclude asymptomatic malaria is a major transmission reservoir requiring year-round surveillance, with particular focus on younger individuals during the wet season.

articles.headline: Three parasitic lipases required for fatty acid scavenging from lysophosphatidylcholine
articles.title_original: Defining the minimal enzymatic requirements for fatty acid scavenging from lysophosphatidylcholine by erythrocytic Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Liu J, Fike KR, Dalal S, Dapper C, Klemba M
articles.details: Using knockout and ligand-dependent knockdown approaches in P. falciparum, three parasite lysophospholipases were identified as essential for fatty acid scavenging from host lysophosphatidylcholine (LPC): exported lipase 2 (XL2), exported lipase homolog 4 (XLH4), and prodrug activation and resistance esterase (PARE). PARE/XL2/XLH4-deficient parasites could not complete replication in defined media with LPC as the sole source of exogenous fatty acids. The authors demonstrate that P. falciparum is dependent on parasite-encoded enzymes for LPC catabolism, with host erythrocyte enzymes not playing a physiologically relevant role.

articles.headline: Five FDA drugs computationally identified as potential PfEMP1 inhibitors
articles.title_original: Computational Drug Repositioning for Targeting PfEMP1: Potential Therapeutics for Cerebral Malaria in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Verma K, Patel K, Yadav A, Gopikrishnan M, Sharma R, Mohan M, Mani A, Bharti PK
articles.details: Using computational screening, molecular dynamics simulations, and ADME analysis, Lumacaftor, Vilazodone, Tucatinib, Lenvatinib, and Hydrocortisone Cypionate were identified as FDA-approved drugs with favorable docking energies (–9.1 to –8.3 kcal/mol) against P. falciparum erythrocyte membrane protein 1 (PfEMP1). Molecular dynamics simulations confirmed stable interactions with favorable potential energy profiles and structural stability. The authors propose these computational approaches as valuable for identifying and repurposing drugs to target PfEMP1-mediated sequestration in cerebral malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: High acceptance for maternal malaria vaccine among Malawian women
articles.title_original: Assessing Readiness for Future Maternal Malaria Vaccines: Knowledge, Practices, and Vaccine Attitudes Among Women of Reproductive Age in Malawi.
articles.authors: Kaur M, D'Alessio F, Kajombo MC, Nkolokosa M, Olesen OF
articles.details: A facility-based cross-sectional survey of women of reproductive age in Malawi found general malaria knowledge and preventive practices to be high, with 53.4% reporting fever during pregnancy. Knowledge and perceived risk awareness were significantly lower in Mpemba compared to Thyolo (aOR 0.30, 95% CI 0.16–0.57 and aOR 0.35, 95% CI 0.18–0.68, respectively). Despite 92% acknowledging possible vaccine harm, 97% reported willingness to accept a future maternal malaria vaccine. The authors conclude vaccine acceptability is high, though context-specific delivery strategies could optimize district-level introduction.

articles.headline: Humidity shapes thermal niche and geographic range of Anopheles stephensi
articles.title_original: Humidity shapes the thermal niche of Anopheles stephensi, an invasive malaria vector.
articles.authors: Johnson B, Huxley PJ, Brown JJ, Hollingsworth BD, Bump ER, Laurent BS, Skrotzki J, Johnson LR, Pascual M, Wimberly MC, Mohanty A, Murdock CC
articles.details: A factorial life-table experiment across five humidities (30–90%) and seven temperatures (16–38°C) demonstrated that humidity markedly modifies the thermal performance of fitness traits in the invasive malaria vector Anopheles stephensi. When integrated into demographic models, humidity suppressed population growth in hot, arid regions while enhancing fitness in humid or high-elevation climates of South Asia and Africa, dramatically altering projections compared to temperature-only models. The authors emphasize the need to integrate multiple environmental drivers to accurately project climate-driven shifts in vector distributions.

articles.headline: Acyl carrier protein essential for apicoplast biogenesis independent of fatty acid synthesis
articles.title_original: Acyl Carrier Protein is Essential for Apicoplast Biogenesis in Malaria Parasites Independent of Fatty Acid Synthesis.
articles.authors: Geher SWR, Falekun S, Pita-Aquino JN, Swift RP, Okada M, Jami-Alahmadi Y, Wohlschlegel JA, Prigge ST, Sigala PA
articles.details: Knockout or knockdown of apicoplast acyl carrier protein (ACP) in blood-stage P. falciparum was lethal, indicating an essential function beyond the canonical role in type-II fatty acid synthesis. ACP loss impaired isoprenoid biosynthesis and blocked apicoplast biogenesis by destabilizing pyruvate kinase II (PKII), the only known source of nucleoside triphosphates in the apicoplast. Using proximity biotinylation and biochemical studies, the authors reveal ACP functions as a critical hub in apicoplast metabolism by stabilizing PKII, independent of fatty acid synthesis.

articles.headline: Adaptive entomological surveillance improves vector monitoring efficiency
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the Entomological Adaptive Surveillance Framework for malaria vector monitoring: a comparative field trial with routine surveillance in Ghana and Mozambique.
articles.authors: Sedda L, Jalilian A, Opiyo M, Gowelo S, Marrenjo DJ, Atta-Obeng C, Boampong E, Oppong SK, Owusu-Akrofi O, Thomsen E, Tatarsky A, Malm KL, Candrinho B, Lobo NF
articles.details: A comparative field trial in Ghana and Mozambique evaluated an entomological adaptive surveillance framework (EASF) using Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling to strategically select sampling locations, versus routine surveillance. EASF outperformed routine surveillance in mosquito catch rates, model predictive accuracy, and reduction in uncertainty, with standardized EASF sites yielding higher catches and more stable predictions (lower coefficients of variation). EASF identified proportionally fewer obsolete sampling locations than routine surveillance, and performance benefits were consistent though varying by country. The authors conclude adaptive surveillance offers an evidence-based approach to improving surveillance efficiency.

articles.headline: Malaria-STH coinfection causes early macrocytic anemia in Nigerian children
articles.title_original: Haematological responses in children under 5 years with malaria and soil-transmitted helminth coinfections in Delta State, Nigeria: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Odibo EO, Nmorsi OPG, Egwunyenga OA, Mgbere O
articles.details: In 269 children under 5 years in Nigeria, 40.9% of toddlers (1–2 years) had malaria-STH coinfection, more frequent in males. Malaria and STH infections, especially coinfection, produced macrocytic hypochromic changes with elevated mean corpuscular volume and reduced mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (p<0.001), despite only mild non-significant reductions in hemoglobin and RBC counts. Parasitemia varied with age (p=0.033) but showed weak correlations with hemoglobin, RBC, and packed cell volume, suggesting anaemia was influenced more by host factors than by parasite burden. Children with Hb SS genotype showed the greatest haematological derangement.

articles.headline: High IPTp-SP missed opportunities among Nigerian pregnant women
articles.title_original: Prevalence and inequalities of missed opportunities for intermittent preventive therapy (IPTp) of malaria among pregnant women: an analysis of 2018 Nigeria DHS.
articles.authors: Ladan SM, Lima NP, Orimadegun AE, Wado YD, Akinyemi JO, Jatau AA, Blumenberg C
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 2018 Nigeria DHS data on 12,498 women (15-49 years). Among women with ≥4 antenatal care visits, 75% received <3 IPTp-SP doses (42.5% of sample). Worst-case scenario prevalence (<4 ANC, <3 IPTp-SP) was 44.7% in 40-49 age group, 50.4% rural residents, 61.6% with no education, and 64.0% in poorest wealth quintile. Poorest uneducated women had 75% probability of worst-case versus 5% in wealthiest educated women.

articles.headline: Helminth coinfection associated with milder malaria in children
articles.title_original: Helminth coinfections mitigate clinical, parasitological, and immune outcomes in Mozambican children with malaria.
articles.authors: Cuamba I, Santano R, Grau-Pujol B, Vidal M, Aguilar R, Cossa A, Jairoce C, Mejia R, Nhabomba A, Muñoz J, Moncunill G, Dobaño C
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 441 malaria-symptomatic children (2-10 years) in Mozambique. Among 74 with P. falciparum malaria, 22% (16) had soil-transmitted helminth coinfection. STH-coinfected children had lower P. falciparum density (P=0.005), lower fever (P=0.047), and downregulated pro-inflammatory cytokines. Elevated antibody responses to specific P. falciparum antigens (SSP2, PTRAMP, MSP3, RH5, α-gal) and total IgE in coinfected children (P<0.05).

articles.headline: Community health volunteers expand malaria diagnosis and treatment access
articles.title_original: Community health volunteers improve access to malaria case management in Siaya County, Kenya.
articles.authors: Odongo W, Seffren V, Obiet K, Seda B, Towett O, Zhou H, McDermott DP, Kariuki S, Staedke SG, Ter Kuile F, Kwambai TK, Samuels AM, Gutman JR
articles.details: Analysis of 1.4 million encounters from 936 community health volunteers across 713 villages (2021-2023). CHV coverage was adequate for 82.5% of villages versus 30% for health facilities. CHVs managed 28.1% of encounters including 12.1% of suspected malaria cases and 15.6% of RDT-positive cases. CHVs recorded higher test positivity rates (82.0% vs 64.3%). Incorporating CHV data increased malaria incidence estimates by 18% (467 vs 549 per 1,000).

articles.headline: ITNs and IRS reduce malaria risk in Zambian under-five children
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with malaria amongst under-five children in Senga Hill District, Northern Province, Zambia, a community-based cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Shaba-Santu A, Moonga L, Ogata S, Hayashida K, Simuunza MC
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional study of 216 under-five children in Senga Hill District (November 2022-August 2023). Overall malaria prevalence 31% (95% CI 26-38%). Correct ITN hanging reduced odds of malaria by 88% (OR=0.012, 95% CI 0.005-0.304). Two ITNs and indoor residual spraying reduced odds by 86% (OR=0.14) and 58% (OR=0.42) respectively. Male children had 204% increased odds (OR=3.04, 95% CI 1.52-6.07).

articles.headline: Ivermectin shows mosquito mortality but limited malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Efficacy of ivermectin for malaria vector control: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials.
articles.authors: Ramadan S, Talkhan A, Mansour A, Kanaan D, Abbas AN, Awad A, Sahel OB, Hamzeh LH, Alfaki A, Hany M, Elnouty M, Amin MHJ
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 10 randomized trials (63,192 participants) evaluating ivermectin versus placebo/standard care. Community-level analyses showed no significant reduction in malaria prevalence. Entomological studies demonstrated increased mosquito mortality (RR 1.89; 95% CI 1.26-2.83). Ivermectin was generally well tolerated with no significant increase in adverse events. Current evidence does not demonstrate consistent reductions in malaria incidence or prevalence.

articles.headline: Guinea health workers identify barriers to malaria diagnosis and treatment
articles.title_original: Healthcare providers' knowledge, perceptions and practices of malaria control strategies in the Republic of Guinea: insights from a qualitative study.
articles.authors: Diallo EM, Langlet A, Onyango LA, Kourouma M, Kambou BM, Diallo FB, Bufferne A, Petrova A, Deyra M, Gay C, Camara A, Gerbaud L
articles.details: Qualitative study (December 2022-March 2023) across 9 health districts in Guinea with 63 individual interviews and 18 focus groups. Identified 264 modalities grouped into 45 subthemes and 9 themes. Participants reported high knowledge of malaria but gaps in insecticide-treated net distribution, use, and larval destruction, and delayed seasonal malaria chemoprevention. Non-compliant practices included overcharging, over-reliance on clinical diagnosis, and provider stigma.

articles.headline: 2La inversion drives Anopheles adaptation across Nigeria's ecological zones
articles.title_original: Spatial distribution of the 2La chromosomal inversion in Anopheles gambiae populations across Nigeria: ecological associations and implications for malaria control.
articles.authors: Adeogun AO, Izekor RT, Babalola AS, Oyeniyi TA, Adetunji O, Adesoye OA, Wahedi J, Busari LO, George A, Ajayi S, Chabi J, Awolola ST
articles.details: Study of 1200 Anopheles mosquitoes across 12 Nigerian states; 999 were An. gambiae s.l. (39.7% An. gambiae s.s., 51.5% An. coluzzii, 8.7% An. arabiensis). Of 399 An. gambiae s.s. karyotyped, distinct south-north cline in 2La inversion frequency observed: 2La/2La homokaryotype dominated arid northern zones, 2La+/2La+ dominant in humid southern forests. Linear regression confirmed significant correlation between inversion frequency and latitude (R2=0.68-0.73, P<0.001).

articles.headline: Microfluidic device quantifies P. falciparum invasion under blood flow
articles.title_original: Microfluidic platform for automatic quantification of malaria parasite invasion under physiological flow conditions.
articles.authors: Kals E, Kals M, Introini V, Vodenicharski B, Kotar J, Rayner JC, Cicuta P
articles.details: Experimental study developed microfluidic device with four channels simulating post-capillary venule dimensions at different flow velocities. Synchronized P. falciparum parasites injected; egress and invasion rates quantified using custom automated video analysis. Deletion of erythrocyte binding antigen 175 (PfEBA175) significantly impacted invasion under flow (P<0.05) but not in static culture. Flow conditions critically affect parasite invasion in genotype-dependent manner.

articles.headline: TLR adjuvants evoke durable antibody responses to liver stage antigens
articles.title_original: Poly(I:C) and R848 adjuvants elicit sizeable humoral immunity to liver stage malaria antigens.
articles.authors: Tandel N, Thakkar M, Ahuja M, Shah J, Trivedi DD, Pied S, Singh AP, Prajapati C, Tyagi RK, Dalai SK
articles.details: Immunization study in BALB/c and C57BL/6 mice with liver stage antigens PfCSP and PbSLTRiP with or without dual toll-like receptor adjuvants (Poly(I:C) and R848). Coadministration of adjuvants evoked greater antibody responses than antigen alone. PfCSP-specific IgG remained detectable through 80 days post-immunization with gradual decline. PbSLTRiP produced progressive increases in total IgG and IgG2c responses.

articles.headline: Acute kidney injury drives mortality in severe malaria complications
articles.title_original: Malaria-Associated Acute Kidney Injury: A Key Driver of Mortality in Endemic Regions.
articles.authors: Olwit G, Gutanamuka TB, Chris-Uchendu HC, Mugisha AI
articles.details: Narrative review (2015-2025) on malaria-associated acute kidney injury (MAKI) epidemiology, pathophysiology, and outcomes. MAKI results from intravascular hemolysis, microvascular obstruction, inflammation, endothelial dysfunction, and volume depletion. P. falciparum most commonly implicated, though P. vivax and P. knowlesi increasingly recognized. MAKI associated with prolonged hospitalization, increased renal replacement therapy need, and higher mortality. Survivors at risk of chronic kidney disease progression.

articles.headline: School-aged children harbor high asymptomatic P. falciparum carriage
articles.title_original: School-aged children as a silent reservoir of Plasmodium falciparum: findings from a cross-sectional survey conducted in Burkina Faso in 2022.
articles.authors: Tarama CW, Soré H, Yade MS, Niyomwungere D, Débé S, Kinda R, Ganou A, Ménard D, Gansané A
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey in July 2022 across three districts in Burkina Faso of 1,127 children (6 months-10 years). Asymptomatic P. falciparum prevalence 28.0% by RDT, 31.9% by microscopy. School-aged children (5-10 years) 66.5% tested positive by microscopy versus 21.6% in children <5 years. Gametocyte carriage 3.9% overall, twice as high in school-aged children (67 vs 32 gametocytes/μL). Microscopy sensitivity 76.7% versus RDT 67.7%.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi malaria affects forest workers in Aceh, Indonesia
articles.title_original: Socio-demographic characteristics and parasite density patterns of Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria in Aceh, Indonesia: insights from hospital-based data.
articles.authors: Maulana T, Marthoenis M, Almira CS, Moe S, Abas AL, Htay MNN
articles.details: Retrospective hospital-based study (January 2023-April 2024) in Aceh Besar, Indonesia of 40 confirmed P. knowlesi patients. Median age 31 years (IQR 25-43). Largest occupational group farmers and plantation workers (67.5%), followed by orangutan caretakers (10%). All presented with fever and headache, treated with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and primaquine with complete recovery. Among 35 with parasite density data: 42.9% high, 45.7% moderate, 11.4% low. Median hospital stay 3 days.

articles.headline: PfVFT1 antibodies inhibit P. falciparum through multiple immune mechanisms
articles.title_original: Vaccine-induced mouse antibodies targeting Plasmodium falciparum PfVFT antigen inhibit blood stages through multiple mechanisms.
articles.authors: Goh YS, Mao H, Hor PX, Loh CY, Chang ZW, Hermsen CC, Nosten F, Sauerwein RW, Rénia L
articles.details: Study screened sera from individuals immunized with P. falciparum sporozoites under drug cover. PfVFT1 was found in all protected individuals. Vaccine-induced mouse anti-PfVFT sera inhibited parasite reinvasion into RBCs, promoted complement deposition inducing parasite lysis, and supported phagocytosis and antibody-dependent cellular inhibition.

articles.headline: Plasmodium kinases regulate male gametogenesis organelle organization
articles.title_original: Correction: Divergent Plasmodium kinases drive MTOC, kinetochore, and axoneme organisation in male gametogenesis.
articles.authors: Yanase R, Zeeshan M, Ferguson DJ, Markus R, Brady D, Bottrill AR, Holder AA, Guttery DS, Tewari R
articles.details: Study reveals how Plasmodium kinases regulate MTOC, axoneme and kinetochore organisation in male gametogenesis, providing key insights into potential targets for malaria transmission control.

articles.headline: Malaria causes majority of acute kidney injury in Nigerian children
articles.title_original: A Study Using Point-of-Care Creatinine Testing in a Nigerian Primary Health Care Centre: Malaria as the Leading Cause of Acute Kidney Injury Particularly in Children.
articles.authors: Ugwem-Ikuru Y, Laurent-Ordu V, Emem-Chioma P, Erekosima I, Lewis D, Poulikakos D
articles.details: Cross-sectional study at a primary health care center. Of 424 patients tested (301 adults, 123 children), malaria was most frequent diagnosis (293 cases, 61.1%). In children, AKI was identified in 70/123 (56.9%): stage 1 in 25 (20.3%), stage 2 in 26 (21.2%), stage 3 in 19 (15.4%). Among 70 pediatric AKI cases, 49 (70%) had malaria.

articles.headline: STAT1 deficiency reduces parasitemia and alleviates malarial anemia
articles.title_original: Role of STAT1 in modulating the host immune response to Plasmodium yoelii 17XL-infected murine blood-stage malaria.
articles.authors: Sun Y, Du C, Xu Q, Chen Z, Feng X, Han S, Yan Y, Wang B, Lv Z, Zhu G, Cao J, Cheng Y
articles.details: Study in P. yoelii 17XL-infected mice identified STAT1 upregulation in spleens. Genetic ablation of STAT1 enhanced survival, substantially reduced parasitemia and splenic parasite burden, and significantly increased hemoglobin concentrations and reticulocyte proportions compared to wild-type mice.

articles.headline: India reports increasing malaria positivity in blood donor screening
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted malaria in India: A national survey of epidemiology and testing practices.
articles.authors: Jacobs JW, Choudhury N, Arora S, Jindal A, Bhatnagar N, Basavarajegowda A, Shastry S, Chandra T, Daniel D, Drews SJ, Bloch EM
articles.details: Survey of 262 blood facilities (May-September 2025): 256 (97.7%) reported routine malaria screening, mostly with RDTs (89.1%). From 2020-2024, 9,275,688 donations tested with 1,231 confirmed positive (13.27 per 100,000 units), with annual positivity rising from 3.4 to 22.0 per 100,000. Only 8 facilities (3.1%) reported TTM cases.

articles.headline: PfAP2-V transcription factor controls P. falciparum invasion and virulence
articles.title_original: The DNA-binding protein PfAP2-V regulates erythrocyte invasion and pathogenesis of the human malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Zhang Y, Wang C, Li Q, Li A, Zhang Y, Su Z, Yu L, Zheng K, Liu T, Jiang N, Zhang Y, Sang X, Feng Y, Chen R, Wei G, Zhang Q, Chen Q
articles.details: PfAP2-V (ApiAP2 family transcription factor) coordinates expression with virulence genes (merozoite antigens, var gene clusters encoding PfEMP1) during blood-stage P. falciparum development. Conditional knockdown decreased intraerythrocytic proliferation, caused surface abnormalities, and markedly reduced cytoadhesion to CD36, ICAM-1, and PECAM-1/CD31.

articles.headline: Nationally integrated mosquito-human behavior surveillance framework feasible
articles.title_original: Nationally representative programmatic surveillance of mosquito and human behaviours that influence human exposure to malaria transmission and the impact of vector control across Tanzania.
articles.authors: Baravuga ME, Michael P, Kihwele F, Masanja B, Mmbaga S, Mlalwe AT, Namango IH, McCallum AJ, Mirzai N, Mwalimu CD, Lazaro S, Killeen GF, Kiware S, Ferguson HM, Govella NJ
articles.details: Surveillance in 25 sentinel districts with rolling cross-sectional design, repeated annually over 3 years and surveyed 3 times across wet/dry seasons. An. arabiensis was widespread; higher proportions caught outdoors (64.9%) vs An. gambiae (58.4%) vs An. funestus (42.4%). People more likely outdoors 18:00-21:00, especially adolescents/adults. Higher temperatures associated with reduced bednet use during dry season.

articles.headline: Healthcare workers show high malaria RDT recording accuracy
articles.title_original: The accuracy of recording malaria rapid diagnostic test (RDT) results in public health facilities in Benin; results from the MaCRA project.
articles.authors: Ahogni I, Avanon H, Hueha C, Kpemasse A, Aissan J, Affoukou C, Accrombessi M, Aponte JJ, Hilton E, Cooper S, Griffith K, Humes M, Lindblade KA, Ngufor C
articles.details: Six-month prospective study in 16 facilities, 35,720 RDTs assessed. Overall agreement between HCW and reference panel was 94.3% (Cohen's kappa=0.88). Misrecorded positives (5.0%) more frequent than negatives (0.7%). Accuracy highest in children under 5 (96.7%), lowest in patients over 15 (91.6%). HCWs with ≥10 years experience and access to electricity/internet performed better.

articles.headline: Microscopy underestimates malaria prevalence in Ethiopian communities
articles.title_original: Microscopy underestimates submicroscopic malaria infections in Ethiopia's southern rift valley: a community-based cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Jima B, Wondale B, Eligo N, Wudineh F, Wegayehu T, Lindtjørn B, Massebo F
articles.details: Community cross-sectional study in Gamo Zone, southern Rift Valley, Ethiopia. Of 4,745 participants, microscopy prevalence was 2.7% (126/4745; 95% CI: 2.2-3.2%). PCR on 1,183 samples confirmed 67% (65/97) of microscopy-positive cases (PCR-corrected prevalence 1.4%). Submicroscopic infections detected in 6.1% (66/1,086) microscopy-negative samples. PCR prevalence ranged 4.1-9.6% vs microscopy 0.5-2.3%.

articles.headline: PfVPS13L1 lipid transfer protein essential for parasite schizogony
articles.title_original: A bridge-like lipid transfer protein is critical for generation of invasive stages in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Guillén-Samander A, Perepelkina N, Horáčková V, Behrens HM, Rodriguez Cruz HO, Mesén-Ramírez JP, Ribeiro-Holbein A, Haberkant P, Stein F, Spielmann T
articles.details: Identified PfVPS13L1, a bridge-like lipid transfer protein (LTP) at ER-membrane contact sites in P. falciparum. PfVPS13L1 mediates bulk transfer of lipids from ER to the nascent inner membrane complex (IMC), a newly-generated organelle required for schizogony. Loss-of-function reduced IMC growth and led to smaller anucleated progeny, impairing schizogony.

articles.headline: Tebuconazole fungicide reactivates chronic malaria in house sparrows
articles.title_original: Chronic exposure to a common azole fungicide is associated with reactivation of chronic Plasmodium infections in farmland birds.
articles.authors: Pigeault R, Bichet C, Bellot P, Brischoux F, Fritsch C, Angelier F
articles.details: Semi-natural study in wild-caught house sparrows chronically infected with malaria (70% at baseline) exposed to tebuconazole for 11 weeks (late autumn to mid-winter). While prevalence declined in controls during winter, it increased by 20% in tebuconazole-exposed individuals. No significant difference in parasite load, suggesting tebuconazole promoted parasite reappearance from exo-erythrocytic stages.

articles.headline: P. vivax LISP-2 liver-stage antigen generates naturally acquired antibodies
articles.title_original: Naturally acquired antibodies to Plasmodium vivax Pv_LISP-2, a potential liver stage vaccine antigen.
articles.authors: Donassolo RA, Soares da Veiga GT, Nisimura LM, Bittencourt NC, Salazar YEAR, Barros DKADS, Lopes SCP, de Sousa TN, Costa FTM, Albrecht L
articles.details: Study evaluated naturally acquired humoral response against Pv_LISP-2 in populations from the Brazilian Amazon exposed to P. vivax infection. Plasma tested via ELISA for IgM, IgG, and IgG subclasses. Acute phase response characterized by high IgM followed by IgG, especially against Pv_L.seq1. IgG1 and IgG3 were predominant subclasses. IgM correlated with parasitemia and symptom days but showed negative association with hemoglobin, hematocrit, and erythrocytes.

articles.headline: LLIN ownership and utilization gaps in endemic communities
articles.title_original: Assessment of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Net (LLIN) Ownership, Utilization, and Associated Barriers in Malaria-Endemic Communities of Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Waldetensai A, Tasew G, Yewhalaw D, Takie H, Gidey B, Kinde S, Gemechu F, Yirga S, Kinfe E, Hailemariam A, Tadesse H, Solomon H, Assefa G, Dilu D, Bashaye S, Wuletaw Y, Abdulatif B, Kebede T, Tadiwos S, Gebrewold G, Hailu S, Tesfaye F, Tollera G, Hailu M, Guiyun Y, Eukubay A, Gebresillassie A
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional survey of 9,222 households and 34,427 individuals. LLIN ownership was 71.5%, but overall utilization was only 59.9%, with higher rates in rural areas (72.7%) than urban areas and among vulnerable groups (pregnant women 78.5%, children under five 67.2%). Barriers included 60.5% lacking confidence in hanging nets and 64.1% misconception that malaria risk is limited to rainy season. Authors concluded utilization remains below the 80% threshold required for community-wide protection.

articles.headline: Emerging pfdhps A581G mutation in P. falciparum during SMC pressure
articles.title_original: Emergence of Plasmodium falciparum pfdhps A581G mutation in Southern Senegal under Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention pressure, 2020-2023.
articles.authors: Diallo MA, Tine A, Sow D, Sene A, Gaye A, Ndiaye YD, Gomis J, Dieye B, Deme AB, Yade MS, Ngom B, Garba MN, Ndiaye IM, Diongue K, Badiane AS, Seck MC, Ndiaye M, Ndiaye D
articles.details: Analysis of dried-blood-spot samples from therapeutic-efficacy studies using targeted amplicon deep sequencing (2020-2023). P. falciparum pfdhfr triple mutant (N51I + C59R + S108N) is near-fixed (>90%), and pfdhps A437G is common (47-100%). The pfdhps A581G mutation emerged in 2022 (8.1% in Kolda) and rose to 14.9% in Kolda and 6.5% in Kédougou by 2023. Although sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine remains clinically effective, the increasing A581G frequency warrants close monitoring.

articles.headline: Imported malaria in Nepali labor migrants from Indian urban hotspots
articles.title_original: Understanding imported malaria in western Nepal: Implications for malaria control and elimination.
articles.authors: Kandel S, Koirala M, Dahal G, Sah RK, Upadhayaya P, Jongcherdchootrakul K, Thitichai P
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional study of 113 imported malaria cases among Nepali labor migrants. All cases were male, predominantly aged 20-29 years (46.02%) and Muslim (84.07%). Almost all (99.11%) had traveled to India for work, primarily to Maharashtra (96.47%) and South Mumbai (95.60%), with 71.43% in catering services. The majority (85.84%) lacked fixed housing, 59.30% were unwilling to use LLINs citing unsuitable living conditions, and only 56.64% had received malaria information before travel.

articles.headline: Anopheles arabiensis habitat preference and competitive dynamics with sibling species
articles.title_original: Blood host preferences and competitive inter-species dynamics within an African malaria vector species complex inferred from signs of animal activity around aquatic larval habitats.
articles.authors: Walsh KA, Kavishe DR, Duggan LM, Tarimo LJ, Msoffe RV, Elisa M, Govella NJ, Eichhorn MP, Kaindoa EW, Butler F, Killeen GF
articles.details: Survey of Anopheles gambiae complex larvae across habitats of varying ecosystem integrity in Tanzania. An. arabiensis was restricted to fully domesticated ecosystems and positively associated with human and cattle activity, while non-vector sibling An. quadriannulatus dominated well-conserved natural ecosystems and was associated with impala, warthog, and bushpig activity. Despite An. quadriannulatus dominance in intact areas, An. arabiensis persisted >40km inside protected areas, suggesting survival on unidentified wild blood hosts, which may confound vector elimination efforts.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 activity and primaquine efficacy in P. vivax malaria
articles.title_original: Reduced cytochrome P-450 (CYP) 2D6 activity and Plasmodium vivax malaria risk in Amazonians: A retrospective, population-based cohort study.
articles.authors: Corder RM, Puça MCSB, Prete CA, Ladeia WA, Rodrigues PT, Johansen IC, de Sousa TN, Ferreira MU
articles.details: Retrospective population-based cohort study of 466 P. vivax-infected participants treated with chloroquine and primaquine (3.5 mg/kg total dose). Those with null/low CYP2D6 activity (AS ≤ 0.25) had twice the risk of recurrence compared to normal activity (AS > 1.0; HR = 2.22, p = 0.004), but vivax incidence did not differ significantly across CYP2D6 activity levels in 5 years of follow-up of 997 exposed individuals (mean 26.6 cases/100 person-years). Authors conclude that excess relapses from severely reduced CYP2D6 activity contribute little to overall vivax burden.

articles.headline: Federated Vision Transformers for privacy-preserving automated malaria detection
articles.title_original: FedMal-XAI: an explainable federated vision transformer leveraging knowledge distillation for privacy-preserving malaria detection.
articles.authors: Bhuiyan TA, Rahman A, Khan FI, Noori FM, Masum AKM
articles.details: Proposes a federated learning system using Vision Transformers (ViT-B/16, DeiT-Tiny, Swin-T, DINOv2) for automated malaria detection from blood smear and segmented RBC images. The federated ViT models outperform CNN ensemble (ResNet50 + VGG16, 98.15% accuracy), with FedDistill-DeiT achieving 97.79%, FedAvg-Swin-T 97.75%, and FedDistill-Swin-T reaching ROC-AUC of 0.9977. Architecture enables cooperative training among decentralized institutions without exchanging sensitive data.

articles.headline: Gp96 heat shock protein in malaria immunity and severe disease
articles.title_original: Heat shock protein Gp96 (Grp94) in malaria: functional insights at the host-parasite interface and therapeutic perspectives.
articles.authors: Djoumoi D, Diouara AAM, Diop M, Nguer CM, Mbengue B, Thiam F
articles.details: Review synthesizing knowledge on host Gp96 and parasite PfGp96 at the malaria host-parasite interface. Elevated circulating Gp96 is associated with severe malaria phenotypes, implicating it as a potential biomarker of host stress and immune dysregulation through functioning as a danger-associated molecular pattern. P. falciparum expresses PfGp96, which retains conserved Hsp90 architecture with parasite-specific adaptations, though its biological functions and validation as a drug target remain poorly defined.

articles.headline: PfNCR1 HLH domain mechanism for cholesterol homeostasis drug targeting
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Niemann-Pick Type C1-Related protein relies on its physicochemical properties for membrane contact site localization required for cholesterol homeostasis.
articles.authors: Ray A, Istvan ES, Goldberg DE, Garten M
articles.details: Mechanistic study of P. falciparum NCR1, a promising anti-malarial drug target at the host-parasite interface. Identified a 141 amino acid helix-linker-helix domain (HLH) unique to Plasmodium spp. that is necessary for PfNCR1 localization to narrow membrane contact sites (~3-4 nm spacing) covering ~half the host-parasite interface. The degree of PfNCR1 localization qualitatively correlates with its ability to maintain cholesterol homeostasis, establishing the HLH domain as a key element for drug targeting.

articles.headline: P. vivax genetic diversity and relapse patterns in low-transmission Ethiopia
articles.title_original: High genetic diversity, clonal activation of hypnozoites and relapse of Plasmodium vivax isolates in low-transmission setting of Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Getachew H, Zhong D, Habtamu K, Abossie A, Demissew A, Tsegaye A, Degefa T, Wang C, Zhou G, Lee MC, Kazura JW, King CL, Yewhalaw D, Yan G
articles.details: Analysis of 370 blood samples from 215 individuals using amplicon deep sequencing of pvmsp1 gene. High population genetic diversity (67 unique haplotypes, Hd = 0.799, ℼ = 0.044, HE = 0.826) but low individual MOI (1.4, 34.6% polyclonal). Of 215 participants, 82 experienced recurrent infections; paired analysis showed 81.3% genetic homology with 55.6% sharing identical alleles, indicating most recurrences were relapses. Authors attribute high diversity to migrant workers introducing parasite genotypes into low-transmission setting.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic and sub-microscopic malaria detection by qPCR in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Uncovering silent malaria reservoirs in Southern Ethiopia: community-based molecular detection of asymptomatic and sub-microscopic infections and their implications for diagnosis and control.
articles.authors: Bogale A, Tefera S, Taye Gebremeskele B, Kassie A, Dube SJ, Tekleselassie T, Gemechu T, Abebe AA
articles.details: Cross-sectional community-based study of 525 participants using microscopy, RDTs, and qPCR for malaria detection. qPCR detected substantially higher prevalence of asymptomatic (31.2%) and sub-microscopic infections (16.6%) than microscopy (17.5% asymptomatic) or RDTs (13.1% asymptomatic). Sub-microscopic infections comprised P. vivax (7.9%), P. falciparum (7.2%), and mixed (1.6%), more common in ages 15-24 and females. Authors conclude qPCR reveals substantial underestimation of true prevalence and persistent transmission reservoirs.

articles.headline: Rising Pfmdr1 Y184F mutation in Ethiopian P. falciparum strains
articles.title_original: Incidence of kelch13 and Pfmdr1 gene mutations associated with antimalarial drug resistance in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Yeshanew TM, Brhane BG, Begashew BA, Bayisa GW, Birhane N
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 studies comprising 11,269 samples and 5,521 positive P. falciparum cases from Ethiopia. Pooled prevalence of kelch13 mutations was R622I 11%, A675V 1%, and P441L 0%, while Pfmdr1 mutations were D1246Y 6% and Y184F 77%. Subgroup analysis revealed Y184F significantly increased over time from 66% (2007-2014) to 91% (2015-2025), highlighting growing antimalarial drug resistance.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccine introduction coverage and community engagement in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the first malaria vaccine introduction: ongoing learning opportunities during the introduction into routine immunisation in Cameroon.
articles.authors: Josue Kedakse TSN, Kaba M, Baonga SF, Mboussou F, Njoh AA, Amani A, Efouba G, Kouontchou Mimbe JC, Njinkeu A, Yohou KS, Nembot Mekoueng R, Takamiya M, Wirngo M, Hedje J, Jalloh MF, Walldorf JA, Habimana P, Impouma B
articles.details: Programme evaluation of malaria vaccine introduction into routine immunization in 42 health districts across 10 regions. First-dose coverage was 56%, with second and third doses at 31% and 28%, respectively. Among caregivers interviewed (n=48), 88% were aware of vaccine introduction, 60% were informed by health workers, 81% cited vaccine protection as main reason to vaccinate, and 60% knew importance of continuing LLINs. Monthly reporting completeness exceeded 95% in most regions except North-West and Adamawa (87%).

articles.headline: Malaria costs US$33 per episode in rural Indonesia
articles.title_original: The household economic burden of human-only and zoonotic malaria, compared to other causes of acute febrile illness in Indonesia.
articles.authors: Abraham P, Lubis IND, Novivanti R, Trianty L, Nisa FA, Kariodimedjo P, Amalia R, Cahyadi RAD, Mossadeq Al Qorny T, Perlambang YA, Permatasari R, Surendra H, Anstey NM, Grigg MJ, Devine A
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 2244 patients (153 malaria-positive) in Indonesia identified P. vivax (97 cases), P. knowlesi (35), P. malariae (12), P. falciparum (3), and P. ovale (1). Mean total cost for malaria episodes was US$33 (SD=57) versus US$17 (SD=38) for non-malarial fever, with indirect costs from productivity loss comprising 70% and 61% respectively. 16% of malaria patients experienced catastrophic health expenditure.

articles.headline: Outdoor occupation and twilight activity increase malaria risk
articles.title_original: The Effect of Head of Household Occupation and Activity at Sunrise and Sunset on Malaria Prevalence in Sussundenga, Mozambique.
articles.authors: Jorgenson B, Earland DE, Bibe AF, Novela A, Muhiro V, Ferrão JL, Searle KM
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 297 individuals in 84 households in Sussundenga, Mozambique assessed associations between head of household occupation, twilight activity, and malaria infection using generalized linear mixed models. Outdoor versus indoor occupation showed adjusted difference in predicted malaria prevalence of 0.015 (95% CI: -0.12 to 0.15). Activity at sunrise and sunset was associated with 0.10 higher predicted prevalence (95% CI: -0.063 to 0.26); the interaction between outdoor occupation and twilight activity showed 0.33 higher prevalence (95% CI: 0.037-0.62).

articles.headline: Anopheles darlingi shows geographic structure and insecticide selection
articles.title_original: Population genomics of Anopheles darlingi, the principal South American malaria vector mosquito.
articles.authors: Tennessen JA, Brosula R, Chabanol E, Bickersmith S, Early AM, Laws M, Kelley KA, Grillet ME, Gamboa D, Lucas ER, Duchemin JB, Quiñones ML, Sallum MAM, Bergo ES, Moreno JE, Nagi S, Arisco NJ, Sooklall M, Niles-Robin R, Castro MC, Cox H, Gendrin M, Conn JE, Neafsey DE
articles.details: Genomic analysis of 1094 Anopheles darlingi mosquitoes from six South American countries (median depth 18×) revealed deep geographic population structure with 13 putative segregating inversions and no evidence of sympatric cryptic taxa. Strong signals of selection were detected on cytochrome P450 genes, plausibly driven by insecticide pressure. Findings highlight vector diversity as an ongoing challenge for malaria elimination in the Western hemisphere.

articles.headline: TRAg7-CD71 interaction drives malaria inflammation and parasitemia
articles.title_original: Interaction of Plasmodium yoelii tryptophan-rich antigen 7 with CD71 on macrophage membrane regulates host inflammatory response.
articles.authors: Sun Y, Chen Z, Du C, Lei Y, Li J, Zhang H, Huang X, Wang B, Zuo S, Lv Z, Cao J, Han S, Cheng Y
articles.details: Study using Plasmodium yoelii demonstrated that parasite tryptophan-rich antigen 7 (TRAg7) interacts with macrophage CD71 receptor and activates NFκB p65 signaling, promoting proinflammatory factor production. Genetic deletion of TRAg7 resulted in decreased parasitemia, reduced inflammation, and improved host survival. These findings reveal how parasite-exported proteins orchestrate inflammatory responses via immune cell receptors.

articles.headline: SNARE proteins mediate nutrient uptake in Plasmodium liver infection
articles.title_original: Host SNARE Proteins Mediate Lysosome and PVM Fusion to Support Plasmodium Liver Infection.
articles.authors: Atchou K, Kramer N, Bindschedler A, Schmuckli-Maurer J, Caldelari R, Heussler VT
articles.details: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout studies in Plasmodium berghei-infected HeLa cells identified four SNAREs (VAMP7, VAMP8, Vti1B, Stx7) recruited to the parasitophorous vacuole membrane with distinct temporal profiles. Double knockouts of VAMP7-VAMP8 and VAMP7-Vti1B significantly reduced parasite infection and growth, with Vti1B playing a dominant role. SNARE depletion impaired lysosome-PVM association, indicating the parasite hijacks host SNARE machinery for nutrient acquisition.

articles.headline: G6PD deficiency prevalence heterogeneous across Indonesia
articles.title_original: G6PD deficiency in Indonesia: a systematic review and update of prevalence and variant maps in the context of malaria elimination.
articles.authors: Sadhewa A, Panggalo LV, Nanine I, Fadilah I, Hutagalung J, Kenangalem E, Limardi PC, Malik SG, Noviyanti R, Pasaribu AP, Poespoprodjo JR, Prameswari HD, Price RN, Puspadewi RT, Setiadi W, Trianty L, Sudoyo H, Sutanto I, Syafruddin D, Kevin Baird J, Ley B, Elyazar IRF, Satyagraha AW
articles.details: Systematic review of 45 studies (1964-2024) assessed G6PD deficiency prevalence in Indonesia. G6PDd (<30% activity) ranged from 0.0-19.9% across 87 sites (n=23,166), while females with intermediate activity (30-70%) ranged from 0.8-44.6% across 35 sites (n=6,729). G6PDd allele frequencies ranged from 0.0-25.9%. Fifteen class B G6PD variants were identified. Authors emphasize heterogeneous distribution is concerning given future implementation of high-daily-dose primaquine will require G6PDd screening.

articles.headline: Vector abundance and species shift in urban Accra, Ghana
articles.title_original: Urban malaria vector dynamics in Accra, Ghana.
articles.authors: Mohammed Sabtiu AR, Akuamoah-Boateng Y, Owusu-Asenso CM, Abdulai A, Sraku IK, Halou DK, Doe RT, Boadu EN, Mensah SKE, Azumah JD, Adu-Barima S, Awotwe LE, Walker A, Yanney SA, Ebuako AA, Eyeson NAS, Appiah AA, Obeng BC, Amoateng G, Danquah GA, Abusah NE, Agbotah EE, Kwarteng RO, Boateng J, Hinne IA, Appiah-Kwarteng C, Forson AO, Aboagye-Antwi F, Afrane YA
articles.details: Entomological survey of 15 sites in Accra during dry and rainy seasons (2022-2024) collected 20,945 host-seeking and 1,613 resting Anopheles mosquitoes. Abundance was highest in irrigation and peri-urban sites and lowest in low socioeconomic areas. Anopheles gambiae s.s. dominated host-seeking populations while Anopheles coluzzii dominated resting populations. Findings identify irrigation and peri-urban areas as transmission hotspots.

articles.headline: IL-10 suppresses novel host-protective malaria immunity pathways
articles.title_original: Early enhanced control of Plasmodium yoelii infection in IL-10-deficient mice is independent of IFN-γ, IL-12, and the humoral response.
articles.authors: Jones MD, O'Neal KA, Zeltner SL, Gouch AR, Stumhofer JS
articles.details: Study using Il10 -/- mice infected with Plasmodium yoelii demonstrated that IL-10 deficiency enhanced early parasite control independent of increased IFN-γ, IL-12, or humoral immune responses. While IL-10 loss reduced germinal center B cell accumulation and plasmablast production, Il10 -/- mice lacking both IFN-γ and B cells could still limit early parasite expansion, indicating IL-10 suppresses host-protective immune pathways beyond those mediated by B cells and interferon-gamma.

articles.headline: Catch-up R21/Matrix-M campaigns optimize vaccine public health impact
articles.title_original: Public health impact of catch-up vaccination or additional booster doses with pre-erythrocytic malaria vaccine R21/Matrix-M: a modelling study.
articles.authors: McCain K, Topazian HM, Challenger JD, Okell L, Winskill P, Ghani AC
articles.details: Stochastic individual-based transmission modeling of Plasmodium falciparum evaluated R21/Matrix-M catch-up campaigns and booster strategies across transmission settings. In moderate-to-high transmission (45% PfPR2-10), catch-up campaigns targeting children 6 months-2 years averted 358 (95% CI 113-570) uncomplicated cases per 1000 doses, compared to 373 (95% CI 240-518) per 1000 doses for school-aged children in low transmission. Extra boosters 10 years post-primary averted most severe cases at low transmission (12 per 1000) but were less effective at high transmission.

articles.headline: Malaria outbreak frequency declining in India, outbreak-prone states identified
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria outbreak years in India provides key insights into malaria elimination.
articles.authors: Yadav CP, Hussain SSA, Tarique ST, Maheshwari V, Rahi M, Sharma A
articles.details: Analysis of district-level malaria case data across India identified 402 outbreak years over the past decade, with peak outbreak years in 2011, 2014, and 2015. A sharp decline followed 2015, with 2020 recording the lowest number of district-wise outbreaks. Seven states (Mizoram, Tripura, Jharkhand, Odisha, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Meghalaya) were identified as "more outbreak-prone," with >70% of their districts experiencing at least one outbreak year between 2011 and 2020.

articles.headline: Macrophage SR-A receptor suppresses transmission-blocking mosquito microbiota
articles.title_original: Host macrophages/monocytes promote malaria transmission by modulating mosquito microbiota via SR-A-mediated phagocytosis.
articles.authors: He B, Li M, Guo S, Fang J, Fan Y, Tan N, Liu T, Li J, Zhu F, Zhang J, Xu W
articles.details: Study in rodent models and humans demonstrated that blood-stage malaria infection recruits macrophages and monocytes to the mosquito midgut, where they suppress transmission-blocking bacteria (E. anophelis, S. sonnei, K. oxytoca) through scavenger receptor A (SR-A)-mediated phagocytosis. Depletion of macrophages/monocytes or anti-SR-A antibody treatment enhanced the transmission-blocking efficacy of anti-Pfs25 monoclonal antibodies, achieving complete blockade of Pfs25-transgenic Plasmodium berghei transmission when combined.

articles.headline: Malaria-dengue co-infections detected in endemic Papua, Indonesia
articles.title_original: Co-infections of malaria and dengue in Timika, a highly endemic malaria area in Central Papua, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Rana B, Trianty L, Santoso MS, Puspitasari AM, Nisa FA, Amalia R, Carlier L, Tetteh K, Noviyanti R, Sasmono RT
articles.details: Screening of 69 microscopy-confirmed malaria samples from Timika, Papua identified three patients (4.3%) with malaria-dengue co-infections: one with DENV-2 and two with DENV-4, alongside P. vivax, P. falciparum, or P. malariae infections. Whole-genome sequencing of DENV-4 genomes revealed Genotype II, phylogenetically related to DENV strains from Makassar, South Sulawesi. Authors highlight diagnostic and clinical challenges posed by overlapping symptoms of both infections.

articles.headline: Mapping the local effectiveness of mass drug administration for malaria using transportability methods
articles.title_original: Mapping the local effectiveness of mass drug administration for malaria using transportability methods.
articles.authors: Roh ME, Tong Y, Heitmann GB, Jia J, Ba EKC, Ndiaye JL, Fogelson A, Milligan P, Seck A, Diallo A, Lo AC, Baiocchi M, Gosling R, Bennett A, Hsiang MS, Benjamin-Chung J

articles.authors: Adegoke TM, Nji AM, Silal SP, Ali IM, Wondeu ALD, Madeleine PF, Sylvanie MT, Tah CF, Antonio-Nkondjio C, Bigoga JD, Leke RFG, Stresman G, Cui L, Oladoja OM, Adegoke GK, Obisesan KO, Mbacham WF

articles.authors: Westaway JAF, Benavente ED, Kucharski M, Nayak S, Huy DTQ, Auburn S, William T, Rajahram GS, Piera KA, Trimarsanto H, Hoon KS, Bourke C, Jantim A, Manah AM, Gotulis W, Moon RW, Amato R, Barber BE, Drakeley C, Anstey NM, Field MA, Bozdech Z, Grigg MJ

articles.title_original: The Institution-based Network on China-Africa Cooperation for Malaria Elimination: fostering a transcontinental hub for strengthening China-Africa cooperation on malaria control and elimination efforts.
articles.authors: Lu S, Ding W, Huang L, Liu L, Xiao N, Abdulla S, Okpeku M, Thiongane A, Gibba B, Kompaore SCB, Lv S, Li S, Weldedawit MA, Zhou XN, Wang D

articles.headline: Central Ethiopia malaria prevalence increases, 2019–2024
articles.title_original: A six years trend analysis of malaria prevalence in Central Ethiopia Region.
articles.authors: Teshome Z, Animut A, Negash Y, Giday M, Aklilu E
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of surveillance data from 12 districts and 2 towns covering 2019–2024. Among 368,490 suspected malaria cases, 86,604 (23.5%) were Plasmodium-positive: 61% P. falciparum and 39% P. vivax. Cases showed an increasing trend from 2019, with peaks in May (15.9% of total cases), autumn (31.5%), and spring (29.5%), coinciding with farming and harvesting seasons.

articles.headline: Angiotensin II treatment for refractory septic shock in malaria
articles.title_original: Angiotensin II use in refractory septic shock due to Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Meconi T, Pazzi M, Papa R, Ruggiano P, Conti D
articles.details: Case report or clinical observation of angiotensin II use for management of refractory septic shock complication in P. falciparum infection; specific clinical outcomes and study design not available from title alone.

articles.headline: Malaria prophylaxis review
articles.title_original: Malaria Prophylaxis.
articles.authors: Stachler L, DeVos E, Samandari T
articles.details: No abstract available; title indicates overview of malaria prevention strategies and approaches.

articles.headline: PfHP1 phase separation regulates Plasmodium var gene silencing
articles.title_original: Phase separation of heterochromatin protein 1 regulates virulence gene expression in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Mamatharani DV, Purkayastha D, Niederwieser I, Rai SK, Gaur P, Ganji M, Mukhopadhyay S, Voss TS, Karmodiya K
articles.details: Study demonstrates PfHP1 undergoes phase separation in vitro to compartmentalize and compact AT-rich DNA. Phase-separation-defective mutants caused derepression of up to 54 of 60 var genes, altered chromatin binding, and dispersed nuclear localization. Hi-C analysis confirmed loss of heterochromatic interactions, establishing PfHP1-mediated phase separation as a key mechanism for var gene silencing and parasite virulence regulation.

articles.headline: Anopheles pseudopunctipennis oviposition exhibits nightly peak at dusk
articles.title_original: Daily pattern of oviposition and fecundity in Anopheles pseudopunctipennis Theobald, the main vector of malaria in Mexico.
articles.authors: Villarreal-Treviño C, González-Cerón L, Ríos-Delgado JC, Penilla-Navarro RP
articles.details: Observational study of oviposition patterns in nulliparous and parous female An. pseudopunctipennis under insectary and semi-field conditions. Primary oviposition peak occurred between 18:00–20:00 (shortly after sunset), with gradual decline until midnight. Nulliparous females ceased oviposition after 24:00; a small percentage of parous females continued until 06:00. Parous females produced significantly more eggs than nulliparous females.

articles.headline: Systematic review of entomological methodology in vector control trials
articles.title_original: A systematic review of entomological outcomes and sampling approaches used in the evaluation of cluster randomised controlled trials for malaria vector control products.
articles.authors: Githu V, Michael P, Biggs J, Cook J, Kiware S, McConnachie A, Johnson P, Ferguson HM
articles.details: Systematic review of 62 cluster randomized controlled trials (1992–2021) of malaria vector control interventions, identifying 13 different entomological outcomes and 12 sampling methods. 70% of trials had high potential design risk due to lack of power analyses and inadequate randomization of sampling points. Only the number of entomological clusters significantly predicted outcome precision. Standardization and clearer reporting guidelines needed.

articles.headline: Malaria cultural history data Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data for cultural history of malaria in Hungary].
articles.authors: Kiss L
articles.details: No abstract available; bracketed title suggests collection of historical or archival data related to malaria in Hungary.

articles.headline: Indole peptidomimetics show activity against resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Indole Peptidomimetics Show Potent and Selective Activity against Drug-Resistant Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Januário MAP, Valdes TA, Maluf SEC, Mendes GR, Bonatto V, Moura IMR, Mourão PS, Corrêa AG, Guido RVC
articles.details: Structure-activity relationship analysis of indole-based peptidomimetics against P. falciparum. Lead compounds LSPN954 and LSPN959 showed IC50 values of 1.7 and 1.0 μM respectively against sensitive strain (3D7), with selectivity indices of 59 and 95, and safety margins (CC50 ≥ 100 μM on human cells). Compounds demonstrated slow-acting profiles, additive effects with artesunate, and retained efficacy against resistant strains (Dd2, K1, Dd2R_DSM265, 3D7R_MMV848) without cross-resistance.

articles.headline: Advances and challenges in Plasmodium falciparum vaccine development
articles.title_original: Recent Advances, Bottlenecks, and Future Directions in Plasmodium falciparum Vaccine Development.
articles.authors: Turan G, Boggio MJ, Othman AS, Nnaemeka V, Hill AVS, Salman AM
articles.details: Review of P. falciparum vaccine development strategies and challenges. Vaccines represent a critical tool for reducing malaria-associated morbidity and mortality (estimated at 282 million cases and 610,000 deaths globally in 2024) and interrupting parasite transmission. The complex parasite lifecycle poses significant challenges in identifying protective antigens and optimal vaccine formulations. Article summarizes current vaccine strategies and discusses key limitations, alongside emerging opportunities for future research directions.

articles.headline: Modeling predicts RTS,S/AS01 reduces malaria burden in Benin
articles.title_original: Assessing the potential impact of the RTS,S/ASO1 vaccine on malaria burden in Benin: a mathematical modelling approach.
articles.authors: Gbaguidi GJ, Loum MA, Gning LD, Tahiho AK, Diallo EM, Koutou O, Dembélé A, Topanou N, Ketoh GK
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study (EMOD) parameterized with epidemiological and entomological data from Benin (2010–2023) assessing RTS,S/AS01 vaccine impact on malaria burden in children under five. Scenario with 90% coverage and 2 booster doses averted 9.8% of parasite prevalence, 11.06% of cases, and 21.83% of severe cases by 2030. Booster-based strategies showed sustained decline in malaria incidence through 2030, compared to limited gains with lower coverage.

articles.headline: Placental malaria common in eastern Uganda pregnant women
articles.title_original: Prevalence of placental Plasmodium falciparum malaria and associated factors among pregnant women in health facilities in Eastern Uganda.
articles.authors: Rosset AA, Gerald B, Hussein M, Doreen N, Wahabu WA, Rudolf B, Hope M, Bagenda CN, Edgar K, Moses O
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 180 pregnant women (mean age 23.2 years) delivering in health facilities. Placental malaria prevalence was 11.67% (95% CI 7.71%–17.28%) by microscopy and 23.89% (95% CI 18.19%–30.71%) by qPCR. Age 15–19 years (aOR 5.87, p=0.023) and no antenatal care attendance (aOR 4.27, p=0.011) were significantly associated with placental malaria. Findings highlight need for improved antenatal care uptake.

articles.headline: Plasmodium ovale frequently recurs despite short parasitemia duration
articles.title_original: Frequent Plasmodium ovale recurrence in coastal Tanzania.
articles.authors: Carey-Ewend K, Shepard H, Yang G, Odas M, Peter EE, Dominick MC, Rutha I, Amagai K, Vasquez-Martinez B, Mulei LA, Chhetri S, Kharabora O, Adams JH, Lin FC, Bailey J, Edwards JK, Gower EW, Juliano JJ, Ngasala BE, Lin JT
articles.details: Prospective follow-up of 74 asymptomatic individuals (adults and children >5 years) with P. ovale parasitemia monitored with biweekly PCR for up to 6 months. Most initial parasitemias were short-lived (median 4 days after screening), but 55% (41/74) developed recurrent P. ovale infection at median 10 weeks later; 9 experienced second recurrence. Microsatellite genotyping showed most recurrences were heterologous. Risk increased in dry season but didn't differ by sex or age.

articles.headline: Plasmodium simium host-jump: genetic evidence from Latin American monkeys
articles.title_original: Genomic Insights Into Host Shifts Between Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium simium in Latin America.
articles.authors: Lefebvre MJM, Degrugillier F, Arnathau C, González C, Rondón S, Link A, Chaves A, Benavides JA, Campos AAS, Dos Santos E, Huff R, da Silva CMD, Vanderhoeven E, De Thoisy B, Fontaine MC, Prugnolle F, Rougeron V
articles.details: Whole-genome analysis of 23 Plasmodium-positive monkey samples across five Latin American countries revealed P. vivax in three Colombian and one Brazilian monkeys (possible host niche expansion), and P. simium in other samples. Genome-wide scans identified recent genetic exchanges between species, indicating P. simium originated from a host jump approximately one to two centuries ago. Positive selection detected in P. simium genes involved in primate-host and mosquito-vector interactions (PvRBP2a, PvRBP1b, PvCMRP1, PvPAT, Pvs47), highlighting zoonotic risks and need for monkey inclusion in malaria prevention.

articles.headline: DHA-rich fish oil shows strain-dependent effects on experimental malaria pathology
articles.title_original: Differential Effects of DHA-Rich Fish Oil Supplementation on Intestinal and Pulmonary Alterations in Experimental Malaria Caused by Plasmodium berghei NK65.
articles.authors: Pereira LPMC, Silva-Rodrigues JH, Pinheiro FM, Machado LF, da Silva ACF, Marques CB, Gomes MT, Gonçalves FMM, Bezerra-Bellei JC, Brum G, Dias VC, Hipólito JR, Bastos AN, Nascimento HLDS, E Silva FMC, D'Avila H, Neto AF, Reis Junior JL, Rocha VN, Ribeiro-Gomes FL, Castro JMA, Scopel KKG
articles.details: C57BL/6 mice infected with Plasmodium berghei NK65 received DHA-rich fish oil supplementation. Although partial preservation of intestinal morphology occurred (reduced intestinal shortening), pulmonary outcomes did not improve.
