articles.authors: Mujuni DP, Sadiq AM, Luhwago E, Mutalemwa D, Mwanga A, Leonard L, Mkwizu EW, Shao ER, Kilonzo KG
articles.details: Case series of 5 patients with imported P. falciparum complicated by malaria-associated acute kidney injury (MAKI). Mean serum creatinine 736 ± 346.4 μmol/L, mean urea 23.1 ± 14.1 mmol/L at admission. Four patients required hemodialysis, one experienced recrudescence. Overall survival was 100%.

articles.headline: Malaria-associated acute kidney injury: systematic review and meta-analysis
articles.title_original: Clinical characteristics and outcome of malaria-associated acute kidney injury in adult patients: a systematic review and meta analysis.
articles.authors: Meena P, Singla V, Deshpande A, Kasturi U, Khan EF, Kumar VRK, Kalyesubula R
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis (2000–2024) of 25 studies with 2,094 MAKI patients, predominantly from South Asia. P. falciparum most common; P. vivax increasingly reported. MAKI incidence 2.4–45.5%, rising to 77% in severe malaria. Pooled incidence 26.5% (95% CI 19.8–34.4). Pooled mortality 19.2% (95% CI 12.9–27.7%). Dialysis required in 20–90%. Acute tubular necrosis most common on histology; 5–20% developed chronic kidney disease.

articles.headline: Low-prevalence P. falciparum clone expands in outbreak, Ecuador Pacific coast
articles.title_original: A previously low prevalence Plasmodium falciparum clone expands in an outbreak in the Pacific Coast of South America.
articles.authors: Cartagenova A, Zapata-Berrones K, Boboy J, Cuero W, Neafsey DE, Early AM, Sáenz FE
articles.details: Study of 72 P. falciparum samples (2019–2021) analysed via 7 microsatellite markers and whole-genome sequencing. All infections monoclonal. Previously low-frequency clone from Pacific Coast of Colombia caused new outbreaks through clonal expansion, showing high genetic differentiation from prior Pacific Coast parasites.

articles.headline: Amodiaquine disrupts glycolysis pathway in Plasmodium falciparum
articles.title_original: Chemoproteomics-based profiling elucidates the antimalarial effects of amodiaquine through disruption of glycolysis process in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Wang J, Wang C, Zhuge R, Tang H, Xia F, Zhang Y, Zhang J, Liu C, Wu J, Chen X, Gao P, Wang J
articles.details: Chemoproteomics study via activity-based protein profiling identified 3 glycolysis-associated enzymes as antimalarial targets of amodiaquine (AQ). AQ binds these enzymes and disrupts glycolytic processes in P. falciparum. Demonstrated complementary effects of artemisunate and AQ on shared molecular targets, suggesting a mechanism for enhanced artemisunate-amodiaquine combination efficacy.

articles.headline: Evolutionary rate covariation predicts protein interactions in Plasmodium
articles.title_original: Evolutionary rate covariation across malaria parasite species enables inference of protein interactions.
articles.authors: Hopson HD, Omelianczyk RI, Ramirez A, Little JH, Clark N, Sigala PA, Leffler EM
articles.details: Study calculated relative evolutionary rates across 22 Plasmodium species' genomes, assigning protein-protein pairs an evolutionary rate covariation (ERC) score. Known pathways and interacting proteins across lifecycle stages showed strong ERC signals. Protein PF3D7_0811600 showed high ERC with RhopH2 and RhopH3 and co-localized with these rhoptry proteins forming an ion channel.

articles.headline: Low-density asymptomatic parasitemia does not predict clinical malaria, Zambia
articles.title_original: Low-density asymptomatic parasitemia in southern Zambia does not lead to clinical malaria and is not associated with household transmission: results from a two-year cohort study.
articles.authors: Schue JL, Martin AC, Matoba J, Sing'anga C, Lubinda M, Katowa B, Musonda M, Berube S, Shields T, Kobayashi T, Hamapumbu H, Simulundu E, Norris DE, Wesolowksi A, Moss WJ
articles.details: Longitudinal cohort of 197 households (1,071 individuals) in Southern Province, Zambia (2018–2020) with monthly P. falciparum qPCR. Of 144 individuals positive at least once (13%), monthly prevalence never exceeded 4%; median density 4 parasites/μL. Clinical malaria incidence 46.7 per 1,000 person-years. No association between asymptomatic parasitemia and subsequent clinical malaria or transmission to household members beyond one month.

articles.headline: Severe cerebral malaria with multi-organ failure treated with artificial liver blood purification
articles.title_original: Successful treatment of severe cerebral malaria with artificial liver blood purification technology: A case report.
articles.authors: Fan C, Dai Y, Du H, Su T, Guo X, Yan Z, Fang H, Yao Y, Zhou X
articles.details: Case report of 43-year-old male with P. falciparum infection complicated by cerebral edema, acute liver and kidney failure, severe thrombocytopenia, coagulation dysfunction, and anuria. Treated with artemether, artificial liver blood purification, and continuous renal replacement therapy with successful recovery. Combined extracorporeal support may be effective rescue therapy for severe malaria with multi-organ failure.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria burden in children 5-10 years, Niger: need to expand SMC
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria among children aged 5-10 years in the Sahelian area: do we need to adapt seasonal malaria chemoprophylaxis?
articles.authors: Djedanem M, Yacoubou MAY, Zakari A, Issa I, Yahaya M, Zaneidou M, Mody I, Onana DB, Testa J, Jambou R
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 35 dispensaries across 7 provinces in Niger (2018–2021) using SARIMA modeling. During July–October peak, children 12–59 months accounted for 33.2% of cases, followed by 10–14 year olds (26.0%) and 2–11 months (22.6%). Older children (5–14 years) together >33% of cases. Southern districts near irrigated agriculture reported 3,000–7,500 cases/month; Sahelian/Saharan districts <2,500.

articles.headline: Mesenchymal stem cells alleviate experimental cerebral malaria by inducing Tr17 cells
articles.title_original: Mesenchymal stem cells alleviate experimental cerebral malaria disease severity by inducing RoRγt(+) Foxp3(+) T regulatory (Tr 17) cells and modulating the dysregulated Th17/Treg axis.
articles.authors: Sharma I, Thakur RS, Chaudhary A, Chauhan R, Singh K, Sadhu S, Awasthi A, Das J
articles.details: Study in P. berghei ANKA-infected mice (experimental cerebral malaria model). Mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) reduced parasitemia, increased survival, decreased hemozoin accumulation, prevented vascular leakage, and protected blood-brain barrier. MSCs modulated Th17/Treg balance by downregulating IL-6 and IL-17, upregulating IL-10. MSCs induced Tr17 effector regulatory T cell subset (RoRγt+ Foxp3+) that suppressed pathogenic Th17 cells.

articles.headline: Feretia apodanthera extracts inhibit Plasmodium in vitro and in vivo
articles.title_original: Harnessing the potential of medicinal plants in the malaria fighting: In vitro and in vivo antiplasmodial activities of Feretia apodanthera Delille (Rubiaceae).
articles.authors: Boni SI, Hien DFS, Koama KB, Youba M, Vaissayre V, Kabre Z, Dussert S, Diabate A, Lefevre T, Yerbanga RS, Haddad M, Meda NR
articles.details: F. apodanthera leaf, stem bark, and root extracts tested against P. berghei in mice using in vitro and in vivo suppressive/curative/prophylactic approaches. Leaf showed highest suppressive efficacy (32.33–76.31% at 125–500 mg/kg doses), 63.41–68.41% curative activity, and 57.10–63.60% prophylactic activity. All extracts non-toxic (LD50 >5000 mg/kg). Results support traditional use and potential for antimalarial phytomedicine development.

articles.headline: Plasmodium infection enhances antitumor immunity in mouse model
articles.title_original: Plasmodium infection fully activates the immune system in peripheral blood and tumor microenvironment in a murine Lewis lung cancer model.
articles.authors: Huang Q, Yang D, Tao Z, Yu Z, Ding W, Tao N, Liao X, Lin H, Cheng Z, Gao S, Kang Z, Xie J, Hu W, Qin L, Chen X, Yu G
articles.details: Lewis lung cancer model in C57BL/6J mice treated with intraperitoneal P. yoelii injection. Plasmodium infection increased CD3+, CD4+, and CD8+ T cells (including memory subsets) and M1/M2 macrophage ratio in blood and tumors while reducing PD-1 expression and myeloid-derived suppressor cells. Tumor growth inhibited; reduced pSTAT3 and TGFβ expression in tumor tissue suggests novel immunotherapy mechanism.

articles.headline: CelTOS genetic diversity high but nucleotide diversity low in Saudi P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and haplotype structure of the CelTOS gene in Plasmodium falciparum from Jazan Province, Saudi Arabia: implications for vaccine development and malaria elimination.
articles.authors: Alothaid H, Alghnnam F, Al-Qahtani AA, Al Marzan A, Abu Alola M, Bohol MFF, Alhamlan F, Shafeai MI, Rudiny FH, Motaen AM, Al-Qahtani AA
articles.details: Analysis of 202 P. falciparum CelTOS sequences from Jazan, Saudi Arabia identified 27 haplotypes (Hd=0.921) with high haplotype diversity but low nucleotide diversity (π=0.0059). Gene-wide dN/dS≈0.36 indicated predominant purifying selection. Weak regional structure (FST≈0) and extensive haplotype sharing reflected strong gene flow in panmictic population. High haplotype diversity supports CelTOS relevance for vaccine development and molecular surveillance in malaria elimination.

articles.headline: PvRipr naturally immunogenic in Brazilian Amazon malaria-exposed individuals
articles.title_original: B-cell epitope mapping and characterization of antibody responses to recombinant PvRipr in malaria-exposed individuals.
articles.authors: Soares IF, Rodolphi CM, Alencar ALC, Matos ADS, Rodrigues-da-Silva RN, Baptista BO, Martorano RM, de Souza HADS, Riccio EKP, de Barros JP, Totino PRR, Daniel-Ribeiro CT, Pratt-Riccio LR, Lima-Junior JDC
articles.details: ELISA on plasma from Brazilian Amazon residents naturally exposed to malaria showed >60% seropositive for IgM or IgG against recombinant PvRipr. Four linear B-cell epitopes identified; B-PvRipr(879–888) and B-PvRipr(923–958) had highest seroprevalence and reactivity indices, strongly correlating with IgG3 (cytophilic subclass associated with protective immunity). Results establish PvRipr's natural immunogenicity and potential as P. vivax vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Aluminum metasurface biosensor detects plasmodium falciparum lactate dehydrogenase
articles.title_original: Experimental plasmonic sensing of malaria using an aluminum metasurface.
articles.authors: Kiyumbi AS, Tame MS
articles.details: Optical biosensor using aluminum metasurface with surface plasmon resonance achieved label-free detection of pfLDH (malaria biomarker). Spectral sensitivity 360 nm/RIU with limit of detection 1.3 nM (45.6 ng/mL). Operates in visible spectral region. Offers potentially cost-effective, high-sensitivity method for active malaria infection diagnosis.

articles.headline: PfHRP2 parasite biomass better reflects malaria severity than circulating parasitemia
articles.title_original: Reevaluating malaria severity metrics: insights from PfHRP2-derived biomass estimates in Ghanaian children.
articles.authors: Konadu EA, Obese AO, Danso-Coffie CK, Addison T, Kyei-Baafour E, Obiri D, Amoah LE, Kusi KA, Gyan B, Oduro D
articles.details: Study of 118 Ghanaian children (58 cerebral malaria, 28 severe malarial anemia, 32 uncomplicated) from 2012–2016 measured PfHRP2-estimated total and sequestered parasite biomass versus circulating parasitemia. In severe syndromes, total and sequestered burden exceeded circulating burden; in uncomplicated malaria, they were similar. Higher sequestered burden associated with coma. PfHRP2-derived biomass showed stronger association with severity than peripheral parasitemia alone.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid resistance intensifying in Anopheles funestus in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Four-year monitoring of the malaria vector Anopheles funestus in central-west Cameroon reveals an escalation of pyrethroid resistance combined with high malaria transmission.
articles.authors: Tazokong HR, Tchouakui M, Wondji M, Boyomo O, Wondji CS
articles.details: Four-year monitoring (2020–2023) of An. funestus in Mibellon, Cameroon assessed phenotypic resistance and sporozoite infection. Pyrethroid resistance significantly increased: permethrin mortality 77.7% (2015) to 23.2% (2023), deltamethrin 46.6% (2016) to 8.5% (2023). Sporozoite infection rates 4–21% (predominantly P. falciparum with mixed P. malariae/P. ovale). G454A-CYP9K1 and 4.3 kb SV alleles increased in frequency. PBO-based nets maintained higher efficacy than pyrethroid-only nets.

articles.headline: Anopheles maculatus carries Plasmodium knowlesi and inui DNA
articles.title_original: Evidence incriminating Anopheles maculatus as a potential vector of Plasmodium knowlesi and Plasmodium inui.
articles.authors: Sebayang BF, van de Straat B, Kurniawan A, Garjito TA, Haq AM, Westaway JAF, Lubis IND, Grigg MJ, Russell TL, Burkot TR
articles.details: Mosquito survey in North Sumatra (July 2022–June 2023) collected specimens via human landing catches. All 234 morphologically identified specimens confirmed as An. maculatus s.s. via ITS2 PCR. Four specimens (1.7%; 95% CI 0.5–4.3%) positive for Plasmodium genus DNA; one positive for both P. knowlesi and P. inui. First detection of zoonotic parasite DNA in wild An. maculatus.

articles.headline: Pregnant women in four countries support malaria and infection vaccines
articles.title_original: Pregnant women's attitudes and intentions toward tuberculosis, malaria, group B streptococcus, and respiratory syncytial virus vaccines in pregnant: Findings from a cross-sectional study of pregnant women living in Brazil, Ghana, Kenya, and Pakistan.
articles.authors: Limaye R, Schue J, Fesshaye B, Singh P, Miller E, Souza R, Jessani S, Temmerman M, Badzi CD, Sauer M, Brizuela V, Karron R
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 1,603 pregnant women in Brazil, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan. Vaccine safety for baby rated most important factor, followed by efficacy for baby and safety for mother. Intent to receive vaccines: GBS 30%, malaria 26%, RSV 25%, TB 19%. ~40% agreed vaccines unnatural, 38% body should develop natural immunity, 19% delayed recommended vaccines. Highlights vaccine hesitancy and acceptance factors.

articles.headline: Hepcidin levels reduced in malnourished children with asymptomatic malaria
articles.title_original: Hepcidin is low in children with moderate acute malnutrition and asymptomatic malaria: secondary analysis of a 2×2×3 factorial randomised trial in Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Helt TW, Kurtzhals J, List KK, Styrishave B, Yaméogo CW, Fabiansen C, Iuel-Brockdorf AS, Ritz C, Briend A, Filteau S, Michaelsen KF, Friis H, Christensen VB
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 1,019 Burkinabe children with moderate acute malnutrition from randomized trial. Children with iron deficiency had 82% (95% CI 76–87) lower serum hepcidin than those without. Symptomatic malaria associated with 103% (95% CI 32–210) higher hepcidin; asymptomatic/recent malaria with 51% (95% CI 35–63) lower hepcidin. After 12 weeks supplementation, hepcidin increased 61% (95% CI 38–87), with 22% higher in lipid-based versus corn-soy supplement.

articles.headline: Growth-inhibitory epitope identified in PfRipr5 vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Identification of a growth-inhibitory epitope in PfRipr5, a malaria vaccine candidate against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Nagaoka H, Fukushima A, Tsuboi T, Takashima E
articles.details: Study identified 20-amino-acid epitope within PfRipr5 (EGF-like domains 5–9 of Rh5-interacting protein) recognized by growth-inhibitory monoclonal antibody 29B11. Epitope recognition associated with cysteine integrity via surface plasmon resonance analysis with single-site kinetics model. PfRipr is part of PCRCR complex essential for erythrocyte invasion. Findings identify tractable epitope target supporting rational vaccine design.

articles.headline: Imported malaria predominates in low-transmission southwestern Uganda sites
articles.title_original: Imported malaria predominates in near-elimination settings in Southwestern Uganda.
articles.authors: Mbabazi M, Kiyaga S, Katairo T, Kabbale KD, Asua V, Kagurusi BA, Wiringilimaana I, Nsengimaana B, Semakuba FD, Nakasaanya J, Ayitewala A, Watyekele E, Nabende I, Kayondo TM, Arinaitwe E, Mulondo J, Tukwasibwe S, Nsobya SL, Agaba B, Maiteki C, Jjingo D, Kateete DP, Kamya MR, Ssewanyana I, Aranda-Diaz A, Conrad MD, Murphy M, Gerlovina I, Epstein A, Rodriguez-Barraquer I, Rosenthal PJ, Dorsey G, Greenhouse B, Briggs J
articles.details: Epidemiologic surveillance with parasite genomics in southwestern Uganda (January 2023–June 2024) at 3 health facilities. Recent overnight travel common in Maziba (87%) and Muko (96%) but rare in Kamwezi (12%). MAD4HatTeR genotyping showed Maziba/Muko cases with higher within-host diversity and lower within-site relatedness; Plasmotrack analysis identified most infections as imported. Kamwezi showed multiple related clusters indicating sustained local transmission. K13 resistance markers more prevalent in Kamwezi.

articles.headline: Natural product inhibitors targeting drug-resistant P. falciparum DHFR
articles.title_original: Computational exploration of natural product inhibitors against mutant Plasmodium falciparum DHFR through pharmacophore modeling, docking, and MD simulations.
articles.authors: Gogoi D, Gogoi B, Dutta PP, Kalita JM, Khataniar A, Sharma MP, Mandhadi JR, Phukan MM, Verma AK
articles.details: This computational study used pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics simulation to identify pfDHFR inhibitors from chemical databases. Two validated pharmacophore hypotheses screened compounds; three lead candidates from the TCM database were identified. ZINC70454408 showed the highest H-bond energy of -14.204 kcal/mol and predicted Ki of 0.04 µM with RMSD of 2.1 Å; two others (ZINC04096650 and ZINC85631105) showed binding energies of -122.64 and -118.92 kcal/mol with Ki values < 0.1 µM. ZINC70454408 was identified as the most promising natural product-based pfDHFR inhibitor candidate.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid resistance in urban Anopheles coluzzii mosquitoes in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Urban evolution of insecticide resistance and susceptibility patterns of Anopheles coluzzii in Southeastern Nigeria: implications for malaria vector control.
articles.authors: Ezeike AK, Danga SPY, Ezenwa VC, Chukas CF, Ogudu EO, Nwosu CM, Ebenebe IN, Chukwunwejim CR, Oli AN, Esimone CO
articles.details: This study assessed insecticide resistance phenotypes and genotypes in Anopheles coluzzii from three Anambra State urban centers. Deltamethrin mortality ranged from 45–63%, and DDT from 20–56%, indicating pyrethroid/DDT resistance; bendiocarb and pirimiphos-methyl showed 100% susceptibility. High kdr L1014F allele frequencies (0.72–0.81) correlated with pyrethroid resistance, while ace-1 G119S frequencies were low (0.07–0.12). Urban An. coluzzii populations exhibit operationally significant pyrethroid and DDT resistance driven by kdr alleles, while remaining susceptible to carbamates and organophosphates.

articles.headline: Community perspectives on Plasmodium knowlesi transmission risk in Sabah
articles.title_original: What does monkey malaria mean to you? A participatory photovoice study in rural Malaysian Borneo.
articles.authors: Naserrudin NA, Rahman AB, Manah AM, Donnie EM, Jamaji L, Lin PYP, Miller A, Krishnan M, Hassan MR
articles.details: From January–May 2024, twenty participants (aged 18–62) from three rural Sabah villages used smartphones to document experiences and perceptions regarding monkey malaria over 20 days, with follow-up focus group discussions. Reflexive thematic analysis generated five interrelated themes: monkey malaria as embedded everyday risk, community resilience through locally derived practices, structural and socio-ecological challenges shaping exposure, human-wildlife-environmental health interface, and cultural knowledge and blended coping practices. The study demonstrates that malaria risk is embedded in daily life and that effective control should go beyond biomedical models to include local priorities, cultural practices, and lived experience.

articles.headline: Mathematical models of temperature, rainfall effects on malaria transmission dynamics
articles.title_original: Comparing three approaches to modelling the effects of temperature and rainfall on malaria incidence in different climatic regions.
articles.authors: Madito GT, Brooke BD, Silal SP
articles.details: Three mathematical modeling approaches to weather effects on malaria transmission were compared using vector-host compartmental models across tropical, semi-arid, and sub-tropical regions. Approaches differed in mosquito biology: (1) biting and mortality rates, (2) temperature effects on life-cycle characteristics, (3) temperature and rainfall effects on behavior and survival. Simulations showed distinct patterns: Approach 1 indicated sustained seasonal transmission (lower in tropical regions); Approach 2 showed extended peaks in tropical/semi-arid regions; Approach 3 showed multiple irregular peaks. Results demonstrated that climatic events can trigger mosquito surges and malaria outbreaks despite strong vector control, and sustained vector control—particularly in moderate-temperature regions—substantially reduces incidence.

articles.headline: Submicroscopic malaria infections and gametocyte carriage in Ethiopian community surveillance
articles.title_original: Community reservoirs of malaria parasites and gametocytes in Arba Minch district, southern Rift Valley, Ethiopia: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Zewde Z, Eligo N, Tesfaye Y, Lindtjørn B, Massebo F
articles.details: Reactive case detection from July–October 2022 in Sile village screened 2,009 individuals following PCR-confirmed index cases. Microscopy-detected prevalence was 3.6% (72/2009; 95% CI 2.8–4.5); nested PCR identified submicroscopic infections at 10.1% (33/326; 95% CI 7.2–13.9), comprising 31% (33/105; 95% CI 22.6–40.8) of total PCR-confirmed cases. Submicroscopic P. vivax was 4.6% (15/326), P. falciparum 4.3% (14/326), and mixed infections 1.3% (4/326). Index cases had higher asexual density (16,177 vs. 1,900/μL; p < 0.001) but lower gametocyte carriage than reactive cases. Gametocyte carriage was higher in P. vivax (69%, 22/32) than P. falciparum (22%, 6/27), indicating community infections sustain transmission.

articles.headline: Nanobodies against Pfs48/45 demonstrate transmission-blocking potential
articles.title_original: Pfs48/45 nanobodies block Plasmodium falciparum transmission.
articles.authors: Lyons FMT, Chmielewski J, Gabriela M, Chan LJ, Tong J, Adair A, Zeglinski K, Gouil Q, Dietrich MH, Tham WH
articles.details: This study identified nanobodies against Pfs48/45, a protein essential for male fertility in P. falciparum sexual-stage parasites that form a complex with Pfs230 on gametocyte surfaces. The most potent nanobody showed strong transmission-reducing activity and recognized an epitope distinct from TB31F (a leading monoclonal antibody) but similar to RUPA-44 and RUPA-117 epitopes. Crystal structure analysis revealed the nanobody-Pfs48/45 complex. The findings demonstrate the potential of nanobodies as a versatile antibody format for reducing malaria transmission.

articles.headline: DNA ligase I is essential for Plasmodium liver-stage parasite development
articles.title_original: Plasmodium DNA ligase I is essential for parasite blood- and liver-stage development.
articles.authors: Pandey E, Mishra S, Varshney A, Habib S, Mishra S
articles.details: This study investigated Plasmodium ligase I (Lig1), the parasite's sole DNA ligase for nuclear and organellar DNA replication/repair. Using conditional mutagenesis in Plasmodium berghei, PbLig1 was found to localize primarily to the nucleus with organellar presence. Conditional knockout in sporozoites demonstrated PbLig1 is essential for liver-stage development: sporozoites lacking PbLig1 invaded hepatocytes but arrested during mid-liver-stage development with limited nuclear division and reduced DNA content. The enzyme is essential for parasite blood- and liver-stage development, particularly for nuclear division during hepatic schizogony.

articles.headline: Review of current and emerging malaria prevention strategies and innovations
articles.title_original: The Evolving Landscape of Malaria Prevention Strategies: A Review of Recent Developments.
articles.authors: Charavanamuttu Y, Agyeman Wamba A, Taylor-Robinson AW, Lampejo T
articles.details: This review synthesizes prevention strategies including vector control (ITNs and IRS facing insecticide resistance), chemoprevention (IPTp and SMC with proven efficacy but uptake below WHO targets and drug-resistance concerns), and immunoprophylaxis (RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M demonstrating reductions in severe disease and mortality). Novel approaches include monoclonal antibodies and genetically modified mosquitoes. The review identifies challenges in equitable access, sustaining efficacy against evolving parasite and vector biology, and integrating interventions into diverse health systems.

articles.headline: Novel sporozoite-specific rhoptry protein identified using proximity-dependent biotin labeling
articles.title_original: Identification of a novel rhoptry protein expressed predominantly in Plasmodium sporozoites.
articles.authors: Oundavong S, Sekine T, Torii M, Ozawa T, Kosako H, Shinzawa N, Ishino T
articles.details: Using proximity-dependent biotin identification (UltraID) with genome editing in Plasmodium berghei, this study screened for sporozoite-specific rhoptry proteins. RON12 (rhoptry neck protein 12), with highest transcript levels in sporozoites, was used as bait. UltraID identified multiple secretory proteins, including a novel rhoptry molecule (PBANKA_1363400) localized to sporozoite rhoptries and predominantly expressed in sporozoites rather than merozoites. The method demonstrates UltraID's capability for sensitive, stage-specific protein identification in Plasmodium sporozoites, with implications for understanding sporozoite invasion.

articles.headline: Potato carboxypeptidase inhibitor inhibits mosquito enzyme for paratransgenesis-based control
articles.title_original: Inhibition of carboxypeptidase b1 from Anopheles stephensi by the potato carboxypeptidase inhibitor: a foundational step toward paratransgenesis-based malaria control.
articles.authors: Monshizadeh Z, Rismani E, Abdi F, Pakdel JD, Ghanbarnejad N, Raz A
articles.details: This study evaluated the inhibitory effect of potato carboxypeptidase inhibitor (PCI) on carboxypeptidase-B1 (CPBAs1) from Anopheles stephensi, an enzyme vital for Plasmodium sexual development in the mosquito midgut. The coding sequences for cpbas1 and pci genes were cloned into pET-23a expression vector, expressed, and purified. PCI efficiently inhibited CPBAs1 enzymatic activity at low concentrations, comparable to 1,10-phenanthroline. The findings indicate PCI has remarkable inhibition activity against CPBAs1 and suitable structural features for potential use as an effector molecule in paratransgenesis—an innovative vector-control approach using symbiotic microorganisms.

articles.headline: Conserved merozoite invasion complex proteins identified across Plasmodium species
articles.title_original: PTRAMP, CSS and Ripr form a conserved complex required for merozoite invasion of Plasmodium species into erythrocytes.
articles.authors: Seager BA, Lim PS, Xiao X, Lai KH, Feufack-Donfack LB, Dass S, Jung NC, Abraham A, Grigg MJ, Anstey NM, William T, Sattabongkot J, Leis A, Longley RJ, Duraisingh MT, Popovici J, Wilson DW, Cowman AF, Scally SW
articles.details: This study characterized PTRAMP, CSS, and Ripr orthologs from P. falciparum, P. vivax, and P. knowlesi to define conserved invasion complex features. PTRAMP and CSS formed disulfide-linked heterodimers in all three species, creating a conserved PTRAMP-CSS-Ripr (PCR) complex. Cross-reactive antibodies targeting the PCR complex differentially inhibited merozoite invasion. Crystal structure analysis revealed an inhibitory epitope on PvRipr's C-terminal tail. Cryo-EM visualization of the P. knowlesi PCR complex confirmed predicted models, demonstrating a conserved invasion scaffold with implications for multi-species vaccine development.

articles.headline: Rainfall-dependent malaria trends in children predicted with generalized additive models
articles.title_original: Malaria incidence, severity and mortality in children under five in Ghana: evidence from generalised additive models.
articles.authors: Bosson-Amedenu S, Eyiah-Bediako F, Anafo AY
articles.details: This study analyzed 132 monthly surveillance observations (2013–2023) characterizing malaria trends in children under five in Tarkwa-Nsuaem, Ghana. Negative-binomial generalized additive models linked incidence, severe cases, and deaths with monthly rainfall and temperature, evaluating rainfall effects as contemporaneous, lagged (1–3-month), and accumulated (3–6-month). The incidence model improved with rainfall memory (AIC 1,269 → 1,234; deviance explained ~80%; adjusted R2 ≈ 0.88). Significant predictors were trend (χ2 ≈ 334, p < 0.001), 1-month rainfall lag (χ2 ≈ 15.4, p = 0.002), and 6-month rainfall accumulation (χ2 ≈ 9.7, p = 0.002). Twelve-month projections estimated ~600–670 monthly incidence cases (95% PI ≈ 250–1,350).

articles.headline: Splenic abscess with concurrent P. falciparum malaria and sickle cell trait
articles.title_original: Pyogenic Splenic Abscess With Concurrent Active Plasmodium falciparum Malaria and Sickle Cell Trait: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Gumbs S, Gikunda W, Das H, Douchee J, Wilkinson CA
articles.details: Case report of a young male with concurrent Plasmodium falciparum malaria and sickle cell trait who developed a pyogenic splenic abscess. Percutaneous drainage yielded purulent fluid with Fusobacterium species. The patient recovered completely after antimalarial therapy and broad-spectrum antibiotics with anaerobic coverage. Highlights the importance of considering anaerobic bacterial pathogens in splenic abscesses among malaria-endemic patients.

articles.headline: Purpura fulminans and gangrene in severe P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Purpura Fulminans With Digital Gangrene in Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria: A Case Presentation.
articles.authors: Palmares I, Morgado C, Dá Mesquita Faustino F, Silva J, Freitas P
articles.details: Case presentation of a 66-year-old woman with cerebral malaria from P. falciparum who developed purpura fulminans and digital gangrene despite early intravenous artesunate and hyperbaric oxygen therapy. The severe complications resulted from microvascular thrombosis driven by endothelial injury and coagulation abnormalities. Case illustrates that effective antimalarial therapy alone cannot always prevent progression to gangrene.

articles.headline: Community health workers most effective for malaria bed net messaging
articles.title_original: Differentiated effects of sources of malaria messages on mosquito bed net use among women of reproductive age in Uganda: a propensity score matched analysis.
articles.authors: Mwebesa E, Agbi DM, Iha DT, Kogei DK, Agasa LO, Kerich GK, Mwangi A
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 3,488 women from Uganda Malaria Indicator Survey (2018-2019) found that 73.2% reported using bed nets. Propensity score matching showed exposure to malaria messages through community health workers (ATT=0.322, 95% CI 0.111–0.533), community events (ATT=0.296, 95% CI 0.085–0.507), and social mobilization (ATT=0.185, 95% CI 0.008–0.362) significantly increased bed net use, whereas television exposure was not significantly associated (ATT=0.051, 95% CI −0.062–0.164).

articles.headline: Gut barrier loss enables microbiota-mediated purinergic immunosuppression in malaria
articles.title_original: Intestinal Barrier Loss Enables Microbiota-Mediated Purinergic Suppression During Malaria.
articles.authors: Polidoro RB, Bednarski OJ, Lehman S, Oliveira FMS, Fusco EM, Bower L, Rangel-Ferreira M, Kour S, Namazzi R, Opoka RO, John C, Brault JJ, Tilston-Lunel A, Alves-Filho JC, Schmidt NW
articles.details: Murine model of Plasmodium yoelii showed that IgA-coated microbiota transfer susceptibility to hyperparasitemia. Malaria infection disrupted intestinal barrier independently of microbiota composition, with systemic adenosine persistence and CD39+ plasmablast expansion in susceptible mice. Ugandan children with severe malaria exhibited distinct purinergic immune signatures. Therapeutic barrier reinforcement or adenosine A2A receptor blockade improved germinal centers and reduced disease severity independent of parasite burden.

articles.headline: Risk factors for malaria infection in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan
articles.title_original: Determinants of malaria infection across different districts of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Haq IU, Mehmood Z, Muhammad A, Akhtar S, Aboagarib EAA, Zaib A, Ahmed SAM, Huwaikem M, Khan GA, Albugami HF, Ahmed B, Qu S
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 768 participants in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa found overall malaria prevalence of 24.5%. Multivariate logistic regression identified independent risk factors: male gender (AOR 1.8, 95% CI 1.1–2.8), ages 16–30 years (AOR 4.2, 95% CI 2.1–8.5), ages 31–50 years (AOR 8.3, 95% CI 4.2–16.3), low education (AOR 3.9, 95% CI 2.6–5.9), income 10,000–50,000 rupees (AOR 17.8, 95% CI 8.1–38.9), large family size (AOR 11.3, 95% CI 5.9–21.7), distance >3 km to healthcare (AOR 3.1, 95% CI 1.8–5.3), and lack of modern toilets (AOR 4.8, 95% CI 2.4–9.4).

articles.headline: Long-latency P. malariae infections: seven cases with 7–50 year latency periods
articles.title_original: Unraveling the origins and features of long-latency Plasmodium malariae infections: a comprehensive epidemiological, clinical, and genetic study of seven cases in Guangdong Province (2014-2022).
articles.authors: Zhang JY, Mao Q, Wu D, Deng ZH, Liu J, Liao YH, Lu WC, Zhang GT, Zhang XC, Chen JD
articles.details: Retrospective analysis identified seven cases of long-latency Plasmodium malariae in Guangdong Province (2014–2022) with female predominance (85.7%) and mean age 75.3 years. Latency periods ranged from 7 to 50 years, with infection sources from historical local transmission (6 cases) or Southeast Asian importation (1 case). Phylogenetic analysis showed nucleotide homology of 95%–97% with Asian lineage strains. Diagnostic delays occurred at median 4 days with non-specific clinical presentation.

articles.headline: Stabilized tandem antigen chimera shows potent malaria transmission-blocking activity
articles.title_original: A stabilized tandem antigen chimera that elicits potent malaria transmission-reducing activity.
articles.authors: Ivanochko D, Miura K, Hailemariam S, Ravichandran R, Song Y, Huang WC, Stoter R, Teelen K, van Gemert GJ, Leaf EM, Chan S, Men C, Semesi A, Shiu C, MacGill RS, Long CA, Jore MM, King NP, Lovell JF, Julien JP
articles.details: Development of a stabilized tandem antigen chimera (STAC) combining Pfs230-D1 and Pfs48/45-D3 epitopes. Nanoparticle-based multimerization in female mice elicited antibodies with transmission-reducing activity comparable or superior to individual multimerized Pfs230-D1 and Pfs48/45-D3 antigens. STAC demonstrates promise as a next-generation transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Limited knowledge of malaria vaccine despite high awareness in Uganda
articles.title_original: Caregivers awareness, knowledge, attitudes, and perceptions towards malaria vaccine in Kole and Kwania Districts, Northern Uganda.
articles.authors: Puleh SS, Kalyango JN, Ocan M, Yeka A, Siu G, Idro R, Akech SI, Acen J, Ejalu DL, Nankinga Z, Afayo R, Nankabirwa JI
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of 574 caregivers in Kole and Kwania Districts, Northern Uganda found 90.1% (95% CI 87.3–92.4) aware of R21 malaria vaccine, but only 13.9% (95% CI 11.2–17.0) demonstrated factual knowledge of key aspects including number of doses (16.4%), dosing intervals (21.0%), and age eligibility (34.2%). Overall, 57.4% expressed positive attitudes and 57.5% positive perceptions tempered by safety concerns.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence increased to 17.7% by 2024 in southwest Ethiopia
articles.title_original: A five-year (2020-2024) malaria surveillance data analysis of Mattu Karl Specialized Comprehensive Hospital, Oromia region, Southwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Endalu T, Mohammed M, Bulcha I, Ushu L, Befkadu Z, Tamiru E, Ketema T, Dereje G, Teferi S
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 100,760 patients tested at Mattu Karl Hospital (2020–2024) found 14,303 (14.2%) positive for malaria. Plasmodium vivax accounted for 49.1%, P. falciparum for 37.8%, and mixed infections for 13.1%. Females represented 51.4% of suspected and 51.8% of confirmed cases. Malaria positivity rose from 9.2% in 2020 to 17.7% in 2024, with concerning increase in mixed infections.

articles.headline: Malaria's role in historical Palestine public health overlooked
articles.title_original: Malaria in Palestine: the elephant in the room.
articles.authors: Alexander A
articles.details: Historical examination of malaria's role in Middle East conflict origins, noting that Palestine over 100 years ago was largely desolate in rural areas due to severe malaria burden. Argues that historical malaria control efforts have been overlooked or suppressed in contemporary discourse.

articles.headline: Malaria-MOI: fast tool for multiplicity of infection estimation from genomics
articles.title_original: Malaria-MOI: A flexible and scalable tool for predicting multiplicity of infection in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Billows N, Phelan J, Thorpe J, Vanheer LN, Tan MK, Campino S, Clark TG
articles.details: Development of Malaria-MOI, a Python tool for inferring multiplicity of infection from whole-genome sequencing. Benchmarked on 27 P. falciparum mixed-clone samples achieved root mean squared error of 0.38, mean absolute error of 0.15, and correlation of 0.78 across 165 diverse loci. Applied to 8,208 P. falciparum field samples showed strong negative correlation with FWS (ρ=−0.76), high sensitivity for monoclonal detection (0.99), and superior specificity (0.76) versus estMOI (0.58).

articles.headline: LLIN usage disparities two years after mass distribution in Côte d'Ivoire
articles.title_original: Disparities in long-lasting insecticidal bed net usage and malaria burden 2 years after a mass distribution campaign in central Côte d'Ivoire: A cross-sectional survey prior to a cluster randomised trial.
articles.authors: Sih C, Assi SB, Dangbenon E, Talbot B, Biggs J, Koffi AA, Alou LPA, Messenger LA, Zoh MG, Camara S, Kulkarni MA, Protopopoff N, N'Guessan R, Cook J
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 1,672 participants in 33 clusters in Côte d'Ivoire found LLIN ownership of 66.3% (95% CI 59.4–73.2) and access of 27.8% (95% CI 22.6–33.1). LLIN usage was lowest in children aged 5–9 years (50.0%, aOR 0.50) and females aged 10–14 years (41.0%), highest in under-five males (68.9%). Malaria prevalence was 41.1% (95% CI 37.2–45.0), with under-fives at 61.3% versus 75.0% in 5–9 year-olds (aOR 1.86, 95% CI 1.24–2.78). LLIN users had 38.5% infection prevalence versus 44.3% in non-users (aOR 0.74, 95% CI 0.58–0.94).

articles.headline: Expert consensus on Plasmodium knowlesi prevention strategy factors
articles.title_original: Exploring influencing factors affecting preventive behaviours of Plasmodium knowlesi malaria among at-risk communities: an application of the nominal group technique.
articles.authors: Md Hanif SA, Hassan MR, Safian N, Alabed AAA, Rafi'i MR, Naserrudin NA, Hussain NAMA
articles.details: Nominal group technique with malaria program experts identified 20 key factors influencing P. knowlesi risk and prevention behaviors in Southeast Asian at-risk communities. Factors were categorized into environmental, human-related, policy/organizational, and vector-related risks, achieving 80% consensus agreement threshold across all voting participants.

articles.headline: Crystal structure and nanobodies targeting P. falciparum fusogen HAP2
articles.title_original: Crystal structure and nanobodies against domain 3 of the malaria parasite fusogen Plasmodium falciparum HAP2.
articles.authors: Lyons FMT, Chmielewski J, Chan LJ, Gabriela M, Chan RWB, Adair A, Tong J, Pymm P, Zeglinski K, Gouil Q, Tham WH, Dietrich MH
articles.details: Study identified nanobodies binding to domain 3 of P. falciparum HAP2 with nanomolar affinities, with two showing cross-species reactivity to other Plasmodium species. Crystal structure of one nanobody-domain 3 complex provided first structural insights into this transmission-blocking target essential for parasite gamete fusion.

articles.headline: γ-Tetrahydrocarboline derivatives as potent PfHDAC1 inhibitors
articles.title_original: Discovery of γ-Tetrahydrocarboline Derivatives as Plasmodium falciparum Histone Deacetylase Inhibitors for Treatment of Malaria.
articles.authors: Ni H, Qian Y, Dai Z, Cai Y, Cao L, Li J, Lai Z, Hu X, Huang Z, Jiang L, Cui S
articles.details: Scaffold-hopping strategy yielded 33 new γ-tetrahydrocarboline derivatives as histone deacetylase inhibitors. Compound 5ac showed potent activity (Pf3D7 IC50 4.1 nM), favorable selectivity indices (358 vs HepG2, 3573 vs HEK293T), improved physicochemical properties, and potent in vivo antimalarial activity through downregulation of invasion-related genes.

articles.headline: Proteomic analysis reveals elevated hemoglobin loss via microparticles in malaria
articles.title_original: Integrative proteomic profiling of malaria-derived microparticles: A mass spectrometry-based study.
articles.authors: Antwi-Baffour S, Adjei JK, Agyemang-Yeboah F, Annani-Akollor M, Kyeremeh R, Asare GA, Gyan B
articles.details: Proteomic analysis of microparticles from 43 malaria-positive patients versus 10 healthy controls identified 1729 proteins in malaria patient microparticles versus 234 in controls. Elevated hemoglobin subunits in malaria samples and parasite culture supernatant suggest hemoglobin loss via microparticles may initiate anemia progression in P. falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Mosquito-borne diseases review: epidemiology, control, and climate impacts
articles.title_original: Review of selected mosquito-borne diseases: arboviruses (dengue, chikungunya, Zika, West Nile, Japanese encephalitis, yellow fever) and parasitic diseases (malaria, lymphatic Filariasis).
articles.authors: Wei YL, Wu Z, Li RL, Tang F
articles.details: Review covering arboviruses and parasitic diseases including malaria, lymphatic filariasis, and dengue. Discusses global distribution, transmission dynamics, control strategies including vector management, vaccines (R21 for malaria, IXCHIQ for chikungunya), chemoprophylaxis, and persistent challenges including insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: Global asymptomatic malaria prevalence in blood donors: regional variations
articles.title_original: Global burden of asymptomatic malaria among blood donors: systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Malede B, Amare GA, Setegn A, Fenta A, Mengist A, Belew H, Muche Y, Abebaw D, Melkamu A, Ashagre A, Jemal M, Teffera ZH, Belayneh M, Abebaw A, Getinet M, Wondmagegn M, Abeje G, Almaw A, Berhan A, Adugna A, Admasu A, Wondimu H, Misganaw T, Wondmagegn YM
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 15 studies estimated global pooled prevalence of asymptomatic malaria among blood donors at 0.32% (95% CI: 0.25–0.39), with substantial regional variation: Africa 15.89% (95% CI: 9.48–22.31), South America 0.79% (95% CI: 0.30–1.89), Asia 0.21% (95% CI: 0.15–0.27). Diagnostic method markedly influenced detection: microscopy 21.86%, PCR 5.03%, rapid diagnostic tests 0.21%.

articles.headline: Proline transporter PfApiAT2 essential for P. falciparum oocyst development
articles.title_original: PfApiAT2 is a proline transporter essential for the transmission of Plasmodium falciparum by the mosquito vector.
articles.authors: Khushu M, Kissel RC, Kauffman J, Taccheri C, Singh N, Summers RL, Plant LD, Wirth DF, Catteruccia F, Bopp S
articles.details: PfApiAT2, a proline-specific transporter, proved essential for early oocyst development in Anopheles gambiae. Halofuginone-resistant and PfApiAT2-knockout parasites exhibited severe oocyst developmental defects and blocked sporozoite production; growth defect was rescued by nutrient supplementation to mosquito vectors, revealing critical nutritional dependency during transmission.

articles.headline: Zanthoxylum chalybeum rootbark extract achieves 76% parasite clearance in mice
articles.title_original: Antimalarial efficacy of aqueous crude rootbark extracts of Zanthoxylum Chalybeum: An in vivo experimental study in Wistar Albino Mice infected with Plasmodium berghei.
articles.authors: Michael O, Lwanira CN, Ocan M, Alele PE
articles.details: In vivo study in Plasmodium berghei-infected Wistar albino mice found aqueous rootbark extracts of Z. chalybeum achieved parasite clearance up to 76%, with significant dose-dependent increases (MD=15.6–17.24 vs control, p<0.001 at 100–400 mg/kg) and fever reduction (MD=−1.5 to −1.78°C, p<0.01). No significant effects on hemoglobin or hematocrit concentrations.

articles.headline: PbHiT: high-throughput CRISPR-Cas9 platform for P. berghei genome editing
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei High-Throughput (PbHiT): a CRISPR-Cas9 System to Study Genes at Scale.
articles.authors: Jonsdottir TK, Paoletta MS, Henriksson J, Bushell ESC
articles.details: PbHiT is a scalable CRISPR-Cas9 protocol enabling efficient genome editing in P. berghei experimental infections. Uses single cloning step generating vectors with guide RNA physically linked to 100 bp homology arms; gRNA serves as unique barcode enabling pooled vector transfections and mutant identification by gRNA sequencing, supporting both knockout and tagging strategies.

articles.headline: Mixed vivax-falciparum infection with disparate parasitemias challenges diagnosis
articles.title_original: Pediatric Mixed Plasmodium vivax-P. falciparum Infection with Disparate Parasitemias: Diagnostic and Surveillance Challenges.
articles.authors: Estela-Zape JL
articles.details: Case report of 9-year-old with six-day febrile syndrome and mixed infection of P. vivax (5500 parasites/µL) and P. falciparum (562 parasites/µL) diagnosed by microscopy and rapid diagnostic tests. Disparate parasite densities highlighted diagnostic challenges for parasite detection. Treatment with artemether-lumefantrine followed by primaquine resulted in favorable clinical evolution with documented parasite clearance.

articles.headline: Afebrile malaria presenting as severe hemolytic anemia in returning traveler
articles.title_original: Afebrile falciparum malaria with severe hemolytic anemia in a returning traveler: a case report.
articles.authors: Liu J, Chen X, Yang J, Xie J, Liu Z
articles.details: Case report of 45-year-old with afebrile P. falciparum malaria (0.9% parasitemia, approximately 1746 parasites/µL) presenting atypically with severe hemolytic anemia (hemoglobin 48 g/L), thrombocytopenia, and intravascular hemolysis progressing to acute confusion and quadriparesis. Diagnosis confirmed by blood smear, HRP-2 antigen testing, and PCR; treatment with intramuscular artesunate followed by dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine led to full recovery.

articles.headline: Anopheles population structure in Uganda shows island-mainland differentiation
articles.title_original: Assessing the population genetic structure and demographic history of Anopheles gambiae and Anopheles arabiensis at island and mainland sites in Uganda: implications for testing novel malaria vector control approaches.
articles.authors: Mwima R, Hui TJ, Lukyamuzi E, Bodde M, Makunin A, Birungi K, Lukindu M, Nanteza A, Muhanguzi D, Lawniczak M, Burt A, Kayondo JK
articles.details: Genetic analysis of 2918 An. gambiae and 173 An. arabiensis at six Uganda sites using 62-locus targeted amplicon sequencing revealed clear species-level differentiation but subtle within-species structure. An. gambiae FST values among populations were low (0.00054–0.028), with mainland populations showing higher connectivity and island populations greater isolation. Effective population size estimates ranged from 146–249 on islands to 4054–8190 on mainland.

articles.headline: Bacteremia in 14.5% of adults with severe malaria
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with concomitant bacteremia among adults admitted with severe malaria at Kayunga Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda.
articles.authors: Abdi FD, Hirsi AM, Ali M, Karshe AH, Sandeyl AA, Hersi AG, Yusuf AA, Bekele HK, Mohamed AA, Jayte M, Ezera A
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 207 adults with severe malaria. Bacteremia was present in 14.5% of participants. Central nervous system symptoms, low peripheral oxygen saturation, hyperparasitemia, and leukocytosis were significantly associated with bacteremia. Most common bacterial isolates were Salmonella typhi (33.3%), Staphylococcus aureus (30%), and Streptococcus spp. (16.7%).

articles.headline: Pfmdr1-N86Y mutation surveillance in P. falciparum isolates
articles.title_original: Molecular surveillance of the Plasmodium falciparum Pfmdr1 N86Y mutation linked to antimalarial drug resistance in Mbouda, Cameroon.
articles.authors: Nadia NAC, Ngouana V, Cedric Y, Djakbe DL, Innes W, Pacome TK, Armand GNG, Edna GDM, Merveille TT, Fuyou Z, Hu H
articles.details: Study of 302 patients at three healthcare facilities. P. falciparum infection prevalence was 28.8%. The Pfmdr1 gene was detected in 95% of isolates; 94% were wild-type N86, 1% were mixed N86/Y86 heterozygotes, and 0% were pure Y86 mutants, with 5% testing negative. These results suggest the importance of monitoring other Pfmdr1 mutations for drug-resistance assessment.

articles.headline: Duffy-null selection in Sudanese Copts via ancient admixture
articles.title_original: Sudan's complex genetic admixture history drives adaptation to malaria in Sudanese Copts.
articles.authors: Vilà-Valls L, Garcia-Calleja J, Prado-Martinez J, Bosch E, Andrés AM, Netea MG, Comas D, Hassan HY
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Whole-genome sequencing study of 125 individuals from five ethnolinguistic groups. Analysis revealed adaptive admixture on chromosome 1 in Sudanese Copts, with Nilo-Saharan ancestry introduced 1,000 to 1,500 years ago. SNP rs2814778 in the ACKR1 gene (encoding the Duffy-null blood group conferring P. vivax malaria resistance) showed a strong selection coefficient (s = 0.0996).

articles.headline: Anopheles farauti flight distance and dispersal patterns
articles.title_original: Estimating the dispersal of the malaria vector Anopheles farauti through a natural ecosystem in north Queensland, Australia using mark release and recapture experiments.
articles.authors: Chow WK, Cooper RD, Pagendam D, Trewin BJ, Beebe NW
articles.details: Mark-release-recapture study with 51 releases of 19,889 marked Anopheles farauti during 2015-2017. Daily survival rates were 0.68 during wet season and 0.75 during dry season. The longest flight distance recorded was 3.51 km, with estimated daily travel of approximately 73 m. These dispersal parameters provide novel insights for modeling malaria transmission dynamics.

articles.headline: P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein diversity before vaccine
articles.title_original: Complexity of Infection and Plasmodium falciparum Circumsporozoite Protein Diversity Prior to Malaria Vaccine Implementation in Kaelé Health District, Cameroon, 2022-2023.
articles.authors: Ali IM, Martinez BV, Tchuenkam VPK, Sadler JM, Gorman CC, Nsango SE, Voundi V, Marcellin AJ, Wanda GY, Bailey JA, Dinglasan RR, Lin JT, Juliano JJ
articles.details: Analysis of 100 P. falciparum dried blood spot samples collected in 2022-2023. Using amplicon sequencing, csp was successfully genotyped in 35% of samples, identifying 22 haplotypes with high heterozygosity (He = 0.908; π = 0.021). The vaccine-matched haplotype was present in 20% of genotyped infections. Complexity of infection varied by gene (ama mean 2.8, sera2 mean 2.1, csp mean 1.3, trap mean 1.2).

articles.headline: Pre-treatment antimalarial drug levels in 24% of patients
articles.title_original: Residual antimalarial drug concentrations before treatment in malaria patients in Asia and Africa: A systematic review and meta-analysis depicting its implication for drug pressure.
articles.authors: Marwa KJ, Zinga M, Mnkungwe HR, Mutagonda R, Kapesa A, Mwaiswelo RO, Mmbando B, Lusingu JPA, Kamugisha E
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 studies including 3,522 malaria patients. Overall, 24.0% (95% CI 16.0%-34.0%) had residual antimalarial drug levels before treatment, with higher prevalence in Africa (31.0%, 95% CI 19.0%-44.0%) than Asia (11.0%, 95% CI 5.0%-18.0%). By drug type, chloroquine and lumefantrine showed highest pre-treatment levels (55.0% and 34.0%, respectively). These findings suggest substantial drug pressure facilitating resistance emergence.

articles.headline: Severe falciparum malaria with thrombocytopenia in adult
articles.title_original: Severe falciparum malaria remains a threat in East Kalimantan: A case report.
articles.authors: Gunawan CA, Mangiri HP
articles.details: Case report of a 31-year-old male with severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria presenting with acute kidney injury, impaired consciousness, jaundice, liver dysfunction, high parasitemia, and severe thrombocytopenia. The patient was treated with artesunate injection and supportive care and achieved full recovery. The case highlights the importance of early diagnosis and prompt treatment in managing severe malaria.

articles.headline: MicroRNAs regulate immune pathways in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Functional evidence of hsa-miR-21-5p and hsa-miR-3158-3p in regulating immune pathways relevant to cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Gupta A, Gupta H
articles.details: In vitro study using MCF7 and HepG2 cell lines transfected with hsa-miR-21-5p or hsa-miR-3158-3p mimics. Cells transfected with hsa-miR-3158-3p showed significant downregulation of NF-κB expression (p < 0.05). Cells transfected with hsa-miR-21-5p showed significant reductions in NF-κB, IL-10, and TLR4 expression (p < 0.05). These findings suggest these microRNAs may regulate immune pathways implicated in cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Correction: malaria prevention in pregnancy stakeholder evaluation
articles.title_original: Correction: Initiating community intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy in Senegal: qualitative evaluation of stakeholder acceptability and experience.
articles.authors: Ndoye T, Lugand MM, Diallo AM, Sakho S, Pouye MM, Dieng K, Faye SLB
articles.details: Correction notice to a previously published article about qualitative evaluation of community intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy in Senegal, assessing stakeholder acceptability and experience with the preventive treatment program.

articles.headline: PfCyRPA genetic diversity in vaccine candidate antigen
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity in the Plasmodium falciparum next-generation blood stage vaccine candidate antigen PfCyRPA in Senegal.
articles.authors: Ba A, Thiam LG, Pouye MN, Guo Y, Patel SD, Sene SD, Diallo F, Li R, Cisse A, Guerra N, Laqqa S, Mangou K, Moore AJ, Sadio BD, Ndiaye JLA, Mbengue A, Vigan-Womas I, Sheng Z, Shapiro L, Bei AK
articles.details: Deep amplicon sequencing study of 93 P. falciparum clinical isolates from Kédougou, Senegal. Analysis revealed PfCyRPA wild-type allele dominance with 15 identified SNPs (5 previously reported, 10 novel singletons), with population prevalence ranging from 1.1% to 9.7%, with V292F most prevalent at 9.7%. Structure-based modeling predicted variable functional impacts on PfRH5 interactions and antibody neutralization, with most SNPs having minor effects on antibody recognition.

articles.headline: Plasmodium prevalence higher in invasive house sparrows
articles.title_original: Avian malaria and invasion success: Contrasting infection patterns in two introduced Eurasian sparrows in North America (Passer domesticus and Passer montanus).
articles.authors: Trigueros AC, Robertson BQ, Parker PG
articles.details: Survey of 306 sparrows across 8 sites in Missouri and Illinois (2019-2022) assessed Plasmodium infection via qPCR. Invasive house sparrows (HOSP) showed higher Plasmodium prevalence (24.1%, 95% CI 17.6%-32.0%) compared to less invasive Eurasian tree sparrows (ETSP) (11.6%, 95% CI 7.6%-17.2%). Relative parasitemia was also significantly higher in HOSP with nearly two-fold higher odds. Pathogen lineage richness was similar between species with one unique lineage per host.

articles.headline: Host heme metabolism enhancement restores artemisinin efficacy
articles.title_original: Targeting Infected Host Cell Heme Metabolism to Kill Malaria Parasites.
articles.authors: Siddiqui FA, Adapa SR, Li X, Miao J, Cui L, Jiang RHY
articles.details: In vitro study exploiting host red blood cell heme metabolism as a therapeutic target. Treatment with 5-aminolevulinic acid (ALA) followed by dihydroartemisinin (DHA) resulted in synergistic parasite elimination, including complete clearance of artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum isolates from the Greater Mekong Subregion with no recrudescence over 3 weeks of culture. Mature RBCs retain truncated heme biosynthesis capable of porphyrin accumulation, while infected RBCs show increased membrane permeability enabling selective ALA uptake.

articles.headline: V2163L mutation blocks P. falciparum AP2-G DNA binding
articles.title_original: A single valine to leucine switch disrupts Plasmodium falciparum AP2-G DNA binding and reveals GDV1's role in ap2-g activation.
articles.authors: Prajapati SK, Dong JX, Morahan BJ, Dotrang T, Barbeau MC, Williams AE, Hupalo D, Wilkerson MD, Dalgard CL, Kafsack BFC, Llinás M, Williamson KC
articles.details: Mutation analysis in P. falciparum showed that valine-to-leucine substitution at position 2163 in AP2-G transcription factor abrogates sexual differentiation by preventing DNA binding to ap2-g consensus motif and gene transcription. The mutation reveals GDV1's critical role in initial activation of the silent ap2-g locus. AP2-G.V2163 is required for MSRP1 expression, marking early sexually committed schizonts.

articles.headline: Rainfall and policy periods predict malaria transmission in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Climatic and governance determinants of malaria transmission in Rivers State, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Egbom SE, Nduka FO, Nzeako SO, Nwafor GO, Bartholomew DC, Nwaigwe CC, Iwu HC, Ogbonna CJ, Nwokike CI, Opara MC, Ezenwaka CO, Anyanwu EO, Nwachukwu JI
articles.details: Time-series retrospective analysis (2007-2021) in Rivers State, Nigeria applied Poisson, quasi-Poisson, and SARIMAX models to malaria incidence data. The quasi-Poisson model and SARIMAX(1,1,0)(1,1,1)₁₂ showed monthly malaria incidence was significantly influenced by seasonality, rainfall, and changes in government malaria-control policy periods. SARIMAX demonstrated superior predictive accuracy for integrating climatic and governance indicators into early-warning systems.

articles.headline: S1PR1 pathway elevation in fatal cerebral malaria patients
articles.title_original: Expression of sphingosine-1-phosphate receptor 1 in the brain of fatal cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Srisook C, Nintasen R, Punsawad C, Glaharn S, Techarang T, Viriyavejakul P
articles.details: Immunohistochemistry study examined S1PR1 expression in archived brain tissues from fatal P. falciparum cerebral malaria (CM), non-CM, and control cases. S1PR1 expression was intense in cerebral blood vessels and neurons of fatal CM patients (all p < 0.001) and negatively correlated with blood S1P levels. The S1P/S1PR1 signaling pathway is implicated in cerebral malaria pathogenesis and represents potential therapeutic targets.

articles.headline: Anopheles species complexes identified in Guyana and Venezuela
articles.title_original: Anopheles species complexes from Guyana and Venezuela malaria endemic areas using COI sequences.
articles.authors: Rubio-Palis Y, Corredor-Medina C, Lenhart AE, Ruiz-López F
articles.details: Molecular taxonomy study analyzed mtCOI gene sequences from Anopheles collected in Guyana (Potaro-Siparuni Region) and Venezuela (five municipalities). Four distinct taxa were identified: An. albitarsis F, An. goeldii, An. oswaldoi A, and An. oswaldoi B. An. oswaldoi A and B occurred sympatrically in Boca de Nichare, Bolívar State; An. albitarsis F was widely distributed in Venezuela as an important malaria vector.

articles.headline: Afghanistan forecast models predict barriers to malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Malaria time series analysis, forecasting, and development of curated intervention strategies for the elimination of malaria from Afghanistan.
articles.authors: Rahimi BA, Habib MN, Wafa MH, Sahrai MS, Singh MP, Lal AA
articles.details: Time-series analysis of malaria surveillance (1990-2023 annual WHO data, 2018-2023 monthly district data from Afghanistan Ministry of Public Health) showed mean annual cases of 153,295 during 2018-2023, with test positivity rate 15.2% (P. vivax 96.5%, P. falciparum 3.2%, mixed 0.3%). Forecasting analysis suggests the country is unlikely to achieve malaria elimination by 2035 under current intervention policy. Nearly one-fourth of cases occurred in districts along the Pakistan border.

articles.headline: C-terminal CSP antibodies associated with RTS,S vaccine protection
articles.title_original: Vaccine-Induced Antibodies to a C-Terminal Plasmodium falciparum Circumsporozoite Protein Epitope Associated With Protection Against Malaria Challenge.
articles.authors: Friedman-Klabanoff DJ, Jensen TL, Gelber CE, Pinapati RS, Tan JC, Deye GA, Regules JA, Bergmann-Leitner ES, Laurens MB, Travassos MA, Goll JB, Takala-Harrison S, Berry AA
articles.details: Serology study using peptide arrays quantified anti-CSP antibody responses from controlled human malaria infection trials in unprotected rCSP vaccine recipients, protected RTS,S recipients, and unprotected RTS,S recipients. Protected RTS,S vaccinees had higher breadth of responses to junctional, central repeat, and C-terminal CSP region peptides, and higher intensity responses to 2 C-terminal peptides including part of Th3R, with higher IgA breadth to C-terminal epitope PNDPNRNV.

articles.headline: IL-6 elevation associated with vaccine adverse events
articles.title_original: Cytokines Associated With Moderate and Severe Adverse Events During a Sporozoite Malaria Vaccine Trial With Controlled Human Malaria Infection.
articles.authors: Jatt LP, Gillespie KM, Van P, Hoffman SL, Jackson LA, Murphy SC, Heath JR, Kublin JG
articles.details: Prospective malaria vaccine trial with controlled human malaria infection investigated cytokine profiles during moderate and severe adverse events (AEs). Interleukin-6 was elevated on days when individuals experienced moderate and severe AEs, identifying a potential biomarker for systemic reactogenicity in malaria vaccine development.

articles.headline: CCR5Δ32 frequency differs in Plasmodium-infected vs uninfected
articles.title_original: Updating the frequency of CCR5Δ32 in Brazil: Descriptive analysis of malaria cases and controls from Acre State, Amazon region.
articles.authors: Ziliotto M, Ellwanger JH, Dombrowski JG, Marinho CRF, Pontillo A, Chies JAB
articles.details: Genetic epidemiology study compared CCR5Δ32 allele frequencies in women from Acre State, Amazon region between Plasmodium-infected individuals (2.88%) and uninfected controls (1.04%). The low Δ32 allele frequencies in Acre compared to other Brazilian regions were attributed to particular ancestry patterns influenced by European colonization.

articles.headline: Nigeria surveillance shows dominant chloroquine-sensitive P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Molecular Surveillance of Malaria in Nigeria Reveals a Low Frequency of Antimalarial Resistance Markers and Clonal Expansion of Chloroquine-Sensitive Infections.
articles.authors: Ajibaye O, Oboh-Imafidon M, Ajibola O, Olusola F, Egwu CO, Amambua-Ngwa BN, Diop MF, Mohammed N, D'Alessandro U, Falade C, Meremikwu M, Olukosi AY, Oriero EC, Amambua-Ngwa A
articles.details: Targeted amplicon sequencing of 895 P. falciparum clinical isolates from Nigeria (mostly 2017-2021) genotyped 101 SNPs and 36 nonsynonymous mutations associated with drug resistance. High complexity infections (≥2 parasite clones) occurred in ~30%; Pfcrt wild-type (chloroquine-sensitive) alleles in 55.1%, with chloroquine-resistant CVIET haplotype in 40.9% (26.5% monoclonal); Pfdhfr resistance alleles in >50%, Pfdhps A437G in 87.3%, and 12 nonsynonymous Pfkelch13 propeller mutations observed. Chloroquine sensitivity is now dominant while antifolate resistance persists.

articles.headline: Machine learning SST index improves long-lead malaria prediction
articles.title_original: A Machine Learning-Based Dynamic SST Index for Long-Lead Malaria Prediction in the Peruvian Amazon.
articles.authors: Pan M, Hu S, Janko MM, Zaitchik BF, Takahashi K, Lescano AG, Munayco CV, Pan WK
articles.details: Retrospective analysis applied self-organizing map machine learning to tropical sea surface temperature (SST) variability for malaria prediction in the Peruvian Amazon. A dynamic SST index showed better performance (higher correlation coefficients, lower RMSE) in generalized linear models compared to traditional ENSO index, with lead times exceeding 3 months. The index captures ENSO evolution and influences malaria through local temperature and humidity changes.

articles.headline: NLM screener app shows limited accuracy for malaria detection
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Accuracy of the National Library of Medicine (NLM) Screener Application in Screening for Malaria Parasites Among Blood Donors at the Korle-Bu Blood Bank, Ghana: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Appiah SB, Osei S, Brown NK, Amoah LE, Owusu EDA
articles.details: Cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study in 300 blood donors at Korle-Bu Teaching Hospital, Accra, Ghana evaluated NLM machine-learning screener app against microscopy, RDT, and PCR (reference standard). PCR identified P. falciparum in 18/300 donors (6.0%). NLM screener showed sensitivity 38.9% (7/18; 95% CI: 20.3-61.4), specificity 60.6% (171/282; 95% CI: 54.8-66.2), PPV 5.9% (7/118), NPV 94.0% (171/182), with negligible agreement (κ = -0.001).

articles.headline: Apicortin marks apical complex but is not essential
articles.title_original: Apicortin defines the Plasmodium apical conoid body but is dispensable for the parasite life cycle.
articles.authors: Zeeshan M, Mishra A, Pashley SL, Markus R, Brady D, Holder AA, Moores C, Tewari R
articles.details: Molecular cell biology study of transgenic P. berghei expressing GFP-tagged Apicortin showed localization at the apical end of invasive parasite stages within mosquito vectors. Super-resolution and expansion microscopy revealed Apicortin forms a distinct ring-like structure in the apical complex region. Deletion of the Apicortin gene had no effect on parasite development, indicating the protein is not essential.

articles.headline: Community knowledge of asymptomatic malaria across endemic countries
articles.title_original: To what extent do people in malaria-endemic countries know asymptomatic malaria infections? A systematic review.
articles.authors: Bouwe T, Ahissou NCA, Kikuchi K, Iwagami M, Nonaka D
articles.details: Systematic review of 11 studies (from 483 articles screened) on awareness of asymptomatic malaria infections in endemic countries. Among general public, knowledge ranged 14.2%–79.8%; health personnel showed 88% awareness. Qualitative studies revealed varied and lacking knowledge of asymptomatic malaria with refusal to adhere to interventions targeting asymptomatic carriers. Authors conclude education on asymptomatic malaria is necessary for disease control and acceleration of elimination.

articles.headline: RTS,S vaccine shows reduced efficacy in high malaria transmission settings
articles.title_original: Ensuring the Public Health Impact of Malaria Vaccination in High-Transmission Settings.
articles.authors: Bell GJ, Emch ME, Moormann AM, Bailey JA
articles.details: Reviews WHO-recommended RTS,S and R21 P. falciparum vaccines. RTS,S displayed lower efficacy in higher transmission settings during trials, with periods of negative efficacy beginning approximately three years after primary vaccine series, partially due to greater natural immunity in control groups. Both vaccines showed safety and efficacy in trials; early RTS,S pilot results suggested reductions in severe disease and death. Authors recommend enhanced surveillance and supplementary interventions in high-transmission settings.

articles.headline: TEX1 protein critical for parasite development in mosquito and liver stages
articles.title_original: The Putative E3 Ubiquitin Ligase TEX1 Is Required for Nuclear Biology and Developmental Progression of Plasmodium berghei in the Liver.
articles.authors: Schmid M, Golomingi R, Franke-Fayard B, Caldelari R, Rehmann R, Roques M, Heussler VT
articles.details: Functional characterization of putative E3 ubiquitin ligase TEX1 in Plasmodium berghei using gene knockout and imaging. TEX1 knockout parasites showed impaired ookinete formation, reduced oocyst numbers and sporozoites reaching salivary glands in mosquito stages; in hepatic stages showed reduced growth, abnormal nuclear division, impaired maturation with dramatic decline in detached cell formation and blood stage infectivity. TEX1 represents a promising target for liver stage antimalarial interventions based on its role in nuclear replication and division.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence higher in inland counties of Liberia among under-fives
articles.title_original: Geographic variation in malaria prevalence among children under five in coastal and inland counties of Liberia: analysis of the 2022 Malaria Indicator Survey.
articles.authors: Tugbeh RS, Veliah G, Jennifer HG, Shaw TEB, Tchakondo S, Gayi KS, Assogba AS, Kandjoni Y
articles.details: Analysis of 2022 Liberia Malaria Indicator Survey with 2,189 children aged 6–59 months. Malaria prevalence was 8.3% in coastal and 12.7% in inland regions (Z=3.33, p=0.001). Non-ITN use: 1.5 times higher odds of malaria; severe anaemia AOR 6.2 (95% CI 2.34–14.81); poor households AOR 6.4 (95% CI 3.06–13.27); infants 0–11 months lower odds AOR 0.04 (95% CI 0.01–0.17); urban vs rural AOR 0.6 (95% CI 0.40–0.86). Targeted interventions recommended for inland counties and high-risk groups.

articles.headline: GIS modeling confirms malaria risk on ancient Anatolia trade routes
articles.title_original: A 'useless marsh … breeding fever and insect pests': assessing malaria risk on roads through Cilicia Pedias (modern Türkiye) and its historical impact in antiquity and the Middle Ages.
articles.authors: Browning DC Jr, Nicovich JM
articles.details: Geographic information system-based multilayer malaria risk model applied to ancient southern Anatolia (Cilicia Pedias). Model-produced risk maps correlate well with historical accounts of malaria-like illness from 4th century BCE through 12th century CE for travelers along major routes between Anatolia, Mesopotamia, and the Levant. Results support interpretation of reported sicknesses as malarial infection and highlight the peril for immune-naïve travelers; demonstrates application of GIS and text-based methodologies in evaluating malaria's impact in premodern Mediterranean.

articles.headline: Correction to metabolic barrier article on PKLR-deficient erythrocytes
articles.title_original: Correction to "Metabolic Rigidity as a Mechanical Barrier to Malaria: Flickering Loss in PKLR-Deficient Erythrocytes".
articles.details: Correction to prior article on metabolic rigidity as mechanical barrier to malaria in PKLR-deficient erythrocytes (no abstract provided).

articles.headline: IDO-1 inhibitor combined with artemether-lumefantrine shows enhanced malaria efficacy
articles.title_original: 8-Fluorotryptanthrin, an immunomodulatory IDO-1 inhibitor enhances the efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine in the treatment of malaria.
articles.authors: Katnoria S, Kumar R, Suri M, Salunke DB, Kaur S
articles.details: 8-Fluorotryptanthrin (C1), an IDO-1 inhibitor, demonstrated strong in vitro activity against P. falciparum (3D7, Dd2 strains) and P. berghei ANKA. In P. berghei-infected BALB/c mice, C1 at 20 mg/kg significantly reduced parasitaemia and restored liver/kidney functions with enhanced survival. Combination therapy of C1 with artemether-lumefantrine achieved complete parasitaemia suppression and full survival with enhanced immune restoration by D7 and D14, with elevated nitric oxide and reactive oxygen species and balanced cytokine profiles (IL-10 and IL-4 increased; TNF-α and IFN-γ reduced on D14).

articles.headline: Epigenetics and chromatin structure regulate var2csa and placental parasite binding
articles.title_original: Epigenetics and chromatin structure regulate var2csa expression and the placental binding phenotype in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Lenz T, Sirel M, Hoppe H, Shafeeq S, Roch KL, Ribacke U
articles.details: Study of VAR2CSA-expressing and placenta-binding P. falciparum parasites. Transcriptional activation of var2csa strongly associated with depletion of repressive H3K9me3 marks. Chromatin conformation capture revealed significant repositioning of var2csa relative to heterochromatic telomeric clusters. 5-methylcytosine occupancy detected in all var genes but independent of transcriptional repression and switching. Findings provide high-resolution insights into mechanisms regulating antigenic variation at the epigenetic and chromatin structure level.

articles.headline: Rare genetic variants bias population genetic inference in malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Rare variation in malaria parasites biases population-genetic inference.
articles.authors: Goldberg A
articles.details: Reanalysis of genomic data from 18 worldwide P. falciparum populations showed distributions enriched for rare variants that bias effective population size estimates and inflate πN/πS ratios (nonsynonymous to synonymous variation). Observed genealogies more consistent with multiple-merger coalescent than Kingman coalescent. Rare variants interact with mathematical properties of ratio statistics to systematically inflate gene-level πN/πS estimates even absent selection. Filtering rare variation reveals previously masked selection candidates including merozoite surface proteins.

articles.headline: Metabolic pathways underlying permethrin resistance in Anopheles gambiae
articles.title_original: Metabolomic changes underpinning permethrin resistance in the Anopheles gambiae malaria vector from Cameroon.
articles.authors: Djova SV, Christopher MW, Ibrahim SS, Kouamo M, Dinglasan RR, Garrett TJ, Wondji CS
articles.details: Metabolomic analysis comparing resistant and susceptible A. gambiae from Cameroon using UHPLC-HRMS/MS. Resistant mosquitoes showed upregulated inosine, nicotinic acid, dipeptides, amino acids, fumarate, uracil, and aldopentose, suggesting altered metabolic detoxification and enhanced energy production. Susceptible mosquitoes showed increased N-acetyl-aspartic acid, xanthurenic acid, 2-hydroxyglutarate, 3-hydroxykynurenine, propanoylcarnitine, and L-pipecolic acid associated with neurotoxicity and energy disruption. Findings elucidate metabolic pathways driving permethrin resistance and pyrethroid cross-resistance emergence.

articles.headline: TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α axis controls host energy metabolism and Plasmodium resistance
articles.title_original: Reprogramming of host energy metabolism mediated by the TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α axis plays a key role in host resistance to Plasmodium infection.
articles.authors: Matteucci KC, Leite NPS, Assis PA, Hirako IC, Pioto F, Ojelabi O, Toller-Kawahisa JE, Vaz LG, Costa DL, DA Silva JS, Alves-Filho JC, Gazzinelli RT
articles.details: Study in P. chabaudi-infected mice showing TNF signaling required for iNOS and HIF-1α expression and enhanced glycolysis in monocytes. TNF signaling suppressed physical activity and food intake while enhancing glucose uptake by liver and spleen and controlling parasitaemia. iNOS−/−, TNFRΔLyz2, and HIF-1αΔLyz2 mice showed impaired TNF release and glycolysis in monocytes, with increased parasitaemia and impaired disease tolerance. Results indicate TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α-induced glycolysis in monocytes plays critical role in host defense and sickness behavior.

articles.headline: Imported malaria in Sri Lanka dominated by P. falciparum and P. vivax
articles.title_original: The profile of imported malaria in Sri Lanka from 2013 to 2023.
articles.authors: Chulasiri P, Fernando D, Seneviratne S, Wickremasinghe R, Aluthweera C, Fernando T, Gunasekera K, Mendis K
articles.details: Analysis of 532 imported malaria cases diagnosed 2013–2023 in malaria-free Sri Lanka. Over half (68.5%) were work-related travelers. P. falciparum comprised 48.1% (mostly Africa), P. vivax 40.5% (mostly India); P. ovale took median 95 days to manifest versus 14 days for other species. P. falciparum showed 7 recrudesced, P. vivax 3 relapsed. Forty-six cases were severe (45 P. falciparum) with one death. Physician diagnosis delay (median 3.41 days) exceeded patient healthcare-seeking time (median 2.42 days, p<0.001).

articles.headline: Anopheles gambiae feeding and resting behavior in Senegal
articles.title_original: Feeding and resting behaviours of natural Anopheles gambiae s.l. populations in an area of low malaria transmission in south-western Senegal: A comparative study between mainland and island settings.
articles.authors: Diop M, Coulibaly Y, Lo C, Dia AK, Ndoye NA, Tine EG, Diagne NS, Coulibaly YI, Sangaré M, Thiaw O, Faye MB, Tall MF, Ciss O, Faye O, Niang A, Konaté L, Lobo NF, Tine RCK, Niang EHA
articles.details: Longitudinal study (September 2020–November 2021) of 765 Anopheles mosquitoes in mainland and island areas of Senegal. A. gambiae comprised 91.1% of specimens. On mainland, indoor-resting females had significantly higher human blood index (HBI) at 76.2% versus 47.6% for outdoor-resting females (p=0.035); on island, HBI was lower with no significant difference between indoor (25.1%) and outdoor (31.1%, p=0.51). Endophilic populations showed greater HBI in mainland versus island settings (p=2.63×10⁻⁵).

articles.headline: Malaria inequities and control disparities in Madagascar
articles.title_original: Understanding inequities in the malaria landscape of Madagascar: a scoping review of current evidence.
articles.authors: Rajaofera MJN, Liu W, Reziky AM, Tomboanona S, Kuang D, Xia Q
articles.details: Scoping review of literature published 2015–2024 on malaria in Madagascar. Found disparities between urban and rural areas, with remote regions experiencing higher disease burden.
