Key interventions included insecticide-treated nets (ITNs), indoor residual spraying (IRS), seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC), artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs), and rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs). Drug resistance, diagnostic failures, and socioeconomic inequalities hindered effective control. Recommends strengthening healthcare systems, improving supply chains, and expanding prevention in underserved areas.

articles.headline: Severe malaria risk factors and treatment gaps in Kenyan hospitals
articles.title_original: Using sentinel surveillance system data to characterize severe malaria illness and quality of malaria case management among hospitalized patients in Kenya, 2017-2024.
articles.authors: Itoh M, Lucchi N, Schultz J, Agogo GO, Munyua P, Chege D, Mwenda DN, Akoth S, Sumbi V, Shieshia M, Kandie R, Onyango EO, Hines JZ
articles.details: Analysis of 8,487 hospitalized patients (June 2017–July 2024) from sentinel surveillance facilities: 2,197 tested malaria-positive, 713 (32.5%) with severe disease, and 16 (0.7%) deaths. Severe illness and death both associated with fever ≥5 days (odds ratios: 3.67 and 8.00, respectively) and referral from another facility (ORs: 3.01 and 3.15). Only 15% of severe cases received both IV artesunate and artemether-lumefantrine per guidelines; 17% received IV quinine. Microscopy concordance with PCR was 79% (21% microscopy-positive cases PCR-negative).

articles.headline: MMS Américas network launches for malaria molecular surveillance
articles.title_original: MMS Américas: a regional network for malaria molecular surveillance.
articles.authors: MMS Américas Network
articles.details: International researchers and public health professionals convened in Panama in August 2025 to discuss malaria molecular surveillance strategies to accelerate elimination in the Americas. The new network MMS Américas will promote sharing of expertise, protocols, and data among participating institutions.

articles.headline: Community health interventions improve malaria awareness in Cameroon
articles.title_original: The role of community co-designed interventions for malaria prevention and control in conflict-affected communities in Cameroon's Southwest and littoral regions.
articles.authors: E O EBM, Yankam BM, Ogundeko-Olugbami O, Donovan L, Counihan H, Hawkings H, Maxwell K, Baker K, Zoungrana Y, Tanue EA, Noukeme BM, Metuge A, Nganmou I, Divine A, Coril E, Gilbert K, Sachop N, Ako S, Pensha J, Tabong AO, Itoe BT, Wanji S, Berryman E, Jarman E, Omam EN, Omam LA
articles.details: Qualitative study (May 2021–April 2024) of 189 participants in conflict-affected southwestern and littoral Cameroon evaluating three co-designed interventions: Community Health Participatory Approach (CoHPA), community health worker (CHW) supportive supervision, and health vouchers. Communities perceived increased knowledge of malaria prevention and control, increased participation in household prevention activities. CHWs valued supervision, which increased trust in CHW services. Vouchers increased treatment access and timely referrals, though unsubsidized comorbidities created payment challenges.

articles.headline: PMCA4b expression influences artemisinin sensitivity via oxidative stress
articles.title_original: Decoding the multifaceted role of erythrocyte PMCA4b in oxidative stress-mediated malaria protection and artemisinin resistance.
articles.authors: Agrohi P, Garg S, Biswas S, Maurya P, Kumar V, Sharma J, Goswami B, Sil SK, Singh MP, Chand SK, Shalini S, Singh OP, Dhangadamajhi G, Mishra N, Ramalingam S, Mallick PK, Singh S
articles.details: Study of ATP2B4 genotypes and PMCA4b expression in Indian population comparing severe malaria, uncomplicated malaria, and healthy controls. Low PMCA4b expression correlated with increased intra-erythrocytic calcium, oxidative stress, and reduced in-vitro P. falciparum growth and artemisinin sensitivity. PMCA4b inhibition via resveratrol confirmed functional role in calcium and oxidative stress regulation. Host genetic variation in PMCA4b influences artemisinin efficacy and serves as potential biomarker for predicting therapeutic response.

articles.headline: Novel microtubule proteins identified in P. falciparum gametocytes
articles.title_original: An atlas of novel microtubule-associated proteins in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Niedermüller K, Piwon N, Mesen-Ramirez JP, Zink L, Lemcke S, Liffner B, Fernandes CF, Farias GB, Alder A, Löw C, Dahan N, Wilson DW, Cubillán-Marín J, Gilberger TW
articles.details: Study employing proximity-dependent biotinylation using PfSPM3 as bait to identify 37 high-confidence proteins in the subpellicular microtubule (SPMT) network of P. falciparum gametocytes. Spatial localization atlas generated for 11 enriched candidates showing distinct localization patterns within the SPMT network. Functional knockout of PF3D7_1003400 interfered with gametocytogenesis and prevented falciform gametocyte development. Findings provide novel insight into cytoskeletal dynamics and inner membrane complex organization required for gametocyte transmission.

articles.headline: Avian haemosporidian parasites in zoo populations across Thailand
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreaks in Humboldt penguins and haemosporidian infections in avian hosts in zoos across Thailand.
articles.authors: Rahmah SA, Jairak W, Thanee S, Saedan A, Mahadthai P, Novianto D, Saiwichai T, Kaneko O, Pattaradilokrat S, Kaewthamasorn M
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Study of 406 blood and tissue samples from 146 Humboldt penguins and 260 individuals representing 38 bird species across 6 Thai zoos. Haemosporidian DNA detected in 40 penguin samples (27.4%) and 125 bird samples (48.0%). Four Plasmodium species identified: P. collidatum, P. relictum, P. juxtanucleare, and P. gallinaceum, plus 7 additional Plasmodium lineages, as well as Haemoproteus sacharovi, 4 Haemoproteus lineages, and Leucocytozoon caulleryi. Multiple parasites previously reported in Thai domesticated and wild bird populations.

articles.headline: PfEMP1-specific antibodies predict lower parasite density in children
articles.title_original: Antibody levels to variant and conserved Plasmodium falciparum antigens predict reduction in parasite burden in Malian children.
articles.authors: Damena D, Dang L, Barry A, Renn JP, Gaoussou S, Mahamar A, Attaher O, Issiaka D, Morrison RD, Misal SA, Dicko A, Duffy PE, Fried M
articles.details: Study using immunoprecipitation-mass spectrometry on plasma from susceptible and semi-immune Malian children. High antibody levels to DBLγ11, DBLζ5, and two Plasmodium helical interspersed subtelomeric proteins (PF3D7_0201600 and PF3D7_0532300) predicted significantly reduced risk of high parasite density infections (odds ratios: 0.74, 0.80, 0.81, and 0.79, respectively). Opsonic phagocytosis of DBLγ11 and DBLζ5 coated beads significantly higher in high-antibody plasma samples (p<0.0001), with phagocytosis index positively correlated with antibody levels (r²=0.61 and r²=0.49).

articles.headline: AI-based malaria diagnosis achieves 87–91% accuracy overall
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Accuracy of Utilizing Artificial Intelligence for Malaria Diagnostic: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Faratisha IFD, Yunita KC, Rahmawati HR, Fitri LE, Winaris N, Muflikah L
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 10 studies with 6,754 patients evaluating AI diagnostic accuracy for malaria. Pooled sensitivity was 87.7% (95% CI: 78.2–93.4) and specificity 91.4% (95% CI: 77.3–97.1) compared to microscopy. AI achieved sensitivity of 90.7% and specificity of 88.3% compared to polymerase chain reaction (PCR). AI-based systems provide high accuracy, automation, and lower costs, serving as a promising complementary tool for malaria diagnosis and control.

articles.headline: Urine-based malaria test insufficient as standalone diagnostic
articles.title_original: Comparative Performance of Blood and Urine Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Detecting Malaria in Febrile Children: A Cross-Sectional Analysis.
articles.authors: Babatola AO, Adeniyi AT, Ojo TO, Ilori BF, Olatunya OS
articles.details: Cross-sectional study comparing malaria rapid diagnostic test (RDT) performance in febrile children using blood and urine samples. Malaria positivity rates were 67.4% for SD Bioline blood RDT, 65.3% for CareStart blood RDT, and 45.3% for Fyodor urine malaria test. Excellent agreement between SD Bioline and CareStart (Cohen's kappa 0.859). Fyodor UMT demonstrated sensitivity 58.6%, specificity 100%, positive predictive value 100%, and negative predictive value 40%, indicating limited utility as standalone diagnostic due to low sensitivity.

articles.headline: Malaria infection impairs adipsin diagnostic specificity for preeclampsia
articles.title_original: Impact of Malaria Infection on the Diagnostic Performance of Adipsin for Preeclampsia in Pregnancy: A Case-Control Study.
articles.authors: Opoku Mensah B, Anim EO, Ahenkorah Fondjo L, Manu AO
articles.details: Case-control study of 200 pregnant women (20–42 weeks gestation) stratified into 4 groups (n=50 each): normotensive without malaria, normotensive with malaria, preeclamptic with malaria, and preeclamptic without malaria. Plasma adipsin levels significantly elevated in malaria and preeclampsia (p<0.001), with highest concentrations in coinfection. Adipsin correlated strongly with C5a (ρ=0.695), IL-6 (ρ=0.687), TNF-α (ρ=0.645), and moderately with parasite density (ρ=0.553). Diagnostic specificity for preeclampsia was reduced in malaria-infected (62.1%, AUC=0.719) versus malaria-negative (87.9%, AUC=0.823) women.

articles.headline: Mosquito breeding sites and poor housing drive malaria outbreak risk
articles.title_original: Epidemiological investigation of a possible malaria outbreak in Arba Minch town, Gamo Zone, Southern Ethiopia, 2023.
articles.authors: Dana DD, Belay MM, Ango TS, Zegene GM, Getachew S, Deressa W
articles.details: Case-control study of 94 case-control pairs (Oct 2022-Dec 2023) reported 5,181 confirmed malaria cases (positivity 22.7%, attack rate 41.2 per 1,000). Plasmodium falciparum comprised 71%. Risk factors: proximity to mosquito breeding sites (AOR 5.45), stagnant water (AOR 5.69), poor housing (AOR 4.40); consistent LLIN use protective (AOR 0.30).

articles.headline: Natural kinase inhibitors identified as potential antimalarial compounds
articles.title_original: Computational screening of natural inhibitors against Plasmodium falciparum kinases: Toward novel antimalarial therapies.
articles.authors: Alruwaili M, Younas S, Elderdery AY, Alruwaili I, Millis J, Khan MU, Ejaz H
articles.details: Computational study screening natural compounds against Plasmodium falciparum casein kinase 2 and cGMP-dependent protein kinase. Pharmacophore-based virtual screening and docking identified Ligand-9 (binding energy -7.490 kcal/mol vs PKG) and Ligand-13 (-11.468 kcal/mol vs CK2) as lead compounds. Molecular dynamics and binding energy calculations supported Ligand-13's potential as antimalarial candidate.

articles.headline: Malaria outbreaks in endemic settings
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreaks in endemic settings?
articles.authors: Steketee RW
articles.details: Title-only abstract; cannot determine study design, population, methods, or results.

articles.headline: Glutathione inhibitors show promise for multidrug-resistant malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Glutathione redox system as a therapeutic target for Plasmodium: Opportunities, Challenges, and future perspectives.
articles.authors: Mahato RP, Kumbham S, Kumar S, Ch S
articles.details: Review article examining glutathione's essential role protecting Plasmodium from oxidative stress. Discusses key enzyme inhibitors targeting GSH redox pathway (GST, GR, GS, GP) and their potential for combination therapies. Identifies GSH metabolic pathways as promising antimalarial drug targets for multidrug-resistant parasites despite challenges in selective targeting and drug resistance.

articles.headline: Civil society urges caution on gene drive mosquitoes for malaria control
articles.title_original: Exploratory conversations with biodiversity-oriented civil society groups on the potential applications of gene drive-modified mosquitoes for malaria control in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Finda MF, Sambo M, Malika G, Njalambaha R, Salum S, Okumu FO
articles.details: Qualitative study with 10 Tanzanian biodiversity-focused civil society organizations exploring perspectives on gene drive-modified mosquitoes for malaria control (8 interviews, 1 focus group, 3 large group discussions). Participants expressed cautious support for research but raised concerns about scientific uncertainty, limited local expertise, and inadequate accountability. They advocated for robust evidence before environmental release and early, inclusive community engagement.

articles.headline: Haematology analyzer improves cost-effectiveness of imported malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: A comparison of the cost-effectiveness of the current standard of care and of the haematology analyser XN31-microscopy combination for diagnosing malaria in a nonendemic country.
articles.authors: Picot S, Bienvenu AL
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis at Lyon University Hospital comparing four diagnostic approaches for imported malaria: microscopy, RDT+microscopy, LAMP+microscopy, and XN-31 analyzer+microscopy. The analyzer combined with microscopy achieved superior diagnostic accuracy and reduced time-to-result, yielding improved cost-effectiveness. Findings specific to French healthcare costs; generalizability requires validation in other settings.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria prevention reduces non-malarial infections and antibiotic use
articles.title_original: Indirect benefits of seasonal malaria chemoprevention for non-malarial pediatric infections and routine antibiotic use in real-world programmatic settings: a pre-post study using positive and negative controls.
articles.authors: Gebreegziabher EA, Ouattara M, Bountogo M, Coulibaly B, Boudo V, Ouedraogo T, Lebas E, Hu H, O'Brien KS, Hsiang MS, Glidden DV, Arnold BF, Lietman TM, Sié A, Oldenburg CE
articles.details: Pre-post study across 51 health facilities in rural Burkina Faso examining effects of monthly sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine/amodiaquine (SMC). Compared SMC administration weeks to post-intervention period, finding declines in pneumonia (0.7 per 1,000 person-weeks; IRR 0.86), diarrhea (0.2 per 1,000 person-weeks; IRR 0.83), acute malnutrition (0.05 per 1,000 person-weeks; IRR 0.71), and antibiotic prescriptions (0.90 per 1,000 person-weeks; IRR 0.88). Malaria diagnoses declined 3.7 per 1,000 person-weeks (IRR 0.62).

articles.headline: Anopheles funestus in Tanzania shows three genetically distinct populations
articles.title_original: Microsatellite analysis of genetic differentiation among populations of the malaria vector Anopheles funestus across mainland Tanzania reveals contrasted patterns of geographic isolation and gene flow.
articles.authors: Mapua SA, Samb B, Nambunga IH, Kweyamba PA, Mkandawile G, Bwanaly H, Kaindoa EW, Odero JO, Masalu JP, Kahamba NF, Hape EE, Galizi R, Govella NJ, Okumu FO, Tripet F
articles.details: Genetic analysis of 654 Anopheles funestus specimens across Tanzania using 12 microsatellite DNA markers. Found moderate genetic divergence among populations (FST up to 0.288 between southeastern coastal and inland regions) despite geographic separation. Neighbor-joining analysis identified three distinct population groups; Mtwara population showed exceptional differentiation. Patterns suggest anthropogenic gene flow rather than isolation by distance shapes population structure.

articles.headline: Cambodia's real-time surveillance system accelerates progress toward malaria elimination
articles.title_original: A bespoke, real-time surveillance system for malaria elimination in Cambodia: architecture, applications and impact.
articles.authors: Ngor P, Rekol H, Sovannaroth S, Eng T, Ry R, Sok K, Yoem R, Ou V, Hem V, Chang P, Try R, Ringwald P, White LJ, Maude RJ
articles.details: Description of Cambodia's locally built Malaria Information System integrating surveillance, diagnostics, treatment, logistics, and entomology. Mobile and web applications enable real-time case reporting at all health system levels with offline functionality. System supports 1-3-7 surveillance model and integrated drug efficacy surveillance. Demonstrates feasibility of locally developed digital systems for malaria elimination when integrated with community networks and strong governance.

articles.headline: Knowledge and barriers shape prevention behaviors for Plasmodium knowlesi in Malaysia
articles.title_original: Synthesis of qualitative evidence on community experiences and perceptions of Plasmodium knowlesi malaria and factors influencing prevention and healthcare-seeking behaviours in Malaysia.
articles.authors: Htay MNN, Mohd NHN, Oo MMT, San LPP, Abas AL
articles.details: Qualitative evidence synthesis of five studies (2022-2024) on community perspectives regarding Plasmodium knowlesi malaria in Malaysia. Analysis identified three themes: (1) community risk perception shaped by knowledge, fear of hospitalization, and income loss; (2) environmental, occupational, and access barriers to prevention and healthcare-seeking; and (3) prevention practices reflecting local knowledge and formal measures. Suggests integrating local perspectives into programs to enhance sustainability.

articles.headline: Baseline survey documents high malaria transmission in Ghanaian vaccine sites
articles.title_original: The Malaria Vaccine Implementation Programme study area in Ghana: results of a household survey prior to the introduction of the RTS,S/AS01 vaccine.
articles.authors: Welaga P, Gyan T, Koram K, Hodgson A, Odei-Lartey E, Agbokey F, Gyaase S, Awini E, Adu-Gyasi D, Amenuvegbe GK, Dadzie D, Adam AR, Hammond J, Sarfo A, Nuhu AR, Peprah PT, Twumwaa L, Boateng E, Snell P, Fogelson A, Okine RNA, Hamel MJ, Milligan P, Moore KA, Binka FN, Afari EA, Asante KP
articles.details: Household survey of 6,778 households (7,768 children aged 5-48 months) conducted Feb-Mar 2019 prior to RTS,S/AS01E vaccine introduction in Ghana. Malaria prevalence by RDT: 21% (95% CI 18-23). ITN ownership 87%; 62% slept under ITN prior night. Among 22% reporting fever in two weeks, 72% sought care, 40% tested for malaria, 42% received antimalarials. Malnutrition (MUAC ≤13.5 cm) present in 17%.

articles.headline: Ancient parasite genomes reveal 50-year adaptation and evolution patterns
articles.title_original: Major features of parasite adaptation revealed by genomes of Plasmodium falciparum population samples archived for over 50 years.
articles.authors: Amambua-Ngwa A, Diop MF, Drakeley CJ, d'Alessandro U, Kwiatkowski DP, Conway DJ
articles.details: Genomic sequencing of 54 Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Gambian placental blood (1966-1971), the oldest population-based archived samples sequenced to date. No drug resistance alleles detected in baseline samples. Comparison across ~50-year period revealed major directional allele frequency changes at gdv1 (sexual conversion regulation), Pfsa1/Pfsa3 (sickle-cell trait associated), and surface/transporter protein loci. Strong positive selection signatures at surface proteins targeted by acquired antibody responses.

articles.headline: Spleen's role in RBC loss during malaria infections
articles.title_original: The role of the spleen in red blood cell loss caused by malaria: A mathematical model.
articles.authors: Moss R, Dini S, Kho S, Barber BE, Buffet PA, Rajasekhar M, Price DJ, Anstey NM, Simpson JA
articles.details: Mathematical model of RBC dynamics incorporating the spleen as a compartment in P. falciparum and P. vivax infections. Calibrated to splenectomy patient data, the model demonstrates the spleen plays a significant role in removing both infected and uninfected RBCs. In chronic infections, the ratio of circulating uninfected RBCs lost to splenic retention per circulating uninfected RBC lost to parasitisation is 17:1 for P. falciparum and 82:1 for P. vivax, substantially higher than previously published estimates.

articles.headline: Wolbachia as emerging vector control strategy in Anopheles
articles.title_original: The symbiotic Wolbachia in Anopheles and its role in reducing the transmission of Plasmodium: updates and prospects.
articles.authors: Nehra R, Dhanda S, Singh K, Singh H, Bharti PK
articles.details: Review of Wolbachia pipientis prevalence and potential in Anopheles mosquitoes for vector control. Natural Wolbachia infection identified in 31 Anopheles species. Highest natural infection rate: 91% in An. gambiae population (DRC, strain wAnsA). Transinfected An. stephensi achieved stable 100% infection frequency with nearly complete cytoplasmic incompatibility and moderate fitness costs. Field deployment requires further research on host-symbiont interactions and long-term population replacement potential.

articles.headline: High malaria prevalence in onchocerciasis co-endemic districts
articles.title_original: Malaria in onchocerciasis-endemic populations: patterns in two districts in the Oti and Volta regions of Ghana.
articles.authors: Ampadu B, Osei S, Ashong Y, de Souza DK, Owusu EDA
articles.details: Retrospective case-control study of 345 participants in Ghana (Nkwanta North District, Oti region and Adaklu District, Volta region). Overall malaria prevalence 42.6% (95% CI 37.5-47.8%), with 53.26% in Nkwanta North and 38.7% in Adaklu. Onchocerciasis/malaria co-infection reached 81.7% in Nkwanta North. Weak but significant association between malaria and onchocerciasis (OR=2.03 overall; OR=3.52 in Adaklu when adjusted for age and sex). RDT sensitivity 89.2%, specificity 71.9% versus nested PCR.

articles.headline: Plant extracts target multiple P. falciparum asexual blood stages
articles.title_original: Targeting the intra-erythrocytic life cycle of Plasmodium falciparum unveils antimalarial drug discovery starting points from Drymaria cordata and Macaranga monandra.
articles.authors: Youbi KA, Tali MBT, Ngouana V, Wendji Monkam DS, Souleyman H, Dongmo YKM, Dongmo KJJ, Nana O, Woutouoba Ntieche D, Dize D, Mbouna CDJ, Tsouh Fokou PV, Boyom FF
articles.details: Antiplasmodial screening of extracts from Drymaria cordata and Macaranga monandra against chloroquine-sensitive (Pf3D7) and multidrug-resistant (PfDd2) P. falciparum strains. M. monandra methanolic bark extract most potent (IC50 vs PfDd2: 2.46 μg/mL, SI>203; IC50 vs Pf3D7: 1.02 μg/mL, SI>487). Active fractions F2 and F3 arrested ring-stage development, lysed trophozoite-infected RBCs, and blocked merozoite egress. UHPLC-MS identified 17 metabolites including lecanoric acid as promising antimalarial candidate.

articles.headline: Hepatocyte autophagy mechanisms limit liver-stage parasite development
articles.title_original: Hepatocyte Autophagy in Malaria: Current Concepts, Emerging Mechanisms, and Future Therapeutic Directions.
articles.authors: Berbudi A, Khairani S, Setyowati EY, Kwarteng A
articles.details: Review of hepatocyte autophagy in Plasmodium liver-stage infection. Hepatocytes employ noncanonical autophagy (CASM) driving LC3 lipidation onto parasitophorous vacuole membrane via V-ATPase-ATG16L1, activating parasite-associated autophagy response (PAAR) to limit early parasite development. Parasite proteins UIS3 and UIS4 enable evasion by sequestering LC3 and remodeling actin. Parasites exploit GABARAP paralogs to activate TFEB for lysosomal biogenesis and nutrient access. Understanding these mechanisms crucial for host-directed therapeutics.

articles.headline: Serological markers detect focal P. falciparum transmission in pre-elimination
articles.title_original: Serological evidence of continued exposure to Plasmodium falciparum among residents in a near-elimination setting.
articles.authors: Daud MM, Rahayu AA, Ilyas M, Hitipeuw D, Fitriana F, Syahada JH, Nuranindita R, Amirudin FA, Saputra EY, Jeon BY, Cahyani NKD, Pandupuspitasari NS, Han JH, Muh F
articles.details: Community cross-sectional study from May-June 2025 in Menoreh Hills villages (Sedayu, Kemejing, Kembaran, Wadas) assessing antibody responses to blood-stage antigens PfEBA175 and PfRh5 in 120 exposed individuals and 24 controls. PfEBA175 seroprevalence ranged 10-40%, significantly higher in Kembaran (40%, p<0.001) and Wadas (23%, p=0.003). PfRh5 demonstrated universal immunogenicity with 53-70% seroprevalence across villages. Village-specific risk factors (indoor biting, previous malaria history, outdoor nocturnal activities, education, travel) correlated with antibody profiles.

articles.headline: Caregivers show high malaria vaccine acceptance with perception-driven disparities
articles.title_original: Determinants of caregivers' acceptance of the malaria vaccine: The case of Bamenda and Bamenda III Health Districts in Cameroon.
articles.authors: Aboki LN, Nlinwe NO, Ebai CB, Niba LL, Atanga MBS
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 1000 caregivers of children ≤5 years in Cameroon assessing malaria vaccine acceptance. Overall acceptance 89.6%. Significant factors: source of information (community health events vs social media, OR=1.663, p=0.037), personal perception (very rejecting vs very accepting, OR=0.098, p<0.001), sex (male vs female, OR=0.704, p=0.028), religion (other vs Christian, OR=0.386, p=0.001), occupation (NGO vs self-employed, OR=2.92, p=0.045).

articles.headline: Transient neuropsychiatric complications follow P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: A Case Series of Transient Neuropsychiatric Manifestations Following Plasmodium Falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Bhattacharjee D
articles.details: Case series reporting transient neuropsychiatric manifestations following P. falciparum malaria. No abstract provided; detailed clinical features and outcomes cannot be extracted from title alone.

articles.headline: Malaria funding correlates with mortality reduction in children
articles.title_original: Funded investments contributed to the reduction of malaria morbidity and mortality in children under five: fifteen years retrospective study from 2009 to 2023 in Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Akotionga EA, Yaro JB, Kote B, Bakiono F, Ouedraogo T, Nassa S, Batiene V, Nitiema AP, Kouyate B, Nebie SP, Yonaba S, Laokri S, Bazira L, Sirima SB
articles.details: Retrospective analysis from 2009-2023 in Burkina Faso examining impact of health investments on malaria outcomes in children <5. Health sector total investment USD 13,545 million with 15.3% allocated to malaria. Malaria case fatality rate decreased from 3.4% to 1.3% in children <5. Three regions showed significantly higher malaria mortality risk: Sahel (IRR=4.1), Boucle du Mouhoun (IRR=3.5), and Nord (IRR=3.4) compared to Centre region.

articles.headline: Triple co-infection with malaria, dengue, COVID-19 causes sepsis
articles.title_original: Sepsis secondary to co-infection with malaria, dengue and COVID-19: a case report.
articles.authors: Guibla I, Zabramba A, Traore SIS, Tankoano AI, Ilboudo CS, Ouédraogo RFA, Ouédraogo MD, Poda A, Traoré IA
articles.details: Case report of 36-year-old woman in Burkina Faso with triple co-infection: P. falciparum malaria, dengue (DEN3 serotype), and COVID-19 complicated by sepsis and multiple organ dysfunction. Clinical presentation: acute respiratory distress, severe hypoxemia, hepatic dysfunction with cytolysis and cholestasis, SOFA score 7. COVID-19 diagnosis delayed 10 days due to overlapping clinical and biological features with malaria and dengue.

articles.headline: Arboviral surveillance identifies dengue in non-malaria febrile patients
articles.title_original: Molecular diagnosis of arboviruses (dengue, Chikungunya, yellow fever, Rift Valley fever, Mayaro, West Nile, and Zika viruses) in non-malaria acute febrile illnesses in Kenya's Garissa County, July 2023, and Mombasa County, July 2024.
articles.authors: Roba A, Kiiru J, Kirui E, Langat P, Mbeneka M, Wanyonyi M, Nasengo J, Lagu HI, Achol E, Affara M, Gehre F, Hassan H, Odiambo E, Dugay F, Hussein A, Okemwa F, Swaleh S, Thani S, Gichana R, Karissa I, Ndia M
articles.details: Prospective arboviral surveillance during 2023-2024 in Kenya using molecular diagnostics. Garissa County: screened 326 malaria-negative febrile patients, identified 1.23% dengue PCR-positive (95% CI 0.36-3.23%). Mombasa County: screened 289 non-malaria febrile patients, identified 9.00% dengue (95% CI 6.17-12.90%). No other arboviruses (Chikungunya, yellow fever, Rift Valley fever, Mayaro, West Nile, Zika) detected. Highlights need for scaling arboviral diagnostic capacity in peripheral health centres.

articles.headline: P. malariae represents overlooked malaria burden with drug resistance
articles.title_original: Plasmodium malariae is an overlooked malaria parasite with emerging challenges.
articles.authors: Shankar H, Kumar G, Ahmed N, Florentin A
articles.details: Review of Plasmodium malariae, the least studied of five human malaria species. Presents with chronic low-grade parasitemia and suboptimal clinical features, challenging diagnosis and control. Emerging evidence indicates increasing antimalarial drug resistance and reduced susceptibility to first-line antimalarials. Diagnostic methods currently unavailable. Chronic infection capacity threatens malaria elimination efforts. Recommends enhanced molecular diagnostics, targeted therapeutics, and improved surveillance systems.

articles.headline: Surveillance system performance and rising malaria cases in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the malaria surveillance system and trends in Jimma town, Southwest Ethiopia, 2024: a mixed-method study.
articles.authors: Nuh AY, Fikru C, Regassa D, Serba HA, Abreham A, Mattew B, Nasir E, Fikadu H, Mulugeta H, Abubekir H, Christine L, Adam MA, Oloock R, Gizaw T
articles.details: A mixed-method assessment of malaria surveillance in 10 health facilities in Jimma Town, Southwest Ethiopia (October 2024) evaluated data from 2012-2016. Malaria cases increased from 432 to 11,824 with P. falciparum accounting for 56.4%. The surveillance system was simple and flexible but limited by incomplete reporting and weak feedback mechanisms. Strengthening data quality and private-sector engagement are essential for Ethiopia's malaria elimination efforts.

articles.headline: Community perspectives on non-invasive malaria diagnostic approaches
articles.title_original: "It doesn't require blood!": Perceptions around non-invasive malaria testing tools in Indonesia, Peru, and Rwanda.
articles.authors: Fargnoli V, Calarco S, Thomas C, Thomas C, Mone Iye CN, Paz Soldan VA, Morrison AC, Serumondo J, Nshimiyimana L, Nsaba Uwera YD, Marbán-Castro E, Shilton S, Tetteh KKA
articles.details: A qualitative study in Indonesia, Peru, and Rwanda (October-November 2023) with 155 participants explored community perceptions of non-invasive malaria diagnostics based on saliva, exhaled volatile organic compounds, and transdermal detection. Participants valued comfort and ease of use compared to blood draws, particularly for children and remote areas. Major concerns were lack of accuracy data compared to established methods and inability to differentiate malaria species.

articles.headline: Iron supplementation shows minimal impact on malaria risk in children
articles.title_original: Oral iron supplements for children in malaria-endemic areas.
articles.authors: Itzkovich M, Neuberger A, Harris I, Yahav D, Paul M, Gilboa M
articles.details: A Cochrane systematic review of 40 trials (33,785 children) in malaria-endemic areas found iron supplementation resulted in little or no difference in clinical malaria risk (RR 0.93, 95% CI 0.87-1.00; high-certainty evidence) but slightly reduced severe malaria risk (RR 0.90, 95% CI 0.81-0.98; high-certainty evidence). Iron plus folic acid may result in little to no difference in severe malaria requiring admission. Iron resulted in little or no difference in mortality or hospitalization rates.

articles.headline: Paracetamol shows no benefit for acute kidney injury in severe malaria
articles.title_original: A phase I/II randomized controlled clinical trial to assess the feasibility, safety, and preliminary effectiveness of paracetamol in resolving acute kidney injury in children with severe malaria.
articles.authors: Paasi G, Namayanja C, Ongodia P, Okalebo CB, Okiror W, Abongo G, Masifa G, Amorut D, Muhindo R, Olupot-Olupot P
articles.details: A phase I/II randomized trial in eastern Uganda with 40 hospitalized children (mean age 6.54 years) with malaria-induced acute kidney injury compared paracetamol 20 mg/kg 6-hourly for 48 hours (n=20) versus tepid sponging (n=20) for renal recovery. Mean time to renal recovery was not significantly different between groups (paracetamol 0.491 hours longer, 95% CI -9.265 to 10.248; p=0.921). No significant differences in adverse events were observed.

articles.headline: COVID-19 vaccination status predicts favorable malaria vaccine intentions
articles.title_original: Exploring factors affecting malaria vaccination intention and COVID-19 vaccine uptake: evidence from a joint analysis in rural Senegal.
articles.authors: Cortaredona S, Sokhna C, Maradan G, Ba EH, Seror V
articles.details: A cross-sectional survey in rural Senegal (2023) of 795 adults found 35.6% had received COVID-19 vaccine and 58.6% expressed favorable intentions toward a hypothetical free adult malaria vaccine. COVID-19 vaccinated individuals were 60% more likely (p<0.001) to report favorable malaria vaccination intentions. Perceived disease severity was more strongly associated with COVID-19 vaccine uptake, while perceived disease susceptibility more strongly predicted malaria vaccine intentions.

articles.headline: Thymol derivatives show antimalarial activity against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Thymol Derivatives as Antimalarial Agents: Synthesis, Activity Against Plasmodium falciparum, ADMET Profiling, and Molecular Docking Insights.
articles.authors: Ali AH, Retnosari R, Jamil SNH, Norazmi NAZ, Zuhaidi ND, Lam SD, Chieng S, Agustar HK, Chandru K, Md Iqbal N, Yee Ling L, Latip J
articles.details: Synthesized thymol derivatives were evaluated against resistant and sensitive P. falciparum strains and P. knowlesi. Compounds 4 and 6 exhibited notable potency against P. falciparum 3D7 (EC50 6.01±1.7 µM and 6.8±1.1 µM) with selectivity indices of 16.5 and 14.6, and were non-toxic to mammalian cells (CC50>99 µM). Molecular docking revealed compounds 3, 4, and 6 had strong binding affinities to PfCRT (-8.4, -8.3, and -8.3 kcal/mol). Compounds 4 and 6 represent promising antimalarial lead candidates.

articles.headline: Primaquine addition dramatically accelerates gametocyte clearance with artemether-lumefantrine
articles.title_original: The Effect of Artemether-Lumefantrine Combined with a Single Dose of Primaquine on Plasmodium falciparum Gametocyte Clearance and Post-Treatment Infectivity to Anopheles arabiensis.
articles.authors: Minwuyelet A, Yewhalaw D, Petronio Petronio G, Di Marco R, Atenafu G
articles.details: A prospective cohort study in Northwest Ethiopia (January-September 2025) of 96 cases compared artemether-lumefantrine (AL) alone versus AL plus single low-dose primaquine. AL+SLD-PQ achieved faster gametocyte clearance: 15.2% versus 28.9% at day 3 (p=0.001) and 0% versus 12.2% at day 7 (p=0.033). Post-treatment infectivity to Anopheles arabiensis was lower with AL+SLD-PQ at day 3 (6.8% vs 28.6%; p=0.001) and day 7 (0% vs 7.3%; p=0.01).

articles.headline: Apical polar ring protein essential for P. falciparum invasion
articles.title_original: The essential function of the apical polar ring during the blood stage of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Gurung P, Back PS, Ali I, Hernandez GM, Dvorin JD
articles.details: Laboratory characterization of PfAPR1, an apical polar ring-resident protein in P. falciparum, revealed its essential role in asexual replication. Conditional knockout caused defects in daughter cell segmentation and subpellicular microtubule organization. PfAPR1-deficient parasites contacted host red blood cells but failed to form tight junctions, resulting in complete invasion blockade. These findings identify the apical polar ring's molecular architecture as a potential antimalarial target.

articles.headline: Msp1 K1 allele associated with severe malaria in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of Plasmodium falciparum Merozoite Surface Protein 1 and 2 Genes and Their Allele Association With Malaria Severity, Seasons, and Sex in Bamenda, North West Cameroon.
articles.authors: Achungu CR, Anong DN, Faustin K, Shey RA, Tabe CJ, Djouosseu CA
articles.journal: Can J Infect Dis Med Microbiol
articles.details: Genetic diversity of P. falciparum Msp1 Block 2 and Msp2 Block 3 genes was evaluated in 281 and 194 samples from Bamenda, Northwest Cameroon. For Msp1 Block 2, all family-specific alleles (RO33, K1, MAD20) were detected with RO33 most prevalent (44.7%), while K1 was linked to severe malaria. For Msp2 Block 3, both family-specific alleles (3D7, FC27) were found with 3D7 most prevalent (56.7%). An association was found between Msp2 Block 3 alleles and sex (χ2=8.856, p=0.012).

articles.headline: Co-creation approach sustains community malaria services in Thailand
articles.title_original: Sustaining community-based malaria services through stakeholder engagement: lessons from co-creation in northeastern Thailand.
articles.authors: Jongdeepaisal M, Sirimattayanant M, Prasert O, Maneenet S, Sopa P, Napchit S, Yuenyao N, Singkham N, Charoensup I, Kanjanasuwan J, Thongard T, Asarath SA, Khirikoekkong N, Osterrieder A, Maude RR, Pell C, Cheah PY, Maude RJ
articles.details: An implementation study in Buntharik district, northeastern Thailand (April 2023-June 2024) conducted 36 engagement activities with approximately 550 participants to co-create locally-owned health materials. The 2024 Buntharik health calendar integrated malaria information with priority local health issues. Key findings emphasized that responding to local health concerns and community expectations was essential for co-creation success, but sustainability requires additional funding sources to maintain local health worker capacity.

articles.headline: Insecticide resistance documented in Indian malaria vectors
articles.title_original: A multicentric study on understanding the bionomics of Indian malaria vectors across diverse eco-epidemiological settings.
articles.authors: Mohanty AK, Eapen A, Singh H, Singh K, Baharia RK, Jain V, Mazumdar D, Sharma S, Shriram AN, Vidhya PT, Sharma A, Thiruvengadam K, Rahi M
articles.details: A multicentric entomological study across 14 districts in 8 Indian states (2021-2023) characterized vector bionomics. Anopheles culicifacies was prevalent in all states with indoor man-hour density of 11.95 in Kanker, Chhattisgarh, and outdoor of 27.16 in Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh. An. minimus exhibited high anthropophagic behavior (human blood index 0.94). Insecticide resistance was documented: An. culicifacies resistant to alpha-cypermethrin and deltamethrin, and An. stephensi resistant to multiple insecticides.

articles.headline: Intermittent preventive treatment reduces school absenteeism in Tanzanian children
articles.title_original: The impact of intermittent preventive treatment for malaria on school absenteeism among primary school-aged children in Handeni District Council, Tanzania: quasi-experimental study.
articles.authors: Kidumule FM, Omary H, Zacharia A, Mringo B, Shonde A, Ngasala B, Ottaru TA
articles.details: A quasi-experimental study in Handeni District Council, Tanzania, followed 346 school-aged children before (January-May 2024) and after (January-May 2025) introduction of intermittent preventive treatment for schoolchildren using dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine. Overall absenteeism decreased from 13% to 10% (p=0.001), while malaria-related absences declined from 27.8% to 11.3%, with attendance increasing by 6.7%. Most pupils (91.9%) received all three treatment doses.

articles.headline: Seven-year malaria trend shows resurgence after 2018
articles.title_original: Analysis of a seven-year trend in malaria prevalence at Pawe General Hospital, Northwestern Ethiopia: a retrospective cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Legesse F, Berhane N
articles.details: Retrospective review of 155,669 suspected cases (2015–2021) found 11,495 confirmed infections (7.4%, 95% CI: 7.2–7.5%), predominantly P. falciparum (90.8%), with P. vivax (6.2%) and mixed infections (3.0%). Males had significantly higher prevalence (8.8% vs 6.2%; OR = 1.48, 95% CI: 1.43–1.54, p < 0.001). After 2018, prevalence resurged. Authors conclude strengthened surveillance needed to investigate vector resistance and environmental changes.

articles.headline: Vector control reduces malaria incidence 41% in high-resolution climate model
articles.title_original: High resolution physically based modelling reveals malaria incidence reduction by vector control measures.
articles.authors: Dieng MDB, Munga S, Tompkins AM, Zornoza MG, Caminade C, Fersch B, Arnault J, Khagayi S, Schwarz M, Kariuki S, Bigogo G, Kunstmann H
articles.details: Hybrid modeling framework linking WRF/WRF-Hydro and VECTRI at 1 km and 50 m resolutions, using 2007–2022 Kenya data, reproduced observed trends with mean monthly deviation ±100–150 cases. ITN coverage reduced entomological inoculation rate by 58% and malaria incidence by 41%, with highest efficacy under warm (~C) and moderately wet (150–250 mm) conditions. Authors conclude integrating environmental process modeling with targeted control strategies provides cost-effective framework for sustainable malaria management under changing climatic conditions.

articles.headline: IPTp-SP delivery effectiveness higher in trained facilities, Mali and Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of antenatal care delivery of intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine in the context of an integrated strategy with seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Mali and Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Koita K, Kayentao K, Worrall E, Huyen Ton Nu Nguyet M, Coulibaly O, Bognini JD, Tinto H, Rouamba T, Dembele M, Biébo B, Diarra S, Briand V, Hill J
articles.details: Cluster-randomised trial compared IPTp-SP delivery integrated with SMC in Mali (79.2% vs 68.7% in intervention vs control, p=0.006) and Burkina Faso (52.3% vs 28.7%, p<0.0001). ANC provider training in enhanced facilities predicted higher IPTp-SP delivery by directly observed therapy. Predictors of receiving IPTp-SP included routine ANC visits, gestational age, and multiparity. ANC providers found the integrated strategy acceptable and feasible for improving delivery effectiveness.

articles.headline: P. ovale accumulation in spleen causes rupture in treated patient
articles.title_original: Splenic accumulation of intact Plasmodium ovale sensu lato-infected red blood cells in a patient presenting with splenic rupture.
articles.authors: Benabdelmoumen G, Itani O, Joste V, Houzé S, Hardy D, Bavozet F, Sylla M, Kho S, Anstey NM, Consigny PH, Sissoko A, Buffet P
articles.details: 35-year-old man with acute P. ovale s.l. infection developed splenic rupture shortly after starting antimalarial treatment (atovaquone-proguanil followed by artesunate). Spleen histology revealed 10-fold accumulation of intact infected erythrocytes in spleen parenchyma versus general circulation, with additional accumulation in extra-splenic vessels suggesting cytoadherence. Findings suggest severe pathophysiologic complications during treatment of ovale malaria.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol of microRNAs in malaria-arbovirus coinfections
articles.title_original: MicroRNAs profiling in malaria and arbovirus coinfection: A systematic review protocol.
articles.authors: Deutou Wondeu AL, Nka AD, Ka'e AC, Ngoufack Jagni Semengue E, Masso S, Fisemou P, Mobolaji AT, Marymag Z, Fomboh Tah C, Nsen Bughe R, Mbu Nji A, Galgani A, Pirrò S, Colizzi V, Fon Mbacham W
articles.details: This is a systematic review protocol (not yet completed) registered in PROSPERO (CRD42024600379). Will search PubMed, Scopus, Web of Science, Embase, CENTRAL, CINAHL from January 2000 onwards for observational studies and trials reporting miRNA expression in malaria-arbovirus coinfections (dengue, chikungunya, Zika). Narrative synthesis will be performed. Aims to identify miRNA expression patterns and potential biomarkers for improved diagnostics and therapeutics of coinfections.

articles.headline: China's malaria elimination: health system and social development analysis
articles.title_original: Malaria elimination practices in China from the perspective of health system and social development.
articles.authors: Song X, Liu Z, Li N, Chen L, Liu M, Liu J, Zhang Y, Wang M, Ren M
articles.details: Qualitative study with semi-structured interviews of 42 key informants from China and international organizations, conducted July 2023–July 2025. Thematic analysis identified factors influencing China's achievement of malaria elimination certification (June 30, 2021): 71.43% cited living environment, 66.67% health education, 64.29% working environment, 59.52% water/sanitation, 57.14% agricultural patterns, 52.38% employment, and 100% health system. Authors conclude social development and healthcare infrastructure are essential for long-term success of malaria control.

articles.headline: Hemozoin-based malaria diagnostics: scoping review of eight technology classes
articles.title_original: Hemozoin as a Diagnostic Biomarker: A Scoping Review of Next-Generation Malaria Detection Technologies.
articles.authors: Berbudi A, Khairani S, Kwarteng A, Otuonye NM
articles.details: Scoping review of 24 studies on hemozoin-based malaria diagnostics (8 technology classes). Magneto-optical systems (Hz-MOD, Gazelle™, RMOD) demonstrated highest operational readiness with robust specificity but reduced sensitivity at low parasitemia. Photoacoustic Cytophone showed promising sensitivity and noninvasive detection. Raman/SERS platforms achieved sub-100 infected cells/mL sensitivity but remain laboratory-bound. Microfluidic and smartphone-based tools offer emerging alternatives. All modalities showed reduced detection of early ring forms. Authors conclude further clinical validation and standardized comparison needed for field deployment.

articles.headline: Community health workers detect and treat childhood malaria, Senegal
articles.title_original: How the One Health approach can make effective the World Health Organization's recommendations on integrated management of childhood illnesses: Case of community-based home management of malaria in the Ferlo of Matam, Senegal.
articles.authors: Kaly JSN, Keita IM, Doucoure E, Sall FB, Ba AM, Mango AA, Gomis BL, Dieng MT, Ndao M, Diallo SM, Diop A, Fall KA, Coly O, Cisse O, Faye M, Diallo I, Thiongane A
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of community-based home management strategy in Ferlo, Senegal targeting children under five. Among 2,307 cases: 1,464 fever, malaria testing rate 98.9% with 21.1% positivity (96.4% treated with artemisinin-based combinations); 209 diarrhea cases (67.9% treated with ORS/zinc); respiratory infections in 96.5% with pneumonia in 55.6% (treated with amoxicillin); 1,156 received vitamin A; 443 dewormed; 129 referred for immunization. Authors conclude strategy improved childhood illness care with added value of One Health transdisciplinary approach.

articles.headline: Illegal gold mining linked to 300% malaria increase in Yanomami
articles.title_original: Investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak: gold mining and malaria.
articles.authors: de Angeli Dutra D, Fontes J, Braga ÉM, Mordecai E
articles.details: Satellite imagery and panel regression analysis (2016–2023) revealed ~300% increase in malaria cases among Yanomami Indigenous group coinciding with illegal gold mining expansion. One standard deviation increase in gold mining associated with 20–46% rise in malaria incidence 1–2 years later. Each standard deviation increase in forest edge perimeter associated with 55% increase in malaria cases. Authors conclude illegal gold mining and resulting forest fragmentation major drivers of high malaria burden.

articles.headline: Systematic review: 62% of Ethiopian malaria patients delay treatment-seeking
articles.title_original: Determinants of delay in treatment-seeking among malaria patients attending health facilities in Ethiopia from 2010 to 2024: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Mekonen EG, Aemro A
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 12 studies (4,511 subjects) from Ethiopia (2010–2024). Pooled prevalence of delay in treatment-seeking (>24 h after symptom onset) was 62% (95% CI 46–77). Determinants included: lack of knowledge, distance from facility, cost, transportation access, being a farmer, self-medication practice, no prior malaria history, low income, no health insurance, large family size, no education, fear of drug side effects, no family death history. Authors recommend health literacy campaigns, fee reduction, community outreach expansion, and engagement with affected communities.

articles.headline: Placental malaria linked to reduced linear growth and increased BMI
articles.title_original: Association between placental malaria, postnatal linear growth, and body mass index in the Dogon Longitudinal Study, Mali.
articles.authors: Merritt C, Vincenz C, Dolo Z, Shedden K, Strassmann BI
articles.details: Prospective cohort study (N=317) from Mali examining placental malaria (PM) and postnatal growth to age 5. Severe parasite density in placenta associated with reduced linear growth at 6 months (−0.83 cm, 95% CI −1.63 to −0.03, p = 0.042), continuing to age 5. Severe density also associated with increased BMI: at 2 years (0.56 kg/m², p = 0.011), 3 years (0.82 kg/m², p = 0.001), 4 years (1.09 kg/m², p < 0.001), 5 years (1.35 kg/m², p < 0.001). Authors provide first evidence PM associated with increased postnatal BMI.

articles.headline: RDT result recording accuracy high in Côte d'Ivoire despite systematic bias
articles.title_original: Accuracy of recording of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.authors: Bedia-Tanoh VA, Konaté-Touré A, Kangah-Kouakou OMA, Mian ANN, Tanoh AM, Humes M, Griffith K, Aponte JJ, Hilton E, Cooper S, Lindblade KA, William Y
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of RDT recording accuracy in Côte d'Ivoire (Aug–Dec 2023). Among 11,161 RDT-register pairs, 57.9% were positive by panel reference. Overall agreement κ = 0.83 (95% CI 0.77–0.88), PPV 90.7%, NPV 94.8%. Negative/invalid results more frequently misrecorded as positive (5.9% vs 2.0%). Recording errors occurred more often when malaria diagnosis or prescription noted, suggesting systematic bias. HCW education, cadre, and experience associated with agreement. Authors conclude enhanced training and RDT-register comparison would improve surveillance data quality.

articles.headline: IL-10 and IFN-γ mark symptomatic malaria in children
articles.title_original: Distinct systemic cytokine signatures define symptomatic malaria in children: insights from a longitudinal study in Togo.
articles.authors: Bara FD, Simfele HC, Koudema T, Bako S, Sakyi ME, Kudakpo AY, Mensah C, Donkor E, Malm FP, Ritter M, Awandare GA, Katawa G, Amenga-Etego LN
articles.details: Longitudinal cohort study in Togo examining 60 children treated with artemether-lumefantrine: 25% were P. falciparum PCR-positive, of which 33.3% developed symptomatic malaria. Symptomatic children exhibited significantly elevated IL-6, IFN-γ, and IL-10 compared to non-infected and asymptomatic groups (p<0.01). IL-10 correlated strongly with parasite density (r=0.911, p=0.001) and negatively with Ct values (r=-0.973, p=0.001).

articles.headline: Insecticide resistance and population structure in An. coluzzii across West Africa
articles.title_original: Genomic population structure and insecticide resistance mechanisms in the malaria vector An. coluzzii across contrasting bioclimatic zones in West Africa.
articles.authors: Amoako EK, Bennett KL, Hernandez-Koutoucheva A, Debrah I, Morang'a CM, Binaansim S, Asoala VA, Ariana C, Malm KL, Awandare G, Miles A, Clarkson CS, Amenga-Etego LN
articles.details: Genome-wide study of An. coluzzii populations in Ghana, Mali, and Côte d'Ivoire using SNPs and inversion karyotypes. Restricted gene flow found between northern savannah and southern forested regions. Northern savannah showed greater Vgsc pyrethroid resistance and metabolic markers (carboxylesterases, Gste2), while coastal Greater Accra exhibited unique selection at Cyp12f and UDP glucuronosyltransferase, suggesting environmental conditions restrict gene flow and shape resistance distribution.

articles.headline: Chromatin dynamics regulate P. falciparum blood-stage gene expression
articles.title_original: Chromatin state dynamics during the Plasmodium falciparum intraerythrocytic development cycle.
articles.authors: Brown AS Jr, Llinás M, Mahony S
articles.details: Genome-wide characterization of chromatin accessibility, histone modifications, and transcription factor binding throughout the 48-hour P. falciparum asexual intraerythrocytic developmental cycle. Study reveals dynamic shifts in chromatin states linked to gene activation/repression and groups transcription factors by chromatin preferences, providing a global overview of how chromatin dynamics coordinate blood-stage parasite development.

articles.headline: Reactive case detection reduces wealth-based malaria prevention inequalities
articles.title_original: Development of a new wealth index for Tanzania: the moderated effect of the implementation of 1,7- malaria reactive community-based testing and response (1,7-mRCTR) by socioeconomic position (SEP) with malaria prevention.
articles.authors: Gao C, Huang S, Yin H, Lu S, Liu L, Mlacha YP, Chaki P, Zhou XN, Xiao N, Richardson S, Wang D
articles.details: Difference-in-Differences analysis of 19,686 households in Tanzania surveyed in 2015 and 2018 during 1,7-mRCTR implementation. Poorest households had significantly lower odds of LLIN ownership (OR=0.62, 95% CI 0.54-0.70) and use (OR=0.53, 95% CI 0.45-0.62) vs. wealthiest. The intervention reduced this gap, with significantly greater increases in LLIN ownership (OR=1.26, 95% CI 1.03-1.56) and use (OR=1.88, 95% CI 1.25-2.82) among poorest households.

articles.headline: Congenital malaria diagnosis and management in non-endemic settings
articles.title_original: Congenital Malaria in Non-Endemic Settings: A Literature Review and Update on Diagnosis and Management.
articles.authors: Patterson CA, Nabarro LE, Kumar S, Nolder D, Chiodini PL
articles.details: Scoping literature review of 26 articles published 2013-2023 on congenital malaria. Proposes definition as peripheral blood smear or PCR detection of Plasmodium spp. in infants <28 days old regardless of symptoms, or infants <1 year without endemic travel history. Presents diagnostic algorithm emphasizing PCR for detecting low parasitemia cases that light microscopy may miss.

articles.headline: Azithromycin-containing IPTp reduces parasitemia at delivery in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Safety and efficacy of azithromycin-containing regimen as an intermittent preventive therapy for malaria in pregnant women: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
articles.authors: Lin IF, Sulaiman H, Tran VK, Basri S, Pham CT, Nguyen LMD, Tey MY, Wu Mph CL, Tam KW, Loh EW
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 8 RCTs in moderate sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance areas comparing azithromycin-containing intermittent preventive therapy (IPTp) with AZ-lacking SP-based regimens. AZ-containing regimens reduced parasitemia at delivery vs. SP-only (OR=0.71, 95% CI 0.57-0.88), with similar safety profiles to SP-based regimens (OR=1.11, 95% CI 0.76-1.62 for adverse events). No significant differences in preterm birth, low birth weight, or neonatal death.

articles.headline: Supply chain failures increase malaria morbidity and mortality in Africa
articles.title_original: Drug stockouts and treatment delays in African health systems: impact on malaria morbidity and mortality.
articles.authors: Obeagu EI, Abdulrahman AO, Anetor JI
articles.details: Review examining multifactorial causes of antimalarial drug stockouts and treatment delays in African health systems, including procurement, supply chain, and infrastructure weaknesses. Discusses how stockouts interrupt treatment, worsen clinical outcomes, facilitate drug-resistance spread, and erode public trust. Emphasizes need for comprehensive strategies including supply-chain strengthening, forecasting/inventory improvements, and enhanced delivery capacity.

articles.headline: Imported P. falciparum in young child with atypical presentation
articles.title_original: Alert for Imported Malaria in Non-Endemic Areas: A Case Report of Atypical Falciparum Malaria in a Young Child and Diagnostic Experience.
articles.authors: Feng J, Zhou Y, Zhang B, Huang M
articles.details: Case report of a 2-year-11-month-old boy presenting with fever and diarrhea after returning from Sudan. Diagnosis identified through blood routine re-examination informed by travel epidemiology history, confirmed as P. falciparum by rapid diagnostic test and microscopy. Exemplifies 'clinical suspicion → targeted laboratory testing → definitive pathogen identification' framework for early detection of pediatric imported malaria with atypical presentations.

articles.headline: Baseline malaria and schistosomiasis prevalence in Malawi primary schools
articles.title_original: Repeated biannual cross-sectional surveys in primary schools set baseline seasonal and spatial surveillance for malaria and schistosomiasis in the Shire Valley Transformation Programme (SVTP), Malawi.
articles.authors: Kapolo P, Chiepa B, Mbewe RB, Kapumba B, Kambewa E, Kaunga L, Coleman S, Chirombo J, Mzilahowa T, Jones CM, Stanton MC, Russell Stothard J
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Four cross-sectional surveys in 21 schools across wet and dry seasons examining 4,176 children aged 7-13 years. Overall malaria prevalence 10.8% (95% CI 9.8-11.7%), urogenital schistosomiasis 36.5% (95% CI 35.1-38.0%), co-infection 5.2% (95% CI 4.5-5.9%). Strong spatial heterogeneity observed with malaria exceeding 25% and schistosomiasis surpassing 50% in certain schools, indicating substantial unmet public health needs.

articles.headline: Polyalthia longifolia shows antiplasmodial and spleen-protective effects in mice
articles.title_original: Spleen protective role, Antiplasmodial and Antioxidant activity of Polyalthia longifolia in mice infected with Plasmodium berghei parasite.
articles.authors: Onobrudu DA, Okpoghono J, Acha JO, Dunkwu CC, Enyi KC, Onyesom I
articles.details: Preclinical study in 36 Swiss albino mice (30 infected with P. berghei NK 65 strain) comparing ethanol extract of P. longifolia leaves at graded doses (100, 200, 400 mg/kg/day) with artemether-lumefantrine (2 mg/kg/day). Treatment dose-dependently reduced parasitemia and increased spleen antioxidants (GSH, GPx, CAT, SOD, TAC, ferritin); standard drug achieved highest survival followed by 400 mg/kg/day extract.

articles.headline: Digital platform improves seasonal malaria chemoprevention delivery in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Lessons from using SALAMA (DIGIT HCM-health campaign management platform) to implement and optimize seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, Mozambique.
articles.authors: Mussa A, Rodrigues M, Enosse SM, Cook L, Bunce L
articles.details: Implementation of DIGIT Health Campaign Management platform for 2024 seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, Mozambique, transitioning from paper-based to digital data collection. Real-time dashboards improved sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine administration and reduced wastage from >10% to ≤5% in Moma district. Administrative coverage rates (75-109%) showed better alignment with survey-based household coverage (~74%) in digital campaigns compared to paper-based (93-105% vs. ~79%).

articles.headline: Molecular basis of RDT failure in P. falciparum with gene deletions
articles.title_original: Molecular, Transcriptomic, and Proteomic Characterization of Plasmodium Infections that Evade Detection by Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Mizan Aman, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Nikulkova M, Kessler A, Wang Z, Patel A, Tolessa T, Teka T, Tesfaye D, Lukas B, Lemessa D, Zemede M, Legesse F, Srivastava H, Sullivan SA, Yan G, Yewhalaw D, Carlton JM
articles.details: Study of 148 individuals with suspected malaria in Ethiopia identified 29 P. falciparum PCR-positive cases, with 26/29 (89.7%) false-negative by PfHRP2/pLDH RDT. Molecular and proteomic characterization revealed 27/29 (93.1%) harbored pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 gene deletions and 22/29 (75.9%) had negligible pfhrp2 transcripts; 10 samples had detectable PfLDH peptides but none had detectable PfHRP2 or PfHRP3 peptides.

articles.headline: GPT-4o outperforms traditional models for malaria death attribution via verbal autopsy
articles.title_original: Comparison of verbal autopsy using a large language model to biologically confirmed causes of death for malaria and other communicable diseases among children in six sub-Saharan African countries.
articles.authors: Carshon-Marsh R, Wen R, Ng TKS, Kamadod R, Bogoch I, Bondy SJ, Witek TJ Jr, Jha P
articles.details: Study compared three computer models (GPT-4o, InSilicoVA, InterVA-5) to assign causes of death using verbal autopsy in 3,129 under-five deaths from six sub-Saharan African countries (enrolled December 2016–December 2022). GPT-4o correctly classified 60 (46.2%) of 130 malaria deaths versus 39 (30.0%) for InSilicoVA and 30 (23.1%) for InterVA-5. At population level, GPT-4o achieved cause-specific mortality fraction accuracy of 0.36 versus 0.07 for InSilicoVA and 0.08 for InterVA-5. Authors conclude GPT-4o demonstrates superior performance in identifying malaria-attributed deaths.

articles.headline: Uganda field epidemiology fellowship strengthens malaria surveillance and policy 2015–2022
articles.title_original: Uganda Public Health Fellowship Program's Contributions to Malaria Control Programs 2015-2022: Strategies, Implementation Challenges, and Opportunities.
articles.authors: Ario AR, Kwiringira A, Migisha R, Kwesiga B, Bulage L, Kadobera D, Kisaakye E, Asio A, Zalwango MG, Zalwango JF, Rutazaana D, Opigo J, Harris JR, Rollins K, Niang M, Boore AL, Nelson LJ, Belay K
articles.details: Two-year training program integrated post-Master's fellows into Ministry of Health programs including National Malaria Control Program. From 2015 to 2022, the Uganda Public Health Fellowship Program led or supported 50 malaria projects: 14 surveillance, 11 outbreak investigations, 7 epidemiological studies, 5 case studies, 6 quality improvement projects, 3 policy briefs, and 4 training projects. Projects informed policy decisions and strengthened surveillance, coordination, and outbreak response.

articles.headline: LILRB1 intronic variant rs7246537 reduces malaria and cerebral malaria risk in Senegalese population
articles.title_original: A Functional Intronic Variant of LILRB1 Associated with Clinical Malaria in the Senegalese Population.
articles.authors: Dieppois C, Chambonnier G, Gallardo F, Torres M, Thiam A, Mbengue B, Dieye A, Rihet P, Paul P
articles.details: Genotyping study in 267 Senegalese individuals (116 severe malaria, 74 mild, 77 controls) identified rs7246537 associated with reduced malaria risk (OR = 0.50, 95% CI: 0.28–0.88, p = 0.0165) and cerebral malaria (OR = 0.44, 95% CI: 0.22–0.86, p = 0.0176). The A allele reduced promoter activity twofold compared to G allele via YY1-binding site modulation. Authors conclude this functional variant reduces malaria susceptibility by modulating LILRB1 expression.

articles.headline: Natural killer cells show reduced IFN-γ production in children with severe malaria
articles.title_original: Altered natural killer cell function in children with severe malaria.
articles.authors: Turyasingura G, Mellencamp KA, Namazzi R, Opoka RO, John CC, Hart GT
articles.details: Flow cytometry evaluation in Ugandan children <5 years with severe malaria (n = 21) versus community controls (n = 19) showed severe malaria associated with higher proportion of LILRB1+ NK cells and higher degranulation only (CD107a+/IFN-γ-) in low-transmission areas. Consistent finding: NK cells with reduced IFN-γ production but unchanged degranulation (CD107a-/IFN-γ+) across both transmission areas in severe malaria versus controls.

articles.headline: Spontaneous splenic rupture as rare complication of Plasmodium vivax infection
articles.title_original: Spontaneous splenic rupture as a rare complication of Plasmodium vivax: a case report.
articles.authors: Mouharir M, Jouabri B, Chahbi Z, Fihri MF, Baba H, Kaddouri S, Elbarni R, Qacif H, Zyani M
articles.details: Case report of a 30-year-old soldier who presented with fever six months post-deployment despite mefloquine chemoprophylaxis; malaria confirmed by blood film. After artemether-lumefantrine initiation, developed acute abdominal pain with hemodynamic instability. Computed tomography revealed subcapsular splenic haematoma with moderate haemoperitoneum. Conservative management (bed rest, fluid resuscitation, transfusion) resulted in favorable outcome with complete pain resolution.

articles.headline: Temporal Fusion Transformers accurately forecast malaria using climate data with R² = 0.95
articles.title_original: Application of a Temporal Fusion Transformer and Long-Term Climate and Disease Data to Assess the Predictive Power and Understand the Drivers for Malaria and Dengue.
articles.authors: Pillay MT, Le MTQ, Takamatsu Y, Phong TV, Kgalane N, Minakawa N
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health
articles.details: Study applied Temporal Fusion Transformers to climate datasets and weekly malaria case records from South Africa and Vietnam to forecast malaria and dengue. Best malaria model achieved R² of 0.95 and MAE of 4.98; dengue models reached R² up to 0.90. Extreme temperature and rainfall were strongest predictors; ENSO and IOD improved longer-range malaria forecasts. Analysis revealed specific temperature and rainfall thresholds associated with elevated transmission.

articles.headline: Chalcogen-phosphoranes inhibit sensitive and resistant P. falciparum with IC₅₀ = 0.7–1.9 μM
articles.title_original: Fast-Acting Chalcogen-Phosphoranes Inhibit Sensitive and Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Strains.
articles.authors: Moura IMR, Barbosa CS, Mendes GR, Aguiar ACC, Cruz FC, Menezes PH, Guido RVC
articles.details: Eight sulfur-containing compounds showed submicromolar potency against P. falciparum 3D7 (IC₅₀s = 0.7–1.9 μM) with selectivity indexes ≥12 over HepG2 and HEK293 cells. Compound 5 induced rapid pyknotic nuclei, showed no cross-resistance with standard antimalarials, and additive interaction with artesunate. Stage-specific assays revealed pronounced activity against ring and trophozoite stages but reduced efficacy against schizonts. In murine P. berghei model, compound 5 showed modest in vivo activity with slightly improved survival.

articles.headline: DHAP achieves 99.2% cure rate; AP baseline efficacy 96.2% in Ghana 2023 uncomplicated malaria
articles.title_original: Therapeutic efficacy of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and artesunate-pyronaridine combinations in the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Ghana, 2023.
articles.authors: Abuaku B, Boateng P, Peprah NY, Asamoah A, Duah-Quashie NO, Matrevi SA, Amoako EO, Quashie N, Owusu-Antwi F, Koram KA, Malm KL
articles.details: One-arm prospective evaluation of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHAP) at five sites and artesunate-pyronaridine (AP) at four sites in children 6 months–9 years with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria (August–December 2023, WHO protocol). PCR-uncorrected cure rates on day 42: DHAP 99.2% (95% CI: 97.6–99.8) with 100% PCR-corrected rates; AP 96.2% (95% CI: 92.9–98.0) with 97.3% PCR-corrected. Day-3 parasitemia prevalence 0.3% (DHAP) and 0.4% (AP); no early treatment failures.

articles.headline: Replication stress increases de novo copy-number variations across P. falciparum genome
articles.title_original: Replication stress increases de novo CNVs across the malaria parasite genome.
articles.authors: Brown N, Luniewski A, Yu X, Warthan M, Liu S, Zulawinska J, Ahmad S, Prasad N, Congdon M, Santos W, Xiao F, Guler JL
articles.details: Low-input genomics study evaluated impact of sub-lethal antimalarial stress on de novo CNV rate in P. falciparum. Significant increase in genome-wide de novo CNVs observed following treatment with antimalarial compound inhibiting replication. De novo CNVs encompassed genes from various cellular pathways participating in human infection. Study emphasizes connection between replication stress, DNA repair, and CNV generation.

articles.headline: miLab automated microscopy achieves 92.1% sensitivity, 89.4% specificity versus conventional microscopy
articles.title_original: Automated microscopy for malaria diagnosis in a reference laboratory in nonendemic settings.
articles.authors: Martín-Ramírez A, Lanza-Suárez M, Berzosa Díaz P, Benito A, Antón-Berenguer V, Rubio JM
articles.details: Prospective analysis of 400 samples (2021–2024) comparing miLab™ digital microscopy with deep learning against conventional microscopy and nested-multiplex PCR in a nonendemic-country reference laboratory. Comparison with thin blood smear: 90.8% concordance, kappa 0.8, sensitivity 92.1%, specificity 89.4%. Parasite density showed correlation coefficient 0.77. Versus NM-PCR: sensitivity 62.8%, specificity 95.4%, concordance 68.9%. Of P. falciparum infections identified by NM-PCR, 63.4% accurately identified by miLab.

articles.headline: Anopheles gambiae dominates coastal areas; A. funestus highest in highland/lake zones in Kenya
articles.title_original: Mapping mosquito diversity in Kenya correlating species distribution with malaria prevalence across varied climatic parameters.
articles.authors: Zablon JO, Goel V, Giesbrecht D, Kariuki L, Mbogo C, Goedel WC, Bailey JA, Matoke-Muhia D
articles.details: Mixed-effect regression analysis of data from 23 Kenyan counties (January 2019–June 2021) examined relationship between climate and mosquito species distribution on malaria prevalence. Malaria prevalence highest in Lake Endemic zone (19%), followed by Coastal (5%), semi-arid seasonal (2%), and low-risk (0.9%). A. gambiae dominated coastal and semi-arid areas; A. funestus highest in highland and lake zones. Insecticide-treated net coverage and environmental factors predicted malaria prevalence.

articles.headline: Outdoor biting of A. gambiae detected in southern Benin with site-specific behavioral variation
articles.title_original: An underestimated risk? Analysis of outdoor malaria transmission in an urban area of Southern Benin.
articles.authors: Konkon AK, Zoungbédji DM, Sovi A, Hoyochi I, Salako AS, Konkon C, Dangnon B, Yahoue G, Lokossou AS, Adjovi RV, Baba-Moussa L, Akogbéto M, Padonou GG
articles.details: Human landing catches conducted indoors and outdoors (April–September 2019) in Porto-Novo, Benin assessed malaria transmission. Of 6,201 mosquitoes collected, 16.2% were A. gambiae s.l. Human biting rates of A. gambiae s.l. varied by site: similar indoors/outdoors in Akron, Hounsouko, Djassin but higher outdoors in Tokpota and Djegan-Daho, indicating exophagic trend. P. falciparum infections detected indoors in Tokpota (4.41%) and Djegan-Daho (3.57%), and outdoors in Djassin (1.26%). Of 250 households surveyed, 86% slept indoors and 14% outdoors despite owning nets.

articles.headline: Healthcare workers lack knowledge managing chikungunya in malaria regions
articles.title_original: Practices and challenges related to management of chikungunya and non-malarial acute febrile illnesses in malaria-endemic areas of Afar region, Northeast Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Zerfu B, Kassa T, Mamo G, Legesse M
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of 82 healthcare workers across 6 facilities in Afar Region, Ethiopia. 93.6% had prior chikungunya information but only 23.4% demonstrated good knowledge; 59.2% of febrile cases were non-malarial. Major gaps identified: lack of diagnostic tools, inadequate training, and clinical misdiagnosis due to overlapping symptoms. Study underscores need for improved diagnostic capacity and workforce training in malaria-endemic areas.

articles.headline: Secondary Anopheles vectors sustain malaria in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Beyond Anopheles gambiae sensu lato: exploring the impact of non-dominant Anopheles species on malaria persistence in high-transmission endemic areas of Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Ngaffo KL, Hien AS, Soma DD, Maiga S, Sougue E, Ouédraogo COW, Delphine KO, Kaboré DPA, Namountougou M, Diabaté A, Josiane ED, Dabiré RK
articles.details: Mosquito surveys conducted 2023-2024 across three ecological zones using human landing catches and pyrethroid spray catches. Total of 1718 secondary Anopheles collected; An. nili predominated (87.5% in 2023, 93% in 2024). Plasmodium falciparum detected in all species except An. funestus. Secondary vectors exhibit outdoor and anthropophilic feeding behaviors that limit indoor-focused interventions.

articles.headline: Cambodia maintained malaria services during COVID-19 pandemic disruptions
articles.title_original: Progress towards malaria elimination: insights from Cambodia's mobile malaria workers during the COVID-19 pandemic.
articles.authors: Manning EK, Huy R, Siv S, Ly P, Tibenderana JK, Vernaeve L
articles.details: Cambodia has made progress toward malaria elimination targeting 2025. During COVID-19, restrictive pandemic measures threatened critical services including case detection and treatment by mobile malaria workers (MMWs) in hard-to-reach populations. With locally tailored implementation and close health authority communication, MMWs minimized disruption of malaria control while ensuring staff and beneficiary protection despite logistical and communication challenges in remote regions.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine superior to pyronaridine-artesunate in malaria-COVID coinfection
articles.title_original: Artemether-lumefantrine versus pyronaridine-artesunate for the treatment of malaria in patients with mild to moderate COVID-19 in Kenya and Burkina Faso: a randomised open-label trial (MALCOV).
articles.authors: Tangara B, Barsosio HC, Marlais T, Kabore JMT, Tiono AB, Otieno K, Wanjiku M, Achieng M, Onyango ED, Ondieki ED, Aura H, Odawo T, Allen DJ, Hannan L, Tetteh KK, Soulama I, Ouedraogo A, Serme SS, Soulama BI, Barry A, Badoum ES, Matthewman J, Brazal-Monzó H, Canizales J, Drabko A, Wu W, Kariuki S, Lesosky M, Sirima SB, Drakeley C, Ter Kuile FO
articles.details: Open-label randomized trial of 143 patients (69 PA, 74 AL) with non-severe malaria and confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection in Kenya and Burkina Faso. By day 7, SARS-CoV-2 clearance was 41% with PA vs 58% with AL (aRR=0.78, p=0.37); by day 14, 80% vs 96% (aRR=0.86, p=0.47). Median day 7 viral load higher with PA (855 vs 81 copies/mL, p=0.023). Parasitological cure rates similar: PA 100%, AL 99%.

articles.headline: Web-based tool visualizes Plasmodium genomic variation globally
articles.title_original: Malaria-GENOMAP: a web-based tool for exploring genomic variation of malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Thorpe J, Billows N, Ngwana-Joseph GC, Ibrahim A, Nolder D, Sutherland CJ, Nguyen THN, Nguyen THB, Nguyen QT, Dombrowski JG, Di Santi SM, Marinho CRF, Phelan JE, Kurowski T, Mohareb F, Campino S, Clark TG
articles.details: Malaria-GENOMAP integrates genomic variant data with geographic maps and gene-level visualization for multiple Plasmodium species. Database includes 1359 P. vivax, 139 P. knowlesi, 158 P. malariae, 47 P. ovale wallikeri, 36 P. ovale curtisi, and 38 P. simium. Tool enables investigation of population structure, geographic associations of mutations, and putative drug resistance markers to inform malaria control efforts.

articles.headline: HRP2 RDTs overestimate malaria prevalence in Ghanaian children
articles.title_original: False-positive and false-negative malaria rapid diagnostic test results: evidence from a nationally representative survey of children in Ghana.
articles.authors: Asare KK, Aboagye YO, Mohammed MW, Darko M, Adjei C, Emmanuels HF, Boakye AN, Asiedu E
articles.details: Analysis of 4417 children from 2022 Ghana Demographic and Health Survey tested with HRP2-based RDTs and microscopy. Malaria prevalence 20.3% by RDT vs 9.9% by microscopy. RDT sensitivity 89.7%, specificity 87.4%, positive predictive value 43.9%, negative predictive value 98.7%. False-negative results more likely in rural areas and indoor-residual-spray-targeted districts, with significant regional variation.

articles.headline: Cambodia explores high-dose primaquine and tafenoquine for vivax
articles.title_original: Moving towards high-dose primaquine or single-dose tafenoquine for Plasmodium vivax treatment in Cambodia: a meeting report from dissemination of results of the EFFORT trial to stakeholders.
articles.authors: Dysoley L, Cassidy-Seyoum S, Adhikari B, Dahal A, Phoumen N, Srey S, Phalla T, Vonn P, Engrkruy B, Sovanarath O, Mengkea H, Rathana P, Makara I, Heng PK, Heng Y, Khlok H, Pong K, Moeng T, Vantha S, Soviet U, Ravuth R, Bunreth V, Vannak K, Sokhann M, Kimmen K, Dy K, Samol S, Lo K, Sokunthea N, Kry H, Kanha L, Naren L, Oum S, Sovann Y, Channavuth R, Bunmeng C, Hughes J, Brindle H, Hu Y, Peng S, Mardi P, Chanry I, Preap S, Heoy S, Sabawoon F, Sokha T, Ringwald P, Lynch CA, Price RN, Tripura R, von Seidlein L, Devine A, Thriemer K
articles.details: Cambodia targeted malaria elimination by 2025; P. vivax now predominant species due to relapses from dormant liver stages. EFFORT clinical trial in Cambodia and three endemic countries compared 7-day high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg total) and single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) to 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg) for P. vivax. Study assessed safety, effectiveness, feasibility, and cost-effectiveness of these treatment options.

articles.headline: Metal isotope signatures reveal malaria anemia mechanisms
articles.title_original: Iron, copper and zinc isotope ratios in malaria infection reveal signatures of the Plasmodium biology and host mice response to the infection.
articles.authors: Costas-Rodríguez M, Abou-Zeid L, Hastuti AAMB, Silva MDCB, de Morais AS, Quadros HC, Moreira DRM, Vanhaecke F
articles.details: Study examined Fe, Cu, Zn concentrations and isotope ratios in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice. Parasitized RBCs showed altered isotopic compositions; hepatic Fe overload accompanied by heavier Fe isotope depletion suggesting compensatory mobilization for anemia. Discrete hepatic Cu deficiency with lighter isotopic composition indicated Cu release into bloodstream through ceruloplasmin. Results argue for central Fe role and auxiliary Cu role in malaria anemia, with Zn responding to host immunity.

articles.headline: Transcription factor regulates Plasmodium berghei development timing
