articles.title_original: Precise gene regulation through transcriptional repression is essential for Plasmodium berghei asexual blood stage development.
articles.authors: Nishi T, Kaneko I, Iwanaga S, Yuda M
articles.details: Conditional knockout study of AP2-family transcription factor PbAP2-TR in P. berghei. PbAP2-TR acts as transcriptional repressor essential for asexual blood stage development, with knockout arresting development at trophozoite stage. PbAP2-TR recruits putative chromatin remodeler PbMORC as co-factor. Time-course transcriptome analysis shows PbAP2-TR-PbMORC complex responsible for precise transcriptional downregulation from early to late trophozoite development.

articles.headline: Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine reduces chlamydia and streptococcus in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Impact of intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine on sexually transmitted and reproductive tract infections: results from a randomized trial in Uganda.
articles.authors: Adrama H, Dela Cruz EJ, Ozarslan N, Kakuru A, Odongo B, Gaw SL, Benjamin-Chung J, Kizza J, Aguti M, Ategeka J, Olwoch P, Nakalembe M, Opira B, Clark TD, Kamya MR, Rosenthal PJ, Dorsey G, Roh ME
articles.details: Secondary analysis of randomized-controlled trial in Uganda comparing monthly IPTp with SP, DP, or DP+SP among 2265 pregnant women. IPTp-SP showed 80% lower Chlamydia trachomatis prevalence at delivery (2.5% vs 12.4%, 95% CI: 67%-88%) and 35% lower Group B Streptococcus prevalence (7.7% vs 11.7%, 95% CI: 1%-57%) compared to IPTp-DP. C. trachomatis associated with preterm delivery (PR=1.86), GBS with term low birthweight (PR=2.08).

articles.headline: Microscopy misidentifies 98% of malaria mixed-infection cases in India
articles.title_original: Plasmodium mono and mixed-infections in India: a tale of see-saw in species misidentification.
articles.authors: Deora N, Pande V, Sinha A
articles.details: Analysis of 2706 microscopy-PCR sample pairs from 16 locations across 11 Indian states revealed 15% overall misidentification rate (408/2706). Critically, >98% of mixed-infections (400/405) misidentified as mono-infections, predominantly as P. falciparum. Jharkhand and Madhya Pradesh contributed >20% of misidentifications. Findings suggest overestimation of P. falciparum burden and underestimation of non-falciparum species in near-elimination settings.

articles.headline: Blood purification rescues severe falciparum malaria with cytokine storm
articles.title_original: The blood purification therapy-based strategy effectively rescued the severe falciparum malaria patient experiencing cytokine storm-driven MODS.
articles.authors: Sun Y, Huang X, Hong B, Song J, Lu Y, Ma J, Wang X, Hu W, Zhang Y, Xuan H
articles.details: Case report of 63-year-old severe falciparum malaria patient with cytokine storm and multiple organ dysfunction syndrome treated with therapeutic plasma exchange plus continuous venovenous hemodiafiltration. IL-6 decreased from 729.65 to 26.42 pg/mL, interferon-gamma from 170.38 to 42.47 pg/mL after first treatment session. Literature review of 29 severe malaria patients receiving blood purification showed good prognosis in 19 with MODS symptoms.

articles.headline: P. vivax congenital and neonatal malaria cases in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax congenital and neonatal malaria with atypical presentation in Western Ethiopia: four cases highlighting diagnostic pitfalls.
articles.authors: Adugna NB, Adugna NB, Geleta DB, Mitike BA, Terefa YA
articles.details: Four neonates (median age 18 days) admitted to a neonatal ICU with malaria: two with fever and poor feeding, one afebrile with omphalitis, one with tachypnea. Plasmodium vivax parasitemia confirmed on smears. Two had thrombocytopenia, one anemia. All received IV artesunate 3 mg/kg plus antibiotics; all discharged by day 7 with no deaths.

articles.headline: Triterpenic esters inhibit Plasmodium falciparum aminopeptidase PfA-M17
articles.title_original: Natural triterpenic phenolic esters target PfA-M17 in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Mamede L, Rangel GW, Schmitt M, Albrecht S, Spichty M, Bero J, Beaufay C, Florent I, Llinás M, Frédérich M, Quetin-Leclercq J
articles.details: Eight triterpenic esters from Keetia leucantha showed antiplasmodial activity via multi-scale analysis. Compounds inhibited aminopeptidase PfA-M17 with activity onset in early-ring stage parasites and perturbations in hemoglobin metabolism.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr bednets overcome pyrethroid resistance in Uganda vectors
articles.title_original: Chlorfenapyr bednets effectively overcome pyrethroid resistance escalation in highly resistant Anopheles malaria vectors in Uganda.
articles.authors: Oruni A, Menze BD, Fotso-Toguem YG, Ngannang-Fezeu VB, Thiomela RF, Tchouakui M, Hearn J, Kayondo J, Wondji CS
articles.details: Experimental hut trials with wild resistant Anopheles mosquitoes in Uganda. Interceptor G2 (chlorfenapyr) net achieved 70.6% mortality against An. funestus and 63.2% against An. gambiae, with odds ratio 18.7 (95% CI 8.05–48.6, P < 0.0001) vs pyrethroid-only Interceptor net. PBO and PPF nets showed lower efficacy. Genetic analysis linked pyrethroid-resistance markers (4.3 Kb-SV, G454A-Cyp9K1) to survival against pyrethroid nets but not Interceptor G2.

articles.headline: Metabolomic shifts in asymptomatic P. falciparum infections in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Exploratory Serum Metabolomic Profiling of Asymptomatic Malaria Children Infected with Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Zinga MM, Kassim Mohamed M, Ojo D, Mohamed Abdoul-Latif F, Mkassy FI, Wilfred D, Marwa KJ, Cacciatore S
articles.details: Exploratory metabolomic study of 48 school-aged children (27 malaria-positive, 21 malaria-negative) from high-transmission Tanzania using 1H NMR spectroscopy. Modest group-level metabolic differences with lower lactate, pyruvate, and branched-chain amino acids in malaria-positive children; 18.5% of malaria-positive showed distinct metabolic subgroup with reduced phenylalanine and alanine and increased glycerophospholipids.

articles.headline: Pv3Rs: Statistical inference of P. vivax recurrence causes
articles.title_original: Pv3Rs: Plasmodium vivax relapse, recrudescence, and reinfection statistical genetic inference.
articles.authors: Foo YS, White MT, Taylor AR
articles.details: Pv3Rs software implements statistical inference to distinguish Plasmodium vivax recurrence causes (relapse from hypnozoites, recrudescence, reinfection) from genetic data, including length polymorphic loci and amplicon sequencing. Freely available on CRAN.

articles.headline: PI4Kβ: Target at parasite-immunity interface in malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Phosphatidylinositol 4-Kinase Beta (PI4Kβ): An Emerging Target at the Interface of Parasite Biology and Host Immunity.
articles.authors: Nimbarte VD, Sonak SS, Mohapatra N, Ishwarkar S, Kumar SR, Mustajar S, Pinto R, Sharanya ST
articles.details: Review of phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase beta (PI4Kβ), an essential Plasmodium lipid kinase central to intracellular signaling and parasite development. PI4Kβ also modulates host immunity through regulation of protein export, immune evasion, and extracellular vesicle formation. Inhibition may disrupt parasite survival and enhance immune recognition.

articles.headline: Granzyme B and exported PfHsp70 in severe malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Is the Association of Granzyme B and the Exported Parasite Hsp70 a Possible Trojan Horse of Severe Malaria?
articles.authors: Dongola TH, Shonhai A
articles.details: Hypothesis that Plasmodium falciparum-exported Hsp70 (PfHsp70-x) on infected RBC membrane may stimulate NK cells to release elevated granzyme B (GrB) seen in severe malaria, potentially driving pathogenesis. Discusses therapeutic prospects of Hsp70-GrB-mediated antimalarial strategy.

articles.headline: Global malaria status and GCC countries epidemiology update
articles.title_original: Malaria Public Health Status: Global Context and Update on Gulf Cooperation Council Countries.
articles.authors: Ahmad T, Alhammadi BA, Almaazmi SY, Arafa S, Blatch GL, Dutta T, Gestwicki JE, Keyzers RA, Meskiri A, Sharafeldin A, Shonhai A, Singh H
articles.details: Review of global malaria burden (deaths fell 864,000 in 2000 to 576,000 in 2019, but progress slowed since 2020). Highlights emergence of widespread P. falciparum resistance to first-line drugs and recent vaccine successes (RTS,S/AS01, R21/MM). Eastern Mediterranean region has experienced overall increase in cases and deaths since 2015.

articles.headline: Unusual Plasmodium spp. presentation in peripheral blood smear
articles.title_original: An unusual presentation of Plasmodium spp. in peripheral blood smear.
articles.authors: Ansari N, Gupta K, Nandwani S, Bir R
articles.details: Case of 7-year-old boy with 6–7 days abdominal pain and fever. Labs showed normocytic, normochromic RBCs with leukocytosis (16.3 × 10³/µL), hemoglobin 9.2 g/dL, thrombocytopenia (35,000/µL), and elevated CRP (144.8 mg/L). Peripheral smear revealed unusual Plasmodium spp. findings (specific characteristics discussed in full text).

articles.headline: Severe imported P. falciparum malaria in travelers, Moscow 2020–2023
articles.title_original: Clinical and epidemiological profile of severe imported Plasmodium falciparum malaria in travelers at hospital admission: A retrospective single-center study in Moscow, Russia.
articles.authors: Emerole K, Tokmalaev A, Mvuania V
articles.details: Retrospective study of 32 patients meeting WHO criteria for severe malaria at Moscow hospital (2020–2023). Median age 41, predominantly male (89%), mostly Russian descent (91.2%). All infected in sub-Saharan Africa; none used chemoprophylaxis. Presented median 5 days after symptom onset. P. falciparum was sole agent. 99% had high parasitemia, 98% altered consciousness, 78.1% profound weakness, 52% jaundice at admission.

articles.headline: Corrigendum: Malaria and inflammatory biomarkers in rural Iraq children
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Analysis of the Relationship Between Malaria and Inflammatory Biomarker Levels in Children in Rural Iraq: Impact of Nutritional Deficiencies, Anemia, and Hematological Disorders" published in Trop Parasitol. 2025 Jul-Dec;15(2):110-117. doi: 10.4103/tp.tp_26_25. Epub 2025 Oct 25. PMID: 41244076; PMCID: PMC12617583.
articles.details: Corrigendum to published article on malaria and inflammatory biomarkers. No detailed results provided in abstract.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccines in Southeast Asian elimination strategies
articles.title_original: Integrating malaria vaccines into Southeast Asian elimination strategies: A cautious path forward.
articles.authors: Parija SC, Poddar A
articles.details: Title only; abstract incomplete in provided data. Article discusses integration of malaria vaccines into Southeast Asian elimination strategies.

articles.headline: Malaria causes 2.4 million cases of maternal anaemia in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: The burden of malaria-attributable maternal anaemia and the impact of preventive treatment across sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Leuba SI, Verity R, Gutman JR, Weiss DJ, Cairns M, Williams JE, Khairallah C, Bojang K, Dodd J, Noor AM, Taylor SM, Otieno K, Coulibaly SO, Kayentao K, Kariuki S, Greenwood B, Desai M, Chandramohan D, Tagbor H, Ter Kuile FO, Madanitsa M, Walker PGT
articles.details: Modeled malaria-attributed maternal anaemia across 12,608 pregnancies in 7 African countries. Without preventive measures, 13.1 million pregnant women exposed to P. falciparum in 2023 would develop 2.41 million cases of moderate/severe anaemia (Hb <9 g/dL) including 600,000 severe cases (Hb <7 g/dL). Under current coverage, IPTp with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine averted 1.10 million moderate/severe and 330,000 severe anaemia cases. A 32% reduction in transmission translated to only 22% decline in anaemia burden, with risk shifting to multigravid women with lower pregnancy-specific immunity.

articles.headline: Routine pre-eclampsia diagnosis shows poor concordance in malaria-endemic settings
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Concordance and Misclassification of Pre-Eclampsia in a Malaria-Endemic Setting: A Comparative Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Opoku Mensah B, Serwaa AA, Dugbatey MK, Anim E
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 618 pregnant women with suspected malaria or pre-eclampsia in primary to tertiary facilities. Routine clinical diagnosis had 50.2% concordance with reference criteria (κ=0.233), with pre-eclampsia sensitivity 61.8% and specificity 62.2%. While malaria parasite density was associated with higher urine protein-creatinine ratio (β=0.17, p<0.001), it did not independently predict diagnostic discordance. Considerable misclassification of pre-eclampsia occurred when malaria was concurrently present.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 13.6% in pregnant women; ITN non-use increases risk 7-fold
articles.title_original: Prevalence, Associated Risk Factors, and the Impact of Malaria on Anemia in Pregnant Women Attending ANC in North Gondar Zone, Northern Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Alula GA, Endale T, Yibeltie G
articles.details: Facility-based cross-sectional study of 308 pregnant women in North Gondar Zone, Ethiopia. Malaria prevalence was 13.6%, with increased risk in women ≥40 years (AOR=4.8, 95% CI 1.4–16.0), rural residents (AOR=2.8, 1.2–6.5), and those with inconsistent/no ITN use (AOR=7.2, 2.9–18.0). Environmental factors including proximity to water bodies (AOR=3.0, 1.4–6.5) and unscreened houses (AOR=3.6, 1.6–8.2) were significant. Among malaria-infected women, 40% had anaemia.

articles.headline: DBLMSP antigens show modular recombination driving polymorphism in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Module-level recombination drives DBLMSP polymorphism and functional conservation in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Duan YW, Chen SB, Wang TY, Yang WX, Kassegne K, Shen HM, Chen JH
articles.details: Analyzed DBLMSP1 and DBLMSP2 surface antigen sequences from samples along China-Myanmar border, revealing modular structure with discrete, recombinable units forming nine distinct genotypes each. Nucleotide diversity and Tajima's D peaked within variable modules while conserved structural elements—including the receptor-binding cleft and SPAM domain—remained under purifying selection. Long-range linkage disequilibrium patterns aligned with module junctions, suggesting modular recombination independently shapes P. falciparum DBLMSP genetic structure with implications for immunogen design.

articles.headline: Genetic vector control strategies may enhance malaria prevention through mosquito modification
articles.title_original: Advancing Malaria Vector Control: Insights Into Mosquito Immunity and Genetic Strategies.
articles.authors: Cleanclay WD, Adedoyin ED, Zakari S, Ogunlana OO, Iweala EEJ, Chinedu SN
articles.details: Review of Anopheles mosquito immunity to Plasmodium and genetic approaches for vector control. Discusses innate immune pathways (pathogen recognition receptors, antimicrobial peptides, melanization) by which mosquitoes limit infection, and Plasmodium evasion mechanisms. Reviews emerging genetic strategies including CRISPR-Cas9 gene drives, Wolbachia-mediated vector competence interference, RNAi-based gene silencing, and self-limiting strategies (RIDL, pgSIT). Identifies mosGILT as an emerging immune regulator for blocking transmission, with discussion of advantages and limitations for practical implementation.

articles.headline: P. falciparum accounts for 82% of malaria infections in nationwide Cameroon surveillance
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium Species Using Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Cameroon: First-Year Data From Routine Malaria Surveillance.
articles.authors: Tabue RN, Neh F, Evina E, Bomba DA, Voundi JV, Fosso J, Zeh AM
articles.details: Nationwide malaria surveillance using P.f/Pan RDTs across all regions of Cameroon (January–December 2024) testing 3,843,507 individuals found 43% malaria positivity. P. falciparum monoinfection accounted for 82% of cases, mixed infections (P. falciparum + Pan) 15%, and Pan monoinfection 3%. Regional prevalence of P. falciparum monoinfection ranged from 80.6% (East) to 92.1% (Far North), with seasonal peak in June–July. P.f/Pan RDTs cannot reliably differentiate individual non-falciparum species, highlighting need for molecular surveillance including HRP2/3 gene deletion monitoring.

articles.headline: cTfh cell profiles in placental malaria associate with maternal haemoglobin and neonatal outcomes
articles.title_original: cTfh Cell Activation and Changes in BTLA and LAG-3 Expression Differentially Associate With Poor Pregnancy Outcome in Plasmodium falciparum Placental Malaria.
articles.authors: Zambo Bitye BM, Mbianda Nana CM, Kwanou Tchakounte BD, Fogang B, Kenfack Tekougang Zangue B, Medouen Ndeumou RS, Ayong L, Megnekou R
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 49 women with P. falciparum placental malaria in Cameroon (March 2022–May 2023). Placental malaria was associated with increased circulating T follicular helper (cTfh) cell frequencies in peripheral and placental blood. PM-positive women showed elevated ICOS+ cTfh cells correlating with parasitemia, while BTLA expression was downregulated. Notably, cTfh frequencies in placental blood positively correlated with infant birth weight but negatively associated with maternal haemoglobin. BTLA and LAG-3 expression in placental blood negatively correlated with birth weight, suggesting altered immune activation influences maternal–neonatal outcomes.

articles.headline: Anti-gSG6-P1 biomarker reveals seasonal Anopheles exposure patterns in Thailand
articles.title_original: Immunological surveillance using anti-gSG6-P1 IgG biomarker reveals spatio-temporal dynamics of Anopheles exposure and gaps in malaria risk assessment in the Greater Mekong Subregion.
articles.authors: Saeung M, Jupatanakul N, Jampeesri N, Afelt A, Chareonviriyaphap T, Manguin S
articles.details: Cross-sectional serological study of 184 participants in Sisaket Province, Thailand across three seasons (rainy, cool-dry, hot-dry 2022–2023). Anti-gSG6-P1 IgG antibodies against Anopheles gambiae salivary antigen were measured via ELISA. Seasonality was the strongest factor influencing anti-gSG6-P1 IgG levels, driven by human behavioral factors including rubber tapping frequency in Anopheles dirus-endemic areas (5–7 days/week: 1.08±0.36 vs 0–4 days/week: 0.96±0.35). Study highlighted limitations of anti-gSG6-P1 biomarker in the Greater Mekong Subregion due to vector species diversity and lack of well-characterized serological response kinetics.

articles.headline: Brazilian Amazon IRS logistics show reactive malaria response rather than risk-based planning
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the insecticide custody chain and its relationship with malaria burden in the Brazilian Amazon: a process and exploratory impact assessment (2019-2023).
articles.authors: Dourado ML, Silva RAE, Castro MC, Peterka CRL, Kluge CR, Castro D, Lima JBP
articles.details: Mixed-methods assessment of IRS insecticide (Etofenprox 20% PM) custody chain in Brazilian Amazon (2019–2023). At national level, insecticide volume was significantly associated with malaria cases (β=0.161, p=0.038, R²=0.74) and deforestation (β=0.626, p=0.034). Time-series analysis revealed reactive allocation patterns with insecticide distribution lagging malaria incidence peaks rather than preceding them based on risk forecasting. Qualitative assessment of three high-incidence municipalities (Barcelos, Tefé, São Gabriel da Cachoeira) identified systemic weaknesses: limited data interoperability, insufficient environmental indicator integration, and poor federal coordination.

articles.headline: P. falciparum proteins enriched in tandem repeats function in immune evasion and binding
articles.title_original: Structural and functional insights from detailed computational analysis of Plasmodium repetome.
articles.authors: Kaur S, Kumar V, Singh K, Beh A, A A, Mishra PC, Hora R
articles.details: Computational proteome-wide analysis of tandem amino acid repeats (TRs) in Plasmodium species. P. falciparum and P. vivax identified as exceptionally rich in TRs, with P. falciparum TRs predominantly in exported parasite proteins involved in immune evasion and host interaction. Gene ontology analysis revealed TR-containing proteins primarily function in nucleic acid and protein binding. While variant surface antigen (VSA) families showed low overall repeat content, all TR-containing VSAs were sero-reactive with antigenic peptides localized to repeat regions. Structure predictions indicated repeats form ordered super-secondary conformations facilitating intermolecular interactions.

articles.headline: Quality improvement initiative achieves 100% malaria reporting sensitivity in 5 weeks
articles.title_original: Quality Improvement Initiative to Improve the Sensitivity of Malaria Reporting under Integrated Health Information Platform, Raigarh, Chhattisgarh, 2023.
articles.authors: Patel Y, Kaviprawin M, Patel K, Ramasamy A, Gahwai DK
articles.details: Quality improvement project in Raigarh, Chhattisgarh using Plan-Do-Study-Act methodology to improve malaria case reporting in Integrated Health Information Platform. Baseline captured only 44% of laboratory-tested cases from primary health centers. Root cause analysis identified inadequate laboratory technician knowledge of data entry and poor feedback from district surveillance unit. Interventions included refresher training for technicians and weekly feedback mechanism. Achieved 100% reporting sensitivity within 5 weeks across four selected primary health centers.

articles.headline: Malaria cases declining in Kanker District; P. falciparum proportion exceeds 90%
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria Cases Reported from Government Health Facilities in District Kanker, Chhattisgarh, January 2018 - December 2022.
articles.authors: Jain T, Ramasamy A, Khare A, Ramkete DK, Nagarajan R
articles.details: Analysis of National Malaria Program surveillance data in Kanker District, Chhattisgarh, India (January 2018–December 2022). Annual Parasite Index (API) declined from 3.0 (2019) to 1.3 (2022), and Annual Falciparum Index (AFI) from 2.5 to 1.1. P. falciparum proportion increased to >90% (2020–2021). Test Positivity Rate (TPR) and Test Falciparum Rate (TFR) declined by approximately half over the period, while Annual Blood Examination Rate (ABER) remained stable at ~30%. In 2022, nearly 2/7 blocks maintained API>2 and 1/7 blocks AFI>2, indicating persistent transmission foci.

articles.headline: Koraput malaria surges; API rises to 4.5 per 1000 in 2023
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria in Koraput District, Odisha, 2019-2023: A Descriptive Analysis of National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme Surveillance Data.
articles.authors: Jena S, Bicholkar A, Jacob QM, Baral P, Murali S
articles.details: Descriptive epidemiological study analyzing National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme surveillance data from Koraput district for 2019–2023. Annual parasite incidence (API) was 2.7 per 1000 overall, with a sharp rise to 4.5 per 1000 in 2023. Geographic variation was substantial: Narayanpatna had the highest API at 38 per 1000, followed by Bandhugaon at 25 per 1000. Males consistently showed higher API than females. Seasonal peaks provide opportunities for pre-emptive control measures before monsoons.

articles.headline: Chhattisgarh surveillance captures only 5.8% to 29% of cases
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Malaria Surveillance System in Government Health Facilities in Six Districts of State Chhattisgarh, India, 2023.
articles.authors: Gahwai DK, Kaviprawin M, Panigrahi DK, Das JK, Patel K, Roy M, Tigga S, Jain T, Patel Y, Ramasamy A
articles.details: Descriptive cross-sectional evaluation of the malaria surveillance system in six Chhattisgarh districts using 1,276 syndromic forms, 110 presumptive forms, and 100 laboratory forms. The system demonstrated low sensitivity: 84 (5.8%) of 1,442 presumptive cases and 3,334 (29%) of 11,510 laboratory forms were captured. Data quality was limited, with only 52.4% of presumptive forms and 72% of laboratory forms having valid entries in key variables. The authors conclude that improved reporting sensitivity and data-quality initiatives are needed to strengthen surveillance.

articles.headline: First congenital P. knowlesi malaria case documented in Bahrain
articles.title_original: Co-infection of Neonatal Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria With Congenital Cytomegalovirus: The First Reported Case From Bahrain.
articles.authors: Mohroofi AD, Ben Turkia H, Aljowder F, Althawadi D, Loni R
articles.details: Case report of the first documented congenital malaria in Bahrain due to Plasmodium knowlesi in a 26-day-old Pakistani male infant presenting with fever, jaundice, lethargy, and hepatosplenomegaly. Laboratory findings included pancytopenia and elevated inflammatory markers. P. knowlesi was confirmed on peripheral blood smear despite negative rapid malaria antigen testing. The infant was co-infected with cytomegalovirus. Treatment included intravenous artesunate followed by oral antimalarials and ganciclovir/valganciclovir for the viral co-infection.

articles.headline: Could neutrophil NETosis be a therapeutic target in severe malaria?
articles.title_original: [Malaria: Could NETosis, neutrophils' defense mechanism involved in severe cases of the disease, be a new therapeutic target?].
articles.authors: Kociánová L, Larribau S, Veltz N
articles.details: This article discusses neutrophil extracellular trap formation (NETosis) as a potential mechanism involved in severe malaria cases and explores whether it could represent a new therapeutic target. No specific empirical findings are available from the title alone.

articles.headline: Vietnamese P. vivax shows 34 distinct MSP-1 haplotypes with regional variation
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphisms of merozoite surface protein-1 ICB 5-6 in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates.
articles.authors: Nguyễn TH, Nguyễn ĐTD, Lê HG, Võ TC, Trinh NTM, Cho M, Van Khanh C, Quang HH, Na BK
articles.details: Analysis of Plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein-1 (pvmsp-1) ICB 5-6 genetic polymorphism from 117 isolates collected in four Vietnamese provinces (Dak Lak, Dak Nong, Gia Lai, Khanh Hoa) between 2018 and 2022. A total of 139 pvmsp-1 ICB 5-6 sequences were identified and clustered into 34 distinct haplotypes at the amino acid level, with recombinant types predominating. Genetic polymorphism was higher in Central Highlands provinces (Dak Lak, Dak Nong, Gia Lai) than in Khanh Hoa (South-Central region), demonstrating regional genetic heterogeneity in P. vivax populations.

articles.headline: Partial artemisinin resistance with pfk13 mutation causes treatment failure
articles.title_original: Treatment Failure and Post-Artesunate Delayed Haemolysis in a Returned Traveller From Uganda With Partially Drug-Resistant Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Travis J, McCarthy K, Chapman P, Huang L, Tan A, Cheng Q, Barber BE
articles.details: Case report of severe P. falciparum malaria in a man from Uganda presenting with hyperparasitaemia of approximately 1.5 × 10⁶ parasites/μL (26%). Initial treatment with intravenous artesunate followed by artemether-lumefantrine showed delayed parasite clearance. Despite initial negative blood film, the patient was readmitted 3 weeks later with recurrent parasitaemia. Molecular testing revealed an A675V mutation in the pfk13 gene, increased ring-stage survival, and reduced susceptibility to lumefantrine, confirming partial artemisinin resistance.

articles.headline: Gene drives tested against real-world malaria parasite diversity
articles.title_original: Gene drives tested against real-world malaria diversity.
articles.authors: Marchal I
articles.details: No abstract provided; title indicates testing of gene drives as a malaria control strategy against natural parasite populations. Specific design, sample sizes, and quantitative results unavailable.

articles.headline: Rainfall predicts 19th-century Ontario malaria mortality; temperature does not
articles.title_original: Ecologies of Risk: Malaria and Settler Landscape Transformation in 19th-Century Ontario.
articles.authors: Cooke A, Brickley MB
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 2,702 deaths attributed to probable malaria in 19th-century Ontario (1831–1900) using civil, cemetery, parish, and municipal records. Children under 5 accounted for over half of deaths; adults in agricultural occupations were disproportionately affected; rural areas in western Ontario showed highest mortality. Generalized additive modeling revealed a strong nonlinear association between rainfall and malaria deaths (p < 0.001), while temperature was not a significant predictor. Settler-driven environmental changes—deforestation, altered hydrology, and agricultural intensification—created ecologies conducive to mosquito breeding.

articles.headline: CXCL10-high microglia: candidate neuroimmune endotype in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: CXCL10(high) Microglia in Cerebral Malaria: Toward Translational Validation.
articles.authors: Rattanapitoon NK, Gordon CN, Charoenphon N, Rattanapitoon SK
articles.details: Perspective identifying CXCL10ᵸᵍʰ TNFαᵸᵍʰ Ki67⁺ microglia as potential drivers of CD8⁺ T cell recruitment during experimental cerebral malaria. The authors propose CXCL10ᵸᵍʰ microglia as a candidate 'neuroimmune endotype' whose translational relevance requires validation in human disease. Recommended validation steps include single-nucleus and spatial transcriptomics of human postmortem tissue, paired plasma/cerebrospinal fluid biomarker analysis, and mechanistic assays of microglial antigen presentation. Strong therapeutic inferences from animal models alone are cautioned against.

articles.headline: RTS,S vaccine rollout in Cameroon: 42% negative, 40% positive sentiment
articles.title_original: Assessing RTS, S malaria vaccine rollout perception in Cameroon: Sentiment analysis from X and facebook using hugging face.
articles.authors: Cynthia AN, Njuaka M, Koomson N, Fogap NZ
articles.details: Social media sentiment analysis of Twitter/X and Facebook posts regarding RTS,S (Mosquirix) malaria vaccine rollout in Cameroon using Hugging Face Transformer library. Sentiment distribution: 42.0% negative, 40.0% positive, and 18.0% neutral reactions, indicating a nearly even split between skeptical and supportive viewpoints. The authors conclude that targeted communication strategies are needed to address public concerns and foster vaccine confidence, with sentiment analysis serving as a real-time monitoring tool for public health messaging.

articles.headline: R21 vaccine protects against intradermal but not venous sporozoite challenge
articles.title_original: Malaria vaccine protection against intradermal or venous parasites: a randomized phase 2b human challenge trial.
articles.authors: Kapulu MC, Orenge F, Kimani D, Kibwana E, Kibet H, Mutahi M, Datoo MS, Bellamy D, Musembi J, Ngoto O, Rashid H, Akinyi S, Mwatasa MH, Nyamako L, Keter K, Gatheru R, Mutiso A, Musyoki J, Mwacharo J, Abebe Y, James ER, Billingsley PF, Ngetsa C, Mosobo M, Makale J, Tawa B, Wamae K, Ochola-Oyier LI, Lawrie A, Ramos-Lopez F, Roberts R, Richie TL, Sim BKL, Hoffman SL, Ewer KJ, Hill AVS, Hamaluba M, Bejon P
articles.details: Phase 2b randomized controlled trial in Kenya comparing R21/Matrix-M (n=38), ME-TRAP (n=24), and control (n=18) with 37 enrolled in controlled human malaria infection using intradermal or direct venous injection of sporozoites with PCR-monitored parasitemia. For intradermal challenge, 0/12 R21-vaccinated volunteers met primary endpoint versus 7/8 (88%) controls and 11/12 (92%) ME-TRAP-vaccinated volunteers (p < 0.0005). For direct venous injection, 5/5 (100%) R21-vaccinated volunteers met primary endpoint (p < 0.0005). R21/Matrix-M is highly protective against intradermal sporozoites but ineffective against direct venous injection.

articles.headline: Treatment appropriateness for severe malaria: 71.8% adequate in Madrid
articles.title_original: Appropriateness of malaria treatment according to World Health Organization criteria for severe malaria in a non-endemic country.
articles.authors: Pérez-Jacoiste Asín MA, Navarro Flores A, Alonso Crespo A, Pérez-Ayala A, Losada Galván I, de Dios B, Hernández-Jiménez P, Gil-Mosquera M, Jiménez-Morillas F, Lumbreras C, Lizasoain M, Herrero-Martínez JM
articles.details: Single-center retrospective study of 64 symptomatic malaria patients (median age 39.5 years) from Hospital 12 de Octubre, Madrid, 2015–2023. Severe malaria occurred in 15 (23.4%); WHO criteria classified severity; parenteral artesunate was standard for severe cases. Antimalarial treatment was adequate to severity in 46 (71.8%) patients. Three severe cases were misclassified and received oral treatment; 15 non-severe cases received unnecessary parenteral artesunate, primarily due to thrombocytopenia (median 33,000/mm³). Treatment appropriateness was generally good, particularly for severe cases.

articles.headline: Mutagenesis screening identifies genes controlling Plasmodium falciparum gametocyte development
articles.title_original: Large-Scale Mutagenesis Screening for Genetic Determinants of Plasmodium falciparum Sexual Development.
articles.authors: Aringbangba O, Pires CV, Chawla J, Prakash P, Sollelis L, Marti M, Adams JH
articles.details: Forward genetic screen using piggyBac mutagenesis of P. falciparum identifies genes essential for gametocyte production and development. Approach categorizes mutants as hypo-producers or hyper-producers, establishing methodology for identifying genetic factors driving gametocyte conversion.

articles.headline: P230Compete ELISA predicts transmission-blocking vaccine Pfs230D1 activity
articles.title_original: Epitope-specific competitive ELISA predicts malaria transmission-blocking vaccine Pfs230D1 activity measured in standard membrane feeding assay.
articles.authors: Meehan CA, Cowles MV, Morrison RD, Yi Y, Gu J, Hume JC, Peyton MP, Sagara I, Healy SA, Renn JP, Duffy PE
articles.details: Epitope-specific competitive ELISA (P230Compete) validated in 171 phase I trial participants. EUF (total IgG) and IgG1F showed strong correlation and excellent prediction of transmission-reducing activity (≥80%), with AUC 0.81 after dose 3 and 0.80 after dose 4. Combining EUF and IgG1F improved predictive performance.

articles.headline: Plasmodium yoelii strains reveal immune mechanisms of malaria severity
articles.title_original: Immune Modulation by Plasmodium yoelii: Insights From Lethal and Non-Lethal Strains.
articles.authors: Lim SYM
articles.details: Review integrating evidence that lethal (17XL) and non-lethal (17XNL) P. yoelii strains exhibit divergent immune responses: 17XL triggers excessive pro-inflammatory cytokine release and immune exhaustion, while 17XNL sustains regulated Th1/Th2 responses. Evidence converges that malaria severity reflects timing and resolution of host immune responses, not parasite load.

articles.headline: Manufacturing, funding, and trust gaps hinder malaria vaccine rollout in Africa
articles.title_original: Challenges to malaria vaccine availability and successful implementation of malaria vaccine program in Africa.
articles.authors: Ojo RJ, Igbayilola YD, Gyebi GA
articles.details: Review of obstacles to malaria vaccine implementation identifies supply constraints as major factor due to insufficient manufacturing capacity. Additional challenges include limited national health budgets, vaccine hesitancy driven by misinformation and past experiences with Western health initiatives, cold chain requirements, and weak pharmacovigilance systems.

articles.headline: Military recruits show 11% malaria prevalence with substantial morbidity in Niger
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria in young adults in Niger: A prospective study in a military setting.
articles.authors: Aminou YY, Hamidou B, Djedanem M, Ramatoulaye HL, Moustapha WA, Testa J, Jambou R
articles.details: Prospective cohort study of 2,840 military recruits (median age 27 years, 91.5% male) monitored from September to December 2023 at three health centers. Confirmed malaria cases totaled 353 (11% prevalence), with 74.5% anemic, 44% hospitalized, and 55.8% temporarily unfit for duty.

articles.headline: Iron, zinc depletion and copper elevation worsen malaria severity
articles.title_original: Essential Trace Elements and Their Impact on Immune Response and Disease Severity in Malaria Infection: A Systematic Review.
articles.authors: Hanifian H, Nateghpour M, Naderi M
articles.details: Systematic review found malaria infection consistently reduced serum iron, zinc, and magnesium while copper levels were often elevated during acute infection. These alterations were associated with dysregulated cytokine production (elevated TNF-α and IL-10), higher parasitemia, and worsened clinical outcomes. Zinc supplementation improved micronutrient status but showed limited impact on malaria incidence.

articles.headline: P. falciparum recrudescence in Italy reveals artemisinin resistance (R561H Pfk13)
articles.title_original: Recrudescence of Plasmodium falciparum malaria imported from Sub-Saharan Africa: a report of two cases and the first detection of artemisinin resistance in Italy.
articles.authors: L'Episcopia M, Poloni A, Corbellino M, Boccolini D, Gigantiello A, Covizzi A, Bernasconi D, Colombo V, Severini C, Antinori S
articles.details: Case report of two P. falciparum malaria cases with recrudescence after treatment with intravenous artesunate and oral dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine. One case harbored the R561H Pfk13 mutation, the validated marker of artemisinin partial resistance. The second case showed no Pfk13 mutations but low blood drug concentration via nasogastric tube administration.

articles.headline: Capacity for malaria control exists; implementation barriers require political will
articles.title_original: We have the means to beat malaria, do we have the will?
articles.authors: The Lancet Microbe
articles.details: Opinion piece examining gap between malaria control capacity and successful implementation.

articles.headline: Molecular PCR detects 1.9% low-density P. falciparum missed by HRP2-RDTs
articles.title_original: Molecular Detection of Plasmodium falciparum Infections Missed by Microscopy and HRP2-Based RDTs in the Dominican Republic.
articles.authors: Cabrera Federo C, Cuevas F, Gonçalves AM, Freitas B, Miguel I, Baptista V, Conte M, Santos-Pereira A, Aquino E, Disla M, Tabata A, Osório NS, Cruz M, Veiga MI
articles.details: Study screened 1,095 suspected malaria cases initially negative by HRP2-RDT and microscopy. Nucleic acid extraction from RDT cassettes was successful in 969 samples (88.5%), with real-time PCR detecting P. falciparum DNA in 18 (1.9%). Notably, 16.7% (3/18) of PCR-positive samples harbored pfhrp2 deletions.

articles.headline: Engineered Plasmodium nanoparticle vaccine achieves sterile protection in mice
articles.title_original: A Plasmodium-derived nanoparticle vaccine elicits sterile protection against malaria in mice.
articles.authors: Shi D, Ma R, Gupta R, Dickey TH, Patel PN, Salinas ND, Tang WK, Queen A, Singleton M, Delbe N, Conteh S, Lambert LE, Duffy PE, Tolia NH
articles.details: Engineered P. falciparum PLP synthase nanoparticles presenting circumsporozoite protein (CSP) and vivax CelTOS antigens were constructed. Three doses elicited high anti-CSP and anti-CelTOS antibody titers and provided complete sterile protection against malaria in a mouse model.

articles.headline: One-step artesunate faster and cheaper than conventional formulation in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Single-step versus conventional injectable artesunate for severe malaria in children: an open label, non-inferiority randomized clinical trial, Democratic Republic of the Congo and United Republic of Tanzania.
articles.authors: Gesase S, Onyamboko M, Fanello C, Abdul O, Kayembe DK, Bakomba SB, Minja DT, Nzambiwishe BK, Ekombolo PE, Malabeja A, Mbwana JR, Deen J, Mtove G, Adhikari B, Mapondela M, Taya C, Mutinda B, Waithira N, Lusingu JP, von Seidlein L, Mukaka M, Dondorp AM, Peto TJ
articles.details: Randomized non-inferiority trial of 200 children (3 months–16 years) with severe malaria comparing one-step versus two-step injectable artesunate. One-step formulation was significantly faster (mean 2 min 22 s vs 3 min 41 s, P<0.0001) and cheaper (US$0.53 vs US$0.84 per patient, P=0.0001) with equivalent parasite clearance (half-life 2.1 h vs 2.0 h, P=0.173) and safety.

articles.headline: Chloroquine-resistant P. vivax affects 7% of Ethiopian malaria infections
articles.title_original: Chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium vivax malaria in Ethiopia: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Wondmagegn YM, Setegn A, Girmay G, Assefa M, Getnet B, Cherkos T, Abebe W, Dessie N, Derso A, Yiheyis Z, Adugna A, Misganaw T, Abere A, Tamir M, Belayneh M, Amare A, Ashagre A, Malede B, Amare GA
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 13 studies (2,190 participants) found pooled chloroquine-resistant P. vivax prevalence of 7.32% (95% CI 2.55–12.08; I²=95.1%). Resistance prevalence was higher in 2003–2009 (10.70%) and Oromia region (9.58%), with 42-day follow-up showing 17.91% prevalence.

articles.headline: Vivax circumsporozoite protein vaccine strategies in mice
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein and vaccine strategies in murine models: A scoping review.
articles.authors: Sampaio MP, Cerilo-Filho M, Almeida MNG, Matos MAT, Silva AA, Fernando DJV, Bras RP, Baptista ARS, de Castro TX, Machado RLD
articles.details: Scoping review of Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein and vaccine approaches evaluated in murine models.

articles.headline: Monoclonal antibodies provide sustained malaria protection
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of Monoclonal Antibodies in Malaria Protection and Prevention of Transmission: A Systematic Review.
articles.authors: Verma K, Singhal R, Anvikar A, Bharti PK, Nitika N
articles.details: Systematic review of 7 studies (n=776) evaluating antimalarial mAbs CIS43LS, L9LS, MAM01, and TB31F. CIS43LS achieved 75–88% protective efficacy at doses ≥10 mg/kg IV with 56–80 day half-life; L9LS showed 66–77% efficacy subcutaneously with 46.1 day half-life. TB31F demonstrated >80% transmission-reducing activity sustained for 160 days. All mAbs exhibited favorable safety (grade 1–2 adverse events only). Authors conclude mAbs are promising scalable tools for seasonal malaria prevention, pending large-scale trials in diverse populations.

articles.headline: Falcilysin emerges as druggable antimalarial target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum falcilysin as an emerging potential drug target for antimalarial drug discovery.
articles.authors: Dalal A, Malik J, Singh P, Rathi B
articles.details: Review identifying falcilysin (FLN), a zinc metalloprotease essential for P. falciparum survival, as an emerging drug target. FLN has a druggable hydrophobic allosteric pocket and plays roles in hemoglobin degradation and apicoplast transit-peptide processing. Multiple small-molecule classes show potent FLN inhibition in biochemical and cellular models. Key challenges include dual-organelle localization and selectivity over human homologs; authors recommend chemical-diversity expansion, covalent/PROTAC modalities, and AI-driven design for advancing candidates.

articles.headline: Gene-drive mosquitoes suppress patient-derived malaria
articles.title_original: Gene-drive-capable mosquitoes suppress patient-derived malaria in Tanzania.
articles.authors: Habtewold T, Lwetoijera DW, Hoermann A, Mashauri R, Matwewe F, Mwanga R, Kweyamba P, Maganga G, Magani BP, Mtama R, Mahonje MA, Tambwe MM, Tarimo F, Chennuri PR, Cai JA, Del Corsano G, Capriotti P, Sasse P, Moore J, Hudson D, Manjurano A, Tarimo B, Vlachou D, Moore S, Windbichler N, Christophides GK
articles.details: Study establishing Anopheles gambiae with non-autonomous gene-drive modifications in Tanzania that robustly inhibited genetically diverse P. falciparum isolates from naturally infected children. Genetic modifications were efficiently inherited when supplemented with Cas9 from a second engineered strain. Authors demonstrate this transgenic approach brings gene-drive technology closer to field application for malaria eradication through targeted dissemination of beneficial traits in wild populations.

articles.headline: DARC genetic diversity and emerging P. vivax in Africa
articles.title_original: Genetic dynamics of the Duffy antigen receptor for chemokines gene and Plasmodium vivax circulation within sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Adeloye F, Lambe KO, Oboh JA, Abdulsalam H, Enyinnnaya C, Nwuba R, Ogundahunsi O, D'Alessandro U, Amambua-Ngwa A, Meremikwu MM, Hughes GL, Heinz E, Oboh MA
articles.details: Population genetic analysis of 223 DARC genes from sub-Saharan African countries (Nigeria, Cameroon, Ethiopia, Madagascar, South Africa). Most regions showed minimal genetic diversity with evidence of selection. South Africa displayed greatest haplotype and nucleotide diversity; Cameroon, Nigeria, and Ethiopia clustered together despite geographic distance. Detection of P. vivax among Duffy-null individuals indicates selection pressure possibly from recent P. vivax emergence, presenting a public health concern for malaria eradication.

articles.headline: P. cynomolgi DBP binding varies between haplotype groups
articles.title_original: Characterisation of erythrocyte binding activity of Plasmodium cynomolgi duffy binding protein haplotypes from Macaca fascicularis in Peninsular Malaysia.
articles.authors: Latif ENM, Adlan ASA, Lau YL, Cheong FW
articles.details: In vitro study comparing erythrocyte binding of PcDBP1(II) and PcDBP2(II) haplotypes using transfected COS-7 cells. Recent PcDBP1(II) isolates showed significantly higher binding to macaque erythrocytes than old isolates (P<0.05), suggesting protein evolution to enhance macaque invasion over time. Neither protein bound human erythrocytes. Authors conclude variations in these DBPs alone do not explain binding differences; additional parasite-host interactions are required for human erythrocyte invasion.

articles.headline: PCR detects more malaria than microscopy or RDTs
articles.title_original: Performance comparison of rapid diagnostic test, microscopy, and polymerase chain reaction for Malaria detection in high-transmission area, Keerom Regency, Papua Province, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Rohkmad MF, Sandy S, Widiyanti M, Adiningsih S, Kesuma AP, Maladan Y, Anggraeni YM, Ramadhani T, Ipa M, Hidajat MC, Kawulur H, Kurniawan CM, Kopo AD, Dharmayanti NI, Garjito TA, Oktavian A, Hendarwan H
articles.details: Diagnostic accuracy study in 234 suspected malaria cases from Keerom Regency, Indonesia. PCR identified 42.3% of malaria cases versus microscopy 26.5% and RDTs 22.6%. PCR revealed substantial number of cases missed by conventional methods, particularly in low-parasitemia situations. Microscopy and RDTs showed significant discrepancies in sensitivity and specificity against PCR as reference. Authors highlight need for enhanced diagnostic strategies to improve case detection in Keerom regency.

articles.headline: Climate warming increases avian malaria in wild birds
articles.title_original: Hidden effects of avian malaria under climate warming.
articles.authors: Braga ÉM, Vieira LMC
articles.details: Long-term study of wild bird population in temperate Northern Europe documenting significant increase in avian malaria parasite prevalence under climate-warming scenario, reflecting disruption of temperature-humidity balance affecting infectious disease spread.

articles.headline: NAT screening detects asymptomatic Plasmodium in donors
articles.title_original: Improving blood safety: NAT-based detection of Plasmodium spp. in blood donors in endemic areas of Brazil.
articles.authors: Gomes de Almeida AC, Shuan Laco AC, Rodrigues MG, Godinho de Siqueira VG, de Moura Brito FB, Gato CM, Mota Cordeiro JS, Hipólito Carneiro JM, Lopes Albuquerque SR, Chaves YO, de Fátima Ferreira da Cruz M, Baptista PA, Addas-Carvalho M, Tarragô AM, de Melo GC
articles.details: Retrospective study of 67,114 Brazilian blood donors screened with NAT PLUS assay. Malaria positivity: 0.01% (8/67,114), all asymptomatic at screening; malaria-related deferrals accounted for 2.6% of total. One donor (12.5% of positive cases) developed symptomatic P. falciparum post-donation confirmed by thick smear. Most positive cases occurred during rainy season. Authors emphasize molecular testing's superior sensitivity for identifying at-risk donors with low-density parasitemia to ensure transfusion safety.

articles.headline: Hypofuran B analogs inhibit P. falciparum in vitro
articles.title_original: Synthesis and In Vitro Activity of Hypofuran B and Analogs Against Plasmodium Falciparum and Trypanosoma Cruzi.
articles.authors: Franco CA, Varejão JOS, Ceravolo IP, Machado VM, Krettli AU, de Melo Resende D, Murta SMF, Martins FT, Pilau EJ, Montes VR, Kohlhoff M, Varejão EVV
articles.details: Synthesis and evaluation of hypofuran B derivatives against P. falciparum and T. cruzi via crossed aldol condensation (yields 20–83%, E/Z ratios 89:11 to 98:2). Most active analogs showed IC50 values 5.35–10.35 µg/mL against P. falciparum. Most promising compound displayed CC50 >400 µg/mL, indicating lower cytotoxicity than chloroquine. Clear structure-activity-toxicity relationships observed; in silico predictions supported favorable drug-like profiles. Authors conclude hypofuran derivatives are promising antimalarial leads.

articles.headline: PCR screening prevents malaria in HCT donors/recipients
articles.title_original: Malaria Screening and Treatment in Hematopoietic Cell Transplant Donors and Sickle Cell Disease Candidates/Recipients: A Case Series Using Malaria Polymerase Chain Reaction Testing and a Literature Review.
articles.authors: Czech MM, Das S, Limerick E, Fitzhugh C, Hsieh M, Cuellar-Rodriguez J
articles.details: Case series of 57 hematopoietic cell transplant donors and 52 recipients with potential malaria exposure. Three asymptomatic donor cases identified (2 by smear/PCR pre-collection; 1 identified retrospectively after transmitting to recipient). Among 52 recipients, 2 developed peri-HCT malaria (1 pre-HCT, 1 donor-derived). Literature review identified 10 detailed HCT malaria cases. Authors demonstrate malaria PCR offers greater sensitivity than conventional methods, prevents unnecessary deferrals, and confirms treatment efficacy.

articles.headline: Bone marrow PCR diagnoses occult malaria parasitemia
articles.title_original: Out of Sight, Should not be out of mind: A perplexing case of Malaria.
articles.authors: Saxena S, Das R, Khurana S, Mirza SA, Joshi KD, Kaur H
articles.details: Case report of 30-year-old male with high-grade fever, chills, vomiting, splenomegaly, and anemia. Multiple peripheral blood smears and RDTs were negative. Bone marrow microscopy revealed rare red blood cells with Plasmodium spp., confirmed by marrow-aspirate PCR. Patient recovered after artemether-lumefantrine and primaquine. Authors underscore bone marrow microscopy and PCR value for malaria diagnosis in patients with unexplained cytopenias and splenomegaly.

articles.headline: Sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine may reduce vulnerable newborns beyond malaria
articles.title_original: Beyond malaria: can intermittent preventive treatment with sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine reduce the number of small vulnerable newborns globally?
articles.authors: Unger HW, Ataide R, Roh ME, Rahman A, Price RN, van Eijk AM, Dorsey G, Ter Kuile FO, Rogerson SJ
articles.details: IPTp-SP reduces low birthweight independent of Plasmodium infection status or drug resistance. Proposed mechanisms include antimicrobial activity against genitourinary infections and pathogenic gut bacteria, direct gut physiology effects, or anti-inflammatory actions. Mechanistic studies and placebo-controlled trials in non-malarious settings are needed to confirm whether IPTp-SP could reduce small vulnerable newborns.

articles.headline: Corrigendum: alpha-gal immune responses in malaria endemic settings
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Naturally acquired immune responses to alpha-gal in malaria endemic settings and pre-clinical efficacy testing with R21/MM" [Vaccine 68 (2025) 127897].
articles.authors: Mukhopadhyay ES, Bellamy DG, Tinto H, Hamaluba M, Truby A, Salman AM, Hill AVS
articles.details: Corrigendum to previously published work on naturally acquired immune responses to alpha-gal in malaria endemic settings and pre-clinical efficacy testing of R21/MM vaccine.

articles.headline: Mosquito feeding preferences and avian malaria Eastern Balkans
articles.title_original: Feeding Preferences of Mosquitoes (Culicidae) from the Eastern Balkans and Their Role in Transmission of Avian Malaria.
articles.authors: Bobeva A, Marinov MP, Klayn S, Ilieva M, Dimitrov D, Zehtindjiev P
articles.details: Study of mosquito feeding preferences collected April–October 2017 and 2019 in deciduous forest and reedbeds. Among 75 engorged mosquitoes with identified blood meal origin, 44 (58.7%) were avian (25 bird species), 30 (40.0%) mammalian (13 species), and 1 (1.3%) reptilian. Avian haemosporidian parasites detected in 11 abdominal (11.6%) and 4 head-thorax (4.2%) samples; most prevalent was Plasmodium relictum SGS1 lineage.

articles.headline: Deforestation drives mosquito ecology and malaria risk Amazon
articles.title_original: Deforestation, Mosquito Ecology, and Malaria Elimination in the Amazon Region.
articles.authors: Grillet ME, Sallum MAM, Conn JE
articles.details: Review examining how Amazon forest degradation affects anopheline vector ecology and human-vector contact rates in relation to malaria transmission. Deforestation identified as main environmental driver of persistent epidemics in tropical humid forest regions. Regional zoonotic malaria increasingly important. Sustainable malaria elimination requires better understanding of mosquito adaptation to anthropogenic pressures on the rainforest.

articles.headline: RTS,S vaccine reduces malaria 30% and severe malaria 58%
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of the RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine in a real-world setting over 1 year of follow-up after the three-dose primary schedule: an interim analysis of a phase 4 study in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi.
articles.authors: Ndeketa L, Haine V, Debois M, Asante KP, Agyapong PD, Kaali S, Devadiga R, Harrison SBE, Boahen O, French N, Kayan K, Ogutu B, Adeniji E, Kariuki S, Owusu-Agyei S, Olewe F, Jere TM, Maleta K, Mategula D, Mzanga P, Phiri VK, Ansah PO, Orimbo J, Ansah NA, Orsini M, Ong'echa JM, Oduro AR, Sifuna PM, Azongo DK, Otieno W, Bangre O, Kaburise MB, Ababio LO, Oyieko JN, Sing'oei V, Amoit SK, Nyangulu W, Schuerman L, Awuni D, Ochieng BO, Onyango I, Odera-Ojwang P, Oguk EA, Mendoza YG, Cherop RY, Okoth GO, Cravcenco C, Chipatala R, Roman F, Oneko M, Savic M
articles.details: Phase 4 post-introduction study in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi enrolling 45,000 children (39,463 analyzed) with 1-year follow-up after 3-dose primary RTS,S/AS01E vaccination. Vaccinated versus unvaccinated: incidence rate ratio 0.70 (95% CI 0.67–0.73) for any malaria, 0.42 (0.30–0.60) for severe malaria, 0.64 (0.56–0.72) for malaria-related hospitalization, and 0.83 (0.64–1.09) for all-cause mortality (non-significant).

articles.headline: Malaria serology for elimination tool India rising burden
articles.title_original: India should consider sero-epidemiology as an additional tool to bolster malaria elimination.
articles.authors: Rahi M, Sharma R, Thiruvengadam K, Perumalsamy N
articles.details: Review of malaria serology's role in elimination strategies. India accounts for >50% of Southeast Asia's malaria burden from P. falciparum and P. vivax; cases increased from 176,522 in 2022 to 255,500 in 2024. Serology detects past exposure, low-density and dormant infections, and measures intervention impact—particularly valuable in low-transmission settings. Routine surveys should incorporate serology alongside rapid diagnostic tests and microscopy.

articles.headline: Plant extract reduces parasitemia and restores malaria-damaged bone
articles.title_original: Osteoprotective Effects and In Silico Profiling of Parinari curatellifolia Planch. Ex Benth. (Chrysobalanaceae) Extract on Acute Plasmodium berghei-Compromised Rat Bone.
articles.authors: Jouonzo J, Kamkumo RG, Ngueguim FT, Efange NM, Tsila SS, Ambamba BDA, Ngondi JL, Ayong L, Lenta BN, Sewald N, Dimo T
articles.details: In vitro and in vivo study of Parinari curatellifolia extract against Plasmodium. Extract showed antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum 3D7 and Dd2 strains. In vivo using P. berghei ANKA, extract reduced parasitemia (p<0.001), improved hematological parameters, and increased bone osteocalcin, TGF-β1, and alkaline phosphatase activity (p<0.001), reversing Plasmodium-induced inflammation and bone damage.

articles.headline: Malaria epidemiology Delhi 2.04% prevalence with co-infections
articles.title_original: Malaria epidemiology, clinical characteristics, and concurrent infections in Delhi, India: A hospital-based study.
articles.authors: Hawadak J, Goel A, Matlani M, Haider M, Kojom Foko LP, Singh V
articles.details: Hospital-based longitudinal study July 2022–November 2023 screening 4,259 febrile patients with 2.04% malaria prevalence (87 cases): P. vivax and P. falciparum. Primary features: chills (80.46%), hepatosplenomegaly (64.37%), myalgia (56.32%), jaundice (51.72%). Severe anaemia most common severe manifestation. Approximately 45% co-infected with dengue, chikungunya, or Salmonella typhus. Risk factors: July–September period (aOR 2.18, p<0.05) and male gender (aOR 1.42, p<0.05).

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistance markers and pfmdr1 signals Ghana 2023
articles.title_original: Molecular Markers of Antimalarial Drug Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum From Kumasi, Ghana, 2023.
articles.authors: Kegya AD, Oppong E, Darko E, Boateng KA, Phillips RO, Heinemann M, Ramharter M, Igiraneza C, Ndoli JM, Mockenhaupt FP, van Loon W
articles.details: Genotyping study of 200 P. falciparum isolates from Kumasi, Ghana (2023): 2.2% non-synonymous pfk13 mutations (including P527H), 29.7% pfcoronin P76S (associated with lower parasite density), predominance of pfmdr1 pattern NFD (suggesting impaired lumefantrine sensitivity), and 64.7% pfaat1 S258L. Day-3 parasite positivity on artemether-lumefantrine examined microscopically in subset. Resistance markers not associated with each other or day-3 positivity.

articles.headline: Cannabis compounds show antimalarial activity preclinical studies
articles.title_original: Cannabis-Derived Compounds Against Plasmodium sp.: A Systematic Review of Preclinical Studies.
articles.authors: de Mendonça Lima T, de Sena LWP, de Sousa ACC, de Freitas GRM, Rotta I, Visacri MB
articles.details: Systematic review of 8 preclinical studies (2001–2022, majority North America) on cannabis compounds against Plasmodium sp. In vitro half-maximal inhibitory concentration ranged 0.16–4.1 μg/mL (mild to high activity). All in vivo studies reported positive effects including moderate antimalarial activity and disease tolerance. Toxicity profile insufficiently studied; most studies had unknown or unclear risk of bias.

articles.headline: 4-nitrobenzoate enhances atovaquone antimalarial efficacy mice
articles.title_original: 4-nitrobenzoate inhibits 4-hydroxybenzoate polyprenyltransferase in malaria parasites and enhances atovaquone efficacy.
articles.authors: Verdaguer IB, Santos MF, Filho MM, Castro GO, Hernández A, Peres MA, Katzin AM, Crispim M
articles.details: Laboratory study of 4-nitrobenzoate (4-NB) inhibition of ubiquinone biosynthesis in P. falciparum. In Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing PfCOQ2, 4-NB inhibited ubiquinone biosynthesis and enhanced atovaquone efficacy and selectivity while preserving proguanil synergy. In vivo, 4-NB improved atovaquone efficacy in P. berghei-infected mice. Identifies PfCOQ2 as a druggable target to support rational antimalarial combination therapies.

articles.headline: GEP1 essential P. falciparum gamete activation transmission target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum gametogenesis essential protein 1 (GEP1) is a transmission-blocking target.
articles.authors: Huppertz F, Caturelli MS, Lehmann LS, Kurth F, Maier AG, Matuschewski K
articles.details: Functional study of P. falciparum gametogenesis essential protein 1 (GEP1) using CRISPR-Cas9. PfGEP1 loss-of-function lines failed to undergo male or female gametogenesis even with xanthurenic acid or temperature stimulation. Field samples (49 cases) revealed two SNPs: V241L (12%) and S263P (20%). GEP1 essential for gamete activation with xanthurenic acid-independent function; proposed as transmission-blocking drug target.

articles.headline: New Plasmodium species in Brazilian bird
articles.title_original: A New Species of Plasmodium of the Subgenus Novyella Infecting White-Shouldered Fire-Eyes (Pyriglena leucoptera) (Aves: Thamnophilidae) in Brazil.
articles.authors: Alves LGM, Pereira PHO, Batista VL, Lopes LE, Braga ÉM
articles.details: A new species of Plasmodium of the subgenus Novyella was identified infecting a white-shouldered fire-eye (Pyriglena leucoptera) in Brazil. Molecular analysis associates the species with lineage PYLEU01, closely related to P. homopolare with 4.18% genetic divergence. Morphology differs from related species by presence of mature amoeboid trophozoites and young meronts located laterally relative to erythrocyte nuclei, with smaller number of merozoites in mature meronts.

articles.headline: Ookinete ultrastructure revealed by volume electron microscopy
articles.title_original: Cellular Hallmarks From Volume Electron Microscopy Reveal Developmental Progression of Plasmodium Ookinetes.
articles.authors: Darif N, Rheinnecker M, Hildenbrand K, Chookajorn T, Dorner LP, Hériché JK, Henriksson S, Funaya C, Hentzschel F, Sandblad L, Billker O, Schwab Y, Frischknecht F
articles.details: Study used volume electron microscopy to investigate Plasmodium ookinete development, examining transformation of fertilized zygotes into motile ookinetes. Analysis revealed intimate mitochondrion-nucleus interactions, different microtubule arrangements, and elongated shapes of micronemes with close interaction with the apicoplast. The work provides an open-access subcellular atlas for ookinete development to aid mechanistic molecular insights and framework for ultrastructural study of other parasite stages.

articles.headline: PfSPZ vaccine safe but not protective in Gabonese children
articles.title_original: Safety, tolerability, and protective efficacy of a radiation-attenuated, whole sporozoite malaria vaccine in children in Gabon: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.
articles.authors: Agnandji ST, Bok J, Alabi A, Kabwende AL, Mbouna A, Bie J, Moukiti E, Lalremruata A, Esen M, Kreidenweiss A, Kc N, Sim BKL, Richie TL, Preston Church LW, McCall MBB, Hoffman SL, Kremsner PG, Mordmüller B
articles.details: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 2 trial of radiation-attenuated P. falciparum sporozoite vaccine in children aged 1–12 years in Gabon (n=134 vaccine, n=66 placebo). Three doses of 9.0 × 10⁵ PfSPZ Vaccine administered by direct venous inoculation on days 1, 8, and 29. At 2–26 weeks post-vaccination, 19% of vaccine recipients and 23% of placebo recipients became blood-smear positive for P. falciparum. Age-stratum-adjusted vaccine efficacy was 9% (95% CI −75 to 53; p=0.78). Vaccine was well tolerated with no treatment-related deaths.

articles.headline: Malaria epidemiology in Meta, Colombia 2017-2021
articles.title_original: Malaria in the department of Meta, eastern Colombia: 2017-2021.
articles.authors: Sánchez Lerma L, Rojas Gulloso A, Pavas Escobar NC, Bernal Otalora JC, Mahecha Bohórquez N, Angulo Ceballos O
articles.details: Study of malaria data from 2016 to 2021 in Meta, Colombia. Most cases were found in Puerto Gaitán, Villavicencio, and Mapiripán. A significant relationship was found between age category and acquiring malaria. Although Meta is not part of the malaria endemic zone, municipalities located near rivers showed enhanced vector contact.

articles.headline: South-South collaboration advancing malaria research and elimination
articles.title_original: Can India-Africa synergies accelerate malaria elimination? A perspective on South-South Collaboration.
articles.authors: Gupta A, Gupta H
articles.details: Perspective on India-Africa partnerships for malaria research and control. Discusses initiatives including ANDI, the Elimination 8 group, and the India-Africa Health Sciences Platform as examples of South-South collaboration among low- and middle-income countries. These partnerships can foster innovation, strengthen local research capacity, improve diagnostic tools, and enhance regional surveillance systems. Identifies barriers including limited funding and fragmented systems, with recommendations for regional investment and regulatory harmonization.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine shows poor efficacy against falciparum in Uganda
articles.title_original: Efficacies of artemether-lumefantrine, artesunate-amodiaquine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in children aged 6 months to 10 years in Uganda: a randomised, open-label, phase 4 clinical trial.
articles.authors: Kamya MR, Nankabirwa JI, Ebong C, Asua V, Kiggundu M, Orena S, Okitwi M, Tukwasibwe S, Agaba B, Kyabayinze D, Opigo J, Rutazana D, Binagwa B, Mugwanya E, Babirye S, Sebikaari G, Condo PM, Appiah G, Nsobya SL, Conrad MD, Rosenthal PJ, Moriarty LF, Yeka A
articles.details: Randomized phase 4 trial of four artemisinin-based combination therapies in 793 children aged 6 months–10 years with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria at three Ugandan sites. Uncorrected adequate clinical and parasitological response for artemether-lumefantrine was 51.8% in Arua and Busia but 79.4% in Agago, significantly lower than other ACTs (98.0% for dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, 99.0% for artesunate-amodiaquine, 73.7% for artesunate-pyronaridine). Parasites carrying PfK13 Cys469Tyr and Ala675Val mutations showed prolonged parasite clearance half-lives. All regimens were well tolerated.

articles.headline: Diversifying antimalarial treatment for uncomplicated falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Diversifying antimalarial treatment for uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Uganda.
articles.authors: Dondorp AM
articles.details: Article addresses diversification of antimalarial treatment strategies for uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Uganda. No abstract available for detailed description of study design, participants, interventions, or quantitative outcomes.

articles.headline: Nuclear medicine imaging for malaria: current gaps and future
articles.title_original: Mini-review and situation report on the role and usefulness of nuclear medicine imaging for malaria.
articles.authors: Duvenhage J, Zeevaart JR, Sathekge MM, Ebenhan T
articles.journal: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging
articles.details: Review of clinical nuclear imaging techniques used in malaria. Although radiopharmaceuticals have added value to understanding some aspects of severe malaria, development has stagnated. Article highlights current limitations in implementing improved malaria-specific radiopharmaceuticals and provides insights on how to overcome these challenges. Focus is on understanding severe malaria pathogenesis and complications.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria reservoir in endemic Nigerian community
articles.title_original: A cross-sectional survey of malaria in asymptomatic and symptomatic individuals in an endemic area of Northcentral Nigeria.
articles.authors: Akoma ON, Okoh FN, Amoke OC
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 328 individuals in Lokoja, Northcentral Nigeria screened for Plasmodium parasitemia during high transmission season (April–October) over 2018–2021. Malaria prevalence was 12.4% (25/202) in asymptomatic individuals and 85.7% (108/126) in symptomatic individuals with no significant difference between groups. Overall P. falciparum prevalence was 40.5% (133/328) with hyperparasitemia; all cases were uncomplicated falciparum-specific. Authors recommend regular monitoring and mass antimalarial administration targeting the asymptomatic reservoir.

articles.headline: Global malaria burden projected to increase through 2030
articles.title_original: The past, present and future of global malaria and neglected tropical diseases: a disease burden assessment from 1990 to 2030.
articles.authors: Li D, Shi Y, Wang R, Hong X, Shi T, Zhu S
articles.details: Analysis of malaria and neglected tropical disease burden from 1990 to 2030 using Global Burden of Disease Study 2019 data and statistical modeling (Joinpoint regression, ARIMA). Western Sub-Saharan Africa had the highest burden in 2019, accounting for half of global cases. Age-standardized mortality rate for malaria and NTDs predicted slow upward trend from 2020 to 2030. Children <5 years had the most serious disease burden across the entire period.

articles.headline: Drug-resistance markers increasing in P. falciparum during SMC, Chad
articles.title_original: Trends in Plasmodium falciparum resistance markers to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine over ten years of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Moissala Health District, Chad.
articles.authors: Grandesso F, Diar MSI, Kouassi F, Sayyad-Hilario J, Ouedraogo P, Djimde AA, Singh S, Dama S
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum resistance mutations in southern Chad during ten years of seasonal malaria chemoprevention with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine. Analyzed 136–256 samples annually for dhfr (N51I, C59R, S108N), dhps (A437G, K540E), pfcrt K76T, and pfmdr-1 N86Y mutations. Triple dhfr mutants declined from 100% (2014) to 83.9% (2023), while quadruple mutants (triple dhfr + dhps A437G) increased from 28.0% to 47.9%. SP and AQ remain effective but rising quadruple mutants associated with partial SP resistance are concerning.

articles.headline: VAR2CSA antibodies protect against placental malaria complications
articles.title_original: VAR2CSA-specific IgG and IgM antibodies are markers of exposure and protection against adverse malaria pregnancy outcomes.
articles.authors: Onwuka AM, Aitken EH, Hasang W, Madanitsa M, Mwapasa V, Phiri K, Ter Kuile FO, Rogerson SJ
articles.details: Study of pregnant Malawian women with malaria (n=321 at enrollment, n=145 during pregnancy). IgG and IgM antibodies to VAR2CSA DBL1X-ID2a domain measured at enrollment and delivery. IgG at delivery associated with lower low birth weight risk in previously uninfected women (aOR 0.43, 95% CI 0.19–0.97, p=0.04). One log higher IgM at enrollment in infected women associated with 23% decreased maternal anemia at delivery (aOR 0.77, 95% CI 0.60–0.99, p=0.04). VAR2CSA antibodies are markers of placental malaria infection and protection against adverse outcomes.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria affects one-third of Nigerians, especially children
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated risk factors of asymptomatic malaria in Nigeria: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Mukhtar A, Ismail M, Usman A, Odetokun IA, Ibrahim MA, Salman AA, Bawa-Sani HG, Nass SS, Goni BW, Shehu MN, Nuhu A, Zhang X, Isah MB
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 25 studies estimated pooled asymptomatic malaria prevalence in Nigeria at 33% (95% CI 26-41), ranging from 25% in the general population to 52% in children. Risk factors included education level (19%), sex (36%), and insecticide-treated net usage (39%).

articles.headline: Fluorescent marker causes female mosquito blood-feeding defect and sterility
articles.title_original: An OpIE2-DsRed marker disrupts female blood-feeding and shortens lifespan in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Sarig A, Sar-Shalom E, Kolley ESM, Yonah ES, Lamdan LB, Lewin AR, Partosh T, Pines G, Bohbot JD, Papathanos PA
articles.details: Study of transgenic Anopheles gambiae with OpIE2-DsRed marker found it caused female blood-feeding defect and sterility, with shortened lifespan in both sexes; a distally curved proboscis explained the defect. A variant promoter OpIE2b produced high marker expression without impairing blood-feeding or lifespan.

articles.headline: Livelihoods and gendered roles drive malaria exposure in Uganda
articles.title_original: Livelihoods as a key social determinant of malaria: Qualitative evidence from Uganda.
articles.authors: Deane K, Atusingwize E, Musoke D
articles.details: Qualitative study (14 key informant interviews, 10 focus group discussions, 11 household interviews) found livelihoods—crop production, livestock husbandry, construction, and dusk/night activities—are key social determinants of malaria in Uganda. Mechanisms included increased mosquito exposure, creation of breeding sites, and gendered care responsibilities exposing children.

articles.headline: Mathematical model explores malaria-COVID-19 co-infection control
articles.title_original: Optimal control analysis of a mathematical model of malaria and COVID-19 co-infection dynamics.
articles.authors: Diabaté AB, Sangaré B, Koutou O
articles.details: Deterministic mathematical model analyzed single and co-infection dynamics of malaria and COVID-19. Basic reproduction numbers were calculated and backward bifurcation conditions identified. Optimal control theory determined disease control strategies using Pontryagin's maximum principle.

articles.headline: Parasitemia levels correlate with liver enzyme alterations in malaria
articles.title_original: The Association between Parasitemia and Liver Enzyme Alterations in Malaria Patients: ABO Blood Group as A Non-Contributory Factor.
articles.authors: Aminizadeh S, Shokri E, Mohebali M, Hanifian H, Zafari S, Edalati M, Bahadory S, Hosseinifard H, Nateghpoor M
articles.details: Case-control study of 50 malaria patients and 50 healthy controls found malaria patients had significantly higher AST, ALT, and ALP levels compared to controls (p=0.001). Liver enzyme levels correlated significantly with parasitemia (p<0.001), but ABO blood group showed no association with liver enzymes (p>0.05).

articles.headline: Baseline study supports tailored SMC in Turkana pastoralist population
articles.title_original: A Baseline Mixed-Methods Study to Inform Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention Delivery in a Semi-Nomadic Population in Turkana, Kenya.
articles.authors: Kostandova N, Kafu C, Chepkwony TJ, Ambani G, Abel L, Rotich B, Kipkoech Kirui J, Kimachas E, Robie E, Lowoi K, Ekai D, Lokoel G, Orinda O, Van Hulle S, Mgeni R, O'Meara WP, Wesolowski A, Genberg B, Menya D
articles.details: Mixed-methods baseline study (198 households, 78 key informant interviews, 31 focus group discussions, 60 household interviews) assessed seasonal malaria chemoprevention feasibility in Turkana. P. falciparum prevalence was 21% by both RDT and PCR. Implementation challenges identified included physical access for pastoralists, stigma, and acceptability differences.

articles.headline: African immunization registries needed for malaria vaccine monitoring
articles.title_original: Need for Real-World Evaluation of Malaria Vaccines and Reliable Vaccination Records in Africa.
articles.authors: Ko YK, Alfvén T, Okomo GO, Kagaya W, Shioda K, Kaneko A, Gitaka J
articles.details: Commentary on real-world effectiveness evaluation of RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccines. Discusses observational study designs (cohort studies, test-negative design) for vaccine effectiveness estimation. Emphasizes critical need for strengthening electronic immunization registries across African countries for robust vaccine monitoring.

articles.headline: Plant-produced antibodies target malaria parasite circumsporozoite protein
articles.title_original: Plant-produced monoclonal antibodies recognize the native circumsporozoite protein on Plasmodium falciparum sporozoites.
articles.authors: Thiganta K, Rattanapisit K, Suwanchaikasem P, Roobsoong W, Bulaon CJI, Phoolcharoen W
articles.details: Successfully produced monoclonal antibodies MS-1797 (targeting circumsporozoite protein NANP repeat region) in plants (Nicotiana benthamiana); variants MS-01 and MS-02 achieved high yields (457.3 and 415.7 µg/g fresh weight) with >90% purity. Antibodies retained antigen specificity and bound native PfCSP on sporozoites by immunofluorescence.

articles.headline: School-age children show highest malaria burden in Ghana municipality
articles.title_original: Malaria test positivity, Plasmodium species distribution, and risk factors in Ho Municipality, Ghana: a retrospective analysis of seasonal and demographic trends (2020-2022).
articles.authors: Squire DS, Afuah GM, Dika ND, Dogbey P, Bohli JK, Logosu PE, Tanson PA, Avorkliya R, Donaldy W, Ablordey K, Kwadzokpui PK
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 27,171 malaria tests from 3 facilities in Ho Municipality, Ghana (2020-2022) found overall test positivity of 8.8%, declining from 9.9% in 2020 to 7.2% in 2022. School-age children (5-12 years) had highest test positivity at 18.0% (aPR=3.26), with seasonal peak in August (13.9%). P. falciparum predominated at 79.9% of confirmed cases.

articles.headline: Young primigravida women face highest malaria risk in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Maternal and household risk factors for malaria in pregnancy and low birthweight: a prospective cohort study from Uganda.
articles.authors: Aguti M, Kizza J, Kakuru A, Nakalembe M, Nankabirwa JI, Gaw SL, Opira B, Ssemukuye T, Ozarslan N, Ranjit A, Dela Cruz E, Clark TD, Roh ME, Jagannathan P, Rosenthal PJ, Adrama H, Olwoch P, Mayende J, Odongo B, John A, Kamya M, Dorsey G
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 2,728 pregnant women in Uganda receiving intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (December 2020-July 2024) found 38.1% parasitemia at enrollment, declining to 6.5% after IPTp initiation. Younger age 16-21 years (RR=1.62) and primigravida status (RR=1.86) increased parasitemia risk. Primigravida women had markedly higher risk of high-grade placental malaria (RR=4.20) and low birth weight (RR=2.14).

articles.headline: Temperature and terrain predict malaria hotspots in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Spatial analysis of malaria incidence and environmental determinants in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Abdo SS
articles.details: Geostatistical analysis of malaria incidence in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia (2022-2023) identified significant spatial clustering (Moran's I 0.558-0.483; p<0.01). Spatial autoregressive regression models identified average maximum temperature (β=0.945, p=0.017) and proportion of highland terrain (β=0.543, p=0.040) as key environmental predictors of malaria incidence.

articles.headline: Arts-based engagement improves community acceptance of vector control
