Interactome analysis confirms PbRpt3 localizes to 19S proteasome regulatory particle and may interact with phospholipid-binding proteins.

articles.headline: Hemozoin drives pro-inflammatory macrophage polarization in vitro
articles.title_original: Malaria pigment hemozoin drives M1 pro-inflammatory macrophage polarization in vitro.
articles.authors: Perego F, Parapini S, Calvo-Alvarez E, Dolci M, Delbue S, Ghezzi S, Vicenzi E, Poli G, Basilico N, D'Alessandro S
articles.details: In vitro study examined hemozoin (malaria pigment) effects on macrophage polarization using primary human monocyte-derived macrophages (MDM) and differentiated THP-1 cells. In non-polarized M0 cells, hemozoin induced M1 pro-inflammatory phenotype, increasing expression and secretion of CXCL8, TNF-α, and IL-1β. In presence of M1 or M2 polarization stimuli, hemozoin further increased CXCL8 and IL-1β in MDM but not in dTHP-1 (TNF-α even reduced in dTHP-1). Hemozoin did not affect M2 markers (PPARG expression, IL-10, STAT6 phosphorylation). Authors conclude hemozoin promotes M1 pro-inflammatory differentiation with model-specific variation.

articles.headline: High asymptomatic malaria infections in national Sierra Leone survey
articles.title_original: Molecular investigation of Plasmodium spp. in Sierra Leone: recommendations on diagnostic methods for malaria control in high-epidemic regions - a nationally representative cross-sectional epidemiological survey.
articles.authors: Zhang M, Mi Z, Wang S, Jalloh MB, Lyu P, Song X, Zhao Y, Zhang J, Xie Y, Wu S, Zheng M, Li X, Yang S, Wang B, Zhao Z, Sevalie S, Sahr F, Hou L, Chen W
articles.details: Nationally representative, age-stratified cross-sectional survey of 2,112 Sierra Leoneans aged ≥2 years conducted during 2022 rainy season found Plasmodium qPCR positivity rate of 63.0% (95% CI 61.0-65.1), regionally weighted 62.9% (95% CI 58.3-67.4). Among 400 typed samples, 97.75% were P. falciparum and 20.5% carried multiple species. Positivity significantly decreased in individuals ≥60 years and was higher in males. Notably, 93.5% of infected individuals were asymptomatic; 18.6% showed low parasitemia. Urban and rural prevalence showed no significant difference. Authors emphasize importance of molecular diagnosis for surveillance and highlight asymptomatic parasite reservoir in population ≥20 years.

articles.headline: Maternal angiogenic proteins linked to newborn growth in malaria pregnancy
articles.title_original: Association between peripheral markers in women with malaria in pregnancy and small newborns: A cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Dos Santos MI, Carvalho Júnior AR, de Oliveira GM, Gomes LC, Prates Syed WA, Separovic EPM, Epiphanio S, Wunderlich G, Marinho CRF, Dombrowski JG
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 386 pregnant women (with and without malaria) from the Amazon region, Brazil, 2013–2015. Peripheral plasma proteins (Ang-1, Ang-2, Tie2, VEGF, sFlt1, VEGFR2, PlGF, sENG, leptin) measured via ELISA. In P. vivax-infected women, sFlt1 associated with newborn weight and length; sENG associated with length. In P. falciparum-infected women, Tie2, sFlt1, and leptin associated with newborn length. Findings suggest understanding maternal protein dynamics may improve interventions in low-transmission areas.

articles.headline: HBV and P. falciparum co-infection rare but clinically relevant in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Hepatitis B Virus and Plasmodium falciparum Co-Infection Among Pregnant Women in Gabon: Prevalence, Biochemical Impact, and Antagonistic Replication Dynamics.
articles.authors: Andeme Eyi AS, Mikelet Boussoukou IP, Omouessi ST, Ondh Obame JA, Makoyo Komba O, Djoba Siawaya JF, Ndeboko B
articles.details: Prospective cross-sectional study of 222 pregnant women in Libreville, Gabon (May 2022–May 2023). HBsAg detected in 9 cases (4.05%): 6 HBV mono-infected (2.7%), 3 co-infected with P. falciparum (1.35%); P. falciparum parasitemia in 58 cases (26.1%). Elevated AST in HBV mono-infected women; liver enzymes normal in co-infected individuals. Inverse relationship between HBsAg and parasitemia observed (ρ = −0.5, p = 0.67, not significant), suggesting possible antagonistic replication dynamics.

articles.headline: Dietary compounds modulate vector susceptibility to P. berghei in American Anopheles
articles.title_original: Dietary para-aminobenzoic acid, uric acid, and antibiotics modulate the susceptibility of Anopheles darlingi and Anopheles albimanus to Plasmodium berghei.
articles.authors: Costa BA, Godoy RSM, Henrique-Gomes L, Santana RAG, Justiniano SCB, Lopes SCP, Monteiro WM, de Melo GC, Secundino NFC, Pimenta PFP
articles.details: Experimental study examining susceptibility of An. darlingi and An. albimanus to P. berghei under dietary post-infection treatments (para-aminobenzoic acid, uric acid, penicillin/streptomycin). Dietary PABA supplementation influenced susceptibility in both species. P. berghei sporozoites detected in hemolymph but not in salivary glands until 28 days post-infection. Results indicate dietary treatments may modulate vector competence and parasite development in American vectors.

articles.headline: P. falciparum k13 gene highly conserved in Ethiopia; no artemisinin resistance detected
articles.title_original: Preliminary analysis of the conserved Plasmodium falciparum k13 gene in Arbaminch and Mirab Abaya, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Jote K, Gebrehiwot Y, Abebe A, Kipayko C, Dieng CC, Lo E, Golassa L, Chala B
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 52 P. falciparum mono-infection samples from Arbaminch and Mirab Abaya, Ethiopia (May–December 2023); median age 19 years (IQR 15–32). Sanger sequencing of k13 gene propeller domain revealed no polymorphisms (pairwise similarity 0.982) with high genetic conservation. Mirab Abaya showed strong homogeneity; Arbaminch isolates slightly divergent. No k13 mutations detected; negative Fu and Li's D statistic suggests selective sweep. Findings indicate continued effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapy in the region.

articles.headline: In silico-designed multi-epitope antigen PfMEDA1 shows promise for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: In silico design and validation of a Plasmodium falciparum multi-epitope diagnostic antigen (PfMEDA1) for the diagnosis of falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Moyeh MN, Shey RA, Mbulli AI, Dieudonne NL, Sofeu-Feugaing DD, Yega VA, Talla RN, Soh-Bang C, Sherinyuy BC, Ngengang AC, Chick JA, Mbacham WF
articles.details: Study designed and validated a chimeric multi-epitope diagnostic antigen (PfMEDA1) from five immunogenic P. falciparum proteins, synthesized and expressed in E. coli. Indirect ELISA tested serum from 223 infected and 81 non-infected individuals from Fako Division, South West Region, Cameroon. Significant difference between groups (p < 0.0001). Diagnostic sensitivity 89.69% (95% CI 84.93–93.35), specificity 75.31% (95% CI 64.47–84.22), Cohen kappa 0.64. PfMEDA1 shows potential as diagnostic antigen pending field validation in varied endemicity settings.

articles.headline: Malaria outbreak in Uganda driven by vector breeding in erosion control pits
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreak facilitated by increased vector-breeding sites from erosion control pits sustained by intermittent rainfall, Mbale District, Uganda, January-June 2019.
articles.authors: Bahizi G, Monje F, Kadobera D, Bulage L, Ario AR
articles.details: Outbreak investigation in Mbale District, Uganda (January–June 2019) identified 8,827 case-patients (attack rate 33%; females 36%, males 27%; ages 5–18 most affected at 2.6%). Case-control study (150 cases vs 150 controls) found stagnant water in erosion control pits strongly associated with malaria (95% cases vs 49% controls, OR = 18, 95% CI 7.0–50). Bed net use protective (69% cases vs 92% controls, OR = 0.17, 95% CI 0.07–0.38); long-sleeve clothing protective (31% vs 72%, OR = 0.19, 95% CI 0.08–0.3). An. gambiae predominant vector.

articles.headline: Metagenomic approach identifies multiple viral pathogens in non-malaria acute febrile illness
articles.title_original: NOMAD: metagenomic characterisation of the viral pathogen composition in outbreaks of non-malaria acute febrile illness cases.
articles.authors: Kulohoma BW, Ng'eno I
articles.details: Study describes NOMAD, a metagenomic approach using profile Hidden Markov Models to characterize viral pathogens in non-malaria acute febrile illness. Applied to simulated and existing datasets (Ebola, hepatitis C, chikungunya, arboviruses). Identified combinations of sequences from multiple viral etiological agents within single disease samples. Provides granular perspective of viral pathogen composition and highlights prevalent strains for routine directed diagnostic testing to improve surveillance in endemic regions.

articles.headline: Multiple essential nodes identified in P. falciparum pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway
articles.title_original: Genetic analysis of pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Rajaram K, Sievert ML, Elahi R, Dillard LB, Blauwkamp J, Absalon S, Prigge ST
articles.details: Genetic analysis of P. falciparum pyrimidine biosynthesis enzymes (PfATC, PfCPSII, PfDHO) revealed multiple essential functions. PfATC knockdown was lethal; N-terminal truncation impaired parasite growth. PfCPSII with Plasmodium-specific insertions found essential for proliferation. PfDHO deletion in engineered uracil-salvaging parasites caused uracil auxotrophy. Findings identify vulnerable metabolic nodes as potential antimalarial drug targets, complementing ongoing development of dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitors.

articles.headline: Novel heterospirocyclic compounds active against drug-resistant P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Novel heterospirocyclic antimalarials with activity against artemisinin- and multidrug-resistant P. falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Theodoridis L, Giannangelo C, El-Saafin F, Prabhath MR, Macraild CA, Sharma P, Merino D, Creek DJ, Carvalho TG
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Study of novel heterospirocyclic compounds (25, 26) showing antimalarial activity within 24 hours against panel of drug-resistant P. falciparum strains. Untargeted metabolomics of infected RBCs suggested compound 25 mechanism involves disruption of pyrimidine biosynthesis and hemoglobin catabolism. Compounds did not induce major toxicity in kidney- or hepatic-derived human cell lines. Heterospirocyclic compounds represent promising opportunity for antimalarial development against drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria with multiorgan failure in child returning from Indonesia
articles.title_original: [Cerebral malaria after travel to Indonesia].
articles.authors: Pollstergaard KK, Vestergaard LS, Aftab H, Poulsen A
articles.details: Case report of young Danish boy with P. falciparum malaria acquired during travel to Indonesia without chemoprophylaxis or mosquito protection. Initial flu-like symptoms misinterpreted as influenza, delaying diagnosis. Presented with cerebral malaria and multiorgan failure requiring intensive care. Rapid treatment with intravenous artesunate resulted in full recovery. Case emphasizes importance of appropriate travel advice, early symptom recognition, and rapid testing in endemic areas.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis reveals malaria hotspots and protective factors in West African children
articles.title_original: Malaria hotspots and risk factors among children under-five years of age across eight West African countries: A geospatial analysis of DHS data.
articles.authors: Aidé ES, Kazienga A, Adegboye O, Sondo P, Tinto H
articles.details: Geospatial analysis using recent Demographic and Health Surveys data from eight West African countries with malaria status by microscopy. Generalized Linear Geostatistical Models incorporated spatial random effects to predict prevalence at 10×10 km resolution. Insecticide-treated net use significantly reduced infection odds in four countries; age <2 years consistently protective across all countries. Marked spatial heterogeneity observed with high prevalence in Benin and Côte d'Ivoire versus lower in Ghana and Liberia.

articles.headline: Relative humidity strongly associated with malaria incidence in The Gambia, 2011–2022
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the significant association between Climatic Variables and Malaria Cases in the Upper River Region of the Gambia.
articles.authors: Okafor UA, Yaffa S, D'Alessandro U, Ojeh VN
articles.details: Study analyzed monthly malaria case data from Upper River Region, The Gambia (2011–2022) against meteorological variables using Kendall rank correlation and negative binomial modeling. Relative humidity associated with increased malaria odds: 3–4% in children <5 years and 6–9% in persons >5 years (p < 0.01). Maximum temperature associated with decreased odds: 20–14% in <5 years and 21–15% in >5 years (p < 0.01). Relative humidity identified as major influencing factor for malaria incidence.

articles.headline: Vivax parasite genome adaptation in Duffy-negative hosts
articles.title_original: Adaptive evolution of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-negative hosts: insights from East African genomes.
articles.authors: Ford A, Dieng CC, Abagero BR, Yan G, Yewhalaw D, Janies DA, Lo E
articles.details: Study analyzed 110 P. vivax isolates from Ethiopia (38 Duffy-negative, 72 Duffy-positive) using genome-wide SNP analysis. Duffy-negative infections showed markedly reduced genetic variation (197,461 SNPs vs 477,561 SNPs in Duffy-positive). Positive selection differed by host genotype: Duffy-negative parasites showed signals in organellar maintenance and vesicle trafficking genes, while Duffy-positive parasites showed selection in drug-resistance and erythrocyte-binding genes. The findings suggest Duffy-negative P. vivax maintains a genetically conserved background reflecting selective constraints and specialized host-parasite interactions.

articles.headline: Climate stress reduces mosquito survival but maintains parasite transmission
articles.title_original: Predicted future climate scenarios impact survival and immune response but not Plasmodium vivax infection in Anopheles aquasalis (Diptera, Culicidae), the primary vector of coastal Central and South America and the Caribbean Islands.
articles.authors: Silva AS, Leda RL, Cella W, Mwangi VI, Santana RAG, Silva-Neto AV, da Silva MNP, Pereira RS, Lopes SCP, Melo GC, Pimenta PFP, Almeida-Val FF, Val AL, Baía-da-Silva DC, Monteiro WM
articles.details: Female An. aquasalis mosquitoes were infected with P. vivax via artificial membrane feeding and exposed to forest control and three IPCC climate scenarios (RCP 4.5, 6.0, 8.5). Parasite development occurred in all conditions with no significant differences in infection rates (54-68%). Mosquito survival declined significantly under RCP 6.0 (18.6% by day 7) and RCP 8.5 (6.5% by day 7) vs control (40%, p<0.001), with altered immune gene expression. Despite reduced vector fitness under high climate stress, parasites retained infectivity.

articles.headline: Artemisinin toxicity and treatment failure in travelers with malaria
articles.title_original: Treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in the nonendemic setting: a narrative review of toxicity, failure, and emerging resistance.
articles.authors: Bottieau E, Mutsaers M, Balerdi-Sarasola L, Yansouni CP, Rosanas-Urgell A
articles.details: Narrative review of P. falciparum malaria treatment in nonendemic countries discusses emerging challenges with artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) and atovaquone-proguanil, including artemisinin-related hemolysis and treatment failures. Identifies factors distinguishing travelers (lack of prior immunity, older age, overweight, comorbidities, comedication) from endemic-area populations. Proposes adaptations for nonimmune travelers including structured clinical follow-up for toxicity and failure detection, and systematic genomic surveillance to monitor emerging resistance.

articles.headline: Community knowledge of traditional antimalarial remedies in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Assessment of knowledge, attitudes, and practices regarding the use of traditional medicine for treating malaria among residents of Woreta Town, Amhara Regional State, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Tadesse TY, Ayele TM, Eshetie A, Yazie TS, Hammesso WW, Dagnew FN, Zelalem AE
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 390 residents in Woreta Town found high community awareness of traditional malaria treatments: 65.1% were aware of antimalarial plants, with Bisana (27.9%), Girawa (26.7%), and Shifarraw (20.1%) most commonly mentioned; 19.3% mentioned animal byproducts like goat bile. Most participants (66.4%) believed integrating traditional medicine with modern practitioners was beneficial. Results indicate substantial community reliance on and belief in traditional remedies in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine efficacy declining; dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine remains effective
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Burkina Faso, 2020-2021.
articles.authors: Gansane A, Tarama C, Lingani M, Debe S, Tiendrebeogo F, Kinda R, Sagnon A, Ganou A, Rouamba T, Sombie S, Some H, Kiendrebeogo S, Bonko MA, Tougri G, Kompaore CS, Wiesner L, Eddis C, Horton B, Kennedy C, Bajic M, Pierre-Louis E, Kahn A, Rosillo S, Patel DS, McCaffery JN, Plucinski MM, Laird V, Ahmed J, Tinto H, Valea I, Moriarty LF
articles.details: Therapeutic efficacy study of 1,078 children with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria receiving artemether-lumefantrine (AL), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP), or artesunate-pyronaridine (AS-PYR). Uncorrected 28-day efficacy ranged from 57.1-64.4% for AL, 96.6-98.9% for DP, and 82.2-90.7% for AS-PYR. PCR-corrected efficacy exceeded 90% WHO threshold for all drugs except AL in one site (89.4%). No artemisinin-resistance markers identified. Findings suggest AL efficacy continues to decline while DP and AS-PYR show promise as alternative first-line therapies.

articles.headline: 9th International Conference on Plasmodium vivax Research proceedings
articles.title_original: Abstracts of the 9th International Conference on Plasmodium vivax Research (ICPvR) 2025.
articles.details: Conference abstracts from the 9th International Conference on Plasmodium vivax Research (ICPvR) 2025.

articles.headline: Highly sensitive RDT identifies more infections but with limited sensitivity
articles.title_original: Assessment of a highly sensitive rapid diagnostic test for reactive case detection of Plasmodium Falciparum infections in Myanmar.
articles.authors: Khine SK, Aung PP, Linn NYY, Monti F, Khin NN, Awabdeh D, Topcuoglu E, Hwang J, Tun KM
articles.details: Reactive case detection study of 1,990 contacts of 51 P. falciparum index cases compared highly sensitive RDT (hsRDT) and conventional RDT (cRDT) against ultra-sensitive PCR. hsRDT detected 78 (3.9%) positive cases vs cRDT's 28 (1.4%), but sensitivity was only 27.9% (95% CI 18.2-39.6%) compared to usPCR's 171 (8.6%) positives. hsRDT demonstrated significantly higher sensitivity than cRDT (27.9% vs 13.7%, p<0.01), but both tests remain limited for detecting low-density infections.

articles.headline: Malaria episodes cost households 44% of monthly income in Burundi
articles.title_original: Economic burden of malaria in children under the age of five in Burundi.
articles.authors: Niyibitegeka F, Manirakiza A, Hezagira N, Sinarinzi P, Abraham P, Devine A
articles.details: Micro-costing study across 15 sites in Burundi estimated cost per malaria episode in children under 5. Average societal cost was US$12.1, with households bearing most costs (US$3.3 out-of-pocket plus US$4.7 productivity loss over 8 days caregiving). Health system costs were US$2.6 for outpatient visits and US$44.0 for inpatient treatment. Household costs represented 44% of monthly income, while health system costs per episode were nearly half of government per capita health expenditure.

articles.headline: Mathematical model reveals endemic equilibrium in malaria transmission dynamics
articles.title_original: Fractal fractional approach to investigate the forecasting and dynamics of malaria disease for early control precaution.
articles.authors: Farman M, Hurdoganoglu U, Hincal N, Gokbulut N, Guler E, Suer K, Sambas A, Hincal E
articles.details: Developed an eight-compartmental fractional-order mathematical model of malaria transmission with subpopulations of humans and mosquitoes. Model was analyzed for existence and uniqueness of solutions and global stability using Lyapunov functions. Numerical simulations at different fractal orders revealed an asymptotically stable endemic equilibrium with rising susceptible human populations and declining infectious mosquito numbers. Findings highlight the necessity of major efforts to reduce malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Spatiotemporal mapping of malaria risk in India across 700+ districts
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria risk in India between 2019 and 2023: a tool for the public to track malaria.
articles.authors: Sam AK, Keshri N, Bhowmick IP, Anvikar A, Phuleria HC
articles.details: Spatiotemporal analysis of 700+ districts in India using district-level surveillance data combined with meteorological and satellite land-cover data. Analysis of 0.33 million cases in 2019 showed decline during COVID-19. P. falciparum concentrated in North-eastern and Central India; P. vivax showed emerging expansion into north-eastern regions. Populations of scheduled castes and tribes and economically marginalized individuals showed associations with drinking water practices, maternal education, and healthcare accessibility. A digital dashboard was developed for public tracking.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence spatially clustered in children; multilevel modeling identifies risk factors
articles.title_original: Exploring spatial variation and multilevel modeling of malaria prevalence among children aged 6-59 months based on RDT in Niger: Insights for public health decision-making.
articles.authors: Mulugeta SS, Masresha BM, Ejigu LA
articles.details: Analysis of 4,724 children from the 2021 Niger Malaria Indicator Survey found 1,121 (23.7%) RDT-positive for malaria with significant spatial clustering (Moran's I = 0.434, p < 0.001). Multilevel logistic regression identified malaria prevalence associated with child age, anemia levels, maternal education, insecticide-treated net use, and number of children sleeping under nets. Distinct hotspots and cold spots were identified. Findings recommend improved ITN coverage and location-specific interventions.

articles.headline: Ethnicity, occupation, malaria species predict delays in care-seeking
articles.title_original: Drivers of delay in reported malaria care-seeking in Myanmar and Thailand.
articles.authors: Htwe KZZ, Aung PL, Sripoorote P, Soe MT, Petchvijit P, Aung PP, Khamsiriwatchara A, Lawpoolsri S, Cui L, Parker DM, Kyaw MP, Sattabongkot J, Kaewkungwal J
articles.details: Longitudinal study in Myanmar (n=2,960) and Thailand (n=15,576) found mean fever duration at diagnosis of 1.8 days (Myanmar) and 2.9 days (Thailand). In Myanmar, delays were associated with Shan ethnicity (TR: 1.48), farmers (TR: 1.12), P. falciparum (TR: 1.10) and P. vivax (TR: 1.23). In Thailand, farmers, lower education, P. falciparum (TR: 1.27) and P. vivax (TR: 1.20) showed longer delays. Shorter delays observed with daily bed net use and prior malaria episodes.

articles.headline: MAP-X maps Plasmodium falciparum protein complex dynamics across lifecycle
articles.title_original: MAP-X reveals distinct protein complex dynamics across Plasmodium falciparum blood stages.
articles.authors: Pazicky S, Tjia S, Farias GB, Piwon N, Philip N, Sobota RM, Waters AP, Gilberger TW, Bozdech Z
articles.details: A thermal proteome profiling method (MAP-X) was applied across seven timepoints in the P. falciparum intraerythrocytic developmental cycle, predicting over 20,000 protein-protein interactions. The technique revealed dynamic alterations in malaria protein complexes and identified moonlighting subunits that dissociate from native complexes to assume different biological functions, providing a resource for uncovering parasite biology.

articles.headline: RIFIN subset linked to severe malaria protection via LILRB1 binding
articles.title_original: A subset of Plasmodium falciparum RIFINs is linked to severe malaria risk reduction and engages LILRB1 through a conserved structural motif.
articles.authors: Kassegne K, Shen HM, Chen SB, Xu SJ, Li J, Xu B, Deitsch KW, Wang Y, Zhou XN, Chen JH
articles.details: A systematic immunoproteomic study using protein microarrays and structural analysis identified a RIFIN subset associated with reduced severe malaria risk. These RIFINs engage the inhibitory receptor LILRB1 through a conserved structural motif anchored by disulfide-bonded cysteines, and logistic regression validated these associations. Crystallographic analyses revealed overlapping binding interfaces for LILRB1 with both RIFINs and MHC-I ligands, suggesting strategies for vaccine design.

articles.headline: Hemoglobin S allele frequency 6.72% in patients with uncomplicated malaria
articles.title_original: Allele frequency of Hemoglobin S among patients with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in N'Djamena, Chad.
articles.authors: Yamti SN, Akouya A, Dezoumbe K, Tawe L, Kgakatsi SRT, Djibagaou AD, Routoubé M, Cappelli G, Atturo S, Dongho GBD, Russo G, Dzomo GRT, Colizzi V, Paganotti GM
articles.details: In 320 patients with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria (2/3 females), the hemoglobin S (HbS) allele frequency was 6.72% as determined by PCR-RFLP. No significant association was found between different HbS genotypes and parasite density. The study confirms a non-negligible frequency of the HbS allele in this population.

articles.headline: Ivermectin reduces scabies in directly and indirectly exposed populations
articles.title_original: Direct and indirect protection against scabies through ivermectin mass drug administration designed for malaria in Mozambique: a substudy nested within a cluster-randomised, controlled trial.
articles.authors: Furnival-Adams J, Houana A, Saute F, Elobolobo E, Rudd M, Nicolas P, Montaña J, Martinho S, Mundaca H, Mbanze J, Soares A, Imputiua S, Ruiz-Castillo P, Ribes M, Sanz A, Mamudo Salé M, Macucha A, Casellas A, Lopez V, Vegove V, Mutepa V, Munguambe H, Xerinda A, Materula F, Rabinovich NR, Engelman D, Chaccour C
articles.details: In a cluster-randomized controlled trial (BOHEMIA) in a co-endemic community, 1,951 participants in 39 clusters received ivermectin 400 μg/kg for 3 consecutive months versus albendazole control. Scabies prevalence fell from 8.10% to 2.11% in the ivermectin group versus 12.30% to 13.71% in controls (aOR 0.18, 95% CI 0.07–0.45, p=0.0002). Ivermectin also protected indirectly exposed children under 5 years (aOR 0.17 at 3 months, p=0.0048; aOR 0.21 at 6 months, p=0.013).

articles.headline: Immunity mechanisms against Plasmodium liver stage infection for prevention
articles.title_original: Exposing the hidden: establishing immunity to Plasmodium liver stage infection.
articles.authors: Quin J, Urrutia Iturritza M, Kina ÜY, Matuschewski K, Ankarklev J
articles.details: This review examines immune responses against the Plasmodium liver stage (pre-erythrocytic phase), highlighting that robust immunity against this stage fails to develop during natural exposure in malaria-endemic countries. The authors discuss how expanding knowledge of key mechanisms in the immune response against liver-stage P. falciparum parasites is shaping current and future intervention strategies aimed at blocking parasite entrance or exit through the liver stage.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria presenting as sudden coma without prior fever
articles.title_original: When malaria hides: a case report of sudden coma without prior fever in cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Muskananfola FV, Purnama A
articles.details: A 19-year-old woman in a malaria-endemic area presented with sudden unconsciousness and rigor without reported prior fever. A rapid diagnostic test was positive for P. falciparum, confirmed by microscopy. The patient was treated with intravenous artesunate followed by primaquine and dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHP), improved during hospitalization, and parasite clearance was confirmed on follow-up microscopy.

articles.headline: Malaria knowledge varies by age, education, and urban/rural location
articles.title_original: Evaluating the General Public's Knowledge of Malaria: A Nationally Representative Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Thalib HI, Khan S, Pereira M, Abouelmagd F, El Said M
articles.details: A cross-sectional analysis of 18,747 Tanzanian individuals aged 15 and older using 2021–2022 Demographic and Health Survey data examined malaria knowledge through standardized questionnaires. Knowledge varied significantly by age (highest in ages 20–24, lowest over 50), education level (secondary or higher education scored better), and location (urban greater than rural, p < 0.05). Media exposure and mobile phone ownership were associated with increased malaria awareness.

articles.headline: HbS and HbC variants protect against severe malaria in Saudi children
articles.title_original: Distribution of HbS, HbC variants, ABO blood groups in relation to malaria infection among Saudi children in Jazan region.
articles.authors: Qahtani SAA, Saleh NM, Alsubaie RS, Almutairi R, Hamid O, Elhassan LA, Abushouk A, Ahmed MS, Nasr A
articles.details: In 587 Saudi children (196 malaria-free controls, 215 uncomplicated malaria cases, 176 severe malaria cases), median age 4–5 years, PCR and ELISA analysis found HbS allele carriers had reduced odds of severe malaria versus controls (OR=0.40, 95% CI 0.22–0.72, P=0.002) and HbC carriers showed stronger protection (P < 0.01). Uncomplicated malaria cases had higher total IgG and IgG subclass levels than both controls and severe malaria cases (P < 0.001).

articles.headline: Adhesion-inhibitory antibodies protect against placental malaria
articles.title_original: A high-throughput assay to measure antibodies that block adhesion of Plasmodium falciparum infected erythrocytes to chondroitin sulfate A.
articles.authors: Dube Y, Hasang W, Teo A, Madanitsa M, Nielsen MA, Ter Kuile FO, Aitken EH, Rogerson SJ
articles.details: A novel adhesion inhibition assay was developed to measure antibodies blocking P. falciparum infected erythrocyte binding to placental chondroitin sulfate A, with strong reproducibility (r=0.82). In 466 Malawian pregnant women, adhesion-inhibitory antibodies were higher in infected multigravidae (46.3%) versus primigravidae (9.7%, p<0.001) at mid-pregnancy. Among 162 women with mid-pregnancy infection, those without placental malaria had higher antibody levels (33.8%) than those with past placental malaria (12.5%; p=0.002).

articles.headline: Monthly DHP-IPT averts 70–90% of malaria cases in school-age children
articles.title_original: Effect of malaria chemoprevention for school-age children across transmission archetypes: a modelling study.
articles.authors: Suresh J, Zimmermann M, Maiteki C, Stahlfeld A, Pratt A, Mathanga DP, Staedke SG, Laufer MK, Drakeley C, Bever C, Cohee LM
articles.details: A modeling study across three epidemiological archetypes (Sahelian, Central, Southern African) at transmission levels (PfPR2-10 5–40%) evaluated intermittent preventive treatment (IPT) of school-aged children with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, artesunate-amodiaquine, or sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine-amodiaquine alongside long-lasting insecticide-treated nets and case management. Monthly dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine averted 70–90% of cases in school-aged children (up to ~2.0 cases/child/year) and 20–60% in younger children and adults.

articles.headline: Gene expression in fikk, phist, surf families linked to cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum gene expression signatures and antibody profiling implicate the fikk, phist, and surf multigene families in severe malaria syndromes.
articles.authors: Cummings BE, Stucke EM, Coulibaly D, Lawton JG, Sobota RS, Kone AK, Kane B, Guindo B, Tangara B, Sissoko M, Maiga F, Traore K, Diawara A, Traore A, Thera A, Dara A, Niangaly A, Daou M, Diarra I, Tolo Y, Sebastian S, Jain A, Nakajima R, Jasinskas A, Silzel EK, Shrestha B, Ouattara A, Munro JB, Laurens MB, Lyke KE, Tebben K, Bogale H, Plowe CV, Sissoko MS, Kouriba B, Takala-Harrison S, Felgner PL, Silva JC, Doumbo OK, Thera MA, Travassos MA
articles.details: A Mali case-control study using RNA-seq compared parasite gene expression in severe malaria syndromes versus uncomplicated malaria controls. Cerebral malaria and concurrent disease showed significant differential expression of multigene families (fikk, phist, and surf) implicated in cytoadherence and erythrocytic remodeling, while severe malarial anemia showed comparatively low differential expression. Custom microarray analysis revealed children with severe malaria developed antibody responses to subsets of these antigens, suggesting potential targets for protective immunity.

articles.headline: Molecular markers of P. falciparum drug resistance and surveillance
articles.title_original: A review on molecular markers of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Abebe W, Woldesenbet D
articles.details: This review examines molecular mechanisms of P. falciparum drug resistance across drug classes. Artemisinin resistance involves Kelch protein 13 mutations; atovaquone resistance involves cytochrome b mutations; chloroquine resistance involves pfcrt, pfmdr1, and plasmepsin II/III; antifolate resistance involves DHFR and DHPS mutations; and amino alcohol resistance involves pfmrp1 and pfnhe1 mutations. The authors recommend strengthening surveillance through transcriptomic, genomic, and proteomic approaches and investigating combination therapies.

articles.headline: Pfhrp2/3 deletions challenge malaria rapid diagnostic tests
articles.title_original: Overcoming the challenges of Plasmodium falciparum histidine-rich protein 2 and 3 deletions in malaria diagnosis and control.
articles.authors: Enyi EO, Nnanna JC
articles.details: This systematic review of 83 studies published 2018–2024 examined Plasmodium falciparum histidine-rich protein 2 and 3 (Pfhrp2/3) deletions. Pfhrp2/3-deficient parasites pose significant diagnostic challenges to rapid diagnostic test (RDT) performance. The review identifies alternative diagnostic approaches and novel RDTs targeting multiple antigens as potential solutions to overcome diagnostic limitations imposed by these deletions.

articles.headline: Pakistani P. vivax shows highest genetic diversity in MSP-3α
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphisms of merozoite surface protein-3α in Plasmodium vivax isolates from Pakistan.
articles.authors: Nguyễn KO, Kang JM, Võ TC, Lê HG, Akhtar S, Nguyễn TH, Nguyễn ĐTD, Cho M, Afridi SG, Na BK
articles.details: Blood samples from 129 Plasmodium vivax-infected patients were analyzed for genetic polymorphisms in the merozoite surface protein-3α (PvMSP-3α), a vaccine candidate. The study identified size polymorphisms in block I and amino acid changes and recombination events in block II as major sources of genetic diversity. Pakistani isolates exhibited the highest genetic diversity compared to P. vivax populations from other geographic regions, with global haplotype network analysis revealing substantial genetic heterogeneity.

articles.headline: PvCyRPA antigen shows strong vaccine potential for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax cysteine-rich protective antigen (PvCyRPA), an important element for vaccine strategies targeting P. vivax, interacts with human erythrocyte surface.
articles.authors: Cebrián-Carmona J, Arévalo-Pinzón G, Barreto-Santamaria A, Gutiérrez-Ortegón DE, Gómez M, Sánchez-Mirón A, Mesa-Valle CM, Molina-Miras A, Garrido-Cárdenas JA, Patarroyo MA
articles.details: This study investigated the expression, localization, and functional role of Plasmodium vivax cysteine-rich protective antigen (PvCyRPA). Recombinant PvCyRPA produced in baculovirus and COS-7 systems bound to both CD71+ reticulocytes and normocytes, demonstrating its role in erythrocyte interaction. Over 90% of sera from malaria-exposed individuals recognized PvCyRPA, confirming strong antigenicity and supporting its potential as a vaccine candidate for anti-P. vivax strategies.

articles.headline: P. yoelii infection inhibits colon cancer in mice
articles.title_original: Plasmodium yoelii infection inhibits colon cancer in mice via modulation of hypoxia-inducible factor-1α expression.
articles.authors: Jiao Y, Ni Y, Ge L, Zhan H, Xie F, Hao N, Xuan L, Xia H, Fang Q, Tao Z
articles.details: This study examined effects of Plasmodium yoelii infection on tumor development in CT26.WT colon cancer-bearing BALB/c mice. Mice were infected with 0.2 mL (5 × 10⁶/mL) P. yoelii-infected red blood cells via intraperitoneal injection. By tumor-bearing day 24, the P. yoelii-infected group showed significantly downregulated HIF-1α (P < 0.01) and VEGFA (P < 0.01) expression, with upregulated VHL (P < 0.01) and p53 (P < 0.01). Tumor growth ceased and significantly shrank (P < 0.001).

articles.headline: Malaria and neglected infections prevalent in pregnant migrants
articles.title_original: Malaria and other neglected infections in pregnant migrants: A retrospective study in Northern Italy.
articles.authors: Todaro L, Sforza A, Zaffagnini A, Cattaneo P, Mazzi C, Rizzolo F, Scarso S, Angheben A, Zammarchi L, Gobbi F, Buonfrate D, Ursini T
articles.details: A retrospective study of 85 pregnant migrant women screened for neglected infections identified malaria in 5 of 29 tested (17.2%), all asymptomatic or mildly symptomatic. Additional findings included Schistosoma serology positivity in 10/36 (27.8%), Strongyloides in 5/84 (5.9%), and HTLV-1/2 in 3/60 (5.0%). The authors conclude that risk-based screening revealed significant prevalence of neglected infections with potential maternal and neonatal consequences, supporting integration of tailored infectious disease screening into antenatal care.

articles.headline: ART-heme adduct biomarker reflects artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Elucidating artemisinin (ART)-heme as a biomarker for ART resistance and ATP-dependent transport of ART in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Liu H, Zhou H, Cai Y, Du S, Huang Z, Xu K, Xing J
articles.details: This in vitro study characterized artemisinin (ART) metabolism in ART-sensitive (Pf3D7/PfDd2) and ART-resistant (Pf6320, K13 C580Y mutation) P. falciparum strains. ART-heme adduct was identified as a major metabolite, forming rapidly and concentration-dependently (50–450 nM) in sensitive parasites but independently of concentration in resistant parasites. ART-heme formation was susceptible to orthovanadate (P-type ATPase inhibitor), and synergistic activity occurred between ART and verapamil. The authors conclude ART-heme may serve as a biomarker for both ART efficacy and resistance.

articles.headline: T follicular helper cell development during malaria infection
articles.title_original: T follicular helper cell development during malaria.
articles.authors: Antonetti RM, Shah M, Butler NS
articles.details: This review examines T follicular helper (Tfh) cell development during malaria infection. CD4 Th1 and Tfh cells are critical for stimulating phagocyte activation and humoral immunity, yet protective immune memory responses rarely develop. The review discusses how malaria induces unique physiologic changes that may generate nonconventional Tfh subsets and examines the phenotypes, functions, and memory development of Tfh cells during Plasmodium infection, while highlighting key knowledge gaps.

articles.headline: Single-cell mapping reveals P. falciparum-mosquito midgut interactions
articles.title_original: Mapping Plasmodium transitions and interactions in the Anopheles female.
articles.authors: Yan Y, Verzier LH, Cheung E, Appetecchia F, March S, Craven AR, Du E, Probst AS, Rinvee TA, de Vries LE, Kauffman J, Bhatia SN, Nelson E, Singh N, Peng D, Shaw WR, Catteruccia F
articles.details: Using single-cell RNA sequencing of parasites and mosquito cells across developmental stages, this study unveiled key transitions in P. falciparum development within the Anopheles midgut. PfSIP2, a Plasmodium transcription factor, was identified as essential for sporozoite infection of human hepatocytes. Parasites preferentially interacted with midgut progenitor cells during epithelial crossing, while tight connections between late oocysts and surrounding muscle cells may ensure parasite adherence. Findings were confirmed in multiple mosquito-parasite combinations.

articles.headline: Novel protein synthesis inhibitor shows antimalarial activity
articles.title_original: A Novel Antimalarial Agent that Inhibits Protein Synthesis in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Bravo P, Diamanti E, Hamed MM, Bizzarri L, Wiedemar N, Passecker A, Brancucci NMB, Albisetti A, Gumpp C, Illarionov B, Fischer M, Witschel M, Schehl T, Hahne H, Mäser P, Rottmann M, Hirsch AKH
articles.details: Compound 31 with a 2-hydroxyphenyl benzamide scaffold displayed potent activity against blood-stage and mature sexual stages of P. falciparum without toxicity to human cells. Resistance selection identified a point mutation in the pfmdr1 gene as the primary mediator of resistance, confirmed via CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing. Proteomics studies revealed the primary mode of action is direct binding to cytosolic ribosomal subunits to inhibit protein synthesis. No cross-resistance was identified with first-line antimalarials.

articles.headline: Sickle cell trait paradoxically alters malaria infection patterns
articles.title_original: Association between sickle cell trait and Plasmodium falciparum infection: evidence for age-specific protection in a cross-sectional study in Guinea-Bissau.
articles.authors: Silva R, Rodrigues A, Arez AP, Medeiros MM
articles.details: A cross-sectional analysis of 601 dried blood samples used β-globin genotyping to examine sickle cell trait (HbAS) and P. falciparum infection. Sickle cell trait was present in 32.0% of individuals. SCT carriers were significantly more likely to have microscopic P. falciparum infection overall (OR = 2.3, 95% CI = 1.4–3.5), with stronger associations in older age groups (15–24 years: OR = 5.0, 95% CI = 1.8–13.8). However, among infected children <5 years old, SCT carriers had lower parasitemia and higher hemoglobin levels.

articles.headline: Chromatin remodeling and RNA binding drive gametocyte sex determination
articles.title_original: Pink or blue: sex determination in malaria gametocytes.
articles.authors: Pradel G, Petter M
articles.details: This review examines molecular mechanisms regulating sex determination in Plasmodium gametocytes at two critical checkpoints. First, chromatin remodeling activates the master regulator AP2-G, triggering a transcriptional cascade that commits parasites to sexual development. Second, post-transcriptional processes including stabilization and suppression of transcripts via RNA-binding proteins guide branching into male or female gametocytes through threshold-trait-dependent mechanisms. The review highlights recent advances in understanding these regulatory mechanisms.

articles.headline: GFAP shows promise but lacks specificity as CM biomarker
articles.title_original: Current status of the GFAP's potentials in cerebral malaria, a systematic review.
articles.authors: Tulantched DSM, Min Z
articles.details: A systematic review identified 24 studies (9 human, 15 experimental) examining glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP) as a biomarker in cerebral malaria. In experimental models, GFAP was consistently elevated in brain and retinal tissues, correlating with astrocyte activation and neuroinflammation. Human studies showed greater variability; several reported no significant increase in plasma GFAP in cerebral malaria patients compared to controls. The authors conclude GFAP shows promise as a biomarker of astrocyte activation but lacks diagnostic specificity, requiring standardized methodologies.

articles.headline: P. falciparum remodels host erythrocyte proteome during blood stage
articles.title_original: Crosstalk between malaria and host proteome during the intraerythrocytic developmental cycle.
articles.authors: Pazicky S, Bozdech Z
articles.details: Review of how P. falciparum modifies and imports specific RBC proteins during asexual intraerythrocytic development to facilitate parasite growth. Discusses evidence that targeting host proteome engagements could inform design of host-directed antimalarial therapies.

articles.headline: Vesicle adapter proteins essential for parasite invasion and intracellular survival
articles.title_original: Vesicle adaptors in malaria parasites show conservation and flexibility of protein sorting machinery.
articles.authors: Cubillán-Marín J, Fröhlke U, Ramón-Zamorano G, Mainye S, Mesén-Ramírez JP, Farias GB, Höhn K, Gilberger TW, Bártfai R, Spielmann T
articles.details: Study revealed AP-1, AP-3, and AP-4 protein adaptors critical for P. falciparum survival. AP-1 required for intracellular growth and biogenesis of invasion organelles; AP-3 and AP-4 required for host cell invasion. Sorting machinery showed similarities to model organisms but unconventional features including tepsin functioning with AP-1.

articles.headline: Chloroquine resistance transporter expressed across parasite life stages
articles.title_original: Stage-dependent expression and vacuolar localization of Plasmodium berghei chloroquine resistance transporter (CRT).
articles.authors: Korbmacher F, Rauch M, Kenthirapalan S, Kooij TWA, Maier AG, Matuschewski K
articles.details: CRT-mCherry P. berghei parasites profiled for spatio-temporal CRT expression. CRT expressed in asexual blood stage localizing to digestive vacuole; abundant in gametocytes and ookinetes. Expression switched off during sporogony and early liver infection, returning in mature liver stages.

articles.headline: Risk score predicts artesunate treatment failure in malaria patients
articles.title_original: Development of a clinical risk scoring system for artesunate treatment failure prediction in malaria patients.
articles.authors: Kim D, Lee DH, Song Y, Kim JS, Gwak HS
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Retrospective study of 98 patients in South Korea (2014–2023) receiving intravenous artesunate; 12.2% experienced treatment failure per WHO criteria. Multivariable analysis identified female sex, parasitemia >5%, and impaired consciousness as independent risk factors. Risk-scoring system predicted failure rates: 0 points=5%, 1 point=16%, 2 points=41%, 3 points=72%, 4 points=90% (AUROC=0.768).

articles.headline: Review: drug-resistant malaria mechanisms and global impact
articles.title_original: The evolving threat of drug-resistant malaria in a globalized world.
articles.authors: Shukla S, Mani A
articles.details: Review highlighting molecular mechanisms of antimalarial drug resistance in P. falciparum and its global impact. Emphasizes urgent need for real-time surveillance systems and deeper understanding of parasite drug-resistance genetics to inform improved treatment strategies and control measures.

articles.headline: Telomere shortening in malaria: infection impacts cellular aging
articles.title_original: Malaria infection and telomere length: A review.
articles.authors: Wakai TN, Fiamitia C, Oba EB, Chinedu SN, Afolabi IS
articles.details: Review of telomere dynamics in malaria pathogenesis. Acute malaria initiates rapid telomere degradation, cellular senescence, and suppressed telomerase with partial recovery after treatment. Prolonged endemic exposure exacerbates telomere shortening via oxidative stress and inflammatory mediators. Discusses telomere length as potential biomarker for malaria severity and treatment outcomes.

articles.headline: Drug-resistance markers in Malawi: artemisinin susceptible, lumefantrine declining
articles.title_original: Emergence of chloroquine-sensitive Plasmodium falciparum and rising resistance to first-line artemisinin partner drugs in Malawi.
articles.authors: Mazigo E, Jun H, Lee WJ, Louis JM, Syahada JH, Fitriana F, Muh F, Ahmed MA, Lu F, Han JH, Kwon TH, Lee SJ, Na S, Chun W, Park WS, Han ET, Kidima W, Han JH
articles.details: Survey of 1,582 P. falciparum isolates from Mzuzu and Lilongwe, Malawi (December 2020–June 2021) showed no pfk13 or pfcrt resistance mutations, maintaining artemisinin and chloroquine susceptibility. However, pfmdr1-NFD haplotype (lumefantrine resistance) present in 42.9% of isolates, and quadruple pfdhfr-pfdhps mutant (SP resistance) in 87.5%. Declining LUM efficacy in ACTs and SP for IPTp pose ongoing risk.

articles.headline: FIKK kinase epitopes for sensitive malaria parasite antigen detection
articles.title_original: Antigenic determinant analysis of FIKK Kinase family to identify novel candidates for diagnosis of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Rajendra Prasad M, Anil Kumar D, Dodwani E, Singh S, Trivedi V
articles.details: Chimeric C-FIKK antigen developed using P. falciparum FIKK kinase epitopes validated with purified antibodies. Detection limit 3 μM for FIKK9.1 with 95.45% accuracy in mock patient samples. No cross-reactivity with host blood components, bacteria, virus, fungi, or algal organisms, suggesting robust diagnostic target.

articles.headline: Arterolane-piperaquine once-daily equals artemether-lumefantrine twice-daily
articles.title_original: Arterolane and piperaquine Vs. artemether and lumefantrine in uncomplicated malaria: A randomized study in Nigeria.
articles.authors: Awodele O, Bello IS, Akodu BA, Ahmed AA, Usman S, Bolarinwa RA, Akinyede A, Adelabu HA, Akande EO, Dey J, Kudrigikar V, Rao PS, Mehta S
articles.details: Phase IV RCT in Nigeria with 350 patients with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria randomized to arterolane-piperaquine (150+750 mg once daily) or artemether-lumefantrine (80+480 mg twice daily) for 3 days. PCR-corrected ACPR 100% on Day 28 in both groups; PCR-uncorrected ACPR comparable (AMP 98.2% vs AL 99.4% Day 28, p=0.352). No significant differences in parasite/fever clearance times or adverse events.

articles.headline: PBPK modeling reveals lysosomal sequestration in pyronaridine pharmacokinetics
articles.title_original: Importance of Lysosomal Trapping and Plasmodium Parasite Infection on the Pharmacokinetics of Pyronaridine: A Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model-Based Study.
articles.authors: Chu WY, Schouten WM, Mavoko HM, Tshiongo JK, Yobi DM, Kabasele FA, Kasereka G, Maketa V, Sevene E, Vala A, Shin J, D'Alessandro U, Kayentao K, Huitema ADR, Dorlo TPC
articles.details: PBPK modeling study with 52 healthy volunteers and 25 malaria patients examined pyronaridine pharmacokinetics. Lysosomal sequestration essential for capturing drug distribution. In malaria patients, low hemoglobin and accumulation in parasite compartments critical; free pyronaridine in parasites >10-fold higher than whole blood. Higher parasitemia on day 1 increased AUC and Cmax; subsequent hemoglobin decline reduced these parameters.

articles.headline: Proteasome inhibitor ONX-0914 shows potent antiplasmodial activity with favorable PK
articles.title_original: Dual-targeting proteasome inhibitor ONX-0914 demonstrates potent antiplasmodial activity for malaria treatment.
articles.authors: Van Truong N, Nguyen TK, Thau NS, Chu TH, Sanaullah B, Venkataramaiah C, Han JH, Na SH, Park WS, Chun WJ, No JH, Han ET
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: ONX-0914 demonstrated potent activity against multiple P. falciparum strains (IC50 <50 nM), including CQ- and ART-resistant parasites, with synergy to artesunate and low human cell toxicity (CC50 >100 μM). In vivo (P. berghei), achieved >95% parasite suppression after 4 days with ED50 7.62 mg/kg oral and 6.52 mg/kg intraperitoneal, low recrudescence (<2% at one month), and favorable PK (bioavailability 23.83%, T1/2 7.9 h IV, 0.7 h oral).

articles.headline: Endothelial adhesion receptors in cerebral malaria brain sequestration
articles.title_original: Mapping the expression of endothelial adhesion receptors for Plasmodium falciparum-infected erythrocytes in fatal cerebral malaria in Malawian children.
articles.authors: Dorovini-Zis K, Li H, Zhe C, Zhang B, Small D, Taylor TE
articles.details: Post-mortem study of 50 Malawian children (27 cerebral malaria, 23 non-malarial death) examined 5 cytoadhesion receptors (ICAM-1, CD36, VCAM-1, E-selectin, thrombospondin) expression in 12 brain regions. ICAM-1 most widely expressed with highest colocalization of receptor-expressing endothelium with sequestered infected RBCs in frontal lobe. Sequestration ratios ~100% across all receptors and brain regions; receptor expression and colocalization greater in brain than peripheral organs.

articles.headline: TLR4 and TLR9 variants associated with P. vivax parasitemia and clinical symptoms
articles.title_original: New Evidence of TLR4 and TLR9 Variants Influencing Parasitaemia and Symptoms of Plasmodium vivax Infection.
articles.authors: Silva MMRI, Correia GMA, Lindenau JD, Cunha MG, Ohnishi MDO, Ventura AMRS, Ribeiro-Dos-Santos Â, Hutz MH, Sortica VA
articles.details: In 148 individuals with symptomatic uncomplicated P. vivax malaria, genotyping revealed that TLR9 rs352140TT homozygotes had significantly higher parasitaemia levels than C allele carriers (p = 0.034), while TLR4 rs1927911GG homozygotes had a higher clinical index than A allele carriers (p = 0.018). The rs1927911 variant in TLR4 intron 1 is newly described as associated with P. vivax malaria symptoms in Brazilian Amazonian populations.

articles.headline: ACKR1/Duffy antigen functions beyond P. vivax erythrocyte invasion
articles.title_original: The emerging roles of ACKR1/Duffy antigen beyond malaria.
articles.authors: Shaikh R, Ghosh K, Gorakshakar A
articles.details: This perspective highlights emerging functions of ACKR1/Duffy antigen beyond its well-characterized role in P. vivax erythrocyte invasion, including roles in inflammation, cancer biology, hematopoiesis, and other diseases with potential therapeutic applications.

articles.headline: STEVOR multiepitope fusion antigen vaccine shows broad immunogenicity for severe malaria
articles.title_original: Conserved multiepitopes in Plasmodium falciparum STEVORs enable rational design of a fusion antigen vaccine construct with broad immunogenicity.
articles.authors: Sun ZS, Yin JX, Zhao HQ, Zhu YS, Chen SB, Shen HM, Xu B, Zhou XN, Wang TY, Yang WX, Duan YW, Chen JH, Kassegne K
articles.details: Conserved multiepitopes were identified in STEVOR antigens across 138 clinical isolates and 342 global strains. A multiepitope fusion antigen (MEFA) vaccine construct achieved 97.15% global HLA coverage and elicited CD4+ and CD8+ T-cell proliferation and IgG antibodies in mice. Recombinant MEFA was recognized by significantly more anti-STEVOR IgG antibodies from nonsevere malaria cases than severe cases, suggesting association with milder disease outcomes.

articles.headline: Lepidobotrys staudtii extract shows activity against drug-sensitive and resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: In vitro antiplasmodial activities of extract and fractions from Lepidobotrys staudtii against sensitive and resistant blood-stage parasites of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Magoudjou Pekam JN, Efange NM, Mishro L, Keumoe R, Ndjakou BL, Ayong L, Njayou FN, Moundipa PF, Rajendran V
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: The ethyl acetate fraction of L. staudtii stem bark showed highest potency with IC50 of 3.4 μg/mL against Pf3D7 (drug-sensitive) and comparable activity against PfINDO (chloroquine-resistant). Ring stage parasites were particularly susceptible (IC50: 2.17–4.87 μg/mL for Pf3D7 and 2.27–6.27 μg/mL for PfINDO). The fraction showed rapid action within 12 h, arrested parasite development and merozoite egress, and displayed no cytotoxicity at concentrations up to 500 μg/mL.

articles.headline: PfCRT and PfDHFR mutations confer antimalarial drug resistance through altered binding interactions
articles.title_original: Computational investigation of mutations in PfCRT and PfDHFR proteins for emerging resistance of Plasmodium falciparum to antimalarial drugs.
articles.authors: Ghosh S, Joshi D, Ponnusamy CS, Sridharan B, Velusamy M
articles.details: Molecular docking and dynamics simulations analyzed how PfCRT and PfDHFR mutations affect binding of nine antimalarial drugs including piperaquine. The PfCRT-K76A piperaquine complex showed binding affinity of −9.5 kcal/mol with moderate structural deviation (0.970 ± 0.202 nm) and greater solvent accessibility (246.01 ± 6.135 nm²). The PfDHFR-N51I piperaquine complex showed stronger binding (−10.8 kcal/mol) but higher structural fluctuation (RMSD: 4.491 ± 1.462 nm) and increased compactness, suggesting restricted ligand accommodation and possible resistance mechanisms.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum Hsp90 C-terminal nucleotide-binding site offers novel drug target
articles.title_original: Investigation of a cryptic ligand binding site on Plasmodium falciparum Hsp90.
articles.authors: Mansfield CR, Taggart EL, Chirgwin ME, Derbyshire ER
articles.details: A cryptic C-terminal nucleotide-binding site on P. falciparum Hsp90 was characterized using affinity experiments and computational methods. Binding experiments with ATP analogs and ATP resin supported specific ATP interactions with a C-terminal truncation of PfHsp90. Limited proteolysis indicated association with ATP, dATP, and ADP, but not AMP or GTP. Mutagenesis studies supported molecular basis for these interactions, suggesting this less-conserved C-terminal site as an alternative route for developing selective antimalarial inhibitors.

articles.headline: Novel pfdhps intron variant associates with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance haplotypes
articles.title_original: A novel intron variant is associated with emerging pfdhps mutant haplotypes in West and Central African Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Hocke EF, Hansson H, Chopo-Pizarro A, Adegbola AJ, Bolaji O, Ngwa Niba PT, Ali IM, Nji A, Mbacham W, Baraka V, Kulaya NB, Kahunu GM, Mavoko HM, Nkoli PM, Sompwe EM, Mbongi D, Mitashi P, Bédia VA, Gnagne PA, Konaté A, Yavo W, Menan H, Beshir K, Mousa A, Sutherland CJ, Alifrangis M, Roper C
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: A non-coding intron mutation (a548383t) in pfpppk-dhps was identified in 929 P. falciparum samples from five West and Central African countries. The 548383t variant was most strongly associated with the VAGKGS haplotype (OR = 211.7, P < 0.001), followed by VAGKAS (OR = 39.2, P < 0.001) and VAGKAA (OR = 33.6, P < 0.001). The mutation was significantly associated with the 431V pfdhps mutation and showed reduced di-nucleotide repeat diversity in 431V-positive variants, indicating possible selective sweeps.

articles.headline: Integrated Community Case Management adherence to malaria guidelines in DRC children
articles.title_original: Assessment of adherence to the National Malaria Guidelines in treatment management of malaria among children under five in integrated community case management in the DRC.
articles.authors: Ibrahimi S, Kadiobo L, Embeke N, Diarra H, Tshiswaka G, Mukumena E, Brunner B
articles.details: A sample of 2,326 children from 30 ICCM sites in the DRC was assessed for guideline adherence. About 63.8% had malaria; 94.8% of those clinically diagnosed received RDT versus 2.8% undiagnosed (p = 0.0001). Those with malaria were more likely to receive treatment (93.4% vs. 6.6%, p = 0.0001), but 18.5% without malaria still received treatment. Kasai Oriental showed higher RDT adherence (AOR = 1.89, CI: 1.51–2.37) versus Lualaba, while Tanganyika showed lower adherence (AOR = 0.40, CI: 0.81–0.92).

articles.headline: HDAC inhibitors show potent activity against asexual and exoerythrocytic P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial activity of pentyloxyamide-based histone deacetylase inhibitors against Plasmodium falciparum parasites.
articles.authors: Dawood WA, Fisher GM, Kremeyer J, Fischer F, Home JL, Goodman CD, Lam KS, Maier AG, Kurz T, Andrews KT
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: A panel of 24 HDAC inhibitors was tested against asexual intraerythrocytic P. falciparum parasites. The most potent compound (4o) had IC50 of 20 nM with >250-fold selectivity for P. falciparum over human cells. Compound 4o was also active against exoerythrocytic stage parasites (IC50 24 nM) but lacked potent activity against late-stage gametocytes (IC50 > 2 μM). The compound caused in situ hyperacetylation of P. falciparum histone H4 and stabilized PfHDAC1, confirming HDAC inhibition as the mechanism.

articles.headline: P. vivax resurgence in Eastern Mediterranean, Western Pacific, Southeast Asia regions
articles.title_original: Indigenous Plasmodium vivax upsurge in the Eastern Mediterranean, Western Pacific, and South East Asia regions - beyond the constant culpability of climate change, COVID-19, and armed conflicts.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Sharma A
articles.details: Epidemiological analysis revealed significant P. vivax upsurges in more than half of countries in the Eastern Mediterranean, Southeast Asia, and Western Pacific regions. Papua New Guinea and Yemen showed consistent increases since 2015. Indonesia and Pakistan experienced sharp rises between 2020 and 2023 (Indonesia +100%, Pakistan +83.4%). Thailand, targeted by WHO E-2025 elimination initiative, appeared unlikely to achieve elimination by 2025. COVID-19, armed conflicts, and climate change do not fully explain resurgence; additional factors such as chloroquine resistance also contribute in some countries.

articles.headline: Microbial metabolites show potent low-nanomolar antiparasitic activity in drug screening
articles.title_original: Screening and hit evaluation of a microbial metabolite library against the pathogenic Plasmodium falciparum and Toxoplasma gondii parasites.
articles.authors: Gancheva MR, Mao EY, Romeo O, Vuong D, O'Handley R, Page SW, Lacey E, Wilson DW
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: A library of 812 microbial natural product compounds was screened against P. falciparum erythrocytic stage and T. gondii tachyzoites. 219 compounds inhibited P. falciparum growth by ≥80% at 2 μg/mL (1–10 μM for >90% of compounds), while 149 showed equivalent activity against T. gondii. Monocyclic thiazole peptides (micrococcin P1, thiocillins) and pleuromutilins exhibited both low antiparasitic IC50 values and low cytotoxicity, highlighting potential for further development.

articles.headline: NMR backbone resonance assignments of P. falciparum flap endonuclease for drug discovery
articles.title_original: (1)H, (15)N and (13)C backbone resonance assignments of flap endonuclease from Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: do Aido-Machado R, Baxter NJ, Rowe ML, Pohare MB, Vitovski S, Sayers JR, Waltho JP
articles.details: NMR spectroscopy assigned backbone resonances of P. falciparum flap endonuclease (PfFEN349) in its substrate-free state. 90% of backbone resonances of residues 2–349 were assigned, with 298 backbone amide resonances identified in the 1H–15N TROSY spectrum (of 337 theoretically-detectible, excluding prolines and N-terminal glycine). Secondary structure prediction from chemical shift analysis using TALOS-N was in good agreement with AlphaFold modeling. These assignments provide a foundation for structure-based drug discovery against PfFEN349 as an antimalarial target.

articles.headline: Plasmodium heme detoxification protein expression and functional studies
articles.title_original: Recombinant expression and purification of Plasmodium heme detoxification protein in E. coli: Challenges and discoveries.
articles.authors: Singh R, Makde RD
articles.details: The researchers attempted to express Plasmodium heme detoxification protein (HDP) recombinantly in E. coli using multiple strategies including orthologs, consensus sequences, fusion partners, and construct optimization. Only one construct (HDPpf-C10, with 44-residue N-terminal truncation and C-terminal 6-His tag) achieved soluble expression. Despite retaining heme-binding domains, HDPpf-C10 showed no detectable heme-to-Hz transformation activity, revealing the N-terminal region's essential role in heme binding and conversion to hemozoin.

articles.headline: Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance reduces IPTp efficacy in pregnancy
articles.title_original: The impact of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance on the effectiveness of intermittent preventive treatment for the prevention of malaria in pregnancy in Africa: an updated systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: van Eijk AM, Stepniewska K, Khairallah C, Rodriguez E, Ahn J, Gutman JR, Ter Kuile FO
articles.details: This meta-analysis of 122 studies (148,693 participants) examined how P. falciparum sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) resistance affects intermittent preventive treatment during pregnancy (IPTp-SP). In west and central Africa, three versus two SP doses reduced malaria parasitemia more effectively in very low-resistance areas (aRR 0.71, 95% CI 0.65-0.78) than low-resistance areas (aRR 0.83, 0.72-0.95). In east and southern Africa, efficacy declined from moderate (aRR 0.63) to high (aRR 0.89) and very high (aRR 0.93) resistance, with similar patterns for low birthweight prevention.

articles.headline: Pyruvate kinase deficiency creates mechanical barrier to malaria
articles.title_original: Metabolic Rigidity as a Mechanical Barrier to Malaria: Flickering Loss in PKLR-Deficient Erythrocytes.
articles.authors: Hernando-Ospina N, Calero M, Solís G, Azcárate IG, Herráez-Aguilar D, Caselli N, Moleiro LH, Segovia JC, Bautista JM, Monroy F
articles.details: This study used high-speed flickering spectroscopy and microrheology to examine how pyruvate kinase (PKLR) deficiency affects red blood cell mechanics. PKLR-mutant erythrocytes exhibited reduced ATP-dependent flickering amplitude and membrane fluidity compared to healthy controls. When infected with Plasmodium yoelii, these metabolically rigidified cells showed mechanical properties that hindered parasite-induced remodeling, suggesting glycolytic insufficiency creates a biomechanical barrier against malaria infection.

articles.headline: Severe malaria in pregnancy: fetal monitoring during mechanical ventilation
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria and Mechanical Ventilation in Pregnancy: The Crucial Role of Fetal Doppler in Monitoring Fetal Well-Being.
articles.authors: Olaya S, Angulo SD, Miranda J, Garay M
articles.details: This case report describes a 33-year-old at 33 weeks gestation who developed severe malaria with respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation. Continuous fetal monitoring with Doppler velocimetry showed reassuring parameters, with normal umbilical and middle cerebral artery flow throughout ventilation. The authors highlight the importance of fetal Doppler surveillance in pregnant patients undergoing mechanical ventilation, noting this represents a rare clinical scenario with limited published evidence on optimal monitoring strategies.

articles.headline: VECTRON T500 broflanilide superior efficacy in indoor residual spraying
articles.title_original: Community evaluation of VECTRON™ T500 (broflanilide) for indoor residual spraying for malaria vector control in Siaya county, Kenya.
articles.authors: Wekesa CN, Polo B, Muchoki M, Omondi S, Kipsum M, Abong'o B, Machani MG, Bange T, Abbey I, Kiptoo P, Mburugu P, Keitany K, Sang D, Ramaita E, Ochomo E
articles.details: A non-inferiority study in Siaya County (June-November 2024) compared VECTRON T500 (broflanilide, 100 mg a.i./m²) to Actellic 300CS (1 g a.i./m²) for indoor residual spraying. Against resistant An. gambiae s.s. Bungoma strain, VECTRON T500 achieved 98.73% mortality versus 80.22% for Actellic (p < 0.001, Cohen's d = 2.27). VECTRON T500 maintained >95% efficacy throughout six months while Actellic declined below 80% after month 4. No adverse events occurred in VECTRON households versus 8% (12/150) in Actellic households.

articles.headline: Anopheles diversity in WHO Eastern Mediterranean region systematically reviewed
articles.title_original: Diversity and spatial distribution of malaria vectors in the WHO Eastern Mediterranean region from 1900 to 2024: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Rashti R, Chavoshi E, Tapak L, Nikpoor F, Ayubi E, Asakereh H, Mohammadi Y
articles.details: This systematic review of 276 eligible studies identified 45 Anopheles species across the WHO Eastern Mediterranean region. The five most frequently studied species were Anopheles stephensi (102 studies), A. superpictus (57), A. dthali (49), A. culicifacies (47), and A. maculipennis (45). Research representation spanned 18 countries, with Iran contributing the largest share of studies (122), followed by Pakistan (35), Sudan (31), and Egypt (30). The review emphasizes the need for ongoing vector surveillance to detect shifts in distribution.

articles.headline: Artemisinin resistance R561H spreads in Tanzania, 2021-2023
articles.title_original: Temporal Trends in Artemisinin Partial Resistance and Other Antimalarial Drug Mutations in Plasmodium falciparum from Kagera Region, Northwestern Tanzania, 2021-2023.
articles.authors: Simkin A, Mandai S, Fola AA, Young NW, Pereus D, Bakari C, Madebe RA, Seth MD, Mrengela RB, Kisambale AJ, Chacha GA, Mandara CI, Francis F, Mbwambo D, Garimo I, Chacky F, Aaron S, Lusasi A, Molteni F, Njau RJA, Kajange S, Nhiga SL, Mohamed A, Juliano JJ, Ishengoma DS, Bailey JA
articles.details: Longitudinal molecular surveillance in Kagera region (2021-2023) genotyped 2,826 P. falciparum isolates from seven districts, tracking artemisinin-resistance (ART-R) mutations. The K13 mutation R561H persisted in border districts (14-26% prevalence) and appeared for the first time in Muleba (5.0%) and Bukoba rural (0.7%) in 2023. Regional R561H prevalence rose from 5.5% (2021) to 11.3% (2022), then stabilized at 6.9% (2023). Additional validated (A675V) and candidate (V568G, P441L) mutations were detected at low frequencies, with marked geographic heterogeneity in antifolate resistance markers.

articles.headline: Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite proteins inhibit liver stage replication
articles.title_original: Two Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage replication.
articles.authors: Vo KC, van Biljon R, Zanghi G, Zavala-Martinez B, Betz W, Vaughan AM, Painter HJ, Kappe SHI
articles.details: This functional study identified two hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins from Plasmodium vivax that suppress schizogony. When individually expressed in Plasmodium yoelii liver stages, each protein blocked liver stage schizogony, leaving parasites small and uninucleate. The proteins (IESI-1 and IESI-2) interact with specific RNA sequences, suggesting they inhibit schizogony through RNA binding. These findings provide the first functional evidence for a potential molecular mechanism underlying hypnozoite formation in P. vivax.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum schizogony replication dynamics and drug targets
articles.title_original: Unusual replication dynamics during Plasmodium falciparum schizogony.
articles.authors: Berners-Lee R, Smith TK
articles.details: This perspective discusses Plasmodium falciparum's unusual asexual replication via schizogony, in which the parasite undergoes alternating asynchronous rounds of DNA replication and nuclear division within shared cytoplasm, followed by mass cytokinesis producing multiple daughter cells. The authors note P. falciparum's highly AT-rich genome and schizogony's unique pressures likely shape replication dynamics. They propose that clarifying these dynamics may reveal parasite-specific vulnerabilities and inform development of new antimalarials.

articles.headline: High malaria knowledge-attitude-practice scores despite persistent transmission
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitude and practice in high endemic settings for malaria: a cross-sectional study in a rural community in central Mozambique.
articles.authors: Lequechane JD, Chale F, Azevedo N, Abílio AP, Muanido AG, Gonçalves L, Manuel JL, Silveira H
articles.details: This cross-sectional KAP study in Nhamatanda district (Sofala province) surveyed household heads before (May 2021) and after (June 2022) the REPELMALARIA intervention. Knowledge of malaria symptoms, transmission, prevention, and treatment remained high in both periods (96.8% before, 91.1% after). Importantly, no significant differences were observed between intervention and control groups, suggesting the intervention did not impact KAP scores and indicating that higher malaria burden in the area is not attributed to deficient knowledge or practices.

articles.headline: Sociodemographic and housing factors drive malaria recurrence in children
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic and ecological factors associated with malaria and malaria recurrence risk in children in Msambweni, Kwale County, Kenya.
articles.authors: Amugongo JS, LaBeaud AD, Bosire C, Bisanzio D, Nzaro M, Ngari M, Vu DM, Ndenga BA, Mutuku FM
articles.details: This cohort and nested case-control study followed febrile children (1-18 years) at Msambweni County Referral Hospital (2014-2018) with 2,371 participants. Malaria prevalence was 41.8% (992/2,371), with 2.5% (60/2,371) experiencing recurrent infections. Malaria risk increased with age (aRR 1.38-1.57 for 2-5 vs <2 years); protective factors included higher maternal education (aRR 0.66), piped/well water (aRR 0.78), and electricity access (aRR 0.61). Recurrent malaria was linked to poor housing, dirt floors, and proximity to rice field breeding sites.

articles.headline: Carboxylesterase Coeae6g drives insecticide cross-resistance in Anopheles gambiae
articles.title_original: Predictive chemoproteomics and functional validation reveal Coeae6g-mediated insecticide cross-resistance in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Balaska S, Grigoraki L, Lycett G, Weetman D, Oladepo F, Colman F, Vontas J, Paine MJI, Ismail HM
articles.details: This study employed competitive activity-based protein profiling with pirimiphos-methyl-oxon to identify serine hydrolases mediating insecticide resistance in Anopheles gambiae. Among 18 active hydrolases identified, the carboxylesterase Coeae6g was selected for functional validation. Functional assays confirmed Coeae6g confers resistance to pirimiphos-methyl and mediates cross-resistance to malathion, bendiocarb, and permethrin. These findings bridge genotype-phenotype gaps and provide a scalable framework to guide insecticide management in malaria vector control.

articles.headline: Placental malaria prevalence and maternal-fetal outcomes: meta-analysis
articles.title_original: Placental malaria: a systematic review and meta-analysis of global burden, risk factors, and maternal and foetal outcomes.
articles.authors: Menon S, D'Alessio F, Chaudhuri N, Onwuchekwa C, Kaur M, Nkumama I, Olesen OF
articles.details: Systematic review of 50 studies (45 from sub-Saharan Africa), 15 using histological diagnosis. Global placental malaria prevalence 17% (95% CI 12–21); 19% in sub-Saharan Africa vs 4% in other regions. Prevalence 45% in HIV-positive women vs 17% in HIV-negative. Stillbirth associated with placental malaria (OR 3.81, 95% CI 1.22–11.94); primigravidae at higher risk. Wide confidence intervals for congenital malaria and fetal outcomes precluded firm conclusions.

articles.headline: Age-structured migration model explains malaria transmission patterns
articles.title_original: Impact of age-structured migration on malaria burden: A modelling-empirical analysis in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Harikripal, Pascal Zabre S, Danquah I, Bhattacharyya S
articles.details: Compartmental model incorporating age-specific demographics and migration. Validated on multiyear reported malaria cases from Ouagadougou and annual migration records from Kossi province, Burkina Faso. Model successfully explained age-cohort incidence patterns. Nonlinear amplification and synchrony in infection prevalence partially driven by age-specific migration. Results support targeted intervention design accounting for behavioral and demographic mobility.

articles.headline: Climate variability drives malaria outcomes in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Climate, Interventions, and Malaria Outcomes in a Warming World: Towards Climate-Smart Malaria Control in Kenya.
articles.authors: Nyawanda BO, Ochomo E, Otieno JD, Keitany K, Machini BK, Vounatsou P
articles.details: Evidence from Kenya shows climate variability effects on malaria outcomes comparable to or exceeding local intervention effects. Transmission displays non-linear, lagged relationships with temperature and rainfall. Insecticide-treated nets and case management reduce incidence and deaths, particularly in young children. Population-level impact depends on timing and location relative to climate-favorable transmission windows. Proposes "climate-smart" malaria control with dynamic intervention targeting based on climate lags and thresholds.

articles.headline: Submicroscopic congenital malaria prevalence in low-endemicity Guatemala
articles.title_original: Prevalence of submicroscopic congenital malaria and associated risk factors in an area of low endemicity of Guatemala.
articles.authors: Franco RG, Ortiz L, Bardají A, Castañeda D, Menendez C, Padilla N, Castellanos ME
articles.details: Prospective cohort study of pregnant women (2009–2011). Random sample of 442 umbilical cord samples analyzed by qPCR showed submicroscopic cord blood malaria prevalence of 12.2% (95% CI 9.3–15.6%). Case-control analysis (54 cases, 51 controls): submicroscopic maternal Plasmodium infection (aOR 7.55, 95% CI 1.84–51.5) and gravidity (aOR 1.49, 95% CI 1.13–2.04) associated with congenital malaria. Prompt diagnosis and treatment of maternal malaria emphasized.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi transmission driven by climate change and deforestation
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi and global changes: socio-ecological drivers and challenges.
articles.authors: Muh F, Qanita NG, Yuwanita MR, Wusono AD, Rahayu AA, Amirudin FA, Firdaus MER, Cahyani NKD, Han JH
articles.details: Review of P. knowlesi epidemiology across Southeast Asia examining climate change, land-use change, and human behavior. Temperature and precipitation variations impact mosquito vectors and parasites; deforestation and land-use alteration facilitate zoonotic transmission from simian hosts to humans. Climate change influences human behaviors and alters mosquito habitats. Understanding human-macaque-mosquito interactions essential for controlling P. knowlesi in regions with intensifying environmental and developmental changes.

articles.headline: IPTp-SP and insecticide-treated nets cost-effective for pregnant women
