articles.details: A community-based cross-sectional study in Haryana, India (n=440 under-five children, 30 ASHAs) found that while ASHAs achieved high fidelity in malaria case management once engaged (treatment initiation 100%, completion 100%, follow-up 87.5%), only 1.8% of mothers initially sought care from ASHAs versus an 80% benchmark (p<0.05). Only 4.0% of children were tested within 24 hours of fever onset. Maternal transmission knowledge was high (92.1%), but 73.6% preferred informal providers.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria in wild chimpanzees causes systemic immune modulation
articles.title_original: Hidden hematological, biochemical and immune costs of asymptomatic malaria infections in semi-wild chimpanzees.
articles.authors: Nowakowski A, Elguero E, Patterson C, Boissière A, Degrugillier F, Sidobre C, Arnathau C, Grentzinger P, Willaume E, Talman AM, Malleret B, Boundenga L, Ngoubangoye B, Prugnolle F, Wassmer SC, Rougeron V
articles.details: In a study of 27 semi-wild chimpanzees from Gabon, overall Plasmodium infection rate was 48.15% with multi-species co-infections including P. gaboni, P. reichenowi, and P. vivax-like. Younger animals had higher infection rates and parasitemia. Infected chimpanzees showed elevated urea, reduced creatinine, and altered cytokines: increased pro-inflammatory markers (TNF, IL-1β, CCL3) and anti-inflammatory IL-10. Ex vivo PBMC stimulation showed higher IL-10 in infected animals, indicating immune modulation.

articles.headline: CSP genetic diversity in Rwanda may influence vaccine performance
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein in Kigali, Rwanda.
articles.authors: Fankem SN, Mbonimpa JB, Kalimba EM, Diallo MT, Souopgui J
articles.details: Study of 245 clinical P. falciparum isolates from Kigali, Rwanda (October 2021–June 2023) identified 48 distinct circumsporozoite protein (CSP) haplotypes with high haplotype diversity (Hd=0.8899) but moderate nucleotide diversity (π=0.00834). Fewer than 1% of sequences matched the 3D7 vaccine strain, and several amino acid positions associated with vaccine escape showed high mutation frequencies, suggesting circulating CSP variants may reduce vaccine performance.

articles.headline: Research priorities identified for seasonal malaria chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Global medium-term research priority setting for seasonal malaria chemoprevention: results from an eDelphi study.
articles.authors: Richardson S, Steele A, Scott S, Ainsworth J, Eckert E, Tchouatieu AM, Sillah JS, Cuomo-Dannenburg G, Okereke E, Okeh DU, Tonks S, Ndiaye JL, Dentinger C, Rassi C, Baker K
articles.details: Online eDelphi consultation (September 2022–September 2023) with 67 experts from 20 countries identified top research priorities for seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC). Highest-ranked priorities included evaluating the duration of prophylactic protection offered by sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine-amodiaquine (SPAQ) and other SMC medicines, and assessing the effect of integrating SMC with other malaria prevention interventions on antimalarial resistance development.

articles.headline: Misconceptions and incomplete IPTp adherence limit malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Assessing the knowledge, attitudes and practices towards malaria prevention and determinants of antenatal care utilization among pregnant women in Sekyere South district, Ghana; a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Zuuri CN, Gyamah DK, Buabeng L, Mensah R
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 422 pregnant women in Sekyere South District, Ghana found 78% had adequate malaria knowledge, yet 56% incorrectly believed malaria spreads person-to-person, and only 48% knew the recommended ≥3 intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp) doses. Insecticide-treated net use was 63%; knowledge of IPTp dosage was significantly associated with uptake (p<0.001), indicating educational gaps rather than supply constraints limit prevention.

articles.headline: P. ovale GAMA protein shows moderate diversity and immunogenicity
articles.title_original: Immunogenicity and gene polymorphism analysis of Plasmodium ovale wallikeri glycosylphosphatidylinositol-anchored micronemal antigen (GAMA).
articles.authors: Ling X, Chen F, Xu S, Tan Y, Sun Y, Han S, Wang Q, Cheng Y
articles.details: Genetic analysis of 38 Plasmodium ovale wallikeri isolates identified 10 single nucleotide polymorphisms in the powgama gene resulting in 13 distinct haplotypes (Hd=0.812) with conserved functional domains (π≈0). Recombinant PowGAMA immunization of mice elicited robust Th1-polarized response with endpoint antibody titers of 1:5,120,000, avidity of 78.37%, and 83.3% sensitivity and 70% specificity in detecting natural infections.

articles.headline: Mean temperature drives malaria transmission across African countries
articles.title_original: Climate change and malaria: an old enemy of Africa is back.
articles.authors: Filho WL, Gbaguidi GJ, Zuñiga RAA, Ibrahim U
articles.details: Analysis of malaria incidence trends using Pearson correlation, joinpoint regression, and linear regression found mean temperature is the main climatic factor affecting P. falciparum transmission in Angola, Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana, Mozambique, and Uganda. Warmer temperatures generally increased malaria risk, except in Ghana where higher temperatures were associated with decreased risk; precipitation showed significant negative association with incidence in Burkina Faso and Uganda.

articles.headline: High malaria and typhoid co-infection in Northern Ghana febrile patients
articles.title_original: Malaria and typhoidal co-infection among febrile patients: a health facility-based cross-sectional study in Northern Ghana.
articles.authors: Dassah SD, Anati MI, Kulariba J, Alidu EA, Senoo DKJ, Adda RB, Agongo G, Motey GA, Amenga-Etego L, Abugri J
articles.details: Health facility cross-sectional study of 332 febrile patients (July–November 2023–2024, Upper East Region, Ghana) found prevalence of malaria 37.7%, typhoid fever 36.7%, and co-infection 14.7%. Risk factors for malaria included Nabdam district residence (adjusted odds ratio=3.87), lacking mosquito nets (AOR=2.3), and presenting with vomiting (AOR=2.45); tap water use was protective against both typhoid fever (AOR=0.36) and co-infection (AOR=0.07).

articles.headline: ASAQ and AL remain highly efficacious in Madagascar
articles.title_original: Efficacy and Safety of Artesunate + Amodiaquine and Artemether + Lumefantrine for the Treatment of Uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum Malaria in Madagascar, 2020.
articles.authors: Harimanana A, Randriamiarinjatovo DNAL, Rakotomanga TA, Irinantenaina J, Moriarty LF, Laird V, Patel D, Abdallah RA, Zhiyong Z, Svigel SS, Kapesa L, Razafindrakoto J, Ralinoro F, Rason MA, Andriamananjara MN, Razanatsiorimalala S, Razafinjato C, Randrianarivelojosia M
articles.details: Randomized trial of 709 children (6 months–14 years) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Madagascar showed polymerase chain reaction-corrected adequate clinical and parasitological response rates of 100% for artesunate-amodiaquine (ASAQ) at all four study sites and 97.6–100% for artemether-lumefantrine (AL). Day 3 slide positivity was 0–1%; adverse events occurred in 10.2% (mild/moderate gastrointestinal issues and headache); no artemisinin-resistance-associated pfK13 mutations were detected in 727 samples analyzed.

articles.headline: Trichuris infection prevalence underestimated; co-infection worsens anemia
articles.title_original: Molecular diagnosis of Trichuris trichiura: Prevalence and associated risk factors in children under five living in a malaria-endemic area in Papua, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Sukmana DJ, Kridaningsih TN, Mufidah H, Kenangalem E, Burdam FH, Susilawati TN, Poespoprodjo JR, Murhandarwati EEH
articles.details: Study of 181 children <5 years in Timika, Papua, Indonesia (April–July 2013) found Trichuris trichiura prevalence was 31.5% by quantitative PCR versus only 13.8% by Kato-Katz microscopy. Children >3 years had 3-fold increased risk (adjusted odds ratio=3.29, p=0.006); hemoglobin ≤10 g/dL increased risk 2-fold (OR=2.46, p=0.02). Co-infection with malaria (9.9% prevalence) increased anemia risk 11.7-fold (OR=11.7, 95% CI: 2.0–67.0, p=0.004).

articles.headline: Lupeol and betulin derivatives show potent in vitro antimalarial activity
articles.title_original: Hemisynthesis of lupeol and betulin derivatives and their in vitro antimalarial activity against Plasmodium falciparum FCR3.
articles.authors: Bafarawa ID, Abd Ghani MS, Ramadani AP, Fuadah S, Othman MBH, Ibrahim MN, Liew SY, Che Omar MT, A Wahab H, Azmi MN
articles.details: In vitro evaluation of synthesized lupeol and betulin derivatives against P. falciparum FCR3 strain identified three compounds with potent inhibitory activity: compound 1 (IC50 0.3±0.3 μM), derivative 4a (IC50 0.03±0.05 μM), and derivative 4b (IC50 0.7±0.8 μM), comparable to or exceeding artemisinin (IC50 0.7±0.3 μM) and chloroquine (IC50 10.3±2.9 μM). Molecular docking showed these compounds bind PfATP6 protein with strong affinity (-9.8 to -8.4 kcal/mol).

articles.headline: Hemozoin crystals move via hydrogen-peroxide-catalyzed propulsion
articles.title_original: Chemical propulsion of hemozoin crystal motion in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Hastings EM, Skóra T, Carney KR, Fu HC, Bidone TC, Sigala PA
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Quantitative imaging and single-particle tracking revealed hemozoin crystal motion in malaria parasites exhibits tight confinement yet is faster than thermal diffusion. Hydrogen peroxide generated at high levels in the food vacuole stimulates crystal motion via surface-catalyzed peroxide decomposition; isolated crystals moved faster in peroxide-containing solutions, and conditions suppressing peroxide formation slowed hemozoin motion inside parasites, suggesting this mechanism reduces oxidative stress from hemoglobin digestion.

articles.headline: Artisanal mining communities show elevated malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Factors Associated With Plasmodium falciparum Infection Transmission in Artisanal Mining and Mining-Free Communities: The Case of Fanteakwa South District, Ghana.
articles.authors: Aninagyei E, Adedia D, Larbi G, Abbew GA, Tukwarlba I, Boatey CA, Sarfo-Bempong B, Acheampong DO
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 341 individuals (240 from artisanal mining, 101 from mining-free communities) in Fanteakwa South District, Ghana found asymptomatic P. falciparum prevalence 53.1% overall, significantly higher in mining communities (64.6%) versus mining-free (27.7%, p<0.001). In mining areas, major risk factors included staying outdoors >2 hours at night (AOR=8.4), net sharing with three people (AOR=4.9), and using nets from open markets (AOR=4.2); stagnant water from mining promotes mosquito breeding.

articles.headline: Severe malaria substantially reduces children's health-related quality of life
articles.title_original: Determining health-related quality of life among children with malaria in Kenya: does the choice of EQ-5D value set matter?
articles.authors: Okech T, Thavorncharoensap M, Chaikledkaew U, Omondi GP, Nyokabi P, Thumbi SM, Thakkinstian A
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 95 Kenyan children (45 with uncomplicated, 50 with severe malaria) from June–September 2024 measured health-related quality of life using proxy-reported EQ-5D-5L questionnaire. For uncomplicated malaria, median utility scores ranged 0.59–0.82 across value sets; for severe malaria, scores were substantially lower (0.09–0.30), with differences across Ethiopian, Ugandan, and Dutch value sets statistically significant. Malaria severity and vaccination status were significant determinants of utility scores.

articles.headline: Larval habitat characterization and vector bionomics inform Kebbi control
articles.title_original: Utilization of vector bionomics, remote sensing and larval habitat characterization data to inform larval source management decision in the high malaria burden state of Kebbi, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Oduola AO, Inyama PU, Samdi LM, Bunza MA, Bagudu A, Okeke I, Yina G, Esema M, Ntadom G, Ambrose K, Mihigo J, Yoshimizu M, Seyoum A
articles.details: Entomological survey across Nigeria with focus on Kebbi State used GIS and satellite imagery to characterize larval habitats. Found An. gambiae s.l. predominant with human biting rates of 42.2 b/p/n indoors and 14.3 outdoors in rainy season, and 83% protection gap. Of 635 identified larval habitats, 97.9% were positive for Anopheles larvae; temporary habitats near houses ranged 18-61% across locations, predominantly rice fields.

articles.headline: Mitochondrial genome sequence correction for Plasmodium pitheci
articles.title_original: Correction: The mitochondrial genome sequence of the Bornean orang-utan (Pongo pygmaeus) malaria parasite, Plasmodium pitheci.
articles.authors: Dharmayanthi AB, Pratama R, Sánchez KL, Nurillah A, Fadiah F, Komara, Nugraha RTP, Saputra S, Nurkanto A, Fahroni A, Irwanto A, Syafruddin D, Adnan IA, Saragih JR, Coutrier FN, Setiadi W, Lobo NF
articles.details: Correction to previously published mitochondrial genome sequence of Plasmodium pitheci, the malaria parasite of Bornean orang-utans.

articles.headline: Cerebral herniation and organ failure as causes of death in pediatric CM
articles.title_original: Cause-specific mortality in a cohort of paediatric cerebral malaria patients.
articles.authors: Wynkoop HJ, Bvalani-Kaunda R, Seydel KB, Moxon CA, Chimalizeni Y, Taylor TE, Birbeck GL, Postels DG, O'Brien NF
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 393 children with cerebral malaria from 2018–2025 found 52 deaths (13%). Cerebral herniation was most common immediate cause (46%), followed by status epilepticus with apnea (17%). Non-neurologic causes accounted for 37%: cardiac failure/shock (13%), respiratory failure (12%), renal failure (6%), hepatic failure (4%). Multiple organ dysfunction was prevalent regardless of cause.

articles.headline: Mining expansion associated with 24% increase in Yanomami malaria
articles.title_original: Mining and malaria in the Brazilian Amazon and in the Yanomami indigenous land.
articles.authors: Castro MC, Arisco NJ, Diniz CG, Ponmattam J, Peterka C, Basta PC, Ferreira MU
articles.details: Study quantified impact of illegal mining on malaria in Yanomami indigenous population. A 1% increase in annual mining area was associated with 24% (95% CrI: 17%–32%) increase in monthly malaria cases. Estimated 102,870 excess malaria cases from 2018–2023 attributable to mining expansion; 2022 cases were likely underreported by 83%.

articles.headline: RDT sensitivity for P. vivax (83%) lower than P. falciparum (95%)
articles.title_original: A cross-sectional survey of Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax in India using rapid diagnostic test and microscopy across 12 sites of varying transmission, 2023-2024.
articles.authors: Dahal P, Singh-Phulgenda S, Nema S, Tripathi PK, Malla WA, Sinha DP, Mohanty A, Chalageri VH, Shrinivasa BM, Singh SP, Singh PK, Singh K, Ranjha R, Baharia RK, Chhajed R, Nain M, Das A, Kumar RS, Gupta A, Rahi M, Flegg JA, Nitika N, Dhorda M, Anvikar AR, Guérin PJ, Bharti PK
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 10,290 febrile participants across 12 Indian sites from September 2023 to April 2024. Found 14.7% malaria positive by RDT (1,516 cases): 72.9% P. falciparum mono-infection, 19.1% P. vivax mono-infection, 8.0% mixed infections. RDT sensitivity was 95.0% for P. falciparum but only 83% for P. vivax; 0.4% of RDT-negative participants were microscopy-positive.

articles.headline: Raman spectroscopy analysis of parasitemia in malaria-infected RBCs
articles.title_original: Two-dimensional correlation spectroscopy (2D-COS) in the analysis of parasitemia in Raman spectra of red blood cells of patients diagnosed with malaria.
articles.authors: Migdalski M, Czepiel J, Moskal P, Birczyńska-Zych M, Kucharska M, Biesiada G, Stokłosa J, Bociąga-Jasik M, Wesełucha-Birczyńska A
articles.details: Study used two-dimensional correlation spectroscopy (2D-COS) analysis of Raman spectra from P. falciparum-infected red blood cells at varying parasitemia levels. Resonance Raman at 442 nm wavelength identified heme intermediates; normal Raman at 785 nm revealed protein and lipid changes. Relative intensity ratio of oxy- to deoxy-heme (I1370/I1354) showed potential for correlating with parasitemia levels.

articles.headline: Integrated vector management shows sustained effectiveness across regions
articles.title_original: Integrated vector management for malaria control: a review of approaches and effectiveness.
articles.authors: Otolorin GR, Castellanos ME, Adegboye OA, McBryde ES
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Systematic review of 14 studies (2009–2024) evaluating integrated vector management impact on malaria control. Integrated strategies consistently outperformed single interventions, with six studies documenting statistically significant transmission reductions (p<0.05). Longitudinal studies in Uganda, Ethiopia, and Nigeria demonstrated sustained declines in malaria cases and vector populations; large-scale programs in China and India confirmed long-term effectiveness.

articles.headline: Zoonotic malaria emerging threat in India
articles.title_original: Tackling the Emerging Threat of Zoonotic Malaria in India.
articles.authors: Divyaparvathy J, Ramesh H
articles.details: No abstract available. Title indicates focus on emerging threat of zoonotic malaria in India.

articles.headline: Anti-CelTOS antibodies block liver-stage and transmission across species
articles.title_original: [Cross-specific anti-CelTOS antibodies for different Plasmodium species inhibiting the liver stage and transmission to the vector].
articles.authors: Varloteau A, Fouquoire L, Ravier L
articles.details: No abstract available. Title indicates research on cross-species anti-CelTOS antibodies that inhibit parasite liver stage and transmission to mosquito vectors.

articles.headline: P. vivax cysteine protease inhibitor shows broad activity
articles.title_original: Partial characterization of a cysteine protease inhibitor of Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Võ TC, Kang JM, Lê HG, Na BK
articles.details: Study identified and characterized endogenous inhibitor of P. vivax cysteine protease (PvICP). Recombinant PvICP shares structural characteristics with orthologous proteins from other Plasmodium species and shows broad inhibitory activity against falcipain family proteases including vivapain-3, vivapain-4, falcipain-3, malapain-2, and malapain-4, with strongest activity against vivapain-3 and vivapain-4. Activity was pH-independent.

articles.headline: Three malaria vectors project expansion, affecting approximately 200 million people
articles.title_original: Climate Change Favors African Malaria Vector Mosquitoes.
articles.authors: van der Deure T, Nogués-Bravo D, Njotto LL, Stensgaard AS
articles.details: Using species distribution modeling of six African Anopheles vectors and climate/land-use data, study projected future range shifts under climate change. Three species (An. gambiae, An. coluzzii, An. nili s.l.) projected to expand suitable habitat substantially. Approximately 200 million additional people estimated to live in highly suitable vector habitat by end of century, with emerging hotspots in Central and East Africa.

articles.headline: Vector diversity impact on malaria prevalence varies regionally
articles.title_original: Vector diversity and malaria prevalence: global trends and local determinants.
articles.authors: Hoi AG, Gilbert B, Mideo N
articles.details: Global analysis of vector diversity (over 70 mosquito species transmit malaria) and malaria prevalence found latitude-dependent relationships. In Africa, environmental factors shaped vector communities, exerting top-down influence on disease prevalence. In Southeast Asia, more complex interactions between rainfall, GDP, and vector diversity determined malaria prevalence. Results highlight vector community composition as key structural factor in disease distribution.

articles.headline: HSP90 inhibitors show divergent resistance patterns in malaria
articles.title_original: Shared Binding Site but Divergent Resistance Profiles Uncover Novel Resistance Mechanisms in Plasmodium HSP90 Inhibitors.
articles.authors: Ko FH, Lukens AK, de Souza ML, Foy A, Hsiao J, Ndiaye T, Okombo J, Yeo T, Park H, Uhlemann AC, Boonyalai N, Kumpornsin K, Girling G, Pasaje C, Godoy L, Ottilie S, Durst GL, Chatterjee A, Niles J, Lee M, Fidock DA, Wirth DF, Winzeler EA
articles.details: Two HSP90 inhibitors targeting the same ATP-binding site in P. falciparum exhibited dramatically different resistance profiles in laboratory cultures. Geldanamycin readily selected 10 distinct resistance mutations conferring up to 22-fold resistance, while AUY-922 required 44 weeks to yield a single A41S mutation with 2-fold resistance. Resistance mutations mapped to different locations; notably, the A41S mutation enhanced AUY-922 binding affinity yet paradoxically correlated with resistance, suggesting binding site identity alone cannot predict resistance risk.

articles.headline: Optimized protocols enhance parasite RNA in low-volume transcriptomics
articles.title_original: Low-volume Plasmodium blood sample processing protocols for untargeted transcriptomics optimized using Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Sauve E, Kattenberg JH, Moris P, Guetens P, Monsieurs P, Rosanas-Urgell A
articles.details: Researchers optimized sample-processing protocols for untargeted transcriptomics of low-density Plasmodium species using P. knowlesi diluted in whole blood as a mock P. vivax infection. White blood cell depletion increased mapped Plasmodium paired-end reads from 5.4% to 37.2–54.5%, while combined globin and rRNA depletion decreased human gene counts by up to 90%. Using mRNA library preparation kits with WBC and globin depletion yielded optimal parasite RNA enrichment, enabling transcriptomics research in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Historical analysis of early malaria control in Palestine
articles.title_original: Why and when successful rural malaria control became a local problem - Palestine 1922.
articles.authors: Alexander A
articles.details: Historical examination of how Zionist settlers in Palestine over 100 years ago addressed obstacles to launching the first sustainable rural malaria control program, contextualizing past challenges with contemporary events.

articles.headline: New malaria treatment shows promise against drug resistance
articles.title_original: First new type of malaria treatment in decades shows promise against drug resistance.
articles.authors: Lenharo M
articles.details: A newly developed type of malaria treatment demonstrates promise in combating drug-resistant parasites. Specific details unavailable without abstract.

articles.headline: AI model successfully identifies Plasmodium parasites in blood smears
articles.title_original: A Case of Plasmodium Identification in Peripheral Blood Smears based on the DeepSeek Intelligent Algorithm.
articles.authors: Zheng Z, Lu M
articles.details: A case report of imported malaria demonstrates the DeepSeek AI model's application for analyzing Plasmodium parasites in peripheral blood smears. DeepSeek successfully identified Plasmodium species and their developmental stages, reducing misdiagnosis and missed diagnosis risks compared to traditional microscopy. The AI analysis completed in minutes, while traditional microscopic examination required several hours or longer, supporting an "AI primary screening + manual review" workflow for grassroots malaria prevention.

articles.headline: Life-cycle stage differences in P. falciparum evolutionary constraint
articles.title_original: Heterogeneous constraint and adaptation across the malaria parasite life cycle.
articles.authors: Perkins SA, Neafsey DE, Early AM
articles.details: Analysis of P. falciparum gene expression across life stages revealed that genes exclusively expressed in sporozoites show signals of weaker negative selection compared to other stages. These differences likely result from variations in cell ploidy, clonal competition, and transmission bottlenecks across the parasite's multi-stage life cycle. The findings suggest that timing of therapeutic interventions targeting specific life stages may impact the rate at which parasite populations evolve resistance.

articles.headline: Avian Plasmodium relictum shows non-specific vector response strategy
articles.title_original: Plastic responses of Plasmodium relictum to competent and non-competent mosquito bites.
articles.authors: Chauvin V, Berthomieu A, Loiseau C, N Tchana C, Angelier F, Rivero A
articles.details: Experimental infections of birds with avian malaria parasite P. relictum exposed to bites from competent (Culex quinquefasciatus) and non-competent (Anopheles gambiae) vectors both induced parasitemia rises, with significantly stronger response to non-competent species. No species-specific modulation of gametocyte conversion or sex ratio occurred, and host inflammatory markers remained similar across treatments. These findings suggest P. relictum employs a generalist, bet-hedging strategy rather than discriminating between mosquito species, with implications for how vector community composition shifts alter transmission dynamics.

articles.headline: Profile of malaria researcher Fitsum Tadesse
articles.title_original: Fitsum Tadesse: shaping malaria research in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Prasad A
articles.details: Biographical article on contributions of a malaria researcher to research advancement in sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Anopheles aquasalis phenotype influences Plasmodium vivax competence
articles.title_original: Phenotypic variation and vector competence in Anopheles aquasalis: A comparative analysis of susceptibility to Plasmodium vivax.
articles.authors: Rodrigues NB, Santana RA, Carvalho BFDS, Martinez EG, Bruce AD, Justiniano SCB, da Silva ÁC, Nacif-Pimenta R, Monteiro WM, de Melo GC, Secundino NFC, Pimenta PFP
articles.details: Laboratory study of two phenotypic variants of An. aquasalis (30BS and 50BS) infected with blood from P. vivax patients in Manaus using membrane feeding assays. Examination of 286 mosquito midguts (147 from 30BS, 139 from 50BS) found similar prevalence between phenotypes (55.10% for 30BS vs 67.63% for 50BS; IRR = 1.11; 95% CI: 0.67–1.84; p = 0.68). However, infection intensity was significantly higher in 30BS mosquitoes with mean oocyst counts of 72.91 compared to 56.94 in 50BS (p = 0.042), suggesting 30BS phenotype is more permissive to parasite development.

articles.headline: Severe malaria outcomes in Italian hospital network
articles.title_original: The Italian network for severe malaria treatment (NOMAL): epidemiology, clinical presentation, management and outcomes in a non-endemic setting.
articles.authors: Bartoli TA, Corpolongo A, D'Abramo A, Angheben A, Bertoli G, de Gaetano Donati K, Cauda R, Antinori S, Attard L, Madeddu G, Tomasoni LR, Pallotto C, Vulcano A, Maffongelli G, Zammarchi L, d'Ettorre G, Navarra A, Girardi E, D'Alessandro U, Nicastri E
articles.details: Epidemiology, clinical presentation, management, and outcomes of severe malaria cases treated in the Italian NOMAL hospital network in a non-endemic setting. Specific clinical findings unavailable without abstract.

articles.headline: Chemical adjuvants boost sporozoite vaccine immunogenicity
articles.title_original: A Trojan horse: Chemical boosting of Plasmodium falciparum whole sporozoite vaccine immunogenicity.
articles.authors: Naar CM, van Schuijlenburg R, van Meerbeek M, Almeida L, van Leeuwen FWB, Roestenberg M
articles.details: Two positively charged dye-adjuvant complexes (Cy5-CL307 and Cy5-MDP) chemically augmented whole sporozoites for malaria vaccine development. Adjuvanting successfully labeled approximately 90% of sporozoites while maintaining viability (84% and 71%), motility (78.7% and 83.6%), and infectivity. SPZCL307 significantly increased immune activation markers in macrophages (CD80, CD25, IL-6, IL-8, TNF, IL-1β, IL-10; p = 0.018–0.043) and showed 18% increased CD137 expression in memory CD8+ T cells (p = 0.01), with trend toward 1.6-fold increased perforin (p = 0.064).

articles.headline: CDC coordinates emergency antimalarial drug distribution network
articles.title_original: A Review of Malaria, Diphtheria, and Botulism Medication Distributions by CDC Port Health Stations From May 2006 to February 2024.
articles.authors: Rubin L, Fowler J, Ranga S, Kirley M, Tchan J, Pacheco M, Preston LE, Waltenburg MA, Akinkugbe O, Kinzer MH, Alvarado-Ramy F, Brown C, Gertz AM
articles.details: The CDC port health station network distributed 2,912 drug shipments (May 2006–February 2024), including intravenous artesunate for severe malaria until 2022. Among 850 shipments with available timing data (December 2019 or later), 83% (702/850) were delivered within 8 hours of authorization. Commercial air transport significantly improved delivery compared to other modes (aOR: 11.43; 95% CI: 6.94–18.81; P < 0.001), while release authorization outside routine work hours was associated with delays (aOR: 5.10; 95% CI: 3.31–7.86; P < 0.001).

articles.headline: P. falciparum prevalence highest in young adults despite control, Senegal
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum carriage in a population under long-term, intensive malaria control in Kedougou region, Senegal: a 1-year cohort study.
articles.authors: Legendre E, Ciré Ba EK, L'Ollivier C, Cissoko M, Katile A, Mehadji M, Serre P, Sokhna C, Ranque S, Danfakha F, Bendiane MK, Sagara I, Gaudart J, Landier J
articles.details: Cohort of 763 from 69 households in four villages was followed for one year with four surveys across seasons. P. falciparum prevalence was below 10% in children <10 years but reached 32.1% in ages 15-24 years and 24.7% in ages 35-49 years during wet season. Clinical burden peaked in ages 10-14 (527 cases/1000 person-years) and 15-19 (631 cases/1000 person-years), over five-fold higher than young children. Authors conclude intensive control protected SMC-eligible children but whole-community interventions are needed to address the parasite reservoir in young adults.

articles.headline: Malaria burden in a region under long-term chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Malaria burden in a region under long-term chemoprevention.
articles.authors: Sow D, Wotodjo AN
articles.details: No abstract provided; cannot extract study design, population, intervention details, or results.

articles.headline: DHA-PPQ maintains efficacy for P. falciparum over decade in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine for treating Plasmodium falciparum malaria from sub-Saharan Africa: a retrospective study.
articles.authors: Ciminelli F, Buonfrate D, Mazzi C, Neumayr A, Camprubí-Ferrer D, Gobbi F
articles.details: Retrospective study of 89 patients (treated 2013-2024) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria from sub-Saharan Africa treated with DHA-PPQ. Baseline median parasitemia was 0.136%. On day 3, 6.7% retained circulating parasites without early treatment failure, with no significant trend in day-3 positivity across the study periods (p=0.339). Authors conclude DHA-PPQ effectiveness has remained stable, though continued surveillance for resistance is needed.

articles.headline: Pericytes mediate cerebral malaria vascular dysfunction via angiopoietin pathway
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum impairs Ang-1 secretion by pericytes in a 3D brain microvessel model.
articles.authors: Long RKM, Korbmacher F, Ronchi P, Fleckenstein H, Schorb M, Mirza W, Mallorquí M, Aguilar R, Moncunill G, Schwab Y, Bernabeu M
articles.details: Using a 3D microfluidics brain microvessel model with human brain endothelial cells and pericytes, P. falciparum-infected RBC egress products reduced Ang-1 secretion and increased vascular permeability with minor pericyte morphological changes. P. falciparum-mediated barrier disruption was partially reversed by pre-treatment with recombinant Ang-1 and Tie-2 activator AKB-9778. Authors conclude pericytes play a novel role in cerebral malaria pathogenesis and targeting the angiopoietin-Tie axis offers therapeutic potential.

articles.headline: Malaria and microgravity as models for age-related erythropoietic dysfunction
articles.title_original: Exploring erythropoiesis: aging through the lens of malaria and microgravity.
articles.authors: Foucambert P, Hang JW, Malleret B
articles.details: This review examines erythropoiesis changes during aging, including alterations in stem cells, transcription factors, erythropoietin, and iron metabolism. It discusses mechanisms underlying high anemia prevalence in elderly from chronic disease, sepsis, and unexplained anemia. The authors propose malaria and microgravity as underexplored models for studying aging RBCs, noting that both Plasmodium infection and spaceflight mirror age-related erythropoietic dysfunction and RBC morphological changes.

articles.headline: Nanoparticles enhance blood-stage malaria drugs and vaccines in preclinical models
articles.title_original: Nanotechnology-based delivery strategies for drugs and vaccines targeting blood stage malaria: A systematic review.
articles.authors: Amos Y, Rubaka C, Sauli E, Swai H, Tumbo A
articles.details: Systematic review of 40 studies (2005–2025) on nanotechnology-based delivery systems (lipid, polymeric, inorganic, and protein-based nanoparticles) for blood-stage malaria treatment and prevention. Functionalized nanocarriers targeting infected RBCs enhanced drug efficacy while reducing systemic toxicity. In vaccine development, nanoparticle antigen carriers elicited strong immune responses, achieving up to 83.3% survival in in vivo preclinical models. Authors highlight nanomedicine's transformative potential despite challenges in scalable production and clinical translation.

articles.headline: Vector species and feeding behavior sustain malaria in urban Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Influence of the dry and humid seasons on the assessment of human exposure to malaria vector bites in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Amara MF, Kouadio KWU, Konaté H, Yao RK, Kouamé BNA, Karabinta A, Diabaté A, Gnankiné O, Namountougou M
articles.details: Entomological study in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso (2023–2024) collected 1455 mosquitoes via HLC and PSC. An. arabiensis (91.20%), An. coluzzii (5.09%), and An. gambiae (3.71%) were identified, with P. falciparum detected year-round and dual P. falciparum/P. malariae infections in 0.16% of mosquitoes in July. Blood meal analysis showed mixed feeding (59.63%), zoophilic (24.41%), and anthropophilic (15.96%) behaviors. Authors conclude persistent transmission results from vector ecological flexibility and high trophic plasticity.

articles.headline: New Plasmodium species identified in Brazilian wood rails with lung pathology
articles.title_original: Hidden in plain sight: Morphological and molecular description of Plasmodium aramidis n. sp. in blood and tissues from Brazilian Wood Rails.
articles.authors: Vieira LMC, Santos DOD, Pacheco MA, Escalante AA, Oliveira AR, Braga ÉM
articles.details: This study describes a new avian Plasmodium species (P. aramidis n. sp.) identified in five Grey-necked Wood Rails from Southeastern Brazil using morphological and molecular methods including cytb sequencing and near-complete mitochondrial genome analysis. P. aramidis caused pathogenic effects including pulmonary edema and hemorrhage, hepatic hemosiderosis in all birds, and exoerythrocytic meronts in lung/heart/skeletal muscle tissues in two of three examined birds. Authors conclude P. aramidis appears common in A. cajaneus but further studies are needed.

articles.headline: Vector species distribution and biting rates peak at specific microclimate conditions
articles.title_original: Ecological diversity of Anopheline mosquitoes in highland agroecosystems in relation to microclimate dynamics: implications for malaria control.
articles.authors: Arisanti M, Yahya Y, Suryaningtyas NH, Sukendra DM, Maha MS, Gunawan G, Isnawati R, Ernawan B, Nurcahyo W, Kaliannagounder K, Pangaribuan HU
articles.details: Entomological study in South Sumatra, Indonesia rice-growing region (April–September) collected 6899 mosquitoes (Anopheles spp. 15.6%, Culex spp. 56.6%), with An. vagus the most abundant Anopheles species. Response surface models showed human biting rate peaked at ~23.5°C and >88% relative humidity (R²=0.656) while animal biting rate peaked at ~22°C and >97% relative humidity. Adults revealed strong exophagic and exophilic behaviors. Authors conclude species-specific climatic sensitivities underscore importance of integrating ecological factors into vector surveillance.

articles.headline: Atovaquone-proguanil fully arrests sporozoites but provides limited vaccine protection
articles.title_original: Complete attenuation of Plasmodium falciparum sporozoites by atovaquone-proguanil.
articles.authors: Borrmann S, Sulyok Z, Müller K, Fendel R, Sulyok M, Friesen J, Lalremruata A, Sandri TL, Nguyen TT, Calle CL, Knoblich A, Sefried S, Ibáñez J, Lorenz FR, Heimann HL, Weller DM, Steuder R, Adukpo S, Granados Bayon P, Molnár Z, Esen M, Metzger W, James ER, Ruben A, Abebe Y, Chakravarty S, Manoj A, Kc N, Murshedkar T, Hafalla JCR, Woldearegai TG, O'Rourke F, Held J, Billingsley P, Sim BKL, Richie TL, Hoffman SL, Kremsner PG, Matuschewski K, Mordmüller B
articles.details: Clinical trial of PfSPZ-CVac with atovaquone-proguanil (AP): three doses of 5.12 × 10⁴ or 1.5 × 10⁵ sporozoites prevented blood-stage infections during immunization. Upon challenge with 3.2 × 10³ sporozoites 10 weeks later, only 2/8 (25%) and 2/10 (20%) of vaccinees were protected, inferior to PfSPZ-CVac with chloroquine. Protection was associated with antibodies to liver-stage antigens LISP2, LSA1, and multi-stage MSP5 but not major surface protein CSP. Authors conclude AP's complete sporozoite arrest could reduce chemoprophylaxis dosing frequency.

articles.headline: RTS,S and R21 vaccines improve malaria protection but gaps remain in durability
articles.title_original: Better late than never: defining the ideal vaccine that targets pre-erythrocytic malaria infection.
articles.authors: Rochfort Peters GM, Baum J
articles.details: This review discusses licensure of two subunit vaccines against P. falciparum: RTS,S/AS01 (GSK) and R21/Matrix-M (Serum Institute), both targeting sporozoites via circumsporozoite protein (CSP). Both require four-dose primary series over 12–18 months to achieve maximum protection. While these vaccines represent a landmark, their level and durability of protection—especially in infants and high-transmission areas—remain uncertain. Authors note that uncertainty has spurred further vaccine development efforts to complement or improve overall vaccine efficacy.

articles.headline: Delayed treatment and indigenous status drive malaria mortality in Amazon Brazil
articles.title_original: Towards Malaria Elimination: A Nationwide Case-Control Study to Assess Risk Factors for Severe Malaria-Related Deaths in Brazil.
articles.authors: Garcia KKS, Laporta GZ, Duarte EC, Soremekun S, Abrahão AA, da Silva AC, de Pina Costa A, Guimarães Lacerda MV, Drakeley C, Ramalho WM, Siqueira AM
articles.details: Case-control study using Brazil's national health databases (2011–2020) identified 632 malaria-related deaths (71.8% in Amazon, 28.1% outside). In Amazon, risk factors included age <6 months or >60 years, delayed treatment (>48 h), illiteracy, and indigenous village residence, while active case detection was protective. In extra-Amazon regions, risk factors were delayed treatment, P. falciparum/mixed infections, and foreign acquisition. Authors conclude deaths concentrate in socially vulnerable populations and timely diagnosis, enhanced surveillance, and coordinated action are essential to achieve Brazil's zero-malaria-death target by 2030.

articles.headline: CSP repeat elasticity essential for sporozoite motility
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium CSP repeats have elastic properties with a critical role in sporozoite motility.
articles.authors: Balaban AE, Kanatani S, Mitra J, Gregory J, Vartak N, Sinnis-Bourozikas A, Frischknecht F, Ha T, Sinnis P
articles.details: Plasmodium sporozoite mutants with truncated or scrambled CSP repeats exhibit impaired motility due to altered adhesion site formation. Using single-molecule fluorescence-force spectroscopy, the central repeat region of CSP was found to behave like a stiff linear spring with elastic properties dependent on its length; these properties are lost when repeats are scrambled. This demonstrates a functional role for the CSP repeat region in Plasmodium infection and sporozoite motility.

articles.headline: IPT reduces malaria in HIV-positive pregnant women
articles.title_original: Intermittent preventive treatment regimens for malaria in HIV-positive pregnant women: Systematic review and meta-analysis of clinical trials.
articles.authors: Elgadi A, Noorallah T, Abdelazim E, Altraifi S, Altraifi H, Elhaj A, Elnaiem W, Hemmeda L
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 13 randomized clinical trials involving 5,226 HIV-positive pregnant women assessed intermittent preventive treatment (IPT) regimens for malaria. IPT showed 55% lower odds of malaria during pregnancy or at delivery (OR=0.45, 95% CI: 0.36–0.56, p≤0.001), with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine being most effective, and reduced placental malaria by 48% (OR=0.52, 95% CI: 0.39–0.70, p≤0.001). IPT reduced maternal anemia by 18% (OR=0.82, 95% CI: 0.69–0.97, p=0.03) but showed no significant effect on adverse birth outcomes.

articles.headline: Imaging flow cytometry visualizes parasitized erythroblasts in malaria
articles.title_original: Quantification and visualization of malaria-infected erythroblasts by imaging flow cytometry.
articles.authors: Imai T, Miyazaki S, Miyazaki Y, Kagaya W, Nakashima M, Ito K, Sase M, Yoshioka-Takeda M, Shimokawa C, Hayashi K, Araki T, Annoura T, Itokawa K, Komagata O, Ha NT, Shimo A, Murakami T, Hisaeda H
articles.details: Researchers employed imaging flow cytometry (ImageStream) to identify and characterize parasitized erythroblasts (pEBs) in mice infected with Plasmodium yoelii 17XNL. At 14 days post-infection, splenic, bone marrow, and peripheral blood cells stained with antibodies against erythroid and nucleated cell markers (TER119, MHC class I) and DNA stain (Hoechst). ImageStream enabled detection of parasitized erythroblasts in spleen and bone marrow but not peripheral blood, with quantification closely matching conventional flow cytometry results.

articles.headline: Deep learning advances AI-driven P. knowlesi blood-stage diagnosis
articles.title_original: Insights into AI-Driven malaria diagnosis: A systematic review with implications for Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Tiong JY, Hasikin K, Ngui R, Divis PCS, Loo CK, Lai KW, Cheong FW, Wan Sulaiman WY
articles.details: Systematic review evaluates artificial intelligence approaches for automated Plasmodium blood-stage identification, with specific focus on Plasmodium knowlesi. Review examines datasets, preprocessing methods, and deep learning techniques including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), transfer learning, ensemble learning, and object detection models (YOLO, Faster R-CNN). Key challenges identified include limited annotated datasets, class imbalance, and model interpretability. Authors emphasize enhanced dataset curation, domain adaptation strategies, and explainable AI are essential for advancing AI-driven P. knowlesi diagnostics.

articles.headline: Twenty-year spatial analysis reveals malaria hotspots shifting in Kenya
articles.title_original: A multidimensional space-time geospatial analysis for examining the spatial trends of vector-borne diseases: 20 years of malaria in Kenya.
articles.authors: Blanford JI, Kioko C
articles.details: Multidimensional space-time analysis examined 20 years of Plasmodium falciparum incidence, mortality, and prevalence data from Kenya (2000–2020) using local hotspot analysis (LHA) and emerging hotspot analysis (EHA) at 10-year intervals. General decline in malaria prevalence was observed with a slight increase between 2016–2018; study identified four key malaria zones plus two additional areas with increasing burden. Bed net and antimalarial use increased over time. Authors conclude space-time methods are useful for identifying high-burden areas and refining malaria elimination strategies.

articles.headline: P. vivax hemoglobin degradation enzymes show genetic conservation
articles.title_original: Characterization of genes involved in hemoglobin degradation in Plasmodium vivax isolates from Chennai, India, and species of non-human primate malaria.
articles.authors: Shalini S, Mishra N, Verma S, Kale S, Mallick PK, Prajapati SK, Kumar A, Srivastava N, Joshi H, Eapen A, Singh OP
articles.details: Study investigated molecular and structural characteristics of three food vacuole enzymes in Plasmodium vivax—plasmepsin IV (PM_IV), heme detoxification protein (HDP), and falcilysin (FLN)—by analyzing genomic DNA from 30 clinical isolates and three chloroquine-resistant reference strains. Several nonsynonymous mutations were detected including c.493G>A (Val165Ile) in PM_IV, c.1537A>G (Asn513Asp) and c.2027G>A (Gly676Asp) in FLN, and a novel in-frame duplication c.28_33dup in HDP. Structural modeling revealed these mutations did not affect active binding sites. Authors conclude genes are highly conserved and represent potential drug targets.

articles.headline: Malaria eradication in Europe: imported cases and climate risks
articles.title_original: Historical evolution of the epidemiology of Malaria in Spain and Europe. Future perspectives.
articles.authors: Marañés Antoñanzas I, López Gallardo Y, García de Oro Á, Gutiérrez González C, Peyró Sánchez R, Sánchez Martínez S
articles.journal: Eur J Clin Microbiol Infect Dis
articles.details: Historical review describes malaria eradication in Spain (1964) and Europe (1970s), with subsequent reintroduction by international travelers and immigrants from endemic regions. Current imported cases occur with extremely infrequent autochthonous transmission due to efficient healthcare systems. Review speculates climatic warming could favor anopheline mosquito proliferation and transmission in southern Europe, and continuing refugee/migrant influx could establish parasite reservoirs. Authors conclude maintaining strong public health surveillance and vector control are essential to prevent malaria re-emergence.

articles.headline: NLRP1 inflammasome variant protects against gestational malaria
articles.title_original: Impact of NLRP1 Met1154Val and IL1B variants on gestational malaria: an unexplored role of NLRP1 in inflammasome activation by Plasmodium spp.
articles.authors: Leal VN, Ribeiro JA, Marra LG, Rio AT, Reis EC, Wunderlich G, Dombrowski JG, Marinho CR, Pontillo A
articles.details: Association study in a cohort of pregnant women from a malaria-endemic region in Brazil examined whether functional single nucleotide variants (SNVs) in inflammasome genes affect Plasmodium infection response and placental malaria development. NLRP1 p.Met1154Val variant was associated with protective effect against Plasmodium infection and correlated with healthier placental state, while IL1B SNVs appeared more prevalent in severe cases. For the first time, researchers demonstrated infected red blood cells induce NLRP1- and caspase-1-dependent pyroptosis in trophoblast cells, identifying a novel inflammasome pathway in gestational malaria.

articles.headline: RDTs variably detect simian Plasmodium species by parasite density
articles.title_original: Can malaria rapid diagnostic tests be used to detect simian malaria?
articles.authors: Madmanee W, Day NPJ, White NJ, Dondorp AM, Malaivijitnond S, Imwong M
articles.details: Evaluation of 14 commercially available rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) on 131 blood samples from non-human primates with confirmed malaria (Plasmodium cynomolgi: geometric mean 73.2 parasites/μL; P. inui: 272.8 parasites/μL; P. coatneyi: 158.4 parasites/μL) and 30 control samples from malaria-free macaques. Overall RDT sensitivity ranged 34.2–94.3% across brands, exceeding 90% for samples with parasitemia >200 parasites/μL. False positives were absent in controls but present in 1–17% of infected samples across RDT brands. Authors conclude RDT performance varies by brand, Plasmodium species, and parasite density, with highest sensitivity for P. inui.

articles.headline: High Wolbachia prevalence in An. dthali enables biocontrol potential
articles.title_original: Wolbachia Infection in Iranian Malaria Vectors: Prevalence and Biocontrol Implications.
articles.authors: Saeedi S, Karimian F, Moosa-Kazemi SH, Nejati J, Bavani MM, Koosha M, Choubdar N, Khosravi G, Oshaghi MA
articles.details: Study examined Wolbachia infection in Iranian malaria vectors collected from nine provinces during 2016–2019, analyzing 1,094 specimens from seven species including Anopheles stephensi, A. culicifacies, A. fluviatilis, A. maculipennis, A. sacharovi, A. dthali, and A. superpictus. Wolbachia DNA was detected exclusively in An. dthali (73.4% infection) and Culex species (77.78%–96.77%), with higher prevalence in males. Phylogenetic analysis revealed supergroup B strains closely related to An. moucheti and An. demeilloni. Authors conclude high An. dthali prevalence is promising for malaria prevention through biocontrol.

articles.headline: C1GALT1-dependent sialic acids mediate P. falciparum RBC invasion
articles.title_original: Sialyl-T Antigen: A Novel Red Blood Cell Determinant for Plasmodium falciparum Invasion.
articles.authors: Mikdar M, Shabani E, Grüring C, Chaand M, Kanjee U, Goldberg JM, Azouzi S, Tennessen JA, Elsworth B, Keutcha C, Barteneva NS, Doench JG, Duraisingh MT
articles.details: Combined RNA interference screening and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout identified Core 1 Synthase Glycoprotein-N-Acetylgalactosamine 3-Beta-Galactosyltransferase 1 (C1GALT1) as a novel host determinant for Plasmodium falciparum invasion. Analyses of C1GALT1-deficient reticulocytes and red blood cells with glycophorin A/B-null MkMk phenotype showed C1GALT1-dependent α(2-3) sialic acid structures in mucin-type O-glycans are crucial for efficient invasion of both sialic acid-dependent and sialic acid-independent P. falciparum strains but not Plasmodium knowlesi. Different P. falciparum strains exhibit variable dependency on distinct sialic acid configurations on red blood cell surfaces.

articles.headline: NOX2 oxidative stress causes cardiac hypercontractility in malaria
articles.title_original: NOX2-induced oxidative stress triggers cardiac hypercontractility during severe malaria.
articles.authors: de Oliveira KCM, Silva AO, Bruno AS, Ito HN, Queiroz-Junior CM, Berg BB, Castor MGME, Machado FS, Ceron CS, Teixeira MM, Cau S
articles.details: Time-course study examined heart function and local redox balance in male Balb/c mice infected with Plasmodium chabaudi, investigating NOX2's role using apocynin treatment (30 mg/kg/day) and NOX2 knockout mice. Isolated hearts from infected mice showed higher contraction indexes at baseline and under isoprenaline stimulation throughout disease course, associated with increased lipid peroxidation (TBARS) and reduced superoxide dismutase (SOD) activity. Apocynin treatment and NOX2 deletion reversed malaria-induced cardiac hypercontractility without affecting parasitemia, and apocynin mitigated cardiac oxidative stress and reduced upregulation of pro-inflammatory cytokines IL-1β and IL-6.

articles.headline: Monocyte dysregulation in symptomatic and asymptomatic P. vivax
articles.title_original: Systems approach identifies monocyte imbalance in symptomatic and asymptomatic P. vivax malaria.
articles.authors: Studniberg SI, Bafit M, Ioannidis LJ, Worley MJ, Trianty L, Utami RAS, Puspitasari AM, Apriyanti D, Coutrier FN, Poespoprodjo JR, Kenangalem E, Andries B, Prayoga P, Price RN, Noviyanti R, Garnham AL, Hansen DS
articles.details: Systems approach integrating transcriptional profiling and mass cytometry in symptomatic versus asymptomatic P. vivax malaria patients. Symptomatic disease featured upregulation of anti-inflammatory pathways and checkpoint receptors with profound downregulation of monocyte function transcripts and significant CCR2+CXCR4+ classical monocyte depletion. Asymptomatic infections also exhibited monocyte dysregulation including impaired inflammasome-related genes, identifying monocyte dysfunction as a key feature of P. vivax infection.

articles.headline: WHO reference reagent enables ELISA standardization for malaria antibodies
articles.title_original: Evaluation of 1(st) WHO anti-malaria reference reagent for competition ELISA harmonisation and development of ADAMSEL analytical platform.
articles.authors: Khatri B, Riese P, Shkarlet H, Mortier D, McShane H, Bowyer PW, Remarque E
articles.details: Multi-laboratory study (three sites) harmonizing competition ELISA (cELISA) for P. falciparum AMA-1 antibody responses using WHO reference serum 10/198. Developed ADAMSEL software and freely available R-statistics platform tool to standardize IgG concentration calculation and minimize analytical errors. Demonstrated effectiveness of WHO reference reagent as standard for cELISA, with automated analytical tools accessible to resource-limited laboratories.

articles.headline: Phenolic compounds inhibit PfATP6 and P. falciparum growth
articles.title_original: Small phenolic inhibitors of PfATP6, a Plasmodium falciparum calcium ATPase, as prototype antimalarials.
articles.authors: Sobota S, Devaraja S, Chung M, Arevalo J, Martinez-Aguirre J, Makeyenko J, Kellen-Yuen C, Eastman S, Derbyshire E, Saade C, Dieudonné T, Paula S
articles.details: Characterized small phenolic compounds with hydroquinone and naphthoquinone scaffolds as inhibitors of PfATP6 calcium ATPase in P. falciparum blood stage assays. Several compounds inhibited parasite growth including di-alkylated hydroquinones, naphthoquinone disulfide, and hexachlorophene. Molecular interactions analyzed via homology modeling, ligand docking, and molecular dynamics simulations, providing first steps toward designing improved small-molecule PfATP6 inhibitors.

articles.headline: Transposon screen reveals P. falciparum genes for sickle-cell survival
articles.title_original: Phenotypic screens reveal Plasmodium falciparum genetic factors associated with infection of sickle-trait cells.
articles.authors: Pires CV, Oberstaller J, Wang C, Gibbons J, Micchelli C, Zhang M, Otto TD, Rayner JC, Taylor S, Adams JH
articles.details: Phenotypic screen using piggyBac transposon-mutant library of P. falciparum identified genetic factors essential for parasite survival in sickle-trait (HbAS) RBCs, which reduce severe malaria risk by over 90%. Parasites cultured in vitro in HbAS and control RBCs with sensitivity assessed via Quantitative Insertion Site Sequencing (QIseq). Identified mutants sensitive to HbAS involved in signaling pathways, exported proteins, and host-interaction genes overlapping with oxidative-stress and artemisinin-response pathways.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 polymorphisms and primaquine levels in P. vivax relapses
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms of the CYP2D6 gene and its relationship with Plasmodium vivax relapses after chloroquine-primaquine treatment in Turbo, Colombia.
articles.authors: Sierra-Cifuentes V, Zuluaga-Idárraga L, Aguirre-Acevedo D, Silva de Barros Puça MC, Nobrega de Sousa T, Lopera-Mesa TM
articles.details: Analysis of CYP2D6 polymorphisms in 71 P. vivax patients treated with chloroquine-primaquine in Colombia. Most patients (81.7%) were normal or ultrarapid metabolizers; 18.3% were poor/intermediate metabolizers. Impaired CYP2D6 activity associated with higher day-2 primaquine plasma concentrations (660.4 vs 313.5 ng/ml; effect size r=-0.51, 95% CI -0.82 to 0.03), but no significant difference in relapse hazard ratio between groups (adjusted HR 1.45; 95% CI 0.39–5.39).

articles.headline: Plasmodium and Babesia show distinct metabolic fingerprints in RBCs
articles.title_original: Shared host, distinct invaders: metabolomic footprints of plasmodium and babesia in host red cells.
articles.authors: Beri D, Rodriguez M, Lobo CA
articles.details: Review comparing metabolomic profiles of Plasmodium and Babesia-infected RBCs. Both parasites exhibited enhanced glycolytic rate, lipid scavenging from RBC membranes, and perturbations in sugars, amino acids, lipids, redox homeostasis, and heme utilization. Key difference: distinct hemoglobin breakdown and amino-acid utilization patterns. Review suggests metabolic profiling may identify new biomarkers and targeted therapies for parasitic infections.

articles.headline: Deep learning with uncertainty guidance for parasite detection
articles.title_original: Uncertainty-guided attention learning for malaria parasite detection in thick blood smears.
articles.authors: Xiong H, Wang Z, Sharan RV, Berkovsky S
articles.details: Developed uncertainty-guided attention learning network with Bayesian channel attention mechanism for automated malaria parasite detection from thick blood smears. Method incorporates pixel-wise attention to identify fine-grained informative features and estimates channel-wise uncertainty to restrict unreliable features, reducing noise and background artifacts. Demonstrated superior performance on two public datasets with highest average precision (AP) scores in both parasite-level and patient-level evaluations.

articles.headline: AI applications and challenges in African malaria control programs
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence in African malaria control programs: opportunities and risks.
articles.authors: Obeagu EI, Ogenyi FC
articles.details: Narrative review synthesizing AI applications in African malaria control programs, including case studies: Kenya's drone-assisted vector surveillance, Nigeria's mobile health platforms for real-time case reporting, and Tanzania's climate-informed forecasting models. Reviews AI-driven bioinformatics for Plasmodium genomic surveillance, parasite life-cycle modeling, and vector-parasite molecular network analyses. Critically examines challenges including data quality, ethics, infrastructure limitations, and potential inequities.

articles.headline: Transdermal microarray patches deliver amodiaquine and artesunate effectively
articles.title_original: Polymeric microarray patches for transdermal delivery of amodiaquine and artesunate: A novel strategy against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Anjani QK, Volpe-Zanuto F, Miatmoko A, Moreno-Castellanos N, Novais JT, Gaitán XA, Hariawan BS, Cahyani DM, Octavia RT, Mubarok AS, Wahyu Setyaningsih WA, Johnston C, Bargieri DY, Donnelly RF
articles.details: Developed dissolving microarray patches (MAPs) for transdermal delivery of amodiaquine and artesunate as artemisinin-based combination therapy. Pharmacokinetic analysis in mice showed artesunate half-life extended 8.5-fold (54.05 vs 6.37 hours) with 21% increased Cmax (1796.02±154.50 vs 1421.93±209.61 ng/mL); amodiaquine half-life prolonged 1.4-fold (57.72±19.16 vs 40.75±7.44 hours). In P. berghei-infected mice, MAP treatment reduced parasitemia by 99.5% within seven days with efficacy comparable to oral administration.

articles.headline: Math model predicts vaccine-plus-control strategy impacts on transmission
articles.title_original: An age-structured mathematical model to assess the combined effects of vaccine and non-vaccine interventions on malaria transmission and burden.
articles.authors: Glèlè Kakaï R, Djomatin LC
articles.details: Age-structured mathematical model assessed combined effects of vaccination (RTS,S and R21) and non-vaccine interventions (long-lasting insecticide-treated nets, treatment access) on malaria transmission dynamics and disease burden. Model incorporated demographic parameters, vaccine efficacy profiles, and waning immunity. Calibration used national-level data from Ethiopia (low transmission setting) and Nigeria (high transmission setting). Preliminary findings suggest vaccination integration with existing control strategies could reduce incidence and mortality over time.

articles.headline: PSSP17 essential for transmission but absent natural deletions
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Plasmodium falciparum PSSP17 essentiality in human-to-mosquito transmission and its prospective candidacy as a biomarker.
articles.authors: López-Gutiérrez B, Hathaway N, Alonso-Palomares L, Nyasembe VO, Kato M, Hamerly T, Parr JB, Juliano JJ, Harbers M, Dinglasan RR
articles.details: Study demonstrated that Plasmodium sexual stage protein 17 (PSSP17/PF3D7_1218800) is dispensable in P. falciparum asexual stages but important for onward transmission by mosquitoes. Analysis of 19,280 publicly available P. falciparum genomes revealed no naturally occurring pssp17 deletions. Findings suggest PSSP17 as candidate biomarker for non-invasive saliva-based rapid diagnostic tests, with assurance that natural immune evasion through gene deletion is unlikely.

articles.headline: Karius test detects P. vivax when blood smear fails
articles.title_original: Travel-Related Malaria Diagnosis on Karius Test Despite Negative Blood Smear.
articles.authors: Weigold JE, Lal S, Youssef DA
articles.details: Case report of P. vivax malaria in a 59-year-old returning from the Amazon rainforest. Despite negative blood smear microscopy and presentation with fever, rash, myalgia, and laboratory abnormalities (leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, transaminitis, hyperbilirubinemia), Karius plasma-based cell-free DNA sequencing successfully detected Plasmodium vivax. Patient required multiple treatment regimens (chloroquine-primaquine, artemether-lumefantrine, quinine-doxycycline with prolonged primaquine) for symptom resolution.

articles.headline: Malaria and COVID-19 seroprevalence in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of anti-SARS-CoV-2 and anti-Plasmodium falciparum and vivax antibodies in urban and rural zones in the West Region of Cameroon.
articles.authors: Kamga RMN, Ajim NC, Soubgwi PF, Noyes H, MacLeod A, Simo G, Adamson WE
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 805 participants detected anti-Plasmodium antibodies in 22.2% overall (31.4% rural, 14.8% urban), with 21% P. falciparum and 2.11% P. vivax. SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence was 60.1% overall. No association was found between SARS-CoV-2 and anti-Plasmodium antibody presence.

articles.headline: Ivermectin proves effective malaria vector control tool
articles.title_original: Contact exposure to ivermectin induces acute mortality and inhibits parasite development in malaria vectors.
articles.authors: Jeena M, Goyal B, Lata S, Kumar G, Pradhan LK, Thakur Y, Kaur G, Verma V, Pandey KC, Pasi S, Kaur J
articles.details: Laboratory study of An. culicifacies and An. stephensi evaluated ivermectin contact toxicity. Topical LD50 values: An. stephensi 0.017 ng/mg, An. culicifacies 0.002 ng/mg; both species showed 100% mortality within 24-48 hours at higher doses. Contact exposure reduced P. berghei oocyst development by 71% at 0.01 µM concentration.

articles.headline: Gut dysbiosis predicts post-discharge death in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Gut bacterial dysbiosis in pediatric severe malaria associates with post-discharge mortality.
articles.authors: Bednarski OJ, Lehman SB, Mzinza D, Kazinga C, Namazzi R, Opoka RO, Ren J, Tran TM, Taylor TE, Seydel KB, John CC, Conroy AL, Schmidt NW
articles.details: Analysis of stool samples from children with severe malaria in two African studies identified dysbiosis with increased Enterobacteriaceae abundance. E. coli abundance predicted post-discharge mortality. Elevated metabolic pathways supporting utilization of host-derived molecules suggest mechanisms promoting Enterobacteriaceae survival in severe malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: MALDI-TOF MS reduces vector surveillance costs 83%
articles.title_original: MALDI-TOF MS for malaria vector surveillance: A cost-comparison analysis using a decision-tree approach.
articles.authors: Karisa J, Rist C, Tuwei M, Ominde K, Bartilol B, Ondieki Z, Musani H, Wanjiku C, Mwangangi J, Mbogo C, Rono M, Bejon P, Maia M
articles.details: Decision tree model compared MALDI-TOF MS to molecular methods for malaria vector identification using 15,000 annual mosquitoes. MALDI-TOF MS achieved 74.48% net time saving, 84% material cost reduction, and 77% labor cost reduction, providing 83% overall direct cost savings for African national programs.

articles.headline: P. vivax relapse reduced under declining transmission
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax Malaria Relapse Risk Depends on Transmission Intensity: Evidence From a Longitudinal Study in Northwest Thailand.
articles.authors: Chu CS, Lwin KM, Burgoine K, Phyo AP, Turner C, San T, Aung AA, Win HH, Htoo K, Soe NL, Christina N, Than HH, Dah N, Pilaseng K, Wiladpaingern J, Proux S, Mukaka M, Parker DM, Carrara VI, Nosten F, White NJ
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 380 adults and children with previous P. vivax received supervised primaquine for 14 days at 0.5 mg base/kg/day. Annual P. vivax incidence decreased from 0.19 to 0.09 infections per person-year. Primaquine failure rate was 75% lower than predicted, suggesting declining transmission reduces hypnozoite burden.

articles.headline: NUAK1 kinase critical for P. falciparum erythrocyte invasion
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum exploits NUAK1 to establish infection in human erythrocytes.
articles.authors: Navarrete DJ, Kim CY, McLelland K, Ramirez MG, Baro B, Salinas ND, Tolia NH, Doerig C, Ong SE, Golkowski MG, Egan ES
articles.details: Study identified NUAK1 (AMPK-related kinase) as host factor for P. falciparum in erythrocytes. Selective NUAK1 inhibitors HTH-01-015 and WZ4003 inhibited parasite growth throughout asexual life cycle and blocked invasion. NUAK1 overexpression partially rescued invasion phenotypes, establishing this signaling pathway as therapeutic target.

articles.headline: PfEMP1 antibodies protect against cerebral malaria infection
articles.title_original: Breaking the bind: PfEMP1-specific antibodies in cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Banda JP, Walker IS, Nyirenda T, Aitken EH, Rogerson SJ
articles.details: Review synthesized evidence that antibodies to EPCR-binding CIDR and dual EPCR/ICAM1-binding DBL domains of PfEMP1 correlate with reduced cerebral malaria risk. Identified key research priorities: epitope mapping across variant repertoires, longitudinal tracking of antibody maturation and post-translational modifications, and development of broadly inhibitory monoclonal antibodies.

articles.headline: Deep learning accurately detects P. falciparum blood parasites
articles.title_original: Rapid and accurate recognition of erythrocytic stage parasites of Plasmodium falciparum via a deep learning-based YOLOv3 platform.
articles.authors: He W, Zhu H, Geng J, Zhu D, Wu K, Xie L, Li J, Yang H
articles.details: YOLOv3 deep learning algorithm analyzed 262 thin blood film images to detect P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes. Achieved 94.41% recognition accuracy with 1.68% false negative rate and 3.91% false positive rate, supporting AI-assisted diagnosis feasibility in resource-limited malaria control settings.

articles.headline: Vector species contributions to malaria transmission, Benin
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal distribution of four malaria vector species and their relative contributions to Plasmodium falciparum transmission along the south-north transect of Benin, West Africa.
articles.authors: Akpodji S, Agbangla C, Padonou GG, Affolabi ZK, Koukpo ZC, Adoha C, Hougbe SZ, Sominahouin A, Tokponnon F, Osse RA, Oussou O, Adjottin B, Odjo EM, Yovogan B, Azondékon R, Salako A, Akogbéto M
articles.details: Entomological surveillance across 18 communes identified differential vector roles: An. coluzzii responsible for 63.01% transmission (EIR 87.7 infecting bites per person/year), An. gambiae 36.7% (EIR 51.1), An. funestus 0.24%. An. gambiae carried higher sporozoite loads (~30% with >10,000 sporozoites) than An. coluzzii, demonstrating transmission contribution depends on both biting and parasite load.

articles.headline: Coustani Group mosquitoes minimally transmit malaria, DRC
articles.title_original: Coustani Group mosquito (Diptera: Culicidae) diversity and malaria transmission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.authors: Hendershot AL, Wat'senga FT, Mampangulu T, Lodi O, Nsungu IT, Manzambi EZ, Okito A, Nzila P, Aubourg MA, Yu X, Likwela J, Fornadel C, Oxborough RM, Lobo NF, Irish SR
articles.details: Molecular and morphological analysis of Coustani Group mosquitoes in DRC identified multiple species groups with different biting times. Low sporozoite detection indicated these mosquitoes contribute minimally to local malaria transmission, though multiple cryptic species complicate definitive assessment of their transmission role.

articles.headline: Thailand's malaria 1-3-7 elimination strategy implementation
articles.title_original: Evaluating Thailand's malaria reactive surveillance and response strategies for malaria elimination: a mixed-method study.
articles.authors: Oo WH, Roobsoong W, Aye Tun N, Khant KM, Htike W, Kengganpanich M, Agius PA, Sattabongkot J, Fowkes FJI
articles.details: Mixed-method evaluation of Thailand's 1-3-7 reactive surveillance and response strategy in three provinces found 54.1% achieved case notification within one day and 98.7% completed case investigation within required timeframes, except in high-burden border areas. Barriers included limited resources, low community participation, and difficult terrain.

articles.headline: Prophylaxis does not select resistant malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Impact of Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole and Chloroquine Prophylaxis on Drug-Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Mutations in Adults with HIV in Malawi.
articles.authors: Fairhurst KJ, Mungwira RG, Divala TH, Nyirenda OM, Masonga R, Joshi S, Fern N, van Oosterhout JJ, Laufer MK, Laurens MB
articles.details: Randomized trial comparing chloroquine and trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (TS) prophylaxis in adults with HIV found no significant differences in resistance mutation prevalence. Parasites from prophylaxis groups showed no increased prevalence of pfdhfr, pfdhps, or pfcrt polymorphisms compared to controls, and no chloroquine resistance mutations detected in breakthrough infections.

articles.headline: STING-IRF3 signaling suppresses protective IL-35+ Breg responses in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Distinct STING-IRF3 activation determines IL-35(+) Breg differentiation in rodent malaria.
articles.authors: Feng A, Li Q, Jiang N, Lv K, Yang Y, Liu T, Su Z, Zhang Y, Sang X, Feng Y, Chen R, Chen Q
articles.details: Study compared C57BL/6 mice infected with P. berghei ANKA (which induces cerebral malaria) versus P. yoelii YM (non-cerebral). IL-35-positive regulatory B cells expanded in P. yoelii-infected mice but not in P. berghei-infected mice. STING-mediated IRF3 phosphorylation suppressed IL-35+ Breg differentiation, potentially contributing to experimental cerebral malaria pathology.

articles.headline: First imported babesiosis case in Honduras mimicked P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: First documented case of imported human babesiosis in Honduras: Diagnostic challenges with malaria falciparum.
articles.authors: Chaver L, Flores H, Padilla W, Rodríguez J, Zúniga C, Medina F, Matamoros G, Araujo ME, Ortiz B, Fontecha G
articles.details: 72-year-old man returning from Massachusetts developed fever, neurological symptoms, and ischemic stroke. Initial thick blood smears suggested P. falciparum; treated with chloroquine-primaquine followed by artemether-lumefantrine, but hyperparasitemia persisted and patient developed multiorgan failure, dying day 33. PCR confirmed Babesia 18S rRNA; retrospective smear review identified Maltese cross morphology.

articles.headline: mRNA vaccine rescues CD8+ T cell responses impaired by hemozoin
articles.title_original: mRNA vaccination overcomes hemozoin-mediated impairment of whole parasite vaccine efficacy for malaria.
articles.authors: Hassert M, Drewry LL, Pewe LL, Hancox LS, He R, Arumugam S, Mix MR, Salem AK, Harty JT
articles.details: In a murine Plasmodium infection model, prior blood-stage exposure compromised radiation-attenuated sporozoite (RAS) vaccine-induced CD8+ T cell responses through hemozoin-mediated impairment of dendritic cell antigen uptake. A lipid-nanoparticle mRNA vaccine encoding Plasmodium CD8+ T cell epitopes restored T cell activation and protection in previously-exposed mice; combined RAS+mRNA vaccination enhanced liver-resident memory T cells and protection in malaria-experienced hosts.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi in macaques predicts human transmission risk
articles.title_original: Multi-pronged surveillance to understand the spatiotemporal correlations among macaques, vectors and humans in Plasmodium knowlesi malaria transmission.
articles.authors: Phang WK, Jeyaprakasam NK, Pramasivan S, Suli ZB, Tam JZ, Hamid MHBA, Abdullah MLB, Rohimly AABI, Ashrat NB, Chuang TW, Nguitragool W, Vythilingam I, Lau YL
articles.details: Integrated surveillance in Peninsular Malaysia (2019-2022) found statistically significant positive correlation between proportion of P. knowlesi-positive humans and Anopheles Leucosphyrus-group mosquitoes within 10 km proximity (rs=0.228, P=0.042). Proportion of P. knowlesi-positive macaques correlated with historical human cases within 6+ km proximity. Macaque infection may serve as useful proxy for persistent transmission and human infection risk.

articles.headline: Mologic LDH RDT achieves 91.9% sensitivity for P. falciparum detection
articles.title_original: Clinical evaluation of a novel Mologic malaria Plasmodium falciparum LDH-dependent lateral flow rapid diagnostic test diagnostic accuracy: a cross-sectional study, Sudan.
articles.authors: Mohamed AO, Hamid MMA, Jebreel WMA, Elaagip A, Mohammed FO, Albsheer MM, Mustafa SA, Ahmed TME, Ahmed SBA, Chamai M, Dittrich S
articles.details: Cross-sectional evaluation in Sudan of 500 patients found Mologic LDH-dependent RDT achieved 91.9% sensitivity (95% CI 87.3-95.2%) and near-100% specificity compared to PCR reference (210/500 PCR-positive). Performance was concordant with expert microscopy (95.24% sensitivity) and comparator HRP2-based RDT (93.81% sensitivity, κ=0.929). Test detected 6 P. falciparum HRP2 Ag-negative samples.

articles.headline: 60% of African children experience >24-hour malaria treatment delays
articles.title_original: Estimating rates of treatment delay for malaria fevers among children in Sub-Saharan Africa 2006-2022.
articles.authors: Lubinda J, Rumisha SF, Dzianach P, McPhail M, Saddler A, Browne A, Sanna F, Gelaw Y, Castle P, Kiss JB, Harris J, Rozier JA, Vargas C, Amratia P, Symons TL, Cameron E, Gething PW, Weiss DJ
articles.details: Analysis of 177 nationally representative surveys (2006-2022) found 60% [95% UI 45.7-72.8] of treated children experienced >24h treatment delays, affecting 33 million; 29% [95% UI 18.9-41.2] faced >48h delays, affecting 16 million. Somalia had highest delay rate (76%), Tanzania lowest (35.3%). Overall improvements stagnated post-2015; poorer and rural populations faced higher rates.

articles.headline: nanosd gene drive achieves 99.5% transmission in male A. gambiae
articles.title_original: The nanosd integral gene drive enables population modification of the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Yen PS, Verkuijl SANR, Capriotti P, Del Corsano G, Yee Kim Guan C, Hoermann A, Inghilterra MG, Aramburu-Gonzalez I, Khan MA, Vlachou D, Christophides GK, Windbichler N
articles.details: The nanosd integral gene drive, targeting the nanos gene in A. gambiae, achieved 98.4% transmission in females and 99.5% in males. Homozygous females showed impaired fecundity with variable ovary underdevelopment due to decreased nanos transcript levels. The minimal genetic modification did not induce widespread transcriptomic perturbations or alter vector competence; susceptibility to Plasmodium infection remained similar to wild-type.

articles.headline: Machine learning accelerates malaria vaccine antigen discovery and efficacy prediction
articles.title_original: From data to immunity: the role of machine learning in advancing malaria vaccine research: a scoping review.
articles.authors: Khanday S, Sayeed M, Jafri NF, Jafri IF, Jafri RF, Ashraf G, Safwat S, Nasr DS
articles.details: Scoping review of 2017-2025 literature identified five key areas of ML application in malaria vaccine development: antigen discovery and prioritization; immune signature identification and efficacy prediction; computational tool development; broad reviews of AI/ML applications; and epidemiological modeling. Most studies conducted in Europe and North America with African collaborations. Key challenges include data quality, model interpretability, and validation.

articles.headline: PbGAC protein mediates P. berghei merozoite invasion via heparan sulfate
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium berghei merozoite protein PbGAC is critically involved in erythrocyte binding during invasion.
articles.authors: Gao J, Jiang N, Zhang Y, Chen R, Feng Y, Sang X, Chen Q
articles.details: P. berghei merozoite protein PbGAC, localized at the extreme apical region, binds heparin and erythrocyte surface heparan sulfate receptors during merozoite invasion. Mass spectrometric analysis identified interactions with secreted proteins involved in invasion. Mice immunized with recombinant PbGAC protein or passively immunized with sera from vaccinated mice demonstrated enhanced immunity against lethal challenge.

articles.headline: Correction to P. vivax liver organoid infection study
articles.title_original: Correction: Human liver organoids are susceptible to Plasmodium vivax infection.
articles.authors: Nitaramorn N, Kobpornchai P, Tongkrajang N, Chaisri U, Imwong M, Kulkeaw K
articles.details: Published correction regarding prior research on Plasmodium vivax infection in human liver organoids.

articles.headline: SNP barcode reveals low genetic differentiation among P. falciparum from Central/West Africa
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and population structures of Plasmodium falciparum parasites imported to China from Central and West Africa determined using a SNP barcode.
articles.authors: Zeng W, Zhu W, Qin Y, Lan H, Cen Y, Sun K, Wu Y, Liang T, Mou Y, Liu C, Tang X, Xiang Z, Huang Y, Cui L, Yang Z
articles.details: Analysis of 181 P. falciparum samples imported to China from Central and West Africa using 24-SNP HRM barcode revealed low nucleotide diversity, with pairwise FST values of 0.001-0.054 indicating low-to-moderate genetic differentiation between populations. Principal component analysis and population structure assessment showed lack of prominent clustering, indicating insufficient differentiation for isolate distinction.

articles.headline: African malaria vectors show greater olfactory gene diversity than Asian vectors
