Meta-analysis shows SMC effective in reducing incidence of uncomplicated malaria, severe malaria, and mortality in children under five compared with controls. Strategy has been used for ten years, but epidemiological context is changing with persistence of dry-season transmission and increasing cases among older children.

articles.headline: Seaweed alkaloids and flavonoids inhibit P. falciparum growth
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial activity of Gracilaria sp. ethanol extract against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Setyaningrum E, Septiani L, Wibowo H, Arifiyanto A, Suryaningkunti JA
articles.details: Study of Gracilaria seaweed ethanol extract's in vitro antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum strain D37. Phytochemical analysis identified saponins, tannins, flavonoids, and alkaloids in the extract. Alkaloids, flavonoids, and derivative chemicals inhibited parasite growth with IC50 of 43.38 μg/mL, suggesting potential as an affordable antimalarial alternative.

articles.headline: Biophysical mechanisms of P. falciparum cytoadhesion revealed
articles.title_original: Contact Dynamics of Cytoadhering Plasmodium falciparum-Infected Erythrocytes in Flow.
articles.authors: Scholz K, Papagrigorakes M, Lettermann L, Pennarola F, Patra P, Dasanna AK, Sanchez CP, Kehrer J, Cavalcanti-Adam EA, Schwarz US, Tanaka M, Lanzer M
articles.details: Study using DNA-based molecular force sensors and high-speed reflection interference contrast microscopy investigated adhesion dynamics of P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes on surfaces with ICAM-1, CD36, or both. Trophozoite-stage parasites showed complex motion behaviors (flipping, sliding, pinning) with patchy adhesion footprints, while schizont-stage parasites displayed more uniform rolling motion. Some adhesion footprints extended distances comparable to endothelial cell length.

articles.headline: High seroprevalence of transmission-blocking antibodies in Tanzanian adults
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum transmission-blocking target proteins Pfs230D1M and Pfs48/45 in Tanzanian populations of diverse malaria transmission intensity.
articles.authors: Mulamba C, Kalinga WF, Mtaka I, Lazaro LO, Kamage J, Nkumama I, Odufuwa OG, Kreppel K, Mekhaiel D, Miura K, Long CA, Olotu AI, Williams C
articles.details: Serosurvey of 290 Tanzanian participants (114 children 5-12 years, 44 adolescents 13-17 years, 132 adults 18-45 years) assessing IgG antibodies to transmission-blocking antigens Pfs230D1M and Pfs48/45. Seroprevalence: 56% (157/281) Pfs230D1M, 49% (141/290) Pfs48/45; approximately 30% seropositive for both. Seroprevalence increased significantly with age (Pfs230D1M: aOR 3.16, 95% CI 1.81-5.53, p≤0.0001; Pfs48/45: OR 3.06, 95% CI 1.79-5.25, p≤0.0001). Two of ten plasma samples tested showed transmission-reducing activity.

articles.headline: Malaria drug libraries yield SARS-CoV-2 protease inhibitors
articles.title_original: Screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture Open Boxes Identifies Potent SARS-CoV‑2 Papain-like Protease (PL(pro)) Inhibitors.
articles.authors: Gawriljuk VO, Noske GD, Fernandes RS, Nakamura AM, Freire MCLC, Godoy MO, Bonatto V, Chelucci R, Andricopulo A, Bakowski MA, Wolff KC, Riva L, Burrows JN, Wells TNC, Laleu B, Martins R, Souza JP, Arruda E, Samby K, Laskar S, Guido RVC, Oliva G, Godoy AS
articles.details: Target-based screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture open-access compound libraries against SARS-CoV-2 main and papain-like proteases identified MMV1634397 as promising papain-like protease inhibitor (IC50 = 0.7 μM). Optimization yielded analogs with improved activity (IC50 values: 0.16, 0.34, 0.06 μM). Most potent compounds exhibited antiviral activity in ACE2-HeLa cells (EC50 = 2.9 and 3.7 μM) with favorable pharmacokinetic properties.

articles.headline: Malaria caused mass US military casualties during Vietnam War
articles.title_original: US Malaria Casualties and Chemoprophylaxis during the Vietnam War.
articles.authors: Shanks GD
articles.details: Historical review of malaria in US military during Vietnam War, when it resulted in as many hospitalizations as combat wounds in 1965 with rates exceeding 10 per 1,000 man-days of exposure. Chloroquine-primaquine chemoprophylaxis proved inadequate; adding daily dapsone was more effective but caused rare agranulocytosis and at least 8 deaths. Post-war, relapsing P. vivax caused thousands of US infections; severe malaria resulted in 78 Army and 46 Marine deaths, with over 200,000 lost man-days per year.

articles.headline: Helminth infection rare in Ugandan children with severe malaria
articles.title_original: Low prevalence of helminth infection in Ugandan children hospitalized with severe malaria.
articles.authors: Capone D, Jahan N, Namazzi R, Opoka RO, John CC
articles.details: Analysis of stool samples from 177 Ugandan children hospitalized with severe malaria (ages 6-48 months, 2014-2017) and 25 community controls, testing for six helminth species by quantitative PCR. Helminth infection prevalence was 5.1% (9/177) in severe malaria children vs 4.0% (1/25) in controls. Helminth infection did not modify risk of severe malaria (aRR = 1.0, 95% CI 0.82-1.3, p = 0.78), consistent with Uganda's successful national deworming program.

articles.headline: High asymptomatic malaria prevalence with diverse Plasmodium species in Rwanda
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic non-falciparum and falciparum malaria in the 2014-15 Rwanda Demographic Health Survey.
articles.authors: Gaither C, Morgan C, Kirby R, Karema C, Gashema P, Zuromski J, White SJ, Topazian HM, Giesbrecht D, Thwai K, Young NW, Goel V, Boyter K, Munyaneza T, Muvunyi CM, Butera JD, Bailey JA, Mazarati JB, Juliano JJ
articles.details: Molecular surveillance using quantitative PCR for four malaria species in 5,050 primarily adult individuals from 2014-2015 Rwanda Demographic Health Survey. Overall infection prevalence 22.3% (95% CI 20.3-24.3): P. falciparum 16.3% (95% CI 14.5-18.1), non-falciparum 8.0% (95% CI 6.6-9.3). Asymptomatic infections with P. ovale, P. malariae, and P. falciparum widely distributed; P. vivax rare. Mixed species infections common with low parasitemias. Continued molecular monitoring recommended to inform malaria control.

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structure reveals transmission-blocking target in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 and Pfs48/45 fertilization complex.
articles.authors: Dietrich MH, Chmielewski J, Chan LJ, Tan LL, Adair A, Lyons FMT, Gabriela M, Lopaticki S, Dite TA, Dagley LF, Pazzagli L, Gupta P, Kamil M, Vaughan AM, Rojrung R, Abraham A, Mazhari R, Longley RJ, Zeglinski K, Gouil Q, Mueller I, Fabb SA, Shandre-Mugan R, Pouton CW, Glukhova A, Shakeel S, Tham WH
articles.details: Cryo-electron microscopy structure of endogenous Pfs230-Pfs48/45 fertilization complex revealed Pfs48/45 interaction with Pfs230 domains 13-14. Transgenic parasites lacking these domains showed reduced oocyst formation and gamete localization defects. Nanobodies against domains 13-14 inhibited complex formation and reduced transmission. These Pfs230 domains were targets of naturally acquired immunity and mRNA lipid nanoparticle immunizations blocked parasite transmission.

articles.headline: Imported malaria cases in New York: surveillance patterns 2018–2024
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in New York: Geographic Patterns and Implications for Emergency Physicians.
articles.authors: Webb Z
articles.details: Surveillance study of imported malaria across New York State from 2018 to 2024. NYC accounted for 79.1% of cases; 11 of 57 counties outside NYC contributed 88.3% of state cases. Cases declined in 2020 coinciding with COVID-19 travel restrictions, then rebounded in 2021. Findings highlight need for travel history assessment and targeted prevention strategies in identified hotspots.

articles.headline: Walnut leaf extract reduces oxidative stress in murine cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Effect of walnut leaves on oxidative stress caused by murine cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Abdel-Gaber R, Alharbi A, Almohawis N, Al Quraishy S, Al-Shaebi E
articles.details: In 30 C57BL/6 mice infected with Plasmodium berghei, daily treatment with Juglans regia leaf extract (JRLE) reduced oxidative stress markers (nitric oxide, malondialdehyde, hydrogen peroxide) in brain tissue compared to infected controls. JRLE increased antioxidant enzyme activities (catalase, superoxide dismutase, glutathione, total antioxidant capacity). The extract demonstrates potential as an antioxidant and antimalarial agent targeting cerebral malaria mechanisms.

articles.headline: Plasmodium telomere maintenance: review of novel antimalarial strategy
articles.title_original: Plasmodium telomere maintenance: uncovering the Achilles' heel for novel antimalarials.
articles.authors: Wakai TN, Anzaku DO, Afolabi IS
articles.details: Review examining telomere maintenance disruption as potential antimalarial approach. Plasmodium maintains continuous telomerase activity and uses unique telomere-binding proteins distinct from humans. Evidence suggests G-quadruplex DNA structures at telomeres may be drug targets. Inhibiting PfTERT blocks telomere elongation and impairs replication. Authors suggest combining telomere-directed therapies with existing antimalarials to overcome resistance.

articles.headline: SARS-CoV-2 and Plasmodium falciparum co-infection in Equatorial Guinea
articles.title_original: Circulation of SARS-CoV-2 and Co-Infection with Plasmodium falciparum in Equatorial Guinea.
articles.authors: López-Farfán D, Ncogo P, Oki C, Riloha M, Ondo V, Cano-Jiménez P, Martínez-Martínez FJ, Molina-de la Fuente I, Comas I, Irigoyen N, Berzosa P, Benito Llanes A, Gómez-Díaz E
articles.details: Antigen testing and microscopy in 1556 volunteers from six health centers (June–October 2021) found 3.03% SARS-CoV-2 prevalence, 22.25% malaria prevalence, and 6 co-infections (0.37%). Genome sequencing of 43 SARS-CoV-2 isolates identified mostly Delta variant introduced from Europe on multiple occasions. Study provides first prevalence estimates of SARS-CoV-2 in this malaria-endemic country and identifies circulating variants.

articles.headline: Plant secondary metabolites: antimalarial compounds and extraction mechanisms
articles.title_original: Plant-derived secondary metabolites for malaria treatment: extraction, mechanisms, and therapeutic potential.
articles.authors: Wassie AT, Ahmed IN, Bachheti RK, Tadesse MG, Wotango AS, Bachheti A, Gonfa YH, Assen AH
articles.details: Review of 34 medicinal plants and their antimalarial secondary metabolites including alkaloids, flavonoids, terpenoids. Potent compounds identified: ceramicine R (IC50 2.80 μM), knipholone clooxanthrone (IC50 14.58 μM for D6, 9.42 μM for W2), joziknipholone A/B (IC50 0.17/0.26 μM), and cajachalcone (IC50 7.4 μM) against Plasmodium falciparum. Review discusses extraction techniques and mechanisms of action as templates for novel drug development against drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: Correction: Ecological drivers malaria vector habitat, Côte d'Ivoire
articles.title_original: Correction: Ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission over 1 year of long-lasting insecticidal net intervention in Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.authors: Talbot B, Alou LPA, Koffi AA, Sih C, Dangbenon E, Zoh MG, Camara S, Assi SB, N'Guessan R, Messenger LA, Protopopoff N, Cook J, Kulkarni MA
articles.details: Correction notice to published study. Original study examined ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission during one-year long-lasting insecticidal net intervention in Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: Metagenomic complexity of asymptomatic Plasmodium infections in Ghana
articles.title_original: Metagenomic complexity of high, seasonal transmission of Plasmodium spp. in asymptomatic carriers in Northern Sahelian Ghana.
articles.authors: Tan MH, Bangre O, Rios-Teran CA, Tiedje KE, Deed SL, Zhan Q, Rasyidi F, Pascual M, Ansah PO, Day KP
articles.details: Metagenomic analysis of 188 afebrile residents in Northern Sahelian Ghana using variable blood volumes revealed high prevalence of mixed-species infections (P. falciparum, P. malariae, P. ovale curtisi, P. ovale wallikeri) and mixed-strain infections. Larger blood volumes showed significantly higher parasite prevalence and complexity. Findings underscore need for sensitive detection of within-host Plasmodium complexity in asymptomatic carriers to optimize surveillance and control.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum PP1 phosphatase regulates parasite transmission
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum PP1 phosphatase is a key regulator of malaria parasite transmission.
articles.authors: Royer L, Colard-Itté E, Tavella T, Lorthiois A, Goussin S, N'Dri M-E, Sabra R, Deiss A, Thiberge S, Lamarque MH, Lavazec C
articles.details: Functional analysis using conditional depletion strategy showed PfPP1 (serine-threonine phosphatase) regulates transmission-stage parasites. PfPP1 controls deformability and permeability of mature gametocyte-infected erythrocytes through dephosphorylation of PKA substrates, enabling gametocyte persistence in blood. PfPP1 also controls gametocyte activation via cGMP/PKG pathway. Results demonstrate PfPP1's pivotal role in P. falciparum transmission from humans to mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Melochia umbellata extract: antimalarial efficacy and organ protection in rats
articles.title_original: Curative antimalarial efficacy and hepato-renal protective effects of Melochia umbellata leaf extract in rats infected with Plasmodium berghei.
articles.authors: Ngouana V, Tsouh Fokou PV, Tsakem Nangap MJ, Kemzeu R, Kamche Youbi A, Boyom FF
articles.details: In Plasmodium berghei-infected rats, Melochia umbellata leaf aqueous extract (MULAE) was tested for curative efficacy and organ protection. LD50 was greater than 5000 mg/kg in acute toxicity tests. Five-day treatment with MULAE 400 mg/kg achieved 86.20% parasitemia suppression, restored hemoglobin to 14.9 g/dL, mitigated weight loss, and improved serum markers (creatinine, ALAT, ASAT, bilirubin, total protein) and histopathology. Findings support M. umbellata's traditional use for antimalarial and organ-protective potential.

articles.headline: High drug-resistance mutations in asymptomatic malaria carriers, Vietnam
articles.title_original: Molecular Profiles of Antimalarial Drug Resistance in Plasmodium Species from Asymptomatic Malaria Carriers in Gia Lai Province, Vietnam.
articles.authors: Lê HG, Võ TC, Kang JM, Van Khanh C, Trinh NTM, Hanh NTL, Cho M, Quang HH, Na BK
articles.details: Survey of 2171 individuals from three districts identified 2.67% asymptomatic submicroscopic malaria infection rate, with P. vivax more prevalent than P. falciparum, plus mixed infections. High frequencies of drug-resistance markers: pfk13 C580Y (100%), pfcrt M74I/N75E/K76T (100%), and pvmdr-1 F1076L (100%) in asymptomatic carriers. Findings emphasize urgent need for improved surveillance to prevent silent spread of drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: Public-private partnerships for climate-resilient malaria control, Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Challenges and opportunities for greater public-private partnership for the implementation of the WHO operational framework for building climate resilient health systems to improve malaria control and elimination in Sub-Saharan Africa: a rapid review.
articles.authors: Lumumba S, Kamau S, Ntwiga I, Muchangi JM, Kiarie J, Kosgei S, Mwamburi M, Kimathi G
articles.details: Systematic review of 11 articles (from 173 identified) examined public-private partnerships implementing WHO Operational Framework for climate-resilient health systems. Identified challenges: ineffective legal frameworks, bureaucracy, limited adherence to guidelines, lack of systemic climate risk integration. Identified opportunities: workforce education (MOOCs), targeted response partnerships with private sector, co-production mechanisms. PPPs remain viable for WHO Framework adaptation despite challenges, particularly given Sub-Saharan Africa technical and financial capacity limitations.

articles.headline: Malaria care-seeking delays: Myanmar and Thailand epidemiologic drivers
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking among febrile patients in Myanmar and Thailand: an accelerated failure time analysis.
articles.authors: Htwe KZZ, Aung PL, Sripoorote P, Soe MT, Petchvijit P, Aung PP, Khamsiriwatchara A, Lawpoolsri S, Cui L, Parker DM, Kyaw MP, Sattabongkot J, Kaewkungwal J
articles.details: Longitudinal study of febrile patients in Myanmar (n=2,960, Dec 2017–Jun 2021) and Thailand (n=15,576, Jan 2018–Jun 2024) using Accelerated Failure Time models. Mean fever duration at diagnosis: Myanmar 1.8 days, Thailand 2.9 days. Longer delays associated with: Shan ethnicity (Time Ratio 1.48 in Myanmar), farmers, uncertain malaria history, P. falciparum (TR 1.10–1.27) and P. vivax (TR 1.20–1.23) diagnosis, and third/fourth quarter diagnosis. Shorter delays with bed net use and non-agricultural occupations.

articles.headline: Voltage-switching mass spectrometry enables perfect malaria detection
articles.title_original: Voltage Switching on a Dual Nano-Electrospray/Atmospheric Pressure Chemical Ionization Platform Provides Higher Discrimination Power for Malaria Detection via Direct Infusion Mass Spectrometry.
articles.authors: Ferguson R, Kulyk DS, Badu-Tawiah AK
articles.details: Mass spectrometry diagnostic for malaria using voltage-switching ionization approach analyzed 9 malaria-infected and 9 uninfected blood samples. Voltage-switching achieved 100% correct classification of all 18 samples, while constant-voltage nESI misclassified 2 samples and APCI misclassified 4 samples. Composite spectra from voltage switching provided richer chemical information and higher discriminatory power than constant-voltage modes.

articles.headline: Selective proteasome inhibitor J-80 shows 90-fold selectivity in vitro
articles.title_original: A recombinant expression system for the Plasmodium falciparum proteasome enables structural analysis of its assembly and the design of selective inhibitors.
articles.authors: Fajtova P, Zhang H, Urich L, da Silva EB, da Silva CH, Almaliti J, Al-Hindy M, Boura E, Kirkman LA, Lin G, Bogyo M, Fidock DA, Gerwick WH, Zhao J, O'Donoghue AJ
articles.details: Study developed a recombinant P. falciparum 20S proteasome platform for drug discovery. Clinical proteasome inhibitors (bortezomib, carfilzomib, marizomib) were potent but lacked selectivity. J-80 inhibited Pf20S β5 with IC50 of 22.4 nM and 90-fold selectivity over human β5. Novel inhibitors TDI-8304 and analogs confirmed potency and selectivity over human constitutive proteasome.

articles.headline: BinaxNOW malaria rapid test achieves 100% sensitivity and specificity
articles.title_original: BinaxNOW vs malaria: A diagnostic showdown: Design and implementation of a verification strategy across 4 academic hospitals in an area with low malaria prevalence.
articles.authors: Turbett SE, Bielawski D, Dighe AS, Kaseta N, Lewandrowski K, Mercer L, Patten C, Sta Cruz ML, Shenoy ES, Branda JA
articles.details: Diagnostic test verification study comparing BinaxNOW Malaria rapid test to blood smear in 294 whole-blood specimens. BinaxNOW correctly identified 100% of Plasmodium falciparum positives with 96% species-level identification and achieved 100% sensitivity and specificity. Multi-site verification across 4 additional academic hospitals confirmed 100% positive predictive agreement, negative predictive agreement, concordance, and reproducibility.

articles.headline: Seasonal wing geometry changes in malaria vector A. maculatus
articles.title_original: Seasonal variation in wing geometry of the malaria vector Anopheles maculatus (Diptera: Culicidae) in Western Thailand.
articles.authors: Sumruayphol S, Chaiphongpachara T, Laojun S
articles.details: Study assessed seasonal variation in Anopheles maculatus wing structure from western Thailand across three seasons using geometric morphometrics. Landmark-based analysis found significant differences in wing structure between hot and dry seasons (p<0.05) and between dry versus hot/wet populations (p<0.05). Outline-based shape analysis showed significant difference only between dry and wet seasons (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Pfcoronin mutations mediate artemisinin resistance via impaired hemoglobin uptake
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular basis of Pf Coronin function in artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Ullah I, Farringer MA, Khushu M, Burkhard AY, Hathaway E, Willett BC, Shin SH, Sharma AI, Martin MC, Harrigan O, Shao KL, Dvorin JD, Hartl DL, Volkman SK, Bopp S, Absalon S, Wirth DF
articles.details: Study identified that resistance-associated Pfcoronin mutations in P. falciparum disrupt Pf Coronin's interaction with Pf Actin and ring-stage localization, impairing endocytosis and reducing hemoglobin acquisition. Pfcoronin and Pfkelch13 mutations function in distinct cellular regions but converge to limit heme availability and artemisinin activation, reducing artemisinin susceptibility during the ring stage. These non-essential proteins shape antimalarial drug response and resistance.

articles.headline: Fever enhances malaria cytoadhesion through endothelial glycocalyx shedding
articles.title_original: Febrile temperature enhances Plasmodium falciparum cytoadhesion by disrupting the endothelial glycocalyx.
articles.authors: Introini V, Long R, Oyerinde OR, Sender SS, Stein F, Hwang GM, Gutierrez BL, Seydel KB, Birbeck G, Bernabeu M
articles.details: Study used 3D fever-on-a-chip model to recapitulate human brain and lung microvessels. Febrile episodes at 40°C rapidly enhanced infected RBC and immune cell binding under flow. Endothelial glycocalyx shedding exposed EPCR and ICAM-1 receptors and drove this phenotype. Broad MMP inhibitor prevented temperature-induced cytoadhesion increase, identifying fever as an amplifier of malaria vascular pathology.

articles.headline: Nanoparticle-based junctional vaccine broadens protection against malaria
articles.title_original: Protective immunity against malaria by a nanoparticle CIS43-based junctional vaccine alone or in combination with R21.
articles.authors: Tripathi P, Koo JH, Chen X, Da Silva Pereira L, Dillon M, Zhang B, Lofgren M, Nguyen KT, Teng IT, Bonilla B, Kerscher S, Kong WP, Ransier A, Stephens T, Tsybovsky Y, Weldon SR, Douek DC, Pierson TC, Batista FD, Idris AH, Seder RA, Kwong PD, Zhou T
articles.details: Pre-clinical vaccine study compared nanoparticle-based CIS43-junctional PfCSP vaccine alone or combined with R21 benchmark vaccine. At 1 μg dose, both vaccines showed comparable reduction in liver burden. At 0.25 μg, junctional vaccine showed modest reduction compared to R21. Combined vaccination induced modestly enhanced protection with different antibody specificities: junctional vaccine elicited antibodies against both junctional and major repeat regions; R21 primarily against major repeats.

articles.headline: COVID-19 vaccine dynamics predict strong attitudes to malaria vaccination
articles.title_original: Attitudes to malaria vaccination in children: what can be learned from their consistency with the temporal dynamics of COVID-19 vaccination intention? A prospective cohort study in rural Senegal (July 2020-November 2023).
articles.authors: Seror V, Cortaredona S, Ba EH, Maradan G, Sokhna C
articles.details: Survey of 600 Senegalese households (July 2020-November 2023) examined attitudes to malaria vaccination for children. Very strong positive attitudes (65.28%) were associated with higher COVID-19 vaccine uptake (p<0.001). Three COVID-19 vaccination temporal patterns identified: continuously strong intention (34.88%), increasingly strong (33.40%), increasingly less strong (31.72%). Households with both members having continuously strong COVID-19 intention were more likely to have strong malaria vaccination attitudes (p=0.001).

articles.headline: Malaria resurgence in Africa is avoidable crisis
articles.title_original: The resurgence of malaria in Africa is an avoidable crisis-here's what we must do.
articles.authors: Kaseya J, Dereje N
articles.details: Commentary on malaria resurgence in Africa as an avoidable crisis. No abstract available; specific findings and recommendations cannot be determined from title alone.

articles.headline: High P. falciparum genetic diversity in Korhogo district
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of Plasmodium falciparum in Korhogo Health District, Northern Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.authors: Benié EMA, Beuret C, Schnoz A, Cantoreggi SL, Ding XC, Silué KD, Nsanzabana C
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 179 P. falciparum samples from dried blood spots collected 2019-2020 in Korhogo Health District, Northern Côte d'Ivoire. Genotyping revealed high genetic diversity: msp1 showed K1 (20 genotypes), MAD20 (23), and RO33 (1); msp2 showed 59 (3D7) and 33 (FC27) genotypes; glurp showed 45 genotypes. Expected heterozygosity ≥0.9 for all three markers indicated highly diverse parasite population.

articles.headline: Malaria-associated anemia results from parasite-driven bone marrow dysfunction
articles.title_original: Malaria and dyserythropoiesis: a mini review.
articles.authors: Liu FF, Li K
articles.details: Mini review synthesizing mechanistic studies of malaria-associated dyserythropoiesis. Four interlocking pathways explain disrupted erythropoiesis: (1) inflammatory cytokines suppress erythropoietin and redirect progenitors toward myeloid fate; (2) hemozoin catalyzes oxidative damage generating 4-hydroxynonenal adducts; (3) parasites prolong gametocyte maturation in erythroblasts and release extracellular vesicles arresting differentiation; (4) hemozoin-laden macrophages remodel erythroblastic islands causing iron restriction. Highlights emerging single-cell/spatial-omics platforms for future study.

articles.headline: P. falciparum coinfection prevalence in HBV-infected Ghanaians
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Helicobacter pylori, Salmonella typhi, Plasmodium falciparum, and Toxoplasma gondii infections and levels of liver function markers among Hepatitis B Virus infected Ghanaians: A cross-sectional study in the Greater Accra Region.
articles.authors: Nyarko EN, Amakye EK, Ofori EK, Appiah M, Anim M, Lartey NL, Ametepe S, Adu EA, Kumi J, Dodoo DND, Adom M, Owiredu E, Kwafo E, Obirikorang C
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 120 HBV-infected persons in Greater Accra Region, Ghana. P. falciparum prevalence 2.5% (95% CI 0.8-7.1). Additional coinfections: H. pylori 40.8%, T. gondii 19.2%, S. typhi 2.5%. HBV-H.pylori coinfection elevated ALT levels (33.5 versus 23.5 IU, p<0.001) compared to HBV-only, indicating coinfections may worsen liver function in HBV-infected persons.

articles.headline: PlasmoDocking: Web tool for virtual screening against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: PlasmoDocking: A User-Friendly Open-Source Web Tool for Virtual Screening Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Enzymes.
articles.authors: Guariero FL, Macedo EP, Laia EB, Evaristo JAM, Evaristo GPC, Zanchi FB
articles.details: PlasmoDocking is a user-friendly web-based platform automating molecular docking simulations using AutodockGPU against 38 predefined P. falciparum enzyme targets. Users submit up to 10 molecular structures for simultaneous multi-target screening and systematic comparison of binding energies with co-crystallized ligands. The open-source tool, developed in Python and Next.js, accelerates malaria drug discovery by simplifying a workflow typically requiring command-line expertise.

articles.headline: 55% malaria self-medication prevalence in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Issues associated with malaria self-medication in sub-Saharan Africa: A systematic literature review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Amaka JI, MacLeod E, Picozzi K, Fyfe J, Ezenyi IC, Ijaiya IS, Attah DD, Godwin BA, Obisike VU, Galamaji MM
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 27 studies from sub-Saharan Africa found pooled prevalence of 55.3% for malaria self-medication. Key drivers included low income, cheap non-prescription drug availability, large family size, lack of health insurance, and difficulty accessing formal healthcare. Self-medication is implicated in rising antimalarial drug resistance. Conclusion: health authorities should engage informal drug markets in frameworks promoting appropriate antimalarial use.

articles.headline: Spatial repellents reduce malaria infection 52% versus controls
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Spatial Repellents in Malaria Prevention: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.authors: Prager M, Bergmann F, Pracher L, Zeitlinger M, Jorda A
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 4 randomized controlled trials in China, Indonesia, and Kenya (6,745 participants total) assessed spatial repellents for malaria prevention. Malaria infection occurred in 20.0% (681/3,399) of spatial-repellent users versus 31.0% (1,055/3,346) of controls, yielding 52% risk reduction (pooled risk ratio 0.48; 95% CI 0.33–0.70; p<0.001). No significant effects on mosquito density or sporozoite rates were observed. High heterogeneity limited estimate certainty, but spatial repellents supported as complementary malaria prevention.

articles.headline: Burkina Faso halts transgenic mosquito malaria control plan
articles.title_original: Burkina Faso halts plan to fight malaria with transgenic mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Kupferschmidt K
articles.details: News report: Misinformation campaign may have eroded trust and led to a 'brutal, humiliating' raid on a research center. No quantitative data provided in abstract.

articles.headline: Correction: Aspartic protease inhibitors for P. vivax antimalarials
articles.title_original: Correction: Computational identification of aspartic protease inhibitors for antimalarial drug development against Plasmodium Vivax.
articles.authors: Alruwaili M, Alhassan HH, Almutary H, Tahir Ul Qamar M
articles.details: Correction notice only; no abstract data provided.

articles.headline: Multi-epitope FREP1 vaccine design targeting Anopheles stephensi transmission
articles.title_original: Designing a multi-epitope vaccine against the midgut-specific fibrinogen-related protein 1(FREP1) of Anopheles stephensi to enhance protection against the malaria parasite: a step beyond traditional vaccine development approaches.
articles.authors: Yadav M, Dahiya N, Singh H, Kataria D, Janjoter S, Sehrawat N
articles.details: Immunoinformatic design of transmission-blocking vaccine targeting fibrinogen-related protein 1 (FREP1), a mosquito midgut protein crucial for parasite transmission. In-silico assessment confirmed allergenicity, antigenicity, toxicity, immunogenicity, population coverage, and conservancy. Molecular docking showed efficient TLR4 receptor interaction; molecular dynamics and immunization profiling indicated high binding energy and immune response potential. Codon optimization and in-silico cloning in E. coli demonstrated efficient expression.

articles.headline: Fractional curative killing of artemisinin-quinoline antimalarial combinations in mice
articles.title_original: Fractional curative killing of pyronaridine or artesunate combinations with tafenoquine, 4-aminoquinolines, or azithromycin in a murine malaria-luciferase model.
articles.authors: Lee G, Aneke JS, Sullivan DJ Jr
articles.details: In a P. berghei ANKA-luciferase murine high-parasitemia model, fractional curative doses were determined for combinations of artesunate or pyronaridine with chloroquine, amodiaquine, tafenoquine, and azithromycin. Tafenoquine/artesunate and pyronaridine/amodiaquine showed fractional doses of 0.83 and 0.93, respectively; others ranged 0.9–1.1 (additive interactions). Parasite reduction rate did not reliably predict curative outcomes. All tested combinations exhibited additive drug interactions in vivo.

articles.headline: Azithromycin analogs kill malaria parasites via chloroquine-like and novel mechanisms
articles.title_original: Characterizing the quick-killing mechanism of action of azithromycin analogs against malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Mao EY, Nguyen W, Jana GP, Maity BC, Pazicky S, Giannangelo C, Reader J, Famodimu MT, Birkholtz L-M, Delves MJ, Creek DJ, Bozdech Z, Laleu B, Burrows JN, Sleebs BE, Gancheva MR, Wilson DW
articles.details: Five azithromycin analogs were investigated for rapid parasite killing. Non-chloroquinoline analogs showed 10- to 42-fold improvement over azithromycin; chloroquinoline-containing analogs showed 2- to 17-fold further improvement. Chemical inhibition assays linked both types to chloroquine-like mechanisms, but analogs showed no reduced efficacy against chloroquine-resistant parasites. Thermal shift proteomics revealed 34–155 different proteins were significantly stabilized, showing minimal overlap with chloroquine targets. The most potent analog demonstrated improved gametocytocidal activity.

articles.headline: P. ovale predominates in asymptomatic infections; novel Kelch13 mutations in South Africa
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium species in asymptomatic individuals in North-Eastern South Africa: 2018 - 2019.
articles.authors: Munzhedzi M, Guler JL, Krivacsy S, Shifflett P, Operario DJ, Dillingham R, McQuade ETR, Bessong PO
articles.details: In 985 asymptomatic individuals from Ha-Lambani, Limpopo Province, South Africa (November 2018–May 2019), asymptomatic Plasmodium prevalence was 7.1% (70/985; 95% CI 0.054–0.087). P. ovale was dominant (57.14%, n=40), followed by P. falciparum (37.1%, n=26), P. malariae (1.43%), P. vivax (1.43%), and mixed P. falciparum/P. ovale (2.9%). Pfk13 sequencing identified 57 SNPs across the gene (≥20% minority level); 70.1% (39/57) were non-synonymous, and none were previously associated with artemisinin resistance. Five novel SNPs in the propeller domain (H719Q, P701T, M472I, I526R, P443S) were detected.

articles.headline: Armed conflict in DRC impacts malaria outbreak risk in children under five
articles.title_original: The impact of armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo on the risk of malaria outbreaks in children under five years old.
articles.authors: Yokolo H, Tague C, Ekouo J, Makeda D, Akilimali A
articles.details: Title only; no abstract provided. Implies analysis of how conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo affects malaria outbreak risk in children <5 years.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine post-G6PD screening cost-effective for vivax radical cure in Brazil
articles.title_original: The cost-effectiveness of tafenoquine following screening with STANDARD™ G6PD screening for the treatment of vivax malaria in the Brazilian Public Health System.
articles.authors: Peixoto HM, Bastos Gottin LL, Brito-Sousa JD, Sampaio V, Daumerie PG, Jambert E, Monteiro W, Lacerda MVG, Devine A
articles.details: Decision-tree model compared tafenoquine (post-G6PD screening for ≥70% activity) with current practice (7-day primaquine 0.5 mg/kg/day without screening) and primaquine screening (≥30% G6PD activity) for P. vivax radical cure in Brazilian adults. Tafenoquine was US$2,894 per DALY averted versus current practice (below willingness-to-pay threshold of US$7,752) and dominated the primaquine screening strategy, averting 0.14 DALYs with US$13 savings. Tafenoquine strategy had >98% likelihood of cost-effectiveness in both comparisons.

articles.headline: Preterm birth increases child malaria infection risk in multigravid mothers' offspring
articles.title_original: Preterm Birth and Malaria Susceptibility in Offspring of Uninfected Multigravid Women.
articles.authors: Barry A, Dang L, Sidibe Y, Issiaka D, Gaoussou S, Hu Z, Dicko Y, Mahamar A, Attaher O, Diarra BS, Keita S, Dicko A, Duffy PE, Fried M
articles.details: Prospective cohort study of 1,679 mother-child pairs in Ouélessébougou, Mali (enrollment November 2010–December 2014; follow-up through July 2016, up to 5 years). Pregnancy malaria (PM) was associated with increased hazard of first malaria infection and clinical malaria in offspring of all gravidity groups. Preterm delivery (versus full-term) was associated with increased hazard of first infection only in offspring of multigravid women (HR 1.76; 95% CI 1.05–2.95; p=0.03). Parasitemia incidence rate ratio for preterm was 2.17 (95% CI 1.25–3.75) and 3.63 (95% CI 1.90–5.93) in offspring of uninfected secundigravida and multigravida women, respectively.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic Plasmodium infection in migrants triggers splenomegaly, anemia
articles.title_original: Clinical Findings in Migrants With Asymptomatic Plasmodium Infections.
articles.authors: Eliasson I, Wyss K, Tafesse Bogale R, Forsblom S, Lindquist E, Rönnberg C, Hansson M, Beshara S, Nordling I, Hertting O, Wångdahl A, Färnert A
articles.details: Retrospective study compared 65 Plasmodium PCR-positive and 54 PCR-negative migrants. Infected individuals had higher rates of splenomegaly (25.4% vs 2.5%), anemia (21.1% vs 6.1%), elevated erythrocyte sedimentation rate (58.1% vs 25.0%), and elevated immunoglobulin M (30.5% vs 10.5%). After antimalarial treatment, these parameters improved in infected individuals.

articles.headline: Anopheles species in Colombian gold-mining areas transmit P. falciparum and P. vivax
articles.title_original: Anopheles species associated with malaria transmission in gold-mining areas of northwest Colombia.
articles.authors: Rojas MA, Ahumada ML, Velasco-Pareja MC, Chaparro P, Yasnot-Acosta MF, Orjuela LI
articles.details: Cross-sectional study in El Bagre, Colombia identified 11 Anopheles species, with An. darlingi harboring P. falciparum and An. nuneztovari and An. punctimacula harboring P. vivax. Entomological inoculation rates ranged from 1.5 to 6.6 infective bites per person per month. Gold-mining excavations were identified as breeding sites for these vector species.

articles.headline: MALDI-TOF MS efficiently identifies secondary malaria vectors in Africa
articles.title_original: MALDI-TOF MS for identification of Afro-tropical secondary malaria vectors.
articles.authors: Tuwei M, Karisa J, Wanjiku C, Kiuru C, Ondieki Z, Odongo T, Mure F, Otieno B, Meli P, Okoko M, Bartilol B, Gona R, Constantino L, Cole G, Anastácio T, Armazia R, Alves C, Rui P, Ramaita E, Rono M, Candrinho B, Mwangangi J, Mbogo C, Charlwood D, Saute F, Rabinovich R, Chaccour C, Maia MF
articles.details: Study in Kenya and Mozambique evaluated MALDI-TOF MS for identifying secondary malaria vectors compared to Sanger sequencing. Overall accuracy was 91% (kappa=0.87), with 100% sensitivity and specificity for most species except An. cf. coustani 2 NFL-2015 and An. ziemanni. MALDI-TOF MS proved as efficient as DNA sequencing for vector identification.

articles.headline: Low immunization coverage in Togo threatens perennial malaria chemoprevention delivery
articles.title_original: Assessing immunization coverage in Southern togo: implications for perennial malaria chemoprevention delivery.
articles.authors: Arikawa S, Tchankoni MK, Gbeasor-Komlanvi F, Atekpe SP, Atcha-Oubou T, Figueroa-Romero A, Orne-Gliemann J, Menendez C, Gonzalez R, Briand V, Ekouevi D
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 583 children (12-23 months) in southern Togo found only 60.7% fully immunized and 15.8% zero-dose. Zero-dose status was associated with low caregiver education (aOR 5.02), negative vaccine safety attitudes (aOR 8.60), and low modern healthcare reliance (aOR 3.06). Key barriers included family discouragement (17.4%), lack of time (15.1%), and COVID-19 impacts (14%).

articles.headline: Ferritin nanoparticle vaccine combines infection and transmission blocking
articles.title_original: A combined designed CSP and Pfs48/45 infection and transmission blocking vaccine for malaria.
articles.authors: Gupta R, Dickey TH, Salinas ND, Patel PN, Ma R, Shi D, Singleton M, Ouahes T, Pham TP, Miura K, Long CA, Lambert LE, Tolia NH
articles.details: Researchers designed a ferritin nanoparticle vaccine presenting CSPj5c (from circumsporozoite protein) and 17-4 (from Pfs48/45) immunogens targeting two parasite life-cycle stages. In mice, the vaccine conferred protection against transgenic P. falciparum sporozoite challenge. Purified IgGs from immunized rabbits demonstrated potent transmission-reducing activity.

articles.headline: Five-flower remedy with artesunate improves outcomes in experimental cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Antimalarial and neuroprotective effects of ethanolic extracts of the five-flower remedy in an experimental cerebral malaria model.
articles.authors: Plirat W, Chaniad P, Phuwajaroanpong A, Konyanee A, Lalert L, Septama AW, Punsawad C
articles.details: In a P. berghei ANKA-induced experimental cerebral malaria mouse model, five-flower remedy (FFR) extract combined with artesunate significantly reduced parasitemia levels (80%), increased survival rates, and reduced neurological deficits. Brain tissue analysis revealed decreased hemorrhage, leukocyte infiltration, and neuronal apoptosis, with reduced brain inflammation and behavioral changes.

articles.headline: Cyclic nucleotide protein at ookinete apex essential for mosquito infection
articles.title_original: A divergent cyclic nucleotide binding protein promotes Plasmodium ookinete infection of the mosquito.
articles.authors: Kwecka D, Wang Z, Eigminas E, Liu L, Regan JC, Kim C, Philip N
articles.details: Study identified a cyclic nucleotide-binding protein (CBP-O) localized to the Plasmodium ookinete apex that is essential for ookinete invasion of mosquitoes. The protein exhibits dual binding to both cGMP and cAMP, with distinct functions for the N- and C-termini. Disruption of PbCBP-O severely compromised ookinete invasion.

articles.headline: Flavin-deficient erythrocytes inhibit P. falciparum parasite proliferation
articles.title_original: Flavin-deficient erythrocytes offer protection against malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Hemasa A, van Loon W, Fu J, Spry C, Jäger J, van Schalkwyk DA, Reverberi R, Ndoli J, Mockenhaupt FP, Contini C, Saliba KJ
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Using FAD-dependent glutathione reductase assays, researchers identified flavin-deficient erythrocytes (FDE) in Ferrara, Italy (23% of 150 individuals) and Huye, Rwanda (13% of 169 individuals). FDE from Italy and in vitro riboflavin-starved erythrocytes inhibited P. falciparum intraerythrocytic proliferation, apparently through oxidative stress. Genetic analysis identified FAD synthase mutations in three individuals with FDE from Rwanda.

articles.headline: Fc engineering enhances anti-CSP antibody protection against malaria
articles.title_original: Analysis of Fc-dependent effector functions of anti-malaria circumsporozoite protein antibodies.
articles.authors: Stefanutti E, Ramani R, Whitener B, Dang H, Bélanger S, Somasundaram L, Cortina K, De Marco A, Tam T, Chai Q, Cameroni E, Gupta R, Schmid MA, Miller JL, Zumsteg AB, Purcell LA, Drewry LL
articles.details: Study examined Fc-dependent effector functions of three anti-CSP monoclonal antibodies (mAbs 317, L9, CIS43) in protecting against malaria. mAbs L9 and CIS43 were more potent drivers of antibody-dependent phagocytosis, NK activation and killing, and complement deposition, while 317 drove enhanced T-cell activation. Fc engineering modulated the activity profile of multiple CSP mAbs.

articles.headline: β-caryophyllene shows dose-dependent repellency against Thai Anopheles species
articles.title_original: COMPARATIVE BEHAVIORAL RESPONSES OF Β-CARYOPHYLLENE AGAINST ANOPHELES MOSQUITO SPECIES, POTENTIAL VECTORS OF MALARIA IN THAILAND.
articles.authors: Lhaosudto S, Sathantriphop S, Kongmee M, Karpkird T, Leepasert T, Ngoen-Klan R, Chareonviriyaphap T
articles.details: Excito-repellency assay tested β-caryophyllene (plant-derived) against four Anopheles species. An. minimus exhibited highest escape rates (100% at 2.5% contact and 5% noncontact concentrations). An. harrisoni showed strong responses at 7.5% (94.34% contact, 72.73% noncontact). An. epiroticus showed minimal response (12-25.42%). No knockdown or mortality occurred.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 21.1% in Ethiopian children under five
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria and Associated Factors among Under Five Febrile Children Attending Ener Amanuel Health Center, Southern Ethiopia: An Institution-based Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Tseha ST, Ebabu M, Aberar T
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 218 febrile children under five in Southern Ethiopia found 21.1% malaria prevalence, with P. falciparum predominant (60.9%). Factors significantly associated with infection included proximity to mosquito breeding sites (p=0.025), not sleeping under insecticide-treated nets (p=0.021), and unsuitable beds for ITN hanging (p=0.002). Authors recommend strengthening ITN utilization and eliminating breeding sites.

articles.headline: Stakeholder analysis identifies barriers to Malakit scale-up in Guiana Shield
articles.title_original: Assessing scalability factors of a malaria intervention using Malakit and target drug administration among hard-to-reach populations: An original approach focusing on key stakeholders - implementers and decision-makers - in the Guiana shield.
articles.authors: Carboni C, Sanna A, Lambert Y, Plessis L, Bardon T, Adenis A, Nacher M, Vreden S, Suárez-Mutis M, Douine M
articles.details: Participatory stakeholder assessment in Brazil, French Guiana, and Suriname (June 2023–March 2024) evaluated scalability of Malakit (self-testing/treatment) and targeted P. vivax drug administration. Study identified Malakit effectiveness as a strength but noted data gaps and regulatory challenges as weaknesses. Financial and regulatory barriers posed threats to scale-up, while malaria elimination initiatives offered opportunities.

articles.headline: Malaria control strategies for hard-to-reach populations
articles.title_original: Malaria in hard-to-reach populations: Challenges and solutions.
articles.authors: Fouque F, Ravasi D, Masoudi GA, Rodriguez-Morales AJ
articles.details: Discusses challenges and solutions for malaria control in hard-to-reach populations. Specific findings not available from title alone.

articles.headline: Transmission diversity and mobility complicate malaria control progress
articles.title_original: Biodiversity of malaria ecosystems, challenges for control: When human diversity is forgotten.
articles.authors: Fouque F, Boutsika K
articles.details: Historical review noting malaria elimination from temperate countries but resurgence in sub-Saharan Africa approximately 50 years ago. Control progress plateaued because transmission context diversity was underestimated. Human population mobility, combined with diverse transmission settings, requires reaching hard-to-reach and mobile populations to sustain elimination gains.

articles.headline: Suriname eliminated P. falciparum using targeted inclusive strategy
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Suriname: how targeted interventions in marginalized populations led to elimination.
articles.authors: Hiwat-Van Laar H, Cairo H, Hardjopawiro L, Eersel M, Vreden S, Adhin M, Panchoe D, van Eer E, Jintie H, van Dijk M, Mesones Lapouble O
articles.details: National surveillance analysis 2000–2024 showed P. falciparum shifted from indigenous/tribal interior communities to mobile migrants in remote gold mining sites, with the last indigenous case in 2018. Strategy provided low-barrier access to care for all at-risk populations. Elimination achieved but faces risk of re-introduction from neighboring countries amid economic constraints.

articles.headline: Village health workers underutilized in malaria elimination efforts
articles.title_original: Role of village health worker in control activities for malaria elimination efforts: A systematic review.
articles.authors: Wangdi K, Wangchuk D, Alamnia T, Cao P, Kotepui M, Suwannatrai AT, Ugyel L, Vardoulakis S, Tsheten T
articles.details: Systematic review of 44 articles (from 14,884 screened) found village health workers in hard-to-reach areas primarily involved in diagnosis/treatment (21/44 studies) and diagnosis-focused activities. Also engaged in behavior change communication, reactive case detection, prevention via LLINs/IPTc, SMC, and health education. Most work focused on routine control rather than elimination; authors recommend integration into elimination programs.

articles.headline: V. amygdalina crude extracts inhibit P. knowlesi in vitro
articles.title_original: Vernonia amygdalina (Delile) exhibits in vitro anti-plasmodial activities against Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Zulkefli NZ, Cheong FW, Amir A, Lau YL
articles.details: Crude extracts of V. amygdalina leaves tested against P. knowlesi using schizont maturation inhibition assays. IC50 values: dichloromethane 1.356±0.23 μg/mL, methanol 1.131±0.16 μg/mL, water 1.973±0.30 μg/mL. Methanol and water extracts non-toxic to Vero cells (CC50 ≥63.73 μg/mL). All extracts achieved selectivity index >10, with methanol extract showing highest parasite selectivity.

articles.headline: Precision sterile insect technique optimized for cost-effective vector control
articles.title_original: Estimated cost and operational structure of pgSIT malaria vector control programs in selected West African countries.
articles.authors: Gendron WAC, Raban R, Mondal A, Sánchez C HM, Zilberman D, Ilboudo PGC, D'Alessandro U, Marshall JM, Akbari OS
articles.details: Precision guided sterile insect technique (pgSIT) generates sterile male A. gambiae for mass release. Study optimized facility size to suppress 9.2 million A. gambiae, improving cost-effectiveness compared to previous estimates using only 15–31% sex-sorting capacity. Potential to interrupt malaria transmission and create local manufacturing capacity and employment.

articles.headline: Severe falciparum malaria causes electrolyte imbalance in children
articles.title_original: Exploring the Effect of Malaria on Biochemical Parameters Among Sudanese Patients with Complicated Falciparum Malaria.
articles.authors: Ali MO, Tamomh AG, Abdulrahman NA, Ibrahim NS, Eltayeb MA, Osman FI
articles.details: Case-control study (200 severe malaria vs. 200 healthy controls) conducted August 2022–January 2023. In infected children, plasma potassium and sodium significantly decreased (p<0.05) while urea and creatinine were unaffected. In infected adults, plasma urea was significantly elevated (p<0.05) compared to adult controls. Results indicate malaria is a risk factor for hyponatremia and hypokalemia in children and azotemia in adults.

articles.headline: Delayed treatment of severe malaria led to fatal hemorrhagic complication
articles.title_original: A fatal case report of Plasmodium falciparum malaria complicated by acute subdural hematoma.
articles.authors: Xie N, Ye H, Jiang X
articles.details: Case report of a patient in late 50s who returned from month-long travel to Burkina Faso and developed fever, headache, and myalgia 3 days post-return. Diagnosis of severe P. falciparum malaria and artesunate treatment began 9 days after symptom onset. Despite effective anti-Plasmodium treatment, patient died from multiple organ failure complicated by acute subdural hematoma.

articles.headline: S. jamaicensis extract shows antiplasmodial and immunomodulatory activity
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial and immunomodulatory activities of dichloromethane extract of Stachytarpheta jamaicensis in Plasmodium berghei infected mice.
articles.authors: Amaechi EC, Owolodun OA, Lambe KO
articles.details: Dichloromethane extract of S. jamaicensis tested in P. berghei (chloroquine-resistant) infected mice at 200, 400, and 600 mg/kg versus piperaquine-dihydroartemisinin control. At 200 mg/kg dose, extract caused significant antiplasmodial effect by day 11 (p<0.05), mediated parasite clearance, and improved survival. High and sustained TGF-β stimulation at 200 mg/kg correlated with prolonged survival.

articles.headline: C. adansonii extracts highly larvicidal against A. gambiae vectors
articles.title_original: Bioactive profiling, larvicidal and pupicidal activities of Crateva adansonii against two developmental stages of malaria vector, Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Ileke KD, Babatunde NA, Akeju AV
articles.details: Ethanolic extracts of C. adansonii leaf, stem, and root tested against A. gambiae larvae and pupae at 1–20% concentrations. Leaf extract at 20% concentration achieved 100% larval mortality in 6 hours. Root extract showed lowest lethal doses (LC50=0.04%, LC90=8.07% at 24 hours). GC-MS identified bioactive fatty acids and heterocyclic compounds. Plant-based approach could reduce eco-toxic effects of synthetic insecticides.

articles.headline: P. falciparum immune response involves complex innate-adaptive interaction
articles.title_original: Overview of the host immune response to P. falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Antwi-Baffour S, Mensah BT, Annison L
articles.details: Review of host immune mechanisms against P. falciparum including innate immunity (macrophages, dendritic cells, NK cells, inflammatory cytokines) and adaptive immunity (T cells destroying infected cells; B cells producing parasite-targeting antibodies). Protective immunity development influenced by parasite diversity, host genetics, and prior exposure. Understanding these mechanisms is essential for vaccine and therapeutic development.

articles.headline: Sub-patent P. falciparum infections high in SMC-covered households, Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Patterns of patent and sub-patent Plasmodium falciparum infections in household members of children under seasonal malaria chemoprevention coverage in the health district of Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: Kambou SAE, Sondo P, Kabore B, Bayala IMC, Kouevi AFC, Compaore EW, Millogo KS, Ismaïla B, Rouamba T, Kazienga A, Karim D, Rouamba E, Dahal P, Otienoburu SD, Raman J, Dhorda M, Bamba S, Guérin PJ, Tinto H
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey (650 participants) during 2022 SMC campaign in Nanoro. P. falciparum detected in 68.6% (446/650): 27.4% patent (both RDT/qPCR positive) and 41.2% sub-patent (qPCR+/RDT−). Patent infection declined with age: 37.7% (5–14 years), 25% (15–24 years), 17.1% (≥25 years). Sub-patent infections highest in 15–24 year group (49.2%). Findings support extending SMC to school-aged children and testing/treating household members.

articles.headline: Indonesia's subnational malaria elimination: progress and challenges
articles.title_original: Shrinking the malaria map in Indonesia: progress of subnational control, elimination, and future strategies.
articles.authors: Herdiana H, Prameswari HD, Puspadewi RT, Fajariyani SB, Diptyanusa A, Theodora M, Supriyanto D, Hawley WA
articles.details: This review examined Indonesia's subnational malaria elimination verification process using published and unpublished data from 1950-2023. As of 2023, 389 of 514 districts and five of 38 provinces had no local transmission, with most progress in western Indonesia. However, over 90% of cases are localized in the Papua region, with incidence exceeding 400 cases per 1000 person-years. The authors conclude that optimized existing interventions and new combinations are required for Indonesia to achieve nationwide elimination by 2030, particularly in high-endemic Papua.

articles.headline: Care-seeking behaviors and P. vivax infection in Myanmar migrants in Thailand
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking behaviours and infection prevalence among short-term Myanmar migrants in Thailand.
articles.authors: Aung PL, Sripoorote P, Inthitanon N, Wattanagoon Y, Hamers RL, Van Nuil JI, Cui L, Nguitragool W, Jones L, Sudewo AG, Sattabongkot J, Parker DM
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 300 Myanmar migrants (mean age 34.5±14.5 years; 47.3% male) in six malaria-endemic villages in Tak Province, Thailand (March-April 2025) found that although 60% recognized the need to seek care for fever or chills, only 35% did so within 24 hours of symptom onset. Six afebrile P. vivax infections (2%) were detected by PCR. Lower malaria knowledge (OR=13.5; 95% CI 1.58-177.0) and lower care-seeking scores (OR=5.86; 95% CI 1.15-57.7) were significantly associated with infection.

articles.headline: Health education increases LLIN acceptance but not timely care-seeking
articles.title_original: Contributions of health education and social mobilization in the implementation of a malaria control programme using long-lasting insecticidal nets.
articles.authors: da Costa FM, de Souza Rodrigues MM, Scarpassa VM
articles.details: From January 2010 to December 2012, intensive health education and social mobilization strategies in 10 rural communities in Porto Velho, Rondônia, Brazil, distributed 8,083 long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs). The intervention achieved 99.93% acceptance of LLIN use and 92.13% of households had sufficient nets, with 57.41% reporting consistent use. However, pre-intervention median monthly malaria cases in the two most endemic villages were 24 (IQR 5-61) and 7 (1-34), and health education did not reduce the proportion seeking diagnosis within 24 hours (65% and 61%, respectively).

articles.headline: Avian malaria parasites show epigenetic host-dependent gene expression
articles.title_original: Generalist Malaria Parasites and Host Imprinting: Unveiling Transcriptional Memory.
articles.authors: García-Longoria L, Palinauskas V, Aželytė J, Marzal A, Ovelleiro D, Hellgren O
articles.details: This experimental study inoculated the avian malaria parasite P. homocircumflexum into different bird species to assess gene expression patterns. Gene expression differed significantly by host species and time postinfection; at 8 days postinfection, 2,647 differentially expressed genes were associated with the donor host, whereas at 16 days postinfection, only 271 DEGs were linked to the recipient host. Single nucleotide polymorphism analyses revealed low genetic differentiation. The authors conclude that P. homocircumflexum undergoes a shift from donor-dependent to recipient-dependent gene expression driven by epigenetic regulation.

articles.headline: Congenital P. falciparum transmission from asymptomatic mother in Portugal
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria and Congenital Acquisition in Infant, Portugal, 2024.
articles.authors: Lopes I, Dias J, Das Neves E, Morais M, Santos-Reis A, Garcia AM, Varandas L, Lopes D
articles.details: P. falciparum infection was diagnosed in a 3-month-old infant in Portugal by optical microscopy. The mother had malaria in Angola 13 months prior, before emigrating to Portugal, and remained asymptomatic throughout pregnancy and after birth. The diagnosis of congenital transmission was confirmed using molecular techniques, representing a case of imported malaria with vertical transmission in a nonendemic setting.

articles.headline: Nosocomial transmission of P. falciparum in Spain, 2024
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024 (Response).
articles.authors: Romero MFL, de Pipaón MRP, Amuedo MDN, Rubio Muñoz JM, Jiménez-Hoyuela JM, Cisneros JM
articles.details: This is a response or correspondence regarding hospital-acquired transmission of P. falciparum in Spain in 2024. No specific methods, results, or details are available from the title.

articles.headline: Nosocomial transmission of P. falciparum in Spain, 2024
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024.
articles.authors: Gómez Perales JL, Mendoza AG, Amares MTG
articles.details: This article reports on hospital-acquired transmission of P. falciparum in Spain in 2024. Without an abstract, specific details about methods, cases, or outcomes cannot be determined from the title alone.

articles.headline: Diagnosis and management of imported malaria in children
articles.title_original: Diagnosis and Management of Imported Malaria in the Emergency Department.
articles.authors: McQueen A, Mannheim J
articles.details: This review article summarizes the diagnosis and management of children presenting with imported malaria to nonendemic countries. It emphasizes key features of travel history, diagnostic challenges in acute settings, and management strategies. The article notes that increasing global migration has led to more children with malaria presenting to nonendemic countries, with significant disease burden particularly in sub-Saharan Africa and South/Central America.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 metabolizer status impacts primaquine efficacy and P. vivax recurrence
articles.title_original: CYP2D6 Genotype and Primaquine Treatment in Patients with Malaria, Venezuela.
articles.authors: Pacheco C, Hernández-Acosta A, Pulido N, Ceballos Y, Saavedra D, Gómez C, Moreno N, Herrera F
articles.details: This study determined CYP2D6 genotypes and metabolizer phenotypes in 96 patients with suspected malaria in Venezuela and found intermediate or poor metabolizer phenotypes in approximately 25% of cases. Among 44 confirmed malaria patients, nine (20%) had P. vivax recurrence. The authors recommend that public health authorities evaluate the benefits of increasing primaquine doses, particularly to optimize efficacy in patients with intermediate or poor metabolizer phenotypes.

articles.headline: P. vivax microgametes present diagnostic challenge in Colombia
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax microgametes in a Colombian malaria case: A diagnostic challenge.
articles.authors: Rodríguez Lugo DA, Morcillo Muñoz JA, Medina-Lozano LJ, Faccini-Martínez ÁA
articles.details: This case report from Colombia describes a diagnostic challenge involving P. vivax microgametes. Without an abstract, specific clinical details or findings cannot be determined from the title alone.

articles.headline: House eave screening reduces malaria vector exposure 5.5-fold in Cameroon
articles.title_original: A low-cost house eave screening technique complements insecticide-treated nets in reducing indoor human exposure to the bites of anopheles mosquitoes in a malaria endemic area of south forested Cameroon: results from a pilot study.
articles.authors: Metitsi Tesongang RD, Elanga-Ndille E, Cheteug Nguetsa G, Awono-Ambene HP, Fomena A, Tabue R, Ndo C
articles.details: This pilot study in south forested Cameroon evaluated a low-cost eave screening technique to reduce human exposure to malaria vectors, comparing screened and unscreened houses. The pre-intervention human malaria prevalence was 38.85% and the entomological inoculation rate (EIR) was 73 infected bites per person per year. The intervention led to a 5.5-fold reduction in EIR in improved houses (from 37.70 to 6.90 ib/p/y) and was cost-effective at $23.60 per modified house.

articles.headline: IRS and BCC with LLINs reduce malaria incidence 22–23% in West Africa
articles.title_original: Efficacy of non-pyrethroid indoor residual spraying or intensive behaviour change communication in combination with long-lasting insecticidal nets for malaria control in west Africa: a pragmatic, cluster-randomised, controlled trial.
articles.authors: Moiroux N, Zongo I, Assi SB, Some A, Tchiekoi NB, Zogo B, Soma DD, Hien DFDS, Taconet P, Ahoua Alou LP, Lefevre T, Cohuet A, Fournet F, Mouline K, Koffi AA, Dabiré RK, Pennetier C
articles.details: This cluster-randomised trial across 39 villages (population 10,750) in Burkina Faso and Côte d'Ivoire compared three strategies: long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) alone (16 villages, control), LLINs plus pirimiphos-methyl indoor residual spraying (11 villages), and LLINs plus intensive behavior change communication (12 villages). Over 215,000 theoretical person-months of follow-up, the LLIN plus IRS group showed 23% reduction in malaria incidence (rate ratio 0.77 [95% CI 0.64-0.93], p=0.0073) and the LLIN plus BCC group showed 22% reduction (0.78 [0.63-0.96], p=0.020) compared to LLIN alone.

articles.headline: Surveillance system sensitivity determines malaria elimination confidence
articles.title_original: Evidence-based decision making for malaria elimination applying the Freedom From Infection statistical framework in five malaria eliminating countries: an observational study.
articles.authors: Stresman G, Nelli L, Wu L, Byrne I, Surendra H, Fernandez-Camacho B, Ruiz-Cabrejos J, Arana LB, Gonçalves AALM, Monteiro DDSR, Desir L, Ureña K, Tejada Beato MJ, Dumont E, Hill M, Grignard L, Elechosa S, Bunagan R, Xuan Thang N, Huong Binh NT, Hong Ngoc NT, Tetteh KKA, Noland GS, Hamre KES, da Veiga Leal S, DePina A, Thang N, Espino FE, Carrasco-Escobar G, Matthiopoulos J, Drakeley C
articles.details: This observational study of 1515 facilities across five countries applied the Freedom From Infection (FFI) framework to assess surveillance system sensitivity and probability of malaria freedom for P. falciparum and P. vivax. Using data from routine facilities, community health workers, and cross-sectional surveys of 10,767 individuals, only 841 of 1515 facilities (55.5%) for P. falciparum and 771 of 1455 facilities (53%) for P. vivax had sufficient surveillance sensitivity to achieve and maintain high probability of freedom. Strong systems were characterized by access to testing and treatment supplies, training on diagnostics and case management within 12 months, and shorter travel times to facilities.

articles.headline: Behavior change communication's role in malaria control
articles.title_original: Can behaviour change communication improve malaria control?
articles.authors: Sherrard-Smith E, Ngufor C
articles.details: This article examines whether behavior change communication can improve malaria control.

articles.headline: Robust surveillance systems essential for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: The road to malaria elimination: why a robust surveillance system is imperative.
articles.authors: Reithinger R
articles.details: This article discusses the importance of robust surveillance systems for achieving malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Novel pfDHFR inhibitors identified for drug-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Integrated computational strategies for the identification of Plasmodium falciparum dihydrofolate reductase inhibitors.
articles.authors: Gogoi D, Dutta PP, Gogoi B, Baruah VJ, Paul R, Sarmah B, Baruah A, Kakoti BB, Kalita B, Dutta P, Yadav RNS
articles.details: This integrated computational and in vitro study identified inhibitors targeting mutant P. falciparum dihydrofolate reductase (pfDHFR) to overcome antifolate resistance. Using pharmacophore modeling on 17 compounds with inhibitory activity spanning four orders of magnitude, researchers developed a model (r = 0.94) to screen chemical repositories with drug-likeness and ADME/Tox assessment. Twenty-eight compounds showed robust binding to resistant pfDHFR variants. The top candidate exhibited IC50 values of 30.822 μg/mL against 3D7 and 126.077 μg/mL against Dd2 strains (p < 0.05), significantly outperforming other compounds.

articles.headline: Stearylamine shows fast-acting antimalarial activity in vitro
articles.title_original: Cationic Lipid Stearylamine Displays Fast-Acting Antimalarial Action Against In Vitro Blood Stages of Chloroquine Susceptible and Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Strains.
articles.authors: Rajendran V, Gurukkalot K, Naveen NC
articles.details: This in vitro study evaluated the cationic lipid stearylamine (SA) against blood-stage P. falciparum using SYBR-Green I assay on drug-sensitive (Pf3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (PfINDO) strains. SA demonstrated fast-acting cytostatic activity with IC50 values for Pf3D7 of 2.17 µg/mL (12h), 1.97 µg/mL (24h), and 1.33 µg/mL (48h); for PfINDO: 3.50 µg/mL (12h), 2.76 µg/mL (24h), and 1.94 µg/mL (48h). Activity persisted through 96h (IC50s: 1.43 µg/mL for Pf3D7, 1.10 µg/mL for PfINDO). Cytocidal potential was confirmed with average IC50 of 1.68 µg/mL after brief exposure followed by recovery in drug-free media. SA induced ROS production with minimal hemolytic effects.

articles.headline: BioMalPar at 21: advancing malaria research and interventions
articles.title_original: BioMalPar at 21 - a coming of age for malaria research and interventions.
articles.authors: Aniweh Y, Dziekan J, Jore MM, Kho S, Mancio-Silva L, Merrick CJ, Nahrendorf W, Ralph SA, Vaughan AM, Verhoef JMJ
articles.details: This article reviews BioMalPar's 21-year contribution to malaria research and interventions.

articles.headline: Novel naphthoquinone hybrids potent against atovaquone-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: A dual In vitro and In silico approach to evaluate 1,4-naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant malaria.
articles.authors: Agarwal P, N'Da DD, van der Westhuizen CJ, van Zyl RL
articles.details: This dual in vitro and in silico study evaluated ten 1,4-naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant (FCR3) P. falciparum. Hybrids D12 and D13 exhibited antiplasmodial activities 61-fold and 52-fold more potent than atovaquone, respectively. Molecular modeling using a Y279S mutant model demonstrated strong correlation between in silico and in vitro results. Structure-activity relationship analysis identified key structural features for favorable binding. In silico predictions indicated good oral bioavailability and high gastrointestinal absorption with no significant toxicity risk, identifying D12 and D13 as promising lead candidates.

articles.headline: PCR diagnostic failure in mixed malaria infections
articles.title_original: Polymerase chain reaction fails to detect mixed malaria infections in siblings from Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Hall AD, Whitehurst DA, Mortensen JE, Prus K, Staat MA, Schlaudecker EP, Prasanphanich NS
articles.details: This case report describes two pediatric siblings from Ethiopia where PCR initially failed to detect P. vivax in what proved to be a P. falciparum/P. vivax co-infection. Peripheral blood smears eventually confirmed co-infection. The cases highlight limitations of current PCR-based diagnostics in detecting mixed infections, potentially leading to delayed or incomplete treatment, particularly in patients from co-endemic regions.

articles.headline: Species sanitation and structural resilience for malaria control
articles.title_original: Safeguarding malaria control gains in Africa through 'species sanitation' and structural resilience.
articles.authors: Okumu F, Odero JO, Charlwood JD, Knols BGJ, Monroe A
articles.details: This article proposes a two-tier strategy to safeguard malaria control gains in Africa, where few efficient Anopheles vectors drive most transmission. The first tier involves aggressive species sanitation targeting ecological vulnerabilities of primary vectors to suppress or eliminate them; the second tier builds structural resilience through improved housing and environmental management. Future innovations including gene-drive mosquitoes and longer-lasting vaccines could amplify impact. Implementation requires human-centered policies, increased domestic funding, cross-sector partnerships, and resilient health systems with planning extending beyond typical 5-year cycles.

articles.headline: Travel blog malaria advice inconsistent and commercially influenced
articles.title_original: Content analysis of malaria-related health information published in online travel blogs.
articles.authors: Din S, Bishay R, Asad U, Flaherty GT
articles.details: This content analysis of 100 online travel blogs found 92 contained malaria-related information with significant inconsistencies. Ten different antimalarial drugs were mentioned, five of which were not appropriate for P. falciparum prevention. Pre-travel consultation was recommended by approximately one-third of blogs. Most blogs were supported by commercial sponsors related to the travel industry or travel health products, indicating potential bias in the advice provided.

articles.headline: Intermediate forest cover increases malaria vector density in Amazon
articles.title_original: Intermediate forest cover and malaria risk in an Amazon deforestation frontier.
articles.authors: Laporta GZ, Valle D, Prist PR, Ilacqua RC, Santos TC, Madeira FP, Silva GM, Brilhante AF, Nolan MS, Sperança MA
articles.details: This pilot natural experiment across 40 sites in Acre state, Brazilian Amazon assessed how landscape structure influences malaria transmission by measuring mosquito vector abundance and infection rates across a gradient of forest cover and deforestation. Sites with intermediate forest cover associated with high fragmentation exhibited higher Nyssorhynchus abundance and greater anopheline infection rates, leading to increased risk of P. vivax and P. falciparum infections. All 16 human infections detected were asymptomatic, representing a persistent transmission reservoir.

articles.headline: Spontaneous splenic rupture in malaria: rare but serious complication
articles.title_original: Case series of spontaneous splenic rupture in patients with malaria: A rare surgical emergency in non-traumatic cases.
articles.authors: Tola GG, Hemba YG, Hussen SK, Matiop MP
articles.details: This case series reported four cases of spontaneous splenic rupture in patients with recent malaria treatment. One 30-year-old previously healthy male from Ethiopia presented with acute left upper quadrant abdominal pain, dizziness, and massive hemoperitoneum requiring emergency splenectomy. The remaining three patients were hemodynamically stable and managed successfully with conservative treatment including monitoring, supportive care, and serial imaging; none required surgery during follow-up. Malaria-associated splenic rupture results from vascular congestion, reticuloendothelial hyperplasia, and fragile splenic capsule.

articles.headline: Malaria develops resistance slower than HIV and TB
articles.title_original: A timeline of reckoning: Tracking the historical rise of antimicrobial resistance across HIV, TB, and malaria.
articles.authors: Williamson T, Adderley J, Quinn K, Clark TG, Lewin SR, Doerig C
articles.details: Comparative analysis of drug resistance timelines across HIV, TB, and malaria. Malaria exhibits slower resistance development to monotherapies compared to HIV and TB. Combination therapies reduce de novo resistance emergence, though pre-existing drug resistance remains a persistent challenge requiring continuous surveillance.

articles.headline: FREP1Q allele confers parasite resistance in Anopheles
articles.title_original: Driving a protective allele of the mosquito FREP1 gene to combat malaria.
articles.authors: Li Z, Dong Y, You L, Corder RM, Arzobal J, Yeun A, Yang L, Marshall JM, Dimopoulos G, Bier E
articles.details: Generated Anopheles stephensi congenic strains carrying protective FREP1Q224 or susceptible FREP1L224 alleles. FREP1Q224 confers robust resistance to both human and rodent malaria parasites with negligible fitness costs. Linked allelic-drive system efficiently drives FREP1Q224 into populations, rendering them refractory to parasite infection.

articles.headline: Dual-dye LAMP assay achieves 100% sensitivity for P. knowlesi
articles.title_original: Establishment of a visual mixed-dye loop mediated isothermal amplification assay for detecting Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.authors: Lai MY, Lau YL
articles.details: Novel dual-colour loop-mediated isothermal amplification assay for Plasmodium knowlesi detection using phenol red and hydroxynaphthol blue dyes. Tested on 55 clinical samples with nested PCR as reference. Demonstrated 100% sensitivity (95% CI: 91.9-100%) and 100% specificity (95% CI: 78.2-100%), offering rapid, cost-effective diagnosis for resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Seven MAA compounds inhibit falcipain-2 with no toxicity
articles.title_original: Screening mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) as potential inhibitors of a malaria-associated cysteine protease falcipain-2.
articles.authors: Singh VK, Sahu N, Kesheri M, Kanchan S, Kumar A, Sinha RP
articles.details: Molecular docking and in-silico screening of 27 mycosporine-like amino acid compounds as potential falcipain-2 inhibitors, a cysteine protease and haemoglobinase essential for P. falciparum erythrocytic development. Seven compounds showed favorable binding properties and no toxicity. Top candidates shinorine and mycosporine-hydroxyglutamicol demonstrated high stability in molecular dynamics simulations.

articles.headline: Microscopy detects more Plasmodium species than rapid tests
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of rapid diagnostic test and peripheral smear microscopy for malaria diagnosis.
articles.authors: Bhise M, Anandani G, Goswami P, Padhi A, Agarwal A
articles.details: Retrospective comparison of rapid diagnostic tests (RDT) and peripheral blood smear (PBS) microscopy in 150 suspected malaria cases. PBS identified 30 positives (20%); RDT detected 20 positives (13.33%), all as P. vivax. PBS detected both P. vivax and P. falciparum, while RDT failed to identify P. falciparum but detected two P. vivax cases missed by PBS.

articles.headline: Su_YN1 bacterium compatible with South American vector
articles.title_original: Compatibility of Serratia ureylitica Su_YN1, Malaria Transmission-Blocking Bacterium, with the Anopheles aquasalis Vector.
articles.authors: Ferreira MADS, Martins ESDR, Katak RM, Campos KMM, Rocha EM, Roque RA, Lalwani PJ, Silva LA, Carmo EJD, de Andrade PP, Wang S, Moreira LA, Jacobs-Lorena M, Ríos-Velásquez CM
articles.details: Evaluated Serratia ureylitica Su_YN1, a transmission-blocking bacterium that impairs P. falciparum and P. berghei development, for compatibility with Anopheles aquasalis. Bacterium did not affect mosquito longevity, blood feeding, fecundity, or fertility, supporting potential for malaria control implementation in South America.

articles.headline: Neonate with P. falciparum and P. vivax coinfection successfully treated
articles.title_original: Congenital malaria in a neonate born in a malaria-endemic area: a case report.
articles.authors: Andarge BD, Almaw K
articles.details: Case report of 48-hour-old male neonate born to 23-year-old Ethiopian primigravida treated with artemether-lumefantrine one week before delivery. Blood film microscopy confirmed coinfection with P. falciparum and P. vivax, parasite density 1120 parasites/μL, with respiratory distress and persistent fever. Successfully treated with intravenous artesunate followed by oral artemisinin-lumefantrine with complete clinical recovery.

articles.headline: Severe malaria and amoebic abscess coexist in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Management of a Pregnant Woman With Malaria, Stillbirth, and a 12 × 11-cm Amoebic Liver Abscess in a Burundian Hospital: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Castaldi MA, Castaldi SG
articles.details: Case report of pregnant woman at 22 weeks gestation in Burundi with concurrent falciparum malaria and 12×11-cm hepatic amoebic abscess resulting in fetal death in utero. First reported case of severe malaria and amoebic liver abscess coexistence in pregnancy. Successfully managed in rural hospital context through staff expertise and ultrasonographic technology, averting maternal mortality.

articles.headline: DTG co-administration reduces lumefantrine plasma concentrations
