articles.headline: Artificial intelligence advances malaria diagnosis and surveillance
articles.title_original: An insight into the role of artificial intelligence in combating malaria: recent developments.
articles.authors: Sachdeva H, Sharma M
articles.details: A literature review examining the application of artificial intelligence (AI) in malaria diagnosis, surveillance, treatment, and vector control. AI-driven solutions improve diagnostic accuracy, facilitate personalized medicine, accelerate drug discovery, and enhance forecasting of malaria transmission dynamics, particularly for drug-resistant parasites. The review emphasizes the potential of machine learning, deep learning, and in silico drug repurposing to improve malaria prevention and eradication with sustained innovation and investment.

articles.headline: Malaria reactive surveillance implementation shows timeliness challenges
articles.title_original: Evaluating malaria reactive surveillance and response strategies in northeast Cambodia: a mixed-methods study.
articles.authors: Win Han Oo, Siv S, Kaungmyat Khant, Htike W, Molyta MM, Lynn T, Aye Tun N, Agius PA, Fowkes FJI
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of reactive surveillance and response (1-3-7 strategy) implementation in Ratanakiri and Stung Treng provinces. In 2020 and 2022, 72% and 59% of cases were notified and investigated within one day, while reactive case detection timeliness was 89% and 45% respectively. Despite challenges including poor network coverage, heavy workload, and inadequate budget, the strategy was deemed acceptable. Improving timeliness to 100% requires increased budget allocation, capacity building, and better community engagement.

articles.headline: LDH-based malaria diagnostics effective when combined with HRP2
articles.title_original: Performance and usability evaluation of three LDH-based malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Kédougou, Senegal.
articles.authors: Sambe BS, Zobrist S, Sheahan W, Soni D, Diagne A, Sarr I, Diatta AS, Diaw SOM, Golden A, Slater H, Jang IK, Roa N, Pal S, Sarr FD, Faye J, Vigan-Womas I, Dieye Y, Cisse M, Domingo GJ, Niang M
articles.details: Cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study of three LDH-based rapid diagnostic tests in febrile patients. Among 220 participants with 70% Pf-positive by PCR, the Pf (pLDH/HRPII) test achieved 78% sensitivity (95% CI 70.9-84.5%) and 89% specificity (95% CI 79.4-95.6%), outperforming microscopy (53% sensitivity) and the LDH lines alone (61-64% sensitivity). The HRP2 line contributed significantly to sensitivity. In populations with low hrp2/hrp3 deletion rates, the PfLDH line alone cannot fully compensate for HRP2 performance.

articles.headline: Delayed treatment and high parasitemia drive severe malaria hospitalization
articles.title_original: Factors associated with hospitalizations due to severe malaria in the non-endemic Brazilian region: a case-control study in the extra-Amazon Region from 2011 to 2019.
articles.authors: da Silva AC, Duarte EC, Marchesini PB, Viana GMR, Ramalho WM, Garcia KKS
articles.details: Case-control study comparing hospitalized malaria patients to non-hospitalized cases using national surveillance data from 2011 to 2019. Key risk factors for hospitalization included high parasite count (>100,000/mm³, OR 3.15), delayed treatment of 8+ days (OR 2.08), low education level (elementary, OR 2.21), and infection outside the region. Active case detection was protective (OR 0.65). The findings reveal that delayed treatment and socioeconomic disparities significantly increase severe malaria risk.

articles.headline: Ethnomedicinal plants for malaria: 40 species from Odisha reviewed
articles.title_original: A review on the ethnomedicinal plants used for the traditional treatment of malaria in the Mayurbhanj district of Odisha, India.
articles.authors: Behera SK, Subudhi L, Mohapatra S, Panda AK
articles.details: Systematic review identified 40 plant species traditionally used for malaria treatment in Mayurbhanj, Odisha, belonging to 24 families with Acanthaceae and Apocyanaceae predominant. Formulations were primarily oral (decoction, paste, juice, powder), with leaves and roots as main plant parts. No side effects were reported in the literature reviewed. The review documents traditional knowledge but highlights the need for further research to understand therapeutic mechanisms and efficacy.

articles.headline: Cameroon Plasmodium falciparum shows high genetic diversity and complex structure
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity and Phylogenetic Relatedness of Cameroonian Plasmodium falciparum Isolates and Comparative Analysis with Global Populations.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Hawadak J, Singh V
articles.details: Genetic analysis of P. falciparum isolates from three regions of Cameroon (Littoral, North, Far North) using msp1/2 markers evaluated allelic family diversity. K1 and MAD20 families showed high structural diversity through 3-amino acid repeats, while RO33 presented mutation points. Phylogenetic analysis revealed K1 and RO33 sequences closest to China/Myanmar populations, while MAD20, IC/3D7, and FC27 were closer to India, Panama, and Papua New Guinea. The Cameroonian isolates demonstrated high genetic structure consistent with complex population diversity.

articles.headline: Ookluc transgenic parasite enables high-throughput transmission-blocking compound screening
articles.title_original: Ookluc: A Plasmodium berghei Line for Identifying Transmission-blocking Compounds.
articles.authors: Novais JT, Calit J, Morosini LG, da Silva VA, Marques RF, Gaitán XA, Bargieri DY
articles.details: Development of Ookluc, a transgenic P. berghei line expressing nanoluciferase exclusively after zygote formation with peak luminescence during ookinete development. This luminescence-based assay provides quantitative readout of gamete-to-zygote and zygote-to-ookinete differentiation. The tool addresses challenges in studying parasite sexual stages and enables high-throughput screening for transmission-blocking compounds targeting gametocytes and post-fertilization stages.

articles.headline: Mathematical model favors treating migrant cases over deportation for malaria control
articles.title_original: A meta-population model of malaria with asymptomatic cases, transmission blocking drugs, migration and screening.
articles.authors: Tchoumi SY, Banasiak J, Ouifki R
articles.details: Two-patch mathematical model of malaria transmission incorporating migration, asymptomatic cases, and transmission-blocking drugs (TBDs) with waning immunity. The model analyzed entry policies for infected migrants in a better-resourced site with TBD availability. Numerical simulations demonstrated that malaria control was improved by allowing entry of detected cases and administering TBD treatment rather than deporting infected individuals and risking illegal reentry.

articles.headline: P. vivax dbp and ebp2 gene amplification varies across Amazon regions
articles.title_original: The pattern of gene amplification of members of the Plasmodium vivax erythrocyte binding-like proteins family across the Amazon rainforest.
articles.authors: Guedes KS, Fernandes GM, Sanchez BAM, Ntumngia FB, Adams JH, Kano FS, Fontes CJF, Sousa TN, Carvalho LH
articles.details: Genetic analysis of 192 P. vivax isolates from three Amazon regions assessed copy number variation (CNV) in dbp and ebp2 genes. Western region isolates showed 25% dbp amplification (up to 8 copies) compared to 2-9% in eastern and southwestern regions (2-3 copies). ebp2 amplification was detected in 15% of western isolates versus 1-4% in other regions. No association was observed between CNVs and host DARC genotypes despite all individuals being DARC positive.

articles.headline: Parasitemia patterns predict cerebral malaria development with 97% sensitivity in mice
articles.title_original: Temporal Parasitemia Trends Predict Risk and Timing of Experimental Cerebral Malaria in Mice Infected by Plasmodium berghei ANKA.
articles.authors: Murin PJ, Daniel-Ribeiro CT, Carvalho LJM, Martins YC
articles.details: Experimental study using machine learning to predict cerebral malaria risk in Plasmodium berghei ANKA-infected mice across three strains (C57BL/6, CBA, Swiss Webster). Among mice studied, 64.5% developed experimental cerebral malaria with onset from day 5-11. Early parasitemia increases ≥0.05 between day 1-3 predicted cerebral malaria development with 97% sensitivity. A Random Forest model predicted exact cerebral malaria onset day with high precision (mean absolute error 0.43, R² 0.64).

articles.headline: Scoping review protocol on gene drive mosquitoes and stakeholder perspectives
articles.title_original: Knowledge, Perspectives, and Risks Perceptions on Gene Drive and Genetically Modified Mosquitoes for Malaria Control among African Stakeholders: A Scoping Review Protocol.
articles.authors: Sogodogo A, Diarra S, Keita S, Diallo I, Hartley S, Telly N, Dolo H, Sanogo D, Kané F, Tangara CO, Diakité M, Sangho O, Kayentao K, Fritz H, Winch PJ, Doumbia S
articles.details: This scoping review protocol (registered on OSF) aims to systematically examine knowledge, perspectives, and risk perceptions of African stakeholders regarding gene drive and genetically modified mosquitoes for malaria control. The study will search multiple databases for peer-reviewed and grey literature using two-stage independent screening and thematic analysis. Results will inform community engagement strategies crucial for successful deployment of these technologies.

articles.headline: Plasma biomarkers identify Plasmodium falciparum infection in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Plasma protein biomarkers of Plasmodium falciparum infection in pregnant women: a high-throughput proteomics study.
articles.authors: Kanoi BN, Waweru H, Kobia FM, Mukala J, Kirira P, Mogere D, Gallini R, Åberg M, Vatish M, Gitaka J, Kamali-Moghaddam M
articles.details: High-throughput proteomics study using proximity extension assay screened plasma from malaria-exposed pregnant women in sub-Saharan Africa to identify biomarkers of P. falciparum infection. Malaria-positive pregnancies showed elevated FCGR2B and heme oxygenase-1, while neurturin and IL-20 were downregulated. IL-20 emerged as the top candidate biomarker warranting validation in larger cohorts. These biomarkers could enable diagnosis of placental malaria infection, which currently requires postnatal histopathology.

articles.headline: Antigen genetic diversity manageable for P. vivax serological exposure markers
articles.title_original: Navigating parasite antigen genetic diversity in the design of Plasmodium vivax serological exposure markers for malaria.
articles.authors: Bareng P, Wu KW, Takashima E, Argyropoulos D, Smith L, Naung M, Mazhari R, Schoffer K, Kiernan-Walker N, Abraham A, Lamont M, Mehra S, Lim P, Sattabongkot J, Monteiro W, Lacerda M, Healer J, Chitnis CE, Tham WH, Tsuboi T, Mueller I, Barry AE, Longley RJ
articles.details: Analysis of global P. vivax genetic data examined how antigen diversity in serological markers affects utility for detecting recent infection and hypnozoite carriage. Testing new antigen haplotypes in Brazil and Thailand cohorts found that while extensive genetic diversity existed (0.4-99% sequence identity to reference Sal-1), most of 8 antigens effectively identified recent exposure despite variation. For antigens where diversity affected performance (e.g., RBP2a), adding multiple antigens to classification models recovered accuracy.

articles.headline: R21/Matrix-M vaccine induces diverse antibody and T cell responses
articles.title_original: R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine drives diverse immune responses in pre-exposed adults: insights from a phase IIb controlled human malaria infection trial.
articles.authors: Kibwana E, Bundi C, Kimani D, Nyamako L, Keter K, Mutiso A, Ogwang R, Bellamy D, Rapi K, Bajer A, Provstgaard-Morys S, Stockdale L, Munoz O, Datoo MS, Lawrie A, Ramos-Lopez F, Roberts R, Hamaluba M, Hill AVS, Bejon P, Ewer KJ, Kapulu M
articles.details: Phase IIb controlled human malaria infection trial in healthy adults from coastal Kenya (n=19 vaccinated, 17 challenged). R21/Matrix-M vaccination increased antibody titers across IgA, IgM, and IgG isotypes and IgG1/IgG3 subclasses with complement-fixing capabilities, maintained over time. Vaccination boosted IgG+ and IgA+ memory B cells with short-lived IgA response. Increased CXCR5+/PD1+ T follicular helper cells with predominantly Th2/Th17 population.

articles.headline: P. vivax malaria with thrombocytopenia reveals underlying chronic lymphocytic leukemia
articles.title_original: Underlying Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia in a Case of Vivax Malaria With Thrombocytopenia: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Saha A, Mukhopadhyay M, Talapatra A, Bandhopadhay T, Sardar S
articles.details: Case report of 64-year-old male with fever, petechial rash, and mucosal hemorrhage presenting with severe thrombocytopenia (platelet count 15,000/mm³), leukocytosis (TLC 29,100/μL), and coagulopathy (INR 4.2). Blood smear positive for P. vivax; flow cytometry revealed underlying chronic lymphocytic leukemia.

articles.headline: Invasive black rats harbor rodent malaria parasite in Gabon
articles.title_original: Rodent malaria parasites detected in the invasive Rattus rattus in Gabon.
articles.authors: Mbou-Boutambe C, Boundenga L, Degrugillier F, Gauthier P, Arnathau C, Rivero A, Granjon L, Rougeron V, Prugnolle F
articles.details: Molecular screening of 527 rodents captured in rural villages (2021-2022) identified two invasive R. rattus individuals positive for P. yoelii. Phylogenetic analysis confirmed identity with strains previously isolated from native rodents in the region. Despite low infection prevalence (0.38%), findings suggest invasive rodents may integrate into local Plasmodium transmission networks.

articles.headline: Rapid multiplex malaria diagnosis using LAMP-MS within 40 minutes
articles.title_original: Rapid and Multiplex Diagnosis of Malaria Using Chelex-100 Extraction and LAMP-MS Assay.
articles.authors: Lim MS, Choe YL, Jang WS
articles.details: Novel diagnostic platform combining Chelex-100 resin-based DNA extraction with multiplex loop-mediated isothermal amplification microscanner (LAMP-MS) assay. Enables simultaneous detection of P. falciparum, P. vivax, pan-malaria, and internal control within 40 minutes from extraction to result. Demonstrates sensitivity and specificity comparable to traditional PCR-based diagnostics with simplified workflow suitable for resource-constrained settings.

articles.headline: P. falciparum alveolin proteins regulate cytoskeletal structure via domain interactions
articles.title_original: Malaria Cytoskeletal Proteins Require Alveolin-Alveolin Interactions for Differential Localization: Recruitment and Organization of Alveolin Proteins.
articles.authors: Cepeda Diaz AK, Back PS, Sreenivasamurthy SK, Dvorin JD
articles.details: Biochemical characterization of four P. falciparum alveolin cytoskeletal proteins examining protein-protein interactions and recruitment to cytoskeletal substructures. PfIMC1e sequentially requires PfIMC1f for basal complex recruitment; PfIMC1c and PfIMC1g localize properly without mutual alveolin interactions. Alveolin domains contain unique signatures for specialized compartment-specific localization and are not interchangeable.

articles.headline: Malaria hemozoin impairs dendritic cell response to bacterial infections
articles.title_original: Malaria-derived hemozoin skews dendritic cell responses to bacterial infections by reducing interferon gene-transcription by SWI/SNF-NuRD.
articles.authors: Lasaviciute G, Tariq K, Sugathan A, Quin J, Polenkowski M, Bujila I, Skorokhod O, Troye-Blomberg M, Sverremark-Ekström E, Östlund Farrants AK
articles.details: Study examined how malaria-derived hemozoin (HZ) co-exposed with bacterial lipopolysaccharide (LPS) alters monocyte-derived dendritic cell responses. Co-exposure reduced expression of IFNγ-controlled genes, HLA-DR, and PD-L1 through chromatin remodeling complex NuRD and decreased NF-κB RELA binding. NuRD replaced the SWI/SNF variant PBAF at specific promoters without chromatin accessibility changes, potentially altering immune responses to bacterial co-infections.

articles.headline: Infant malaria antibody affinity evolves differently by parasite stage
articles.title_original: Development of high affinity antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum merozoite and sporozoite antigens during infancy and adulthood.
articles.authors: Lugaajju A, Idro R, Kiwuwa S, Beeson JG, Drew DR, Mortazavi SE, Linse S, Persson KEM
articles.details: Longitudinal study in Uganda followed newborn infants and mothers for 9 months measuring antibody affinity (dissociation rate constant, kd) to P. falciparum antigens. Affinity against merozoite antigens AMA1 and MSP2 decreased from birth to 6 months, then increased to 9 months but did not reach maternal levels. Affinity against sporozoite antigen CSP remained unchanged throughout study period.

articles.headline: Insecticide-treated nets reduce falciparum malaria risk by 34% in African children
articles.title_original: Effects of vector control interventions on spatio-temporal changes of falciparum malaria risk in children aged 2-10 in sub-Saharan African regions during 2011-2020.
articles.authors: Chol CJ, Belay DB, Fenta HM, Chen DG
articles.details: Spatiotemporal Bayesian regression analysis of P. falciparum prevalence data from 634 administrative areas in 45 sub-Saharan African countries (2011-2020). Prevalence in children aged 2-10 declined from 21.32% (2011) to 16.75% (2016) with slight resurgence in 2017. Each unit increase in insecticide-treated net utilization annually associated with 34.07% reduction in malaria infection risk.

articles.headline: Malaria terminology evolved through China's medical modernization
articles.title_original: Malaria in China: a discourse-historical perspective.
articles.authors: Miao P
articles.details: Historical discourse analysis of malaria conceptualization in late Qing and Republican China using English-Chinese dictionaries and periodicals. Three phases identified: early missionary dictionaries (1822-1860s) emphasized symptom-based translations; 1870s-1920s period witnessed terminological competition between 'nueji' and 'zhangqi' reflecting tensions between traditional Chinese and western parasitological frameworks; by 1930s-1940s 'nueji' became institutionally dominant while Plasmodium was lexicalized as 'nueyuanchong' yet immunity mechanisms remained unexplained.

articles.headline: P. vivax causes rare hemophagocytic syndrome with pancytopenia in pregnancy
articles.title_original: A Rare Presentation of Plasmodium vivax Malaria: Pancytopenia due to Hemophagocytic Syndrome in a Pregnant Woman.
articles.authors: Lema GD, Dagnaw AB, Gebeyaw ED
articles.details: Case report of 22-year-old pregnant woman (9 weeks gestation) from Debre Berhan, Ethiopia presenting with fever, headache, nausea, vomiting, and fatigue. Clinical examination revealed hypotension, pale conjunctiva, hepatosplenomegaly, and pancytopenia with elevated serum ferritin and triglycerides. Blood smear confirmed P. vivax (trophozoite and gametocyte stages); diagnosed with hemophagocytic syndrome; achieved full recovery with antimalarial and supportive treatment.

articles.headline: Vivax malaria recurrence prevalent despite standard treatment in Indonesia
articles.title_original: Postmalaria elimination: Overview of recurrent malaria cases in Riau Province, Indonesia.
articles.authors: Lesmana SD, Afandi D, Rosdiana D, Murtono H, Azzuhra MN, Panjaitan GR
articles.details: Retrospective descriptive study of 4,622 malaria cases in Riau Province (2019-2022) identified 232 recurrent malaria cases occurring >28 days post-treatment. Could not definitively distinguish relapse from reinfection. Recurrent cases predominantly P. vivax (94.83%, n=220) with smaller P. falciparum proportion (4.74%, n=11) and one mixed infection. All cases received standard treatment; however, 73.45% did not receive complete after-treatment evaluation.

articles.headline: Malaria elevates inflammatory markers with nutritional and hematologic dysfunction
articles.title_original: Analysis of the relationship between malaria and inflammatory biomarker levels in children in rural Iraq: Impact of nutritional deficiencies, anemia, and hematological disorders.
articles.authors: Al-Sultany AK, Al-Mamoori ZZ, Kareem MN, Mohsein OA
articles.details: Case-control study of 150 malaria-infected children vs 50 controls (age 5-8, rural Iraq, 2023-2025). Malaria significantly elevated inflammatory markers: CRP (18.4 vs 5.2 mg/L, P<0.001), IL-6 (10.2 vs 3.1 pg/mL, P<0.001), TNF-α (12.5 vs 4.0 pg/mL, P<0.001). Malaria cases showed lower hemoglobin (9.8 vs 12.5 g/dL, P<0.001), iron (45.3 vs 90.2 μg/dL, P<0.001), and vitamin D/zinc levels with higher malnutrition (65%), anemia (70%), and blood disorders (40%).

articles.headline: Plasmodium detected in cattle but not humans in Indonesia study
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium spp. in humans and cattle: Implications for zoonotic malaria transmission in Indonesia.
articles.authors: Hasyim H, Jassey B, Yudhastuti R, Misnaniarti M, Liberty IA, Sunarsih E, Priyanto L, Dalilah D, Yusri Y, Taufiq FH, Maharani FE, Hakim L, Herlinda S
articles.details: Cross-sectional study from March–July 2024 involving 41 humans and 43 cattle in Indonesia, tested by PCR. All 41 human samples were negative (0%, 95% CI: 0.0%–8.5%), while 1 of 43 cattle samples tested positive for Plasmodium (2.33%, 95% CI: 0.06%–12.0%). Findings suggest cattle may serve as parasite reservoirs, warranting integrated animal health surveillance.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine treatment failure in imported P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: [Successful Treatment of Two Cases of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria that Developed Recrudescence after Artemether-Lumefantrine Therapy].
articles.authors: Arslan M, Berberoğlu U
articles.details: Case report of two P. falciparum-infected men returning from endemic countries (Gabon and Uganda) treated with artemether-lumefantrine. Both showed recrudescence with high fever and chills after initial treatment. One was cleared after six doses of artemether-lumefantrine; the other required supplementary quinine and doxycycline treatment. Cases illustrate treatment failure risk despite initial parasitemia clearance.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria: epidemiology, pathogenesis, clinical care, and sequelae
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria.
articles.authors: Postels DG, Katangwe-Chirwa T
articles.details: Review of cerebral malaria as the most lethal P. falciparum form, clinically defined as unexplained coma with parasitemia. Case fatality is 1 in 6 children, with many survivors experiencing neurologic, cognitive, or behavioral sequelae. Acute seizures are common and associated with worse outcomes. Post-discharge care gaps exist for educational challenges, behavioral abnormalities, and epilepsy management.

articles.headline: Plant-based silver nanoparticles show larvicidal activity against vectors
articles.title_original: Eco-Friendly Synthesis of Silver Nanoparticles and Its Larvicidal Property Against Malaria, Filariasis, and Dengue Vectors.
articles.authors: Raguvaran K, Kalpana M, Devapriya P, Kalaivani S, Angelina M, Krismastuti FSH, Rasool A, Maheswaran R
articles.details: Green synthesis of silver nanoparticles using Aerva lanata flower extract tested against three mosquito species. Larvicidal LC50 values were 6.501 ppm for Cx. quinquefasciatus, 20.875 ppm for An. stephensi, and 22.649 ppm for Ae. aegypti. Oxidative stress was identified as the mechanism of action. Biosafety testing showed minimal toxicity to Artemia salina.

articles.headline: In silico multi-epitope vaccine targeting blood-stage P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Development of a multi-epitope vaccine candidate targeting blood-stage of malaria through immunoinformatics approach.
articles.authors: Mandal S, Khushi, Chanu WP, Srivastava R, Patgiri SJ, Natarajaseenivasan K
articles.details: Immunoinformatics pipeline identified 30 vaccine-fit epitopes from 133 candidates targeting four P. falciparum proteins (PfPHB1, PfPHB2, PfHSP70, PfGARP). Final vaccine construct comprised 570 amino acids including 12 B-cell, 11 CTL, and 7 HTL epitopes. Predicted 95.04% global population coverage with strong TLR5 binding. In silico immune simulation predicted robust immune response.

articles.headline: P. simium genomics reveal zoonotic origin and ongoing transmission
articles.title_original: Plasmodium simium: birth and evolution of a zoonotic malaria parasite species.
articles.authors: de Albuquerque NRM, Ladeia WA, Scalsky RJ, Dwivedi A, Stabler TC, Rodrigues PT, de Oliveira TC, Silva JC, Ferreira MU
articles.details: Whole-genome analysis of 31 P. simium isolates and 1,086 P. vivax isolates revealed P. simium as a discrete lineage with greatest similarity to P. vivax from Latin America and Brazil. P. simium displays half the nucleotide diversity of P. vivax, consistent with recent origin. Sympatric isolates from monkeys and humans as closely related as half-siblings indicate ongoing zoonotic transmission in Serra do Mar region.

articles.headline: Pf8: Genome variation database of 33,325 P. falciparum samples
articles.title_original: Pf8: an open dataset of Plasmodium falciparum genome variation in 33,325 worldwide samples.
articles.authors: Abdel Hamid MM, Abdelraheem MH, Acheampong DO, Adam I, Aide P, Ajibaye O, Ali M, Almagro-Garcia J, Amambua-Ngwa A, Amenga-Etego L, Aniebo I, Aninagyei E, Ansah F, Apinjoh TO, Ariani CV, Auburn S, Awandare GA, Balmer A, Bejon P, Boene S, Bwire G, Candrinho B, Chidimatembue A, Chindavongsa K, Comiche K, Conway D, Dara A, Diakite M, Djimde A, Dondorp A, Doumbia S, Drury E, Fanello CA, Ferdig M, Figueroa K, Gamboa D, Golassa L, Gonçalves S, Guindo MDA, Hamaluba M, Hanboonkunupakarn B, Howe K, Hussien M, Imwong M, Ishengoma D, Jeans J, Kabaghe A, Kamuhabwa A, Kindermans JM, Konate DS, Kwiatkowski DP, Lee C, Lee SK, Lee SJ, Ley B, Llanos-Cuentas A, Marfurt J, Matambisso G, Maude RR, Maude RJ, Mayor A, Mayxay M, Maïga-Ascofaré O, McCann RS, Miles A, Miotto O, Mohamed AO, Morang'a CM, Murie K, Ngasala BE, Nguyen TN, Nolasco O, Nosten F, Noviyanti R, O'Connor Í, Oboh M, Ochola-Oyier LI, Olufunke Falade C, Olukosi A, Olumide A, Olusola FI, Onyamboko MA, Oriero EC, Oyibo WA, Pannebaker D, Pearson RD, Phiri K, van der Pluijm RW, Price RN, Quang HH, Rajkumar Devaraju V, Randrianarivelojosia M, Ranford-Cartwright L, Rayner JC, Rovira-Vallbona E, Rowlands K, Ruano-Rubio V, Sanchez JF, Saúte F, Shettima S, da Silva C, Simpson VJ, Suddaby S, Takken W, Thu AM, Toure M, Unlu E, Valdivia HO, van Vugt M, Waithira N, Wellems T, Wendler J, White N, Wuendrich Ogidan R
articles.details: MalariaGEN's Pf8 release provides open-access curated genome variation data from 33,325 P. falciparum samples across 99 studies and 122 locations over 50+ years. Dataset includes 12 million genomic positions with drug-resistance markers: CNV calls in gch1, crt, mdr1, and plasmepsin2/3, plus hrp2/hrp3 deletions associated with RDT failures. Largest open eukaryotic genome variation dataset with web apps and Python tools.

articles.headline: Giovanni Battista Grassi and the history of malaria research
articles.title_original: Giovanni Battista Grassi (1854-1925): a forgotten Italian scholar and his fundamental studies on malaria.
articles.authors: Martini M, Orsini D
articles.details: Historical review of Italian scientist Giovanni Battista Grassi and his contributions to malariology. Grassi worked after Laveran's identification of Plasmodium parasites as protozoa, contributing to understanding malaria transmission vectors and parasite species classification. Article places Grassi among founders of the Italian school of malariology.

articles.headline: P. cynomolgi misdiagnosed as P. vivax in zoonotic co-infection
articles.title_original: Molecular and Microscopic Challenges in Detecting Plasmodium cynomolgi Co-Infections with Plasmodium vivax: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Yaacob MA, Radin Hisam RS, Parina Ismail N, Md Yusuf NA, Rubio Muñoz JM, Hashim S, Zhueng TJ
articles.details: Case report of a 32-year-old male with mixed P. vivax and P. cynomolgi infection initially misidentified as P. vivax alone by microscopy and qPCR. Co-infection was confirmed by real-time PCR and sequencing. Diagnostic challenge reflects morphological similarities and genetic homology between species. Case highlights need for improved molecular diagnostics as P. cynomolgi emerges as significant zoonotic threat.

articles.headline: Brucea javanica extracts inhibit P. falciparum with additive effects
articles.title_original: Growth Inhibition and Additive Effect to Antimalarial Drugs of Brucea javanica Extracts on Asexual Blood-Stage Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Kangwanrangsan N, Niramolyanun G, Praikongkatham C, Chantree P, Martviset P, Pankao V
articles.details: In vitro study of plant extracts against asexual blood-stage P. falciparum. Root extract showed IC50 of 0.41 ± 1.14 µg/mL; fruit extract IC50 of 0.26 ± 1.15 µg/mL. Extracts arrested parasite development at ring stage and caused nuclear clumping and pyknotic cell death. Root and fruit extracts demonstrated additive effects with artesunate and chloroquine, significantly reducing IC90 levels.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr-treated nets reduce malaria more than pyrethroid alone
articles.title_original: Effectiveness and Efficacy of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Nets for Malaria Control in Africa: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.authors: Demissie DB, Fetensa G, Desta T, Tiyare FT
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 randomized controlled trials involving 21,916 households evaluated LLIN effectiveness. Chlorfenapyr-treated nets reduced malaria prevalence to 25.96 per 100 children versus 41.60 per 100 in control pyrethroid-only nets (10% relative risk reduction). Piperonyl butoxide nets achieved 32.38 per 100. Chlorfenapyr reduced vector density 23%, compared to 7% for pyrethroid-only and 12% for piperonyl butoxide.

articles.headline: Immunoinformatics-designed vaccine from P. falciparum antigens
articles.title_original: Search continues: Exploring immunoinformatics platforms for designing an effective multiepitope malaria vaccine candidate.
articles.authors: Osuji C, Etuk-Udo G
articles.details: In silico structural bioinformatics pipeline designed multiepitope vaccine targeting variant surface antigens expressed during P. falciparum life cycle. Thirty vaccine-fit epitopes were selected from pool of 133, creating final construct with 570 amino acids (12 B-cell, 11 CTL, 7 HTL epitopes). Predicted 95.04% global population coverage with strong molecular binding and simulated robust immune response.

articles.headline: P. vivax proteins interact with Basigin for red cell entry
articles.title_original: Majority of the erythrocyte binding proteins of the Pvfam "a" family of Plasmodium vivax interact with Basigin to assist parasite entry into the host cell.
articles.authors: Tripathi M, Santoshi M, Sharma YD, Rathore S
articles.details: Study used solid-phase binding assay and surface plasmon resonance to demonstrate that 7 of 10 Pvfam "a" family erythrocyte binding proteins (PvTRAg, PvTRAg33.5, PvTRAg35.2, PvTRAg34, PvTRAg36, PvTRAg38, PvTRAg69.4) interact with erythrocyte receptor Basigin. Each protein reduced parasite growth in culture; combinations showed higher parasite growth inhibition rates than individual proteins, suggesting multiple parasite ligands interacting with the same erythrocyte receptor is important for invasion.

articles.headline: CD8+ T cells drive tubule-interstitial damage in malaria kidney disease
articles.title_original: CD8(+) T cells promote tubule-interstitial damage in malaria-induced acute kidney injury.
articles.authors: Teixeira DE, Alves SADS, Pinheiro AS, Silva LS, Silva-Aguiar RP, Peruchetti DB, Pádua TA, Souza MC, Caruso-Neves C, Pinheiro AAS
articles.details: Using adoptive transfer, antibody depletion, and FTY720 treatment in mice, researchers investigated CD8+ T cells' role in malaria-induced acute kidney injury (MAKI) pathogenesis. Transfer of malaria-infected donor T cells reproduced tubule-interstitial damage in recipients with increased CD8+ T cell accumulation in kidneys. Selective CD8+ depletion protected from tubular injury but not glomerular changes; FTY720 prevented proteinuria and reduced urinary γ-glutamyl transferase (γ-GT), a marker of tubular injury, but did not alter glomerular markers. Results demonstrate CD8+ T cells specifically drive tubule-interstitial injury in MAKI.

articles.headline: Machine learning tool automates malaria oocyst counting and analysis
articles.title_original: OocystMeter, a machine-learning algorithm to count and measure Plasmodium oocysts, reveals clustering patterns in the Anopheles midgut.
articles.authors: Peng D, Marrogi E, Nelson E, Liu Q, Rinvee TA, de Vries LE, Thornburg K, Singh N, Shaw WR, Catteruccia F
articles.details: OocystMeter is a Mask R-CNN system trained on 11,178 P. falciparum oocysts in A. gambiae midguts annotated by expert parasitologists, achieving parasitologist-level accuracy (Spearman correlation 0.998 for oocyst counts, 0.978 for size measurements). Spatial analysis revealed non-random clustering independent of midgut anatomy, particularly in infections with fewer than 75 oocysts/midgut. The tool accelerates analysis, reduces human bias, and provides spatial coordinates for advanced parasitology studies, freely available as both software and web tool.

articles.headline: mRNA vaccine provides complete protection against lethal P. yoelii
articles.title_original: An mRNA Vaccine Expressing Blood-Stage Malaria Antigens Induces Complete Protection Against Lethal Plasmodium yoelii.
articles.authors: Ott AC, Loll PJ, Burns JM Jr
articles.details: Two mRNA vaccines encoding PyMSP119 fused to PyMSP8 were tested: one for protein secretion (PyMSP1/8-sec) and one for membrane-bound antigen (PyMSP1/8-mem). Immunized outbred mice were challenged with lethal P. yoelii 17XL parasitized red blood cells. PyMSP1/8-sec achieved 100% survival with 50% of animals showing no detectable parasitemia (<0.01%), and mean peak parasitemia significantly lower than recombinant vaccine control. PyMSP1/8-mem provided even higher protection with more animals achieving undetectable parasitemia.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling evaluates vaccination with malaria prevention strategies
articles.title_original: Vaccination and combined optimal control measures for malaria prevention and spread mitigation.
articles.authors: Eme KA, Kamrujjaman M, Alam M, Ali MA
articles.details: A deterministic mathematical model analyzed malaria transmission dynamics and intervention effectiveness, including sensitivity analysis of the basic reproduction number (R0). Equilibrium analysis identified key parameters impacting R0. Findings indicate that although current vaccines are not 100% effective, vaccination could significantly reduce transmission when combined with other strategies like mosquito nets and insecticide spraying. Results emphasize the importance of comprehensive multi-strategy approaches for effective malaria control.

articles.headline: Ivermectin mass drug administration cost-effective across Africa
articles.title_original: Wonder drugs and where to use them: a forecast of ivermectin's impact on malaria in Africa.
articles.authors: Greenhalgh S, Roman A, Provencher A
articles.details: Using malaria transmission data from 41 sub-Saharan African countries, researchers developed a mathematical model to evaluate mass drug administration (MDA) of ivermectin informed by a Burkina Faso study showing transmission inhibition and mosquito killing. Annual MDA scenarios at 12.5%, 25%, and 50% population coverage averted 248.7, 261.4, and 288.7 malaria incidences per thousand people respectively, and saved 5.4, 5.7, and 6.3 disability-adjusted life years. MDA was cost-effective in 41, 18, and 6 countries for the 12.5%, 25%, and 50% scenarios respectively.

articles.headline: P. vivax circumsporozoite protein shows high repeat polymorphism
articles.title_original: Evolution of tandem repeats in putative circumsporozoite protein to enhance its function: A recent and exclusive event in Plasmodium vivax in India.
articles.authors: Dash M, Pande V, Das A, Sinha A
articles.details: Sequencing of putative circumsporozoite protein (PvpuCSP) in 71 P. vivax isolates from endemic India revealed 37 different alleles with extensive repeat region length polymorphism (1.8–2.2 kb), while N- and C-terminal regions were well-conserved (~80% of gene). Episodic diversifying selection in the repeat region was supported by statistically significant p-values, contributing to amino acid diversity at various phylogenetic levels. High repeat polymorphism makes PvpuCSP a potential marker for estimating genetic diversity and infection complexity in P. vivax populations.

articles.headline: Acridone compound T111 targets all three Plasmodium life stages
articles.title_original: Potent Acridone Antimalarial against All Three Life Stages of Plasmodium.
articles.authors: Kancharla P, Dodean RA, Li Y, Zhang X, Kelly S, Charlton J, Binauhan A, Garcia L, Caridha D, Madejczyk MS, Jin X, Dennis WE, Kudyba K, McEnearney S, Lee PJ, Blount C, DeLuca J, Vuong C, Pannone K, Dinh HT, Mdaki K, Leed S, Martin ML, Pybus BS, Chimong A, Cui L, Vega-Rodriguez J, Okitwi M, Kreutzfeld O, Docan C, Green C, Liu Y, Agulay A, Mirsalis J, Farrell SN, McFadden GI, Goodman CD, Probst AS, Nilsen A, Catteruccia F, Rosenthal PJ, Riscoe M, Cooper R, Roth A, Kelly J
articles.details: Acridone T111 was tested against blood, liver, and mosquito-stage parasites using in vitro, ex vivo, and in vivo assays. Oral single-dose administration achieved cure in a blood-stage rodent model and inhibited sexual parasites; T111 showed activity against relapsing parasites in non-human primate liver cells and prevented parasite development in mosquitoes. T111 showed synergy with tafenoquine and inhibited mitochondrial electron transport chain with a mechanism distinct from other antimalarials, with favorable therapeutic index in rat toxicology studies.

articles.headline: Generative AI reveals population genetics of malaria vectors
articles.title_original: On the use of generative models for evolutionary inference of malaria vectors from genomic data.
articles.authors: Eneli AA, Siu PC, Perez MF, Burt A, Fumagalli M, Mathieson S
articles.details: Unsupervised machine learning quantified the genetic structure of Anopheles gambiae complex populations across sub-Saharan Africa. A novel generative deep learning algorithm inferred the joint evolutionary history of populations sampled in Guinea and Burkina Faso, West Africa. A migration model fit better than a non-migration model, with the generative approach outperforming summary-statistics methods in capturing population genetic differentiation. Findings demonstrate machine learning and generative models are valuable for understanding malaria vector evolution, including demography and natural selection.

articles.headline: Intensive malaria control drives parasite population collapse
articles.title_original: Impact of intensive control on malaria population genomics in Eastern Myanmar.
articles.authors: Li X, Arya G, Thu A, Landier J, Parker D, Delmas G, Reyes A, Lwin K, Sriprawat K, Nosten F, Anderson T
articles.details: Genome sequencing of 2270 P. falciparum infections from malaria posts in Kayin State over 58 months (2015–2020) was conducted following intensive control (rapid detection/treatment and MDA) that reduced transmission by 97%. Parasites were genetically depauperate with 1726 infections comprising 166 unique genomes (≥90% identity-by-descent); 9 families accounted for 62.5% of parasites. Artemisinin resistance alleles showed minimal change until 2020 when kelch13-R561H became predominant; MDA produced parasite founder effects and positive relatedness correlations for ≤20 km distances, revealing transmission chains.

articles.headline: Severe malaria causes massive placental hemorrhage in pregnancy
articles.title_original: A rare presentation of massive subamniotic hemorrhage in a pregnant woman with severe malaria: A case report.
articles.authors: Tsegaye MA, Abchale AB, Elssa MW, Mekuria KN, Biza NK, Gemechu FA, Tesfay RH
articles.details: A 28-year-old pregnant woman with severe P. falciparum infection developed massive subamniotic hemorrhage resulting in preterm delivery. The placenta contained approximately 800 mL of lysed blood. The mother recovered with minimal postpartum bleeding, but the neonate died in the neonatal ICU from prematurity complications. This case emphasizes the importance of early detection and management of placental hematomas in pregnant women with malaria.

articles.headline: Malaria infection causes amino acid and micronutrient depletion
articles.title_original: Malaria-induced dysregulation of selected micronutrients (Ca, Mg, Na, K, Zn, Fe, Co) and plasma free amino acids; implications for pathogenesis and host immunity in a Nigerian population.
articles.authors: Ekeanyanwu CR, Chikezie PC, Ekeanyanwu CL
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 200 Nigerian participants (malarious and healthy controls) measured plasma amino acids by HPLC and micronutrients (calcium, magnesium, sodium, potassium, zinc, iron, cobalt) by ICP-MS. Malaria infection caused significant alterations (p < 0.01) in amino acid profiles, with depletion of essential amino acids (leucine, valine) and immune-related amino acids (arginine, tyrosine, tryptophan, glutamine, methionine, cysteine). Malarious individuals exhibited significant deficiencies in calcium, sodium, zinc, and iron (p < 0.01). Results suggest malaria induces complex metabolic dysregulation that may contribute to disease severity.

articles.headline: TRAP glycosylation essential for P. falciparum mosquito-stage transmission
articles.title_original: Glycosylation of Plasmodium falciparum TRAP is essential for infection of mosquito salivary glands.
articles.authors: Gupta P, Rezakhani N, Pazzagli L, Patel H, Zanghi G, Kamil M, Watson A, Vigdorovich V, Camargo N, Knutson E, Sather DN, Vaughan AM, Swearingen KE
articles.details: CRISPR-generated P. falciparum TRAP glyco-null mutants (T256A, W250F) showed major fitness defects comparable to complete TRAP knockout. Sporozoites failed to productively colonize salivary glands, exhibited severely impaired gliding motility and hepatocyte invasion. Protein abundance was significantly reduced despite normal transcript levels, demonstrating that TRAP TSR glycosylation (O-fucosylation at Thr256 and C-mannosylation at Trp250) is critical for proper protein expression and mosquito-stage function.

articles.headline: Graphene SPR biosensor for stage-specific P. falciparum malaria detection
articles.title_original: Design and simulation of a graphene-integrated SPR biosensor for malaria detection.
articles.authors: Tene T, Arias Arias F, Paredes-Páliz KI, González García JC, Bonilla García N, Vacacela Gomez C
articles.details: Theoretical simulation of graphene-integrated surface plasmon resonance (SPR) biosensor using silicon nitride and thiol-tethered ssDNA for label-free malaria detection. Two system configurations (Sys3 and Sys4) were optimized for sensitivity across parasite stages: ring, trophozoite, and schizont. Sys3 achieved sensitivities of 353.14, 291.14, and 263.26°/RIU respectively, exceeding comparable SPR platforms. Design demonstrates potential for point-of-care stage-specific malaria diagnostics, though limited to simulation.

articles.headline: Novel kelch13 A675T and widespread drug resistance in Burundi P. falciparum
articles.title_original: A Novel Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 A675T Mutation and High Levels of Chloroquine and Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine Resistance in Burundi.
articles.authors: Kayode T, Niyukuri D, Holzschuh A, Da Silva G, Huwe T, Lerch A, Nyandwi J, Koepfli C
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of 157 P. falciparum isolates from Cibitoke Province, Burundi identified a novel pfkelch13 A675T mutation (with A675V validated in Rwanda), though no validated artemisinin-resistance-associated kelch13 mutations were found. Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) resistance was widespread, with quintuple haplotypes in 82.3% and sextuple haplotypes in 12.3% of mono-infections. Prevalence of pfcrt IET (94.9%) and pfmdr1 N-F-D (57.9%) indicate sustained chloroquine resistance. Findings suggest emerging artemisinin resistance and partner drug resistance threatening malaria control programs.

articles.headline: K13 R622I dominance and RDT-negative phenotype in northern Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Artemisinin resistant kelch13 R622I and RDT negativity approaching predominance in northern Ethiopia and emerging C580Y of African origin threaten falciparum malaria control.
articles.authors: Zeleke AJ, Fola AA, Tollefson GA, Niaré K, Leonetti A, Taropawala O, Marglous J, Crudale R, Brhane BG, Assefa A, Kiyuka P, Parr JB, Hailu A, Aemero M, Bailey JA
articles.details: Year-long molecular surveillance of 903 P. falciparum cases in northwest Ethiopia identified k13 R622I mutation in 44.3% (52% in Gondar Zuria vs 40% in Tach Armachiho, p<0.001), with markedly higher RDT negativity in R622I mutants (48.3% vs 30.7% in wild-type, p<0.001). Ninety-eight percent of R622I samples carried the mdr1 NFD haplotype. Whole-genome sequencing identified k13 C580Y in 2 patients (0.4%) with clonal nature and distinct flanking haplotypes from Southeast Asian origin, suggesting local de novo emergence—the first Horn of Africa report.

articles.headline: DNA repair pathways differentiate core and subtelomeric genome regions in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum DNA repair dynamics reveal unique roles for TLS polymerases and PfRad51 in genome diversification.
articles.authors: Vishwanatha A, Zhang X, Liu YJ, Leung A, Herring M, Chan A, Calhoun S, Deitsch K, Kirkman L
articles.details: Gene-knockout studies in P. falciparum examining DNA repair mechanisms revealed distinct pathways between conserved core genome and diverse subtelomeric regions. PfRad51 was essential for homologous recombination (HR)-mediated repair in the core genome, while Rad51-independent homology-directed repair operated in subtelomeric regions across all knockout lines (PfRad51Δ, PfPolζΔ, PfRev1Δ). Translesion synthesis (TLS) polymerase knockouts showed cell-cycle-dependent irradiation hypersensitivity, while PfRad51 deletion showed uniform sensitivity. Differential DNA damage responses maintain chromosomal distinction in genome diversification.

articles.headline: Low artemisinin resistance but sustained drug-pressure markers in Brazilian P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Tracking Drug Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum: Genetic Diversity of Key Resistance Markers in Brazilian Malaria Hotspots.
articles.authors: de Abreu-Fernandes R, de Queiroz LT, Almeida-de-Oliveira NK, de Lavigne Mello AR, de Aguiar Barros J, Pratt-Riccio LR, Melo GC, Brasil P, Daniel-Ribeiro CT, Menard D, Ferreira-da-Cruz MF
articles.details: Genetic sequencing of 141 P. falciparum samples collected from Brazil (2013–2023) identified no pfk13 mutations associated with artemisinin resistance, supporting current ACT efficacy. The NYCDY haplotype dominated pfmdr1 (70%), with rare N86Y (4%) and no Y184F polymorphisms; half of samples carried the SVMNTMCGI haplotype in pfcrt. Greater genetic diversity was observed in pfcrt than pfmdr1. Significant intragenic linkage disequilibrium in pfcrt and pfmdr1, but not between genes, reflects ongoing drug pressure and underscores the need for continuous surveillance.

articles.headline: Optimize MDA and antimalarial strategies under drug resistance to prevent progression
articles.title_original: Optimizing MDA and antimalarial treatment in the presence of drug resistance for effective malaria control.
articles.authors: Nimpa MM, Teytsa HN, Mbang J, Wondji CS, Djidjou-Demasse R
articles.details: Compartmental mathematical model evaluating mass drug administration (MDA) and antimalarial treatment strategies under varying drug-resistance prevalence. Global sensitivity analysis identified critical factors affecting malaria dynamics: MDA coverage, treatment access across infection states, mutation rates from sensitive to resistant parasites, and initial resistance prevalence. Optimal control analysis showed that at low mutation rates, combined antimalarial drugs and MDA with gradual antimalarial reduction over time ensured sustainable control; at higher mutation rates, strategies should prioritize broader MDA deployment while reducing antimalarial reliance to minimize resistance development.

articles.headline: Ungulate Plasmodium forms distinct lineages separate from primate and rodent parasites
articles.title_original: Bringing to light unnoticed data on the genetic and host diversity of ungulate Plasmodium.
articles.authors: Makanga BK, Boundenga L, Bitome-Essono PY, Arnathau C, Rougeron V, Prugnolle F
articles.details: Phylogenetic reanalysis of previously overlooked genetic data on Plasmodium infecting African ungulates (genera Tragelaphus, Syncerus, Cephalophus) revealed that ungulate Plasmodium parasites form a monophyletic group distinct from those infecting primates, rodents, and bats. Two main clades were identified, with Cephalophus parasites clustering with previously described sequences while Tragelaphus and Syncerus form distinct lineages representing likely novel species. The analysis clarifies evolutionary relationships and highlights the need for expanded knowledge of ungulate Plasmodium diversity in underexplored regions and host species.

articles.headline: Liver-targeted CD81 siRNA blocks P. falciparum sporozoite infection in vivo
articles.title_original: Protection from malaria infection using liver-targeted siRNA.
articles.authors: Steel RWJ, Schepis A, Nguyen T, Milstein S, Yucius K, Tu HC, Fishilevich E, Haslett P, Kappe SHI
articles.details: CD81-targeted, GalNAc-conjugated small interfering RNA (siRNA) was used to silence hepatic CD81, the critical entry receptor for P. falciparum sporozoites. In a mouse malaria model, CD81 siRNA blocked parasite liver infection in a dose-dependent manner and prevented the onset of blood-stage infection. Results were confirmed in human liver-chimeric mice, where the approach reduced P. falciparum infection, demonstrating siRNA as a promising host-based prevention strategy targeting the pre-erythrocytic stage.

articles.headline: IRS reduced malaria transmission by 10% in Nigerian pilot intervention
articles.title_original: Modelling the Effect of Indoor Residual Spraying on Malaria Transmission in Nassarawa State, North-central Nigeria.
articles.authors: Suleiman AG, Kareem L, Obi C
articles.details: SIRS compartmental model of malaria transmission was fitted to surveillance data from a Nassarawa State Local Government Area (LGA) where indoor residual spraying (IRS) was conducted (2012–2013). The estimated transmission rate in the intervention LGA was lower than in a control LGA (0.95 versus 1.05), with IRS activities reducing the transmission rate by 10% over two years. The modest decrease was attributed to implementation factors and data-collection uncertainties, highlighting the need for robust surveillance and evaluation strategies to properly assess IRS impact on disease transmission.

articles.headline: Ultra-sensitive RDT superior to conventional RDT in asymptomatic pregnant women
articles.title_original: Comparison of diagnostic accuracy of ultra-sensitive and conventional rapid diagnostic tests for malaria in asymptomatic pregnant women at a tertiary hospital in North-Central Nigeria.
articles.authors: Anenga UM, Swende TZ, Hembah-Hilekaan SK
articles.details: Cross-sectional comparison of conventional (co-RDT) and ultra-sensitive rapid diagnostic tests (us-RDT) for P. falciparum detection in 107 asymptomatic pregnant women (antenatal clinic, Nigeria) using microscopy as reference standard. Overall P. falciparum prevalence was 14% by microscopy, 12.1% by us-RDT, and 2.8% by co-RDT. The us-RDT demonstrated superior diagnostic performance: sensitivity 66.7% vs 13.3%, positive predictive value 83.3% vs 50.0%, negative predictive value 94.7% vs 87.4%, and test accuracy 93.5% vs 86.0% (p=0.002), with similar specificity (97.8%).

articles.headline: Imported malaria epidemiology and diagnostic challenges in Chengdu City 2016–2023
articles.title_original: [Epidemiological characteristics of imported malaria cases in Chengdu City from 2016 to 2023].
articles.authors: Feng L, Wang L, Jiang X, Tan K, Li P
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Surveillance of 463 imported malaria cases in Chengdu City (2016–2023) showed P. falciparum predominance (71.27%) with cases returning primarily from Africa (94.17%). Cases peaked in May, June, and December among male migrant laborers (male:female 21.05:1) aged 20–50 years, with 35.85% presenting for healthcare on disease onset day. Definitive diagnosis was made at initial presentation in 74.78% (341/456). Diagnostic accuracy varied substantially by institution type: county-level 91.43%, city-level 82.45%, provincial 80%, with diagnostic capacity low in township (11.11%) and village clinics (0%), indicating need for improved malaria diagnostics in grassroots healthcare institutions.

articles.headline: Adolescent malaria vaccine willingness and demographic factors
articles.title_original: Willingness of secondary school adolescents to get vaccinated against Malaria: a cross-sectional study in Enugu, South-East Nigeria.
articles.authors: Nduagubam OC, Ossai EN, Chinawa AT, Onukwuli VO, Uwaezuoke NA, Okafor CN, Chinawa JM
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 496 secondary school adolescents from six schools. Overall 68.1% willing to receive malaria vaccine. Age <15 years: AOR=2.2 (95%CI: 1.1–4.7); males: AOR=0.6 (95%CI: 0.4–0.9) vs females; good vaccine knowledge: AOR=2.2 (95%CI: 1.4–3.4) vs poor knowledge.

articles.headline: Propolis extracts inhibit Plasmodium falciparum in vitro
articles.title_original: In vitro and in silico investigations of Propolis-derived phytochemicals as potential inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Khan DA, Hasan MN, Boonhok R, Sungkanu S, Singhaboot Y, Shorna AA, Hasan A, Chotivanich K, Wilairatana P, Siyadatpanah A, Norouzi R, Sama-Ae I, Mitsuwan W, Paul AK, de Lourdes Pereira M, Sundar SS, Mahboob T, Wiart C, Labana RV, Chimplee S, Nissapatorn V
articles.details: In vitro and computational study of ethanol-extracted Propolis against P. falciparum NF54. Kermanshah propolis showed highest anti-Plasmodium activity (IC50=6.69±1.44 μg/mL). Tectochrysin and galangin exhibited strong binding affinity to PfLDH (−7.8 and −7.5 kcal/mol, respectively), superior to chloroquine and quinine. Molecular dynamics simulations confirmed stable protein-ligand interactions.

articles.headline: Malaria parasite population genomics during elimination program
articles.title_original: Malaria parasite population genomics during an elimination program in Eastern Myanmar.
articles.authors: Li X, Arya GA, Thu AM, Landier J, Parker DM, Delmas G, Reyes A, Lwin KM, Sriprawat K, Nosten F, Anderson TJC
articles.details: Sequencing of 2,270 P. falciparum genomes from 5,014 dried blood spots collected over 58 months (November 2015–August 2020). Parasites showed genetic depauperateness with 1,726 single-genotype infections comprising 166 unique genomes (≥90% IBD). Kelch13 diversity remained stable 2016–2019; by 2020 one clonal genotype (kelch13-R561H) predominated. Mass drug administration produced founder effects supporting intervention efficacy.

articles.headline: Plasmodium infection alters host odor and mosquito attraction
articles.title_original: [Changes in murine skin odors following Plasmodium infections and their impact on mosquito attraction].
articles.authors: Li J, Li M, He B, Liu T, Zhu F, Zhang J, Xu W
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Experimental study of 20 mice (10 infected with P. yoelii, 10 controls) over six days. Parasitemia increased to 51.00±3.61% by day 6; mosquito attraction increased from 50.45% to 66.84%. Infected mice showed elevated p-cresol, ethylbenzene, and nonanal but reduced toluene, benzaldehyde, and indole. Skin microbiota shifts included increased Streptococcus and Rothia, decreased Lactococcus.

articles.headline: Faculty enrichment program in applied malaria modeling
articles.title_original: Building contextually-relevant training programs for scientific development: process and lessons learned in implementing two iterations of a faculty enrichment program in applied malaria modeling.
articles.authors: Onyango L, Ouédraogo-Ametchie G, Stahlfeld A, Holden TM, Richter R, Runge M, Toh KB, Agorinya I, Mensah B, Gerardin J
articles.details: Two-cohort faculty enrichment program (2022–2023) training participants in applied malaria modeling for public health decision-making. Qualitative analysis found participants valued technical skill development, research abilities, and communication skills. Participants reported challenges with cross-disciplinary learning and adapting to new teaching approaches. Training programs should include continuous feedback mechanisms responding to emerging participant needs.

articles.headline: RAA-nanopore sequencing for Plasmodium species identification
articles.title_original: [Establishment and preliminary evaluation of recombinase-aided isothermal nucleic acid amplification combined with nanopore sequencing for identification of Plasmodium species].
articles.authors: Lin W, Chen L, Zhang C, Wei H, Tang C, Wang R, Lin L, Lin M
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Diagnostic validation of recombinase-aided isothermal amplification (RAA) combined with nanopore sequencing on 49 dried blood spot samples. RAA-nanopore achieved 92% sensitivity, 97.33% specificity, and 96% accuracy compared to nested PCR (nPCR) gold standard, superior to RT-qPCR (88.24% sensitivity, 97.32% specificity, 95% accuracy). Successfully identified P. vivax, P. ovale, P. malariae, P. falciparum, and mixed infections.

articles.headline: Malaria and malnutrition in tribal areas of India
articles.title_original: Malaria and malnutrition in tribal areas of India: implications for paediatric health.
articles.authors: Verma AK, Shrivastava S, Saxena N
articles.details: Mini-review examining intersection of malaria, malnutrition, and intestinal infections in tribal populations. Discusses how frequent malaria and intestinal infections alter gut flora, exacerbating malabsorption and malnutrition. Proposes integrated public health approaches addressing both nutritional and malaria-specific needs of tribal populations to break the cycle of poverty and infectious disease.

articles.headline: Plasmodium immune escape mechanisms and vaccine implications
articles.title_original: [Progress of researches on mechanisms underlying immune escape of Plasmodium].
articles.authors: Wu Y, He J, Cao X, Li J, Shen S, Du Y, Tan C
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Narrative review of Plasmodium immune escape strategies: antigenic variation, epigenetic regulation, IgM antibody antagonism, cGAS-STING signaling activation, splenic immune function suppression, and molecular camouflage. Parasites employ diverse mechanisms to evade host immune recognition and clearance, posing challenges to malaria control programs. Synthesis of these advances aims to inform development of more effective malaria vaccines and antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention reduces pediatric infections
articles.title_original: Indirect Benefits of Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention for Non-Malarial Pediatric Infections and Routine Antibiotic Use in Real-World Programmatic Settings: A Pre-Post Study Using Positive and Negative Controls.
articles.authors: Gebreegziabher EA, Ouattara M, Bountogo M, Coulibaly B, Boudo V, Ouedraogo T, Lebas E, Hu H, O'Brien KS, Hsiang MS, Glidden DV, Arnold BF, Lietman TM, Sié A, Oldenburg CE
articles.details: Pre-post comparison in rural Burkina Faso (51 health facilities) comparing periods during and after monthly SMC (sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine). Post-SMC period showed reductions in non-malarial diagnoses: pneumonia 0.7 per 1,000 person-weeks (95%CI: 0.3–1.0), diarrhea 0.2 (95%CI: 0.1–0.4), acute malnutrition 0.05 (95%CI: 0.001–0.09), and antibiotic prescriptions 0.90 (95%CI: 0.4–1.4).

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria and multi-organ dysfunction from P. vivax
articles.title_original: Cerebral Malaria and Multi-Organ Dysfunction Caused by Plasmodium vivax in an Adult: A case from Oman with a literature review.
articles.authors: Al Kindi SM, Chhetri S, Al Aamri ZZ, Dhuri SD, Al Balushi ZY, Khamis F
articles.details: Case report from Oman of a 38-year-old Pakistani male with P. vivax-confirmed cerebral malaria and multi-organ dysfunction syndrome (MODS), verified by peripheral blood smear and polymerase chain reaction. Patient was treated with intravenous artesunate and supportive intensive care, resulting in full neurological recovery. Case illustrates P. vivax as a rare but important cause of cerebral complications and severe malaria.

articles.headline: RTS,S and R21 malaria vaccines for sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Mitigation of Malaria in Sub-Saharan Africa through Vaccination: A Budding Road Map for Global Malaria Eradication.
articles.authors: Alum EU, Ainebyoona C, Egwu CO, Onohuean H, Ugwu OP, Uti DE, Alum BN, Echegu DA
articles.details: Narrative review of malaria vaccination strategies in sub-Saharan Africa. RTS,S/AS01 vaccine demonstrated moderate efficacy (30–55%) preventing malaria in children under five in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi. R21/Matrix-M candidate showed up to 77% efficacy. Implementation barriers include limited funding, vaccine hesitancy, poor healthcare infrastructure, and community misconceptions. Integration with routine immunization improves vaccine acceptance.

articles.headline: Rheumatoid factor in asymptomatic hepatitis B surface antigen carriers
articles.title_original: Prevalence of rheumatoid factor in asymptomatic chronic hepatitis B surface carriers in the context of endemic malaria: cross-sectional study at University Hospital of Cocody, Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.authors: Goran-Kouacou APV, Yéboah OR, Assi AUA, Séri YJ, Adou AH, N Guessan K, Dassé SR
articles.details: Cross-sectional study conducted in 2024 at a hospital in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire of 120 asymptomatic chronic HBsAg carriers without autoimmune disease or active malaria. Rheumatoid factor positivity prevalence 12.5% (95%CI: 6.54–18.46). Advanced age ≥30 years independently associated with positivity (aOR: 3.32; 95%CI: 1.06–10.39; p=0.04); no significant associations with sex or consultation reason.

articles.headline: Health education role in China's malaria elimination progress
articles.title_original: [Application of health education during the progress towards malaria elimination in China: a review].
articles.authors: Zhou M, Lin K
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Review of community-based, school-based, and media-based health education interventions during China's malaria elimination campaign. China achieved WHO certification of malaria-free status in 2021 following decades of integrated control efforts. Article discusses public awareness, prevention knowledge, and strategies to reduce re-establishment risk of imported malaria in the post-elimination stage.

articles.headline: Rapid diagnostic test sensitivity varies for P. vivax malaria
articles.title_original: [Comparison of Rapid Diagnostic Tests for the Detection of Plasmodium vivax Malaria].
articles.authors: Ku B, Jung H, Shin HI, Lee MR, Ju JW, Lee HI
articles.details: Comparison of four RDT kits approved in Republic of Korea for detecting all five human malaria parasites, using samples confirmed by nested PCR. Sensitivity ranged from 70.9–96.5% overall; low-parasitemia samples showed greater variation (43.8–93.8%). All kits achieved 100% specificity, identifying performance differences relevant for field diagnosis.

articles.headline: Reference genomes for malaria mosquito Anopheles darlingi
articles.title_original: Two chromosomal reference genome sequences for the malaria mosquito, Anopheles ( Nyssorhynchus) darlingi, Root, 1926 from French Guiana and Peru.
articles.authors: Gendrin M, Heu K, Moreno M, Gamboa D, Vinetz JM, Tong C, Conn JE, Johnson HF, Heaton H, Wagah MG, Collins JC, Krasheninnikova K, Pelan SE, Pointon DB, Torrance JW, Tracey A, Uliano-Silva M, Wood JMD, von Wyschetzki K, McCarthy SA, Lawniczak MKN, Neafsey DE, Makunin A
articles.details: Two chromosome-level genome assemblies of Anopheles darlingi from wild populations in French Guiana and Peru, each approximately 180 megabases with three chromosomal pseudomolecules and assembled X chromosome. Complete mitochondrial genomes are 15.4 kilobases. The two assemblies differ by inversions in chromosome arm 2R.

articles.headline: Chitosan nanocomposites enhance antimalarial activity in resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: In Vitro Antimalarial Activity of Chloroquine-Crocus Sativus Conjugated to Chitosan Nanocomposits against 3D7 and K1 Strains of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Khezri A, Nateghpour M, Motevali Haghi A, Elmi T, Rahimi Foroushani A, Shafii Ardestani M, Hanifian H
articles.details: In vitro study of chloroquine and saffron (Crocus sativus) conjugated to chitosan nanocomposites tested against sensitive and resistant P. falciparum strains. IC50 values in the resistant strain were 5 μg/ml (chloroquine), 1 μg/ml (nano-chloroquine), 12.5 μg/ml (saffron), and 3.12 μg/ml (nano-saffron). Combination therapy showed synergistic effects with statistical significance (P<0.05).

articles.headline: Severe P. falciparum malaria with acute kidney injury: full recovery
articles.title_original: Managing Acute Kidney Injury in Severe Falciparum Malaria: Insights from a Challenging Case.
articles.authors: Putra DE, Kasman I, Boro AMB, Purnama A, Zulfikar E
articles.details: Case report of a 22-year-old male from Indonesia with severe P. falciparum malaria (parasitemia 9.7%) complicated by acute kidney injury (serum creatinine 3.52 mg/dL or 311 μmol/L), pulmonary edema, and jaundice. Treated with intravenous artesunate for 6 days, followed by oral dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine for 3 days, single primaquine dose, and two hemodialysis sessions; achieved complete recovery without sequelae.

articles.headline: Avian malaria parasites alter metabolic rate and immune response
articles.title_original: Dynamics of resting metabolic rate and innate immune response in malaria-infected Eurasian siskins.
articles.authors: Erokhina M, Bushuev A, Platonova E, Khaitov V, Davydov A, Mukhin A
articles.details: Experimental study of juvenile siskins infected with Plasmodium relictum (SGS1, Northern Palearctic lineage) and P. ashfordi (GRW2, Central/Southern African lineage), measuring resting metabolic rate (RMR) and interleukin-6 (IL-6) as markers of disease energetic cost and innate immune response. SGS1 infection reduced both RMR and IL-6; GRW2 showed initial IL-6 reduction (not sustained) and distinct RMR dynamics.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria in pregnancy causes adverse maternal and infant outcomes
articles.title_original: Detrimental infant and maternal outcomes of undiagnosed asymptomatic malaria in pregnancy.
articles.authors: Beeson JG, Opi DH
articles.details: Article examining undiagnosed asymptomatic malaria infections during pregnancy in low malaria transmission areas, where most infections evade standard diagnostic tests. Reports that these missed infections have substantial negative impacts for both mother and developing fetus, though specific quantitative outcomes are not detailed in the abstract.

articles.headline: Spatial variation of P. falciparum in urban Bamako school children
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum infection in school-aged children in urban and peri-urban schools of Bamako, Mali.
articles.authors: Toure M, Kane F, Sanogo D, Keita S, Keita B, Aro AZ, Keita M, Konate D, Diawara SI, Yaro AS, Kone A, Magassa M, Kante S, Tangara CO, Tangara K, Coulibaly H, Cisse B, Thiam SM, Traore AS, Shaffer JG, Sogoba N, Diakite M, Doumbia S
articles.details: Cross-sectional prevalence study of 6–13-year-old children in urban and peri-urban schools of Bamako stratified into four ecological transmission zones. P. falciparum prevalence varied 0–15.5% between sites. Anemia was associated with 2.38-fold increased odds of infection (95% CI 1.57–3.61). Living in urban riverside areas conferred 3.37-fold increased odds (95% CI 2.05–7.45). Travel history and fever were not associated with infection (p>0.05).

articles.headline: Historical error: Korea's malaria elimination status misattributed
articles.title_original: Misattribution of Korea's malaria elimination status in 1979.
articles.authors: Seol R, Kim Y
articles.details: Historical source-based review examining the persistent incorrect belief that WHO declared Republic of Korea malaria-free in 1979. WHO records show the 1979 declaration actually applied to Democratic People's Republic of Korea; WHO/WPRO documented that malaria had not been eliminated in ROK as of 1980. The misattribution originated from misinterpretations in US DoD documents and propagated through unverified citations.

articles.headline: Plant extract nanocomposites restore malaria-damaged splenic architecture
articles.title_original: Histopathologic effect and immunomodulatory of Indigofera oblongifolia with silver nanoparticles against splenic injury by Plasmodium chabaudi infection in mice.
articles.authors: Murshed M, Al-Tamimi J, Alzaylaee H, Ahmeda H, Al-Quraishy S
articles.details: Mouse model study of Indigofera oblongifolia leaf extract conjugated with silver nanoparticles (IOLE AgNPs) versus chloroquine phosphate following Plasmodium chabaudi infection in female C57BL/6 mice. Histopathological and immunohistochemical analyses showed IOLE AgNPs more effectively restored splenic architecture, reduced inflammatory markers, and improved immune responses compared to chloroquine-treated and control groups.

articles.headline: P. falciparum carbonic anhydrase as antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum η-carbonic anhydrase.
articles.authors: Supuran CT, Capasso C
articles.details: Review of Plasmodium falciparum carbonic anhydrase (PfCA), an η-class carbonic anhydrase involved in pyrimidine biosynthesis. Carbonic anhydrase levels are elevated in parasite-infected red blood cells. Various inhibitor compounds achieved low nanomolar IC50 values. Acetazolamide and an ureido-substituted benzenesulfonamide demonstrated potent growth inhibition of P. falciparum-infected RBCs, though further structural characterization is needed.

articles.headline: Antifolate resistance mutations in zoonotic P. cynomolgi Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Molecular analysis of DHFR and DHPS gene mutations in Plasmodium cynomolgi from humans and macaques in Southeast Asia.
articles.authors: Sangsri R, Jongkon N, Choowongkomon K, Malaivijitnond S, Day NPJ, Dondorp AM, Imwong M
articles.details: Molecular analysis of DHFR and DHPS genes in Plasmodium cynomolgi from 9 human samples (Cambodia: 8 monoinfections, 1 mixed with P. vivax) and 29 macaque samples (Thailand). Human samples were 100% identical to the RO strain, carrying no DHFR mutations and one DHPS mutation (V451I). Macaque samples carried DHFR mutations N44T and C49S with two DHPS haplotypes (72.4% wild-type I7, 27.6% I7 mutation). Protein modeling indicated mutations did not significantly affect antifolate drug binding.

articles.headline: AI and health workers comparable in malaria RDT interpretation accuracy
articles.title_original: Malaria RDT (mRDT) interpretation accuracy by frontline health workers compared to AI in Kano state, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Frade S, Cooper S, Smedinghoff S, Hattery D, Ruan Y, Isabelli P, Ravi N, McLaughlin M, Metz L, Finette B
articles.details: A comparative study examined mRDT interpretations by frontline healthcare workers, trained panel readers, and AI-based computer vision algorithms in Nigeria. AI achieved a weighted F1 score of 96.4 versus 95.3 for FHWs, matching panel readings 96.38% for positives and 97.12% for negatives. Notably, AI correctly identified 90.2% of 163 faint positive tests versus 76.1% by FHWs, demonstrating AI's potential to enhance diagnostic accuracy in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Novel biosensor technologies advance malaria diagnostics in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Advances in malaria detection.
articles.authors: Ike BW, Kathiresan V, Miya L, Karpoormath R
articles.details: A review examined current and emerging malaria diagnostic tools, highlighting biosensor technologies using nanotechnology as alternatives to conventional detection methods. These user-friendly devices require minimal technical expertise and are ideal for point-of-care testing in resource-limited settings, offering improved monitoring capability and analytical performance to support malaria control efforts in high-burden zones.

articles.headline: IgA antibodies may enhance malaria immunity and vaccine effectiveness
articles.title_original: Targeting mucosal immunity in malaria control: the underexplored role of IgA.
articles.authors: Muwonge H, Ssewanyana I, Yeka A, Byakika-Kibwika P
articles.details: A review synthesized evidence on Immunoglobulin A (IgA) responses to malaria, examining its potential to block parasite entry, activate complement, and modulate inflammation. The authors explored vaccine platforms (oral, nasal, prime-boost) that might harness IgA alongside IgG to reinforce existing interventions and prevent parasite establishment at mucosal or skin interfaces, though protective efficacy remains unestablished.

articles.headline: Global malaria burden declined 1990-2021 with regional disparities remaining
articles.title_original: Global, regional, and national burden of neglected tropical diseases and malaria, 1990-2021.
articles.authors: Tuergan T, Abulaiti A, Tulahong A, Zhang R, Shao Y, Aji T
articles.details: A Global Burden of Disease analysis of malaria from 1990-2021 found decreasing case numbers and age-standardized rates, with positive correlation between estimated annual percentage change and human development index scores. Decomposition analysis highlighted positive impact of aging and epidemiological shifts on disease burden reduction. Projections forecast continued decline through 2046, though significant disparities remain across regions and countries.

articles.headline: VK210 PvCSP variant most prevalent globally; vector susceptibility varies
articles.title_original: Updated global distribution of Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein variants and their correlation with vector susceptibility: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Sampaio MP, Cerilo-Filho M, Amaral YAGD, Almeida MNG, Correa RI, Fernando DJV, Reis NF, Miceli LA, Silva JRS, Storti-Melo LM, Baptista ARS, Machado RLD
articles.details: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 94 studies examined Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein variants, finding VK210 as the most prevalent variant globally in humans and vectors. Meta-analysis revealed 22 of 94 Anopheles species (23.4%) were susceptible to PvCSP variants, with all 22 species (100%) susceptible to VK210 mono-infections and 14 species (63.6%) to VK247 mono-infections. The P. vivax-like variant was identified exclusively in Brazil and in vectors A. oswaldoi and A. daeneorum.

articles.headline: Priority-setting in malaria programs constrained by funding and alignment
articles.title_original: What are the priority-setting approaches for HIV/AIDS, TB and malaria programmes in Ghana? A qualitative perspective from key informants.
articles.authors: Aryeetey GC, Koduah A, Affram AA, Owusu R, Ruiz F, Vassall A, Nonvignon J
articles.details: A qualitative study of 10 key informants from Ghana's Global Fund-supported malaria programs identified four priority-setting approaches: health needs identification, stakeholder participation, transparency, and contextual factors. Main barriers included inadequate funding and the need to align priorities with funders' agendas. Explicit priority-setting approaches were limited in use despite global expansion of such frameworks.

articles.headline: Expansion microscopy enables ultrastructural visualization of malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Mosquito Tissue Ultrastructure-Expansion Microscopy (MoTissU-ExM) enables ultrastructural and anatomical analysis of malaria parasites and their mosquito.
articles.authors: Liffner B, Silva TLAE, Vega-Rodriguez J, Absalon S
articles.details: A study developed Mosquito Tissue Ultrastructure-Expansion Microscopy (MoTissU-ExM), a method that physically expands biological samples approximately 4.5-fold while preserving host and parasite ultrastructure. The technique enables visualization of oocysts and sporozoites in situ within dissected mosquito salivary glands and midguts, providing improved resolution compared to conventional light microscopy for studying malaria parasite cell biology.

articles.headline: Ellagic acid inhibits P. falciparum with greater potency than artemisinin
articles.title_original: Effects of Ellagic Acid on Hemoglobin Ingestion, Transport, and Digestion in the Digestive Vacuole of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Thuduhena AC, Abu-Bakar N, Hamzah SN
articles.details: An in vitro study examined ellagic acid's effects on chloroquine-sensitive (3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (W2) P. falciparum strains. Ellagic acid significantly inhibited both strains (P < 0.001), with IC50 values of 1.0 ± 0.7 nM and 1.3 ± 0.1 nM respectively, exceeding artemisinin's potency (IC50-3D7 = 2.2 ± 0.2 nM; IC50-W2 = 4.3 ± 0.3 nM). Transmission electron microscopy revealed disruption of hemoglobin metabolism in early trophozoite stages.

articles.headline: False-positive malaria RDT in African tick bite fever case
articles.title_original: False-Positive Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Test Likely Due to African Tick Bite Fever: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Zewude RT, Ahmad SZ, Joseph T, Boggild AK
articles.details: A case report describes a febrile returned traveler to South Africa with an initial false-positive malaria rapid diagnostic test, subsequently confirmed to have African tick bite fever through rickettsial serology. Subsequent RDT and malaria thick- and thin-film blood examinations failed to detect malaria parasites. The case highlights diagnostic test performance characteristics and the risk of premature diagnostic closure.

articles.headline: Complete chromosomal genome sequence generated for Anopheles nili mosquito
articles.title_original: A chromosomal reference genome sequence for the malaria mosquito, Anopheles nili, Theobald, 1904.
articles.authors: Nsango SE, Ayala D, Agbor JP, Johnson HF, Heaton H, Wagah MG, Collins JC, Krasheninnikova K, Pelan SE, Pointon DB, Sims Y, Torrance JW, Tracey A, Uliano-Silva M, Wood JMD, von Wyschetzki K, McCarthy SA, Neafsey DE, Makunin A, Lawniczak MKN
articles.details: A chromosomal reference genome assembly was generated for Anopheles nili from a wild population in Cameroon, spanning 195 megabases with three chromosomal pseudomolecules including the assembled X sex chromosome. The complete mitochondrial genome was also assembled at 15.4 kilobases in length, providing a reference sequence for the malaria mosquito vector.

articles.headline: P. falciparum dominance with spatio-temporal variation in Kenya highlands
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal characterization of malaria prevalence in a peri-urban resource limited setting of Western Kenya Highlands.
articles.authors: Muzame BA, Omukunda E, Mulama D, Okoth P
articles.details: A longitudinal study in Mbale town, Vihiga County, Kenya (December 2019–November 2020) recruited 768 malaria-confirmed patients. Plasmodium falciparum constituted 98.7% of cases, while P. malariae, P. vivax, and P. ovale represented 1.3%. Linear regression analysis showed effects of age, gender, and location on prevalence (R² = 0.7), with malaria prevalence varying monthly in relation to rainfall and temperature changes.

articles.headline: Dehydration and fever associated with AKI in severe malaria patients
articles.title_original: Factors associated with acute kidney injury and outcomes in patients with malaria in a district hospital in Rwanda.
articles.authors: Umuhire L, Dushimiyimana V, Nkuranyabahizi M, Ngendahayo F, Shyaka JC, Ngerageze I, Rajeswaran L, Chironda G
articles.details: A retrospective study of 122 severe malaria patient files from a Rwanda district hospital (2016–2017) found acute kidney injury in 16.3% of cases. Clinical factors significantly associated with AKI were dehydration (p=.01), high-grade fever (p=.002), profuse sweating (p=.034), vomiting (p=.043), and diarrhea (p=.025). Of 20 patients with AKI, 55% recovered completely, 15% died, and 30% were transferred for hemodialysis.

articles.headline: Gender disparities in malaria-typhoid progression cultural beliefs among rural farmers
articles.title_original: The relationship between gender and cultural beliefs of malaria into typhoid progression among rural rice farmers in Central Kenya.
articles.authors: Woldu D
articles.details: Cross-sectional study (N=250) of Kikuyu rice farmers in central Kenya examining gender differences in cultural beliefs about malaria progressing to typhoid. Over 62% of women and 47% of men held this belief; women were more than twice as likely to believe in malaria-typhoid progression (aOR 2.15; 95% CI 1.21–3.79; p<0.009). Authors conclude cultural factors underlie gender disparities in understanding this belief system, with implications for women's health outcomes.
